title: A Hen Turkey Braised
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hen turkey
- turkey stuffing
- bacon sliced
- sheep's trotters
- Bombay onion sliced
- carrots
- chopped sweet herbs
- whole pepper corns
- lime peel
- clove of garlic
- gravy made from giblets, trimmings, and scraps
- wine-glassful of Madeira
- minced truffles
- potatoes
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Truss the bird nicely
2. Lard its breast
3. Stuff it with very carefully made turkey stuffing
4. Place it in a deep stew-pan upon a bed of sliced bacon
5. Put a couple of sheep's trotters, cut into small pieces, round it, with a sliced Bombay onion, two carrots, some chopped sweet herbs, whole pepper corns, lime peel, and a clove of garlic
6. Pour into the pan a good pint of gravy made from the giblets, trimmings, and any scraps, with a wine-glassful of Madeira
7. Cook the bird gently with live coals on the stew-pan lid, as well as under the vessel, for about three hours
8. Brown the larding on the breast by passing a red hot iron close to it
9. Strain the gravy, thicken it, add some minced truffles, and send it round in a boat
10. Serve with potatoes and cauliflower accompanying

title: A Two-Pound or One-Seer Plum Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lb. or 2 seers butter, perfectly free of water
- 2 lb. or 1 seer good clean sugar
- 2 lb. or 1 seer raisins, cleaned, stoned, and dried
- 2 lb. or 1 seer currants, cleaned, stoned, picked, and dried
- 2 lb. or 1 seer Jordan almonds, blanched and sliced very fine
- preserved ginger, orange-peel, lemon-peel, and pumpkin in small pieces and well dried, mixed
- 1 tablespoonful cinnamon, finely pounded and sifted
- 1 tablespoonful nutmegs, finely grated
- 1 tablespoonful dried orange-peel, finely pounded and sifted
- 2 tablespoonfuls English caraway-seeds, cleaned and picked
- 1 tablespoonful mace, finely pounded and sifted
- 1 lb. or 1/2 seer finely-sifted flour
- 1 lb. or 1/2 seer soojee
- 40 eggs, new or fresh laid
- 1 claret-glass brandy of the best quality

## Directions

1. Have two large glazed earthen preserving-pans; put the sugar into one and bruise it well down, breaking all the lumps
2. Add to it three pounds and three-quarters of butter
3. Throw in one by one all the yolks of the forty eggs, and throw the whites into the other preserving-pan
4. Mix the sugar, butter, and the yolks the whole while briskly and without ceasing
5. While one man is mixing these ingredients another ought to beat up the whites of the eggs unceasingly for nearly an hour
6. After the butter has been well mixed with the sugar and eggs, dredge in all the finely-pounded spices and the caraway-seeds
7. After a while dredge in the flour and soojee in small quantities at a time, well mixed
8. Add the currants, raisins, and preserves, with the almonds
9. Pour in the brandy very gradually and in small quantities at a time
10. Last of all add the well-beaten whites of the forty eggs, stirring very briskly to effect a perfect mixture
11. Fill quickly into the moulds, lined with paper and well buttered
12. Bake without a moment's delay in a brisk baker's oven

title: Achar of Amlaki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe and fresh amlaki fruits
- sugar
- powders of chillies
- pepper
- aniseed
- cardamom seeds
- pasted or powdered ginger
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Make a few deep cuts on each of some ripe and fresh amlaki fruits and remove the seeds if you can
2. Boil them in plenty of water till soft
3. Drain out the water and wash with fresh cold water
4. Drop them in a boiling syrup of sugar and boil for 20 or 25 minutes
5. Make a paste of powders of chillies, pepper, aniseed, cardamom seeds, pasted or powdered ginger in adequate quantities with little mustard oil
6. Smear the boiled fruits with it and preserve in an airtight jar

title: Achar of Arum
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- arum
- 1 seer of arum pieces
- 1 seer of green tamarind leaves or 1/2 seer of tamarinds
- 1 chhatak of salt
- 1/2 powa of mustard paste
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Peel an arum and cut in big pieces
2. Boil 1 seer of the pieces with 1 seer of green tamarind leaves or 1/2 seer of tamarinds till soft
3. Wash them with fresh water
4. Smear with 1 chhatak of salt and a paste of 1/2 powa of mustards
5. Dry them in the sun for 2 or 3 days
6. Keep them immersed in plenty of mustard oil
7. Let at least a week elapse before it is taken

title: Achar of Brinjals
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of brinjals
- 1.5 powa of thrashed green mangoes
- pasted ginger
- powdered mustard - 1 chhatak
- salt - 1 chhatak
- powdered black cumin - 0.5 chhatak
- flour-paste
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks of a few brinjals weighing 1 seer and make hollows within them by removing the cores with a knife.
2. Stuff them with thrashed green mangoes 1.5 powa mixed with pasted ginger, powdered mustard and salt 1 chhatak each and powdered black cumin 0.5 chhatak.
3. Close the openings with the stalks with a little flour-paste and dry them in the sun till the natural colour of the brinjals fades away and the surface becomes rough.
4. Then keep them immersed in oil.

title: Achar of Ginger
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of ginger
- 1 powa of salt
- 0.5 chhatak of powdered pepper
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Peel 1 seer of ginger and wash them.
2. Then cut them in pieces, smear with 1 powa of salt and 0.5 chhatak of powdered pepper and keep aside for a few hours.
3. Then keep them immersed in lemon-juice for a week, keeping all the while in the open atmosphere.
4. Then pick the pieces out and keep covered in a stone or earthen pot.

title: Achar of Lemon
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- lemons
- 1 powa of salt
- 1 seer of lemons

## Directions

1. Rub some lemons against a piece of blanket till all the minute oily particles on the skin of the lemons are completely removed
2. Make a few cuts on each, smear 1 powa of salt on 1 seer of the lemons and keep in an airtight glass jar
3. Keep the jar in the open atmosphere
4. Achar will be ready after about a month and can be preserved for about a year

title: Achar of Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green mangoes
- 1 powa of salt
- 1 seer of mango pieces
- powders of chillies
- powdered mustard
- salt
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks of a few green mangoes and cut them in halves
2. Throw away the seeds, mix 1 powa of salt with 1 seer of the pieces and dry them perfectly in the sun
3. Mix them with powders of chillies mustard and salt 1 chhatak each and enough mustard oil to saturate them, and keep in an airtight jar
4. Keep the jar in the sun for several days

title: Achar of Mangoes (French Preparation)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green mangoes
- salt
- powders of aniseed
- chillies
- cloves
- cinnamon
- paste of ginger
- cardamom seeds
- thread
- vinegar
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Peel a few green mangoes and cut them in such a way that the seeds are easily removed keeping the mangoes entire
2. Smear them with salt and dry them in the sun for 2 or 3 days
3. Fill up their interiors with a mixture of powders of aniseed, chillies, cloves and cinnamon, a paste of ginger and cardamom seeds
4. Bind them with pieces of thread and keep immersed in vinegar with a little mustard oil at the top in an airtight glass jar

title: Achar of Mangoes with Sugar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green mangoes
- salt
- powders of chillies
- pepper
- treacle or concentrated syrup of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the mangoes in halves and dry them in the sun after smearing with salt
2. Mix them with powders of chillies and pepper and enough treacle or concentrated syrup of sugar to saturate them
3. Keep them in an airtight jar and keep the jar in the sun for several days

title: Achar of Onion
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- pasted ginger
- salt
- powdered black cumin
- broken chillies
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel some onions and half boil them.
2. Then smear them with pasted ginger, salt, powdered black cumin and broken chillies in adequate quantities, and keep them immersed in vinegar in a covered jar.

title: Achar of Sajina Flower
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 powa of seedless ripe tamarinds
- water
- 1 powa of washed and perfectly dried sajina flowers
- 1 powa of salt
- 1 powa of mustard
- 0.5 chhatak of powdered chillies
- 1 powa of mustard oil
- pieces of green chillies (optional)
- dried pieces of brinjals (optional)

## Directions

1. Make a thick paste of 1.5 powa of seedless ripe tamarinds with a little water and mix it with 1 powa of washed and perfectly dried sajina flowers.
2. Also mix with 1 powa of salt and a paste of 1 powa of mustard and keep in an airtight jar for a day.
3. Then mix with 0.5 chhatak of powdered chillies and 1 powa of mustard oil, dry them in the sun for a whole day and keep in the jar.
4. Pieces of green chillies and dried pieces of brinjals may be added if desired.

title: Achar of Tamarind
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tamarinds
- salt
- mustard oil
- powders of chillies, mustard, aniseed and other spices (except turmeric)

## Directions

1. Shell some ripe tamarinds, mix with salt and dry them thoroughly in the sun.
2. Mix with enough mustard oil, powders of chillies, mustard, aniseed and any or every other spices except turmeric, and put them in an earthen pot.
3. Put the pot in the sun at intervals of 2 or 3 days with the lid open allowing the sunbeams to fall directly into it.

title: Adarsha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 powas sugar
- 1 seer besam of any kind of pulse
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil a syrup of 3 powas of sugar
2. Add a seer of besam of any kind of pulse
3. Stir vigorously till it is a very stiff dough
4. Take the pan down
5. Prepare cakes of the mass
6. Fry in plenty of ghee till brown

title: Akwari
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ seer ghee
- 1 seer coarse flour (atta)
- 1 powa curd
- ½ tola cardamom seeds
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Beat ½ seer of ghee vigorously till it is perfectly white
2. Mix with 1 seer of coarse flour (atta), 1 powa of curd and ½ tola of cardamom seeds
3. Knead the mass to a dough
4. Make balls of it and press them a little flat
5. Fry in plenty of boiling ghee to a light brown colour
6. Keep them immersed in a very concentrated syrup of sugar till saturated
7. Take them out in a dish and dry them in air

title: Alderman's Mock Turtle Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf's head
- brain cakes
- forcemeat and egg balls
- eight calves' feet
- soup herbs
- salt
- ground black pepper
- pie-pastry
- about twenty or thirty green leaves of spinach

## Directions

1. Make an extra rich hash of a calf's head, cutting the pieces from the cheeks two and a half to three inches long, and one and three-quarters to two inches wide.
2. Slice the tongue, and cut into large-sized shapes.
3. Prepare brain cakes, and plenty of forcemeat and egg balls.
4. Make an extra strong stock with eight calves' feet.
5. Season it highly with soup herbs, salt, and plenty of ground black pepper.
6. Simmer until the meat begins to drop away from the bones.
7. Strain through a coarse sieve, in order to get a very thick stock, passing as much of the dissolved meat through as possible.
8. Line a deep pie-dish with a thick and rich pie-pastry, and arrange in it the hash, egg and meat balls, and brain cakes, with some twenty or thirty green leaves of spinach, cut up to about the size and shape of the meat.
9. Pour over the whole as much stock as will fill the dish, cover over with pastry, and bake.

title: Almond and Curd Tartlets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- half pound sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- two ounces of blanched sweet almonds
- three bitter almonds
- two table-spoonfuls of orange flower water
- juice of four limes
- two potatoes
- a quart of milk
- four ounces of good butter
- one glass of brandy
- one glass of maraschino
- finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Line six tart-let tins with puff paste
2. Beat half pound sugar with yolks of six eggs in a basin
3. Pound together in a mortar two ounces of blanched sweet almonds, three bitter ones, two table-spoonfuls of orange flower water, the juice of four limes, and two potatoes (mealy ones) boiled, drained, and passed through a hair sieve
4. Mix the eggs, sugar, and almond paste
5. Turn a quart of milk to curd, crumble it, and beat it up with four ounces of good butter till quite smooth
6. Mix it thoroughly with the eggs, almonds, and other ingredients, adding a glass of brandy and one of maraschino
7. Fill the patties with this mixture
8. Bake till thoroughly hot in a moderate oven and serve with finely sifted sugar dusted over them

title: Almond Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a pound almonds
- two tablespoonfuls orange-flower water
- more than one pint milk
- one teaspoonful corn-flour
- yolks of six eggs
- pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound almonds with two tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
2. Add more than a pint of milk, thickened with a teaspoonful of corn-flour, and the well-beaten-up yolks of six eggs
3. Sweeten to taste with pounded loaf sugar
4. Stir it over a slow fire till it thickens, but do not allow it to boil
5. Serve up in glass custard-cups

title: Alternative Good Batter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- table-spoonful of salad oil
- table-spoonful of brandy
- yolk of one egg
- flour
- water
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. Beat up equal parts of salad oil and brandy, say a table-spoonful of each.
2. Add the yolk of an egg, and incorporate with this sufficient flour to make a thick paste.
3. Thin to the required consistency by the addition of water.
4. Reserve the whipped white of the egg to finish with.

title: Alu Chach-Chari
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee
- red chillies
- raw potatoes
- powdered pepper
- turmeric
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Heat some ghee with a few red chillies and brown a little in it pieces of some raw potatoes
2. Add powdered pepper, turmeric and salt
3. Stir a little and add water
4. Add a little more ghee when the water dries up, stir for a while and take down

title: Am-Sandesh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer of brownish powder-like sugar (kasir chini)
- one seer of hard chhana
- cardamom-seeds
- pieces of almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare a syrup of one seer of brownish powder-like sugar (kasir chini) and cool it.
2. Drive out the whey from chhana keeping it under high pressure for a few hours.
3. Make a fine paste of one seer of hard chhana without whey by pasting with the hands on a wooden plate.
4. Heat the syrup in a thick iron pot and when it is warm add the pasted chhana.
5. Stir constantly and vigorously with a wooden ladle for the whole sandesh will taste bitter if only slightly burned.
6. When the mass is sticky, take the pot off fire, place on a circular stand of straw and stir as before, adding cardamom-seeds, pieces of almonds and pistachios if desired, till cold.
7. Smear the interior of a mango-shaped cast with the juice of a kind of ginger scented like green mangoes each time as a ball of the sandesh is pressed into the cast to give the desired shape.
8. Transfer on a wooden plate when moulded into shapes.
9. Keep them in earthen pots.

title: Am-Satta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe and sweet mangoes
- ghee

## Directions

1. Wash any number of ripe and sweet mangoes, peel them and squeeze the juice from them
2. Strain the juice through a fine piece of linen and collect in several flat, stone or porcelain dishes smeared with ghee
3. Tilt the dishes so that the juice spreads evenly over the whole surfaces and dry them in the sun
4. Repeat the same process every day till the layer of dried mango juice in each dish is as thick as is desired
5. Scratch the extreme ends of each layer with the tip of a knife holding it against the centre of each dish, and slowly remove the layers intact from the dishes
6. Place them on a clean piece of linen and dry them perfectly in the sun
7. Smear them with a little ghee
8. Cut them in pieces and serve

title: American Corn
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen tender cobs of Indian corn
- a large pat of fresh butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Choose half a dozen tender cobs of Indian corn, they should be still greenish otherwise they will be too tough
2. Boil them, and then with a silver fork, strip the corn from the cob
3. When wanted for the table, melt a large pat of fresh butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
4. Empty the corn into the pan, stir it about adding butter, pepper and salt, till it is smoking hot
5. Serve

title: American Gingerbread
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter melted
- a pound and a half of dried and sifted flour
- the same quantity of brown sugar
- a quarter of a pound of pounded ginger
- nine eggs, the yolks and whites separately beaten
- one glass of rose-water
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Mix all well together, and beat for an hour
2. With a spoon spread it over flat tin pans, about the thickness of a penny-piece
3. Bake it of a light brown, and while warm cut it into oblong pieces, and place them on end till cool, when they will be very crisp

title: Amrit
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water
- milk
- egg yolks
- powdered garam-masalla
- saffron
- ghee

## Directions

1. Mix equal weights of sugar and water, and boil
2. If a little milk is added the impurities will rise to the surface in the form of scum, which is to be removed from time to time
3. Take down when the syrup is a bit sticky
4. Take the yolks of some eggs in a cup, add powdered garam-masalla, a little saffron and sugar, and beat thoroughly
5. Put the liquid in a clean thick linen piece with a small round hole in the middle and tie it up into a bag putting a thumb under the hole
6. Remove the thumb, press the bag with the fingers and allow the liquid to fall in a thin stream on plenty of boiling ghee
7. Take them out as soon as they are fried hard on both sides and put them in the syrup of sugar

title: Amrithi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 seer of sabeda
- 0.5 seer of middlings
- 3 powas of water
- a tea-spoonful of lime-water
- a pinch of saffron
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 seer of sabeda and 0.5 seer of middlings with 3 powas of water and a tea-spoonful of lime-water
2. Keep aside for 10 or 12 hours
3. Add a pinch of saffron and a little more water if necessary to make a thin paste
4. Fry as before in ghee in suitable designs, the common one being a big circle with a continuous spiral rings round its circumference
5. Keep immersed into a syrup of sugar

title: Amrithi with Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 powa of sabeda
- 1 chhatak of middlings
- 1 powa of water
- 0.5 seer of hard chhana
- a pinch of saffron
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1.5 powa of sabeda and 1 chhatak of middlings with 1 powa of water
2. Keep aside for 10 or 12 hours
3. Then mix with a fine paste of 0.5 seer of hard chhana, a pinch of saffron if desired and an adequate quantity of water making a thick paste
4. Fry Amrithi or Jilapi with this paste as before
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Amritti
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- rice-flour
- water
- eggs
- saffron
- ghee

## Directions

1. Keep some rice-flour moistened with water for several hours
2. Mix it with double its quantity of the contents of eggs and beat thoroughly
3. Add a little saffron for colouring and proceed as with Amrit

title: Amritti with Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- tender minced meat
- fried and powdered khowa kheer
- besam of grams
- ginger-juice
- powdered cardamom seeds
- salt
- saffron
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make pastes of 1/2 powa of almonds and 1/2 seer of tender minced meat after boiling it
2. Mix these with 1 powa of fried and powdered khowa kheer, 1 seer of besam of grams, ginger-juice 1/2 chhatak, a little powdered cardamom seeds, salt, a pinch of saffron and about 3 powas of water
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Put this semi-liquid mass in a pot or linen-bag with a small hole at the bottom shutting it with a thumb
5. Bring it above a pan of boiling ghee and let the liquid fall in a stream in the pan in round spirals making any design
6. Fry both sides by turning them
7. Drop them in a syrup of sugar
8. Serve after about an hour when they are saturated with the syrup

title: Amsi or Amchur
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mature yet green mangoes
- 1 chhatak of pasted turmeric
- 1 seer of mango pieces
- 1 powa of salt

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks of some mature yet green mangoes and cut them in four pieces without peeling them
2. Remove the seeds and smear 1 chhatak of pasted turmeric with 1 seer of the pieces
3. Wash them after a few hours and again mix 1 powa of salt with them and dry them perfectly in the sun for several days

title: Anchovy Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six anchovies in oil
- yolks of two raw eggs
- four slices of bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Take six anchovies, wipe them free from oil, split them open, remove the spines, and pass the fish through a hair sieve
2. Put the pulp in a bowl and stir into it the yolks of two raw eggs
3. Cut four nice rounds or slices of bread and fry them in butter till of a bright golden tint
4. Arrange them on a very hot silver dish and cover them up
5. Melt a table-spoonful of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan which should be dipped into a bain-marie or any vessel containing boiling water

title: Anchovy Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six anchovies in oil
- yolks of two raw eggs
- four slices of bread
- butter
- table-spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Wipe anchovies free from oil
2. Split them open and remove the spines
3. Pass the fish through a hair sieve
4. Put the pulp in a bowl and stir into it the yolks of two raw eggs
5. Cut four nice rounds or slices of bread and fry them in butter till of a bright golden tint
6. Arrange them on a very hot silver dish and cover them up
7. Melt a table-spoonful of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan which should be dipped into a bain-marie or any vessel containing boiling water
8. Stir into the melted butter the anchovy pulp mixture and let it thicken
9. When quite hot, pour it over the four toasts and send the dish up immediately

title: Another Dentifrice Recipe
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of borax
- three pints of boiling water
- a teaspoonful of tincture of myrrh
- a tablespoonful of spirits of camphor

## Directions

1. Dissolve two ounces of borax in three pints of boiling water
2. Before quite cold, add a teaspoonful of tincture of myrrh and a tablespoonful of spirits of camphor
3. Bottle the mixture for use
4. One wineglassful of the solution, with half a pint of tepid water, is sufficient for each application

title: Another Recipe
body:
## Ingredients

- lard, four ounces
- castor oil, an ounce
- spermaceti, a quarter of an ounce
- salad oil, an ounce and a half
- white wax, a quarter of an ounce
- tincture of lytse, a drachm and a half
- oil of roses, verbena, bergamot, or cloves, twenty drops

## Directions

1. Melt the wax, spermaceti, and lard with the oils in a glazed earthen pipkin
2. When nearly cold add the scent

title: Another Recipe
body:
## Ingredients

- beef marrow, three-quarters of a pound
- spring water
- rose-water
- storax, half an ounce
- gum benjamin, half an ounce
- odoriferous cyprus-powder, half an ounce
- cinnamon, two drachms
- cloves, a drachm
- white of egg
- flour
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Clean beef marrow well from bone and fibre
2. Wash it in water fresh from the spring, which must be changed night and morning for ten days
3. Steep it in rose-water for twenty-four hours
4. Drain it
5. Take half an ounce each of storax, gum benjamin, and odoriferous cyprus-powder, two drachms of cinnamon, and a drachm of cloves
6. Let these ingredients be all powdered and well mixed with the marrow
7. Put them in a pewter pot which holds about a pint and a half
8. Make a paste of white of egg and flour, and lay it on a piece of linen
9. Place a second linen to cover the pot very tight and keep in the steam
10. Place the pot in a copper vessel of water, and keep it steady, so that the water may not reach or touch the covering
11. As the water evaporates, add more, boiling hot
12. Keep it boiling four hours without ceasing
13. Strain the pomade into small jars or boules
14. Cork when quite cold
15. Take care to touch it only with silver

title: Another Variety of Sweets of Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- raisins 2 tolas
- pieces of almonds 2 tolas
- pieces of pistachios 1 tola
- cardamom seeds 1/2 tola
- powdered aniseed 1/4 tola
- cows' ghee 1 teaspoonful
- khowa kheer 1 chhatak
- crystals of sugar-candy 1 chhatak
- very little camphor
- sugar 3 powas
- scraped and pasted kernel of not-so-ripe cocoa-nut 3 powas
- pasted hard chhana 1 powa
- 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Fry raisins, pieces of almonds, pieces of pistachios, cardamom seeds and powdered aniseed with a teaspoonful of cows' ghee
2. Mix with khowa kheer, crystals of sugar-candy and very little camphor
3. Boil a syrup of three powas of sugar in a brass pot
4. Add 3 powas of scraped and pasted kernel of not-so-ripe cocoa-nut and 1 powa of pasted hard chhana
5. Stir vigorously on mild heat till the mass sticks to the sides of the pot and ladle
6. Take off the fire and mix thoroughly with 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose
7. Make balls of it when slightly warm
8. Stuff with the spiced kheer
9. Press in casts
10. Eat them up within a week

title: Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- one pound of butter
- four eggs
- twelve ounces of loaf sugar powdered very finely
- candied peel
- caraway comfits

## Directions

1. Rub the butter and sugar into the flour with the hand, and by means of the eggs convert it into a stiff paste
2. Roll it out half an inch thick, and cut into square or round cakes
3. Pinch up the edges to the height of about an inch, and on the top of each cake place some slices of candied peel and some large caraway comfits, pressed down so as to imbed about half of each in the cake
4. Bake in a warm oven upon iron plates

title: Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- one pound and a quarter of treacle
- half a pound of sugar
- two ounces of ginger
- three-quarters of a pound of butter (melted)
- a small quantity of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Mix all together and roll out to about the thickness of half an inch, or not quite so much
2. Cut into cakes, and bake in a moderate oven

title: Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven pounds of currants well picked and cleaned
- Half a pound of finely-chopped beef suet
- Half a pound of the lean of sirloin of beef minced raw
- Half a pound of citron, lemon, and orange peel cut small
- Two pounds of fine moist sugar
- One ounce of mixed spice
- The rinds of four lemons and four oranges
- One bottle of brandy
- White wine
- The juice of the four lemons and oranges

## Directions

1. Mix well the currants, suet, beef, citron, lemon and orange peel, sugar, mixed spice, and rinds of lemons and oranges
2. Put in a deep pan
3. Mix the bottle of brandy and white wine and the juice of the four lemons and oranges
4. Pour half over, and press down tight with the hand
5. Then add the other half and cover closely
6. It may be made one year, to use the next

title: Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of three new-laid eggs
- One pound of sifted white sugar
- Flour for the cake

## Directions

1. Beat to a stiff froth the whites of three new-laid eggs, and add to them one pound of sifted white sugar
2. Flour the cake, and then wipe it off
3. Apply the icing by means of a knife smoothly
4. Then bake in a slow oven

title: Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- some green mangoes
- cold milk which has been previously boiled

## Directions

1. Boil to a pulp some green mangoes without peel or stones
2. Pass through a sieve, and sweeten to taste
3. Mix into it very gradually some cold milk, which has been previously boiled
4. Keep stirring until it has acquired the thickness of an ordinary cream custard
5. Fill into glass cups
6. Grate a little cinnamon or nutmeg over them

title: Another Way to Boil Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash them very clean, put them on in cold water, cover the saucepan, and let them boil quickly
2. As soon as the water boils pour it off, and cover them with cold water
3. Add a little salt, and when the water boils pour it off instantly
4. Dry them, and take off the skins before serving

title: Another Way to Boil Spinach
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- salt
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. After being nicely picked and well washed, put it into a saucepan, with no more water than adheres to it
2. Add a little salt
3. Cover the pan closely, and boil it till tender, frequently shaking it
4. Beat it quite smooth, adding butter and cream, and make it quite hot
5. Spinach may be served with poached eggs, or fried sausages laid on it

title: Apple Charlotte
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- bread
- apples
- apricot jam
- lime juice
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Butter a small but deep pie-dish or plain mould
2. Cut a thin piece of bread to form the top of the charlotte the size and shape of the top of the mould, butter it on both sides, and put it into the mould
3. Line the side of the mould with moderately thin strips of bread and butter, buttered on both sides
4. Cover both the inverted top and sides of the mould, making a case of bread and butter
5. Within the case place layers of apples cut small with apricot jam spread between each layer, some lime juice sprinkled over them all, and a good allowance of white sugar
6. When packed, cover the bottom of the mould with slices of bread and butter, the same as that used for the top and sides
7. Bake till the bread browns nicely
8. Use plenty of good butter
9. Turn it out very carefully
10. Serve with cold custards accompanying the charlotte

title: Apple Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- water
- bit of lemon-peel
- bit of butter the size of a walnut
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and slice some apples
2. Boil them in water with a bit of lemon-peel
3. When tender, mash them
4. Add to them a bit of butter the size of a walnut, and some brown sugar
5. Heat, and serve in a sauce-tureen

title: Apricot Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabool apricots or those preserved for tarts
- sugar
- apricot stones blanched
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash the apricots thoroughly in several waters
2. Parboil and reduce them to a pulp
3. Pass them through a sieve, rejecting all the skin
4. Add sugar as directed in the foregoing recipe and a handful or two of the apricot stones blanched
5. Boil the whole until it has thickened sufficiently
6. Pour it into buttered moulds
7. Put it into an expiring oven or some warm place to dry
8. When quite firm turn it out of the moulds

title: Apricot Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabool apricots or apricots preserved for tarts
- sugar
- apricot stones

## Directions

1. Take the Cabool apricots or those preserved for tarts
2. If the former, wash them thoroughly in several waters
3. Parboil and reduce them to a pulp
4. Pass them through a sieve, rejecting all the skin
5. Add sugar as directed in the damson cheese recipe
6. Add a handful or two of the apricot stones blanched
7. Boil the whole until it has thickened sufficiently
8. Pour it into buttered moulds
9. Put it into an expiring oven or some warm place to dry
10. When quite firm turn it out of the moulds

title: Apricot Cream à la Moscovite
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- apricot jam
- pint and a half of milk
- yolks of ten eggs
- ounce and a half of dissolved gelatine
- sugar
- half a pint of whipped cream
- a glass of noyeau

## Directions

1. Pass a pot of apricot jam through a fine sieve
2. Boil a pint and a half of milk
3. Mix into it when cool the yolks of ten eggs and make a rich custard
4. Stir into the custard when cool, an ounce and a half of dissolved gelatine, and then the strained jam
5. If not sweet enough, add a little sugar
6. Set the liquid in a mould upon ice
7. When you perceive that it is beginning to congeal, add half a pint of whipped cream, and a glass of noyeau
8. Stir the contents of the mould together
9. Bury it in ice for at least two hours
10. Serve as cold as possible

title: Apricot Effervescing Drink
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint juice of bruised apricots
- half a pound sugar
- one ounce tartaric acid
- water
- two tablespoonfuls syrup
- one scruple carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Filter until clear the juice of bruised apricots
2. Make into a syrup with half a pound of sugar
3. Add one ounce of tartaric acid
4. Bottle and cork well
5. To a tumbler three parts full of water add two tablespoonfuls of the syrup and a scruple of carbonate of soda
6. Stir well and drink while effervescing

title: Apricot Iced Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar
- two tablespoonfuls of apricot jam
- the juice of a lemon
- half an ounce of blanched bitter almonds pounded with a little rose-water
- a pint of cream

## Directions

1. Mix a tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar with two of apricot jam, the juice of a lemon, and half an ounce of blanched bitter almonds pounded with a little rose-water
2. add a pint of cream, stir all well together before putting it into the freezing-pot, and freeze it as directed above

title: Artichoke Scallops
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- globe artichokes
- silver plated coquille shells
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- thick cream
- very finely sifted bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes
2. When cold, strip them of their leaves, scraping off all the pulp which adheres to them with a silver dessert-knife
3. Extract the chokes, and add the bottoms of the artichokes to the leaf pulp
4. Mash the whole together with a silver fork, dust it with salt and pepper
5. Mix a coffee-cupful of thick cream with it, stir it well
6. Butter the coquille shells and fill them with the artichoke puree
7. Dust over the surface a layer of very finely sifted bread-crumbs
8. Sprinkle some little bits of butter over the crumbs
9. Bake till thoroughly hot, brown the surface with a hot iron, and serve on a napkin

title: Arum Stalks with Hilsa Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hilsa fish heads, tails, gills and entrails
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- arum stalks
- ghee or oil
- cumin
- powdered cumin
- pepper
- coriander
- chillies
- water

## Directions

1. Break or cut the hilsa fish portions piecemeal and thoroughly wash them
2. Fry after smearing with powdered turmeric and salt
3. Peel fresh and tender arum stalks and cut in pieces an inch long
4. Boil and drain off the water
5. Boil ghee or oil with a few entire cumins
6. Add salt, powdered cumin, pepper, coriander and chillies and stir for 2 or 3 minutes
7. Add the boiled vegetable and stir for a few minutes more
8. Add water and on boiling add fish
9. Take down when no soup is left

title: Aska Pitha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Salt
- Scraped cocoa-nut kernel
- Water
- Oil

## Directions

1. Make a thick paste of powdered rice, salt and some scraped kernel of cocoa-nut, if desired, with water
2. Heat an earthen frying pot, smear with a little oil
3. Add a small tea-cupful of the paste and cover up
4. Take out when properly boiled using mild heat

title: Asparagus
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- fresh butter or Denmark tinned butter
- lime
- maitre d'hotel or anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Gently warm the asparagus in its own tin in the bain-marie-pan (immersed in a bath of hot water)
2. Drain it from its liquor as soon as it is hot
3. Turn it carefully into a hot dish
4. Put a pat of fresh, or of the Denmark tinned butter on the top of it
5. Give it a squeeze of a lime
6. Serve

title: Asparagus a la Francais
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolk
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Boil it, and chop small the heads and tender parts of the stalks, together with a boiled onion
2. Add a little salt and pepper, and the beaten yolk of an egg
3. Heat it up
4. Serve it on sippets of toasted bread, and pour over it a little melted butter

title: Asparagus Consomme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- clear, well-flavoured consomme for eight
- small tin of asparagus

## Directions

1. Open the tin of asparagus and place the pieces on a dish, draining all the liquor from the tin
2. Choose American asparagus if you can, because it is so much greener than either the French or English
3. When drained, cut the tender ends of the asparagus into pieces half an inch long using a dessert-knife
4. Put them away carefully, and throw the tough ends into the consomme
5. When strained and ready to serve, add to the soup the chopped green points
6. Beat it up to concert pitch, and serve

title: Aspic Jelly
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-foot
- bacon bone or slice or two of ham or bacon
- scraps of raw meat
- young fowl
- mixed vegetables
- cold water
- tea-spoonful of caramel
- sugar
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Put into a stew-pan an ox-foot cleaned and cut up with a bacon bone or a slice or two of ham or bacon
2. Add any scraps of raw meat such as cutlet trimmings or a young fowl cut up as for fricassee with a few mixed vegetables as for soup
3. Add a cup of cold water and cover the pan
4. Set it on the fire, shaking it occasionally
5. When the pieces of meat begin to take colour, add a little more water
6. In about half an hour, pour in enough water to cover the contents of the pan completely
7. Put in a tea-spoonful of caramel and let the vessel simmer for three hours very gently
8. When ready, strain the liquor off into a bowl and let it get cool
9. Skim it carefully and add a tea-spoonful of sugar and table-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
10. Clarify it and strain it finally through a clean cloth
11. This produces a clear, amber-coloured liquid which will set of its own accord without isinglass if put in the ice-box

title: Atap Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- two seers of water
- one seer of rice

## Directions

1. Heat water in a pan with two seers of water for every seer of rice
2. When water boils pour the rice, washed clean, in it and close the lid
3. After a few minutes when the boiling water becomes practically unmanageable let the surplus steam escape from the pan either by removing the lid entirely or by pushing it to one side
4. Take the minimum amount of water which will be enough for boiling them properly and yet not in excess to be removed
5. If in the middle of the preparation the water runs short add a little more water to prevent the rice from being scorched
6. Use slow heat in this method

title: Badshahi Khechuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried moog-dal i seer
- Rice |- seer
- Ghee i powa
- Potato ^ seer or more
- Onion |- seer
- Raisins ^ powa
- Almonds i chhatak
- Ginger 1 chhatak
- Pistachios ^ chhatak
- Salt ^ chhatak
- Cumin i tola
- Turmeric i tola
- Pepper i tola
- Garam-masalla ^ tola
- A few cassia leaves
- Red chillies
- One ripe cocoa-nut (if desired)
- Seeds of cardamoms
- Pieces of cinnamon
- Entire cloves

## Directions

1. Drop rice when the water boils
2. Drop pulse when the rice are half boiled
3. Then add salt and browned pieces of onions and potatoes
4. After this add browned pieces of pistachios, almonds and raisins
5. The pasted kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut or its milk may be added now if desired
6. Add pepper, cumin, turmeric and some red chillies, all powdered, when the boiling is almost complete
7. Then heat the ghee with broken red chillies, seeds of cardamoms, pieces of cinnamon, entire cloves and cassia leaves
8. Add this to the khechuri when the smell evolves
9. Pour in the juice of the ginger just before taking the boiling pot off the fire
10. Stir continually after adding the spices

title: Badshahi Roti
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Husked kalai pulses 1½ powa
- Ghee
- Curd ¾ powa
- Pasted ginger ½ chhatak
- Powdered garam-masala ½ tola
- Powdered pepper
- Salt
- Flour 1 seer
- Water

## Directions

1. Half boil 1½ powa of husked pulses of kalai
2. Fry them with ghee
3. Bind them with a clean piece of linen
4. Boil in steam above boiling water in a pot with the lid on
5. Make a paste of them when they are soft
6. Mix with curd ¾ powa, pasted ginger ½ chhatak, powdered garam-masala ½ tola, a little powdered pepper and salt
7. Again boil a little in steam
8. Mix 1 chhatak of ghee with 1 seer of flour
9. Make a stiff paste with water
10. Make medium balls, stuff them with the spiced paste of pulses
11. Roll into discs of medium thickness
12. Fry both sides of each on mild heat sprinkling ghee little by little
13. Take down when browned

title: Badshahi-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 15 to 20 eggs
- 3 powas of sugar-candy
- a table-spoonful of rose water
- 1/2 tola of musk

## Directions

1. Take the whites of 15 to 20 eggs in a deep pan and churn the mass with a churn-staff till the pan is filled with white foams
2. Add a syrup of 3 powas of sugar-candy and churn again till thoroughly mixed up
3. Heat the mass on mild heat and stir constantly
4. Take down when broken pieces of the whites of the eggs float on the surface
5. Mix with a table-spoonful of rose water and 1/2 tola of musk
6. Keep covered till it is served

title: Bael Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- bael fruit
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select fruit that is half ripe
2. Remove all the seeds and gum carefully
3. Pass the pulp through a coarse sieve into a preserving-pan with the help of a little water
4. Add sugar to taste
5. Simmer over a slow fire for half an hour or until the fruit and sugar have acquired the consistency of jam
6. Let it cool and then bottle

title: Bael Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- bael fruit
- strong syrup

## Directions

1. Select fruit that is rather less than half ripe
2. Cut it into firm slices a quarter of an inch thick
3. Carefully remove the seeds together with the gum by which they are surrounded
4. Throw the slices into cold water
5. When all the bael is ready, remove it from the water
6. Simmer it in a strong syrup over a slow fire for half an hour or until it has become of a rich light brown colour
7. Bottle it when cool, taking care that the fruit is well covered with syrup

title: Bael Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- one perfectly ripe sweet bael
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a perfectly ripe sweet bael, and scoop out the whole contents into a bowl
2. make a paste of it with a little water
3. then add sugar to taste, and as much water as will bring it to the consistency of good honey
4. then pass it through a fine sieve, leaving all the fibres and seeds behind

title: Bainequins or Little Fondues of Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of butter
- a quarter of a pint of water
- a pinch of salt
- black pepper
- two ounces of flour
- two ounces of grated Parmesan
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Put one ounce of butter in a roomy sauce-pan, with a quarter of a pint of water, a pinch of salt, and a dust of black pepper
2. Boil it, and add two ounces of flour
3. Stir over the fire for four minutes
4. Mix with it two ounces of grated Parmesan, and two eggs, one after the other
5. Put the paste thus formed on a buttered baking sheet in lumps the size of a hen's egg
6. Flatten them slightly
7. Brush them over with an egg
8. Bake in the oven
9. Serve on a napkin very hot

title: Baked Cauliflower
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cauliflower
- butter
- white pepper
- salt
- breakfast-cupful of veloute au fromage
- grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Choose a nice cauliflower and boil it carefully; it should be under rather than over-done.
2. Slice the stem evenly so that the cauliflower will sit up straight.
3. Place it in a neat dish that will stand the fire, well buttered, with the flower in the centre and the tender leaves neatly arranged round it.
4. Give it a dust of white pepper and a cream of salt.
5. Pour round, so that all the crevices may be filled, a breakfast-cupful of veloute au fromage.
6. Sprinkle a layer of grated Parmesan over the surface.
7. Bake the dish in a quick oven for ten minutes or a quarter of an hour.
8. By passing a red hot iron just over the top, brown the surface of the cheese.

title: Baked Crab
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice crab
- black pepper
- maccaroni
- a tea-cupful of mild cheese
- butter
- a coffee-cupful of well made white sauce
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Choose a nice crab, have it boiled, cleaned, and picked
2. Place the meat in a soup plate and give it a dust of black pepper
3. Boil till tender sufficient maccaroni to line the bottom of a small pie-dish, and grate a tea-cupful of mild cheese
4. Rub the bottom of the pie-dish with butter, place the maccaroni thereon, and pour a coffee-cupful of well made white sauce over the maccaroni
5. Shake a dust of grated cheese over its surface
6. Place the crab meat, well worked with melted butter, over the maccaroni, and, as you arrange it, dust it with black pepper
7. Over the crab dredge a nice dressing of the grated cheese, about one-eighth of an inch deep, and pour a little melted butter over the surface
8. Bake till the top takes colour, and serve

title: Baked Fillets of Snipe on Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight snipes
- stock
- vinegar
- red currant jelly
- sherry
- eight pieces of fried bread
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- crisply fried bread-crumbs
- Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Slightly roast eight snipes
2. Fillet them, saving the trails
3. Make the richest sauce you can of the bones, moistened with stock and helped up with vinegar, red currant jelly, and a little sherry
4. Prepare eight pieces of fried bread for the eight breasts and butter them
5. Spread the trails over them, pepper and salt them
6. Place a breast of snipe upon each trail toast
7. Bake till quite hot
8. Just before serving, pour your thick rich sauce, reduced almost to a glaze, over them
9. Let crisply fried bread-crumbs surround your toasts
10. Use Nepaul pepper

title: Baked Mullet
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good sized mullet
- a dozen prawns
- butter
- the crumb of a roll
- milk
- one anchovy
- curly parsley
- chopped marjoram
- a cold boiled onion
- pepper
- salt
- two raw eggs
- a breakfast cupful of broth
- maitre d'hotel butter
- hock or light wine like chablis

## Directions

1. Clean the mullet carefully
2. Boil the prawns
3. Shell and clean the boiled prawns, wash them well, and dry them
4. Pound the prawns thoroughly in a mortar with a little butter and the crumb of a roll soaked in milk
5. Pass through the sieve
6. Season with anchovy wiped free from oil and chopped fine, minced curly parsley, chopped marjoram, and shredded cold boiled onion
7. Dust with pepper and a little salt
8. Work together thoroughly with two raw eggs
9. Lay the mullet on a flat dish and wipe it dry
10. Fill it with the prawn stuffing
11. Sew up the fish securely
12. Butter a pie-dish and place the mullet therein
13. Pour a breakfast cupful of broth round it
14. Spread a little butter upon the top
15. Bake for about ten minutes or a quarter of an hour in a quick oven
16. Place a pat of maitre d'hotel butter on top of the mullet before serving
17. Pour a spoonful or two of hock or light wine like chablis round it during baking
18. Baste the fish now and then during cooking

title: Baked Mushrooms
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen mushrooms
- an ounce of butter
- half an ounce of flour
- half a pint of made gravy
- chopped stalks from mushrooms
- a table-spoonful of chopped curled parsley
- a tea-spoonful of minced onion
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- pepper
- a spoonful of cream

## Directions

1. First, be careful in cleaning them; cut the stalks down, leaving only a little of them within the hollow of the mushroom
2. Peel off the skin that covers the convex side of the fungus
3. Brush away any particles of earth or sand that may adhere to the pink gills on the concave side, and by patting the top of each mushroom, shake out all grit
4. When satisfactorily prepared, clean the stalks you cut off, and chop them up
5. Put into a sauce-pan an ounce of butter and stir into it half an ounce of flour
6. When mixed, add half a pint of made gravy
7. Stir well and throw in the chopped stalks, a table-spoonful of chopped curled parsley, a tea-spoonful of minced onion, a salt-spoonful of salt, and a dusting of pepper
8. Simmer the sauce until it is thick and rich
9. Add a spoonful of cream, and then strain it
10. Butter a silver dish liberally, place the mushrooms upon it head downwards
11. Fill their hollow parts with the thick sauce
12. Set the dish in the oven (a brisk one) for ten minutes
13. As soon as the mushrooms flatten themselves, they are done
14. Serve on the same dish wrapping a napkin round it

title: Baked Whitings with Herbed Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- whitings
- sauce blanche
- parsley
- shallot
- marjoram
- garden cress
- green chilli
- fresh mushrooms

## Directions

1. Bake the whitings very gently.
2. Make sauce blanche in which a bunch of parsley minced fine, a very little shallot, a little marjoram, some garden cress, and a little green chilli (if liked) have been mixed.
3. For true fines herbes if fresh mushrooms are available: use equal portions (say a table-spoonful) of chopped mushroom and parsley, with a tea-spoonful of chopped shallot.
4. Serve the whitings with the herbed sauce.

title: Ball Curry of Liver and Udder
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of liver
- one pound of udder
- pepper
- salt
- herbs
- bread-crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Get one pound each of liver and udder
2. Thoroughly wash and parboil them
3. Cut them into pieces, put into a mortar, and pound them to a pulp
4. Mix with pepper, salt, herbs, bread-crumbs, and an egg
5. Make into balls, and curry them in the same manner as any of the foregoing forcemeat ball curries

title: Balls of Kalai
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of pulses of mash-kalai
- water
- whites of 2 or 3 eggs
- plenty of ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Keep 1 seer of pulses of mash-kalai under water for 7 or 8 hours and then make a paste of them
2. Mix with the whites of 2 or 3 eggs if desired
3. Beat the paste thoroughly
4. Fry small balls of it in plenty of ghee
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till served

title: Balls of Kalai
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of pulses of mash-kalai
- whites of 2 or 3 eggs (if desired)
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Keep 1 seer of pulses of mash-kalai under water for 7 or 8 hours and then make a paste of them
2. Mix with the whites of 2 or 3 eggs if desired, and beat the paste thoroughly
3. Fry small balls of it in plenty of ghee
4. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till served

title: Balusai
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- butter 1 powa
- fine flour 1 seer
- a little powdered cardamoms
- very little camphor or essence of rose
- water or milk
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly butter, fine flour, a little powdered cardamoms and very little camphor or essence of rose
2. Make a stiff paste with water or milk
3. Heat thoroughly
4. Make balls of it
5. Press them in the middle with a thumb
6. Fry hard in plenty of ghee
7. Keep immersed in a very concentrated syrup of sugar for a few hours

title: Banana Cakes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 powa of flour
- 1 powa of bananas
- 1 powa of milk
- 1/2 powa of sugar
- cardamom seeds
- ghee

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly flour or powdered rice, ripe bananas, milk, sugar and a pinch of powdered cardamom seeds to a thick paste
2. Heat a little ghee or oil in a round bottomed pan
3. Add 2 or 3 table-spoonfuls of the paste and cover the pan up completely
4. Remove the cakes when they are solid and boiled after about 1 or 1 1/2 minutes
5. Continue in this way

title: Bande-cai Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- bande-cai pods
- butter
- white pepper
- salt
- anchovy sauce
- bread
- cream
- egg yolk
- Parmesan cheese
- Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the bande-cais, and when cold, scrape out the seeds and pulp from each pod into a small basin, using a silver spoon for the operation.
2. Give the pulp a dusting of white pepper, and salt to taste, with a few drops of anchovy sauce.
3. Fry rounds, or slices of bread, according to the number you want, in butter, and set them to keep crisp and hot in the oven.
4. Take a small sauce-pan, place it in the bain-marie, or over a very moderate fire; melt a dessert-spoonful of butter in it.
5. Stir into it the bande-cai pulp, and two good table-spoonfuls of cream with the yolk of one egg.
6. Continue stirring one way until the contents of your sauce-pan look nice and thick, and steaming hot.
7. Pour the mixture over the toasts, and serve.
8. Dust grated Parmesan cheese over the surface of the toasts as an embellishment, and serve with Nepaul pepper handed round.

title: Bandecai Salad
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- young bandecais
- anchovy fillets
- iced salad dressing
- crushed ice

## Directions

1. Gather bandecais young and boil till tender.
2. Arrange the bandecais on a flat dish placed over some crushed ice.
3. With a dessert-knife slit each one open longitudinally.
4. In the slit put a fillet of anchovy.
5. Pour a little iced salad dressing over all.

title: Bandecu au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- bandecais
- white pepper
- grated cheese
- milk
- egg yolk
- cream

## Directions

1. Butter a small pie-dish liberally
2. Fill it with the pulp and seeds of some well boiled, tender bandecais
3. Dust the pulp with white pepper, and some grated cheese
4. Pour a little milk in which the yolk of an egg has been beaten (or cream if you can allow it) in amongst the pulp
5. Dust a layer of cheese over the surface
6. Bake for ten or twelve minutes
7. Alternatively, the bandecai mixture can be baked in coquille shells, which should be served on a napkin

title: Bandoline for the Hair
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of olive oil
- one drachm of spermaceti
- one drachm of oil of bergamot
- six drops of otto of roses
- half a drachm of annatto (if colour be desired)

## Directions

1. Mix two ounces of olive oil with one drachm each of spermaceti and oil of bergamot
2. Heat and strain
3. Beat in six drops of otto of roses
4. If colour be desired, add half a drachm of annatto

title: Bandoline for the Hair
body:
## Ingredients

- olive oil, two ounces
- spermaceti, one drachm
- oil of bergamot, one drachm
- otto of roses, six drops
- annatto, half a drachm

## Directions

1. Mix two ounces of olive oil with one drachm each of spermaceti and oil of bergamot
2. Heat and strain
3. Beat in six drops of otto of roses
4. If colour be desired, add half a drachm of annatto

title: Bara with Brinjals
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender brinjals
- water or rice
- green chillies
- onions
- salt
- rice-flour
- oil

## Directions

1. Boil a few tender brinjals in water or in rice.
2. Remove the stalks and peel.
3. Mix with minced green chillies, onions, salt and rice-flour.
4. Beat thoroughly.
5. Form into cakes and fry in oil.

title: Bara with Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chhana
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Drain off the water from the chhana by pressure.
2. Mix powdered turmeric, salt and a little flour.
3. Form into cakes and fry.

title: Bara with Fish and Egg
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of fish
- boiled potato
- powdered pepper
- chillies
- salt
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of fish and remove the bones.
2. Mix boiled and pasted potato about a third of the quantity of fish, proper quantities of powdered pepper, chillies and salt with the boiled fishes.
3. Form cakes, dip into beaten egg-contents, roll on bread crumbs and fry in ghee.

title: Bara with Fishes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of hilsa, chitol or any other big fish
- water
- powdered turmeric
- besam or rice-flour
- powdered turmeric
- red chillies
- ginger
- salt
- minced onions
- boiling oil or ghee

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of hilsa, chitol or any other big fish in water with a little powdered turmeric
2. Remove the bones
3. Add some besam or rice-flour, a little powdered turmeric, red chillies, ginger, salt and some minced onions, if desired
4. Beat thoroughly
5. Form cakes and drop them in boiling oil or ghee after the bubbles die out and the vapour arises
6. Fry till golden-brown

title: Bara with Ol or Mun
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ol or mun
- lemon juice
- powdered red chillies
- aniseeds
- salt
- powdered rice or flour

## Directions

1. Peel and dry them in the sun for 2 or 3 days.
2. Boil in water with a little lemon juice.
3. Grind them to a paste.
4. Add powdered red chillies, aniseeds, salt and powdered rice or flour.
5. Beat thoroughly.
6. Form into cakes and fry.

title: Bara with Palta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulse of khesari or peas
- palta leaves
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a paste of some moistened pulse of khesari or peas with some palta leaves.
2. Mix salt and beat.
3. Form cakes of it and fry.

title: Bara with Roes of Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- roes of fishes
- besam
- powdered turmeric
- pepper or chillies
- salt
- onion-juice or minced onion

## Directions

1. Beat the roes of fishes with some besam, a little powdered turmeric, pepper or chillies, and salt.
2. Add a little onion-juice or minced onion if desired.
3. Form small balls or flat cakes and fry them in oil.

title: Bara with Roes of Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish roes
- besam
- powdered turmeric
- pepper or chillies
- salt
- onion-juice or minced onion
- oil

## Directions

1. Beat the roes of fishes with some besam, a little powdered turmeric, pepper or chillies, and salt
2. A little onion-juice or minced onion may be added
3. Form small balls or flat cakes and fry them in oil

title: Bara with Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- rice or husked grams
- water
- powdered chillies
- salt
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Scale the shrimps and shred off the flesh portions
2. Grind them to a paste with some rice or husked grams left moistened for several hours
3. Add powdered chillies and salt, mix thoroughly
4. Form cakes, and fry in ghee or oil

title: Bara with Shrimps and Cocoa-Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- flesh portions of a few big-sized shrimps
- kernel of a half-ripe cocoa-nut
- bread crumbs
- minced onions
- powdered turmeric
- red chillies
- salt
- fresh egg

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the flesh portions of a few big-sized shrimps with the kernel of a half-ripe cocoa-nut.
2. Add some bread crumbs, minced onions, a little powdered turmeric, red chillies, salt and the contents of a fresh egg.
3. Beat thoroughly.
4. Form cakes and fry.

title: Bara with Shrimps and Cocoa-nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big-sized shrimps
- kernel of a half-ripe cocoa-nut
- bread crumbs
- minced onions
- powdered turmeric
- red chillies
- salt
- egg

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the flesh portions of a few big-sized shrimps with the kernel of a half-ripe cocoa-nut
2. Add some bread crumbs, minced onions, a little powdered turmeric, red chillies, salt and the contents of a fresh egg
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Form cakes and fry

title: Barfi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- eggs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Mix some sugar with the contents of a few eggs and beat thoroughly
2. Smear the interior surface of an enamelled pot with ghee and pour the liquid into it
3. Place this pot on hot water to coagulate the liquid
4. Then cut it into cubes and immerse in a syrup of sugar

title: Barfi of Almonds
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of shelled almonds
- 1 powa of khowa kheer
- 0.5 tola of powdered cardamom seeds
- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 0.5 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 1 seer of shelled almonds moist for about an hour
2. Skin them and make a paste of them
3. Mix the paste with 1 powa of khowa kheer and 0.5 tola of powdered cardamom seeds
4. Boil 1 chhatak of ghee in a pan
5. Add the paste and stirring a little add a concentrated syrup of 0.5 seer of sugar
6. Continue stirring till thick
7. Smear a flat plate with a little ghee
8. Pour the mass in it
9. Cut in cubes when solid

title: Barfi of Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of sugar
- 1 seer of paste of hard chhana
- 1 tola each of pieces of almonds and pistachios
- a pinch of powdered cardamoms

## Directions

1. Heat a syrup of 1 seer of sugar
2. When warm add 1 seer of a paste of hard chhana
3. Stir constantly and vigorously
4. Add pieces of almonds and pistachios 1 tola each and a pinch of powdered cardamoms when thick
5. Take the pan off fire when the mass is very thick
6. Stir as before
7. Transfer the mass in a wooden plate when a little cooled
8. Cut in cubes when solid

title: Barfi of Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- a few half-ripe cocoa-nut kernels
- 1/2 seer of khowa kheer
- 1 tola of powdered cardamoms
- 1 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Scrape a few half-ripe cocoa-nut kernels and make a paste of them.
2. Mix 1/2 seer of this paste with 1/2 seer of khowa kheer and fry the mixture a little without ghee or oil.
3. Then add 1 tola of powdered cardamoms and a syrup of 1 seer of sugar.
4. Stir constantly till very thick and gluey.
5. Take the pan off fire and transfer the mass in a flat plate.
6. Cut in cubes when cold.

title: Barfi of Grams
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of pulses of grams
- 1 1/2 powa of ghee
- saffron
- powdered cardamoms
- 1/2 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 1/2 seer of pulses of grams moist for 5 or 6 hours and make a paste of them.
2. Fry the paste with 1 1/2 powa of ghee to a reddish brown colour.
3. Then add a pinch of saffron, another of powdered cardamoms and syrup of sugar 1/2 seer.
4. Stir incessantly till quite thick when transfer it in a flat plate smeared with ghee and cut in cubes when congealed.

title: Barfi of Grams
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 seer of pulses of grams
- 1.5 powa of ghee

## Directions

1. Keep 1.5 seer of pulses of grams moist for 5 or 6 hours
2. Make a paste of them
3. Fry the paste with 1.5 powa of ghee

title: Barfi of Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tola of powdered cardamoms
- 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose
- 1 seer of khowa kheer
- 1 seer of sugar
- 1/2 powa of broken pieces of sugar-candy

## Directions

1. Mix 1 tola of powdered cardamoms and 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose with 1 seer of khowa kheer.
2. Boil a syrup of 1 seer of sugar, add 1/2 powa of broken pieces of sugar-candy and the kheer, and stir till very thick.
3. Transfer it in a flat plate and cut in cubes when slightly warm.

title: Barfi of Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet ripe mangoes
- cow's ghee
- sugar syrup
- powdered cardamoms

## Directions

1. Peel some sweet ripe mangoes
2. Strain off the juice through a clean piece of linen
3. Boil the juice in an enamelled pot
4. Stir constantly adding cow's ghee little by little till very thick
5. Take it off fire
6. Mix with an adequate quantity of very concentrated syrup of sugar and powdered cardamoms
7. Pour it in a flat plate
8. Cut in cubes when solid

title: Barfi of Moog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seers of pulses of moog
- 1 seer of ghee
- 1.5 tolas of pieces of almonds
- 2 tolas of raisins
- 1 tola of pieces of pistachios
- 2 tolas of powders of pepper
- 1 tola of powders of coriander
- 0.5 tola of powders of aniseed
- 0.5 tola of saffron
- 2 seers of sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 2 seers of pulses of moog moistened for 2 or 3 hours
2. Make a paste of them
3. Beat 1 seer of ghee till very white
4. Mix it with the pasted moog, pieces of almonds 1.5 tolas, raisins 2 tolas, pieces of pistachios 1 tola, powders of pepper 2 tolas, coriander 1 tola, aniseed 0.5 tola and saffron 0.5 tola
5. Heat the mass in a pan and stir constantly till red
6. Pour in a syrup of 2 seers of sugar
7. Continue stirring till the mass is very thick
8. Pour it in a flat plate
9. Cut it in cubes when cold

title: Barfi of Oranges
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- 1 1/2 seers of milk
- 3 powas of sugar
- 4 drops of essence of rose
- powdered cardamoms
- 1 tola each of pieces of almonds, pistachios and raisins

## Directions

1. Peel some oranges and further skin them separating the grains.
2. Boil 1 1/2 seers of milk till it reduces to only 3 powas and then add 1 1/2 seers of the grains of oranges.
3. Boil on mild heat and stir constantly till the mass is very thick.
4. Remove the pan off fire and mix thoroughly with a very concentrated syrup of 3 powas of sugar.
5. Add 4 drops of essence of rose, a little powdered cardamoms, pieces of almonds, pistachios and raisins 1 tola each.
6. Boil again if necessary till very thick and gluey when remove it in a flat plate and cut in cubes when solid.

title: Barfi of Pistachios
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of pistachios
- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1/2 powa of pasted khowa kheer
- powdered cardamoms
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Skin 1 powa of pistachios after keeping them moistened for about an hour and make a paste of them.
2. Fry the paste with 1 chhatak of ghee till reddish brown and add 1/2 powa of pasted khowa kheer, a little powdered cardamoms and a syrup of 1 1/2 powa of sugar.
3. Stir constantly till thick when transfer it in a flat plate and cut in cubes when cold.

title: Barley-water for the Sick Chamber
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful Robinson's patent barley
- one tablespoonful cold spring water
- one quart boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix smoothly the barley and cold spring water into a smooth paste
2. Gradually add one quart of boiling water
3. Boil it gently for ten minutes, stirring constantly
4. Strain when cold

title: Bavarian Cream with Liqueur
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of an ounce of gelatine
- half a liqueur-glass of brandy or sherry
- a few drops of vanilla essence
- one ounce of cocoa powder
- one pint of boiling milk
- two ounces of sugar
- yolks of eight eggs
- one ounce of dissolved gelatine
- one coffee-cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Melt three-quarters of an ounce of gelatine in a stew-pan over the fire, with half a liqueur-glass of brandy or sherry, and a few drops of vanilla essence
2. Strain it through a tamis cloth, till clear, and set it to get cool
3. Take one ounce of cocoa powder, and stir it into a pint of boiling milk, adding two ounces of sugar
4. When thoroughly mixed, strain the milk through a piece of muslin
5. Let it get quite cold, and then add to it the strained yolks of eight eggs, making therewith a rich custard
6. Set the custard upon ice, having stirred an ounce of dissolved gelatine into it
7. When it begins to set, add a coffee-cupful of whipped cream, mix thoroughly
8. Place a mould on ice and pour into it a layer of the vanilla jelly half an inch thick
9. When set, pour in about an inch of the cream, then a layer of jelly when the cream has set, and so on until the mould is filled
10. Serve after it has rested on ice for an hour

title: Beans
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- salt
- ground turmeric
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut the two ends and peel off the hard fibres from the two edges, if there are any.
2. Boil in saline water.
3. Smear ground turmeric.
4. Fry with oil.

title: Beans à la Bourgeoise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- table-spoonful of tinned butter
- table-spoonful of flour
- water from cooking beans
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil and skin the beans
2. Turn beans into a stew-pan over a slow fire with a table-spoonful of tinned butter
3. Mix with them a table-spoonful of flour
4. Moisten with some of the water in which the beans were boiled
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. When nice and creamy, serve

title: Beckty Fish Gravy Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- beckty fish
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice and wash in salt like the hilsa before being cooked
2. It is not usual to put any acid in the beckty fish curry

title: Beef a la Mode
body:
## Ingredients

- round of beef
- four pounds of lard or fat bacon
- half a tablespoonful of cinnamon powdered
- half a seer or one pound of finely-powdered pepper
- one tablespoonful of cloves powdered
- four tablespoonfuls of chutnee strained through muslin
- claret-glassful of mixed sauces such as Harvey, walnut, Worcestershire, tap, tomato
- plain water
- half a pint of vinegar
- bay-leaves
- peppercorns
- claret or champagne

## Directions

1. Corn a round of beef in every particular as directed in recipe No. 123
2. Twenty-four hours previously to its being cooked lard it as follows with the undermentioned ingredients
3. Mix the ground pepper, ground hot spices, and strained chutnee with a claret-glassful of mixed sauces
4. Cut up into long narrow slips the lard or bacon to correspond in thickness with the larding-pin
5. Lay the slips into the mixture of spices, sauces, etc., for an hour or two before larding the beef
6. Lard the beef through and through, and as closely as possible
7. Cook it the next day, either in plain water with half a pint of vinegar and with bay-leaves and peppercorns, or in a preparation of claret or champagne with vinegar, bay-leaves, etc.

title: Beef Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of well-selected meat
- one-inch square pieces

## Directions

1. Cut it up into one-inch square pieces, rejecting all the scraggy parts
2. Cook it in every respect according to the instructions given in recipe No. 16 for cooking a gravy chicken curry
3. Allow it to simmer for a much longer time than any other curry, or until the beef becomes tender

title: Beef Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef
- one-inch square pieces

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of beef into one-inch square pieces
2. Follow all the instructions given in recipe No. 23
3. Allow it to simmer for a much longer time over a slow fire, until the beef is perfectly tender

title: Beef Forcemeat Ball Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- more than two pounds of good fat beef
- lard or ghee
- salt
- pepper
- garden herbs
- bread-crumbs
- broth or milk
- egg

## Directions

1. Get rather more than two pounds of good fat beef; wash it thoroughly, and cut it into pieces, rejecting all veins and scraggy portions
2. Put about two pounds of it into a mortar and pound it fine, removing all fibres, veins, &c.
3. Mix with the pounded beef a teaspoonful of salt, pepper, and garden herbs, and two tablespoonfuls of bread-crumbs
4. Add a little of the broth, or in its absence some milk
5. Mix the whole well together
6. Beat up the yolk and white of the egg, add it to the mixture, and make into balls about the size of large walnuts
7. Roll them in bread-crumbs
8. After heating the pot, melt the lard or ghee, and fry brown the ground ingredients, sprinkling a tablespoonful of cold water over them
9. Add the coftas or balls with salt to taste, and fry or brown them
10. Pour into the pot either a cup of broth or of water, and allow to simmer for about two hours

title: Beef Grenadins with Mashed Potato
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- undercut of beef
- marinade
- clarified dripping
- mashed potato
- fried bread
- butter
- flour
- beef gravy
- sherry
- caramel
- mushroom ketchup
- Harvey sauce
- Anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Cut and trim a nice undercut of beef in slices as for grenadins.
2. Steep them all day en marinade and drain.
3. Either grill, or fry them very carefully in clarified dripping.
4. Drain again, and serve in a circle round a ring of mashed potato, with a heart-shaped crouton of fried bread between each slice of meat.
5. For the sauce: Melt half an ounce of butter in a small sauce-pan, mix into it half an ounce of flour.
6. Stir in, when the butter and flour have amalgamated, a breakfast-cupful of beef gravy, half a glass of sherry, a tea-spoonful of caramel, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, one of Harvey, and one and a half of Anchovy sauce.
7. Stir well, and pour piping hot into the centre of the potato ring.

title: Beef Olives
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of steak
- Oxford or Bologna sausage meat
- hot butter
- onions
- flour
- sweet herbs
- mushroom trimmings
- pepper
- salt
- good gravy or stock

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of steak two inches wide by six inches long.
2. Put on each at one end a piece of Oxford or Bologna sausage meat the size of a pigeon's egg.
3. Roll up each olive tightly and neatly, and tie it up with a piece of thread.
4. Fry them in hot butter until they begin to take colour.
5. Take them out, remove the string from each olive, and lay them by.
6. Fry some onions a gold colour in butter, add a very little flour, sweet herbs, a few mushroom trimmings, pepper and salt.
7. Moisten with some very good gravy or stock; let the sauce boil, then strain it.
8. Carefully lay the olives in it to simmer till done and ready to be served; the sauce should cover them in the sauce-pan.

title: Beef or Mutton Baked with Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- one or two small onions
- milk
- one egg
- salt
- pepper
- beef or mutton chops
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil some potatoes.
2. Peel and pound them in a mortar with one or two small onions.
3. Moisten them with milk and an egg beaten up.
4. Add a little salt and pepper.
5. Season slices of beef or mutton chops with salt and pepper, and more onion, if the flavour is approved.
6. Rub the bottom of a pudding-dish with butter, and put in a layer of the mashed potatoes, which should be as thick as a batter, and then a layer of meat, and so on alternately, till the dish is filled, ending with potatoes.
7. Bake in an oven for one hour.

title: Beef Steak and Pigeon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- a slice of good steak
- two pounds of beef
- one chittack or two ounces of ghee
- a teaspoonful of salt
- two fresh limes
- four young pigeons
- twelve oysters
- twelve curry onions
- a teaspoonful of ground pepper
- some sweet herbs
- a dessertspoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Cut up the steak into pieces three inches long, and two inches or two and a half wide, by half an inch thick.
2. Cut and divide each pigeon into four pieces.
3. Put up two pounds of beef with sufficient water to make a good strong gravy, throwing in all the scraggy parts and other rejections of the steak and pigeons.
4. Warm the ghee, and fry in it the sliced onions.
5. Throw in, well dredged with the flour, the steaks and pigeons, and after frying a while add the pepper, salt, soup herbs, and some of the rind of the limes, and about half the beef gravy.
6. Set the whole on a slow fire, and simmer until the meat is tender.
7. Allow to cool.
8. Then add the oysters and the remaining gravy, with the juice of two limes.
9. Put into a dish lined with pastry, cover over the whole with a pastry crust, and bake.

title: Beef Steak with Sorrel Puree
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- beef steak
- gravy made from scraps and trimmings
- vegetables
- two good handfuls of carefully picked sorrel
- salt
- pinch of soda
- two ounces of butter
- flour
- teaspoonful of sugar
- yolks of three eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Make some gravy with scraps and trimmings and strain it off and keep it handy
2. Take a frying-pan, melt a piece of butter at the bottom of it, and shred an onion therein
3. Lay the steak upon it, and turn it about until it is well browned on both sides
4. Lift it up and place it in your stew-pan, with the gravy and vegetables, to simmer gently till done, which you can test with a fork
5. Prevent fast cooking; the slow simmering process is the thing needful
6. Strain the vegetables from the gravy, and cut up into neat dice, to garnish the top of the steak
7. Save the gravy for the sorrel
8. Put two good handfuls of carefully picked sorrel in plenty of boiling water, with a little salt, and a pinch of soda, blanch for five minutes, drain, and chop the leaves small on a board
9. Melt two ounces of butter in a sauce-pan to which add a little flour, the sorrel, a tea-spoonful of sugar, and the steak gravy stirring vigorously to prevent the leaves catching at the bottom of the pan
10. When the gravy and butter are thoroughly well absorbed, turn the sorrel out, stir in the yolks of three eggs beaten up in a little milk, and pour the puree round the steak, which should have been kept hot
11. The sorrel should be got ready beforehand

title: Beef Vindaloo
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fat beef
- half a cup of good vinegar
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- ground condiments
- ghee or lard
- peppercorns
- bay-leaves

## Directions

1. Cut up two pounds of fat beef into large squares, and steep them in the vinegar, together with half a teaspoonful of salt and all the ground condiments, from eighteen to twenty-four hours.
2. Warm the ghee or lard and throw in the meat, together with the condiments and vinegar in which it had been steeped, adding a few peppercorns and bay-leaves.
3. Allow to simmer gently over a slow fire for two hours, or until the meat is perfectly tender, and serve up hot.

title: Beef Vindaloo
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fat beef
- half a cup of good vinegar
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- six ounces or three chittacks of ghee or lard
- one tablespoonful of bruised garlic
- one tablespoonful of ground garlic
- one tablespoonful of ground ginger
- two teaspoonfuls of ground chilies
- one teaspoonful of roasted and ground coriander-seed
- half a teaspoonful of roasted and ground cumin-seed
- two or three bay-leaves
- a few peppercorns
- four or five cloves, roasted and ground
- four or five cardamoms, roasted and ground
- six small sticks of cinnamon, roasted and ground

## Directions

1. Cut up two pounds of fat beef into large squares
2. Steep them in the vinegar, together with half a teaspoonful of salt and all the ground condiments, from eighteen to twenty-four hours
