3. Warm the ghee or lard and throw in the meat, together with the condiments and vinegar in which it had been steeped
4. Add a few peppercorns and bay-leaves
5. Allow to simmer gently over a slow fire for two hours, or until the meat is perfectly tender
6. Serve up hot

title: Beef à la Mode
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. (2 kilos) of thick beefsteak cut square
- nearly 1/4 lb. (3 hectos) of raw fat bacon
- pepper
- rather less than a pint of white wine
- a wine-glass of brandy
- a pint of stock
- a pint of water
- two calves' feet already boned and blanched
- a little less than one ounce of salt (30 grammes)
- fully one pound (500 grammes) of carrots
- one onion
- three cloves
- one faggot of herbs
- two pinches of pepper
- ten glazed onions

## Directions

1. Take about 4 lbs. (2 kilos) of thick beefsteak cut square.
2. Take nearly 1/4 lb. (3 hectos) of raw fat bacon, cut off the rind, which should be put aside to blanche, and then cut the bacon in strips for larding, about one-third of an inch thick, and sprinkle them with pepper.
3. Lard the meat, and tie it up as for the pot-au-feu.
4. Place the piece of meat in a stew-pan with rather less than a pint of white wine, a wine-glass of brandy, a pint of stock, a pint of water, two calves' feet already boned and blanched, and the rind of the bacon also blanched.
5. Put it on the fire adding a little less than one ounce of salt (30 grammes).
6. Make it boil, and skim it as for pot-au-feu; next, having skimmed it, add fully one pound (500 grammes) of carrots, one onion, three cloves, one faggot of herbs, and two pinches of pepper.
7. Place the stew-pan on the corner of the stove, cover it, and allow it to simmer very gently for four hours and a half.
8. Try the meat with a skewer to ascertain when it is sufficiently cooked, then put it on a dish with the carrots and the calves' feet, and keep them covered up hot until serving.
9. Strain the gravy through a fine tammy; remove carefully every atom of grease, and reduce it over the fire about a quarter.
10. Untie the beef, place it on the dish for serving, add the calves' feet each having been cut into eight pieces, the carrots cut into pieces the size of corks, and ten glazed onions.
11. Arrange the calves' feet, the carrots, and onions round the beef, pour the sauce over the meat keeping the surplus for the next day.
12. Taste it in order to ascertain if sufficiently seasoned.

title: Beef, Mutton, or Kid Pellow
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef or a small but good leg of mutton or two legs of a kid
- sliced onions
- ginger
- salt
- water
- fried onions
- hard-boiled eggs
- fried bacon

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of beef, and cut up as for a curry, or take a small but good leg of mutton, or two legs of a kid, rejecting the loin
2. Make a good, strong gravy with seasoning of sliced onions, ginger, and salt, with water, which when cooked down will be reduced to about sufficient only to cover the rice
3. Proceed to make the pellow in all respects as directed in the chicken pellow recipe
4. If using beef, it is not further used for the table
5. If using kid or mutton, treat the legs the same as the chicken, and serve up with fried onions, hard-boiled eggs, and fried bacon

title: Beef-Steak and Oyster Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef collops
- boiled bacon
- oysters
- spiced pepper
- finely minced lime peel
- beef gravy
- oyster liquor from tin
- lime rind
- blade of mace
- glass of sherry
- tablespoonful of walnut or mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for beef cutting and roll a nice piece of boiled bacon within each collop
2. Place at the bottom a layer of beef collops, then a layer of oysters drained from the tin liquor, beef again, oysters again, and so on, till the dish is packed
3. Season with spiced pepper and finely minced lime peel
4. Make the gravy thus: To about a pint of good beef gravy, add the liquor strained from the tin of oysters, the rind of a lime, a blade of mace, a glass of sherry and a tablespoonful of walnut or mushroom ketchup
5. Heat up gently, skimming off the scum which the oyster liquor may throw up
6. When thoroughly blended, strain the gravy off, and pour it into your packed pie-dish, reserving about a coffee-cupful to pour through the top of the crust at the end of the baking

title: Beefsteak and Kidney Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- paste
- kidney
- cold cooked bacon
- spiced pepper
- gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin collops
2. cut the kidney into strips
3. place a thin slice of cold cooked bacon and kidney over each collop
4. season with spiced pepper and roll each collop up
5. Line the pudding basin with paste
6. fill it with layers of collops
7. pour in among the collops a little gravy
8. close the paste securely and boil for three hours

title: Beefsteak and Oyster Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- paste
- oysters
- cold cooked bacon
- spiced pepper
- gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin collops
2. place a thin slice of cold cooked bacon over each collop
3. season this with spiced pepper
4. roll an oyster inside each collop
5. Line the pudding basin with paste
6. fill it with layers of collops
7. pour in among the collops a little strong gravy
8. close the paste securely and boil for three hours

title: Beefsteak Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- paste
- cold cooked bacon
- spiced pepper
- strong gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin collops
2. place a thin slice of cold cooked bacon over each collop
3. season this with spiced pepper, and roll each collop up
4. Line the pudding basin with paste
5. fill it with layers of collops
6. pour in among the collops a little strong gravy
7. close the paste securely and boil for three hours

title: Beefsteak Stewed with Vegetables
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice steak
- butter
- half a pint of water or broth
- one Bombay onion
- a good handful of French beans
- a plant of celery
- two carrots
- a table-spoonful of walnut ketchup
- a salt-spoonful of sugar
- a glass of Marsala
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Choose a nice steak, fry it to begin with in butter till it turns a nice brown colour on both sides
2. Put it into your stew-pan, with half a pint of water (or broth), one Bombay onion cut into quarters, a good handful of French beans, a plant of celery and two carrots sliced, a table-spoonful of walnut ketchup, a salt-spoonful of sugar, a glass of Marsala, pepper, and salt
3. Cover the pan, and stew the steak ever so gently till the meat parts easily when tested by the fork
4. Lift it out, strain the gravy, place the steak on a hot silver dish, arrange the vegetables cut up into very small dice tastefully over it
5. Thicken the gravy with a little butter and flour, add a dessert-spoonful of Marsala, and pour it boiling hot over the steak

title: Beetroot Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender leaves and stalks of the beetroot
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- hot fried toast

## Directions

1. Boil the beetroot leaves and stalks
2. Drain them like spinach
3. Chop them up
4. Heat them in a sauce-pan with some butter, salt and pepper
5. Spread them upon hot fried toast
6. Serve with as little delay as possible

title: Beetroot with Poulette Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beetroot
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- cress
- butter
- vinegar
- poulette sauce

## Directions

1. Bake the beetroot
2. After having thus cooked it, cut it into slices
3. season them with pepper, salt, chopped parsley, and cress
4. give them a turn or two in a pan with a pat of butter and a few drops of vinegar
5. Dish up
6. pour a poulette sauce over them

title: Beignets a la Pignatelli
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of water
- one and a half ounces of butter
- salt
- pepper
- four and a half ounces of flour
- one ounce of grated Parmesan
- eggs
- one ounce of lean cooked ham

## Directions

1. Put one pint of water in a stew-pan with one and a half ounces of butter, season with salt and pepper
2. Boil, and add four and a half ounces of flour and one ounce of grated Parmesan
3. Stir over the fire for three minutes
4. Add sufficient eggs to turn the mixture to a smooth paste
5. Add to the paste one ounce of lean cooked ham finely chopped
6. When mixed, form the paste into fritters
7. Fry in plenty of hot fat

title: Beignets d'Avenches
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- new loaf
- milk or cream
- favourite essence
- boiling fat or butter
- apricot or any nice preserve

## Directions

1. Cut the loaf into slices three quarters of an inch thick
2. Trim off all crust and cut into convenient slices
3. Marinade the slices in milk, or in cream if available, flavoured with your favourite essence
4. Take up the slices and drain them
5. Fry them in a deep bath of boiling fat, or butter, till a golden yellow
6. Spread apricot or any nice preserve over them
7. Serve hot

title: Beignets d'Avenches
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- new loaf
- milk or cream
- favourite essence
- boiling fat or butter
- apricot preserve

## Directions

1. Take a new loaf and cut it into slices three quarters of an inch thick
2. Trim off all crust, cut into convenient slices
3. Marinade in milk, or in cream if available, flavoured with your favourite essence
4. Take up the slices, drain them
5. Fry them in a deep bath of boiling fat, or butter, till a golden yellow
6. Spread apricot or any nice preserve over them, and serve hot

title: Beignets Soufflés
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint water
- pinch of salt
- butter the size of an egg
- one tablespoonful sugar
- rasped peel of three lemons
- sufficient flour to form a thick paste
- two eggs complete
- yolks only of two more eggs
- whites of two eggs
- boiling fat
- powdered white sugar

## Directions

1. Put about a pint of water in a sauce-pan with a pinch of salt, a piece of butter the size of an egg, a tablespoonful of sugar, and the rasped peel of three lemons
2. When the whole boils, throw in gradually sufficient flour to form a thick paste
3. Let it remain ten minutes
4. Work into it, off the fire, two eggs complete, and the yolks only of two more, reserving the whites for whisking to a froth
5. Add the froth and let it rest awhile
6. Proceed to fry by dropping pieces of it the size of a walnut into boiling fat
7. The paste will swell in the process of frying, and if the fat be sufficiently heated hollow balls of a fine golden colour will be produced
8. Serve them piled upon a dish, with a plentiful dusting of powdered white sugar

title: Beshmi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of ghee
- 1 seer of besam of grams
- 1 seer of ghee
- 1 powa of sugar
- 1 tola of powdered pepper
- powdered garam-masalla (if desired)

## Directions

1. Mix 1 powa of ghee thoroughly with 1 seer of besam of grams and heat
2. Stir constantly
3. Add 1 seer of ghee more in 2 or 3 instalments
4. Add 1 powa of sugar when the besam is fried red
5. Take down when it is thoroughly mixed up
6. Mix with 1 tola of powdered pepper
7. Powdered garam-masalla may also be added if desired
8. Make balls while the mass is yet warm

title: Better Preparation of Sweets of Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel 1 powa
- khowa kheer or pasted hard chhana 1 powa
- pasted almonds 1 chhatak
- sugar syrup 5 powas
- pieces of pistachios
- powdered cardamom seeds

## Directions

1. Heat together scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel, khowa kheer or pasted hard chhana, pasted almonds and a syrup of sugar in a brass pot
2. Stir continuously and vigorously till the mass sticks to the sides of the pot and ladle
3. Take care that it does not burn
4. Take it off fire
5. Mix with pieces of pistachios and powdered cardamom seeds
6. Make balls of it when slightly warm
7. Press in flat semi-circular or other shapes

title: Bharta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish of medium size
- clean linen piece
- fine mud
- ginger
- cumin
- pepper
- chillies
- cardamom
- flour
- salt
- ginger-juice
- ghee

## Directions

1. Flake a fish of medium size, remove its intestines and wash.
2. Wrap it up with a clean linen piece, coat with fine mud and burn on fire to a brown colour.
3. Remove the earth and the linen piece carefully.
4. Remove the bones, mix with ginger, cumin, pepper, chillies, cardamom, pasted or powdered, a little flour, salt and ginger-juice.
5. Fry with ghee.

title: Bhoonee Kitcheeree
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve large curry onions
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- dal
- rice
- a few slices of green ginger
- peppercorns
- salt to taste (say one dessertspoonful)
- a few cloves
- three or four cardamoms
- half a dozen bay-leaves
- small sticks of cinnamon
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the twelve large curry onions lengthways into fine slices
2. Warm the pot carefully
3. Warm up two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
4. While bubbling, throw in the sliced onions, removing them immediately they become of a bright brown colour
5. Set the fried onions aside
6. Throw in the dal and rice (having previously allowed all the water in which they were washed to drain through a colander)
7. Fry until the dal and rice have absorbed all the ghee
8. Add a few slices of green ginger, some peppercorns, salt to taste (say one dessertspoonful), a few cloves, three or four cardamoms, half a dozen bay-leaves, and as many small sticks of cinnamon
9. Mix well together
10. Add as much water only as will entirely cover over the whole of the rice and dal
11. Put a good-fitting cover on, and set over a slow fire, reducing the same from time to time as the water is being absorbed
12. Occasionally shake the pot, or stir its contents with a wooden spoon to prevent the kitcheeree from burning
13. Serve up quite hot, strewing over it the fried onions

title: Bhoonee Kitcheeree
body:
## Ingredients

- rather more than three-quarters of a coonkee of bassmuttee or cheeneesuckur
- half a coonkee of dal
- twelve large curry onions
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- a few slices of green ginger
- some peppercorns
- salt to taste (say one dessertspoonful)
- a few cloves
- three or four cardamoms

## Directions

1. Take rather more than three-quarters of a coonkee of bassmuttee or cheeneesuckur and half a coonkee of dal
2. Take twelve large curry onions and cut them up lengthways into fine slices
3. Warm up two chittacks or four ounces of ghee (but before doing so be careful to warm the pot)
4. While bubbling, throw in the sliced onions, removing them immediately they become of a bright brown colour
5. Set the fried onions aside, and throw in the dal and rice (having previously allowed all the water in which they were washed to drain through a colander)
6. Fry until the dal and rice have absorbed all the ghee
7. Add a few slices of green ginger, some peppercorns, salt to taste (say one dessertspoonful), a few cloves, three or four cardamoms

title: Bhuni-Khechuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine atap rice 1 seer
- Fried moog-dal 1 seer
- Potato 1 seer
- Ghee 1/2 seer
- Onion 1 powa
- Turmeric 1 tola
- Salt 3 1/2 tolas
- Pepper 1 tola
- Cumin 1/2 tola
- Coriander 2 tolas
- Aniseed 1/2 tola
- Cloves 1/2 tola
- Cardamom 1/8 tola
- Cinnamon 1/2 tola
- A few red chillies and cassia leaves
- Water 4 seers

## Directions

1. Pulverise the coriander, aniseed, half the pepper and half the cloves, bind them in a piece of clean cloth, and boil with some cassia leaves in 4 seers of water till it is reduced to 2 1/2 seers.
2. Throw away the boiled spices and keep the akhni water covered.
3. Wash and dry the rice and pulse, and brown them in half the ghee for 5 or 6 minutes.
4. Add akhni water and put the lid on.
5. Do not stir much.
6. Apply slow heat.
7. Brown the pieces of potatoes and onions in another pan and drop them with turmeric, pepper and cumin, all powdered, and salt in the boiling rice when they are half boiled.
8. Take down when the mass is fully boiled.
9. Boil the rest of the ghee with the cassia leaves and red chillies, and pour the mass in.
10. Finish by adding the powdered cloves, cardamoms and cinnamon.
11. Try to keep the grains of rice and pulse entire.
12. If meat is to be added, boil the meat while preparing akhni water, brown the boiled meat and drop them in the khechuri with the browned potatoes.

title: Bibinca Dosee, or Portuguese Cocoanut Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- two cocoanuts
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- half a pound of rice-flour, finely sifted
- butter

## Directions

1. Extract a cupful of milk from two cocoanuts, and set it aside
2. Make a syrup of three-quarters of a pound of sugar
3. Mix into the syrup half a pound of rice-flour finely sifted, and the cocoanut milk
4. Boil over a good fire, stirring the whole while until it thickens
5. Pour it into a buttered pudding-dish, and bake it of a rich light-brown colour

title: Blanc
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of fat bacon
- a quarter of pound of beef suet
- a bacon bone or some broken-up chicken bones
- a couple of carrots
- two large sweet onions
- a bunch of curly parsley
- a tea-spoonful of dried thyme or marjoram
- a dozen pepper corns
- a tea-spoonful of sugar
- one of salt
- warm water

## Directions

1. Cut up as small as possible a quarter of a pound of fat bacon, and a quarter of pound of beef suet, and put the pieces with a bacon bone, or some broken-up chicken bones, into a stew-pan
2. Add a couple of carrots, and two large sweet onions cut up small, a bunch of curly parsley, a tea-spoonful of dried thyme or marjoram, a dozen pepper corns, a tea-spoonful of sugar and one of salt, and stir well over a brisk fire till the fat melts well, and the vegetables begin to fry in it
3. Do not let them brown, but pour in before that stage arrives enough warm water to cover the contents of the pan
4. Draw the pan to the edge of the fire
5. Cook the contents of it at first gently, gradually adding heat, and finally encouraging evaporation by actual boiling, and stirring the contents of your pan to prevent them catching
6. When the liquor has diminished to about a quarter of its original quantity, strain it off into a bowl
7. When required, this vegetable essence should be carefully skimmed poured into a sauce-pan, and sufficient water should be added to cook the celery or other vegetable that may be put into it

title: Blanc Mange
body:
## Ingredients

- three-fourths of an ounce of isinglass
- water
- a quart of good rich milk
- a stick of cinnamon
- lemon-peel
- a few bitter almonds well pounded
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Boil isinglass till dissolved in as much water as will cover it
2. When lukewarm, add to it gradually a quart of good rich milk, with a stick of cinnamon, some lemon-peel, and a few bitter almonds well pounded
3. Sweeten to taste
4. Boil for five or six minutes, stirring all the while
5. Strain through muslin into moulds
6. Place in a pan of cold water to congeal

title: Blanc Mange Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of sweet almonds
- six bitter almonds
- rose-water
- three-fourths of an ounce of isinglass
- water
- a quart of good rich milk
- the peel of a small lemon
- a stick of cinnamon
- good clean sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound with a little rose-water two ounces of sweet and six bitter almonds
2. Dissolve three-fourths of an ounce of isinglass in a little water
3. When nearly cool, mix it into a quart of good rich milk
4. Mix in the almonds the peel of a small lemon and a stick of cinnamon
5. Sweeten to taste with good clean sugar
6. Let it stand for two or three hours
7. Put it into a pan and let it boil for six or eight minutes, stirring constantly
8. Strain through muslin and keep stirring until nearly cold
9. Pour it into moulds

title: Blancmange
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten bitter almonds
- two ounces of shelled sweet almonds
- rose water
- breakfast cupful of milk
- pint of fresh milk
- five ounces of sugar
- vanilla essence
- one ounce of dissolved gelatine
- coffee-cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Blanch ten bitter almonds and two ounces of shelled sweet almonds
2. Pound them to a paste with a little rose water
3. Dilute with a breakfast cupful of milk
4. Let the liquid stand for an hour and then strain it
5. Put into a pint of fresh milk five ounces of sugar and vanilla essence to taste
6. Pour it into an enamelled sauce-pan and boil slowly till the sugar is dissolved
7. Stir in an ounce of dissolved gelatine
8. Pour the liquid through a strainer into a basin
9. Add the almond liquor and a coffee-cupful of cream
10. Pour the mixture into a mould
11. Set it in ice and turn it out when firmly set

title: Blanquette of Fowl
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a well fed fowl
- buttered paper
- clear stock
- a glass of sherry
- vegetables, especially celery
- a bag of savoury herbs
- lime peel
- a pinch of sugar
- six pounded sweet almonds
- one bitter almond
- pellets of tongue
- sweetbreads
- button mushrooms
- oysters
- a few slices of truffle
- the liver of the fowl
- grated lean bacon or ham
- a table-spoonful of cream or the yolks of two raw eggs

## Directions

1. Slightly roast a well fed fowl, covering the breast with buttered paper to prevent its burning
2. When done, put it on a dish, and let it get cold
3. Carve it neatly, cutting the white meat off in nice little round fillets, and saving the wings, merry-thought, &c.
4. Take the bones, skin, giblets, trimmings, and coarser meat of the drumsticks, and beat them well with your pestle
5. Throw them into a sauce-pan, with as much clear stock as will cover them, a glass of sherry, and the usual proportion of vegetables, especially celery, a bag of savoury herbs, lime peel, and a pinch of sugar
6. Make an excellent fowl consomme, adding six pounded sweet and one bitter almond
7. When quite ready, strain it off, and thicken it as for veloute
8. Put into the white sauce all your pieces of fowl, with some pellets of tongue cut like gun wads rather thickly, some sweetbreads, some button mushrooms, a few oysters trimmed free from their beards, a few slices of truffle, the liver of the fowl minced, and some grated lean bacon or ham
9. Gently heat up the blanquette, and let it simmer for half an hour over a low fire
10. Add a table-spoonful of cream, or the yolks of two raw eggs beaten in a cup with a little of the sauce, and stir well in as a finishing touch

title: Boiled Chops
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of mutton, trimmed into chops
- onion
- pepper corns
- scraps of beef
- chicken bones
- cold game
- lean ham or bacon
- two Bombay onions
- carrot
- young turnip
- celery
- two tomatoes
- curled parsley
- liqueur glass of brandy
- table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- salt-spoonful of salt
- salt-spoonful of sugar
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Choose a good neck of mutton, and trim the little chops as neatly as possible.
2. With the trimmings of meat and bone make a broth, assisted by an onion, some pepper corns, any scraps of beef, chicken bones, cold game, lean ham or bacon, in short any useful sundries.
3. When done, skim, and strain it, you ought to have a breakfast-cupful of it.
4. Scald the tin and cool it in cold water.
5. Cover the bottom of the tin with slices of onion, and arrange the chops thereon.
6. Cover the chops with two Bombay onions sliced fine, a carrot sliced, a young turnip sliced, a stick of celery cut into half inch lengths, two tomatoes sliced and drained, and a bunch of curled parsley.
7. Pepper the chops pretty freely with black pepper before covering them.
8. Add to the broth a liqueur glass of brandy, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, a salt-spoonful of salt, and the same of sugar.
9. Pour in the broth, and seal the lid of the tin, all round the rim, with stiff paste, fixing it securely.
10. Put the tin into boiling water, and keep it on the fire for two hours.
11. At the time of serving, the lid should be cut off, and the tin, wrapped in a napkin, should be placed upon an ordinary dish, and sent to table immediately.

title: Boiled Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- rice
- powdered pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Drop a few eggs, after washing them, in boiling rice
2. Pick out when the rice is ready, shell and serve them with powdered pepper and salt

title: Boiled Fish
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. After having thoroughly cleansed and wiped the fish, rub it over with a little vinegar, and place it on the drainer of the fish-kettle, so that when done it may be lifted out without risk of breaking up.
2. Put plenty of salt, and a dessert-spoonful of vinegar, into the water in which the fish is to be boiled.
3. Let the water be cold, and in sufficient quantity to cover the fish.
4. Place the kettle on a brisk fire, and boil the fish as fast as possible.
5. Skim off all scum that rises, and take care to suspend operations, the moment the fish is done.
6. Allow ten minutes per pound as a fair average of the time required for this operation; but the cook should test it now and then with the point of a skewer, and as soon as the flesh parts easily from the bone, let him decide that it is ready.
7. Never let your fish, after it is done, remain soaking in the water in which it has been cooked; drain it at once.
8. If ready too soon, let it rest on the drainer over the hot kettle, and cover it with a napkin.
9. Be very particular to drain every drop of water from the fish before you serve it.

title: Boiled Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of fish
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- water
- powdered mustard
- mustard oil
- onions

## Directions

1. Rub powdered turmeric and salt on pieces of fish and boil in the minimum quantity of water
2. Remove the bones and mix to a paste with powdered mustard, mustard oil and a little more salt, if necessary
3. Serve with sliced onions

title: Boiled Ham
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- cold water
- Madeira or Marsala (optional, for festivals)
- three carrots
- one head of celery
- three Bombay onions
- one clove of garlic
- a dozen whole peppers
- pieces of lime peel
- bunches of parsley
- thyme
- marjoram
- fine crust raspings or pounded baked crumbs

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for twenty-four hours, changing the water at least three times
2. Scrub the ham well and trim it, scraping off all discolorations
3. Place it in your ham-kettle and cover it with cold water (for a festival a bottle of Madeira or Marsala should be poured in with the water)
4. Let it come gently to the boil, removing all scum that may rise
5. When quite clear, throw in three carrots, a head of celery cut up (leaves and all), three Bombay onions, a bag containing a clove of garlic, a dozen whole peppers, and some pieces of lime peel, with bunches of parsley, thyme, and marjoram
6. Boil on till the vegetables are done, and then let the kettle simmer gently for four or five hours
7. When done, let it remain till nearly cold
8. Lift the ham from the water and detach the outer skin (it will roll off easily)
9. Dredge some fine crust raspings or some pounded baked crumbs over it

title: Boiled Peas
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- sugar
- green mint
- shelled peas
- butter
- finely-pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Put one quart of water with a teaspoonful of salt, one of sugar, and half an ounce of green mint on the fire
2. When it boils, pour in a pint measure of shelled peas
3. Boil quickly
4. When done, drain and turn them out upon a frying-pan with an ounce of butter
5. Sprinkle a little salt and finely-pounded sugar over them
6. Work the pan till the butter melts and is blended with peas
7. Empty them upon a hot dish and serve

title: Boiled Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound or a coonkeeful of rice
- water

## Directions

1. Wash half a pound or a coonkeeful of rice
2. put it to boil in a large quantity of water, over a brisk fire
3. Keep the cover of the pot partially open, and stir the rice to prevent an entire overflow of the water
4. On the subsiding of the water or the bubbling, reduce the fire, until the grains of rice, without being pappy, are quite soft
5. Remove the pot from the fire and add a quart of cold water
6. Drain all the liquid, which is conjee
7. Replace the pot over a gentle charcoal heat, to allow all moisture to evaporate, assisting the process by occasionally shaking the pot, or stirring its contents gently with a wooden spoon
8. Time to boil: half an hour

title: Boiled Rice
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- boiling water
- pinch of sugar
- salt-spoonful of salt
- juice of a quarter of a lime

## Directions

1. Having thoroughly sifted, and cleansed the rice, cast it into boiling water with a pinch of sugar, a salt-spoonful of salt, and the juice of a quarter of a lime.
2. Stir the grains as they are cooking with a wooden spoon every now and then, and in about twelve minutes test them by taking a few grains out of the water, and pinching them between the finger and thumb.
3. As soon as the grain is tender, check the boiling by a dash of cold water, remove the vessel from the fire, and invert it, holding the rice securely with the lid yet leaving space enough for the escape of the water.
4. When quite dry, re-invert the pan, shaking the well drained grains of rice in the hot vessel in which they were cooked.
5. Cover the pan with a clean cloth, and let it rest on the hot-plate, or over a very low fire to complete the drying.

title: Bole Comadree, or Portuguese Cocoanut Pudding with Jagree
body:
## Ingredients

- two cocoanuts
- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of finely-sifted rice-flour
- egg yolk
- half a pound of jagree
- some grains of aniseed
- butter

## Directions

1. Extract a cupful of milk from two cocoanuts, and set it aside
2. Make a syrup of half a pound of sugar
3. Mix into it half a pound of finely-sifted rice-flour, and set aside
4. Fry with the yolk of an egg all the scrapings of the two cocoanuts, half a pound of jagree, and some grains of aniseed
5. Mix the whole thoroughly together
6. After the oven is well heated and ready to receive the pudding, pour the mixture into a well-buttered pudding-dish, and bake over a slow fire until it is perfectly set

title: Bombay Onions Stuffed with Kidneys
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bombay onions
- sheep's kidneys
- anchovies
- parsley
- lime
- butter
- spiced pepper
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Take six large Bombay onions and have ready ten sheep's kidneys, scalded and skinned, but uncooked.
2. Boil the onions till three parts done and take them out and drain them.
3. Slice off the top of each one (as you would treat an egg) and carefully remove the inside, leaving a hollow big enough to hold two small kidneys cut into eight pieces.
4. Take two anchovies, pick out their spines and cut the fish into little squares.
5. Have some minced parsley handy, a lime, and some of the inside of the onion that you scooped out, minced and peppered.
6. Put a lump of butter into the onion case first, then a little of the minced onion peppered.
7. Add your pieces of kidney, with little bits of anchovy here and there.
8. Crown the top again with minced onion and a pat of butter.
9. A drop or so of lime juice should be given during the packing, and an occasional dust of spiced pepper.
10. Place the cap on again, and when the six onions are stuffed, lay them in a buttered baking dish.
11. Bake in a slow oven for an hour.
12. When done, pour a brown sauce (separately made) over them and serve in their own dish.

title: Bombay Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds or one seer of soojee
- butter
- the yolk of an egg
- finely-sifted flour
- ghee
- lemon-juice
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Half roast the soojee, then boil it in water until it becomes very thick
2. Butter a soup-plate or any other dish of about the same depth
3. Pour the boiled soojee into it
4. When it has cooled and congealed, cut it into eight or more cakes
5. Rub the cakes over with the yolk of an egg and dredge with finely-sifted flour
6. Fry in ghee until they acquire a rich brown colour
7. Arrange them in a dish and pour over them a thick syrup flavoured with lemon-juice

title: Bombay Pudding Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- a good sweet custard
- a coconut
- butter
- grated nutmeg
- a wineglassful of brandy
- good puff paste

## Directions

1. Make a good sweet custard and set it aside
2. Rasp fine a coconut and fry it in a little butter with grated nutmeg
3. Pour into it gradually a wineglassful of brandy, stirring all the time
4. Have a pudding-dish lined with a good puff paste
5. Pour the fried coconut gradually into the custard, stirring well all the while
6. Fill the pudding-dish with the mixture
7. Bake it in a gentle oven for fifteen to twenty minutes, or until the pudding is cooked

title: Bonde
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer middlings
- 1/2 seer sabeda
- water
- saffron
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a thin paste of 1/2 seer of middlings and 1/2 seer of sabeda with water
2. It may be coloured with a pinch of saffron
3. Boil ghee in a pan and hold on it a ladle with big perforations
4. Pour the solution in the ladle and shake it up and down that big drops may fall in the ghee
5. Take them out when fried hard and red
6. Keep in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Bonde with Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet ripe mangoes
- middlings 1.5 powa
- sabeda 0.25 powa
- besam 0.5 powa
- powdered cardamom seeds
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Peel some sweet ripe mangoes of the best qualities and strain the juice in an enamelled pot up to 1.5 seers
2. Mix with it middlings 1.5 powa, sabeda 0.25 powa, besam 0.5 powa and a little powdered cardamom seeds
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Fry big Bonde with it and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Boudins de Saumon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- a good rich custard with savoury seasoning of salt and pepper
- a tin of salmon
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Butter China ramaquin cases or small dariole moulds, one for each guest
2. Make a good rich custard with salt and pepper seasoning instead of sugar
3. Empty a tin of salmon and choose a nice piece for each mould
4. Place the pieces in a colander and remove all oily liquid with cold water
5. Dry with a clean cloth
6. Put them into the moulds, pouring the custard round them
7. Steam the moulds until set
8. Turn out the boudins carefully
9. Send round hot with Hollandaise sauce

title: Bouillabaisse
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- any sort of small fish, such as small pomfret, whitings, soles, mullets, or robal
- two pounds of mixed fish
- two Bombay onions, sliced
- two fairly large tomatoes, emptied of their seeds and cut into quarters
- one carrot
- two slices of lime with the pithy skin cut off
- six cloves
- a dozen pepper corns
- a clove of garlic
- the peel of a lime pared as finely as possible
- a salt-spoonful of saffron
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- one pod of capsicum, sliced
- a wine-glass of salad oil
- a wine-glass of chablis
- a pint and a half of cold water
- a heaped up table-spoonful of freshly-chopped curled parsley

## Directions

1. Put the whole into a stew-pan
2. Add to it a wine-glass of salad oil, one of chablis, and a pint and a half of cold water
3. Boil for about half an hour
4. Just before serving, add a heaped up table-spoonful of freshly-chopped curled parsley
5. Remove the bag of condiments before serving
6. Serve the fish in a deep dish with some of the broth round it
7. Send up the rest of the broth first, poured boiling hot over some slices of stale bread

title: Bouillabaisse
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- any sort of small fish, such as small pomfret, whitings, soles, mullets, or robal
- two pounds of mixed fish
- two Bombay onions
- two fairly large tomatoes
- one carrot
- a couple of slices of lime with the pithy skin cut off
- six cloves
- a dozen pepper corns
- a clove of garlic
- the peel of a lime pared as finely as possible

## Directions

1. Take any sort of small fish, such as small pomfret, whitings, soles, mullets, or robal, — the greater the variety the better, — and for two pounds of mixed fish, slice up two Bombay onions, two fairly large tomatoes emptied of their seeds and cut into quarters, and one carrot
2. prepare a couple of slices of lime with the pithy skin cut off, a bag containing six cloves, a dozen pepper corns, a clove of garlic, and the peel of a lime pared as finely as possible

title: Bouilli à la Milanaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rolled rib meat
- egg
- dry cheese
- bread-crumbs
- maccaroni

## Directions

1. After draining the rolled rib meat and letting it get cold, place it on a dish that will stand the oven.
2. Brush it over with egg.
3. Dredge over it a coating of very finely grated dry cheese, and bread-crumbs, in equal proportions.
4. Set it in the oven to heat thoroughly, and brown the outside of the crumbs with a hot iron used salamander fashion.
5. When ready, put the meat on a dish, and trim round it a circle of hot maccaroni freshly boiled.

title: Bouillon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of the ribs of beef, weighing about five pounds
- two carrots
- a turnip
- a handful of French beans
- a large Bombay onion
- half a head of celery
- two leeks
- a bunch of parsley
- salt
- cold water
- caramel
- sugar
- pepper corns
- mixed sweet herbs
- Madeira
- tarragon

## Directions

1. Purchase a nice piece of the ribs of beef, weighing about five pounds, with two carrots, a turnip, a handful of French beans, a large Bombay onion, half a head of celery (leaves and all), two leeks, and a bunch of parsley.
2. Detach the meat from the bones en masse, roll it up compactly, and tie it firmly with a string.
3. See that your soup kettle is thoroughly clean, put in the bones (chopped up) first, and place your bundle of meat above them.
4. Pour in cold water enough to cover the top of the meat with one and a half ounce of salt and nothing else.
5. Set the pot near the fire, and let the contents come as slowly as possible to the boil, skimming off every atom of scum as it rises, and adding from time to time a little cold water to retard ebullition.
6. Keep the vessel open throughout the operations.
7. Once boiling with a clean surface, cast in your vegetables (the carrots first, followed by the turnip) all nicely washed, trimmed, and cut up.
8. Give the soup a tea-spoonful of caramel (burnt sugar colouring), a dessert-spoonful of sugar, a dozen pepper corns, and a bag containing mixed sweet herbs (a tea-spoonful of each).
9. Watch the vessel narrowly, and as soon as boiling begins again, ease off the fire, and let the contents of the kettle simmer till the vegetables are nicely done.
10. Remove the vegetables and put the meat broth and bone back on a low fire to simmer slowly until the meat is nice and tender.
11. Strain the soup from the meat, and set it to cool.
12. When the broth is cold, take off any fat that may have risen to its surface, and return the liquor to the sauce-pan.
13. Cut up some of the vegetables into nice pieces and place them in the soup tureen.
14. Let the contents of your sauce-pan come almost to the boil, and pour them over the vegetables.
15. Add a dessert-spoonful of Madeira and a dried leaf or two of tarragon to improve it.

title: Boulettes de Pommes de Terre
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight fairly-sized potatoes
- yolks of five eggs
- finely-minced parsley
- marjoram
- cream
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- whites of three eggs
- boiling fat

## Directions

1. Mash eight fairly-sized potatoes
2. Pass the puree through the sieve
3. Work into it the yolks of five eggs
4. Season it with a little finely-minced parsley and marjoram
5. Moisten it with enough cream to bring it to the consistency of thin paste
6. Add salt, pepper, and a dust of nutmeg
7. Add the whites of three of the eggs whipped to a stiff froth
8. Prepare a bath of boiling fat
9. Fry the boulettes by passing tea-spoonfuls of the paste one after the other into the fat
10. When each little spoonful reaches the fat, it will swell up
11. As soon as it turns a rich golden yellow, it is done

title: Brain Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- a teaspoonful of finely-sifted flour
- grated nutmeg
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Previously boil down the brains
2. Bruise them
3. Add a teaspoonful of finely-sifted flour
4. Add some grated nutmeg, pepper, and salt
5. Roll out like piecrust to the thickness of a rupee
6. Cut into cakes of the size of Spanish dollars
7. Fry them

title: Brain Cakes Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- raw egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Take the brains and remove any veins
2. Chop well with a knife
3. Add salt, nutmeg, and pepper
4. Add a little raw egg and flour enough to make them stick together
5. Mix well
6. Make into cakes about the size of the top of liqueur glasses
7. Fry them brown on both sides

title: Braised Leg of Mutton with Vegetables
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- turkey stuffing
- broth made from the bone
- liqueur-glass of brandy
- sliced onions
- carrot
- bunch of mixed sweet herbs
- dried herbs
- whole peppers
- bunch of parsley
- clove of garlic
- glass of port
- red currant jelly
- anchovy vinegar
- turkey forcemeat
- haricots verts
- mashed potato

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from a leg of mutton and fill its place with turkey stuffing
2. Tie the meat back in shape and set it in a stewing-pan
3. Add as much broth made from the bone as will half cover it
4. Throw in a liqueur-glass of brandy, a couple of sliced onions, a carrot cut up, a bunch of mixed sweet herbs or two dessert-spoonfuls of dried herbs, some whole peppers, a bunch of parsley, a clove of garlic, and one glass of port
5. Set the pan on the fire with some live coals on the lid as well
6. Let this stew till done very slowly, keeping it from absolutely boiling point
7. When ready, strain off the sauce in which it has been cooked
8. Thicken the sauce by stirring in a table-spoonful of red currant jelly, half one of anchovy vinegar, and a glass of port
9. Serve the mutton garnished with balls of turkey forcemeat fried in butter
10. Pour the rich brown gravy round it and let haricots verts and mashed potato accompany this dish

title: Braised Loin with Forcemeat
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a loin
- slices of bacon
- the meat of the undercut
- kidney
- the nicest fat
- herbs
- spiced pepper
- chopped bacon
- a little onion
- mushroom or truffle parings
- four eggs
- gravy made from the loin's own bones and trimmings
- a glass of Madeira
- a clove of garlic
- scraps of vegetables of all kinds
- boiled maccaroni

## Directions

1. Bone the loin and introduce a number of slices of bacon under the flap
2. Fill the inside with a forcemeat made with the meat of the undercut chopped up small, with the kidney (scalded first), and some of the nicest fat, seasoned with herbs, spiced pepper, chopped bacon, a little onion, and any mushroom or truffle parings
3. Bind the salpicon with four eggs, and spread it thickly over the inside of the boned loin
4. Roll the joint up, and tie it firmly into shape, do not skewer it
5. Braise the loin in a gravy made from its own bones and trimmings, with a glass of Madeira, a clove of garlic, and scraps of vegetables of all kinds arranged round it
6. When done and nicely browned, lift up the loin and remove the string
7. Dish it on a layer of boiled maccaroni
8. Strain off the gravy, throw away the garlic, and pass all the vegetables through the sieve
9. Thicken the gravy, add the vegetable pulp, and when thoroughly incorporated, pour it round the loin and serve

title: Brawn
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ox head
- water
- vegetables
- flavouring
- salt
- spiced pepper
- powdered dried thyme
- marjoram
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut out the eyes, break the bones of the jaws, and remove the brains
2. Let the whole soak for an hour or two in cold water
3. Put it into a stew-pan with water enough to cover it
4. Boil very slowly, then add vegetables and flavouring as if for soup
5. When the meat is quite tender and you can pick the bones away from it easily, strain the meat from the broth and vegetables
6. Free the meat from every fragment of bone
7. Cut up the meat whilst it is hot and juicy, en masse rather small
8. Season with salt and spiced pepper whilst doing so (or if you have no spiced pepper, mix a table-spoonful of powdered dried thyme and marjoram blended with a tea-spoonful of pepper, and dust it freely into the meat)
9. Press the meat tightly down in a round brawn tin
10. Let it get cold
11. Turn out whole and slice for breakfast or lunch

title: Brawn with Ox-Head, Tongue, and Bacon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-head
- well cured tongue
- thick slice of bacon

## Directions

1. Stew the ox-head as in the foregoing recipe
2. Boil the tongue
3. Cut up both the tongue and bacon whilst they are quite hot
4. Season as before explained
5. Stir the chopped meats well together
6. Press the whole tightly down in a brawn tin
7. Let it remain three or four hours with a heavy weight above it
8. When required, dip the tin into hot water to loosen the sides of the brawn
9. It will slip out fully formed and ready for the table

title: Bread Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of water
- crumb of a French roll or slice of bread
- 1 minced onion
- whole pepper
- 1 gill of cream
- bit of butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil in a pint of water the crumb of a French roll or of a slice of bread, a minced onion, and some whole pepper
2. When the onion is tender drain off the water, pick out the peppercorns, and rub the bread through a sieve
3. Put it into a saucepan, with a gill of cream, a bit of butter, and a little salt
4. Stir it till it boils, and serve in a sauce-tureen

title: Bread Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of water
- the crumb of a French roll or of a slice of bread
- a minced onion
- whole pepper
- a gill of cream

## Directions

1. Boil in a pint of water the crumb of a French roll or of a slice of bread, a minced onion, and some whole pepper
2. When the onion is tender drain off the water, pick out the peppercorns, and rub the bread through a sieve
3. Put it into a saucepan, with a gill of cream

title: Bread Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of good milk
- a good sized white onion
- a dozen pepper corns
- six cloves
- a blade of mace
- a pinch of grated nutmeg
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- stale bread-crumbs
- a good table-spoonful of cream

## Directions

1. Take a good sized white onion, peel off the outside skin, cut it into quarters, and put them, with a dozen pepper corns, six cloves, a blade of mace, a pinch of grated nutmeg, and a salt-spoonful of salt, into a sauce-pan containing not less than half a pint of good milk.
2. Use a very low fire to retard the boiling-stage, watching your sauce-pan narrowly as milk boils up so rapidly.
3. Remove the pan as soon as the surface of the milk looks frothy; let it cool a little, and replace it, continuing the operation until the onion is done, and adding a little milk from time to time to make good the loss by evaporation.
4. Strain it off through a piece of muslin into a bowl, and add to it, spoonful by spoonful, the stale bread-crumbs you have already prepared, till you have attained the right consistency.
5. Heat the sauce up again, and finish it off with a good table-spoonful of cream at the moment you serve it.

title: Bread Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bellary onion
- pint of milk
- pepper corns
- nutmeg
- cloves
- grated bread-crumbs
- cream

## Directions

1. Simmer till tender a Bellary onion in a pint of milk with a dozen pepper corns, a pinch of nutmeg, and four cloves
2. Strain when the onion is quite pulpy
3. Pour the strained milk over the grated bread-crumbs, and heat it up again
4. Add a spoonful of cream before serving

title: Breakfast or Dinner Rolls
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of flour
- one dessert-spoonful of good butter
- two tea-spoonfuls of Yeatman's powder
- one salt-spoonful of salt
- four table-spoonfuls of milk

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour with one of the wooden spoons after having spread the latter in the enamelled pan
2. Sprinkle the salt over it
3. Mix your dough as lightly as you can, using both wooden spoons, and shaking the milk into the flour by degrees
4. When nicely formed, add the baking powder (last thing of all mark) and stir it well into the dough
5. Divide it into four equal portions
6. Pat them into shape with the spoons
7. Place them in four patty-pans well buttered
8. Put them on the baking-sheet
9. Slip into the oven, which should have been heated to such a degree that you can hardly bear your hand inside it
10. Bake until the rolls brown very slightly, having risen into nice round forms

title: Breasts of Mutton a la Ste. Menoult
body:
## Ingredients

- breasts of mutton
- carrots
- onions
- spices
- gravy
- egg yolk
- grated bread
- clarified butter
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Stew them with carrots, onions, and spices in gravy, and when done drain them and take out the bones
2. Flatten the meat between two dishes, and when cold cut it into the form of cutlets or hearts
3. Brush them with the beaten yolk of an egg
4. Roll them in grated bread, then in clarified butter, and again in the grated bread
5. Bake them in an oven till of a fine brown colour, and serve them with an Italian or any other sauce

title: Briaal Soup, or Soup Elegant
body:
## Ingredients

- two large shoulders of veal
- white pepper
- white salt
- white stocks of celery
- whites of two eggs
- calf's head
- calf's tongue
- fancy macaroni
- pale sherry

## Directions

1. Cut up the two shoulders of veal small, bones and all
2. After washing thoroughly, boil over a brisk fire with white pepper and the best white salt
3. Skim well the scum that rises
4. Reduce the fire and allow it to simmer until the meat falls off the bones
5. Strain the soup and let it cool
6. Thoroughly free it of all fat
7. Return it into a clean digester and add more salt and white pepper if necessary, and some white stocks of celery
8. Boil it and clear it with the whites of two eggs well beaten up
9. Strain through flannel and set aside
10. Take the best and most transparent parts of a calf's head and the tongue, and boil perfectly tender without reducing them to shreds
11. Remove all the scum that rises to the surface
12. Lay the boiled tongue and meat out on a clean dish
13. Slice the tongue fine and cut out all manner of devices, such as diamonds, squares, circles, hearts, stars, etc.
14. Do the same with the best and cleanest parts of meat selected from the head
15. Take care that no particles of scum or other impurities be adhering to them
16. Where any does adhere, rinse it off in a little of the cleared soup
17. Place them carefully into the tureen in which it is purposed to serve up the soup
18. If fancy macaroni be procurable, boil a tablespoonful tender, free of all particles of dust or powder, and add to the cut-up meat and tongue
19. Over the meat and macaroni pour the boiling-hot soup
20. Add to it a wineglassful of the palest sherry and serve up hot

title: Bright Onion Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of veal
- pepper
- salt
- water
- soup herbs
- white of an egg
- pure silvery white Patna onions

## Directions

1. Cut up the shoulder of veal small, breaking all the bones
2. Wash it thoroughly
3. Put it into a pan with pepper, salt, and water
4. Boil it well and remove all the scum as it rises
5. Reduce the fire and let it simmer until the meat is perfectly dissolved
6. Strain it and cut away all the fat
7. Add soup herbs and more pepper and salt if required
8. Give it a boil up and clear it with the white of an egg well beaten up
9. Slice very fine some pure silvery white Patna onions
10. Steep them in boiling water, changing the water three or four times, every ten minutes
11. Drain away all the water and add the onions to the soup
12. Boil and serve up hot

title: Bringal Bhahjee
body:
## Ingredients

- young full-sized bringals
- mustard oil
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the bringals thoroughly and slice them about an eighth of an inch thick
2. Dry them
3. Steep them for half an hour in the ground condiments and salt
4. Fry in oil
5. Serve up hot

title: Brinjal Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice
- two fine young brinjals of large size
- salt
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the sliced onions, chilies, and lime-juice in the manner directed for potato burta
2. Carefully and thoroughly roast the brinjals in a quick ash fire
3. Remove the ashes and burnt parts of the skin if any
4. Open the brinjals and with a clean spoon remove the contents to as near the skin as possible
5. Add a good teaspoonful of salt and a teaspoonful of mustard oil to the brinjal contents
6. Work these with a spoon to a perfect pulp, throwing away the lumps or shreds if any
7. Mix with it all the onions, chilies, and lime-juice
8. If not to your taste, add more salt or lime-juice according to fancy

title: Brinjal Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three brinjals
- a clove of garlic
- a tea-spoonful of minced onion
- one tea-spoonful of green chilli
- one tea-spoonful of vinegar
- a very little green ginger
- salt
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Boil two or three brinjals, let them get cold, scrape out the whole of the inside of the pods, pass this through the sieve to get rid of the seeds.
2. Rub a soup-plate with a clove of garlic, empty the brinjal pulp therein, dress it with a tea-spoonful of minced onion, one of green chilli, one of vinegar, and a very little green ginger.
3. Season with salt and black pepper, pat the mixture into a little mould, and serve in a saucer.

title: Brinjal Fritters
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- young brinjals
- salt and water
- batter
- fat
- Dutch sauce

## Directions

1. Parboil some young brinjals in salt and water, let them get cold, and slice them in longitudinal pieces of a convenient size and thickness for fritters — two inches long, and a quarter of an inch thick
2. Pick out the seeds
3. Prepare the batter, dipping the pieces of brinjal in it, and frying them a crisp golden yellow in abundance of fat
4. Drain them quite dry, dust a little salt over them, and serve them with Dutch sauce

title: Brinjals
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender brinjals
- salt
- ground turmeric
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut a few tender brinjals in very big pieces.
2. Smear salt and ground turmeric.
3. Fry in oil.

title: Brinjals au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of brinjals
- butter
- cream
- white pepper
- salt
- anchovy sauce
- grated Parmesan or any dry mild cheese

## Directions

1. Boil a couple of nice brinjals till tender
2. Cut them in halves lengthways
3. Scoop out the inside with a silver spoon
4. Pass it through the sieve to get rid of the seeds and put it into a bowl
5. Butter the now empty cases or pods
6. Stir into the inside part that you scooped out a good spoonful of cream
7. Season with white pepper, salt, and few drops of anchovy sauce
8. Mix thoroughly and re-fill your cases
9. Shake over the surface a layer of grated Parmesan or any dry mild cheese
10. Bake for seven or eight minutes and serve

title: Brinjals in Pulse-Meal Batter
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- brinjals
- pulse-meal (besam)
- salt
- poppy-seeds
- black-cumin
- aniseeds
- oil or ghee

## Directions

1. Cut the brinjals in thin slices.
2. Dip them in a thick solution of pulse-meal beaten with salt, poppy-seeds, black-cumin and aniseeds.
3. Fry them in plenty of boiling oil or ghee till brown.
4. Eat while hot.

title: Brinjals with Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight very young brinjals, the size of a small hen's egg
- butter
- grated mild cheese
- a cupful of well made thick brown gravy

## Directions

1. Parboil eight very young brinjals, the size of a small hen's egg
2. Cut them in halves lengthways
3. Pick out the seeds with the point of a vegetable-knife
4. Butter them, and dredge over the surface of each a layer of grated mild cheese
5. Arrange them neatly on a silver dish, well buttered, or any dish that will stand the oven
6. Bake for ten minutes
7. Pour round them a cupful of well made thick brown gravy, and serve in the dish in which they were cooked, placed on a napkin

title: Brinjals with Sauce and Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight very young brinjals
- seeds
- butter
- grated mild cheese
- thick brown gravy

## Directions

1. Parboil eight very young brinjals, the size of a small hen's egg
2. Cut them in halves lengthways
3. Pick out the seeds with the point of a vegetable-knife
4. Butter them, and dredge over the surface of each a layer of grated mild cheese
5. Arrange them neatly on a silver dish, well buttered, or any dish that will stand the oven
6. Bake for ten minutes
7. Pour round them a cupful of well made thick brown gravy, and serve in the dish in which they were cooked, placed on a napkin

title: Brisket of Beef Trambland
body:
## Ingredients

- two chittacks of butter
- one tablespoonful of finely-sliced onions
- one tablespoonful and a half of flour
- half a teaspoonful of ground pepper
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one large cupful of pure milk
- two wineglassfuls of vinegar
- half a dozen pickled gherkins or French beans
- six hard-boiled eggs
- fresh brisket of beef

## Directions

1. Heat or melt in a saucepan two chittacks of butter free of water.
2. Fry to a light brown a tablespoonful of finely-sliced onions.
3. Add a tablespoonful and a half of flour, which must be put in very gradually, stirring the whole time.
4. Add half a teaspoonful of ground pepper, and one teaspoonful of salt.
5. When these have been well mixed, pour in gradually a large cupful of pure milk, and lastly two wineglassfuls of vinegar.
6. Keep stirring to prevent the sauce lumping.
7. Mince fine half a dozen pickled gherkins or French beans, and mince up also the yolks and whites of six hard-boiled eggs.
8. Boil a fresh brisket of beef, and dish up quite hot.
9. Pour over it the sauce, over which sprinkle the minced pickle, and then cover the whole with the minced eggs.

title: Brown Onion Sauce for Gravy
body:
## Ingredients

- one chittack or two ounces of butter, free of water
- half a dozen well-selected white Patna onions finely sliced
- half a chittack or one ounce of flour
- stock
- pepper
- salt
- a tablespoonful of port wine
- a tablespoonful of mushroom catsup
- lemon-juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat one chittack or two ounces of butter, free of water, in which fry to a light brown half a dozen well-selected white Patna onions finely sliced
2. Stir into it gradually half a chittack or one ounce of flour
3. Add a little stock and some pepper and salt, boil up for a few minutes
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Add a tablespoonful of port wine, and the same of mushroom catsup
6. Lemon-juice or vinegar may be added if a sharper gravy be required

title: Brunoise Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- consomme
- carrots
- turnips
- celery
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- powdered loaf-sugar
- chopped onion
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Make consomme enough for your party.
2. Cut some carrots, turnips and celery into dice.
3. Melt a piece of butter in a sauce-pan, add pepper, salt, and a tea-spoonful of powdered loaf-sugar.
4. First put in the dice of carrots and toss them on the fire till they begin to brown.
5. Next add the celery, and lastly, the turnips with a little chopped onion.
6. Work them all together, and after a few minutes, add some of the consomme.
7. Set the sauce-pan by the side of the fire, and let it simmer.
8. Skim off the butter that is thrown up to the surface in the form of scum very carefully.
9. When clear, add the rest of the consomme.
10. Let it come to the boil, and the soup will be ready to serve.
11. Add a dessert-spoonful of Madeira to the whole tureen.

title: Brussels Sprouts a la Creme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Serve with a coffee-cupful of boiling cream poured over them

title: Brussels Sprouts a la Lyonnaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- Bombay onion
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Fry a Bombay onion cut into thin rings in some butter
3. When a golden colour, add the sprouts and toss them together in the pan for a minute
4. Serve hot

title: Brussels Sprouts a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- butter
- parsley
- lime
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Toss them in butter in a saucepan with some minced parsley and the juice of a lime sprinkled over them, and salt and pepper to taste

title: Brussels Sprouts a la Poulette
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- poulette sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Send to table with a libation of good poulette sauce

title: Brussels Sprouts au Beurre
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Toss them simply in a good allowance of melted butter, with pepper and salt

title: Brussels Sprouts au Jus
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- brown gravy
- spice

## Directions

1. Boil the Brussels sprouts
2. Gently simmer them in rich brown gravy, not thickened, but slightly flavoured with spice

title: Bubble and Squeak
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- ready-boiled peas
- cauliflower
- cabbage
- potatoes
- turnips
- carrots
- pepper
- salt
- sliced ginger
- good stock

## Directions

1. Put into a pot cold meat cut into thin slices two inches square, with ready-boiled peas, cauliflower, cabbage, potatoes, turnips, and carrots cut up, with pepper, salt, and sliced ginger, and with as much good stock as will cover the meat and vegetables.
2. Allow the whole to simmer until the meat and vegetables have absorbed half the stock.
3. Serve it up bubbling and squeaking.

title: Buns
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- six ounces of butter
- two teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- a quarter of a pound of sugar
- one egg
- nearly a quarter of a pint of milk
- a few drops of essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Mix together one pound of flour, six ounces of butter, two teaspoonfuls of baking powder, a quarter of a pound of sugar, one egg, nearly a quarter of a pint of milk, and a few drops of essence of lemon
2. Bake immediately

title: Buttered Eggs
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a tablespoonful of cream or of milk
- a dust of pepper
- a piece of butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Break three eggs into a bowl with a salt-spoonful of salt, a tablespoonful of cream or of milk, and a dust of pepper.
2. Mix them well.
3. Melt a piece of butter the size of an egg in your omelette-pan.
4. Pour in your mixture, stir it about unceasingly until it is lightly set, and turn it out.
5. Tomato pulp may be mixed with the eggs, and any nice meat such as ham, tongue, corned beef, game, &c., may be minced up, and added to them.

title: Béchamel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- an onion
- an ounce of melted butter
- meat and bones
- water
- a tea-spoonful of powdered sugar
- vegetables
- a quarter of a pound of preserved butter
- a sliced carrot
- a sliced onion
- two ounces of flour
- clear broth
- half a pint of cream
- a small tinful of mushrooms chopped
- salt
- pepper
- a gill of cream

## Directions

1. Slice up the onion, and shake the rings in an ounce of melted butter at the bottom of the stew-pan
2. Do not let them take colour, but add your meat and bones at once
3. Cover them with water, omitting the browning stage
4. Make a clear consommé, adding a tea-spoonful of powdered sugar plain, and all the vegetables
5. Carefully prepare and skim to obtain a pellucid broth
6. Strain and keep ready for use
7. Take a sauce-pan, and melt a quarter of a pound of preserved butter in it
8. Fry gently in that for ten minutes a sliced carrot and a sliced onion
9. Before they take colour, add two ounces of flour
10. Stir for five minutes, and add by degrees your clear broth
11. Add half a pint of cream, a small tinful of mushrooms chopped, salt, and pepper
12. Stir over the fire till boiling
13. Permit the sauce to simmer slowly for an hour, taking off all fat that may rise
14. At the end of the hour, strain the Béchamel into a sauce-pan, and place it in the bain-marie
15. Before using, a gill of cream may be finally stirred into the sauce as you take it from the bain-marie

title: Cabbage and Cauliflower
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- cauliflower
- salt
- mustard oil
- powdered mustard

## Directions

1. Take only the interior tender portion of the cabbage
2. Tie it in a clean piece of cloth
3. Drop it into the boiling rice only a few minutes before taking down from the oven
4. Treat the boiled cabbage with salt, mustard oil, and powdered mustard if desired
5. Use the same method with cauliflowers

title: Cabbage and Cauliflower
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage, interior tender portion
- cauliflower
- salt
- mustard oil
- powdered mustard

## Directions

1. Take only the interior tender portion of the cabbage and tie it in a clean piece of cloth.
2. Drop it into the boiling rice only a few minutes before taking down from the oven.
3. Treat the boiled cabbage with salt, mustard oil, and powdered mustard if desired.
4. Use the same method with cauliflowers.

title: Cabbage Chou an Sauce Blanche
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- sauce blanche
- cream

## Directions

1. Half-boil your cabbage and take it out and drain it
2. Divide it into quarters
3. Make a nicely flavoured sauce blanche, adding a little cream if you can spare it
4. Place the quarters in this and complete their cooking therein
5. Serve, pouring the sauce over them

title: Cabbage Leaves Stuffed (Feuilles de Chou Farcies)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- pounded quenelle meat of chicken and ham or tongue
- anchovy
- egg
- white tape
- brown gravy
- bacon

## Directions

1. Boil the head of cabbage till the leaves become pliant
2. Take it from the water and gently detach a number of leaves whole and dry them on a clean cloth
3. Have ready some pounded quenelle meat of chicken and ham or tongue with an anchovy or any artistic mixture of savoury meats bound with an egg
4. Arrange a dessert-spoonful of this on a cabbage leaf and roll it carefully up in the form of a sausage
5. Wrap two or three more leaves round this and tie them up with white tape
6. Make six or eight of these rolls
7. Simmer them gently in some good brown gravy till the leaves are done
8. Pick out your rolls and untie the tapes and dispose them tastefully in the hot dish
9. Thicken the gravy and pour it over them
10. Garnish with sippets of crisply fried bacon and serve

title: Cabbage with Bacon and Herbs
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- thick slice of bacon
- shallot
- parsley
- marjoram
- thyme
- sugar
- pepper
- salt
- gravy
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Thoroughly clean the cabbage and cut into quarters
2. Steam the quarters or plunge them into boiling hot water and boil for a quarter of an hour, then take them out and drain
3. Mince a thick slice of bacon and a little shallot, parsley, marjoram, and thyme, with a pinch of sugar, and pepper and salt to taste
4. Put all in a stew-pan and set it on the fire
5. As soon as the bacon melts, lay your cabbage quarters in it and pour round them sufficient gravy to half cover them
6. Let this simmer gently till the cabbage is done
7. Lift out the quarters and place them in a hot dish and cover them up
8. Strain the gravy, thicken it with flour and butter, and pour it over the cabbage

title: Cabbage with Rice and Gravy
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- rice
- gravy
- cheese

## Directions

1. Par-boil the cabbage and cut it up into pieces the size of an egg
2. Put them with an equal quantity of half-boiled rice into as much gravy as will cover them
3. Simmer till done then serve
4. Hand round grated cheese with this dish

title: Cajure
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer of soojee
- four tablespoonfuls of ghee
- half a seer of sugar
- a quarter of a seer of milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix one seer of soojee with four tablespoonfuls of ghee
2. Add half a seer of sugar
3. Mix well together
4. Pour in gradually a quarter of a seer of milk
5. Add as much flour as will make a good dough
6. Knead it well, and then allow it to stand for two or three hours
7. Have some ghee melted; take the dough of the size of walnuts
8. Shape them like shells and fry them in the melted ghee until they acquire a rich brown colour

title: Cakes with Ginger
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa besam of moog
- water
- ghee
- asafoetida ½ tola
- powdered cumin ½ tola
- pepper ½ tola
- pasted ginger 1 chhatak
- lemon juice 1 table-spoonful
- ½ seer besam of moog
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1 powa of besam of moog with some water and fry it in small cakes in boiling ghee till very hard
2. Powder them
3. Mix the powder with fried and powdered asafoetida ½ tola, powdered cumin and pepper ½ tola each, pasted ginger 1 chhatak and a table-spoonful of lemon juice
4. Make a stiff paste of ½ seer of besam of moog with water
5. Make balls of it, press them flat, stuff with the above spiced preparation and close them again making a little flat and elongated cakes
6. Fry them in plenty of ghee and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Camp Consommé from Tinned Beef
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- gravy from tinned beef
- fat from the gravy
- dried sweet herbs
- onion
- vegetables
- pepper-corns
- spice
- salt

## Directions

1. Skim the fat that may rise to the surface of the gravy
2. Put fat into a sauce-pan with a bag of dried sweet herbs, an onion cut into quarters, any vegetables you can spare, some pepper-corns, a pinch of spice, and salt according to the quantity
3. Simmer this gently to extract the flavour of the things you have added
4. In about a couple of hours you will have an excellent consommé, quite fit to be served as soup

title: Canapes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread
- butter
- green butter
- prawn or lobster meat
- Nepaul pepper
- lettuce
- beetroot
- mayonnaise sauce
- chopped olive or chopped capers

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread a quarter of an inch thick, and two inches long if heart-shaped, two inches in diameter if round, and two inches square if rectangular
2. Fry these a pale golden colour in butter
3. Set them on a dish to get cold
4. Spread a layer of green butter over each piece of fried bread
5. Place a layer of prawn or lobster meat pounded with butter and slightly seasoned with Nepaul pepper
6. Smooth this with a dessert-knife
7. Place a leaf of lettuce cut from the golden heart upon the top of the prawn meat
8. Place a piece of beetroot shaped with your cutter on top
9. Pour a dessert-spoonful of rich, thickly worked, mayonnaise sauce, iced, over each canape when thus prepared and placed in the dish for serving
10. Sprinkle a little chopped olive, or chopped capers, or the two mixed over each cap of mayonnaise dressing
11. The dish should stand on ice before serving

title: Candied Bael
body:
## Ingredients

- bael fruit
- boiling clarified sugar or syrup

## Directions

1. Select fruit as for the preserve
2. Cut it into slices and remove the seeds and gum
3. After steeping it in cold water, drain it
4. Put it in a preserving-pan with sufficient boiling clarified sugar or syrup to cover it
5. Simmer it over a slow fire for half an hour or until it becomes quite tender
6. Remove the pan from the fire
7. Lay the fruit on some fresh oiled paper spread on tin trays
8. Expose it to the sun where it will crystallize in a few hours, and the oil will prevent it adhering to the paper

title: Candied Pulwal
body:
## Ingredients

- pulwals
- hot or boiling clarified sugar or syrup

## Directions

1. Observe the same process as directed for pulwal preserve
2. Use hot or boiling clarified sugar or syrup
3. Expose the preserve to the sun spread out on fresh oiled paper to dry

title: Capillaire
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of water
- three pounds of lump sugar
- one pound of soft sugar
- the whites and yolks of two eggs well beaten up
- two ounces of the best orange-flower water

## Directions

1. To a quart of water add three pounds of lump sugar, one pound of soft sugar, and the whites and yolks of two eggs well beaten up
2. Boil it gently, and skim well
3. On the scum ceasing to rise, remove the pan from the fire
4. Add two ounces of the best orange-flower water
5. Strain through flannel

title: Capon in the French Style
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a capon
- a pint of good stock
- an onion
- carrots, sliced
- half a glass of sherry
- pepper and salt
- a few slices of fat bacon
- a couple of tablespoonfuls of minced truffle
- sippets of dry fried bacon
- slices of lime

## Directions

1. Braise the capon with a pint of good stock round it, an onion and carrots sliced, half a glass of sherry, and pepper and salt to taste
2. Pin a few slices of fat bacon over the breast and use the larding needle
3. Braise gently till done
4. Lift the capon out of the pan, strain and reduce the gravy in which it was cooked
5. Add a couple of tablespoonfuls of minced truffle and pour this over the bird before serving
6. Garnish the dish with sippets of dry fried bacon and slices of lime

title: Capon with Maccaroni
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- strips of fat bacon
- sweet onions
- broth
- carrot
- celery leaves and stalks
- onions
- pepper corns
- bunch of parsley
- dried thyme
- glass of sherry
- liver
- tomato pulp
- maccaroni
- puree of young turnips
- potatoes a la maitre d'hotel

## Directions

1. Lard the capon with strips of fat bacon, and fill the cavity of its carcass with a couple of sweet onions
2. Choose a deep stew-pan, and place the bird therein, with a carrot cut into slices, celery leaves and stalks, two onions, pepper corns, a bunch of parsley, some dried thyme in a muslin bag, and a glass of sherry
3. Braise the bird in this till done
4. Meanwhile pound the liver you boiled in the broth, and get ready some tomato pulp
5. Lift out the capon, and dish it upon some previously boiled maccaroni
6. Strain off the gravy in which it was braised, thicken it, add the liver, and tomato pulp
7. Give it a rapid boil up, and pour it over the bird
8. Serve with puree of young turnips, and potatoes a la maitre d'hotel

title: Carrots a la Flamande
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound and a half of tender carrots
- 1 ounce of butter
- a pinch of salt
- 1 pinch of sugar
- a coffee-cupful of water
- 2 yolks of eggs
- a coffee-cupful of cream
- half an ounce of butter
- a table-spoonful of chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Choose a pound and a half of tender carrots, blanch them in scalding water, scrape off their tough skin, and trim them in slices the eighth of an inch thick
2. Put the pieces into a stew-pan with one ounce of butter, a pinch of salt and one of sugar, and a coffee-cupful of water
3. Cover the pan, and simmer for twenty minutes, shaking the pan occasionally to ensure even cooking
4. When done, remove the pan, let its contents cool a little, and then stir in two yolks of eggs, a coffee-cupful of cream, and half an ounce of butter
5. Add a table-spoonful of chopped parsley and serve

title: Carrots, Flemish Way
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- parsley
- young onions
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Prepare (after boiling) in the form of dice, balls, stars, crescents, &c.
2. stew with chopped parsley, young onions, salt and pepper, in plain melted butter, or good brown gravy

title: Casserole of Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- good mealy potatoes
- butter
- cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and boil some good mealy potatoes, pound them, and mix with them some butter, cream, and a little salt
2. put them about an inch and a half high upon a dish, and leave an opening in the centre
3. bake it of a light brown colour, and take out as much more from the centre as will admit of a ragout, fricassee cutlet, or macaroni being put in

title: Cauliflower au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- butter
- cheese
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Dispose the pieces of cauliflower in a well-buttered dish that will stand the oven
2. Pour over them some melted butter
3. Dust some grated cheese over them, pepper and salt
4. Bake for ten minutes and serve

title: Cauliflower Au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- butter
- grated cheese
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Dispose the pieces of cauliflower in a well-buttered dish that will stand the oven
2. Pour over them some melted butter
3. Dust some grated cheese over them, pepper and salt
4. Bake for ten minutes
5. Serve

title: Cauliflower Au Gratin With Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- two ounces of Parmesan or any mild grated cheese
- one ounce of butter
- one and a quarter ounce of flour
- one and a half pint of water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil or steam the head first till all but done, which you must test with a skewer
2. Drain it thoroughly
3. Dissolve two ounces of Parmesan or any mild grated cheese in a sauce composed of one ounce of butter, one and a quarter ounce of flour, one and a half pint of water, pepper, and salt
4. Arrange the flower in a neat pie-dish, either whole if large enough, or in pieces with the green leaves introduced between each piece
5. Pour the sauce well round this
6. Dust a layer of cheese over the surface
7. Bake and serve as soon as the top takes colour
