8. A red-hot iron passed closely over the surface of the dish will brown it nicely

title: Cauliflower Au Gratin Without Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- fine stale bread-crumbs
- olives
- capers
- anchovy
- hot melted butter or salad oil

## Directions

1. Arrange your pieces of cauliflower as before explained
2. Strew over them some fine stale bread-crumbs, with some olives, a few capers, and an anchovy chopped up small
3. Pour over this a cupful of hot melted butter
4. Bake for ten minutes
5. Serve

title: Cauliflower with Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- tomato sauce or sauce blanche or bechamel or sauce piquante
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil or steam the cauliflower carefully
2. Cut the stalk flat so that the cauliflower can sit up with the flower in the centre and the leaves round it
3. Pour about it a good tomato sauce or a plain sauce blanche, bechamel, or sauce piquante
4. Dust some finely rasped crumbs over the whole

title: Cauliflowers
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflowers
- ground turmeric
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut them in medium pieces and wash.
2. Boil in water.
3. Smear ground turmeric and salt.
4. Fry them in oil.

title: Cauliflowers in Pulse-Meal Batter
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflowers
- pulse-meal (besam)
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Dip cauliflowers, boiled or not, in a thick solution of besam with salt.
2. Fry in oil to crispness.
3. Serve while hot.

title: Caviare Toasts
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight slices of fried bread
- caviare roe
- ounce of butter
- Nepaul pepper
- juice of a lime
- mayonnaise sauce
- olive

## Directions

1. Prepare six or eight neatly shaped slices of fried bread
2. Take from a tin of caviare enough roe to cover each piece nicely
3. Choose a small sauce-pan, melt an ounce of butter in it
4. Stir in the caviare, dust it well with Nepaul pepper, and add the juice of a lime
5. When piping hot, spread the caviare over the fried bread (which should have been kept in the oven) and send up the canapes without delay
6. Alternatively, spread the caviare cold over the cold fried bread, mask the surface with thick mayonnaise sauce, and garnish with a turned olive upon each

title: Celery as Entremets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- water
- butter
- Dutch sauce

## Directions

1. trim the heads neatly and cut short, say five or six inches in length
2. split lengthwise in two or four pieces according to the thickness of the head
3. plunge the pieces into fast boiling water
4. as soon as tender, drain, turn out upon a hot silver dish
5. serve like asparagus with a pat of butter melting over them
6. send a piping hot Dutch sauce round in a boat

title: Celery Stewed in Blanc a la Moelle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- blanc
- beef marrow
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Stew celery in blanc as described
2. Pile the celery in the centre of the dish
3. Thicken the blanc in which it was cooked, and pour it over the pile
4. Break the bone, take out the raw marrow, cut it into dice
5. Blanch them for five minutes in scalding water, drain them, and heat them up gently in a little of the sauce en bain-marie
6. Put a dessert-spoonful each of this into little croustades of fried bread, and serve round the celery as a garnish
7. Cut your croustades square, out of stale bread, and hollow a little space in each to hold the marrow

title: Celery with Butter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four heads of fine white celery
- salt
- water or milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Trim four heads of fine white celery very neatly
2. Wash them carefully, and when convinced that no earth remains hidden in the leaves, boil them in salt and water, or in milk if you can afford it
3. When tender, drain, split each piece in half
4. Serve as hot as possible upon a silver dish like asparagus
5. Butter plainly melted, as for Dutch sauce, should alone accompany the vegetable thus delicately dressed

title: Ceylon Moss, Seaweed, and Iceland Moss Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- moss or weed
- fresh water
- a wineglass of the best vinegar per seer or two pounds of the weed
- clarified sugar or syrup
- cochineal (optional)

## Directions

1. Steep the moss or weed for two or three days in fresh water, changing the water two or three times a day
2. Wash it well once before boiling it
3. To every seer or two pounds of the weed add a wineglass of the best vinegar
4. Allow it to simmer over a gentle fire until it thickens, so as to congeal in a glass
5. Strain the moss or weed through a towel
6. Pour the liquid into clarified sugar or syrup, and boil them together for half an hour
7. Pour the jelly into large wide dishes, and when quite cold cut it into cakes
8. If desired, the jelly may be coloured or tinted with cochineal

title: Chamcham
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer hard chhana
- 1/4 chhatak middlings or sabeda
- cardamom seeds
- rose-water one tea-spoonful
- milk
- sugar syrup
- crystalline sugar

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana and mix thoroughly with 1/4 chhatak of middlings or sabeda, seeds of a few cardamoms, a tea-spoonful of rose-water and a little milk if necessary to make a mud-like paste
2. Make round and long finger-like cakes of it
3. Drop them in a boiling syrup of sugar
4. Pick them out when a little brownish in colour
5. Roll on crystalline sugar

title: Chamcham with Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer khowa kheer
- milk
- rose-water 2 or 3 tea-spoonfuls
- cardamom seeds
- powdered aniseeds
- pistachios
- camphor
- sugar syrup
- crystalline sugar

## Directions

1. Make a muddy paste of 1/2 seer of khowa kheer with milk and 2 or 3 tea-spoonfuls of rose-water
2. Mix with the seeds of a few cardamoms, a little powdered aniseeds, some pieces of pistachios and very little camphor
3. Prepare cakes as before
4. Boil in a syrup of sugar on mild heat till hard and well boiled
5. Pick them out and roll on crystalline sugar
6. Serve when cool

title: Chandra-Prova
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee 1 chhatak
- flour 1 powa
- powdered rice 3 chhataks
- middlings 1 chhatak
- moistened and pasted pulses of kalai 0.5 powa
- husked sesame 1 powa
- pasted almonds
- salt
- aniseeds
- cardamom seeds
- ginger-juice
- powdered pepper
- water
- milk 2.5 seers
- sugar
- rose water
- camphor

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly ghee with flour, powdered rice and middlings
2. Add moistened and pasted pulses of kalai, double the quantity of husked sesame and pasted almonds
3. Add adequate quantities of salt, aniseeds, cardamom seeds, ginger-juice, powdered pepper and water to make a not-so-stiff paste
4. Beat thoroughly
5. Make squares as before and fry in ghee
6. Boil say 2.5 seers of milk till it is less than half
7. Add sugar as desired, a little rose water, very little camphor and the fried squares
8. Boil a few minutes more and serve in cups when cool

title: Chappatee or Hand-bread
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat-flour
- water
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Make a good dough of flour and water
2. Take a piece about the size of an egg
3. Roll it out to the circumference of a half-plate
4. Bake it over an iron or earthen plate

title: Charlotte Russe with Burnt Almonds
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- finger biscuits
- couple of ounces of sugar
- quarter of a pound of blanched Jordan almonds
- pint and a half of very rich custard
- half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
- breakfast-cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Line a mould with finger biscuits as described in Menu No. IV.
2. Put a couple of ounces of sugar in a copper-pan; melt it till it is very hot.
3. Stir into it a quarter of a pound of blanched Jordan almonds for a few minutes.
4. Spread the almonds on a dish to cool.
5. When cold, pound them to a puree saving a few, which should only be minced roughly, and stirred into the ice when nearly formed.
6. Mix a pint and a half of very rich custard and stir the burnt almond puree into it while quite hot.
7. Strain, whip, and add half an ounce of dissolved gelatine.
8. Put the custard into the pail, adding a breakfast-cupful of whipped cream, and finish as explained in the menu previously quoted.

title: Chatni of Alubokhara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- alubokharas 1 powa
- raisins 1 powa
- dates 1 powa
- ½ powa of oil
- panch-foron
- broken chillies
- 3 powas of water
- 1 powa of sugar
- salt
- pasted turmeric

## Directions

1. Wash alubokharas, raisins and dates 1 powa each and keep the raisins and alubokharas under water for about a quarter of an hour in an enamelled pot
2. Heat ½ powa of oil and fry a generous pinch of panch-foron and some broken chillies in the oil
3. Fry a little the alubokharas, raisins and dates in it
4. Add 3 powas of water and when it boils add 1 powa of sugar, a generous pinch of salt and a little pasted turmeric
5. Take down when the desired thickness of the soup is reached

title: Chatni of Green Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mature yet green mangoes
- mustard seeds
- panch-forons
- broken chillies
- 1 chhatak of oil
- 1 1/2 seer of water
- 1 seer of mango pieces
- 1 powa of cleansed raisins
- 1/2 powa of salt
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems of a few mature yet green mangoes and cut them in two or four pieces without peeling them
2. Throw away the seeds
3. Fry some mustards, panch-forons and broken chillies in 1 chhatak of oil and then add 1 1/2 seer of water
4. When it boils add 1 seer of the pieces of mangoes, 1 powa of cleansed raisins and 1/2 powa of salt
5. Add 1 1/2 powa of sugar when the pieces are well boiled and take down when the soup is thick

title: Chatni of Papaw
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of seedless ripe tamarind
- 1½ powa of water
- big and green papaw
- 1 powa of papaw slices
- mustard seeds
- panch-forons
- broken chillies
- ½ chhatak of oil
- 1 chhatak of cleansed raisins
- salt
- mango-scented ginger juice 1 table-spoonful
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of seedless ripe tamarind thoroughly with 1½ powa of water and throw away the refuse
2. Peel a big and green papaw and cut in thin and long slices after removing the seeds
3. Boil 1 powa of the slices in water and drain out the water when they are fully boiled
4. Fry a few mustard, panch-forons and broken chillies in ½ chhatak of oil
5. Add the boiled pieces of papaw, 1 chhatak of cleansed raisins, the tamarind water and a generous pinch of salt
6. Mix with a table-spoonful of the juice of mango-scented ginger and a little sugar when the soup is thick, and take down

title: Chatni of Pine-Apple
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe pine-apple
- ½ seer of pine-apple pieces
- lime water
- ½ powa of raisins
- ghee
- panch-forons
- mustard seeds
- cardamom seeds
- sugar 1 powa
- salt
- lemon-juice 1 table-spoonful

## Directions

1. Peel thickly a ripe pine-apple, remove the eyes, cut in thin and small pieces and keep ½ seer of the pieces in lime water for a few minutes
2. Wash with fresh water and boil them with ½ powa of raisins with a little water
3. On softening remove the pan off fire
4. Heat a little ghee in another pan and fry a few panch-forons, mustard and cardamom seeds in it
5. Add the mass, sugar 1 powa, a pinch of salt and a table-spoonful of lemon-juice
6. Boil for some time and take down when the juice is thick

title: Chatni of Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of seedless ripe tamarind
- 1 powa of water
- potatoes
- 2 or 3 broken chillies
- mustards
- panch-foron
- 1 chhatak of mustard oil
- 1 powa of potato slices
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of seedless ripe tamarind thoroughly with 1 powa of water and throw away the refuse
2. Peel and cut some potatoes in round and thin slices
3. Fry 2 or 3 broken chillies, a pinch of mustards and another of panch-foron in 1 chhatak of mustard oil
4. Fry 1 powa of the slices of potatoes in it
5. When the slices are half browned add the tamarind water and a generous pinch of salt
6. Take down when the soup is thick after mixing with a little sugar

title: Chatni of Pudina Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of pudina leaves
- 2 or 3 green chillies
- tamarind pulp
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a paste of a handful of pudina leaves with 2 or 3 green chillies
2. Mix with tamarind pulp, salt and sugar adequate quantities
3. Serve

title: Chatni of Pumpkin
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ powa of seedless ripe tamarind
- 1½ seer of water
- tender pumpkin
- mustard seeds
- broken chillies
- panch-forons
- 5 chhataks of oil
- ½ seer of pumpkin pieces
- ½ powa of cleansed raisins
- salt
- ½ powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix ½ powa of seedless ripe tamarind with 1½ seer of water and throw away the refuse
2. Peel a tender pumpkin and cut in very small thin and long pieces
3. Fry a few mustard, broken chillies and panch-forons in 5 chhataks of oil and pour in the tamarind solution
4. Add ½ seer of the pieces of pumpkin, ½ powa of cleansed raisins and a generous pinch of salt
5. A little later add ½ powa of sugar and take down when the soup is thick

title: Chatni of Pumpkin
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of seedless ripe tamarind
- 1 1/2 seer of water
- tender pumpkin
- mustard seeds
- broken chillies
- panch-forons
- 5 chhatak of oil
- 1/2 seer of pumpkin pieces
- 1/2 powa of cleansed raisins
- salt
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 powa of seedless ripe tamarind with 1 1/2 seer of water and throw away the refuse
2. Peel a tender pumpkin and cut in very small thin and long pieces
3. Fry a few mustard, broken chillies and panch-forons in 5 chhatak of oil and pour in the tamarind solution
4. Add 1/2 seer of the pieces of pumpkin, 1/2 powa of cleansed raisins and a generous pinch of salt
5. A little later add 1 1/2 powa of sugar and take down when the soup is thick

title: Chaud-Froid
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- plain clear jelly
- nice liqueur
- greengages
- apricots
- cherries
- strawberries
- pears
- vanilla blanc-manger
- preserved apples
- prunes

## Directions

1. Make enough plain clear jelly, flavoured with any nice liqueur, to fill a pretty border mould
2. Get ready a mixed collection of dessert fruits such as greengages, apricots, cherries, strawberries, pears, &c., cut them into pieces
3. Garnish the mould as tastefully as you can
4. When the garnish has set, complete the jelly with layers of the remaining fruit, and set it in ice
5. Make a breakfast-cupful of vanilla blanc-manger, dipping slices of preserved apples into it to counterfeit fillets of chicken masked in white sauce
6. Set them on ice for the blanc-manger to congeal, and then pack the centre of the jelly with them
7. Garnish the white fillets with slices of prunes to represent truffles, and a few plain slices of apple cut with fretted edges to imitate cocks-combs

title: Chaud-Froid de Filets de Foie Gras
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a tin of foie gras
- chicken chaud-froid glaze
- ground rice
- butter
- points d'asperges, flageolets, or a macedoine
- pure cream

## Directions

1. Open a tin of foie gras and slice it very carefully in slices about half an inch thick
2. Out of these slices trim some nicely shaped fillets, Cashmere shawl patterns, or ovals
3. Put them on a flat dish and mask them with chicken chaud-froid glaze
4. When the glaze has set, trim each fillet neatly
5. Set them in a circle with their masked sides outwards upon a socle of ground rice which should be spread with butter for them to adhere to it
6. Fill the centre of the circle with points d'asperges, flageolets, or a macedoine, moistened with pure cream
7. Serve very cold

title: Cheese Fingers
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of puff paste
- a pinch of salt
- two ounces of grated Parmesan or other mild cheese
- a very little cayenne, Nepaul pepper, or a few drops of tabasco

## Directions

1. Work the ingredients together
2. Roll the paste out about half an inch thick
3. Cut it into oblong shapes, about three inches long
4. Bake and serve as hot as possible on a napkin

title: Chhana and Dhonka
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chhana
- ghee or oil
- flour
- potato
- coriander
- pepper
- turmeric
- ginger
- salt
- curd
- water
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Take some chhana and drain off the water under high pressure
2. Cut the chhana in rectangular pieces half inch thick and brown them in ghee or oil
3. Mix some flour with the chhana if it fails to take the form
4. Brown some medium pieces of peeled potatoes in oil or ghee
5. Add pasted coriander, pepper, turmeric, ginger and salt in adequate quantities
6. Add a little curd and water after a time
7. Add browned chhana when it boils
8. Season the curry with a little ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves and red chillies
9. Add some more ghee and powdered garam-masalla before taking down
10. Keep a little thick soup

title: Chhana Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice 1 seer
- Chhana 1.5 seer
- Ghee 1.5 powa
- Sugar 0.5 powa
- Seeds of the big quality cardamoms 0.5 tola
- Pistachios 0.5 powa
- Almonds 0.5 powa
- Raisins 0.5 powa
- Saffron 0.5 tola
- White pepper 1 tola
- Cassia leaves 16 in number
- Ginger 2 tolas
- Cinnamon 0.5 tola
- Salt 4 tolas
- Water 3 seers
- Flour 0.5 powa

## Directions

1. Drain off the water from good and fresh chhana by high pressure and cut it with a knife in pieces of medium size
2. If the chhana is too soft to take any form, mix it with about 0.5 powa of flour
3. Brown the pieces of chhana in hot ghee
4. Heat some ghee in a boiler and cover its bottom with a layer of the cassia leaves
5. On them add the rice, washed and dried, along with the cardamom seeds, pistachios, almonds and raisins, peeled and cut in pieces where necessary
6. Make a paste of or powder the white peppers and the ginger and mix them with the 3 seers of water with saffron and salt dissolved in it
7. Pour this water on the rice, close the lid and apply a gentle heat
8. When it begins to boil add the browned chhana
9. When the rice have almost softened add sugar, the rest of the ghee and powdered cinnamon
10. Shake the boiler or stir its contents thoroughly so that the rice undergo complete turning
11. Boil for some time again and take off the fire when the mass is fully boiled

title: Chhanabara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer chhana
- 2.5 powas sabeda
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1 seer of chhana (not hard) with 2.5 powas of sabeda
2. Make a moderately stiff yet fine paste of the mixture adding a little water if necessary
3. Make spherical balls of it
4. Fry them in ghee
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Chhanabara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of chhana (not hard)
- 2.5 powas of sabeda
- water

## Directions

1. Mix 1 seer of chhana (not hard) with 2.5 powas of sabeda and make a moderately stiff yet fine paste of the mixture adding a little water if necessary
2. Make spherical balls of it
3. Fry them in ghee
4. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Chhanch
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- khowa kheer
- ghee
- eggs
- sugar
- cardamom seeds
- raisins
- almonds
- rose water
- flour

## Directions

1. Fry a half cap of khowa kheer in ghee and grind it finely
2. Mix it with two cups of the contents of eggs, some sugar, cardamom seeds, raisins, pasted almonds and a little rose water, and beat thoroughly
3. Take a few casting moulds for shaping and smear their interior surfaces with a little ghee
4. Stuff them with the mixture, close them with a little flour-paste and boil in water for about 15 minutes
5. Then open the joints of the moulds and the food is ready to be served

title: Chhanch
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup khowa kheer
- ghee
- egg contents
- sugar
- cardamom seeds
- raisins
- pasted almonds
- rose water
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Fry half cup of khowa kheer in ghee and grind finely
2. Mix with two cups of egg contents, sugar, cardamom seeds, raisins, pasted almonds and rose water
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Smear interior surfaces of casting moulds with ghee
5. Stuff moulds with mixture
6. Close with flour paste
7. Boil in water for about 15 minutes
8. Open joints of moulds and serve

title: Chhanchra
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- oil
- saks
- potato
- patol
- brinjal
- bean
- radish
- gourd
- cauliflower
- squash
- jhinga
- chichinga
- bori
- powdered mustard
- chillies
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- ghee
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Fry the waste portions of big fishes in the same way as in other curries
2. Prepare 1 or 2 kinds of saks
3. Cut some other unpeeled vegetables in pieces and wash
4. Mix with powdered turmeric
5. After some time brown them in oil
6. Add sak and fish and stir
7. A little later add water, powdered mustard, chillies, coriander, pepper, cumin and salt
8. Take down when no soup is left
9. Ghee and powdered garam-masalla may be added if desired

title: Chhola or Ghana (Gram)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Chhola-dal 1/2 seer
- ghee 1 powa
- coriander 1 tola
- pepper 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- ginger 1/4 tola
- sugar 1/2 tola
- salt 1 tola
- cloves 1/4 tola
- cardamom 1/4 tola
- cinnamon 1/4 tola
- cassia leaves a few
- red chillies a few
- panch-foron 1 pinch
- water 1 seer
- chhatoo of grams a handful

## Directions

1. Heat half the ghee in a pan and when the bubbles die out add a few red chillies broken in two, ginger and half the cloves, cinnamon, and cardamom, all of them ground.
2. Stir for about a minute and add the dal, washed and dried, in it.
3. After frying for about 5 minutes add the specified amount of hot water.
4. Add the sugar, half the cassia leaves and powdered or pasted turmeric, coriander, pepper and cumin when the pulses are half boiled.
5. If the water becomes unmanageable some of it may be taken out to be added later on.
6. Add salt and the chhatoo when the pulses are fully boiled.
7. Heat the rest of the ghee in another pot with the panch-foron and the rest of the cassia leaves, cloves, cinnamons and the seeds of the cardamoms, all entire.
8. When the smell of the spices comes out add the ghee in the boiling dal.
9. Stir the dal for a few minutes and take down when it is moderately thick.

title: Chhola, Khesari and Peas
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chhola, khesari or peas
- salt
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Wash cleanly and keep any of the dals moist under water for about two hours.
2. Grind them into a paste when they are soaked with water.
3. Tie the paste loosely in a piece of cloth.
4. Drop it into the rice when the latter start boiling.
5. Mix salt and mustard oil.

title: Chicken a la Ville
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of chicken
- egg yolks
- bread crumbs
- boiling fat
- potato duchesses
- parsley
- minced mushrooms

## Directions

1. Stew pieces of chicken in their own broth
2. Drain them and make a rich veloute with the broth, thickening it with yolks of egg like custard
3. Dip the pieces of chicken in the thick sauce, and let them get cold
4. Bread-crumb them, and fry them a golden yellow in boiling fat
5. Drain them, and pile them on a napkin, garnished with small potato duchesses, and fried parsley
6. Send the rest of the sauce round in a boat after adding to it a heaped-up tablespoonful of minced mushrooms

title: Chicken a la Villeroy
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of nice chickens
- egg yolks
- bread-crumbs
- boiling fat
- small potato duchesses
- parsley
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut up and cook a couple of nice chickens as explained for poulet a la St. Lambert
2. When the pieces of chicken have been stewed in their own broth, drain them
3. Make a rich veloute with the latter, thickening it with yolks of egg like custard
4. Dip the pieces of chicken in the thick sauce, and let them get cold
5. Bread-crumb them, and fry them a golden yellow in boiling fat
6. Drain them, and pile them on a napkin, garnished with small potato duchesses, and fried parsley
7. Send the rest of the sauce round in a boat after adding to it a heaped-up table-spoonful of minced mushrooms

title: Chicken and Tongue Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- ox tongue
- six mutton cutlets from the neck
- chopped parsley
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut up a chicken as if for curry
2. Slice up a cold-boiled, home-cured tongue
3. Trim six nice mutton cutlets from the neck as if for an entree
4. Throw the chicken scraps, the tongue skin and trimmings, and all the remnants of mutton left after shaping the cutlets, into a large sauce-pan with the materials for gravy flavouring
5. Make a nice broth with them
6. When the broth is nearly ready, give it half a glass of Madeira, and strain when it is finished
7. Pack the pie thus: a dust of chopped parsley at the bottom, then the mutton cutlets, above them the slices of tongue, and the chicken at the top
8. Pour in the gravy, garnish the top with hard-boiled eggs
9. Cover the pie with a good crust, and bake

title: Chicken Chaud-Froid
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two good-sized chickens
- flour
- bones, skin, necks, pinions
- onion
- celery
- carrot
- bunch of parsley
- almonds
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour
- aspic or plain strong meat jelly
- yolks of three eggs
- thick cream
- creme de topinambours
- carrots
- green peas
- truffle
- cock's comb

## Directions

1. Cover the breasts of two good-sized chickens with paper, and roast them without letting them take colour
2. When cold, remove the breast meat as neatly as you can, also the flesh of the thighs and drumsticks
3. Out of the pieces thus obtained, trim a number of neat fillets as nearly the same size as possible
4. Dredge a little flour over them and cover them up
5. Take all the bones left after the above operation, skin, necks, pinions, &c., and make a strong chicken broth with them, adding an onion, some celery, a carrot, a bunch of parsley, a few almonds, pepper, and salt, to flavour it well
6. When the broth is ready, strain it into a bowl and skim it
7. Proceed to make with it a rich veloute sauce, using for a pint of broth an ounce of butter and one of flour
8. Strain it and let it get cold, then add to its thickness by stirring into it one-third of its quantity of liquefied aspic, or plain strong meat jelly, and reducing it over the fire till it coats the spoon
9. Take it from the fire, and add the yolks of three eggs, and (if available) a table-spoonful of thick cream
10. Pour some of this over the pieces of chicken completely coating each piece with a thick white glaze
11. Put them away so that the glaze may cool and set
12. Prepare a border of creme de topinambours decorating the top of the mould with carrots and green peas
13. After steaming it, let the mould get cold, and then set it in ice
14. When quite cold, turn it out carefully upon a cold silver dish
15. Fill the hollow in the centre with the pieces of glazed chicken, introducing a slice of truffle here and there as you arrange them
16. Garnish the top of the chicken with cold boiled cock's comb and truffles
17. Let the dish remain in the ice-box until the time arrives for it to go to table

title: Chicken Country Captain
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces ghee
- half a teaspoonful ground chilies
- one teaspoonful salt
- a quarter of a teaspoonful ground turmeric
- twenty onions

## Directions

1. Cut up in the usual way an ordinary curry chicken.
2. Warm the ghee and fry the sliced onions, which when brown set aside.
3. Fry the ground turmeric and chilies, then throw in the chicken and salt, and continue to fry, stirring the whole, until the chicken is tender.
4. Serve it up, strewing over it the fried onions.

title: Chicken Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- one chittack or two ounces of ghee
- two breakfast-cupfuls of water
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- half a teaspoonful of ground coriander-seed

## Directions

1. Take a usual full-sized curry chicken and divide it into sixteen or eighteen pieces
2. Warm the pot and melt in it the ghee
3. Immediately it begins to bubble, throw in all the ground condiments, stirring until quite brown
4. Put in the cut-up chicken and the salt, and stir up to a good light-brown colour
5. Add the water and allow the whole to simmer over a slow fire until the chicken is quite tender and the liquid reduced to about half its original quantity
6. The operation of cooking or simmering will take from a half to three-quarters of an hour

title: Chicken Curry
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a young chicken
- flour
- onion
- carrot
- half a dozen pepper corns
- celery
- salt
- sugar
- cold water
- milk of cocoanut or almond
- six good shallots or two small white onions
- two ounces of Denmark, Normandy, or other good tinned butter
- garlic
- curry stock powder
- curry paste
- wine-glassful of cocoanut milk
- breakfast-cupful of broth
- an ounce of butter or clarified beef suet
- bay-leaf
- chutney
- sweet-acid
- pounded almond and cocoanut
- spice
- grated green ginger

## Directions

1. Choose a nice young chicken and cut it up neatly as for a fricassee, place the pieces aside, and dredge over them a little flour.
2. Take all the trimmings, neck, pinions, leg bones, feet, head, and any scraps of meat that can be spared, and cast them into a sauce-pan with an onion sliced, a carrot sliced, half a dozen pepper corns, a bit of celery, a pinch of salt and one of sugar.
3. Cover them with cold water and make the best broth you can.
4. When ready, strain the contents of the sauce-pan into a bowl, and skim it clean to obtain a good breakfast-cupful of weak stock.
5. Make a breakfast-cupful of milk of cocoanut, or almond.
6. Take your stew-pan, and having sliced up six good shallots, or two small white onions, cast the rings into it with two ounces of tinned butter.
7. Add a finely-minced clove of garlic, and fry till the onions turn a nice yellow brown.
8. Add a heaped-up table-spoonful of the stock powder, and one of the paste, or, if you have not made the latter, two table-spoonfuls of the powder.
9. Cook the curry-stuff with the onions and butter for a minute or two, slowly, adding by degrees a wine-glassful of the cocoanut milk, and then also by degrees the breakfast-cupful of broth.
10. Simmer for a quarter of an hour until you have a rich, thick curry gravy or sauce.
11. Place the stew-pan en bain-marie.
12. Take a frying-pan and melt in it an ounce of butter, or clarified beef suet, add a shallot cut up small, and fry for a couple of minutes.
13. Put the pieces of chicken into the frying-pan, and lightly fry them.
14. As soon as slightly coloured, transfer the pieces of chicken to the stew-pan in which they should rest for at least half an hour, marinading in the curry gravy.
15. Place the stew-pan over a gentle fire, and if the liquid be found insufficient to cover the pieces of chicken, stock, if available, or water, should be added.
16. Encourage a gentle simmering process, during which add the bay-leaf, chutney, and sweet-acid.
17. If powder without fresh paste has been used, put in the pounded almond and cocoanut with a little spice and grated green ginger.
18. Taste the curry gravy, and if a little more acid or sweet be found necessary, make the proper correction.
19. As soon as the pieces of chicken have become tender and thoroughly stewed, stir in a coffee-cupful of cocoanut milk, and in three minutes the operation will be complete.
20. If a semi-dry or dry curry be required, further reduce the gravy by simmering with the lid off, stirring the pieces of meat continually with a wooden spoon to prevent their catching at the bottom of the pan.
21. When the proper amount of absorption has been attained, remove the pan and serve.

title: Chicken Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- full-sized curry chicken
- one chittack and a half or three ounces of ghee
- one breakfast-cupful of water
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful each of ground turmeric and chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- twelve onions cut lengthways, each into six or eight slices
- half a teaspoonful of ground coriander-seed

## Directions

1. Take a full-sized curry chicken and divide it into sixteen or eighteen pieces
2. Melt the ghee in a warm or heated pot
3. Fry brown the sliced onions and set aside
4. Fry the ground condiments, stirring the whole
5. When brown, add the cut-up chicken with the salt, and fry to a rich brown
6. Chop the fried onions and put into the pot with one cup of water
7. Allow to simmer over a slow fire for about one hour, when the chicken will be perfectly tender, and the liquid reduced to a thick consistency, and to half its original quantity

title: Chicken Forcemeat Ball Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- a good fat chicken
- a quarter of a pound of beef suet
- salt
- pepper
- garden herbs
- bread-crumbs
- egg
- lard or ghee
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Procure a good fat chicken and a quarter of a pound of beef suet
2. Put the suet into a mortar with all the fleshy parts of the chicken, and pound to a pulp
3. Make a stock of gravy of the bones
4. Mix with the pounded meat all the several ingredients named in the beef forcemeat ball curry recipe, with the addition of an egg well beaten up
5. Make into balls, roll in bread-crumbs, and curry as directed for beef forcemeat ball curry

title: Chicken Malay Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- one chittack and a half or three ounces of ghee
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one cup of strong cocoanut milk
- one dozen onions

## Directions

1. Cut up the chicken in the usual manner
2. Warm the ghee
3. Fry and set aside the sliced onions
4. Fry brown the ground condiments
5. Add the chicken and salt
6. When fried brown, pour in the cocoanut milk and the fried onions finely chopped
7. Allow to simmer over a slow fire
8. The Malay doopiaja will be ready in an hour

title: Chicken Malay Gravy Curry with White Pumpkin or Cucumber
body:
## Ingredients

- one full-sized curry chicken
- six cucumbers or a quarter of a white pumpkin
- ghee
- ground condiments
- ground hot spices
- salt
- two cups of cocoanut milk
- lemon-grass

## Directions

1. Divide the chicken as before directed
2. Remove the green skin and the part containing the seeds from the pumpkin or cucumber, then cut it up into sixteen pieces of about two inches square, and steep in water
3. Fry in the ghee all the ground condiments, including the ground hot spices
4. When brown, add the cut-up chicken and salt
5. Fry to a fine bright light brown
6. Put in the pumpkin, having previously allowed all the water to drain away through a colander
7. Pour in the two cups of cocoanut milk, the lemon-grass, and hot spices
8. Allow the whole to simmer over a slow fire for about half an hour, when the curry will be ready
9. The blade of lemon-grass is not dished up

title: Chicken Pellow
body:
## Ingredients

- a good-sized chicken
- one pound of beef
- two cupfuls of clean water
- onions
- ginger
- salt
- twelve onions
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- half a pound or a coonkee of the best bassrauttee or cheeneesuckur
- a few cloves
- four or five cardamoms
- half a dozen small sticks of cinnamon
- peppercorns
- a blade or two of mace
- one dessertspoonful of salt
- ghee or butter
- two hard-boiled eggs
- half a dozen bits of finely-sliced and fried bacon

## Directions

1. Take a good-sized chicken and clean, truss, and boil it with one pound of beef in two cupfuls of clean water, seasoning it with onions, ginger, and salt
2. When sufficiently cooked, but yet quite firm, remove the chicken, and set it and the gravy aside
3. Cut up twelve onions lengthways into fine slices
4. Warm your pot
5. Melt in it two chittacks or four ounces of ghee, and as it bubbles, throw in the sliced onions and fry to a light brown
6. Remove and set aside the fried onions
7. Put in half a pound or a coonkee of the best bassrauttee or cheeneesuckur, having drained away all the water in which it was washed, and fry
8. On the rice absorbing the ghee, throw in a few cloves, four or five cardamoms, half a dozen small sticks of cinnamon, some peppercorns, a blade or two of mace, and one dessertspoonful of salt
9. Mix up the whole, and pour over it the gravy in which the chicken and beef were boiled, or as much of it only as will entirely cover the rice
10. Close the pot immediately with a close-fitting cover, and set on a slow fire
11. As the gravy continues to decrease or to be absorbed, keep reducing the fire
12. Shake up the pot occasionally, or stir its contents, to prevent the pellow from burning
13. Brown the boiled chicken in a pan with ghee or butter
14. Place the chicken, either whole or cut up, on the centre of a dish, covering it with the pellow
15. Strew over it the fried onions, garnishing it besides with two hard-boiled eggs, cut into halves, or in some device, and with half a dozen bits of finely-sliced and fried bacon

title: Chicken, St. Lambert Fashion
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two nice chickens
- onion
- pepper corns
- meat gravy
- lime peel
- ketchup
- salt
- carrot
- French beans
- sweet almonds
- blade of mace
- yolks of three eggs

## Directions

1. Cut up two nice chickens as if for fricassee
2. Steep the pieces in cold water for half an hour, then drain them
3. Select the following pieces: the four wings, the four legs, two breasts, and four thighs, and put them aside covered up
4. Take all that remains (the backs, pinions, necks, livers, gizzards, and trimmings), and with an onion cut up, some pepper corns, a few spoonfuls of meat gravy, a bit of lime peel, a tea-spoonful of ketchup, and salt, make as good a pint of broth as you can
5. When ready, fish out the livers, and strain the broth
6. Put into the stew-pan the selected joints of the chickens, cover them with the strained broth, and stew them gently till done, with one carrot, and a handful of French beans
7. Pick out the chicken, strain the broth, and put the vegetables aside
8. Proceed with the broth to make a nice veloute (rich white sauce) flavoured with a dozen sweet almonds pounded, and a blade of mace
9. Thicken in the bain-marie with the yolks of three eggs
10. When satisfactory, strain and put in the pieces of chicken, heating them without boiling
11. Cut the carrot, French beans, with the livers you saved, into dice
12. When you dish the chicken, garnish your entree with them. A casserole of mashed potatoes, shaped like the pastry case of a vol-au-vent, and nicely ornamented, can hold the chicken, and the garnish should be sprinkled over the surface

title: Chili Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- birds'-eye chilies
- vinegar
- blotting-paper

## Directions

1. Pick, clean, and put into a glass-stoppered bottle one chittack or two ounces of birds'-eye chilies
2. Pour over them a pint and half of the best vinegar
3. After a month's time filter through blotting-paper a pint of the vinegar
4. Add to what remains half a pint more of vinegar
5. Expose it to the sun for a few days, when the second portion will also be ready for use

title: Chocolate Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces fine Spanish chocolate with vanilla flavour
- water
- two pints pure milk
- powdered loaf sugar
- salt
- yolks of eight eggs

## Directions

1. Rasp three ounces of fine Spanish chocolate, which has the vanilla flavour
2. Make a paste of it with the smallest possible quantity of water
3. Put two pints of pure milk over the fire, and let it boil
4. Add powdered loaf sugar to your taste, and a little salt
5. Beat up the chocolate with some of the milk as it boils, and mix it well
6. Pour it into the boiling milk, which you must keep in motion
7. Add the yolks of eight eggs well beaten up
8. Keep stirring in, or rather milling the mixture, until of sufficient consistency
9. When cool enough put the custards into glasses

title: Chocolate Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of chocolate
- half a pint of milk
- few drops of vanilla essence
- two tea-spoonfuls of potato-flour or corn-flour

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate into half a pint of milk
2. Add a few drops of vanilla essence
3. Boil the milk so that the chocolate may become thoroughly dissolved
4. Thicken the flavoured milk with two tea-spoonfuls of potato-flour or corn-flour
5. Strain it, and set it to get cold

title: Chocolate Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of chocolate
- half a pint of milk
- a few drops of vanilla essence
- two tea-spoonfuls of potato-flour or corn-flour
- yolks of four eggs
- a glass of liqueur
- whites of six eggs

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate into half a pint of milk
2. Add a few drops of vanilla essence
3. Boil the milk so that the chocolate may become thoroughly dissolved
4. Thicken the flavoured milk with two tea-spoonfuls of potato-flour or corn-flour
5. Strain and set to get cold
6. Beat up the yolks of four eggs and a glass of liqueur with the cold batter
7. Add the whites of six eggs in the shape of a stiff froth just before baking
8. Pour into a proper souffle case or tin
9. Bake until done

title: Christmas Plum Pudding (Indian Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a half of cleaned and picked raisins
- half a pound of currants
- three-quarters of a pound of finely-grated bread-crumbs
- half a pound of finely-sliced mixed peel
- three-quarters of a pound of finely-minced suet
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- half a teaspoonful of finely-powdered mixed spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, and mace)
- half a pound of butter free of water
- twelve eggs well beaten
- a wineglassful of brandy
- flour
- brandy sauce

## Directions

1. Mix cleaned and picked raisins, currants, finely-grated bread-crumbs, finely-sliced mixed peel, finely-minced suet, and sugar well together
2. Mix with half a teaspoonful of finely-powdered mixed spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, and mace)
3. Moisten the mixture with half a pound of butter free of water, twelve eggs well beaten, and a wineglassful of brandy, stirring well the whole time
4. Butter a large piece of cloth or napkin and dredge it well with flour
5. Put the mixture into it and tie it down tightly
6. Boil it steadily for seven hours
7. Take it out of the boiler and hang it up immediately until the day it is intended to be eaten
8. Boil again for fully two hours, ensuring the water is boiling before the pudding is put into it
9. Turn it out of the towel and serve up with brandy sauce

title: Claret Jelly
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bottle of sound claret
- six ounces of white sugar
- a glass of brandy
- the rind of one well-washed lime
- the juice of three limes
- one tea-cupful of raspberry syrup or half a pound of raspberry jam
- an ounce of isinglass
- crystallized cherries
- iced cream

## Directions

1. Boil all together claret, white sugar, brandy, lime rind and lime juice, and raspberry syrup or jam
2. Add an ounce of isinglass, and strain through muslin
3. Decorate a jelly mould with crystallized cherries, set it upon ice
4. Pour some of the liquid jelly into the mould, and set the cherries
5. Gradually add the liquid till the mould is completed
6. Thoroughly consolidate the jelly in ice, and serve with iced cream

title: Clear Fish Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary clear consomme
- one large crab or two small ones
- a quarter ounce of dried basil
- a table-spoonful of Madeira
- the juice of half a lime

## Directions

1. Make ordinary clear consomme for the number you expect
2. Boil the crab, drain and clean it
3. Pick out the flesh, saving that of the claws for garnishing
4. Pound the rest of the meat and shells in a mortar
5. Put the whole of it into the consomme you have made, with a little bag containing a quarter ounce of dried basil
6. Boil gently for half an hour
7. Strain through a fine sieve, or tamis cloth
8. Heat it up again, and pour it into your soup tureen over some little quenelles made of the claw meat you saved
9. Add a table-spoonful of Madeira and the juice of half a lime

title: Clear Mock-Turtle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- stock meat
- half an ox-head unskinned or a sheep's head
- water
- an ounce of salt
- two Bombay onions in quarters
- a head of celery
- a couple of carrots
- the rind of two limes
- a large bunch of parsley
- a quarter ounce of dried basil
- a teaspoonful of dried thyme
- a teaspoonful of marjoram
- a dozen pepper corns
- two anchovies
- half a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup
- a tablespoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of caramel
- a glass of Madeira
- the juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Procure the stock meat as usual, and either half an ox-head unskinned, or a sheep's head, well, clean the head thoroughly, keeping the skin on, and scalding it to get rid of the hair or wool
2. Remove every atom of brain and wash the meat in several waters
3. Set it to soak awhile in cold water, and then place it in your soup kettle
4. Cover it with water and let it simmer slowly for an hour, skimming all fat and scum that may rise
5. Take it up (it will be about half done), remove the bones and set it, with a weight upon it, on a dish to flatten and get cold
6. Throw your shin, &c., into the soup kettle, adding to the cold head liquor already there, the bones just removed, sufficient cold water to cover the bone and meat completely and an ounce of salt
7. Proceed as for consomme skimming very carefully, and retarding the boiling point as much as you can by periodical additions of water
8. When boiling takes place, ease off the fire, and add two Bombay onions in quarters, a head of celery, a couple of carrots, the rind of two limes, a large bunch of parsley, a quarter ounce of dried basil, a teaspoonful of dried thyme, and of marjoram (the herbs in a muslin bag), a dozen pepper corns, two anchovies cut up, half a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup, a tablespoonful of sugar, and a teaspoonful of caramel
9. Simmer slowly now for about three hours, skimming the surface occasionally, and on no account permitting the vessel to boil
10. Strain very carefully, and set the soup in a bowl to cool and throw up all grease
11. Cut the cold head into one-and-a-half-inch squares, skim the cool soup well, and pour it into a large sauce-pan adding the pieces of head
12. Let the pieces cook slowly for half an hour, then drain them, and again strain the soup
13. Clarify it if necessary adding a glass of Madeira, and the juice of a lime
14. Heat this up finally when required, and pour the soup into the tureen over a dozen or so carefully selected pieces of the head arranged therein
15. Serve with limes cut into quarters, which should be handed round followed by the Madeira

title: Clear Mulligatunny
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- above three pints of clear consommé
- two ounces coriander seed
- one ounce cummin seed
- one ounce fenugreek or maythee
- half an ounce mustard seed
- two cloves garlic
- dozen black peppercorns
- four or five leaves hurreapliool or Izodia neem

## Directions

1. Put coriander seed, cummin seed, fenugreek, mustard seed, garlic cloves, black peppercorns, and hurreapliool leaves into a muslin bag without pounding or bruising
2. Boil the bag with the soup
3. Remove as soon as the flavour is satisfactory
4. Finish with a few drops of tabasco or chilli-vinegar if tabasco be unobtainable

title: Clear Partridge Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three partridges
- soup meat
- Madeira
- sugar
- chilli vinegar

## Directions

1. Buy three partridges in addition to your customary soup meat
2. Pluck the birds, draw them, and cut them up, breaking all bones of any size with a mallet
3. Set the soup meat for consomme as usual
4. When you have obtained as strong a stock as possible therefrom, strain it, and let it get cool
5. Remove all fat that may rise to the surface
6. When quite clear, pour it into a large sauce-pan, adding all the pieces of partridge, including the livers of the birds
7. Set this on the fire to come slowly to the boil, skimming it very carefully
8. After it has boiled, slack off the fire, and let the contents of your sauce-pan simmer slowly for an hour or more
9. Strain the soup from the bones, clarify
10. Give it half a glass of Madeira, a pinch of sugar, and a few drops of chilli vinegar to finish with

title: Clear Prawn Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary beef consomme
- a dozen good-sized prawns
- pepper corns
- a dessert-spoonful of dried basil
- a bunch of parsley
- celery leaves
- half a glass of Madeira

## Directions

1. Prepare your ordinary beef consomme from your daily allowance of soup meat
2. Boil a dozen good-sized prawns, save the liquor in which you have cooked them, and reduce that a little
3. Throw into it the meat which you pick from the shells, and the shells pounded
4. Add the beef consomme, some pepper corns, a dessert-spoonful of dried basil tied up in a muslin bag, a bunch of parsley, and some celery leaves
5. Let this simmer gently
6. Strain after three hours' simmering
7. Clarify it, and add half a glass of Madeira
8. Quenelles of the prawn meat may be used as garnish

title: Clear Snipe Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bright clear consomme
- six snipes
- a pinch of salt
- chopped parsley
- a little thyme
- Nepaul pepper or spiced pepper
- a very little red currant jelly
- a few drops of portwine
- a little bread-crumb soaked in stock
- two eggs
- half a glass of Madeira

## Directions

1. Prepare a bright clear consomme for your eight covers
2. Slightly roast six snipes; let them get cold
3. Divide them into convenient pieces, mash them in a mortar and throw the whole, with the exception of the livers of the birds, into the consomme
4. Let it come to the boil, skimming carefully, and then remain simmering all the afternoon
5. Towards evening, strain the liquor from the bones, and set it to cool
6. Pick from the remnants of the snipe sufficient meat to form a dozen tiny quenelles the size of an olive
7. Pound this meat in a mortar with the saved livers, a pinch of salt, some chopped parsley, a little thyme, and a dust of Nepaul pepper or spiced pepper
8. Stir in a very little red currant jelly and a few drops of portwine
9. Stiffen this with a little bread-crumb soaked in stock, and bind the whole with two eggs
10. Let it get cold, roll it out, and divide it into little portions
11. Form them between two tea-spoons, and poach till done just before serving the soup
12. Put them into the soup at the last minute
13. Meanwhile clarify the consomme, assisted by half a glass of Madeira, and again heat almost to boiling point

title: Clear Soup with Custard Tablets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- consomme for eight covers
- yolks of four eggs
- water
- salt
- cochineal
- spinach-greening
- butter
- parsley
- shallot
- Parmesan

## Directions

1. Mix the yolks of four eggs with a little water and a pinch of salt
2. Strain the mixture
3. Divide it into three equal portions
4. Colour one with cochineal, one with spinach-greening, and leave the third plain
5. Pour them into three little moulds previously buttered
6. Dip them into a pan of hot water
7. Steam just long enough to set the custards
8. Take them off the fire, and when cold, turn the moulds out on a napkin
9. Cut them up into dice or any pretty shapes with your vegetable cutter as gently as possible
10. Add them to the soup just before serving
11. Hand round grated Parmesan

title: Climbing Fish with Cabbage
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage 1 medium sized or 2 to 2.5 seers chopped
- potato 1/2 seer
- green legumes of peas 1 seer
- climbing fish 1 seer
- ghee 1/2 powa
- oil 1/2 powa
- salt 4 tolas
- ginger 1 chhatak
- turmeric 2.5 tolas
- coriander 1/8 tola
- pepper 1 tola
- cumin 3 tolas
- garam-masalla 1 tola
- chillies
- cassia leaves
- panch-foron

## Directions

1. Prepare the climbing fishes, keep entire and fry in oil.
2. Take out the green peas from its legumes and fry them with pieces of potatoes and cassia leaves in ghee or oil.
3. Chop the tender white portion of the cabbage and fry it with ghee or oil till light brown.
4. Then add the potatoes, peas, fishes, all the spices pasted together and salt.
5. Water will come out of the vegetables when salt is added but a little more water may be necessary to boil them.
6. Season the curry with ghee boiled with a pinch of panch-foron, cassia leaves and chillies.
7. Add pasted garam-masalla before taking down.

title: Cocoa-Nut with Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- a few big sized lobsters or shrimps
- powdered turmeric
- chillies
- pepper
- cumin
- ginger
- mustard
- salt
- mustard oil
- minced onions
- half-ripe cocoa-nut
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Remove the tips of the heads and all the scales of a few big sized lobsters or shrimps and wash.
2. Rub them with powdered turmeric, chillies, pepper, cumin, ginger, mustard, salt and mustard oil in small quantities.
3. Add minced onions if desired.
4. Stuff them into a half-ripe cocoa-nut stone broken in two with the kernel inside and the water drained off.
5. Close the two parts with a little flour paste and boil in water for an hour or more.

title: Cocoanut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cocoanut kernel
- turmeric
- salt
- oil
- potatoes
- pepper
- cumin
- coriander
- ginger
- water
- ghee
- red chillies
- cassia leaves
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Take out the kernel of a ripe cocoanut, peel off the skin, and cut in small pieces.
2. Mix turmeric and salt, and brown a little with oil.
3. Add an equal quantity of browned pieces of peeled potatoes.
4. Add powdered pepper, cumin, coriander, ginger, salt and water in adequate quantities.
5. Season the curry when it is boiled with ghee, or oil heated with a few red chillies and cassia leaves.
6. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down when no soup is left.

title: Cocoanut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cocoanut
- ground turmeric
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Break the stone of a ripe cocoanut and bring out the kernel with a knife.
2. Remove the brown skin of the kernel and cut it in small pieces.
3. Smear ground turmeric and salt.
4. Brown them in oil.

title: Cocoanut Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pounded cocoanut
- minced onion
- green chilli
- green ginger
- an atom of garlic
- tamarind juice
- red pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix pounded cocoanut with minced onion and green chilli, green ginger, and an atom of garlic, moistened with tamarind juice, and seasoned with red pepper and salt.

title: Cocoanut Milk
body:
## Ingredients

- a cocoanut
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Scrape or rasp the nut with the aid of the khoornee into very fine particles.
2. Put it into a deep vessel, and pour boiling water over it until the whole of the scraped cocoanut is covered.
3. Allow it to steep for ten or fifteen minutes.
4. Carefully strain through a clean napkin into another vessel or cup.
5. Return the pulp into the original vessel, and pour more boiling water over it.
6. Continue this operation of steeping in boiling water and straining until you have obtained the required quantity of the extracted milk of the cocoanut.
7. Throw away the pulp.

title: Cocoanut Pittas
body:
## Ingredients

- one cocoanut, finely scraped
- jagree
- a few grains of black cardamom seed
- finely-sifted rice-flour
- boiling-hot water

## Directions

1. Scrape finely a cocoanut, brown it with some jagree and a few grains of the black cardamom seed, and set aside
2. Prepare a pastry of finely-sifted rice-flour, kneaded with boiling-hot water
3. Take as much as the size of a duck's egg, and press it out flat in the palm of your hand to the size of a large saucer
4. Put a tablespoonful of the fried cocoanut into it, and close it up in a half-moon shape, with the help of a little water
5. Have a wide-mouthed large earthen pot of boiling water; stretch and tie over its mouth a napkin, and steam the pittas or cakes over them
6. They will be ready in half an hour, and may be eaten hot or cold

title: Coconut Rice Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- a breakfastcupful of fine rice
- water
- a hard coconut
- boiling-hot water
- date jagree or treacle to taste
- a few grains of aniseed
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak a breakfastcupful of fine rice in water until quite soft
2. Scoop out the contents of a hard coconut
3. Extract all the milk with a little boiling-hot water
4. Boil the rice in it and sweeten it to taste with some date jagree or treacle
5. Put in a few grains of aniseed
6. Pour the mixture into a buttered pudding-dish and bake it slightly

title: Coffee Made With Percolator
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee berries
- butter or salad oil
- boiling water
- boiling milk

## Directions

1. Get really good coffee berries and be willing to pay more than the usual price for them
2. Roast a tablespoonful of berries at a time in a frying-pan over a very low fire
3. Melt a little butter, sufficient to lubricate the berries
4. Stir the berries about until they turn a light Havannah brown, discarding any berry that takes a darker tint
5. Grind them at once as coarsely as your mill will admit, with grains quite as large as those of rifle gun-powder
6. Make the coffee as soon afterwards as possible
7. Heat the coffee-pot thoroughly
8. Fill the upper chamber of the percolator according to your requirements
9. Ram the coffee down firmly
10. Pour boiling water in tea-spoonfuls at a time through the upper strainer upon the powder
11. Add water slowly so that the coffee will become thoroughly soaked and the essence will be as strong as possible
12. As soon as the coffee has run through, pour the rich essence into your cups
13. For cafe au lait, fill the cups with boiling milk; for cafe noir, fill with a little boiling water
14. For a party, make the essence just before dinner and keep it covered, then reheat gently at the time it is wanted

title: Cold Boiled Pork Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold boiled pork
- sixteen one-inch square pieces

## Directions

1. Cut from the remains of cold boiled pork sixteen one-inch square pieces
2. Doopiaja it in the way directed for a chicken
3. The time required to simmer will not exceed that allowed for the chicken doopiaja

title: Cold Corned-Beef Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice
- cold corned beef

## Directions

1. Steep sliced onions and chilies in lime-juice
2. Have the red well-corned part of a cold round of beef nicely pounded
3. Add to it the onions, chilies, and as much of the lime-juice as may be desirable

title: Cold Ham Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- cold ham
- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice

## Directions

1. Make in the same way as the beef and tongue burtas

title: Cold Moringa Root Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cupful of moringa or horse-radish root scrapings
- mayonnaise or tartare sauce
- cream

## Directions

1. Rasp the moringa or horse-radish root till you have a cupful of fine scrapings
2. Mingle them with an ordinary mayonnaise or tartare sauce, iced
3. Add cream if desired, or use the usual mixture of eggs, oil, mustard, and vinegar

title: Cold Tongue Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- well-corned cold tongue
- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice

## Directions

1. Make in the same manner as the cold beef burta using the remains of a well-corned cold tongue

title: Collared Brisket
body:
## Ingredients

- brisket of beef
- saltpetre
- suckur or brown sugar
- one seer of salt
- lime-juice

## Directions

1. Bone a brisket of beef
2. Rub into it saltpetre, suckur or brown sugar, and one seer of salt with some lime-juice
3. Keep it in the brine for thirty-six hours, rubbing it continually
4. Remove it from the brine and clear away all the salt
5. Roll the beef tightly into a collar and secure it well
6. Inclose it in a stout duster
7. Boil it

title: Colouring for Jellies, Creams, Ices, and Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty grains of cochineal
- Twenty grains of alum
- Twenty grains of cream of tartar
- One gill of water

## Directions

1. Boil very slowly in a gill of water, till reduced to one half, twenty grains of cochineal, and the same quantity of alum and cream of tartar finely pounded
2. Strain, and keep it in a small phial

title: Common Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- nearly half a pound of flour
- five well-beaten eggs
- a quart of good milk
- fresh lard
- pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. With nearly half a pound of flour mix five well-beaten eggs
2. Add, by degrees, a quart of good milk
3. Fry them in fresh lard
4. Serve them with pounded loaf sugar strewed between each

title: Consomme au Maccaroni
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- maccaroni
- milk or weak stock
- consomme
- sherry
- tabasco or chilli vinegar
- grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Proceed to make a bright clear consomme in the usual way
2. Boil till tender in milk or weak stock some pieces of maccaroni
3. When done, drain them and let them get cold
4. Cut them into thin rings
5. Add them to your consomme just before serving with a dessert-spoonful of sherry to which a few drops of tabasco or chilli vinegar have been added
6. Let a plate of grated Parmesan be handed round with the soup

title: Consomme aux Oeufs Poches
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- clear gravy soup
- eggs
- dried tarragon
- grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Carefully poach the eggs (one for each person), neither too lightly nor too hard
2. Trim the eggs neatly leaving as little margin of white as possible
3. Heat the soup plates and place the poached eggs in them
4. Pour the soup over the eggs
5. Flavour the consomme with a few leaves of dried tarragon
6. Serve with grated Parmesan handed round on a separate plate

title: Consomme aux Points d'Asperges
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- clear, well-flavoured consomme
- a small tin of asparagus

## Directions

1. Prepare a clear, well-flavoured consomme for eight
2. Take a small tin of asparagus, open it, and turn the pieces out upon a dish, draining from them all the liquor of the tin
3. Choose American asparagus if you can, because it is so much greener than either the French or English
4. When drained, cut the tender ends of the asparagus into pieces half an inch long using a dessert-knife
5. Put them away carefully, and throw the tough ends into the consomme
6. When strained, and ready to serve, add to the soup the chopped green points
7. Beat it up to concert pitch, and serve

title: Consomme aux Quenelles
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- bright, clear consomme
- a little dried basil
- a table-spoonful of Madeira
- prawn quenelles

## Directions

1. Make a bright, clear consomme according to directions
2. Add a little dried basil with the vegetables
3. Add a table-spoonful of Madeira to finish
4. Add the poached quenelles to the soup at the last moment

title: Consomme d'Abatis
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- giblets of the goose
- an ounce of butter
- an onion
- a glass of Marsala
- beef consomme
- a clove of garlic
- sweet herbs
- a stick of celery
- a carrot
- a dozen pepper corns
- a dessert-spoonful of sugar
- salt
- caramel
- half a glass of Madeira
- a drop or two of tabasco

## Directions

1. Clean the giblets carefully and cut them up small
2. Put them with an ounce of butter into a stew-pan with an onion shredded finely
3. Fry them a pale golden colour
4. Add a glass of Marsala and a little beef consomme
5. Reduce to a glaze
6. Pour in the remainder of the consomme
7. Throw in an onion, a clove of garlic, a muslin bag of sweet herbs, a stick of celery, a carrot, a dozen pepper corns, a dessert-spoonful of sugar, and one of salt
8. Simmer for two hours
9. Strain the liquor
10. Colour it with caramel
11. Let it get cold and skim off all grease
12. Clarify if necessary
13. Add half a glass of Madeira and a drop or two of tabasco
14. Serve very hot

title: Consomme de Laitue
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- clear consomme
- one large or two small cabbage lettuces
- a couple of slices of bacon
- stock
- two cloves
- an onion
- a tea-spoonful of sugar
- one tea-spoonful of salt
- a tea-spoonful of dried herbs

## Directions

1. Make a clear consomme as usual
2. Pick and wash one large or two small cabbage lettuces
3. Dip them into boiling water for a few minutes
4. Take them out and cut them into quarters
5. Tie them together again
6. Butter a stew-pan and place a couple of slices of bacon at the bottom
7. Lay the lettuces on them and cover them with stock
8. Add two cloves, an onion, a tea-spoonful of sugar, one tea-spoonful of salt, and a tea-spoonful of dried herbs
9. Simmer the lettuces until done
10. Take them out, drain them, and when dry, cut them into shreds with a dessert-knife
11. Put the shredded pieces at the bottom of your tureen
12. Pour the consomme, boiling hot, over them and serve

title: Consomme de Volaille
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good full-sized fowl
- water
- a Bombay onion
- fresh vegetables
- a bag of mixed sweet herbs
- a clove of garlic
- a dozen pepper corns
- a pinch of parsley seed
- a few drops of celery essence
- a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- a tea-spoonful of sugar
- a dessert-spoonful of salt
- caramel
- a dessert-spoonful of Marsala
- a dissolved dessert-spoonful of Julienne

## Directions

1. Kill a good full-sized fowl
2. cut it up
3. put it, giblets and all, into a stew-pan
4. cover it with water
5. let it come very slowly to the boil
6. skim off the scum which may rise during that process
7. when the boiling stage has been attained, take the pan from the fire for a minute
8. throw into it a Bombay onion, cut into quarters
9. add any fresh vegetables you may have brought out
10. add a bag of mixed sweet herbs, a clove of garlic, a dozen pepper corns, a pinch of parsley seed, a few drops of celery essence, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, a tea-spoonful of sugar, and a dessert-spoonful of salt
11. let the pan boil again till the onion is soft
12. reduce the fire for the simmering stage
13. When the pieces of fowl are nice and tender, the broth is ready
14. lift up your pan and strain off the broth into a bowl
15. tint slightly with caramel and serve hot with a dessert-spoonful of Marsala and a dissolved dessert-spoonful of Julienne

title: Consomme with Vegetables
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts carrots, turnips, leeks, onions, and celery
- two ounces fresh butter
- one tea-spoonful powdered sugar
- pepper
- salt
- broth from the consomme
- lettuce leaves
- sorrel leaves

## Directions

1. Cut carrots, turnips, leeks, onions, and celery into thin strips not more than the eighth of an inch across, and an inch long
2. Put them into a sauce-pan with two ounces of fresh butter, a tea-spoonful of powdered sugar, a little pepper, and a pinch of salt
3. Toss them lightly on the fire until they take colour slightly, say for five minutes or so
4. Cover them with a little broth from the consomme and carefully remove the grease thrown up by the butter
5. Let the vegetables thus cooked remain in the gravy near the fire, nice and hot, until the time of serving
6. Add the vegetables with a few leaves of lettuce and of sorrel finely shredded to the rest of the consomme
7. Bring to the boil, finally skim, and serve

title: Coorkits
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- minced onions
- cassia leaves
- ghee
- minced meat
- water
- loaf
- milk
- eggs
- salt
- powders of pepper
- powders of garam-masalla
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Fry minced onions and cassia leaves with enough ghee
2. Fry 1/2 seer of minced meat with these for about 10 minutes and add 1/2 seer of water
3. When the water dries up add 1/2 powa of loaf pasted with 1/2 powa of milk, 3 or 4 eggs, salt, powders of pepper and garam-masalla
4. Stir thoroughly and take down when the mass is a stiff paste
5. Make about 25 coorkits of round and long shape, dip into beaten eggs and fry in plenty of ghee

title: Corn-Flour Blanc Mange
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of corn-flour per quart of milk
- a stick of cinnamon
- lemon-peel
- sugar to taste
- pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Use the rice blanc mange recipe
2. Use the proportion of two tablespoonfuls of corn-flour to every quart of milk

title: Court Bouillon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- cold water
- vinegar
- carrots
- onions
- garlic
- sweet herbs
- spices
- pepper
- salt
- parsley or celery

## Directions

1. Having placed the fish in the fish-kettle with enough cold water to cover it, add a glass of vinegar, some slices of carrots and onions, and a clove of garlic.
2. Add sweet herbs, and spices tied up in a muslin bag, with pepper, salt, and parsley or celery.
3. The proportions of all these must depend upon the quantity of fish to be boiled, the skill of the cook, and the taste of the company.
4. When nearly done, half draw the fish-kettle from the fire, and let the fish simmer gently till the moment of serving.
5. A mixture of white wine such as chablis, sauterne, or hock, and water, in equal parts, may be used instead of the vinegar and water.

title: Court Bouillon a la Nantaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Court bouillon a la Nantaise is made of milk and water in equal parts, salt, and pepper in proportion.

title: Crab Cofta Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twelve crabs full of the red coral
- salt
- pepper
- herbs
- bread-crumbs
- egg
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chillies
- ground turmeric
- ground peppercorns
- ground garlic
- bay-leaves
- lemon-grass
- water

## Directions

1. Select ten or twelve glieewalla kakaralis, or crabs full of the red coral
2. Wash them thoroughly, then boil them
3. Remove all the meat and coral out of the shells, pound to a pulp
4. After mixing all the ingredients and fixing them with an egg well beaten up, make into balls
5. Cook them in all respects according to the directions for lobster cofta curry
6. Simmer for about half an hour

title: Crabs in Shell
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- salt
- onion
- ginger juice
- lime-juice
- pepper
- mushroom catsup
- butter
- stock
- bread-crumbs
- coral

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the crabs in salt
2. Remove them out of the shells and pick and clean them well, and reserve the coral for dressing
3. Chop and mince fine the crabs
4. Add some onion and ginger juice, a little lime-juice, pepper, and salt, and a little mushroom catsup
5. Melt some butter, and fry the mixture in it until the butter be absorbed
6. Add a little stock, and remove from the fire immediately the stock begins to dry
7. Butter the shells, and fill with the mixture
8. Take some finely-sifted bread-crumbs and grind down the coral, and put it over the mixture on the shells, with the bread-crumbs, and bits of butter
9. Bake for a few minutes

title: Cream of Aniseed
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty drops of essential oil of aniseed
- one quart of spirits of wine
- one quart of syrup

## Directions

1. Put twenty drops of essential oil of aniseed in a quart of spirits of wine
2. After shaking it well, mix with it a quart of syrup
3. Filter and put it in bottles

title: Cream of Cinnamon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of spirits of wine
- two drops of oil of cinnamon
- two drops of oil of roses
- one quart of syrup
- red tincture

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine add two drops of oil of cinnamon and two of oil of roses
2. Shake it well until the oil has thoroughly dissolved
3. Add a quart of syrup and a sufficient quantity of red tincture to produce a bright full colour
4. Filter and bottle it

title: Cream of Cinnamon
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of spirits of wine
- two drops of oil of cinnamon
- two drops of oil of roses
- a quart of syrup
- a sufficient quantity of red tincture

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine add two drops of oil of cinnamon and two of oil of roses
2. shake it well until the oil has thoroughly dissolved
3. add a quart of syrup and a sufficient quantity of red tincture to produce a bright full colour
4. filter and bottle

title: Cream of Citron
body:
## Ingredients

- sixty drops of the oil of citron
- one quart of spirits of wine of the strength of sixty-two degrees overproof
- one quart of syrup
- two ounces of yellow colouring matter

## Directions

1. Put sixty drops of the oil of citron into a quart of spirits of wine of the strength of sixty-two degrees overproof
2. Shake it well
3. Mix with it a quart of syrup and two ounces of yellow colouring matter
4. Filter the whole through filtering-paper
5. If not sufficiently bright, filter it a second time through some fresh paper
6. Bottle it

title: Cream of Cloves
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of spirits of wine of the strength of sixty-two degrees overproof
- forty drops of oil of cloves
- one quart of syrup
- yellow colouring matter

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine of the strength given in the foregoing recipe add forty drops of oil of cloves
2. Shake it well
3. Mix with it a quart of syrup, and as much yellow colouring matter as will give it a good colour
4. Filter through filtering-paper
5. Bottle immediately

title: Cream of Mint
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of spirits of wine
- twenty-five drops of oil of mint
- three drops of oil of citron
- a quart of syrup
- green colouring tincture

## Directions

1. Drop into a quart of spirits of wine twenty-five drops of oil of mint and three of oil of citron
2. shake it well
3. add a quart of syrup and as much green colouring tincture as may be necessary
4. filter and bottle

title: Cream of Noyau
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof
- twenty drops of good essential oil of bitter almonds
- six drops of oil of orange
- one quart of syrup

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof add twenty drops of good essential oil of bitter almonds and six drops of oil of orange
2. Shake it well
3. Add a quart of syrup
4. Filter it through paper until it is quite clear

title: Cream of Vanilla
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve drops of tincture of vanilla
- a quart of spirits of wine
- a quart of syrup

## Directions

1. Put twelve drops of tincture of vanilla into a quart of spirits of wine
2. shake it well
3. add a quart of syrup
4. when well mixed, let it stand for a quarter of an hour
5. filter it two or three times through filtering paper, but do not filter again if it comes out bright and clear the first time

title: Creamed Jerusalem Artichokes in Mould
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of artichokes
- milk
- half a pint of cream
- four eggs
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes in milk
2. Mash the artichokes, and pass them through the hair sieve
3. Add the cream and the eggs well beaten up whites and all
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Put the mixture into a well-buttered mould, and steam it for one hour
6. Turn it out, and garnish it with tomato puree, Parmesan sauce, or a rich veloute

title: Creme or Pain de Volaille
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- large fowl or two good sized chickens
- two sheep's feet
- giblets
- two ounces of fat bacon
- pepper
- salt
- crumb of bread
- stock
- half a tin of white mushrooms
- four whole raw eggs
- truffles

## Directions

1. Cut off all the white meat from the fowl for the puree
2. Make a strong essence with all the bones, skin, scraps, two sheep's feet cleaned and cut up, and the giblets
3. Strain the essence when done, let it get cold, and skim it
4. Thicken the essence until as thick as a rich mayonnaise sauce, strain it, and set it in the bain-marie
5. Melt two ounces of fat bacon in a sauce-pan
6. Throw in pepper, salt, and the meat of the fowl and work them together for five minutes over a bright fire
7. Empty the contents into a mortar and pound the meat and bacon to a paste
8. Add half their bulk of crumb of bread soaked in stock and half a tin of white mushrooms
9. Pass the whole through a hair sieve
10. Moisten the puree with some of the sauce while pounding it
11. When finished, add four whole raw eggs
12. Butter a plain mould and fill it with the puree, introducing a good allowance of truffles cut into dice
13. Steam it as you would a pudding
14. Let the mould get cold and turn it out
15. Reduce the remainder of the sauce to a white glaze and let it get cool
16. Pour the glaze gently over the mould until it coats it completely

title: Crepe de Poisson
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin pancake
- bacon
- mince
- egg
- cold sauce
- butter
- egg for brushing
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Picture to yourself a nicely-made thin pancake
2. spread it out upon a flat dish, and cut it into pieces two inches wide, and three inches long
3. Upon the surface of each piece, place a thin slice of bacon slightly smaller each way than the crepe
4. over the bacon put a tablespoonful of any nice mince, well worked with an egg or two, and a little cold sauce to give it moisture and cohesion
5. roll up your crepes, put them on a buttered tin
6. brush them with a whipped egg
7. bread-crumb them, and bake brown in the oven

title: Croquettes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold mashed potatoes
- chopped parsley
- shallot
- chopped thyme or marjoram
- spices
- fat

## Directions

1. Work the cold mashed potatoes very much as previously described
2. Flavour them with a little chopped parsley, a very little shallot, a little chopped thyme or marjoram, or spices if you like
3. Form them into rolls or tablets
4. Fry them a golden brown

title: Croquettes de Volaille aux Points d'Asperges (Chicken Croquettes with Asparagus Points)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice sized chicken or small fowl
- fowl meat
- cold boiled tongue
- truffles
- ounce of butter
- table-spoonful of flour
- stock
- salt
- pepper
