- powdered thyme
- two eggs
- juice of a lime
- mashed potato
- crispy fried curl of bacon
- tin of asparagus
- chicken broth
- almond
- cream
- anchovy vinegar

## Directions

1. Lightly roast a nice sized chicken or small fowl
2. Cut off the best meat and put it aside
3. Save all bones, skin and scraps and make a nice clear white broth with them
4. Take of the fowl meat two parts, of cold boiled tongue one part, and of truffles one part
5. Mince all very finely and mix them together
6. Melt an ounce of butter in a sauce-pan and stir into it a table-spoonful of flour
7. Moisten it with some stock and then add the mince
8. Flavour it with salt, pepper, and a little powdered thyme to taste
9. Stir it over the fire for three or four minutes
10. Take it off the fire and add two eggs beaten up with the juice of a lime and strained
11. Spread the mince out upon a large dish and let it get cold
12. Divide it into six or eight egg-shaped croquettes
13. Introduce in the centre of each a piece of truffle the size of a shilling
14. Bread-crumb them and fry them a very light gold colour
15. Prepare a circle of mashed potato and place it in the dish
16. Dispose the croquettes carefully round it
17. Between every croquette put a crispy fried curl of bacon
18. Place a slice of truffle upon each croquette
19. For the asparagus points, cut off the green ends of the stalks of a tin of asparagus
20. Heat them gently up in veloute made with the chicken broth, slightly flavoured with almond, and enriched with a good spoonful of cream
21. Give them a few drops of anchovy vinegar
22. Pour them into the middle of your potato circle

title: Croquettes of Duck à la Bordelaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- black pepper
- salt
- an onion
- one clove of garlic
- a table-spoonful of melted butter
- a breakfast-cupful of strong gravy
- the rind of a lime
- a dozen pepper corns
- a pinch of sugar
- water
- flour
- a tumblerful of sauterne
- minced bacon, lean and fat in equal shares
- the bird's liver
- truffle

## Directions

1. Roast the bird lightly and set it to cool, then cut off all the meat from the bones, saving every atom of skin, bone, liver, &c.
2. Put the meat away for awhile, giving it a good dusting with black pepper and salt
3. Mince an onion, and one clove of garlic and stir the mince into a table-spoonful of melted butter at the bottom of a small sauce-pan
4. Let it fry a pale brown, now throw in all your fragments of duck, having mashed the bones in a mortar, a breakfast-cupful of strong gravy, the rind of a lime; a dozen pepper corns, a pinch of sugar, and salt to taste
5. Add water enough to cover the bones well, and let the contents of the sauce-pan simmer, skimming off the scum that rises
6. In half an hour you will have a well-flavoured broth
7. When quite satisfied with the broth, strain it off, let it cool, skim it, and thicken it plainly with flour and butter
8. Place the sauce-pan in the bain-marie-pan, and let it keep hot there
9. Take a tumblerful of sauterne, put it into a small sauce-pan with a table-spoonful of minced onion, a salt-spoonful of black pepper, and a pinch of salt
10. Boil till the liquid has been reduced to half its original quantity
11. Strain it into the previously made sauce
12. Give the whole a simmer for ten minutes and serve

title: Croquettes of Fowl Truffed
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fair sized fowl
- buttered paper
- stock
- two onions
- a clove of garlic
- a carrot
- sweet herbs
- pepper
- salt
- half a glass of sherry
- butter
- flour
- the yolks of two eggs
- the juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Roast a fair sized fowl, protecting the breast from burning by a buttered paper
2. When partly done, carve the bird carefully, picking on all the white meat you can
3. Remove the skin from these pieces and put them aside
4. Break up the carcass, and throw it, with all fragments of skin and bone, and the giblets of the fowl previously saved, into a sauce-pan, with as much stock as will cover the whole
5. Add two onions, a clove of garlic, a carrot, sweet herbs, pepper, and salt, and simmer the contents of your pan as long as you can, finally adding half a glass of sherry, and bringing the broth to the boil
6. Strain it, remove all fat that may rise, and reduce the sauce a little
7. Thicken it with butter and flour, take it off the fire, and stir in the yolks of two eggs beaten up with the juice of a lime

title: Croustades de Bandecai
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pastry
- bandecai forcée
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Line four little patty pans with pastry as laid down for cheese fingers
2. Bake and cool them
3. When ready, fill each case with a spoonful of bandecai forcée as explained for bandecai toast
4. Heat thoroughly in the oven dusting a little grated cheese over the surface of each croustade
5. Serve on a napkin

title: Croustades de Lapin à la Reine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight stale dinner rolls
- butter
- small rabbit
- tongue ham or lean bacon
- sweet-breads
- cocks' combs
- sliced mushrooms
- grated lime peel
- truffle
- onion
- carrot
- milk of almonds

## Directions

1. Take eight stale dinner rolls, which have been lightly baked in cylindrical tins about two inches and a half in diameter.
2. Cut them so as to leave a case about an inch and a half deep.
3. Scoop out every atom of crumb, and trim the tops that you cut off neatly, so as to form a lid for the top of the hollowed rolls.
4. Fry the cases and their lids in butter till of a golden yellow, and set them on one side to dry.
5. Choose a small rabbit, some tongue ham or lean bacon, a few sweet-breads if you can get them, and have ready some cocks' combs, sliced mushrooms, a little grated lime peel, and a slice of truffle for the top of each croustade.
6. Remove the fillets from the rabbit's back and divide them into pieces, or cut them with a cutter about the size of a shilling.
7. Put the rest of the rabbit into a stew-pan with the usual vegetables to yield a good broth, and with it make a rich white sauce flavoured with some milk of almonds.
8. Butter a saute-pan, and cast into it your pieces of rabbit fillet with some slices of onion and carrot.

title: Croustades de Truffes à l'Espagnole
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- short-bread paste
- butter
- truffles
- rich Espagnole sauce
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Line as many little open tartlet pans as you have guests with some carefully made short-bread paste, using an oval shape not much larger than the bowl of a table spoon.
2. Butter them well first to keep them from burning.
3. Preserve their hollow centres while baking them by a piece of stale bread, to be removed afterwards.
4. Cut up some truffles in dice, using enough to yield a good dessert-spoonful per head.
5. Warm the minced truffles gently in some rich Espagnole sauce flavoured with a dash of Madeira, and keep it en bain-marie.
6. When wanted, fill the crisp croustades with a spoonful each of the truffles and sauce.
7. Dish them en serviette, and send them round with the bird.

title: Croute au Pot
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- stock from soup
- butter
- slices of carrot
- turnip
- onion
- pieces of celery
- pieces of cabbage
- tinned loaf

## Directions

1. Cut off the bottom crust of a tinned loaf, with the same thickness of crumb as of crust
2. Cut this out into squares half the size of a visiting card, or in rounds the size of a rupee
3. Soak them in some stock (from the soup) and put them in a buttered tin into the oven where they should remain until crisp and dry
4. Slices of carrot, turnip, onion, and pieces of celery, that have been cooked in the pot-au-feu should be added to the soup before serving
5. Add some pieces of cabbage boiled separately to this soup
6. The crusts being put in at the very last moment

title: Crème de Riz aux Haricots Verts
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- stock
- rice
- French beans
- butter
- flour
- milk
- egg yolk
- sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Make your stock for soup as usual
2. Boil a tea-cupful of rice till tender, work it through a sieve
3. Amalgamate the pulp so obtained with the soup gravy by butter and flour as described for purees
4. Cut into dice a handful of jugged French beans
5. Stir the dice into the soup as it thickens
6. Add a coffee-cupful of milk, with which the yolk of an egg has been mixed, off the fire, just before serving
7. The milk of a few pounded sweet almonds may be mixed with the rice pulp if the flavour be liked

title: Cucumber
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumber
- ground turmeric
- salt
- oil
- water

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into two halves a well developed cucumber.
2. Remove the seeds and cut into slices.
3. Smear with ground turmeric and salt.
4. Fry in oil.
5. Sprinkle water from time to time to make them soft.

title: Cucumber Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three heaped up table-spoonfuls cucumber strips
- a tea-spoonful of finely-minced onion
- one tea-spoonful of chopped green chilli
- one tea-spoonful of parsley
- a dessert-spoonful of vinegar
- sugar
- a dessert-spoonful of salad oil
- salt
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumber into thin strips an inch long; say three heaped up table-spoonfuls.
2. Mix with them a tea-spoonful of finely-minced onion, one of chopped green chilli, and one of parsley.
3. Moisten with a dessert-spoonful of vinegar in which a pinch of sugar has been dissolved, a dessert-spoonful of salad oil, and dust over it salt and black pepper at discretion.

title: Cucumbers a la Poulette
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good sized cucumber or 2 small ones
- plenty of boiling water
- half an ounce of butter
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- half a pint of poulette sauce

## Directions

1. Take a good sized cucumber or two small ones; cut them lengthwise into quarters, remove the seeds, and peel off the green skin
2. Cut them into pieces two inches long and one inch thick
3. Put them into a stew-pan with plenty of boiling water, half an ounce of butter, and a tea-spoonful of salt
4. Simmer them until three parts done
5. Drain the liquor off, and turn the pieces of cucumber out upon a clean dish
6. Cut each piece in half and cover them up
7. Make half a pint of poulette sauce, put the pieces of cucumber into it
8. Warm gently in the bain-marie, and serve

title: Curacao
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of water
- a pound of dark brown sugar-candy
- a hundred and twenty drops of oil of bitter orange
- a quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of water in a very clean pan
2. add to it, bit by bit, a pound of dark brown sugar-candy
3. when the latter is dissolved, increase the fire and let the syrup boil up
4. pour it into a deep dish to cool
5. dissolve a hundred and twenty drops of oil of bitter orange in a quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof
6. mix with the syrup when quite cold
7. filter and bottle the liqueur

title: Cure's Omelette with Tunny and Herring Roes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a slice of preserved tunny
- soft roes of two herrings a la sardine
- a little shallot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- butter
- six well mixed eggs
- a few drops of lime juice
- minced shallot and parsley

## Directions

1. Finely mince together half a slice of preserved tunny and the soft roes of two herrings a la sardine with a little shallot and a dessert-spoonful of parsley.
2. Toss the mixture in butter awhile.
3. Stir into a basin containing six well mixed eggs.
4. Cook the mixture as already explained.
5. Turn it out, when ready, into a hot dish containing a little melted butter, a few drops of lime juice and some minced shallot and parsley.

title: Curry Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of dhunnia, or coriander-seed, roasted
- one ounce of jeerah, or cumin-seed, roasted
- two ounces of huldee, or dry turmeric
- two ounces of lal mirritch, dry chilies
- two ounces of kala mirritch, black pepper, roasted
- two ounces of rai, or mustard-seed
- one ounce of soat, or dry ginger
- one ounce of lussan, or garlic
- four ounces of nimmuck, salt
- four ounces of cheenee, or sugar
- four ounces of chunna or gram dal without husk, and roasted
- English white wine vinegar
- good sweet oil or mustard oil

## Directions

1. Carefully pound and grind down the ingredients in the proportions given with the best English white wine vinegar to the consistency of a thick jelly.
2. Warm some good sweet oil, and while bubbling fry in it the mixture until it is reduced to a paste.
3. Let it cool, and then bottle it.
4. Great care must be taken not to use any water in the preparation, and mustard oil is better adapted than sweet oil for frying the mixture in.

title: Curry Powder
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lbs of turmeric
- 2 lbs of coriander-seed
- 1 lb of cummin-seed
- 2 lbs of poppy-seed
- 1 lb of fenugreek
- 1/2 lb of dry-ginger
- 1/2 lb of mustard-seed
- 1 lb of dried chillies
- black pepper corns
- salt, a quarter of a bottle

## Directions

1. Weigh everything most carefully, and even after the various ingredients have been cleaned, weigh them again, and also weigh the husks that have been removed.
2. Parch the coriander-seed and fenugreek very carefully, i.e., roasted like coffee berries, before being pounded.
3. Clean and dry each of the other ingredients separately.
4. Pound each ingredient separately and sift well.
5. Mix the whole of the powders, sprinkling in salt by degrees during the process.
6. Thoroughly cleanse and dry bottles in the sun.
7. Fill bottles and cork tightly down, with tops securely waxed over.
8. Some recommend that when the powder has been mixed, it should be browned in melted butter over the fire, then dried in the sun, and powdered again, in order to tone down the strong flavour of the cummin-seed.

title: Curry with Crabs and Pumpkin
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs of big size
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- oil
- pumpkin
- powdered pepper
- cumin
- mustard
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- panch-foron

## Directions

1. Shell a few crabs of big size and cut their fleshes in pieces
2. Thrash their edible legs
3. Rub them with powdered turmeric and salt and fry in oil
4. Peel and chop a very tender pumpkin into very fine pieces
5. Fry them with the brains of the crabs in some oil
6. Add salt and water will come out of the vegetable
7. When this water dries up add a little more water with powdered pepper, cumin and mustard and the fried crabs
8. After boiling for some time season the curry with some oil boiled with a few cassia leaves, red chillies and panch-foron
9. Take down when the curry is moderately thick

title: Cussoondee
body:
## Ingredients

- A hundred green mangoes
- half a pound each of ground chilies, ginger, and garlic
- half a pound of bruised mustard-seed
- two pounds of tamarinds without the stones
- salt
- warmed or cooked mustard oil

## Directions

1. Peel and slice fine a hundred green mangoes
2. Steep them in salt for twelve hours
3. Put them under a heavy pressure for two hours, and drain away all the water
4. Mix with them half a pound each of ground chilies, ginger, and garlic, half a pound of bruised mustard-seed, two pounds of tamarinds without the stones, and some salt
5. Pour over it as much warmed or cooked mustard oil as will entirely cover it
6. Cook it for ten to fifteen minutes over a brisk fire
7. When cold bottle it, taking care that it is kept several inches well under the oil, and that it is well corked

title: Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer milk
- one stick cinnamon
- sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- one tablespoonful rice flour
- rose water or orange-flower water or vanilla

## Directions

1. Take a seer of milk and a stick of cinnamon, and boil down to half the quantity
2. Add sugar to taste
3. Beat up quickly the yolks of four eggs, and add them gradually to the milk, stirring it continually
4. Thicken with a tablespoonful of rice flour
5. Take it off the fire, and flavour with rose water, orange-flower water, or vanilla

title: Custard Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of cream or milk
- six eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour
- half a small nutmeg grated, or an equal quantity of pounded cinnamon
- a tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix with a pint of cream or milk six well-beaten eggs, two tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour, half a small nutmeg grated, or an equal quantity of pounded cinnamon, a tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar, and a little salt
2. Put it into a cloth or buttered basin, that will exactly hold it
3. Boil it for half an hour
4. Serve with wine sauce

title: Cutlet of Hilsa Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big pieces of hilsa fish
- pasted ginger
- onions
- salt
- curd
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Rub some big pieces of hilsa fish with pasted ginger, onions and salt
2. Keep them merged in a sufficient quantity of curd for 3 or 4 hours
3. Dip them into eggs beaten with salt
4. Roll on bread crumbs
5. Fry till brown in ghee
6. Serve while hot

title: Cutlet of Other Fishes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- salt
- fish pieces of big-size
- water
- eggs
- turmeric
- bread crumbs
- ghee
- mustard

## Directions

1. Mix a little ginger-juice, onion-juice and salt with a few pieces of fish of big-size
2. Boil in water and remove the bones keeping the pieces intact
3. Again add ginger-juice, onion-juice and salt
4. Leave them for some time
5. Dip them into eggs beaten with salt and turmeric
6. Roll on bread crumbs
7. Fry in plenty of ghee
8. Serve with mustard while hot

title: Cutlet of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps of medium size
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Remove the scales except those at the ends of the tails
2. Remove the heads and the black threads at the backs of a few shrimps of medium size
3. Flatten them with a hammer and wash
4. Add ginger-juice, onion-juice, powdered turmeric and salt
5. Keep them covered for some time
6. Dip them holding the tail into eggs beaten with turmeric and salt
7. Roll on bread crumbs
8. Fry in plenty of ghee
9. Serve while hot

title: Cutlets à La Soubise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one neck of mutton
- pepper and salt
- one Bombay onion
- two handfuls of sorrel
- one lettuce
- two table-spoonfuls of butter
- half a pint of gravy slightly thickened with flour
- one tea-spoonful of white sugar
- three salt-spoonfuls of salt
- black pepper
- mashed potato
- water-cress

## Directions

1. Choose a first rate neck of mutton and divide it into the neatest cutlets you can
2. Give them a dusting of pepper and salt
3. Place them on marinade for the rest of the day
4. Take a light sauce-pan and melt the butter at the bottom of it
5. Throw into it the onion very finely shredded
6. Toss this about till it turns a pale yellow
7. Add the whole of the sorrel and the lettuce also finely cut up
8. Stir the vegetables about in the melted butter till they begin to change colour
9. Pour into the sauce-pan about half a pint of gravy slightly thickened with flour
10. Stir this well and put in a tea-spoonful of white sugar, three salt-spoonfuls of salt, and a good dusting of black pepper
11. Let the vegetables boil for about five minutes
12. If the sauce is too thick, dilute it with a little more gravy till it assumes the consistency of a rich puree
13. Ease the fire and let the sorrel simmer for half an hour
14. Drain the cutlets from their marinade, dip them in melted butter, and grill them over a bright clear fire
15. Prepare a circle of mashed potato, fill it with the puree burning hot
16. Arrange the cutlets round the outside of the circle with bunches of water-cress for garnish

title: Dai-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- moistened pulses of kalai
- salt
- ghee or oil
- ghee
- mustard seeds
- curd

## Directions

1. Grind some moistened pulses of kalai to a paste.
2. Add salt and beat thoroughly.
3. Prepare cakes of it and fry in ghee or oil till brown.
4. Boil a little ghee with a few mustard seeds and add some curd.
5. Stir and take down.
6. Keep the fried baras immersed in the curd for several hours, and then serve.

title: Dal Pittas
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary pie-crust
- dal chur churree
- ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare an ordinary pie-crust and the dal chur churree
2. Roll out the pastry, cut into circles of the size of saucers
3. Put into them a tablespoonful of the dal, and close them
4. Fry in ghee of a light brown colour
5. Serve hot

title: Dal with Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulses of grams and moog
- big sized shrimps and fish
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- oil
- moog-dal or chhola-dal
- water
- cumin 1½ tola per seer
- pepper 1 tola per seer
- turmeric 1 tola per seer
- salt 2 tolas per seer
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- ghee
- powdered garam-masala

## Directions

1. Prepare big sized shrimps and fishes of the higher classes
2. Keep the scales on the heads of shrimps and fry in oil
3. Use pieces of big fish and its head, entire or divided, preferably that of Rohit, Katla or Bhetki fish
4. Fry and break the head in small fragments
5. Smear salt and powdered turmeric on the pieces before frying
6. Wash fried moog-dal or chhola-dal and boil with water
7. Chhola-dal may be fried a little before boiling
8. Add cumin, pepper, turmeric and salt all powdered for every seer of raw pulse when they are half-boiled
9. Add the fried shrimps or pieces of fish or the broken head with the bones or both fish and head
10. Stir thoroughly
11. Fry a few cassia leaves and red chillies with some ghee and pour these into the boiling dal
12. Boil till the dal is moderately thick
13. Add ghee and powdered garam-masala, and stir before taking down

title: Dal-Bhaja
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- husked pulses of grams
- water
- salt
- powdered chillies
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Keep some husked pulses of grams under water for 2 or 3 hours.
2. Fry the moistened pulses with salt and powdered chillies with sufficient ghee or oil on gentle heat, stirring all the while, till they are brittle to eat.

title: Dalna of Shrimps and Cocoa-Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- flesh portions of shrimps
- kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut
- pasted onions
- powdered turmeric
- chillies
- salt
- besam or flour
- ghee
- big pieces of potatoes
- patois or cauliflowers
- oil
- water
- powdered cumin
- pepper
- coriander
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Take the flesh portions of some shrimps and the kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut in the proportion 2 to 1 respectively and grind them to a paste.
2. Add some pasted onions, a little powdered turmeric, chillies, salt and besam or flour in a quantity such that when beaten the mass forms a very thick paste.
3. Spread the mass on a flat dish making uniform thickness and cut it in squares.
4. Fry the pieces in ghee.
5. Brown some big pieces of potatoes and patois or cauliflowers with oil and add water with powdered cumin, pepper, coriander, turmeric and salt.
6. Add the fried pieces on boiling.
7. Season the curry when it is fully boiled with some ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves and red chillies.
8. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla before finish.

title: Dalna with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- ghee
- potatoes
- powdered pepper
- cumin
- coriander
- turmeric
- salt
- water
- chopped onions
- red chillies
- cassia leaves
- powdered garam masala

## Directions

1. Shell hard boiled eggs and brown entire or halved with ghee
2. Brown big pieces of potatoes
3. Add powdered pepper, cumin, coriander, turmeric and salt
4. Stir and add water
5. Add eggs when boiling
6. Fry chopped onions, red chillies and cassia leaves in ghee
7. Season curry with fried mixture
8. Boil for some time more
9. Add powdered garam masala before taking down

title: Dalpooree
body:
## Ingredients

- dal chur churree
- ordinary pie pastry
- ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare a dal chur churree
2. Put it into a marble mortar, and reduce it to a fine paste
3. Prepare an ordinary pie pastry
4. Take two pieces of the prepared dough, each of the size of a walnut
5. Shape them into two small bowls
6. Take as much of the dal paste as will make a ball of the size of a walnut
7. Put it into one of the bowls of dough, and cover it over with the other bowl
8. Roll out the whole very carefully to the size of a dinner-plate
9. Fry in ghee of a very light yellow colour
10. Serve hot

title: Dam with Karola
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Karola 1.5 seer
- pepper 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- coriander 1 tola
- salt 1 tola
- ginger 1/2 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- curd 0.5 powa
- sugar 0.5 powa
- ghee 0.5 powa
- ripe coconut
- cassia leaves
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Scrape out the kernel of the coconut, mix them with sugar, pasted ginger and garam-masalla and fry them with ghee
2. Remove the seeds from the tender karolas by cutting off one end of each
3. Fill the hollows with this stuff and close the ends with a little flour paste
4. Fry them in ghee
5. Boil some ghee in another pan with the cassia leaves and powdered pepper, cumin, coriander and turmeric
6. Add the curd, sugar and a little hot water
7. Add the karolas and salt when it boils
8. Take down when the soup is thick after adding powdered garam-masalla

title: Dam with Mocha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped mocha 0.5 seer
- ghee 1 chhatak
- curd 1 chhatak
- salt 1 tola
- coriander 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- ginger 1/4 tola
- poppy-seed 1/4 tola
- garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- sugar 1/4 tola
- pepper 1/2 tola
- turmeric 1/2 tola
- cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Keep the chopped mocha in water for a few hours
2. Bind them with a few cassia leaves in a clean piece of cloth and suspend the ball in a pan half full of water so that it does not touch the bottom
3. Cover the lid and boil for half an hour
4. Boil some ghee in a pan, add ground poppy-seed, ginger, coriander, pepper, cumin and last of all the boiled mocha in it
5. Stir for a few minutes
6. Add turmeric, salt, curd, sugar and a little water
7. Cover the lid and boil on mild heat
8. Boil some ghee and fry the powdered garam-masalla in it, and season the curry with this
9. Stir thoroughly and take down

title: Dam with Potato
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato 0.5 seer
- ghee 1 chhatak
- curd 1 chhatak
- almonds 2.5 tolas
- coriander 1 tola
- salt 1 tola
- pepper 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- sugar 1/4 tola
- garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Boil ghee in a pan and brown the pieces of potatoes in it
2. Add to them almonds, coriander, pepper, turmeric, salt and lemon-juice, all pasted together
3. Stir for a few minutes
4. Add curd and sugar and stir for two minutes more
5. Add water
6. Add powdered garam-masalla when the soup is thick
7. Take down

title: Damson Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- damsons that have been bottled for tarts
- loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Take damsons that have been bottled for tarts
2. Pass them through a sieve and reject the skins and stones
3. To every pound of the strained pulp add half a pound of loaf sugar broken small
4. Boil the whole until it has thickened
5. Pour it into buttered moulds
6. Put it in an oven or warm place to dry
7. When quite firm, remove it from the moulds and serve up

title: Darbesh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- besam
- middlings
- water
- saffron
- ghee
- sugar syrup
- raisins
- almonds

## Directions

1. Make a thin paste of equal quantities of besam and middlings with water
2. Take half of the solution in another pot and mix with saffron
3. Fry bonde of medium size separately with the two solutions
4. Take them out of the ghee before they are hard
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated
6. Mix the red and yellow bonde thoroughly and take them out
7. Mix with raisins, pieces of almonds and a little hot ghee
8. Make balls of it

title: Dedo-Monda
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of hard chhana
- 1/2 to 1 powa of khowa kheer
- 1 seer of sugar syrup
- 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose
- a little powdered cardamom seeds
- pieces of pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare sandesh with hard chhana 1 seer, khowa kheer 1/2 to 1 powa and a syrup of 1 seer of sugar
2. When it is prepared mix with 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose and a little powdered cardamom seeds
3. Add pieces of pistachios as desired
4. Form balls of it
5. Throw them with force on a flat plate
6. Join two together on the flattened sides while they are yet warm
7. They will stick to each other when cold

title: Delicious Curry Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- strong beef soup
- onions
- a chicken
- curry condiments
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- tamarind
- half a dozen kurreeah fool leaves

## Directions

1. Prepare a strong beef soup
2. Slice some onions, and cut up a chicken
3. Take curry condiments as directed above, omitting the coriander and cumin seed
4. Melt two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
5. Fry and set aside the sliced onions, then fry the condiments, add the cut-up chicken, and fry that also
6. In a part of the beef soup boil a spoonful of tamarind, so as to separate the stocks and stones
7. Strain and stir it into the fried chicken
8. After a while add the remainder of the beef soup, with half a dozen kurreeah fool leaves, and the fried onions
9. Close up the pot, and continue to simmer the whole until the chicken is quite tender
10. Serve up hot

title: Dentifrice
body:
## Ingredients

- whiting
- two ounces of camphor
- a few drops of brandy
- half an ounce of powdered myrrh

## Directions

1. Scrape as much whiting to a fine powder as will fill a pint pot
2. Moisten two ounces of camphor with a few drops of brandy
3. Rub it into a powder
4. Mix with the whiting half an ounce of powdered myrrh
5. Bottle it, and keep it well corked down, taking small quantities out in a separate bottle for daily use

title: Devil with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- ghee
- cassia leaves
- cloves
- cardamom seeds
- minced meat
- minced onions
- ginger juice
- powdered turmeric
- coriander
- garam masala
- salt
- sugar
- flour paste
- pasted mustard
- cucumber
- sliced onions
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Shell hard boiled eggs and remove yolks by cutting on one side
2. Boil ghee and add cassia leaves, cloves and cardamom seeds
3. When spice smell evolves add minced meat mixed with minced onions, ginger juice, powdered turmeric, coriander, garam masala and salt
4. Singe meat until water evaporates
5. Mix with sugar before taking down
6. Stuff hollows of boiled eggs with spiced meat
7. Close openings with flour paste
8. Brown eggs in ghee
9. Serve with pasted mustard, cucumber pieces, sliced onions and boiled potatoes

title: Dhakai Parota
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ghee
- salt
- 1 seer of fine flour
- 1/2 seer of water

## Directions

1. Prepare the mass as in Parota
2. Make only 4 balls of it
3. Press each ball to a round disc and fold it twice, smearing ghee at each fold
4. Repeat the process 10 or 12 times till the disc is very soft
5. Fry both sides one after the other with enough ghee on very mild heat till it is crisp and brown
6. Serve while hot

title: Dhonka of Shrimps and Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cocoa-nut and shrimps ball
- ghee or oil
- big pieces of potatoes
- minced onions
- ground turmeric
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- ginger
- salt
- water
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Prepare a ball of cocoa-nut and shrimps, cut it in small cubes and fry in ghee or oil.
2. Brown some big pieces of potatoes and minced onions.
3. Add ground turmeric, coriander, pepper, cumin, ginger, salt and just enough water to cover the potatoes.
4. On boiling add the fried pieces of cocoa-nut and shrimps.
5. Season the curry with ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves and red chillies.
6. Add powdered garam-masalla before taking down.

title: Dhonka with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- egg contents
- ghee or oil
- ripe cocoa nut kernel
- ground turmeric
- salt
- potatoes
- water
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- ginger
- onions
- cassia leaves
- minced onions
- red chillies
- powdered garam masala

## Directions

1. Smear interior surface of cup with ghee
2. Pour egg contents into it
3. Place cup on hot water covered with lid
4. Take down when hard boiled
5. Cut in small squares and fry in ghee or oil
6. Cut cocoa nut kernel in small pieces
7. Smear with ground turmeric and salt and fry
8. Brown pieces of potatoes
9. Add water with coriander, pepper, cumin, turmeric, ginger, onions and salt ground to paste
10. Add cocoa nut pieces and eggs when boiling
11. Fry cassia leaves, minced onions and red chillies in ghee and season curry with them
12. Add powdered garam masala before finish

title: Dhonka with Eggs and Bananas
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- egg contents
- green bananas
- potatoes
- ghee or oil
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- turmeric
- onions
- salt
- cassia leaves
- minced onions
- red chillies
- powdered garam masala

## Directions

1. Prepare egg baras with green bananas
2. Brown pieces of potatoes in ghee or oil
3. Cook as Dhonka with Eggs without ginger

title: Dilkhosh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 seers pure milk
- crystalline sugar
- cardamom seeds
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Boil 3 or 4 seers of pure milk
2. Make the heat very mild so that the milk may no longer boil
3. Fan the surface of the milk with a small hand fan when creams will form
4. Remove the creams constantly to the sides of the boiling pan with a thin stick till the formation of creams ceases
5. Remove the pan off fire
6. Transfer the creams on a plate
7. Beat thoroughly adding requisite quantities of crystalline sugar, cardamom seeds and essence of rose
8. Form balls of the paste and serve

title: Dolma of Patols
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- ginger-paste
- browned pieces of onions
- powdered garam-masalla
- pepper
- lemon-juice
- patols
- flour-paste
- eggs
- besam
- rice-flour
- cream of milk

## Directions

1. Scale some lobsters, wash, rub with salt and powdered turmeric, and fry
2. Make a paste of them on a curry-stone
3. Mix ginger-paste and again fry with some browned pieces of onions
4. Mix powdered garam-masalla, pepper and lemon-juice with them
5. Remove the soft skin off some patols by rubbing with a knife
6. Cut one of their ends and remove the seeds with a pin
7. Stuff them with the pasted spiced fish or meat
8. Close the ends with a little flour-paste or stitch them up
9. Dip them into eggs beaten with a little besam, rice-flour, cream of milk and salt
10. Fry to crispness in plenty of ghee

title: Dolma with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- salt
- ginger juice
- minced onions
- powdered turmeric
- red chillies
- pepper
- almonds
- raisins
- aniseeds
- cardamom seeds
- ghee
- sugar
- patols
- flour paste
- liquid beaten egg contents
- bread crumbs or powdered biscuits

## Directions

1. Shell hard boiled eggs and mince finely
2. Add salt, ginger juice, minced onions, powdered turmeric, red chillies, pepper, almonds, raisins, aniseeds and cardamom seeds
3. Mix thoroughly
4. Boil ghee with pinch of sugar and fry this mixture in it
5. Skin patols by rubbing against blunt edge of knife
6. Cut off one or both ends and remove seeds with pin
7. Fill hollows with prepared egg mixture
8. Close ends with flour paste
9. Dip into liquid beaten egg contents
10. Roll on bread crumbs or powdered biscuits
11. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Domestic Pasty
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of mutton
- cold-boiled pickled pork, about a pound
- gravy beef, about a pound
- chicken
- six shallots
- a dozen pepper corns
- peel of a lime
- two carrots
- bunch of parsley
- coarse stalk of celery with its leaves
- table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- bouquet of sweet herbs
- warm water
- anchovies, a couple
- hard-boiled eggs
- spiced pepper
- thyme and marjoram, about a tea-spoonful in all

## Directions

1. Cut as many slices as possible from the meat that remained untouched at the tail end of the saddle, trimming each slice free from burnt skin and laying upon a separate plate until about a pound and a half of slices are obtained
2. Cut off all remnants of good lean that still adhered to the bones and put them into a bowl
3. Break the bones and cast into a large stew-pan with every atom of skin, fat, gristle that can be found left in the dish after the trimming operation
4. Into the stew-pan with the mutton bones and scraps, throw six shallots, a dozen pepper corns, the peel of a lime, two carrots cut up, a bunch of parsley, a coarse stalk of celery with its leaves, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, a bouquet of sweet herbs, the pound of gravy beef cut up, and all the trimmings of the chicken - neck, legs, feet, pinions and giblets
5. Cover with warm water and simmer gently for about three hours until the ingredients produce a pint and a half of excellent broth
6. Strain off the broth and set to cool
7. Pound the lean remnants which had been saved in the bowl with a couple of anchovies in the mortar and pass through the sieve
8. When the broth is quite cold, skim off the fat and make a regular puree with it and the pounded mutton
9. Pack the pie-dish by first sprinkling finely minced parsley, then a double layer of sliced mutton, over that a layer of sliced lean pork, another of mutton, and so on alternately until there is just enough space left to accommodate the joints of the raw chicken
10. Neatly dispose the chicken joints on the top with little bits of lean pork dotted in between them
11. Garnish the surface with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters
12. Sprinkle a little spiced pepper over the layers of meat and some very finely chopped thyme and marjoram, about a tea-spoonful in all
13. Patiently pour the puree over everything, giving time for the liquid to settle in and amongst the contents of the pie-dish until the gravy comes within an inch of the top
14. Shake minced small parsley over the surface as a last touch
15. Make the paste and cover in the pasty
16. Bake the pie and at the end of baking, gently pour some of the puree which had been saved for the purpose into the pie through the vent in the centre of the crust
17. Place an ornamental flower cut in paste over the aperture
18. Glaze the crust

title: Dopenaji of Livers
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled livers
- spices

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as Meat Dopenaji.
2. Fry the pieces of livers for 10 minutes only.
3. Leave practically no soup.

title: Dressed Ox-Cheek
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-cheek head
- quart of good gravy
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- vinegar
- catsup or white wine
- forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Prepare it as directed for stewing
2. Cut the meat into square pieces
3. Make a sauce with a quart of good gravy, thickened with butter mixed with flour
4. Season with salt and pepper, a little cayenne, and a tablespoonful of vinegar
5. Put in the head, and simmer it till quite tender
6. A few minutes before serving add a little catsup or white wine
7. Forcemeat balls may be added

title: Dried Prawns
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- salt
- turmeric

## Directions

1. Strip the prawns of their shells
2. Keep them for a day in salt mixed with turmeric
3. String and put them out in the sun daily for fifteen or twenty days

title: Dry Fish Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice
- Arabian dried beckty
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Prepare onions, chilies, and lime-juice as before
2. Take a part of the Arabian dried beckty and well broil it
3. Remove all the bones and pound the fish to nearly a powder
4. Mix it thoroughly with a teaspoonful of mustard oil
5. Add the onions, chilies, and lime-juice

title: Dry Fruit Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- dry dates
- khobanee or Arabian apricots
- allohlokara
- English prunes
- Normandy dry pippins
- French vinegar
- good clean sugar
- finely-sliced ginger
- peppercorns
- sticks of cinnamon
- salt

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of dry dates, khobanee or Arabian apricots, allohlokara, English prunes, and Normandy dry pippins
2. Wash and clean them thoroughly, particularly the Arabian dry fruits
3. Dry them well in the sun
4. Stew the dry dates for ten to fifteen minutes
5. Cut them up into rings, and throw away the stones
6. Make a syrup of good French vinegar, in the proportion of a quarter of a pound of good clean sugar to a quart of French vinegar
7. Quarter the pippins
8. Arrange them and the other fruit in a wide-mouthed bottle in alternate layers, with finely-sliced ginger, peppercorns, sticks of cinnamon, and small sprinklings of salt
9. Pour over the whole as much of the vinegar syrup as will entirely cover the fruit
10. Cork the bottle well down
11. Expose it in the sun for a few days
12. It will be fit for use in a month

title: Duchess Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of fine stale crumbs
- half a pint of boiling milk
- four ounces more of crumbs
- four ounces of dry, finely minced suet
- pinch of salt
- three ounces of crushed ratafias
- three ounces of candied peel
- lemon essence
- five eggs
- four ounces of sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Grate four ounces of fine stale crumbs and put them in a basin
2. Pour over them half a pint of boiling milk
3. Cover the basin with a plate and let the liquid soak into the crumbs and get cold
4. Stir into the basin four ounces more of crumbs, four ounces of dry, finely minced suet, a pinch of salt, three ounces of crushed ratafias, three ounces of candied peel sliced thin, and a few drops of lemon essence
5. Whisk five eggs well with four ounces of sugar, continue to beat them together until the sugar is dissolved
6. Pour the eggs into the basin with the other ingredients and mix well
7. Turn all into a buttered basin and boil for two hours

title: Duck Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- half a teaspoonful of ground coriander
- half a teaspoonful of ground cumin seed

## Directions

1. Divide as you would a chicken
2. Cook the duck in the same manner as chicken doopiaja
3. Allow it to simmer a little longer than the chicken doopiaja
4. Mix half a teaspoonful each of ground coriander and cumin seed with the condiments

title: Duck Stuffing
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three Bombay onions the size of Badminton balls
- eight tender looking sage leaves
- five ounces of bread-crumbs
- spiced pepper
- salt
- one ounce of butter or suet
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Take three Bombay onions the size of Badminton balls, wash, peel, and boil them in two waters to extract the acrid flavour
2. Whilst these are boiling, take eight tender looking sage leaves, and scald them in boiling water for five minutes
3. Take them out, and when the onions are tender, turn them out and drain them dry
4. Mince them with the sage leaves, very fine
5. Add to this, five ounces of bread-crumbs
6. Dust over the mixture a liberal allowance of spiced pepper and salt
7. When nicely worked together, add an ounce of butter or suet cut into dice
8. Bind the ingredients with three eggs, it will now be ready for use

title: Duck Vindaloo
body:
## Ingredients

- a young, full-grown, but tender duck
- vinegar
- salt
- ground condiments

## Directions

1. Take a young, full-grown, but tender duck; cut it up as for a curry, and put it through the same course of pickling from eighteen to twenty-four hours before being cooked.

title: Duckling and Green Peas
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- duckling
- green peas
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Roast the duckling and do not stuff it
2. Let the basting be frequent and froth the breast up nicely to finish with
3. Serve accompanied by green peas and cook the potatoes according to any of the recipes given at page 136

title: Ducks with Green Olives
body:
## Ingredients

- young, full-grown, tender ducks
- good beef stock
- sliced onions
- bottle of olives stoned

## Directions

1. Choose young, full-grown, tender ducks
2. Feather and singe them as directed in recipe No. 129
3. Wash them in three or four cold waters
4. Stuff the ducks according to recipe No. 325
5. Bake in a deep dish in a moderate oven until brown
6. Add a good beef stock with sliced onions
7. Bake until the stock is reduced
8. Remove the ducks
9. Put into the pan the contents of a bottle of olives stoned
10. Allow to bake for ten or fifteen minutes to soften the olives

title: Ducks with Green Olives
body:
## Ingredients

- young, full-grown, tender ducks
- beef stock
- sliced onions
- bottle of olives stoned

## Directions

1. Choose young, full-grown, tender ducks; feather and singe them as directed in the foregoing recipe, after which wash them in three or four cold waters
2. Stuff the ducks according to recipe No. 325, and bake in a deep dish in a moderate oven until brown
3. Add a good beef stock with sliced onions, and bake until the stock is reduced
4. Remove the ducks, and put into the pan the contents of a bottle of olives stoned, and allow to bake for ten or fifteen minutes to soften the olives
5. Place the ducks on a clean dish, arrange the olives round the ducks, and pour the gravy over
6. Serve up hot

title: Dugdhakupika
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seers of milk
- 1 powa of sugar
- 1 seer of hard chhana
- 3 powas of fine flour
- milk
- plenty of ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Boil 2 seers of milk with 1 powa of sugar stirring constantly till it is a stiff paste
2. Scrape together this kheer from the pan and beat thoroughly
3. Make a paste of 1 seer of hard chhana and mix it thoroughly with 3 powas of fine flour
4. Make a stiff dough of them with milk
5. Prepare elongated cakes of it stuffed with the previously prepared kheer
6. Fry them in plenty of ghee
7. Drop them in a syrup of sugar
8. Serve when saturated with syrup

title: Dumpode Duck (Eastern Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good fat duck
- a teaspoonful each of mustard, sweet oil, and mixed sauce
- bones and giblet of the duck
- pepper
- salt
- soup herbs
- bay-leaves
- two pounds and a half of good beef
- the liver of the duck
- half a pound of beef suet
- a dessertspoonful of chopped garden herbs
- a tablespoonful of grated bread-crumbs
- half a teaspoonful of mixed hot spices pounded
- a teaspoonful each of black pepper and salt
- half a teaspoonful of essence of anchovies
- one chittack and a half or three ounces of ghee
- ready-boiled mixed vegetables
- hot West-Indian pickle

## Directions

1. Take a good fat duck; feather, clean, and bone it without hurting the skin.
2. Pour into it a mixture made up of a teaspoonful each of mustard, sweet oil, and mixed sauce.
3. Make a gravy of the bones and giblet, seasoning it with pepper, salt, soup herbs, and a few bay-leaves.
4. Mince together with the liver of the duck two pounds and a half of good beef, half a pound of beef suet, a dessertspoonful of chopped garden herbs, a tablespoonful of grated bread-crumbs, half a teaspoonful of mixed hot spices pounded, a teaspoonful each of black pepper and salt, and, for those who like it, half a teaspoonful of essence of anchovies.
5. Mix these well together, and stuff the duck.
6. Melt one chittack and a half or three ounces of ghee.
7. Put in the duck.
8. Pour over it the giblet gravy, and allow it to cook until tender.
9. Then glace the duck, as also some ready-boiled mixed vegetables, and serve up, surrounding the duck with the vegetables and some hot West-Indian pickle.

title: Dumpode Goose (Indian Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good fat tender goose
- a dessertspoonful each of mustard, sweet oil, and mixed sauces
- bones and giblet of the goose (liver excepted)
- pepper
- salt
- soup herbs
- bay-leaves
- three pounds of beef
- a quarter of a pound of beef suet
- a quarter of a pound of fat bacon
- the liver of the goose
- a tablespoonful of chopped garden herbs
- a dessertspoonful of powdered black pepper
- a dessertspoonful of mixed hot spices finely powdered
- two tablespoonfuls of finely-grated bread-crumbs
- a dessertspoonful of salt
- one teaspoonful of essence of anchovies
- two chittacks and a half or five ounces of ghee
- boiled turnips
- carrots
- onions
- potatoes
- English pickles

## Directions

1. Take a good fat tender goose; feather, clean, and bone it carefully without destroying the skin.
2. When every bone has been removed, pour into the goose a mixture composed of a dessertspoonful each of mustard, sweet oil, and mixed sauces.
3. Take all the bones and the giblet, the liver excepted, and make a good gravy seasoned with pepper, salt, soup herbs, and bay-leaves.
4. Mince very fine three pounds of beef, a quarter of a pound of beef suet, a quarter of a pound of fat bacon, and the liver of the goose.
5. Take of chopped garden herbs a tablespoonful, powdered black pepper a dessertspoonful, mixed hot spices finely powdered a dessertspoonful, finely-grated bread-crumbs two tablespoonfuls, salt a dessertspoonful, and essence of anchovies, if liked, one teaspoonful.
6. Mix the above well together, and stuff the goose.
7. Melt two chittacks and a half or five ounces of ghee.
8. Put in the goose, and pour over it the soup made of the bones and giblet, and allow it to stew until quite tender.
9. Then glace the goose, as also some boiled turnips, carrots, onions, and potatoes, and serve up hot, surrounded with the vegetables and some English pickles.

title: Dutch Sauce (Hollandaise)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- a little pepper
- a little salt
- the squeeze of a lemon

## Directions

1. Melt butter plainly in a sauce-pan, flavoured with a little pepper, a little salt, and the squeeze of a lemon.
2. Allow this to settle over the fire.
3. Pour free from the sediment at the bottom of the pan into a piping hot metal sauce-boat.

title: Eau de Cologne
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve drops of oil of neroli
- twelve drops of oil of citron
- twelve drops of oil of bergamot
- twelve drops of oil of orange
- twelve drops of oil of rosemary
- a drachm of cardamom-seeds
- a pint of spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Put twelve drops each of oil of neroli, citron, bergamot, orange, and rosemary, and a drachm of cardamom-seeds, into a pint of spirits of wine
2. Let it stand for a week

title: Egg Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
- a teaspoonful of very finely sifted flour
- tender leaves of parsley, finely chopped
- white pepper
- salt
- yolk of a raw egg

## Directions

1. Grind down to a powder or paste the yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
2. Add a teaspoonful of very finely sifted flour
3. Add some tender leaves of parsley, finely chopped
4. Add a little white pepper and salt
5. Grind and mix all well together with the yolk of a raw egg
6. Roll into small balls
7. Boil for two or three minutes

title: Egg Bara with Green Banana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green banana
- eggs
- minced onions
- besam
- salt
- ginger-juice
- powdered turmeric
- aniseed
- black-cumin
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Boil a developed green banana and peel it
2. Make a muddy paste with the boiled banana, the contents of 3 eggs, minced onions, a little besam, salt, ginger-juice, powdered turmeric, aniseed and black-cumin by beating them thoroughly
3. Form small cakes of it and fry to crispness in ghee or oil
4. Serve while hot

title: Egg Bara with Soup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- ghee
- powdered pepper
- cumin
- turmeric
- ginger
- salt
- water
- egg-baras
- minced onions
- red chillies
- cloves
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Brown some pieces of potatoes in ghee
2. Add powdered pepper, cumin, turmeric, ginger and salt with water
3. Add fried egg-baras on boiling
4. Season the curry with boiling ghee with minced onions, red chillies and cloves fried in it
5. Boil for some time more
6. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla, and take down with some soup

title: Egg Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, six or eight
- ghee
- ground condiments
- sliced fried onions
- water

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, shell, and cut into halves
2. Set them aside
3. Use ghee, ground condiments, and sliced fried onions in all respects the same as for a chicken doopiaja
4. Observe precisely the same method of cooking, keeping in mind that a smaller quantity of water and a shorter time to simmer will suffice, as the eggs are already cooked or boiled

title: Egg Curry with Chunna Ka Dal
body:
## Ingredients

- chunna ka dal, half a cupful
- eggs, hard-boiled

## Directions

1. Parboil the dal
2. Curry the dal first
3. When about nearly cooked, throw in the hard-boiled eggs
4. Finish the simmering immediately the dal is soft or tender

title: Egg Curry with Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, six or eight
- ghee
- ground condiments
- sliced fried onions
- water
- English green peas

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, shell, and cut into halves
2. Use ghee, ground condiments, and sliced fried onions in the same manner as recipe No. 71
3. Allow the curry to simmer until the peas are quite tender

title: Egg Cutlets
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- besam
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- ghee
- ginger juice
- onion juice
- powdered biscuit or suji

## Directions

1. Shell and slice hard boiled eggs into four pieces
2. Dip into thick solution of besam with powdered turmeric and salt
3. Fry in plenty of ghee until brown
4. Dip again into beaten egg contents with powdered turmeric, ginger juice and onion juice
5. Roll on powdered biscuit or suji
6. Fry as before

title: Egg Fry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- powdered or pasted turmeric
- salt
- flour or powdered rice

## Directions

1. Shell hard boiled eggs
2. Cut lengthwise and crosswise on both ends
3. Smear with powdered or pasted turmeric and salt
4. Dip into thick solution of flour or powdered rice mixed with salt
5. Fry in plenty of ghee
6. Eggs may also be halved and fried

title: Egg Fry with Brinjals
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender brinjals
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- egg contents
- middlings
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Cut tender brinjals in slices of medium thickness
2. Rub with salt and powdered turmeric
3. Leave for some time until soft
4. Dip into beaten egg contents with salt
5. Roll on middlings
6. Fry in plenty of ghee or oil

title: Egg Fry with Cauliflowers
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower flowers
- water
- salt
- ground turmeric
- egg contents
- powdered bread or biscuit
- ghee or oil
- poppy seeds
- minced onions
- green chillies

## Directions

1. Cut flowers in small pieces
2. Boil in water with salt and ground turmeric
3. Dip into beaten egg contents with salt and ground turmeric
4. Roll on powdered bread or biscuit
5. Fry in plenty of ghee or oil
6. Poppy seeds, minced onions and green chillies may be mixed with egg contents if desired

title: Egg Fry with Cocoa-nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cocoa nut kernel
- salt
- turmeric
- red chillies
- raisins
- almonds
- moistened pulses of grams
- beaten egg contents

## Directions

1. Scrape out kernel of ripe cocoa nut
2. Mix with salt, turmeric, red chillies, raisins, almonds and moistened pulses of grams all powdered or pasted
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Form cakes
5. Dip into beaten egg contents
6. Fry

title: Egg Fry with Flowers of Gourd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- gourd flowers
- salt
- egg contents
- minced onions
- ground turmeric
- besam or rice flour

## Directions

1. Remove stalks, placentas and sepals of flowers
2. Dip into beaten egg contents with salt, minced onions, ground turmeric and besam or rice flour
3. Fry
4. Serve while hot and crisp

title: Egg Fry with Mun Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender amrita mun leaves
- water
- besam
- salt
- powdered red chillies
- turmeric
- egg contents
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Cut tender amrita mun leaves in big squares
2. Boil in water
3. Dip into thick solution of besam beaten with salt, powdered red chillies and turmeric
4. Fry in ghee or oil
5. Dip again into beaten egg contents with salt and ground turmeric
6. Fry

title: Egg Fry with Patol
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- patols
- egg contents
- powdered turmeric
- ginger juice
- salt
- powdered bread or biscuits
- ghee
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Dip skinned patols into liquid egg contents beaten with powdered turmeric, ginger juice and salt
2. Roll on powdered bread or biscuits
3. Fry in ghee
4. Thin slices of peeled potatoes may be fried in the same way

title: Egg Poach
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- contents of an egg
- ghee or oil
- ground pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour the contents of an egg on hot ghee or oil in a pan so that the yolk falls entire and in the centre.
2. Trim the sides, if necessary, round the yolk.
3. Take it out instead of turning over when the lower side is browned.
4. Serve with ground pepper and salt.
5. Alternatively, drop the contents of an egg keeping the yolk entire on boiling water.
6. Tilt the pan to give it a plump appearance.
7. Take the egg out as soon as the white is set.
8. Serve with ground pepper and salt.

title: Egg Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice 1 seer
- eggs about 20 in number
- potato 1 seer
- pure cows' ghee ½ powa
- ordinary ghee 1½ powa
- salt 3 to 4 tolas
- one tablespoonful of milk
- saffron ½ tola
- cloves 1 tola
- cardamom ½ tola
- cinnamon ½ tola
- cassia leaves ½ tola
- turmeric ½ chhatak
- ginger ½ chhatak
- coriander seeds 2 tolas
- cumin seeds ½ tola
- pepper ½ tola
- pistachios ½ chhatak
- almonds 1 chhatak
- raisins 1 chhatak

## Directions

1. Pulverize half the cloves, cardamoms, turmerics and all the cinnamons, gingers, coriander seeds, cumin seeds and peppers, and boil them with a few cassia leaves and 1 tola of salt in about 4 seers of water.
2. Put the lid on and take down when the water is halved.
3. Strain off the coloured water with a strainer in another pot and throw away the refuse. This is akhni water and keep it covered.
4. Boil fully about 16 eggs, cut into halves, smear them with the rest of the turmeric and 1 tola of salt, both powdered, and brown them in ghee.
5. Add a small quantity of powdered peppers smeared on the boiled eggs, if desired.
6. Peel the potatoes, cut into thick circular disks and brown them also in a similar way.
7. Brown also the pistachios, almonds and raisins, peeled and cut into pieces where necessary.
8. Dissolve the saffron in one tablespoonful of milk and mix it thoroughly with the rice, washed and dried, with ½ powa of pure cow's ghee and the rest of the salt, till the rice are coloured.
9. Fry this rice with ghee for about 6 or 7 minutes.
10. Heat the rest of the ghee in a boiler and take half of it out.
11. Make the layers of the following in order on the ghee in the boiler: cassia leaves; rice; pistachios, almonds, raisins and the rest of the cloves and cardamoms, all of them mixed together; eggs; potatoes; and then again another series of layers in the same order.
12. Sprinkle the heated ghee which was taken out of the boiler on the uppermost layer.
13. Add the spiced water and see whether it stands at least 2 inches above the uppermost layer. If it does not, add sufficient water again.
14. Cover up the boiler and boil on mild heat.
15. When the water almost dries up, add the contents of 3 or 4 fresh eggs and stir.
16. Heat the mass for a few minutes more and take down.

title: Egg Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 eggs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil three or four eggs about a quarter of an hour
2. Put them into cold water, take off the shells
3. Cut three of the whites and four yolks in small pieces
4. Mix them with melted butter, and heat it well

title: Egg Snow
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of six eggs
- three ounces of sugar
- one pint and a half of milk

## Directions

1. Beat up the whites of six eggs to a stiff froth with three ounces of sugar
2. Have a pint and a half of milk previously sweetened in a sauce-pan on the fire
3. When it boils, drop your egg-froth in separate table-spoonfuls upon its surface
4. A few seconds will cook each spoonful of froth on one side, then turn it over, and when cooked on the other side, serve

title: Egg with Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of meat
- salt
- curd
- turmeric
- pepper
- cumin
- coriander
- red chillies
- garam masala
- ginger
- onions
- ghee
- egg contents
- powdered biscuit

## Directions

1. Wash big pieces of meat
2. Mix with salt, curd, turmeric, pepper, cumin, coriander, red chillies, garam masala, ginger and onions all ground to paste
3. Boil ghee and add meat
4. Allow to simmer until water evaporates
5. Beat egg contents with salt, powdered turmeric, pasted onion and ginger juice
6. Dip each piece of meat into egg mixture
7. Roll on powdered biscuit
8. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Egg-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- fried and powdered garam-masalla
- cumin
- onions
- ginger
- besam
- ghee

## Directions

1. Take the contents of a few fresh eggs in a pot
2. Add fried and powdered garam-masalla and cumin, finely chopped onions, minced ginger and a little besam, and beat thoroughly
3. Form small thin cakes of it and fry in boiling ghee
4. Take them out with a spoon when they are brown

title: Egg-Chop
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- egg contents
- minced onions
- besam
- ginger juice
- salt
- powdered pepper
- red chillies
- beaten egg
- powdered biscuits or bread crumbs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes, peel and rub to paste
2. Mix with equal quantity of egg contents
3. Add minced onions, besam, ginger juice, salt, powdered pepper and red chillies
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Form cakes
6. Dip into beaten egg
7. Roll on powdered biscuits or bread crumbs
8. Fry in plenty of boiling ghee

title: Enchor
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender green jack-fruits
- water
- oil
- potatoes
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- turmeric
- red chillies
- salt
- bori
- ghee
- cassia leaves
- panch-foron
- milk
- sugar
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Rub the hands with a little oil so that the gum may not stick
2. Peel the skin thickly off one or two tender green jack-fruits and cut in small pieces throwing away the central stem
3. Boil them with water
4. Boil some oil and brown pieces of peeled potatoes in it
5. Add the boiled enchor and stir for a minute
6. Add water with powdered coriander, pepper, cumin, turmeric, red chillies and salt in adequate quantities
7. Add browned bori when it boils and put the lid on
8. Season the curry with ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves, red chillies and panch-foron
9. Add a little milk and sugar may be added
10. Take down when a little soup remains after adding a little more ghee and powdered garam-masalla

title: Endive
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- endive leaves
- salt
- consomme
- rich béchamel
- butter
- cooked chicken

## Directions

1. Trim endives by picking off the outside leaves, and cutting off the green tips
2. Sever the heads by a cut across the stalk, detaching all the leaves therefrom
3. Examine and clean every leaf
4. Drain leaves dry
5. Cast them into a roomy vessel full of boiling water with half an ounce of salt
6. Boil for twenty-five minutes
7. When tender, drain and press out the water
8. Chop fine on a clean board
9. Simmer the endive leaves in consomme for two hours
10. Increase the fire, stirring till the leaves have absorbed the broth
11. Moisten them with some rich béchamel and a pat of butter
12. Use as foundation for a blanquette à la Talleyrand with neat pieces of cooked chicken heated up in the endive sauce

title: English Curry with Crabs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- salt
- onions
- butter
- ginger-juice
- lemon-juice
- powdered pepper
- water
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Shell a few crabs and keep their fleshes aside for a while after mixing with salt
2. Keep the brains in a pot and thoroughly clean the shells
3. Cut the fleshes in small pieces and fry them in butter with slices of onions, ginger-juice, lemon-juice, powdered pepper, salt and the brains
4. Add a little water and take down when boiled
5. Stuff the shells with this mass, bread crumbs and butter
6. Apply heat under the shells for some time

title: English Curry with Rohit Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish 1/2 seer
- 4 eggs
- molten butter 1 chhatak
- milk 1 powa
- bread crumbs 1/2 chhatak
- fine flour 1/2 chhatak
- powdered pepper 2 tolas
- mace 2 tolas
- salt 1 tola

## Directions

1. A piece of any big fish will do.
2. Cut it in small pieces and mix half the butter with them.
3. Then mix eggs, bread crumbs, pepper and salt.
4. Boil with so much water that no water is left after half an hour.
5. On another stove boil the milk with flour.
6. Take down when the milk is somewhat thick.
7. Add to it butter, mace and the boiled fishes, and stir to mix.

title: Epigrammes de Mouton aux Epinards
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- white wine
- water
- onion
- tomatoes
- carrots
- celery
- whole pepper
- salt
- garlic
- parsley
- dried thyme
- egg
- stale bread-crumbs
- fat
- spinach
- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- flour
- caramel colouring
- sherry
- red currant jelly
- cornichons or gherkins

## Directions

1. Braise a breast of mutton in a stew-pan with some white wine and water, an onion, two tomatoes, carrots, celery, whole pepper, salt, a clove of garlic, some parsley, and a tea-spoonful of dried thyme
2. When sufficiently done to enable you to remove the bones, draw the pan from the fire and take out the breast
3. Pick out the bones and place it flat on a dish with a heavy weight upon it
4. Strain the broth and vegetables in which the meat was cooked, putting the vegetables aside and setting the gravy to cool
5. When the breast has become thoroughly cold, remove the weight and divide the meat into eight nice collops
6. Brush them over with egg and bread-crumb them with some very finely sifted stale bread-crumbs crisped in the oven
7. Let them stand for an hour and repeat the process, recrumbing them again
8. Fry them in boiling fat a nice golden yellow, take them out and drain them dry
9. Arrange them round a ring of spinach garnished with hard-boiled eggs neatly cut up and fried parsley
10. For the sauce: skim every atom of fat from the gravy, put a lump of butter in a sauce-pan and work a little flour into it when melted
11. Gradually add your gravy and stir well and let the sauce thicken
12. Add some caramel colouring, a dessert-spoonful of sherry, and a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
13. Put this into a hot sauce boat and serve
14. A few dice of sliced cornichons or minced mushrooms may be mingled with the sauce

title: Espagnole
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of sheep's trotters chopped in pieces
- a ham or bacon bone, or a few lean slices of either
- any raw cutlet trimmings
- two pounds of beef gravy meat cut into squares
- a couple of onions
- an ounce of butter
- a breakfast-cupful of broth, or water
- water
- two carrots sliced
- a turnip
- a good piece of celery
- a clove of garlic
- half a dozen pepper corns
- a spoonful of dried sweet herbs
- a bunch of parsley
- burnt sugar colouring
- salt to taste
- two ounces of butter
- two ounces of flour

## Directions

1. Cut up a couple of onions and throw them into a stew-pan with an ounce of butter, fry them a golden yellow
2. Add a breakfast-cupful of broth, or water, and the pieces of meat previously prepared
3. Shake the pan every now and then, and let the meat take colour
4. Add water enough to cover the meat completely
5. Reduce the fire, and let the contents of the pan come slowly to the boil, skimming carefully during that period
6. When the surface seems nicely clear of grease and scum, add a cupful of cold water and two carrots sliced, a turnip, a good piece of celery, a clove of garlic, half a dozen pepper corns, a spoonful of dried sweet herbs tied up in a bag, a bunch of parsley, some burnt sugar colouring, and salt to taste
7. As soon as the vegetables have been cooked, remove the pan from the fire and pick out the vegetables
8. Place it so that it may simmer slowly for a couple of hours
9. Lift it up, and strain off your gravy
10. Take a sauce-pan and melt two ounces of butter at the bottom of it
11. Stir in two ounces of flour and make a roux
12. When the colour satisfies you, add by degrees, stirring as you do so, the pint or so of strong gravy that you strained from the stew-pan
13. Let the contents of your sauce-pan come to the boil, stirring the whole time
14. Strain the sauce through your tin strainer into a clean sauce-pan
15. Set the vessel in the bain-marie to remain hot till wanted

title: Essence of Chilies
body:
## Ingredients

- best dried Patna chilies
- salt
- vinegar
- muslin

## Directions

1. Pick one chittack or two ounces of the best dried Patna chilies
2. Expose them to a light sun for an hour
3. Pound them to as fine a powder as possible
4. Put the powder into a stoppered bottle with a teaspoonful of salt
5. Pour over it as much vinegar only as will form a limp paste
6. Expose it to the sun for a few days
7. Pass it through muslin, adding to it as much more vinegar as will reduce it to the consistency of some thick sauce

title: Essence of Lemon-peel
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of lemon-peel, cut very thin and without any particle of the white skin
- eight ounces of spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Steep six ounces of lemon-peel, cut very thin and without any particle of the white skin, in eight ounces of spirits of wine well corked

title: Essence of Roses
body:
## Ingredients

- two drachms of otto of roses
- a pint of rectified spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Mix two drachms of otto of roses and a pint of rectified spirits of wine

title: Essence of Truffles
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- the contents of a small bottle of truffles
- a pint of clear chicken broth
- a glass of Madeira
- some sweet herbs
- pepper
- salt
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take the contents of a small bottle of truffles, and boil them for twenty minutes briskly in a pint of clear chicken broth, flavoured with a glass of Madeira, some sweet herbs, pepper, salt, and a little nutmeg.
2. Remove the sauce-pan from the fire, and when the liquid is cold, pick out the truffles (they will do for any other dish), and strain the cold essence.

title: European Fry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big pieces of fish
- minced or ground ginger
- onion
- turmeric
- salt
- eggs
- powdered turmeric
- bread crumbs
- ghee or butter

## Directions

1. Smear big pieces of fish with minced or ground ginger, onion, turmeric and salt
2. Dip them into egg-contents beaten with powdered turmeric and salt
3. Roll on bread crumbs and fry in hot ghee or butter

title: Every-Day Salad Mixture
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- very-hard boiled yolks of two eggs
- tea-spoonful of powdered mustard
- scant salt-spoonful of salt
- pinch of sugar
- tea-spoonful of minced shallot
- salad oil
- raw yolks of three eggs
- dessert-spoonful of tarragon or other aromatic vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the very-hard boiled yolks of two eggs into a slop basin, with a tea-spoonful of powdered mustard, a scant salt-spoonful of salt, a pinch of sugar, and a tea-spoonful of minced shallot.
2. Bruise these with a wooden or silver spoon, and work them to a paste with a little salad oil.
3. Add oil by degrees till your paste is about the consistency of batter, then toss into it one by one the raw yolks of three eggs, continue the working, and add oil, till you have a nice rich sauce coating the spoon pretty thickly.
4. Dole out a dessert-spoonful of tarragon or other aromatic vinegar, and mix it thoroughly with the other ingredients.
5. Taste your sauce by dipping a leaf of lettuce into a spoonful of it, and finish it off, as regards further addition of oil or vinegar, according to discretion.
6. Strain it through the little block tin strainer to get rid of every lump, and the little bits of onion.
7. Put the sauce-boat into the ice box as soon as it is filled.

title: Excellent Cheesecakes, known at Richmond as Maids of Honour
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of curd free of the whey
- six ounces of butter
- four yolks of eggs
- sugar and nutmeg to the taste
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients well
2. Line patty-pans with puff paste
3. Fill them with the mixture
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Excellent Fish Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- two anchovies
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- half a teaspoonful of flour
- yolks of three eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of tarragon vinegar
- parsley
- green onions
- a bay-leaf
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash and bone two anchovies
2. Rub them up in a mortar with a quarter of a pound of butter and half a teaspoonful of flour
3. Put these into a small saucepan
4. Add to the yolks of three eggs well beaten up
5. Add two tablespoonfuls of tarragon vinegar
6. Add a small bunch of sweet herbs, consisting of parsley, green onions, and a bay-leaf
7. Add a little salt, pepper, and nutmeg
8. Stir these over the fire until the sauce is thick, being careful not to let it boil
9. Serve it up in a sauce-tureen

title: Fig Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely grated breadcrumbs
- minced dried figs
- sugar
- chopped suet
- grated nutmeg
- Yeatman's baking powder
- flour
- eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of a pound of finely grated breadcrumbs, half a pound of minced dried figs, three ounces of sugar, six ounces of chopped suet, and a little grated nutmeg
2. Mix a teaspoonful of Yeatman's baking powder with an ounce of flour
3. Add this to the other ingredients with four eggs well beaten
4. Moisten with a little milk sufficiently to form a firm paste
5. Turn the mixture into a buttered mould and boil for three hours

title: Filet De Boeuf A L'Italienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender fillet of beef
- bacon
- mushroom
- parsley
- shallot
- pepper
- thyme
- marjoram
- lime peel
- garlic
- sweet onion
- carrot
- butter
- tomato puree
- beef gravy
- glazed onions
- haricots verts
- Brussels sprouts
- maccaroni
- cheese

## Directions

1. Take a tender fillet of beef and preserve it whole after trimming it into shape
2. Make an incision in it lengthways
3. Insert a long strip of bacon, fat and lean in equal parts, previously rolled in a fines herbes mince composed of a table-spoonful each of finely chopped mushroom and parsley, a dessert-spoonful of minced shallot, and some pepper
4. Tie up the fillet carefully with tape
5. Take a good slice of bacon, mince it very small with thyme, marjoram, lime peel, a clove of garlic, half a sweet onion and a carrot
6. Shake this mixture in a little butter at the bottom of a stew-pan
7. When it melts, place the fillet upon it and turn it gently till it browns nicely
8. Pour in a pint of tomato puree diluted with beef gravy to the consistency of ordinary pea-soup
9. Simmer your fillet in this till it is done, it ought to be kept at least a couple of hours at a gentle heat
10. When ready to serve, strain off the sauce
11. Place the fillet upon a very hot dish and remove the tape
12. Garnish with glazed onions, haricots verts, Brussels sprouts, or any nice vegetable
13. Pour a little of the sauce over it
14. Have ready some hot boiled and drained maccaroni in a sauce-pan
15. Empty all the sauce that remains amongst the maccaroni
16. Shake into it a table-spoonful of finely grated cheese
17. Toss the whole over the fire for a minute and dish it separately in a very hot dish
18. Serve the two together immediately

title: Fillets of Pomfret aux Fines Herbes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pomfret
- onion
- carrot
- celery
- parsley
- salt
- beaten egg
- minced parsley
- cooked onion
- minced mushroom
- white pepper
- chablis or sauterne
- butter
- flour
- sugar
- egg yolk
- anchovy vinegar
- minced marjoram
- minced garden cress

## Directions

1. Divide a good sized pomfret into eight nice fillets
2. Put the bones and trimmings with an onion, a carrot, a bit of celery, sprig of parsley and a pinch of salt into some water and boil them
3. Spread your fillets on a board and brush them over on one side only with a beaten egg
4. Shake over them a teaspoonful of finely minced parsley, the same of cooked onion chopped fine, a dessert-spoonful of minced mushroom, and a little white pepper
5. Roll up your fillets and pin them into shape with a small skewer
6. Strain off your liquor from the bones and trimmings and give it a tablespoonful of chablis or sauterne
7. Set your fillets in it to simmer gently till done (about twelve or fourteen minutes)
8. Take them out and draw out the skewers
9. Set them on a very hot dish and cover them up
10. Melt a lump of butter quickly in a sauce-pan and work a spoonful of flour into it
11. Throw in a little salt with a pinch of sugar and moisten with as much of the fish broth as will make a nice white sauce to cover your fillets
12. Add the yolk of an egg, a tea-spoonful of anchovy vinegar, a tablespoonful of very finely chopped parsley, a tea-spoonful of minced marjoram, and a tea-spoonful of minced garden cress
13. Pour it over your fillets and serve

title: Fillets with Anchovy Cream
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fillets cut from the undercut of a sirloin
- bacon for larding
- six anchovies from the tin
- half a pint of good veloute
- good cream

## Directions

1. Lard the fillets with bacon and marinate all day
2. Broil the fillets over a bright fire
3. Serve them round a chaplet of mashed potato, in the centre of which you may place a puree of spinach, or a bunch of water-cress
4. Take six anchovies from the tin, wipe them dry, free them from the oil, and pass them through the sieve
5. Add the pulp to about half a pint of good veloute
6. Heat it up, and as you serve, enrich the sauce with a ladleful of good cream
7. Serve the sauce round very hot, in a boat

title: Financière Ragout
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice tender fowl
- an onion
- a bit of celery
- a spoonful of dried herbs
- a sliced carrot
- two or three pepper corns
- equal portions of mushrooms and truffles
- melted butter
- a glass of sherry
- a pint of Espagnole
- whole button mushrooms
- sliced truffles
- cocks-combs
- sweet-breads
- tongue
- grated ham
- bacon
