## Directions

1. Choose a nice tender fowl, lightly roast it, and save the choicest fillet meat for your ragout
2. Take the legs, thighs, skin, bones, liver, giblets, scraps, and trimmings, and break up the bones
3. Make the strongest broth you can with the remnants, flavoured with an onion, a bit of celery, a spoonful of dried herbs, a sliced carrot, and two or three pepper corns
4. Reduce it as strong as possible, and then strain
5. Take equal portions of mushrooms and of truffles; cut them up, and toss them in melted butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
6. Work them well for two or three minutes, then add your fowl-essence, with a glass of sherry
7. Complete the sauce with your pint of Espagnole
8. Garnish the ragout with whole button mushrooms, sliced truffles, cocks-combs, sweet-breads, tongue, grated ham, and sippets of crisply fried bacon
9. Carefully stew the fowl fillets in the sauce
10. Gently heat up the fillets in the sauce

title: Fine Icing for Tarts and Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of refined loaf sugar
- White of an egg

## Directions

1. Pound and sift four ounces of refined loaf sugar
2. Beat up the white of an egg, and by degrees add to it the sugar till it looks white and is thick
3. When the tarts are baked, lay the icing over the top with a brush or feather, and then return them to the oven to harden, but take care that they do not become brown

title: Fines Herbes Vinegar
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of tarragon vinegar
- one table-spoonful of minced garden-cress
- one table-spoonful of minced marjoram
- one clove of garlic
- two small green capsicums
- one minced shallot

## Directions

1. To half a pint of tarragon vinegar, add a table-spoonful of minced garden-cress, a table-spoonful of minced marjoram, a clove of garlic, two small green capsicums shred, and one minced shallot
2. Cork your bottle down tightly, seal it with wax, and set it in the sun
3. In a week or two, strain the liquid and take it into use

title: Fish Cofta Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled or fried fish
- salt
- pepper
- herbs
- bread-crumbs
- egg
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Use cold boiled or fried fish which is best adapted for making coftas
2. Follow the instructions as already given at length in the foregoing recipes
3. Use mustard oil which is best adapted for fresh-fish curries

title: Fish Cooked Au Bleu
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- red wine
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. The preparation is exactly like court bouillon, red wine being substituted for white.

title: Fish Curry with Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut
- hot water
- pieces of fish
- oil
- pieces of onions
- pieces of garlic
- red chillies
- ghee
- milk of cocoa-nut
- pasted almonds
- vinegar
- pasted ginger
- pepper
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut, add hot water and strain out the vegetable milk.
2. Fry pieces of fish in oil.
3. Then fry some pieces of onions, a few pieces of garlic and red chillies with ghee, and add the milk of cocoa-nut with fried pieces of fish and pasted almonds.
4. Add vinegar, pasted ginger, pepper and salt.
5. Take down when the soup is thick.
6. Alternatively, mix the pieces of fish with powdered turmeric, egg and bread crumbs, fry in ghee and boil with milk of cocoa-nut.
7. Add salt, pasted ginger and pepper and make the soup thick.

title: Fish Jhal
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- powdered chillies
- cumin
- pepper
- coriander
- turmeric
- salt
- fried pieces of onions

## Directions

1. Slightly brown pieces of any kind of fish and add water covering them, with powdered chillies, cumin, pepper, coriander and turmeric.
2. Add a little more salt and fried pieces of onions if desired.
3. The soup must be thick and scanty.

title: Fish Kulia
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (1 seer, weighing between 3 to 8 seers)
- potato (1/2 seer)
- onions (1/2 powa)
- oil (3 chhataks)
- ghee (1/2 powa)
- curd (1/2 powa)
- salt (2 tolas)
- ginger (1 1/2 chhatak)
- turmeric (1 1/2 tola)
- cumin (2 tolas)
- pepper (1 tola)
- coriander (1/2 tola)
- garam-masalla (1/2 tola)
- chillies
- cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Make a paste of all the spices including onions except half the turmeric and garam-masalla which are to be pasted separately
2. Smear the pasted turmeric and half the salt with the pieces of fish
3. Cut the potatoes in big pieces and brown them separately in oil
4. Boil half the ghee and add the pasted spices, stirring constantly
5. Add curd when the spices turn reddish brown and stir again, adding water little by little when necessary (about 15 minutes)
6. Add the potatoes and stir for a couple of minutes
7. Add sufficient water and when it boils add fish and salt
8. Season the curry when it is boiled with ghee boiled with cassia leaves
9. Add garam-masalla before taking down
10. Keep a little fairly thick soup

title: Fish Mooloo
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- cocoanut
- hot water
- green chilies
- onions
- garlic
- ghee
- vinegar
- ginger
- whole pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry the fish and let it cool
2. Scrape a cocoanut and put a teacupful of hot water into it
3. Rub it well and strain it and put aside
4. Then put two spoonfuls more of water and strain this also
5. Cut up three or four green chilies and as many onions as you like, with half a garlic
6. Fry them with a little ghee
7. Whilst frying put the last straining of the cocoanut water in with the ingredients till it is dry
8. Then add the first water of the nut
9. Pour the whole over the fish, with some vinegar, ginger, whole pepper, and salt to your taste

title: Fish Paturi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- thick pieces of a fish weighing 1 or 2 seers
- powdered turmeric
- mustard
- salt
- enough chillies
- mustard oil
- banana leaf

## Directions

1. Rub some thick pieces of a fish weighing 1 or 2 seers with powdered turmeric, mustard, salt, enough chillies and mustard oil
2. Arrange them in one layer on a piece of banana leaf smeared with mustard oil
3. Cover with another banana leaf and bind with a thread
4. Heat it in a frying pan on charcoal fire
5. Turn it over when one side is cooked
6. Use plenty of oil

title: Fish Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish 3 lbs
- Rice 1 lb
- Ghee 0.5 lb
- Ginger 0.5 lb
- Cassia leaves 0.5 tola
- Pepper 1 tola
- A few red peppers
- Coriander seeds 0.5 lb
- Garam-masala 1 tola
- Salt 4 tolas
- Water 4 lbs

## Directions

1. Take off the scales and intestines of a fresh fish and cut it into big pieces
2. It will be the best if only the middle portion of a fish weighing 6 or 7 lbs be taken
3. Wash the pieces of fish thoroughly with clean water
4. Wash the ginger, coriander seeds and peppers and make a paste of the ginger
5. Tie the paste of ginger and the entire corianders and peppers loosely in a clean piece of cloth
6. Drop the pieces of fish and the bundle of spices along with the salt in 4 lbs of boiling water and put the lid on
7. After about 15 minutes take out the pieces of fish excepting the head which is to be taken out when properly boiled
8. Take down the pot when the juice is only 2 lbs
9. If you like you may brown the pieces of fish in ghee or oil without boiling them but the head whether fried or not must be boiled
10. Heat about 0.5 lb of ghee in a boiling pot and put the cassia leaves and the garam-masala, all entire, and a few red peppers broken in two, in it
11. When the smell of the spices evolves, put the rice, washed and dried, in it and stir
12. After 6 or 7 minutes when the rice are properly fried add the akhni water (the juice of the fish and spices) and cover with the lid
13. The boiled peppers may be pasted and added with the akhni water
14. Apply gentle heat and take down when the rice are fully boiled
15. Make a hole in the boiled rice, put a few pieces of fish in it and cover it up with rice again
16. Do this till all the pieces of fish are buried in the rice
17. Lastly add the rest of the ghee after heating it
18. Keep the polao hot for about an hour before serving

title: Fish Preserved
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish pieces
- Turmeric powdered
- Salt
- Molten butter
- Garam-masalla powdered
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the pieces of fish
2. Wipe them with a clean piece of linen
3. Smear with powdered turmeric and salt
4. Fry in molten butter and remove the bones as far as possible
5. Mix molten butter, salt, powdered garam-masalla and pepper to a paste
6. Mix it with the pieces of fish
7. Keep air tight in a dry and cool cellar for long preservation

title: Fish Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of cooked fish
- savoury custard

## Directions

1. Steam pieces of cooked fish in a savoury custard
2. Turn out like a pudding
3. Serve with any nice fish sauce

title: Fish Rolled in Pancakes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice fish
- equal amount of prawns
- chopped mushrooms
- rich velouté
- couple of eggs
- flour for pancakes
- melted butter
- eggs for coating
- bread-crumbs
- spiced pepper
- Dutch sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the fish and prawns
2. When cold, make a nice salpicon, or coarse mince of them, with some chopped mushrooms
3. Mix the mince in a sauce-pan with some rich velouté, and bind it with a couple of eggs
4. Let it get cold again, and divide it into six nice portions
5. Make a large pancake, or two small ones, and when not quite done, take them from the pan
6. Spread them on a flat dish, and from them cut six pieces about four inches square
7. Place your salpicon portions in the centre of each, and wrap them up neatly
8. Set the folded pancakes on a well-buttered flat silver dish
9. Egg them, bread-crumb them, pour some melted butter over them
10. Bake until a nice golden brown
11. Serve in the same dish, laid upon a napkin

title: Fish Soup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- oil
- vegetables (peeled and cut in big pieces)
- brinjals
- water
- coriander
- cumin
- pepper
- turmeric
- chillies
- onions
- salt
- fried bori
- cassia leaves
- panch-foron

## Directions

1. Prepare fish and fry in oil
2. Brown all the vegetables, peeled and cut in big pieces, except brinjals
3. Add water with coriander, cumin, pepper, turmeric, chillies and onions, all pasted together
4. On boiling add salt, brinjals and fried fishes
5. Add fried bori if desired
6. When the vegetables are boiled season the soup with boiling oil with a few cassia leaves, chillies and a pinch of panch-foron browned in it
7. Keep the soup thin

title: Fish Soup with Milk of Cocoa-nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cocoa-nut
- water
- fish
- oil
- flour solution
- cassia leaves
- salt
- ghee
- cardamom seeds
- ginger
- chillies
- cloves

## Directions

1. Scrape out the kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut and make a paste of it
2. Add sufficient boiling water and churn
3. Strain out the milk and throw away the refuse
4. Fry the pieces of fish in oil
5. Add the vegetable milk, a little solution of flour and cassia leaves
6. Add salt when the fishes are boiled
7. Season the soup with boiling ghee with cardamom seeds, minced ginger, chillies and cloves fried in it

title: Fish Sweets
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish, half seer
- potato, one and a half powa
- milk, one chhatak
- butter, one chhatak
- 3 or 4 hen's eggs
- flour, about one powa
- salt, one tola
- syrup of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of a big fish and remove the bones.
2. Add boiled and pasted potatoes, then all the other ingredients and lastly flour little by little while beating the mass thoroughly.
3. Form small balls and fry in ghee.
4. Serve while hot or prepare as above with only half the amount of salt and keep the fried balls immersed in a syrup of sugar till they are saturated, then serve.

title: Fish with Cream of Milk
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- big fish
- water
- 1 or 1-1/2 powa of water
- 1/2 chhatak of butter
- soda
- 1/2 powa or more of cream of milk
- flour
- salt
- powdered pepper
- egg milk

## Directions

1. Smear butter on both sides of a big piece of paper
2. Wrap the middle portion of a big fish, after washing it, with the paper and fix it with pins
3. Boil it for about an hour on mild heat in a covered pan with 1 or 1-1/2 powa of water mixed with 1/2 chhatak of butter
4. Take care that the paper does not get scorched
5. Drop a pinch of soda in a cup containing 1/2 powa or more of cream of milk and heat it on boiling water
6. Add to it a pinch of flour, salt, a little butter and powdered pepper
7. Take off the paper and pour this cream of milk on the fish

title: Fish with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- salt 1 tola
- a table-spoonful of oil
- powdered mustard
- pepper 1 tola
- a pinch of sugar
- vinegar 1 chhatak
- yolks of a few boiled eggs
- 0.5 seer of boiled fish
- beaten eggs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Mix salt, a table-spoonful of oil, powdered mustard and pepper, a pinch of sugar, vinegar with the yolks of a few boiled eggs
2. Mix it with 0.5 seer of boiled fish after removing the bones
3. Form rectangular blocks of it 0.5 inch thick
4. Dip them into beaten eggs
5. Fry in ghee

title: Fish with Flattened Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish 3 powas
- flattened rice 1 powa
- oil 1/2 powa
- ghee 1/2 powa
- curd 1/8 powa
- onions 1/8 powa
- ginger 1/8 chhatak
- salt 2 tolas
- turmeric 1 tola
- a few chillies
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Rui fish is the best for this purpose.
2. Wash thoroughly the pieces of fish and the flattened rice, mix powdered turmeric and salt with both, and fry the fish with oil and the flattened rice with half the ghee.
3. Again boil the remaining ghee, add ginger, onions and chillies, all pasted, and stir till deep brown.
4. Add curd and stir a few minutes more.
5. Pour in water and on boiling add the fish and the flattened rice.
6. Dry up the soup.
7. Add a little powdered garam-masalla if desired.
8. Try to keep the ingredients entire.

title: Fish-Bread
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled pieces of fish
- boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- cumin
- ginger
- garam-masala
- butter
- fried onions

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from boiled pieces of fish
2. Mince an equal quantity of boiled potatoes
3. Mix salt, pepper, cumin, ginger and garam-masala, all powdered, with both of them separately
4. Knead the fish and make thin circular discs of it
5. Arrange the discs of fish and potatoes in alternate layers in a baking pan with some butter in it
6. Bake on very mild heat with the lid on
7. Add fried onions to add zest to their taste

title: Fish-Cake
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of fish
- salt
- powdered pepper
- minced onions
- boiled and pasted potatoes
- eggs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of fish and remove the bones
2. Add salt, powdered pepper, minced onions, boiled and pasted potatoes equal in quantity with the fish, and eggs
3. Mix them to a thick paste
4. Spread it on a plane with a uniform thickness of 0.5 inch
5. Cut it in small squares
6. Fry with ghee

title: Fish-Fuluri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of boiled fish
- boiled and minced potatoes
- salt
- powdered almonds
- pepper
- butter
- eggs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from pieces of boiled fish and mix with a third of its quantity of boiled and minced potatoes.
2. Add adequate quantities of salt, powdered almonds, pepper.
3. Add butter and eggs.
4. Beat thoroughly.
5. Form cakes, roll on bread crumbs and fry with butter.
6. Serve with lemon-juice.

title: Fish-Puri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of the pieces of a big fish
- salt
- turmeric
- ginger
- turmeric
- cumin
- pepper
- coriander
- garam-masala
- a pinch of sugar
- ghee
- 0.5 powa of ghee
- 2 seers of flour
- water

## Directions

1. Boil 1 seer of the pieces of a big fish with salt and turmeric
2. Remove the bones
3. Add ginger, turmeric, cumin, pepper, coriander, garam-masala, salt and a pinch of sugar, all powdered
4. Beat thoroughly and brown a little with ghee
5. Mix 0.5 powa of ghee with 2 seers of flour
6. Add water and knead it to a moderately soft lump
7. Form small balls of it
8. Flatten them a little and stuff with a little of the spiced fish
9. Press these stuffed balls into thick round discs
10. Fry in plenty of ghee
11. Serve while hot

title: Flowers of Sweet Gourd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- flowers of sweet gourd
- pulse-meal or rice-flour
- salt
- black-cumin
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks and sepals of the flowers.
2. Dip them in a thick solution of pulse-meal or rice-flour mixed with a little salt and some black-cumin.
3. Fry them one by one or a few at a time in excess of oil.

title: Fonds d'Artichauts à la Bargoule
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six artichoke bottoms
- salt
- pepper
- four ounces of finely minced bacon
- a quarter ounce of butter
- a quarter ounce of flour
- a coffee-cupful of broth
- one tablespoonful of very finely minced parsley
- one tablespoonful of finely minced mushrooms
- one dessert-spoonful of finely chopped white onion
- a very thin slice of bacon
- a breakfast-cupful of good gravy

## Directions

1. Scoop out the chokes and drain six artichoke bottoms of a fair size
2. Give them a dust of salt and pepper and put them on a clean dish
3. Prepare the farce by combining four ounces of finely minced bacon, a quarter ounce of butter and the same weight of flour, a coffee-cupful of broth, one tablespoonful of very finely minced parsley, one tablespoonful of finely minced mushrooms, and one dessert-spoonful of finely chopped white onion
4. Stir the mixture over the fire for five minutes
5. Fill the hollows of the artichoke bottoms with the farce
6. Tie a very thin slice of bacon over each fond
7. Put them in a stew-pan with a breakfast-cupful of good gravy
8. Put the stew-pan into the oven and bake for twenty minutes
9. Ascertain if tender, then dish up and serve

title: Fonds d'Artichauts à la Moelle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichoke bottoms
- beef marrow
- coquilles or pastry cases
- Espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the fonds till tender in blanc
2. Prepare beef marrow by cutting it into pieces and blanching them in scalding water
3. Take as many coquilles as you have fonds
4. Put a little of the marrow into each shell
5. Place a fond over that marrow
6. Fill the cavity of the fond with marrow
7. Heat the coquilles hot
8. Just before serving, pour over each a little Espagnole sauce

title: Fonds d'Artichauts à la Provençale
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichoke bottoms
- salad oil
- six cloves of garlic
- pepper
- salt
- lime

## Directions

1. Prepare the artichokes as usual without the farce
2. Place them in a pie-dish with enough salad oil to keep them moist and safe from burning
3. Add six cloves of garlic, pepper, and salt
4. Put the dish into the oven and bake until the fonds are tender
5. Remove the garlic
6. Give them a dust of pepper and the squeeze of a lime
7. Dish up and serve

title: Fondue or Cheese Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of butter
- one table-spoonful of flour
- one breakfast-cupful of hot milk
- three ounces of grated Parmesan cheese
- one clove of garlic
- one tea-spoonful of mustard powder
- grated nutmeg
- black pepper
- salt
- yolks of four eggs
- one table-spoonful of milk
- whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Melt an ounce of butter in an enamelled sauce-pan
2. Stir into it a table-spoonful of flour
3. When the two are well amalgamated, put in a breakfast-cupful of hot milk, and about three ounces of grated Parmesan cheese
4. Stir the mixture on a very slow fire till it assumes the appearance of thick cream, being careful that it does not become too hot or boil
5. Put in one clove of garlic, a tea-spoonful of mustard powder, a dash of grated nutmeg, and some black pepper
6. Mix thoroughly, and if upon tasting you find that it is required, add a little salt
7. Keep on stirring the mixture at a very moderate heat for quite ten minutes
8. Remove the garlic, take the sauce-pan from the fire, stirring occasionally till the contents are nearly cold
9. Add the yolks of four eggs previously beaten up with a table-spoonful of milk, and well strained
10. Mix well
11. Incorporate swiftly with the mixture the whites of five eggs beaten to a froth
12. Pour this into a deep round tin, and put it into the oven which must not be too hot
13. Bake for twenty to thirty minutes until the fondue is ready for the table
14. Send it quickly to the table in its tin, with a napkin folded round it

title: Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- Three ounces beef suet
- one ounce fat bacon
- three ounces raw or dressed veal
- two ounces grated bread
- grated lemon-peel
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- finely-minced parsley
- beaten yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Mince very finely the beef suet, fat bacon, raw or dressed veal, grated bread, lemon-peel, nutmeg, pepper, salt, and finely-minced parsley
2. Mix all well together
3. Bind with the beaten yolks of eggs
4. Make it into croquets or balls, the size of large nutmegs
5. Fry them in ghee or clarified beef dripping, or use it for stuffing

title: Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of beef suet
- one ounce of fat bacon
- three ounces of raw or dressed veal
- two ounces of green bread
- a little grated lemon-peel
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- finely-minced parsley
- beaten yolks of eggs
- ghee or clarified beef dripping

## Directions

1. Mince very finely the following ingredients: three ounces of beef suet, one of fat bacon, three of raw or dressed veal, two of green bread, a little grated lemon-peel, nutmeg, pepper, salt, and finely-minced parsley
2. mix all well together, and bind with the beaten yolks of eggs
3. make it into croquets or balls, the size of large nutmegs, and fry them in ghee or clarified beef dripping, or use it for stuffing

title: Forcemeat Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- pounded veal or mutton
- minced beef suet or fat of veal
- grated bread-crumbs
- fat bacon
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- beaten yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Take an equal quantity of meat, suet, and grated bread-crumbs
2. Add a bit of fat bacon chopped
3. Season with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
4. Mix all well together with the beaten yolk of an egg

title: Forcemeat Balls Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of beef or veal
- half a pound of udder
- finely-grated crumbs of a slice of stale bread
- a tablespoonful of finely-chopped parsley
- milk or gravy
- a teaspoonful of finely-pounded pepper
- a teaspoonful of salt
- whites and yolks of two raw eggs

## Directions

1. Mince and pound beef or veal and udder to a pulp in a mortar
2. Remove all gristle and parts not pulped
3. Mix with it the finely-grated crumbs of a slice of stale bread and a tablespoonful of finely-chopped parsley
4. Soften down the whole with some milk or gravy
5. Add a teaspoonful of finely-pounded pepper and a teaspoonful of salt
6. Rub down the whole well together
7. Add the whites and yolks of two raw eggs, well beaten up
8. Make into balls, the size of small nutmegs for soup, or the size of large walnuts or ordinary croquets or rissoles for garnishing made dishes

title: Forcemeat for Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of a large beckty, hilsa, or any sort of white fish
- minced suet
- grated bread
- chopped oysters
- boiled parsley chopped
- pounded onion
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon-peel
- well-beaten yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Pick from the bones the meat of a large beckty, hilsa, or any sort of white fish
2. Mince it finely
3. Add the same proportions of minced suet and grated bread
4. Add a few chopped oysters and some boiled parsley chopped
5. Season with a little pounded onion, cayenne pepper, salt, nutmeg, and lemon-peel
6. Mix all well together
7. Bind it with the well-beaten yolks of eggs
8. Roll it into small balls
9. Fry them

title: Forcemeat Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five large onions
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. Peel four or five large onions
2. Scoop out the inside
3. Fill them with forcemeat
4. Roast them in an oven
5. Serve with roast turkey or fowl

title: Fowl a la Cardinal, or Dummode Capon or Fowl
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- sweet oil
- mustard
- sauces
- liver
- one pound and a half of good beef
- one pound and a half of beef suet
- garden herbs
- bread-crumbs
- powdered mixed hot spices
- finely-powdered black pepper
- salt
- half a dozen oysters
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Feather the bird, clean it, and remove every bone very carefully without injuring the skin.
2. Make a good strong broth or gravy of the bone and giblet, reserving the liver.
3. Pour into the bird a mixture of sweet oil, mustard, and sauces in the proportion of one teaspoonful of each.
4. Mince the liver together with one pound and a half of good beef, one pound and a half of beef suet, a dessertspoonful each of finely-chopped garden herbs and finely-grated bread-crumbs, a teaspoonful each of powdered mixed hot spices, finely-powdered black pepper, and salt, if liked, and half a dozen oysters.
5. Mix all well together, and stuff the bird.
6. Melt two chittacks or four ounces of ghee, add to it the giblet gravy, cook and glace the bird in it, as also some vegetables, and serve up hot, adding a little cayenne pepper to the gravy to make it piquant.

title: Fowl Cutlet
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- salt
- powdered pepper
- pasted onions
- yolks of eggs
- powdered biscuits
- ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare the fowl and cut it in 5 or 6 slices
2. Remove the bones as far as possible and thrash both sides of the slices with a stone
3. Mix them with salt, powdered pepper, pasted onions and the yolks of eggs
4. Roll on powdered biscuits and fry in plenty of ghee

title: Fowl Doopiaja Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- fowl doopiaja or chicken cofta curry

## Directions

1. Prepare the shell of the bread the same way as the prawn loaf
2. Stuff the shell with either a fowl doopiaja or with the chicken cofta curry
3. Remove all the bones of the fowl before the bread is stuffed with the curry
4. Bake and serve hot

title: Fowl Dopenaji
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl 0.5 seer
- ghee 0.5 powa
- onions 1 powa
- pieces of garlic
- salt 1 tola
- cassia leaves
- turmeric 0.5 tola
- garam-masalla 0.5 tola
- pepper 0.5 tola
- cumin 0.5 tola
- coriander 0.5 tola
- water 0.5 seer

## Directions

1. Cut the fowl in big pieces and mix with salt.
2. Make a paste of all the spices together and mix with 0.5 seer of water.
3. Cut the onions in slices, fry half of them with a little salt and keep aside.
4. Boil ghee and fry a few cloves, cassia leaves and garlic in it, add the other half of onions and brown them slightly.
5. Add the fowl and stir constantly for about 20 minutes adding the spiced water little by little if necessary.
6. Add the whole of the spiced water and cover the pan.
7. Take down when no soup is left.
8. Place the fried onions on the fowl.

title: Fowl Juice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water 1.5 seer
- salt
- pieces of onions
- powdered turmeric
- pepper
- powdered arrowroot
- cassia leaves
- cloves
- aniseeds
- ghee

## Directions

1. Kill and prepare a chicken and cut in 4 or 5 pieces.
2. Boil the pieces in about 1.5 seer of water with salt, a few pieces of onions, a little powdered turmeric and pepper under cover.
3. If the juice is to be made thick add a little powdered arrowroot.
4. Take down when the soup is only 1.5 powa and strain it out.
5. Fry a few cassia leaves, cloves and aniseeds in a little ghee, and add the soup.
6. Take down when it boils.
7. Strain the soup out through a clean piece of linen.
8. This soup is given to patients on medical advice.
9. The fowl may be utilised in preparing chops and cutlets.

title: Fowl Kabab
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- onion paste
- ginger paste
- chilli paste
- coriander
- cumin
- garam-masala
- curd
- ghee
- salt
- minced meat
- powdered pepper
- almonds
- boiled potatoes
- white of boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a fowl and thrust the neck, legs, etc. into the body to make it a single lump of flesh.
2. Make a paste of onions, ginger, chillies, coriander, cumin and garam-masala, and mix with it curd, ghee and salt.
3. Smear the lump with the pasted spices and keep aside for half an hour.
4. Stuff its bowels with these spices or with minced meat fried with salt, powdered pepper, pieces of almonds, boiled potatoes and the white of boiled eggs.
5. Pass a thin iron rod through the lump lengthwise and roast all the sides on charcoal fire dropping ghee at intervals till deep brown.

title: Fowl Roast
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- curd
- salt
- onion paste
- ginger paste
- chilli paste
- coriander
- cumin
- garam-masala
- minced meat
- powdered pepper
- almonds
- boiled potatoes
- white of boiled eggs
- ghee
- onion pieces
- cassia leaves
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare a fowl, domestic or wild, and thrust the neck, legs, etc. into the body.
2. Smear it with curd, salt and a paste of onions, ginger, chillies, coriander, cumin and garam-masala.
3. Its bowels may be stuffed, if desired, with these spices or with minced meat fried with salt, powdered pepper, pieces of almonds, boiled potatoes and the white of boiled eggs.
4. Boil some ghee, fry a few pieces of onions and cassia leaves in it and then the fowl till deep brown.
5. Add a little hot water and cover up.
6. Take down when the meat is soft and the soup is very thick.

title: French Beans
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- French bean pods
- soda
- salt
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Gather the pods young
2. Peel all round to get rid of the delicate fibre
3. Nip off the ends
4. Plunge into boiling water with a pinch of soda, at least a tea-spoonful of salt and one of sugar mixed in

title: French Beans
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- French beans
- butter
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Trim the French beans as explained
2. Put them into a jar or steamer with a table-spoonful of butter, a dessert-spoonful of sugar, and a large tea-spoonful of salt
3. Steam the jar as described for peas
4. When done, drain the beans and serve with a pat of butter boiling hot

title: French Beans a la Creme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked French beans
- boiling cream

## Directions

1. Pour a coffee-cupful of boiling cream over the beans

title: French Beans a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked French beans
- maitre d'hotel butter

## Directions

1. Stir a table-spoonful of maitre d'hotel butter into them when piping hot

title: French Beans Au Lard
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat bacon
- shallot
- boiled French beans

## Directions

1. Mince some fat bacon with a little shallot
2. Work them awhile in the sauce-pan
3. Add the boiled French beans
4. Toss them about for a minute or two and serve

title: French Beans Au Sauce au Fromage
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked French beans
- flour
- butter
- water from cooking beans
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- egg yolks
- milk or cream
- mild grated cheese

## Directions

1. Make a sauce blanche with flour and melted butter, some of the water in which the beans were cooked, salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg
2. Enrich it with the yolks of two eggs and a little milk or cream
3. Dredge into it a table-spoonful of mild grated cheese
4. Pour it over the beans piping hot

title: French Beans Au Sauce Blanche
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked French beans
- flour
- butter
- water from cooking beans
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- raw egg yolk or cream
- lime-juice

## Directions

1. Make a sauce blanche with flour and melted butter, some of the water in which the beans were cooked, salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg
2. Stir into this the yolk of a raw egg or a spoonful of cream
3. Give it two or three drops of lime-juice
4. Add over the beans

title: French Beans Aux Fines Herbes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked French beans
- butter
- parsley
- common garden cress
- shallot
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Turn the cooked beans out into a hot dish
2. Melt a lump of butter in a little sauce-pan with some finely-chopped parsley, some common garden cress, some finely-minced shallot if approved, some pepper, and a pinch of grated nutmeg
3. Pour over the beans and serve

title: French Blinis
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet
- minced anchovies
- olives
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Lard the fillet carefully, and tie it up into a convenient shape for roasting
2. Roast it over a clear fire, and when sufficiently done, serve it with some minced anchovies and olives, tossed in melted butter, poured over it at the last moment

title: French Cutlet
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled and peeled potatoes
- powdered pepper
- white of eggs
- salt
- eggs
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- powdered turmeric
- chillies
- pieces of almonds
- raisins
- minced meat
- fried and powdered khowa kheer
- curd
- sugar
- lemon-juice
- powders of garam-masalla
- powders of cumin
- ghee
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make a paste of some big boiled and peeled potatoes and mix thoroughly with powdered pepper, the white of eggs and salt
2. Mix eggs, ginger-juice, onion-juice, powdered turmeric, chillies, pieces of almonds and raisins with minced meat, and keep aside for an hour
3. Fry the spiced meat with ghee, form flat oval cakes of it
4. Dip into beaten eggs and fry in plenty of ghee
5. Give a thick coating of the pasted potatoes over these chops, dip again into beaten eggs, roll on bread crumbs and fry in plenty of ghee

title: French Melted Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of fresh butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- salt
- 1/2 gill of water
- 1/2 spoonful of white vinegar
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix in a stewpan, with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, a tablespoonful of flour, a little salt, half a gill of water, half a spoonful of white vinegar, and a little grated nutmeg
2. Put it on the fire, stir it, and let it thicken, but do not allow it to boil, lest it should taste of the flour

title: French Mutton Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen chops cut from a breast of mutton
- juice of onions
- ginger
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- boiling ghee or lard

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen chops cut from a breast of mutton, throwing away the intermediate bones— that is to say, allow the meat of two chops to remain on one bone
2. Wash, dry, and steep the chops for an hour or two before dinner in the juice of onions, ginger, and garlic— say four teaspoonfuls of the first to three of the second and two of the last
3. Mix on a large board pepper, salt, and flour, with which dredge the chops thoroughly, and fry quickly in boiling ghee or lard, taking care in turning over and removing the chops not to use a fork or anything likely to occasion any wound to the chops, which should be held by the bones with a pair of pincers
4. Serve up hot immediately they have become of a good rich brown colour

title: French Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- seven eggs
- four tablespoonfuls of pounded loaf sugar
- four tablespoonfuls of flour
- one pint of cream or milk
- grated peel and juice of one lemon
- two tablespoonfuls of rose-water

## Directions

1. Beat separately the yolks and whites of seven eggs
2. Beat with the yolks four tablespoonfuls of pounded loaf sugar, the same quantity of flour, one pint of cream or milk, the grated peel and juice of one lemon, and two tablespoonfuls of rose-water
3. Add the beaten whites the last thing
4. Allow three tablespoonfuls to each pancake

title: French Rolls
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of flour
- a dessert-spoonful of butter
- one small egg
- two tea-spoonfuls of Yeatman's powder
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- four table-spoonfuls of milk

## Directions

1. Work the butter thoroughly into the flour
2. Beat the egg up briskly with the milk, and strain it into another cup
3. Dust the salt over the flour
4. Gradually add the eggy-milk till the dough is formed
5. Mix the baking powder into it thoroughly
6. Form the dough into two nice oblong rolls
7. Place them on a sheet of well buttered paper, on the baking tin
8. Set them in the oven
9. Look at them after twenty minutes' baking
10. Take them out as soon as their colour indicates that they are done

title: French Savoury Pie-Crust
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of flour
- half a pound of clarified suet
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- a quarter of a pint of water
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Empty a pound of flour into a bowl, and rub lightly into it half a pound of clarified suet
2. add a tea-spoonful of salt, and complete the dough by adding to it by degrees a quarter of a pint of water in which two eggs have been beaten
3. Roll the paste out, give it two or three turns, and finish it as in the foregoing receipt

title: Fresh Tomato Gravy Sauce for Made Dishes
body:
## Ingredients

- forty tomatoes
- soup herbs
- salt
- stock
- a dessertspoonful of arrowroot or corn or other flour
- a teaspoonful of good English vinegar

## Directions

1. Take forty tomatoes (halved)
2. Add some soup herbs and salt
3. Boil them in a little stock
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Replace on the fire
6. Thicken with the addition, more or less, of a dessertspoonful of arrowroot or corn or other flour, to obtain any required consistency
7. Finally add a teaspoonful of good English vinegar
8. If a sharper gravy sauce be desired, instead of the vinegar add either a dessertspoonful of tapp sauce

title: Fresh Tomato Gravy Sauce for Made Dishes
body:
## Ingredients

- forty tomatoes, halved
- some soup herbs
- salt
- stock
- a dessertspoonful of arrowroot or corn or other flour
- a teaspoonful of good English vinegar

## Directions

1. Take forty tomatoes (halved), some soup herbs, and salt; boil them in a little stock
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Replace on the fire, and thicken with the addition, more or less, of a dessertspoonful of arrowroot or corn or other flour, to obtain any required consistency
4. Finally add a teaspoonful of good English vinegar; if a sharper gravy sauce be desired, instead of the vinegar add either a dessertspoonful of tapp sauce or a teaspoonful of chili vinegar

title: Fricandeau of Beef
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two undercuts of the sirloin
- fat bacon
- trimmings of the meat
- two ounces of carrot
- two ounces of onion
- salt
- sugar
- pepper
- half a pint of good gravy
- a pint more gravy

## Directions

1. Get two undercuts of the sirloin, if one be too small, trim them into a neat shape, and attach them together by two good skewers.
2. Lard them freely with fat bacon. If you cannot lard, having no needle, you must introduce a slice of bacon into each fillet by making therein a longitudinal incision; slip into it your slice of bacon, and pin the lips of the incised meat together with a small skewer.
3. Put the trimmings of the meat, two ounces of carrot sliced, two ounces of onion sliced, a pinch of salt, a pinch of sugar, and a pinch of pepper into the stew-pan.
4. Place your fillets upon the vegetables, and pour into the pan half a pint of good gravy.
5. Let this cook up to boiling point, and keep it on the fire till the broth has somewhat reduced and thickened.
6. Add a pint more gravy, and let it simmer for an hour with the pan half covered.
7. Close your pan: put some live charcoal on the lid, which lift every five minutes or so to admit of your basting the meat under it.
8. Continue this until you have glazed the fricandeau, then take it out, and dish it on a very hot dish.
9. Strain the gravy from the stew-pan, skim off any grease there may be, pour it over the meat, and serve.

title: Fricandellans
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of tender lean beef
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh suet
- four eggs
- half a pint of rich cream
- grated and sifted bread
- salt
- pepper
- good stock or boiling water

## Directions

1. Mince about two pounds of tender lean beef and three-quarters of a pound of fresh suet
2. then pound till it is as smooth as a paste, and carefully pick out all the threads and sinews
3. add four well-beaten eggs, half a pint of rich cream, and as much grated and sifted bread as will make it sufficiently consistent to form into rolls resembling corks
4. season with salt and pepper
5. Boil the corks in some good stock, or in boiling water, or fry them

title: Fried Bacon with Peas
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- thick slice of bacon

## Directions

1. Cook the peas as recommended, cut a thick slice of bacon into small dice, and crisp.
2. Mix them with the peas, and serve.

title: Fried Cakes with Brinjals
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender brinjals
- water or rice
- minced green chillies
- onions
- salt
- rice-flour
- oil

## Directions

1. Boil a few tender brinjals in water or in rice
2. remove the stalks and peel
3. Mix with minced green chillies, onions, salt and rice-flour and beat
4. Form into cakes and fry in oil

title: Fried Cakes with Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chhana
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Drain off the water from the chhana by pressure
2. Mix powdered turmeric, salt and a little flour
3. Form into cakes and fry

title: Fried Cakes with Cocoa-Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cocoa-nut kernel
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- black cumin
- aniseed

## Directions

1. Break the stone of a ripe cocoa-nut and scrape out the kernel with a toothed iron grater
2. Grind them again into a paste
3. Add powdered turmeric, salt, a few entire black-cumin and aniseed
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Form cakes of the paste and fry

title: Fried Cakes with Dal
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- khesari pulse, peas or grams
- red chillies
- onions
- aniseed
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- oil

## Directions

1. Wash and keep some pulse of khesari, peas or grams under water for a few hours
2. Grind the moistened pulse into a paste with red chillies and onions
3. Add entire aniseed, salt, and powdered turmeric
4. Beat well, form cakes of it and fry in plenty of oil

title: Fried Cakes with Kancha-Til
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- kancha-til sesame seeds
- powdered rice or flour or pulse-meal
- salt
- aniseed

## Directions

1. Wash and keep them under water for a while
2. Grind into a paste
3. Add either powdered rice or flour or pulse-meal, and salt and entire aniseed
4. Beat thoroughly, form into cakes and fry

title: Fried Cakes with Onions
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulse-meal
- salt
- red chillies
- onions
- oil

## Directions

1. Make a paste of pulse-meal, salt and ground red chillies
2. Add some onions chopped into fine pieces and beat thoroughly
3. Form cakes and fry them in plenty of oil

title: Fried Cakes with Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- powdered pepper
- red chillies
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- besam
- eggs
- oil

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes and then peel them
2. Rub them into a paste and mix powdered pepper, red chillies and salt
3. Form thin cakes and dip them into a thick solution of besam with salt or into the contents of eggs beaten with salt, and fry them in plenty of oil
4. Or slice the boiled potatoes and dip them into either of the two and fry them similarly

title: Fried Cakes with Sweet Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- powdered rice or flour
- salt
- sugar
- powdered black-cumin
- aniseed
- oil
- khowa kheer

## Directions

1. Boil them in water, peel and rub into a paste
2. Add some powdered rice or flour, salt, sugar and powdered black-cumin and aniseed, and mix thoroughly
3. Form cakes and fry in oil
4. A little khowa kheer mixed with sugar may be put inside each cake

title: Fried Dal or Dhonka
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulses of grams
- peas
- khesari
- salt
- aniseeds
- black cumin
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Keep the pulses moistened under water for a few hours.
2. Grind them to a paste.
3. Mix salt and bind it in a clean piece of cloth.
4. Drop it in boiling rice or boiling water.
5. Remove it when boiling is complete.
6. Mix some entire aniseeds and black cumin.
7. Press it into a flat disc half inch thick.
8. Cut into small pieces and fry them in ghee or oil.

title: Fried Eel Fillets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- batter
- oil or fat
- plain sharp sauce

## Directions

1. Slightly boil eels first to get rid of their oiliness
2. Cut them into fillets
3. Dip fillets in batter
4. Fry in oil or fat (lots of it)
5. Serve with a plain sharp sauce

title: Fried Plantains
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe plantains
- egg yolk
- flour
- melted ghee
- crushed crystallized sugar

## Directions

1. Slice or divide very ripe plantains lengthways into two
2. Brush them slightly with the yolk of an egg
3. Dredge with flour
4. Fry in melted ghee
5. Serve up hot, sprinkled with crushed crystallized sugar

title: Fried Puli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Boiling water
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of powdered rice with boiling water, adding the necessary amount of water at a time
2. Make medium balls
3. Stuff them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
4. Give them the shape of an almond
5. Fry in ghee

title: Fried Sole à la Colbert
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- egg
- fine bread-crumbs
- fat
- fine salt
- maitre d'hotel butter

## Directions

1. Make an incision along the back-bone on the upper side of the sole, from and up to within an inch of the head and tail.
2. Slip a knife under the flesh on each side of the cut, and loosen it from the bones.
3. Egg and bread-crumb the fish with finely sifted crumbs.
4. Fry it in plenty of fat, with the side bearing the incision uppermost.
5. The edges of this will curl outwards in the process of frying, and the opening thus made should be filled at the time of serving with plenty of maitre d'hotel butter.
6. Sprinkle the sole with fine salt.
7. While the fish is being drained in front of the fire, the back-bone may be removed carefully, but this is not essential.

title: Fried Whitings Sharp Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three nice whitings
- batter
- boiling fat
- Bombay onion
- clove of garlic
- butter
- table-spoonful of chopped parsley
- tea-spoonful of sugar
- coffee-cupful of vinegar
- pint of beef gravy
- tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
- table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- table-spoonful of minced cornichons

## Directions

1. Divide three nice whitings in fillets
2. Dip them in batter and fry them a golden yellow in a bath of boiling fat
3. Drain them
4. Fry a Bombay onion finely minced with one clove of garlic also minced in butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
5. When turning brown, put in a table-spoonful of chopped parsley, a tea-spoonful of sugar, a coffee-cupful of vinegar, and a pint of beef gravy
6. Stir in a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly and a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
7. As soon as the mixture becomes nicely flavoured and the juices appear to be extracted, strain off the sauce
8. Cut up a table-spoonful of minced cornichons and add the mince to the strained gravy
9. Heat it up and send it round with your fried fish in a boat

title: Fried Whitings with Remoulade Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- whitings
- butter
- shallots
- flour
- chicken stock
- marjoram
- garden cress
- parsley
- capers
- French mustard
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- salad oil

## Directions

1. Fry the whitings
2. Toss in butter a couple of shallots, finely minced
3. Add a dessert-spoonful of flour and half a pint of chicken stock
4. Take a table-spoonful of mixed marjoram, garden cress, parsley, and capers, all very finely minced, and add them to the sauce
5. Add a dessert-spoonful of French mustard, pepper, and salt to taste, and a little grated nutmeg
6. Add a dessert-spoonful of salad oil (optional)
7. When the sauce has boiled a minute or two, it is ready
8. Serve with the sauce in a boat

title: Fries of Fishes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Remove the flakes, fins, gills, etc. from the body of the fish.
2. Cut it near the head and bring out the intestines keeping the gall-bladder intact, otherwise the fish will taste bitter.
3. Cut the fish in big pieces and wash them thoroughly.
4. Smear powdered turmeric and salt.
5. Heat mustard oil in a pan till the bubbles die out and the vapour of the oil arises.
6. Drop the pieces of fish in it and fry to crispness.
7. They must not be turned over till one side is thoroughly browned, otherwise they will be broken to pieces.
8. Make deep cuts lengthwise on both sides in cases of climbing fishes.
9. Fries of scaleless fishes are not generally very tasteful.
10. Small fishes are very tasteful as fries.
11. Hilsa is a very oily fish and does not require much oil for frying, as oil comes out from the pieces of fish while frying.
12. This oil may be used for frying other pieces, it is also taken with rice with sufficient salt.

title: Fritot De Volaille
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a well grown chicken
- salad oil
- juice of a lime
- an onion
- pepper
- salt
- milk
- flour
- hot fat
- crisply fried parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up a well grown chicken as if for fricassee
2. Marinade the pieces in salad oil with the juice of a lime, an onion sliced in rings, pepper and salt
3. Half an hour before serving, take out the onion
4. Wipe the pieces of chicken on a cloth
5. Dip them in milk
6. Flour them well
7. Fry in plenty of hot fat accelerating the heat during the process
8. When the pieces are cooked and fried a golden yellow colour, pile them on a napkin garnished with crisply fried parsley
9. Serve with a nice sauce such as bread sauce, poivrade, or Robert

title: Frost or Icing for Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of four eggs
- Three-quarters of a pound of double-refined sugar, pounded and sifted through a lawn sieve
- Juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Beat till very light the whites of four eggs, and add gradually three-quarters of a pound of double-refined sugar, pounded and sifted through a lawn sieve
2. Mix in the juice of half a lemon
3. Beat it till very light and white
4. Place the cake before the fire, pour over it the icing, and smooth over the top and sides with the back of a spoon

title: Fruits of Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ seer khowa kheer
- ½ powa sugar
- 1 powa milk
- 1 tola powdered cardamom
- powdered aniseed
- 1 chhatak ghee
- 1 chhatak pasted almonds
- 1 chhatak pasted pistachios
- essence of rose
- clove
- mango-scented ginger juice
- entire poppy-seed

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly khowa kheer ½ seer, sugar ½ powa, milk 1 powa, powdered cardamom 1 tola, a pinch of powdered aniseed, ghee 1 chhatak, pasted almonds and pistachios 1 chhatak each, together
2. Heat the mass and stir till it is a stiff paste
3. Take down and scrape together the paste sticking to the sides of the pan
4. Mix with 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose
5. Beat the mass to a very fine paste
6. Make balls of it and give them the colour of the fruits to be made
7. Press them in its cast
8. Fix a clove in the place of the stalk
9. For mangoes, smear the balls also with the juice of mango-scented ginger
10. For lichies, fry some entire poppy-seed without ghee or oil and then give them a deep red colour
11. Sprinkle them on the lichies of kheer giving the look of real fruits
12. The fruits may be decorated with their respective leaves and branches

title: Fry of Fish Roes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish roes
- water
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- besam

## Directions

1. Take out the roes of the fish and wash gently with water without damaging them
2. Smear powdered turmeric and salt, and fry them entire or piecemeal
3. Or, dip the pieces into egg-contents beaten with powdered turmeric and salt, roll on bread crumbs and fry
4. Or, beat with salt, powdered turmeric and a little besam, and fry in cakes like bara

title: Fry of Lobsters or Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- powdered pepper
- salt
- minced potatoes
- eggs
- powdered turmeric
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the scales except that on the head and wash thoroughly
2. Remove that on the head, make a deep cut lengthwise and stuff it with powdered pepper, salt and minced potatoes
3. Dip it into the liquid contents of eggs beaten with salt and powdered turmeric
4. Roll on flour and fry on mild heat

title: Fry of Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- curd
- salt
- turmeric paste
- cumin paste
- chilli paste
- onion paste
- ginger paste
- garam-masala paste
- minced meat
- ghee
- garlic pieces
- cassia leaves
- water

## Directions

1. Mix curd, salt, pastes of turmeric, cumin, chillies, onions, ginger and garam-masala with minced meat.
2. Boil some ghee, fry a few pieces of garlic and cassia leaves in it and then the meat till the water coming out of it completely dries up.
3. A little water may be added once or twice, if necessary, to boil the meat to softness.

title: Fry of Mutton Fat, Heart, etc.
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton fat
- powdered or pasted turmeric
- ginger
- onions
- chillies
- salt
- besam
- oil or ghee
- livers
- hearts

## Directions

1. Cleanse some fat, mix it thoroughly with powdered or pasted turmeric, ginger, onions, chillies and salt
2. Form balls
3. Dip into a moderately thick solution of besam
4. Fry in plenty of oil or ghee
5. Boil small pieces of livers and hearts
6. Mix onions, turmeric, chillies, ginger, ground to a paste, and salt with the boiled pieces
7. Fry with oil or ghee

title: Fry of the Intestines of Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- intestines of big fresh fish
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- oil
- besam

## Directions

1. Remove the dark portions containing the undigested foods and wash
2. Mix with powdered turmeric and salt, and fry in oil
3. This is taken with rice or used to season sak chach-chari of every kind
4. Or, cut in big pieces, mix with salt and turmeric, dip into a well-beaten paste of besam and fry

title: Ful-Kapi Chach Chari
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower 1 seer
- Potato 1 powa
- Green peas 1/2 powa
- Bori 1/2 powa
- Oil 1 powa
- Ground turmeric 1 tola
- Red chillies 1/2 tola
- Pepper 1/2 tola
- Cumin 1/2 tola
- Coriander 1 tola
- Cinnamon 1 tola
- Salt 2 tolas
- Cloves, cardamoms, cassia leaves and panch-foron
- Water about 1/2 seer

## Directions

1. Brown the bori in oil and keep them aside
2. Brown all the vegetables, peeled and cut in pieces, and add water with all the powdered spices except the panch-foron, cassia leaves and a few red chillies
3. Add bori and salt when it boils
4. Season the curry with the rest of the oil heated with the remaining spices
5. Take down when the water dries up

title: Gaganpanthia
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of besam of gram
- 1 tola each of powders of pepper, white cumin and coriander
- 1/2 tola of salt
- water
- plenty of oil or ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix together thoroughly 1/2 seer of besam of gram, powders of pepper, white cumin and coriander 1 tola each and salt 1/2 tola
2. Make a stiff dough with water
3. Form finger-like thick and long sticks
4. Fry them in plenty of oil or ghee
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar

title: Gaja
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fine flour 1 seer
- ghee 1 powa
- entire black sesames 1 1/2 chhatak
- some entire aniseeds
- ghee for frying
- sugar for concentrated syrup

## Directions

1. Mix together fine flour, ghee, entire black sesames and some entire aniseeds
2. Make a very stiff paste with as little water as possible
3. Make balls of it or cut with a knife in cubes
4. Fry hard in plenty of ghee
5. Keep immersed in a very concentrated syrup of sugar till saturated
6. Take them out and dry in the air

title: Galantines of Quails
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boned quails
- spiced pepper
- fat bacon
- lamb's liver
- birds' livers
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- parsley
- dessert-spoonful of spiced pepper
- grated lime peel
- chopped truffles
- cold sheep's tongues
- red currant jelly
- glass of sherry
- half a wine-glass of vinegar

## Directions

1. Trim cold sheep's tongues and cut eight nice fillets the size of a walnut from the centre of them
2. Place boned birds breast downwards on a board and dust them over with spiced pepper
3. Melt a couple of thick slices of fat bacon in a frying-pan
4. When melted, throw in three table-spoonfuls of the lamb's liver with those of the birds too, cut into dice, and one onion shredded finely
5. Cook the liver in this, and when done, empty the contents of the pan, melted bacon and all, into a mortar
6. Throw in the remains of the sheep's tongues which were left after making the fillets, a pinch of salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
7. Work this to a paste and pass it through the sieve
8. Add some finely minced parsley, a dessert-spoonful of spiced pepper, a little grated lime peel, and a table-spoonful of chopped truffles
9. Work this again thoroughly and spread a layer of it over the flattened quails
10. Place one of the tongue fillets in the centre of each quail and spread another layer of forcemeat over each
11. Gather the birds into shape neatly, sewing the skin together securely
12. Stew the little galantines in the previously made gravy, with a little red currant jelly, a glass of sherry, and half a wine-glass of vinegar
13. When done, lift them out and drain off the gravy
14. Reduce the gravy to a glaze
15. Paint the galantines over with the glaze when cold
16. Arrange them upon a dish which should be set upon ice
17. Garnish the dish with parsley and slices of lime
18. Serve with sauce tartare, also iced

title: Game Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- snipe, quails, partridges, hares, pigeons, teal, wild duck
- tender beef or good mutton collops
- slices of bacon, ham, or Bologna sausage
- game livers
- truffles
- one pound and a half of gravy beef
- mashed game bones
- bacon or ham bone or slices
- two sheep's feet
- vegetables for soup-making
- bouquet of sweet herbs
- clove of garlic
- mixed spice
- pepper corns
- caramel
- salt
- one glass of Madeira or fruity sherry
- teaspoonful of reduced vinegar
- one table-spoonful of red currant jelly
- half a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup
- flour
- spiced pepper
- shallot
- paste

## Directions

1. Make a forcemeat from the livers of all the game, prepared as foie-gras forcemeat with truffles
2. Bone all the game, mashing the bones with a pestle
3. Make a rich gravy from the gravy beef, mashed game bones, bacon or ham bone, two sheep's feet, soup vegetables, sweet herbs, garlic, mixed spice, pepper corns, and caramel
4. Strain the broth and add one glass of Madeira or fruity sherry, one teaspoonful of reduced vinegar, one table-spoonful of red currant jelly, and half a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup
5. Boil the gravy again and set it to cool
6. Carefully bone the game and cut into neat pieces if large
7. Cast skin, scraps, and trimmings into the stock-pot
8. Arrange the trimmed meat on a large flat dish, dredge with flour, and dust with spiced pepper
9. Rub the pie-dish well with a shallot
10. Spread a thin coating of forcemeat over the bottom and up the sides of the pie-dish
11. Place a good layer of tender beef or mutton collops, each rolled round a slice of bacon, over the forcemeat
12. Pack the game meat with slices of ham, bacon, or Bologna sausage dotted in and frequent dustings of spiced pepper until the dish is filled
13. Pour in the gravy
14. Cover with a nice light paste and bake

title: Game Raised Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two partridges
- two quails
- four snipes
- back fillets of a good hare
- spiced pepper
- livers of the birds
- liver of the hare
- spare meat of the hare
- a quarter of a pound of ham
- half a pound of fat bacon
- finely chopped shallot
- eight fine truffles
- an ounce of butter
- a claret-glassful of Madeira
- liver and bacon forcemeat
- marjoram leaves
- bones and trimmings of the game
- two sheep's feet
- truffle liquor

## Directions

1. Bone two partridges, two quails, and four snipes
2. Cut out the back fillets of a good hare
3. Give the meat a dusting of spiced pepper, and cover it up
4. Take the livers of the birds and that of the hare, and mince them finely
5. Mince the spare meat of the hare, and a quarter of a pound of ham
6. Throw the whole of the mince into a frying-pan, in which half a pound of fat bacon cut into dice has been tossed with a finely chopped shallot
7. Work the mince about for ten minutes
8. Empty the contents of the frying-pan into a mortar, and pound the mixture to a paste
9. Cut eight fine truffles into dice the size of a pea
10. Melt an ounce of butter in the frying-pan, and throw in the truffles
11. Toss for a couple of minutes, and add a claret-glassful of Madeira
12. Let the dice boil in this, and then pour them with the liquor into a bowl
13. Spread a layer of liver paste over the inside of each bird
14. Put a spoonful of truffles upon it, and roll the bird up
15. Cut the hare into slices, and treat them in the same way
16. Put a layer of the liver paste over the bottom of the pie
17. Dot it over with truffles
18. Arrange the rolls of hare meat over that
19. Spread some liver paste over them, a few truffles, and some thin slices of fat bacon
20. Arrange a layer of snipe, and continue until the pie is tightly filled
21. Cover the topmost layer with fat bacon, sprinkled over with marjoram leaves
22. Cover the pie closely and bake
23. When done, pour into it a little very strong jelly made of the bones and trimmings of the game, two sheep's feet, and the truffle liquor

title: Garibaldi Cream
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of cream
- a pint of milk
- 1 oz. of isinglass
- sugar to taste
- yolks of eight eggs
- cochineal
- spinach greening

## Directions

1. Make a rich custard with a pint of cream and a pint of milk, 1 oz. of isinglass, sugar to taste, and the yolks of eight eggs
2. Flavour it with any essence you like
3. Strain it, and divide it into three basins
4. Colour the first a bright red with cochineal, the second green with spinach greening, and leave the third its original colour
5. Whip each separately to a froth
6. Lay a mould on ice, pour some of the red cream into it to the thickness of about an inch
7. When this is set, pour in a similar layer of the plain cream, and when this is set, pour in a layer of the green cream
8. Go on pouring in layers in the same way until the mould is filled
9. When the cream is quite set, turn it out and serve

title: Gateau De Pistache
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- the weight of eight eggs in their shells of finely-powdered sugar
- that of two eggs of potato flour
- the same weight of pistachio nuts blanched and skinned
- eight eggs
- four eggs (whites)
- four eggs (whites)
- two eggs (whites)
- a little lime juice
- six ounces of sugar
- strips of citron
- preserved cherries

## Directions

1. Beat up the sugar and the yolks of eight eggs well together with an egg whisk or with a fork until the mixture assumes a white creamy appearance
2. Sprinkle in half the potato flour while beating the mixture all the time
3. Add the whites of four eggs whisked to a stiff froth
4. Put in the rest of the flour in the same manner
5. Add the remaining four whites beaten to a froth
6. Lastly add the pistachio nuts pounded to a paste in a mortar
7. Bake in a slow oven
8. Meanwhile put the whites of two eggs into a basin with a little lime juice and six ounces of sugar
9. Well work the mixture with a wooden spoon
10. As it gets thin, keep on adding more sugar until you get a smooth paste of the consistency of batter
11. Lay the icing evenly on the cake with a spatula
12. Put it into the oven for a minute to set the icing
13. Ornament it quickly with strips of citron and preserved cherries
14. Put it aside to get cold

title: Gateau Napolitain
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- powdered lump sugar the weight of twelve eggs in their shells
- half that weight of potato flour
- twelve eggs
- a few drops of essence of lemon
- apricot jam
- chocolate icing
- bonbons or sweet-meats

## Directions

1. Separate the whites from the yolks of the eggs
2. Beat up the yolks with the sugar, adding a few drops of essence of lemon
3. Whisk the whites to a stiff froth
4. Mix the two together and incorporate the potato flour, beating it all the time with the egg whisk
5. Pour into a plain mould, buttered
6. Bake in a quick oven until done
7. When cold, cut the cake in horizontal slices half an inch thick
8. Spread half the slices of cake with apricot jam, and half with chocolate icing
9. Arrange the slices one over the other to form the cake again
10. Trim the slices neatly
11. Ice the cake completely over with chocolate icing, keeping it hot
12. Ornament the cake with bonbons or sweet-meats before the icing has time to cool

title: Genoises au Chocolat
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of butter
- 4 ounces of powdered loaf sugar
- eggs
- 1/2 ounce of fine flour
- buttered flat tin
- apricot jam
- chocolate icing
- powdered loaf sugar
- grated chocolate
- vanilla essence
- water

## Directions

1. Take 1/4 pound of the freshest butter, put it in a bowl, and warm it until it can be beaten with a spoon
2. Add to it 4 ounces of powdered loaf sugar, and beat the two together until a smooth white cream is obtained
3. Add one egg, and keep on beating the mixture till it is smooth again
4. Add three more eggs in the same manner
5. Remove the germ of the eggs
6. Incorporate quickly 1/2 ounce of fine flour with the mixture
7. As soon as it is smooth, pour it out to the thickness of 1/4 inch on a buttered flat tin
8. Put it into the oven at once
9. When done (in about ten to fifteen minutes), turn out the slab of Genoise, and put it to cool, underside uppermost, on a sieve
10. Take a slab of Genoise, spread on the top of it the thinnest possible coating of apricot jam
11. Apply a coating of chocolate icing
12. Put it into a very hot oven for rather less than a minute
13. Take it out, and place it in a cold place to get cool
14. Cut it up with a sharp knife in any shapes you like
15. For chocolate icing: Put into a sauce-pan 1/4 pound of powdered loaf sugar, 2 ounces of grated chocolate, a tea-spoonful of vanilla essence, and about a gill of water
16. Stir on the fire until the mixture assumes the consistence of a thick smooth cream

title: German Cutlet
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender meat without bones
- stone
- curd
- salt
- eggs
- pastes of onions
- ginger
- garlic
- ghee
- mustard
- onions
- boiled potatoes
- cucumber

## Directions

1. Take slices of tender meat without bones weighing about 3 chhataks each and thrash them with a stone taking care that each piece remains entire
2. Keep them mixed up for about an hour with a mixture of curd, salt, eggs, pastes of onions, ginger and garlic
3. Fry both sides of them with ghee on slow heat keeping stretched with something heavy placed on them
4. Serve with mustard, slices of onions, boiled potatoes and cucumber

title: Ghee-Champa
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- champa-note sak 1 seer
- ghee 1 powa
- garam-masalla 0.5 tola
- minced ginger 1 chhatak
- coriander 1 tola
- salt 1 tola
- pepper 0.5 tola
- panch-foron a pinch
- water

## Directions

1. Dissolve salt in a tea-cup of water and boil the sak in it.
2. Boil ghee in a pan with the minced ginger and panch-foron, and add into it the boiled sak and powdered coriander and pepper, and stir thoroughly for a few minutes till the sak are non-sticky.
3. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down.

title: Ghugni
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- potatoes
- powdered turmeric
- salt

## Directions

1. Shell hard boiled eggs and cut in small pieces
2. Cut potatoes in small pieces
3. Smear powdered turmeric and salt with them separately
4. Brown the potatoes and eggs

title: Ghugni
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- potatoes
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- ghee
- red chillies
- peas
- ground red chillies
- pepper
- cumin
- water

## Directions

1. Shell some hard-boiled eggs and cut in small pieces.
2. Cut some potatoes in small pieces.
3. Smear powdered turmeric and salt with them separately.
4. Brown the potatoes fully and the eggs slightly in ghee.
5. Boil ghee with a few red chillies and add fully moistened big sized peas.
6. Add ground red chillies, turmeric, pepper and cumin, and stir.
7. Add water and salt after a time.
8. Add potatoes and eggs on boiling.
9. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down when no soup is left.
10. Serve while hot.

title: Ghugni (Simple)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Entire grams ^ seer
- Potato ^ seer
- Ghee i powa
- Poppy-seed ^ powa
- Garam-masalla ^ tola
- Pepper i tola
- Cumin i tola
- Coriander i tola
- Some red chillies
- A ripe cocoa nut
- Ginger i chhatak
- Turmeric i tola
- Salt i tola
- A pinch of aniseed
- Almonds (if desired)
- Pistachios (if desired)

## Directions

1. Keep the grams under water for about 6 hours and then boil them in water till they are quite soft
2. Take the husks out that come out easily and drain off the water
3. Heat the garam-masalla, pepper, cumin and coriander in a pot without ghee or oil for a few minutes and then powder them with turmeric
4. Similarly heat the poppy-seed and pulverize them
5. Mix them together
6. Brown the pieces of potatoes and those of the kernel of the cocoa-nut in half a powa of ghee or oil and then add the boiled grams
7. Stir for 5 minutes, add the powdered spices, and stir for 5 minutes more
8. Then add a little water and salt, and stir
9. Take down when the water dries up and the mass is quite soft
10. Now heat half a powa of ghee (not oil) with the aniseed and red chillies
11. Brown the pieces of almonds and pistachios in it
12. Then add the boiled mass and stir

title: Ghugni of Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- garam-masala
- pepper
- cumin
- onion paste
- meat cubes
- ghee
- oil
- potato pieces
- cassia leaves
- ginger pieces
- salt
- water
- barabatees
- peas

## Directions

1. Mix garam-masala, pepper, cumin and onions, pasted together, with small cubes of meat and fry to crispness with ghee or oil.
2. Add an equal quantity of small pieces of potatoes fried with cassia leaves and very small pieces of ginger.
3. Boil with a little water, add salt and take down when no soup is left.
4. Barabatees and peas, moistened, boiled to softness and fried, may be added in place of or along with fried potatoes.

title: Ginger Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- bruised green ginger
- ginger beer

## Directions

1. Use bruised green ginger instead of mint, and ginger beer instead of lemonade

title: Ginger Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- three-quarters of a pound of good moist sugar
- one ounce of the best Jamaica ginger
- three-quarters of a pound of lard melted
- four eggs well beaten

## Directions

1. In two pounds of flour well mix three-quarters of a pound of good moist sugar and one ounce of the best Jamaica ginger
2. Have ready three-quarters of a pound of lard melted, and four eggs well beaten
3. Mix the lard and eggs together and stir into the flour, which will form a paste
4. Roll out in thin cakes and bake in a moderately heated oven

title: Ginger Pop
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce well-bruised green ginger cleaned of all rind
- one ounce cream of tartar
- one pound white sugar
- some toddy
- rind and juice of one large lime
- four quarts water
- one claretglassful good fresh toddy

## Directions

1. Boil the ginger, cream of tartar, sugar, toddy, and lime rind and juice in four quarts of water for twenty minutes
2. When nearly cold, add one claretglassful of good fresh toddy
3. Let it stand for six hours
4. Put into soda-water bottles. It will fill eight or nine bottles.

title: Gingerbread Nuts
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of flour
- a pound of sugar
- three pounds and a half of treacle
- half an ounce of caraway-seeds
- half an ounce of allspice
- two ounces of butter
- half an ounce of candied lemon-peel
- three ounces of ground ginger
- half an ounce of coriander
- the yolks of three eggs
- a wineglassful of brandy

## Directions

1. Work the butter to a cream, then the eggs, spice, and brandy, then flour, sugar, and then hot treacle
2. If not stiff enough, a little more flour must be added in rolling out, but the less the better

title: Gingerbread Spiced
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of treacle
- one egg
- four ounces of moist sugar
- an ounce of powdered ginger
- a quarter of an ounce each of mace, cloves, allspice, and nutmeg powdered
- a pound of oiled butter
- sufficient flour to make a stiff paste

## Directions

1. Mix well, and make into thick pieces, which should be brushed over the top with white of egg and baked for an hour in a moderate oven

title: Globe Artichokes a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- trimmed globe artichoke quarters
- butter
- lime juice
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare artichoke quarters as directed and boil until nearly done
2. Toss in butter
3. Serve on a hot silver dish with the melted butter, a squeeze of lime juice and a sprinkling of very finely chopped curled parsley

title: Globe Artichokes au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- trimmed globe artichoke quarters
- gravy
- bread-crumbs
- mushroom
- parsley
- shallot
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare artichoke quarters as directed and boil until nearly done
2. Neatly dispose the pieces upon a silver dish, with a little gravy round them to keep them from burning
3. Dust over with very finely sifted bread-crumbs, chopped mushroom, parsley, and a little shallot
4. Season with pepper and salt, and place a piece of butter on the top of each piece
5. Bake for ten minutes and serve hot

title: Globe Artichokes Boiled
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- globe artichoke
- salt
- water
- soda
- Dutch sauce
- anchovy vinegar or lime-juice

## Directions

1. Soak the globe artichoke well in salt and water to get rid of the insects which may be hidden between the leaves
2. Set it head downwards in boiling water, with soda and salt
3. Boil till the leaves part easily from the core
4. Drain it, and dish it hot
5. Serve with Dutch sauce in which a few drops of anchovy vinegar, or lime-juice have been introduced

title: Gokul-Pitha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of khowa kheer
- 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sabeda
- hot milk (if necessary)
- flour 1 powa
- sugar 1/2 powa
- milk about 3 chhataks
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Make stiff dough of 1/2 seer of khowa kheer and 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sabeda with a little hot milk if necessary
2. Make small balls of it, press them flat and round like luchi
3. Dip them into a thick paste of flour 1 powa sugar 1/2 powa and milk about 3 chhataks
4. Fry in plenty of ghee
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar

title: Golapi Khechuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice ^ seer
- Fried moog-dal ^ seer
- Potato |- seer or more
- Onion i powa
- Ghee ^ powa
- Salt 2 tolas
- Ginger i tola
- Turmeric ^ tola
- Cumin ^ tola
- Pepper ^ tola
- Garam-masalla ^ tola
- A few cassia leaves
- Red chillies
- Almonds (optional)
- Pistachios (optional)
- Raisins (optional)
- Rose-water

## Directions

1. Heat the cassia leaves and red chillies with half the ghee and when the chillies turn black drop rice and pulse, washed and dried, and stir for about 5 minutes
2. Then pour in water and cover the boiling pot
3. Add salt, turmeric, cumin, pepper and ginger, all pasted or powdered, when the rice are half-boiled
4. Brown pieces of potatoes and onions in ghee or oil and drop them in the khechuri
5. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla before finish
6. Almonds, pistachios and raisins, peeled and cut in pieces, may be added if desired after browning them in ghee
7. Mix adequate quantities of rose-water with the khechuri in every dish before serving

title: Golapjam
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard chhana 1/2 seer
- sabeda (powdered atap rice) 1/2 seer
- water if necessary
- sugar for syrup
- dry sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer hard chhana
2. Mix thoroughly with 1/2 seer of sabeda with a little water if necessary
3. Make medium balls of it
4. Boil in a syrup of sugar till stiff
5. Take them out and smear with dry sugar

title: Golden Wasser or Dantzie Brandy
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of spirits of wine
- twelve drops of oil of aniseed
- six drops of oil of cinnamon
- three drops of oil of roses
- eight drops of oil of citron
- a quart of syrup
- a few squares of leaf-gold

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine add twelve drops of oil of aniseed, six of oil of cinnamon, three of oil of roses, and eight of oil of citron
2. shake it well until the oils dissolve
3. add a quart of syrup
4. filter through filtering-paper
5. before bottling the liqueur, stir into it a few squares of leaf-gold cut into very little bits

title: Golla-Sandesh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 seer of syrup of sugar
- 1 seer of hard chhana

## Directions

1. Heat syrup of sugar 1.5 seer and when slightly warm add a paste of hard chhana 1 seer.
2. Stir continuously and vigorously till sticky.
3. Take it off fire and stir as before till cold.
4. Make balls of it and serve.

title: Goolgoola Another Way (as usually served on the tea-table)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chittacks or four ounces of soojee
- four eggs, well beaten up
- four chittacks or eight ounces of milk
- three chittacks or six ounces of ghee
- crushed crystallized sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the soojee and eggs, beating them well together
2. Gradually add the milk
3. Melt down three chittacks or six ounces of ghee in a small but deep pan
4. Pour into the boiling ghee in one spot the mixture, a dessertspoonful at a time
5. Fry until of a rich brown colour
6. Serve up hot, sprinkled with crushed crystallized sugar

title: Goolgoola, or Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- half a seer or one pound of flour
