- half a seer or one pound of sugar
- half a seer or one pound of milk
- half a dozen small sticks of cinnamon
- a little yeast
- half a seer of ghee
- water

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with the yeast and a little milk
2. Add water sufficient to bring it to a thick consistency
3. Put into it gradually the sugar and the remainder of the milk
4. Place it on the fire, adding the cinnamon
5. Keep stirring it with a large spoon until it is again reduced to a thick consistency
6. Remove it from the fire, and when it has cooled make it up into small balls
7. Fry them in ghee

title: Gooseberry Mould
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- gelatine
- water
- maraschino or curaçoa
- custard

## Directions

1. Turn out a basin of liquid jelly of gooseberries
2. Sweeten them to taste in their own syrup
3. Melt an ounce of gelatine in water and stir into the basin containing the fruit
4. Place this in a mould and set it to get firm in the ice-box
5. A glass of maraschino or curaçoa may be stirred into the syrup with manifest advantage
6. Serve with the best creamy custards, also set in the ice-box, in glasses

title: Gopal-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of fine besam of moog
- 1 powa of fine flour
- 1 powa of sugar
- 1/2 powa of middlings
- 1/2 powa of scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel
- 1 chhatak of entire black sesames
- 4 tola of powdered garam-masala
- 1/2 pinch of camphor
- hot water
- ghee or oil
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take fine besam of moog 1/2 seer, fine flour 1 powa, sugar 1 powa, middlings 1/2 powa, scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel 1/2 powa, entire black sesames 1 chhatak, powdered garam-masala 4 tola and a half pinch of camphor, and mix together.
2. Make a stiff paste with hot water.
3. Make balls of it.
4. Fry in ghee or oil.
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar for 12 to 24 hours.

title: Gopal-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- moog flour 1 seer
- fine flour 1 powa
- sugar 1 powa
- middlings 1 powa
- scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel 1 powa
- entire black sesames 1 chhatak
- powdered garam-masalla 4 tola
- a half pinch of camphor

## Directions

1. Mix together all ingredients
2. Make a stiff paste with hot water
3. Make balls of the paste
4. Fry in ghee or oil
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar for 12 to 24 hours

title: Gopchop
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer fine flour
- ½ seer sugar syrup
- ½ seer ghee
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Mix together fine flour 1 seer, a syrup of ½ seer of sugar, ghee ½ seer and 3 or 4 drops of essence of rose
2. Prepare balls of the mass
3. Arrange them in a big metal dish
4. Cover it by another of the same size
5. Close the join with some flour paste
6. Place the whole thing on burning coals
7. Surround it on all sides and above with more burning coals
8. Remove the ashes when the coals are burnt up
9. Serve the balls when cool

title: Govindabhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ seer khowa kheer
- ½ seer sabeda
- ½ powa besam
- powders of pepper ½ tola
- powders of cardamom ½ tola
- saffron
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a moderately thick paste of khowa kheer ½ seer, sabeda ½ seer, besam ½ powa, powders of pepper and cardamom ½ tola each and very little saffron with water
2. Beat thoroughly
3. Fry this paste in very fine drops in plenty of boiling ghee till reddish brown in the same way as Mihidana
4. Take them out with the same perforated ladle
5. Drop in a syrup of sugar
6. Take them out again when saturated
7. Form into big balls like Mihidana

title: Gravy Fish Curries
body:
## Ingredients

- mustard oil, one chittack or two ounces
- water, two cups
- ground onions, four teaspoonfuls
- ground turmeric, one teaspoonful
- ground chilies, one teaspoonful
- ground garlic, a quarter of a teaspoonful
- fish

## Directions

1. Thoroughly clean, rub, and wash the fish in salt and water before cooking
2. Use the condiments as specified, cooking the fish in the oil with the ground spices and water

title: Green Butter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a couple of good handfuls of spinach
- six full sized anchovies from the tin
- curled parsley sufficient to fill a table-spoon generously
- capers sufficient to fill a tea-spoon, heaped up

## Directions

1. Weigh a quarter of a pound of butter such as described, and ice it
2. Boil a couple of good handfuls of spinach, drain them thoroughly, pass the leaves through the hair sieve, and save all the pulp obtained, in a bowl
3. Take six full sized anchovies from the tin, wipe them free from oil, pick out their back bones, pass them through the hair sieve, and save the pulp
4. Mince as finely as possible sufficient curled parsley to fill a table-spoon generously
5. Mince also as finely as possible capers sufficient to fill a tea-spoon, heaped up
6. Having these ingredients ready, first colour the butter by working into it, as lightly as you can, enough of the spinach pulp

title: Green Butter with Herring Roes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a handful of spinach
- pepper
- soft roes of two herrings a la sardine from the tin
- a large bunch of curly parsley
- a dozen capers

## Directions

1. Make a quarter of a pound of fresh butter from cream that you have set at home, and put it in ice
2. Boil a handful of spinach till tender, and then pass it through the sieve
3. Save the pulp carefully and give it a dust of pepper
4. Take the roes (soft) of two herrings a la sardine from the tin, wipe them carefully to get rid of the oil
5. Pound them in a mortar, and pass them through the hair sieve
6. Mince very finely a large bunch of curly parsley, so as to have at least a heaped up table-spoonful of it when minced
7. Mince a dozen capers
8. Mix the whole of the ingredients together with a wooden butter bat, and shape it as you like
9. Set it again in ice till wanted

title: Green Duck Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- young tender duck
- half a teaspoonful of coriander seeds
- half a teaspoonful of cumin seeds

## Directions

1. Cut it up exactly as you would a chicken
2. Curry it in the same manner
3. Introduce half a teaspoonful each of coriander and cumin seeds in this curry
4. Allow it to simmer for an hour and a half

title: Green Herb Forcemeat for Turkey or Veal
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lime
- six ounces dry, well sifted, stale bread-crumbs
- one dessert-spoonful of chopped thyme (green), or one table-spoonful of dried thyme leaves powdered
- one dessert-spoonful of chopped marjoram (green), or one table-spoonful of dried marjoram leaves powdered
- one heaped table-spoonful minced parsley
- three ounces fresh beef-suet or butter
- salt
- pepper
- three well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Pare a good sized lime as finely as possible avoiding the slightest particle of white pith, and mince the peel as small as possible
2. Weigh six ounces of dry, well sifted, stale bread-crumbs
3. Measure a dessert-spoonful of chopped thyme (green) and one of marjoram (green), or take a table-spoonful of the dried leaves powdered — half and half, and powder the leaves to get rid of atoms of stalk and stick
4. Mince the parsley fine to the extent of a heaped up table-spoonful
5. Chop up three ounces of fresh beef-suet, or butter if suet cannot be got
6. Mix all these together with two spoons in a large dish
7. Dust the whole well with salt and pepper
8. Bind the mixture with three well-beaten eggs
9. Work this together, and the stuffing will be fit to use

title: Green Mango Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground fresh mint-leaves
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of sugar
- two ordinary large-size green mangoes
- clean water

## Directions

1. Peel the mangoes, divide, and throw them into clean water
2. Remove the stones
3. Bruise them to a perfect pulp with the aid of the curry-stone and muller, ensuring the stone is perfectly clean
4. Mix the sugar well with the pulp
5. If the mango is very acid, add a little more sugar
6. Mix it with the salt and ground condiments
7. Add more salt or sugar if required

title: Green Mango Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- mangoes slightly under the middling size
- clean water
- lime-water
- good syrup (two pounds of sugar to every twenty-five mangoes)

## Directions

1. Select mangoes slightly under the middling size, taking care that they are not bruised or injured in any way
2. Steep them in clean water
3. Grate the outer coat, or peel very finely, so as to remove thoroughly a fine coat of green from the surface
4. Cut them sufficiently lengthways to extract the stones
5. Throw them into lime-water
6. Remove them into a copper preserving-pan with clean water
7. Parboil them, skimming them well
8. Throw them into a sieve, and allow all the water to drain away
9. Have a large quantity of good syrup prepared, allowing two pounds of sugar to every twenty-five mangoes
10. Throw the mangoes into the syrup, and allow them to simmer
11. Cut away the scum until the sugar inclines to crystallize
12. Remove the pan from the fire, and put the preserve into wide-mouthed bottles
13. Before corking them down, examine the syrup every two or three days, and if it is becoming thin, reboil it
14. Just as the boiling is about to be finished, put the mangoes into it to warm up
15. Repeat this precaution every time the syrup has been reboiled, until there is no further appearance of fermentation
16. Then securely cork down the bottles

title: Green Mint Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, clean mint-leaves
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Put into a wide-mouthed bottle enough fresh, clean mint-leaves to fill it loosely
2. Fill it up with good vinegar
3. After it has been stopped close for two or three weeks, pour off the vinegar clear into another bottle
4. Keep it well corked for use
5. Serve with lamb or kid when fresh mint cannot be obtained

title: Green Mint Vinegar Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, full-grown, green mint-leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Fill a wide mouthed bottle with fresh, full-grown, green mint-leaves
2. Pour in a quart of vinegar
3. After ten or fifteen days strain away the liquor
4. Re-fill the bottle with fresh leaves
5. Pour back the liquor
6. After it has steeped for ten or fifteen days longer, strain and bottle for use as required

title: Green Mint-juice
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen large stalks of good fresh mint
- 1 dessertspoonful of water or brandy

## Directions

1. Pick and clean half a dozen large stalks of good fresh mint
2. Pound the leaves in a mortar with a dessertspoonful of water or with brandy
3. Put them into muslin and squeeze out all the liquor
4. Juice may be extracted a second time by a little more water or brandy being added, and the leaves rebruised and pressed through muslin

title: Green Peas Stewed
body:
## Ingredients

- quart of good peas
- lettuce
- small onion
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- egg
- mint
- gravy

## Directions

1. Put a quart of good peas into a stewpan, with a lettuce and small onion sliced small, but not any water
2. add a piece of butter the size of an orange, pepper and salt to taste
3. stew gently for two hours
4. Beat up an egg, and stir into them (or a lump of butter will do as well)
5. mint should be stewed (if it can be procured) with them, and ought to be chopped fine, and stirred in with some good gravy

title: Green Peas with Mint
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- contents of a tin of petits pois
- a large spoonful of fresh butter
- a tea-spoonful of sifted sugar
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- a little bundle of mint leaves

## Directions

1. Wash the contents of a tin of petits pois in cold water by emptying the tin into your strainer and pouring a jug of water over them
2. Put them into a jar with a close fitting cover with a large spoonful of fresh butter, a tea-spoonful of sifted sugar, and a tea-spoonful of salt with a little bundle of mint leaves
3. Secure the top of your jar and immerse it within a large sauce-pan with water that should half cover the jar
4. Steam the peas until thoroughly hot
5. Stir in a little more butter, pick out the mint, and serve on a very hot silver dish

title: Greengages in Cream
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- greengages
- one ounce of gelatine
- liqueur-glass of maraschino
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Turn the greengages out into a glass dish.
2. Dissolve an ounce of gelatine and stir it with a liqueur-glass of maraschino into the syrup.
3. Set the dish in the ice-box to get firm and cold.
4. Serve with a canopy of whipped cream resting on the surface of the congealed fruit.

title: Grenadins of Beef
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of beef or tender meat from the ribs
- fat bacon
- meat and bones
- glass of Marsala
- yolks of seven eggs
- five ounces of butter
- salt
- pepper
- dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
- table-spoonful of chopped parsley
- water
- potato
- crisply fried bacon
- flageolets or petits pois

## Directions

1. Trim fillet into thick heart-shaped fillets large enough for one person each
2. Lard them with fat bacon
3. Set them to marinade all day as described in Chapter VIII, page 65
4. Drain them and stew them gently in as rich a stock as you can make from the meat and bones
5. Add a glass of Marsala to the stock
6. Keep them hot in the bain-marie when done
7. Put into a stew-pan the yolks of seven eggs, one ounce of butter, one pinch of salt, and a little pepper
8. Stir over a low fire till the yolks begin to thicken
9. Take the pan off the fire and stir in one ounce of butter more
10. Stir over the fire for two minutes and again remove the pan, adding another ounce of butter
11. Repeat the process again twice, using in all five ounces of butter
12. Stir till the sauce is rich and creamy looking
13. Finally add a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar and a table-spoonful of chopped parsley
14. Add a little water with the butter to prevent curdling
15. Dish the grenadins round a ring of carefully mashed potato, introducing a slice of crisply fried bacon between each of them
16. Serve the sauce piping hot in a boat
17. Put flageolets a la maitre d'hotel or petits pois in the hollow formed by the potato ring

title: Grilled Fowl with Bread Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- butter
- water-cress

## Directions

1. Bone your fowl, and make a nice strong broth with the bones
2. Flatten out the fowl like a steak, butter it and grill it smartly over a bright fire
3. Serve with bread sauce and fried potatoes a la Lyonnaise
4. Garnish with water-cress

title: Guava Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- guavas (boiled for jelly)
- lime-juice
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. After all the water or juice has drained from the guavas boiled for jelly, pass the fruit or pulp through a sieve, rejecting the seeds
2. Add lime-juice and sugar to taste
3. Boil over a slow fire to a consistency stiff enough for it to remain unmoved in a spoon
4. Rub a little butter in a mould
5. Fill it with the cheese while hot
6. Place it in a heat, or in an expiring oven, to dry
7. The colour may be improved with the aid of cochineal

title: Guava Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe guavas
- fresh clean water
- good clean sugar
- lime-juice (ten large juicy limes to every hundred guavas)

## Directions

1. Select ripe guavas, and as they are peeled and quartered throw them into a large bowl of fresh clean water
2. Boil them in as much other clean water as will only cover the fruit
3. When perfectly tender, so as to dissolve to the touch, strain through a fine sieve or towel without breaking or pressing the fruit
4. Allow it to drip through for twelve to eighteen or twenty-four hours if necessary
5. Put the juice on the fire again without a cover to the preserving-pan
6. Boil and skim well
7. Add gradually good clean sugar to your taste
8. When nearly done, add lime-juice in the proportion of ten large juicy limes to every hundred guavas
9. After it has boiled until no more scum rises, and the jelly is quite clear, pour it while warm into glass or stone jars
10. Cork them down when quite cold

title: Gujia
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- kheer 1/2 seer
- sugar 3 chhataks
- powdered cardamoms 1/4 tola
- powdered sugar-candy 1/2 powa
- pastes of almonds and pistachios 1/2 chhatak each
- minced almonds and pistachios
- saffron
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Heat kheer and 3 chhataks of sugar
2. Stir till the mass is non-sticky
3. Take down and mix with powdered cardamoms, powdered sugar-candy and pastes of almonds and pistachios
4. Make small balls of it
5. Stuff with minced almonds and pistachios
6. Immerse in a syrup of sugar coloured with saffron

title: Gutikabab
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of a big fish
- curd
- ginger
- coriander
- chillies
- cloves
- cardamoms
- lemon juice
- besam of grams
- a little water
- ghee
- salt
- powdered garam-masala
- beaten yolks of eggs
- bread crumbs or middlings

## Directions

1. Boil some pieces of a big fish and remove the bones
2. Add curd, ginger, coriander, chillies, cloves, cardamoms, lemon juice, besam of grams and a little water
3. Mix to a paste
4. Fry it in ghee till dry
5. Grind it and make a muddy paste by adding curd, salt and powdered garam-masala
6. Form balls of it
7. Dip them into a cup containing beaten yolks of eggs
8. Roll on bread crumbs or middlings
9. Fry in ghee
10. Serve while hot

title: Haggis
body:
## Ingredients

- heart
- lights
- one pound of minced suet
- two or three large onions
- two small handfuls of oatmeal
- pepper
- salt
- bag

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the heart and lights.
2. Parboil and mince them very small.
3. Add one pound of minced suet, two or three large onions minced, and two small handfuls of oatmeal.
4. Season highly with pepper and salt, and mix all well together.
5. The bag being perfectly clean and sweet, put in the ingredients.
6. Press out the air, sew it up, and boil it for three hours.

title: Hair Grease
body:
## Ingredients

- lard, a quarter of a pound
- salad oil, three tablespoonfuls
- palm oil, enough to give it a colour
- white wax, a little

## Directions

1. Dissolve a quarter of a pound of lard in a basin of boiling water
2. When cold, strain off the water and squeeze the lard dry in a cloth
3. Melt it in a pipkin
4. Mix well with it three tablespoonfuls of salad oil and enough palm oil to give it a colour
5. When cold, or nearly so, scent it and put it into pots
6. A little white wax may be added to make it thicker or stiffer

title: Half Pound Plain Loaf
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of flour
- two tea-spoonfuls of Yeatman's powder
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- four or five table-spoonfuls of water

## Directions

1. Work this as above, reserving the baking powder to the last
2. Set the dough in a tin, or form it in the well-known cottage shape
3. Bake

title: Halua of Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- sugar
- ghee
- a little powdered garam-masala
- very little saffron

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and ghee in equal quantities, each being double of the quantity of eggs
2. Add a little powdered garam-masala and very little saffron
3. Heat the mass stirring constantly till it begins to form red crystals
4. Take it down and serve

title: Halua of Ginger
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of ginger
- water
- flour
- 1/2 seer of ghee
- 3 powas of sugar

## Directions

1. Skin 1/2 seer of ginger, cut them in pieces and boil in about 1/2 seer of water till the water is reduced to 1/2 powa or less.
2. Then make a paste of the boiled pieces and mix with 1 1/2 powa of flour and the water in which they were boiled.
3. Fry this mass with about 1/2 seer of ghee and then pour in a syrup of 3 powas of sugar.
4. Stir constantly and take down when thick.

title: Halua of Green Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mature yet green mangoes
- water
- cleansed raisins
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel a few mature yet green mangoes, take out the edible portions and throw away the stones
2. Thrash them thoroughly with a stone and keep in water for about half an hour
3. Mix them thoroughly and drain out the water
4. Wash them again with fresh water
5. Boil them with a little water
6. Add cleansed raisins and salt as required
7. When they are boiled add sugar equal to half the quantity of mangoes
8. Take down when the mass is like a stiff dough
9. Serve after a day

title: Halua of Green Papaws
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two big green papaws
- 1/2 seer of papaw pieces
- aniseeds
- cardamom seeds
- 1/2 powa of ghee
- 1/2 powa or 1 chhatak of cleansed raisins
- salt
- 3 chhataks of sugar
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Peel one or two big green papaws and cut in pieces throwing away the seeds
2. Boil 1/2 seer of the pieces in water and when fully boiled drain out the water
3. Make a paste of the boiled pieces as far as practicable
4. Fry a few aniseeds and cardamom seeds in about 1/2 powa of ghee and then add the paste, 1/2 powa or 1 chhatak of cleansed raisins and salt as required
5. A little later add 3 chhataks of sugar and the juice of a lemon
6. Take down when the mass is like a stiff dough

title: Halua of Milk
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of milk
- 1 powa of sugar-candy
- 1/2 powa of ghee
- 1/2 powa of almonds

## Directions

1. Boil the milk on mild heat.
2. Add the syrup and ghee to it in four instalments at intervals.
3. Last of all add the pasted almonds.
4. Take down when thick.
5. Stir all along to prevent the formation of cream.

title: Halua of Moog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of pulses of moog
- water
- 1 powa of ghee
- a few aniseeds
- 1 1/2 powa of milk
- 1 powa of sugar
- cardamom seeds
- raisins
- almonds
- pistachios
- camphor

## Directions

1. Keep 1/2 seer of pulses of moog in water for 3 or 4 hours and then make a paste of them.
2. Heat 1 powa of ghee with a few aniseeds and when the bubbles die out fry the paste with it stirring constantly to prevent the mass becoming hard.
3. Then add 1 1/2 powa of milk, 1 powa of sugar, pinch of powdered cardamom, raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios.
4. Take down when thick and mix with very little camphor.

title: Halua of Papaws
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe papaws
- ghee
- milk
- sugar
- raisins
- almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Peel a few ripe papaws, remove the seeds, etc., and make a liquid-like paste of them.
2. Strain it through a very fine piece of linen if necessary.
3. Heat a little ghee and when the bubbles die out add the paste of papaws and stir constantly.
4. A little later add adequate quantities of milk, sugar, pastes of raisins, almonds and pistachios.
5. Take down when the mass begins to crystallise when properly thick.

title: Halua of Raisins
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of raisins
- 3 chhataks of ghee
- a few cassia leaves
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- cardamom seeds
- camphor

## Directions

1. Remove the stems of 1/2 seer of raisins and wash them.
2. Heat 3 chhataks of ghee with a few cassia leaves and when the bubbles die out fry the raisins with the ghee till they begin to melt.
3. When they begin to melt pour in a syrup of 1/2 seer of sugar and stir constantly.
4. Take down when thick and mix with the seeds of a few cardamoms and very little camphor.

title: Halua or Mohon-Bhog with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa middlings
- 1/2 powa ghee
- liquid contents of 8 ducks' eggs
- 1/2 powa sugar
- very little saffron
- powdered garam-masalla
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Brown 1 powa of middlings with 1/2 powa of ghee and cool them a little.
2. Add the liquid contents of 8 ducks' eggs beaten with 1/2 powa of sugar and very little saffron.
3. Heat on mild charcoal fire.
4. Add powdered garam-masalla and very little camphor when the mass is thick, and stir on till the desired thickness is reached.
5. Egg-halua is now ready to be served.

title: Halua Ordinary
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of ghee
- a few cassia leaves
- 1 powa of middlings
- 1/2 powa of raisins
- 1 powa of sugar
- 1 1/2 powa of water
- almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Heat 1/2 powa of ghee with a few cassia leaves and when the bubbles die out fry 1 powa of middlings and 1/2 powa of raisins with the ghee till brown.
2. Then add 1 powa of sugar and stirring for about a minute pour in 1 1/2 powa of water.
3. Add pieces of almonds and pistachios if desired.
4. Stir constantly and take down when thick.

title: Halua-Papru
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 5/8 seer of fine flour
- 1/2 seer of boiled milk
- 2 seers of sugar
- 1 seer or more of ghee
- a pinch of saffron
- seeds of a few cardamoms
- raisins
- a paste or pieces of pistachios

## Directions

1. Mix 5/8 seer of fine flour with 1/2 seer of boiled milk and heat
2. Stir constantly
3. After a time add a concentrated syrup of 2 seers of sugar
4. When partly thick add 1 seer or more of ghee little by little
5. Last of all add a pinch of saffron, the seeds of a few cardamoms, raisins and a paste or pieces of pistachios as desired
6. Take down when very thick
7. Pour in a flat dish
8. Cut in cubes when cold and solid

title: Ham with Madeira
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-boiled ham
- butter
- table-spoonful of Madeira

## Directions

1. Obtain a few nice slices of a good, well-boiled ham
2. Toss them in butter in a frying-pan, with a table-spoonful of Madeira
3. Pass them from the pan to the plate as hot as possible

title: Ham with Madeira
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of well-boiled ham
- butter
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Toss ham slices in butter in a frying-pan with a tablespoonful of Madeira
2. Pass the slices from the pan to the plate as hot as possible to accompany the turkey

title: Hare Patties, Financiere Fashion
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- best puff-paste
- hare
- Espagnole sauce
- oysters
- chicken
- rabbit
- tongue
- sweet-breads
- liver
- cocks-combs
- truffles
- mushrooms
- game

## Directions

1. Make enough of the best puff-paste to form eight or nine pastry cases of the usual patty shape
2. Bake them in the oven, and put them aside when done
3. As serving time approaches, fill each with a share of thoughtfully composed ragout a la financiere of hare
4. Heat in a quick oven for a minute or two, and serve

title: Hare Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- bacon
- forcemeat
- gravy beef
- spiced pepper
- pounded mace
- port
- vinegar
- red currant jelly

## Directions

1. Make like the rabbit pie exactly, with two slight variations
2. Mix a glass of port, half a glass of good vinegar, with a dessertspoonful of red currant jelly into the gravy
3. Sprinkle a little pounded mace over the meat in addition to the ordinary spiced pepper

title: Hare Soup (Puree)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- shin and a half of beef
- hare
- bacon or ham bone
- lean slices of bacon or ham
- anchovy
- Bombay onions
- carrots
- marjoram
- thyme
- parsley
- rind of limes
- pepper corns
- butter
- flour
- hare blood
- half pint of portwine
- red currant jelly
- crystal vinegar or lime juice

## Directions

1. Make your ordinary amount of stock from a shin and a half of beef
2. As the stock is being made, clean, and cut up the hare, saving the blood
3. Let the pieces soak for half an hour, or so, in cold water
4. Put the pieces of hare into a large stew-pan with a bacon, or ham bone, or a few lean slices of either, an anchovy, three Bombay onions cut into quarters, three carrots sliced, a bag containing a dessert-spoonful each of marjoram, thyme, and parsley, the rind of two limes, and a dozen pepper corns
5. Cover the whole with the stock
6. Let this simmer very gently for three hours, after it has once slowly attained boiling point
7. Strain the liquor from the meat, &c., and set it to get cool
8. Pick the meat out, and lay the pieces on a large dish
9. Choose the back fillets, pick the meat from the bones, and pound it to a paste in a mortar, moistening it with a little soup
10. Work it through the sieve, and save the pulp
11. Take another stew pan, place it on the fire, melt a couple of ounces of butter in it, and work into it a good table-spoonful of flour
12. Add a little soup with which the blood has been mixed, continue stirring, and add soup and pounded meat till you have exhausted the whole, stirring without ceasing
13. Add half a pint of portwine, a table-spoonful of red currant jelly, and a table-spoonful of crystal vinegar, or lime juice
14. Let the soup come to the boil, so as to thicken properly, and become thoroughly blended
15. Serve

title: Haricots Verts
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- French beans
- good butter
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Remember not to allow your cook to cut the beans into vermicelli-like strips
2. Peel off the external fibre which runs round the outside edge of the pod, leaving the pods so peeled intact
3. Put the beans into a roomy vessel with a table-spoonful of good butter, a dessert-spoonful of sugar, and a salt-spoonful of salt
4. Cover the vessel tightly and steam it for an hour in boiling water
5. When done, drain the bean pods, and serve them with a spoonful of melted maitre d'hotel butter or a coffee-cupful of poulette sauce

title: Harira
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ seer fine flour
- ½ seer ghee
- 1 seer milk
- 2½ powas sugar
- powdered cloves
- saffron
- raisins
- pieces of almonds
- pieces of pistachios

## Directions

1. Fry ½ seer of fine flour with ½ seer of ghee till brown
2. Add a seer of milk
3. When it boils add a very concentrated syrup of 2½ powas of sugar
4. Add a pinch of powdered cloves and very little saffron
5. Raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios may be added as desired

title: Harira
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 seer of fine flour
- 1/4 seer of ghee
- 1 seer of milk
- 2 1/2 powas of sugar
- a pinch of powdered cloves
- very little saffron
- raisins
- pieces of almonds and pistachios

## Directions

1. Fry 1/4 seer of fine flour with 1/4 seer of ghee till brown
2. Add a seer of milk and when it boils add a very concentrated syrup of 2 1/2 powas of sugar
3. Add a pinch of powdered cloves and very little saffron
4. Add raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios as desired
5. Stir constantly throughout the whole preparation
6. Take down when very thick

title: Hash of Cold Roast Hare
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast hare
- large onion
- pepper-corns
- celery
- any vegetable
- spice
- any sauce that may have been left
- a couple of glasses of red wine
- water
- butter and flour
- a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- half a glass of vinegar
- a little more red wine

## Directions

1. Trim off as much of the meat as you can find in slices
2. Cut out what remains of the stuffing
3. Break up all the bones and put them with the skin and scraps into a stew-pan
4. Add a large onion cut up, pepper-corns, a bit of celery if possible, any vegetable, a little spice, any sauce that may have been left, a couple of glasses of red wine and enough water to cover the bones
5. Simmer these ingredients for an hour and a half
6. Strain off the gravy
7. Thicken it with butter and flour
8. Flavour it with a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, half a glass of vinegar, and a little more red wine
9. Heat up the slices of hare in this sauce taking care that they do not boil
10. Serve with the stuffing sliced and fried in butter as a garnish

title: Hen Turkey Braised with Vegetables
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hen turkey
- gravy
- white wine
- onions
- carrot
- turnip
- French beans
- peas
- head of celery
- potatoes a la Duchesse
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Braise a hen turkey in gravy and a little white wine, with a couple of onions, a carrot, a turnip, a handful of French beans, a breakfast-cupful of peas and a head of celery
2. When done, dish the turkey
3. Strain the gravy
4. Thicken it, and cut up the vegetables into dice
5. Add them to the gravy again
6. Heat this in a sauce-pan quickly, and pour it over the bird
7. Serve with potatoes a la Duchesse and a cauliflower

title: Hilsa Fish Boiled in Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hilsa fish
- salt
- powdered mustard
- turmeric
- mustard oil
- green chillies
- banana leaves

## Directions

1. Do not wash the fish after cutting it to pieces, wash after flaking
2. Rub the pieces of fish with salt, powdered mustard, turmeric, a quantity of mustard oil and a few green chillies cut lengthwise
3. Wrap them up with banana leaves and boil
4. Tender leaves of gourd or pumpkin may be substituted for banana leaves and may be eaten with fish
5. Or, break the stone of a half-ripe cocoa-nut in two and drain off the water
6. Stuff it with the pieces of fish rubbed with the spices, etc.
7. Join the two parts with flour-paste and heat uniformly on all sides on charcoal fire
8. Or, wrap the pieces, after rubbing them with spices, etc., with banana leaves and put the bundle in the middle of a mass of hot boiled rice whose starchy water has just been drained off
9. Take out after 15 or 20 minutes
10. Or, put the pieces in a shallow pot after preparing them
11. Close it with a lid and place on boiling water

title: Hilsa Fish Gravy Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- hilsa fish
- salt
- tamarind acid or amchoor (sliced green mango dried in salt)

## Directions

1. Throw away the head and tail
2. Cut the fish into slices of rather more than half an inch thick
3. Wash the slices in several waters with salt to rid them of all 'besine' before currying
4. Cook with the acid of tamarind or amchoor as an improvement

title: Hilsa Fish Roasted with Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pasted ginger
- chillies
- turmeric
- garam-masala
- salt
- minced meat
- oil
- boiled potatoes
- powdered pepper
- big sized hilsa fish
- mustard oil
- powdered turmeric
- mustard
- green chillies
- green banana leaves

## Directions

1. Mix pasted ginger, chillies, turmeric, garam-masala and salt with some minced meat and fry with oil
2. Flake a big sized hilsa fish, preferably without roes
3. Wash and remove the head, entrails and tail
4. Cut deep lengthwise on both sides of the fish
5. Smear it well with a paste of mustard oil, powdered turmeric, mustard, green chillies and salt
6. Stuff it full with the meat and stitch the opening
7. Wrap it up 8 or 10 fold with green banana leaves
8. Roast both sides alternately on glowing charcoal till the outermost leaves are burnt up
9. Remove the leaves and serve

title: Hilsa Fish Unfried with Soup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hilsa fish
- salt
- turmeric
- mustard
- water
- cassia leaves
- green chillies
- ginger

## Directions

1. Flake a fresh hilsa fish and remove the fins
2. Wash clean so that it does not require any more washing after being cut in pieces
3. Smear the pieces with salt, pasted turmeric and enough pasted mustard
4. Boil water with cassia leaves, green chillies cut in halves and pasted ginger
5. Add the fish mixed with spices
6. Take down after properly boiling
7. Keep thin soup

title: Hilsa Fish with Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of hilsa fish
- salt
- mustard
- turmeric
- chillies
- mustard oil
- half-ripe cocoa-nut
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Smear some pieces of hilsa fish with adequate quantities of salt, mustard, turmeric, chillies, all powdered, and sufficient mustard oil.
2. Stuff the stone of a half-ripe cocoa-nut breaking it in two and join the two parts water-tight with a little paste of flour.
3. Boil it for an hour.
4. Then open it, take the lump out and serve.

title: Homard a l'Americaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tin of lobster
- Bombay onion
- ounce of butter
- sherry glass of chablis or sauterne
- breakfast-cupful of rich thick brown sauce
- breakfast-cupful of tomato puree
- Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Open a tin of lobster
2. Choose all the larger pieces and put all the mashed fragments aside to be used in bouchees or croquettes for some other meal
3. Wash and drain the firm pieces, dry them, and cut them up into quarter inch collops
4. Pile them in the centre of a dish that will stand the oven
5. Cut up a good sized Bombay onion and fry it in an ounce of butter
6. Add off the fire a sherry glass of chablis or sauterne
7. When the onion seems cooked, stir in a breakfast-cupful of rich thick brown sauce and the same quantity of tomato puree
8. Add a strong suspicion of Nepaul pepper and reduce the mixture for five minutes
9. When nice and thick, pour the sauce over the lobster
10. Put the dish into the oven and when thoroughly hot, serve

title: Home-Cured Tongue
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue of from three to four pounds weight
- best bazar salt (one and a half lb)
- saltpetre (one oz)
- bay-salt (one oz)
- spiced pepper (one oz)
- moist sugar (five oz)
- juice of three limes
- sherry-glass of good vinegar
- cold water (one pint and a half)

## Directions

1. Cleanse and wipe the tongue thoroughly
2. Rub the tongue with the bazar salt and spiced pepper, expending about a quarter of a pound of salt
3. Put the tongue aside and let it drain for the rest of the day to get rid of any blood
4. Take a roomy enamelled sauce-pan and put into it the remaining bazar salt (about a pound), the bay-salt, saltpetre, the lime juice, vinegar, and a pint and a half of cold water
5. Boil these over a low fire, removing all scum as it rises
6. When clear of scum and well boiled, set the liquor to get cold
7. Make a separate syrup with the sugar, diluting it with water in a small sauce-pan, and heating it gently till free from scum and smooth
8. Let the syrup get cold
9. Amalgamate the salt liquor and syrup with a wooden spoon
10. Pour the completed brine into the tub
11. After the tongue has been drained for six or eight hours, place it in the brine in the evening
12. Let it remain for a couple of days, being frequently turned over and basted during that period
13. On the evening of the second day's soaking, drain the tongue from the brine
14. Hang it to dry in the smoke of the kitchen fire for a couple of days
15. Wrap it in paper during the smoking stage
16. Soak it a little before cooking in cold water

title: Home-Made Cream Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a breakfast-cupful of rich cream
- a tea-spoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Mix a tea-spoonful of salt with a large breakfast-cupful of rich cream
2. Stir it well
3. Pour the cream into a slop basin in which a clean piece of soft linen has been laid
4. When the cream has been thus turned into the cloth, draw the ends of the cloth together, holding the cream as it were in a bag
5. Tie it tightly with string
6. Hang the bag in a cool place to drip
7. When the dripping of moisture from the bag ceases, the cheese is ready
8. Take the bag down, turn the contents out into a clean cloth
9. Mould it into a circular form, or shape it in a neat square
10. Serve it on a dish garnished with green leaves
11. Let it stand in the ice-box till wanted
12. Use a porous sort of cloth for the operation, so as to encourage the escape of the whey from the cheese
13. It is sometimes advisable to change the cloth during the draining process

title: Horse-Radish Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cupful of root shavings from the moringa or drum-stick tree
- half a pint of chicken broth
- the yolks of three eggs
- a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- a very little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape as finely as you can a cupful of the root shavings.
2. Simmer them in half a pint of chicken broth.
3. When done, thicken the broth custard-wise with the yolks of three eggs beaten up with a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar.
4. Add pepper, salt, and a very little grated nutmeg, and serve in a sauce-boat.

title: Horseradish Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- finely-scraped horseradish
- vinegar
- cayenne
- black pepper
- celery-seeds
- bruised onions

## Directions

1. To three ounces of finely-scraped horseradish add a quart of vinegar and a drachm of cayenne, some black pepper and celery-seeds, and one ounce of bruised onions
2. After eight or ten days filter the vinegar
3. It will serve as an excellent relish for cold beef, salads, and for the preparation of mustard

title: Hot Sweet Mango Chutnee
body:
## Ingredients

- A hundred green mangoes
- the syrup of four pounds of sugar and three quarts of vinegar
- four pounds of tamarinds, stoned and strained
- three quarts of vinegar
- eight or ten bay-leaves
- one pound of ground chilies
- two pounds of sliced ginger
- one pound of cloves of garlic
- one pound of raisins
- two pounds of salt

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the mangoes into fine slices, and steep them in salt for twenty-four to thirty-six hours
2. Remove the mangoes from the salt water, and boil them in three quarts of vinegar
3. When quite cool, lay them in a preserving-pan
4. Sprinkle over them the remaining salt, add all the condiments, tamarinds, raisins, &c.
5. Allow the whole to simmer for half an hour, stirring all the time, with the syrup
6. It should not be bottled until quite cold

title: Hot Tartare Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of melted butter
- an ounce of minced onion
- an ounce of chopped carrot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- one of garden cress
- one clove of garlic
- claret-glass of tarragon vinegar
- claret-glass of water or broth
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- one of sugar
- a large spoonful of mustard

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of melted butter an ounce of minced onion with an ounce of chopped carrot, a dessert-spoonful of parsley, one of garden cress, and one clove of garlic
2. When of a golden hue, add equal parts (claret-glass each) of tarragon vinegar, and water or broth, a tea-spoonful of salt, and one of sugar
3. add a large spoonful of mustard
4. strain, and serve hot

title: Household Curry-Stuff
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of turmeric
- 8 lbs. of coriander-seed
- 2 lbs. of cummin-seed
- 1 lb. of poppy-seed
- 2 lbs. of fenugreek
- 1 lb. of dry-ginger
- 1/2 lb. of mustard-seed
- 1/2 lb. of dried chillies
- 1 lb. of black pepper corns

## Directions

1. Do not be alarmed at the quantity

title: Hubab Curry
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender mutton or veal
- bacon
- white onion
- green ginger
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- curry sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into thickish pieces, about an inch square and half an inch thick.
2. Cut out of some slices of good bacon some pieces an inch square also, but about a quarter the thickness of the mutton.
3. Cut up some pieces of parboiled white onion upon the same pattern as the bacon, and some thin slices of green ginger to match.
4. Impale these mixed pieces upon small plated or silver skewers, or upon thinly-cut wooden ones, maintaining the order: first a piece of mutton, then a piece of bacon, then a bit of onion, and lastly, the thin slice of green ginger.
5. Repeat this until the skewer is filled, then go on with another.
6. Lightly toss the hubabs in butter in a saute-pan with an onion sliced, a tea-spoonful of salt, and one of sugar.
7. Simmer the hubabs in a good curry sauce as recommended for chicken curry.

title: Hulluah
body:
## Ingredients

- half a seer of soojee
- one seer of water
- half a seer of sugar
- three chittacks or six ounces of ghee
- a few white cardamoms
- a few small sticks of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Steep half a seer of soojee in one seer of water for twelve hours, or, if the hulluah be made in the winter, let it soak for eighteen hours
2. Strain the milk of soojee through a coarse duster, rejecting only such impurities as remain unstrained
3. Add to the milk half a seer of sugar
4. Boil it, stirring it all the time, and as it thickens add three chittacks or six ounces of ghee, warmed with a few white cardamoms and a few small sticks of cinnamon
5. Continue stirring it

title: Hulluah Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- half a seer of soojee
- half a seer of ghee
- half a seer of sugar
- half a seer of almonds
- half a seer of raisins
- a few white cardamoms
- sticks of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of the sugar and set it aside
2. Roast or brown the soojee and set it aside
3. Melt the ghee and fry the soojee with the spices in it
4. Put in the almonds and raisins, stirring all the time
5. Last of all add the syrup and continue to cook and stir it until it thickens
6. Remove into moulds or shapes while hot

title: Hunter's Beef, or Spiced Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- round of beef
- large quantities of finely-powdered pepper and hot spices
- saltpetre
- salt
- suckee
- lemon-rind
- juice of lemon
- two tablespoonfuls of finely-powdered spices and pepper
- skins of fat
- strong coarse pie-crust

## Directions

1. Corn a round of beef as per recipe No. 123 with the addition of large quantities of finely-powdered pepper and hot spices
2. Rub some of the pepper and spice well in with the saltpetre
3. Puncture the beef well the whole time with a needle to insure the saltpetre and spices penetrating
4. After the dry saltpetre and spices have been well rubbed in, prepare a mixture of salt, saltpetre, suckee, lemon-rind, pepper, and spice
5. Rub in one-fourth of the mixture, continuing to puncture the beef
6. Add subsequently to the brine the juice of lemon
7. Observe closely all the instructions given in recipe No. 123
8. On the seventh day remove the beef from the brine
9. Rub it well with two tablespoonfuls of finely-powdered spices and pepper
10. Inclose it thoroughly in skins of fat, and then in a strong coarse pie-crust
11. Bake it in a good oven, preferably a baker's oven

title: Hussanee Beef Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef
- 1.5 chittacks ghee
- 1.5 teaspoonful of salt
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of turmeric
- half a teaspoonful of ginger
- half a cupful of water
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one teaspoonful of chilies
- half a cupful of tyre or dhye
- finely-sliced ginger
- small curry onions

## Directions

1. Cut up two pounds of beef into small squares not exceeding one inch, and pass them on the silver or bamboo pins alternately with half an onion and a slice of ginger.
2. Warm the ghee and brown the ground condiments.
3. Put in the sticks of meat, and brown, stirring the whole.
4. Add the tyre and a little water, and allow to simmer over a slow fire for nearly two hours, when the curry will be ready.
5. Serve up on a curry-dish without removing the sticks.

title: Iced Apricot Cream Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- finger biscuits or slices of sponge cake
- jam
- cold custard
- liqueur
- half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
- chopped crystallized apricots
- coffee-cupful of rich cream

## Directions

1. Make a cake case with finger biscuits or slices of sponge cake and jam and cold custard
2. Flavour it with a dash of liqueur and mix into it half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
3. Work the mixture in the ice pail until it begins to freeze well
4. Stir into it a good allowance of chopped crystallized apricots and a coffee-cupful of rich cream well whipped
5. Mix well in the ice pail and then fill a mould with the frozen mixture
6. Completely cover it with lumps of ice
7. Leave it thus for an hour
8. Turn it out and serve

title: Iced Pudding with Cherries
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- preserved cherries
- liqueur-glass of kirsch

## Directions

1. Make a rich custard, retaining the pale yellow colour.
2. Add the preserved cherries to the partly frozen custard, with a liqueur-glass of kirsch.
3. In all other respects follow the directions given in Menus No. I and III.

title: Iced Rice a l'Imperiale
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of the best rice
- a pint and a half of good boiling milk
- six ounces of sugar
- a coffee-cupful of boiling cream
- mixed dessert fruits (greengages, cherries, apricots)
- preserved ginger
- citron
- a table-spoonful of maraschino or noyeau
- eight eggs
- two whites of egg
- two ounces of sugar
- a coffee-cupful of whipped cream
- a liqueur-glass of liqueur

## Directions

1. Gently boil a quarter of a pound of the best rice till about half done, then drain it
2. Put the rice into a stew-pan with a pint and a half of good boiling milk, and six ounces of sugar
3. Add a coffee-cupful of boiling cream, stir well, and simmer till the rice is done
4. Let it get cold
5. Mince up some mixed dessert fruits, greengages, cherries, apricots, &c., (preserved ginger, and citron if you like)
6. Moisten the fruit mince with a table-spoonful of maraschino or noyeau and put the mince away upon a plate
7. Strain the milk and cream from the rice, add milk enough to fill the mould you have chosen, and turn it to a rich custard with eight eggs
8. Set the freezing pail in the ice, put the custard into it, and work it with the spatula till nearly frozen
9. Make meringue with two whites of egg: put two ounces of sugar into an enamelled sauce-pan, and heat it with a little water till nearly boiling
10. Whip two whites of egg, and add them to the sugar, whipping all the time
11. Add the rice and meringue to the nearly frozen custard
12. Continue working the spatula, and when the mixture is all but frozen, add a coffee-cupful of whipped cream, the minced fruit, and a liqueur-glass of liqueur
13. Stir well, freeze a little longer, then fill your ice mould, and bury it in ice until it is required

title: Imitative Bears' Grease
body:
## Ingredients

- hogs' lard, eight ounces
- flowers of benzoin, one-eighth of an ounce
- palm oil, one-eighth of an ounce

## Directions

1. Melt together hogs' lard and flowers of benzoin and palm oil until combined
2. Stir until cold
3. Scent at pleasure

title: Imperial Pop
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces cream of tartar
- one ounce bruised ginger
- one pound and a half white sugar
- one ounce lemon-juice
- one gallon and a half boiling water
- two tablespoonfuls yeast

## Directions

1. Pour the boiling water on the cream of tartar, ginger, sugar, and lemon-juice
2. Add two tablespoonfuls of yeast
3. Mix
4. Bottle and tie down the corks as usual

title: Indian Corn à l'Américaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Indian corn or maize, young
- melted butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Strip corn from the young pod
2. Boil like peas
3. Drain thoroughly
4. Toss in melted butter
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Serve with plenty of butter

title: Indian Gingerbread
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve ounces of pounded loaf sugar
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- one pound of dried flour
- two ounces of pounded ginger
- a quarter of an ounce of cloves
- a quarter of an ounce of cinnamon
- a small teacupful of water

## Directions

1. Mix the ginger and the spice with the flour
2. Put the sugar and a small teacupful of water into a saucepan
3. When it is dissolved add the butter
4. As soon as it is melted mix it with the flour and other things
5. Work it up, form the paste into cakes or nuts, and bake them upon tins

title: Indian Lemon Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- four chittacks or eight ounces of butter free of water
- six chittacks or twelve ounces of white sugar
- twelve fresh eggs
- four wineglassfuls of lemon or lime juice
- four tablespoonfuls of finely-grated bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Mix the butter and the sugar
2. Add the yolks of the eggs
3. Add the lime-juice and bread-crumbs
4. When the oven is ready add the whites of the eggs well beaten up
5. Put the whole into a buttered pudding-dish
6. Bake it immediately

title: Indian Mode of Preparing Perfumed Oils
body:
## Ingredients

- flowers
- til or sesamum seed, wetted or damped

## Directions

1. Place on a large tray a layer of the flowers, about four inches thick and two feet square
2. Put some of the til or sesamum seed, wetted or damped, about two inches thick on this
3. Place another layer of flowers, four inches thick on top
4. Cover the whole with a sheet, held down by weights at the sides
5. Allow to remain for eighteen hours
6. Remove the flowers and replace by layers of fresh flowers
7. Repeat the operation three times, each layer of fresh flowers being allowed to remain eighteen hours
8. After the last process, take the seeds in their swollen state and place them in a clean mill
9. Express the oil, which will possess the scent of the flowers
10. Keep in prepared skins, called dubbers

title: Indian Ortolans
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cholum birds
- bacon
- vine leaf
- tape
- buttered toast
- water cress
- sauce bigarade

## Directions

1. Truss the cholum birds, put a slice of bacon inside them
2. Wrap a vine leaf round each bird and a piece of bacon over the vine leaf
3. Tie the leaf and bacon in their place with a tape
4. Roast over a brisk fire
5. Serve each little bird on a square of hot buttered toast, garnished with water cress
6. Sauce bigarade should go round in a boat

title: Indian Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- three well-beaten eggs
- a pint of new milk
- three tablespoonfuls of flour
- some sugar
- a little pounded cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Add to three well-beaten eggs a pint of new milk, three tablespoonfuls of flour, some sugar, and a little pounded cinnamon
2. Mix all well together
3. Fry in butter
4. Brown the upper side for a minute before the fire
5. Serve it, cut into four, with pounded sugar strewed over it

title: Indian Way of Making Calf's-Foot Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve large or full-sized calves'-feet
- one pound or half a seer of sugar
- eight limes
- two oranges
- half a dozen blades of lemon-grass
- one tablespoonful of mixed spices
- six eggs
- a handful of isinglass
- a claretglassful of sherry

## Directions

1. Thoroughly wash the feet, break them up and boil them
2. Allow all the meat to dissolve over a slow fire
3. Skim away every particle of fat
4. Strain the liquid through a coarse napkin
5. Add the sugar, all the hot spices, and the rinds of two lemons and one orange
6. Simmer the whole for some time
7. Squeeze in the juice of the eight limes and the two oranges, together with the isinglass and lemon-grass
8. When it begins to thicken strain it
9. Reboil until it is reduced to the required quantity, skimming all the fat
10. Beat the whites of the six eggs to a good light froth
11. Add this to the jelly, and pour it from one pan into another several times, until it clears
12. Add the sherry and strain it through flannel, returning it quickly two or three times until it runs perfectly bright and clear
13. Fill into glasses or moulds before it congeals

title: Italian Cooking of Mutton
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat containing enough fat
- curd
- salt
- ghee
- pastes of ginger
- onions
- garlics
- chillies
- coriander
- garam-masalla
- entire cumins
- broken chillies
- cassia leaves
- pieces of onions
- hot water
- fried pieces of almonds

## Directions

1. Mix curd, salt, ghee, pastes of ginger, onions, garlics, chillies, coriander and garam-masalla with pieces of meat, preferably from the thighs, weighing about 1 powa each
2. Keep covered for 2 or 3 hours
3. Boil enough ghee and fry some entire cumins, garam-masalla, broken chillies, cassia leaves and sufficient pieces of onions in it
4. Add the meat to these and fry for some time
5. Add hot water so that the meat is just boiled while no soup is left
6. Add a little ghee before taking down
7. Serve with fried pieces of almonds and onions

title: Jelly for Cooked Birds, Meats, or Made Dishes
body:
## Ingredients

- eight calves'-feet
- pepper
- salt
- two onions
- a head of celery
- two carrots
- three or four quarts of water
- a dozen cloves
- the juice and rind of a lemon
- a tablespoonful of soy or any other dark-coloured, rich, and well-flavoured sauce
- the whites of four eggs
- a wineglass of brown sherry
- flannel

## Directions

1. Boil down eight calves'-feet, with some pepper and salt, two onions, a head of celery, and two carrots, in three or four quarts of water
2. When perfectly boiled down strain it lightly without bruising the onions or carrots
3. Let it cool, and remove all the fat
4. With a dozen cloves and the juice and rind of a lemon, boil it again, adding a tablespoonful of soy or any other dark-coloured, rich, and well-flavoured sauce
5. Beat up to a light froth the whites of four eggs, and clear the soup or jelly
6. Add a wineglass of brown sherry, and run or drip it through flannel
7. Pour what you require over the ready cooked or dressed meat into moulds
8. Let the rest cool in some large flat dish, and cut it up small for garnishing the meat or bird when served up

title: Jelly of Green Mangoes and Other Fruits
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mature yet green mangoes
- sugar - 1.5 times the weight of the pulp
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks of a few mature yet green mangoes, wash and boil them.
2. Squeeze the boiled mangoes and strain the pulp through a clean piece of linen.
3. Mix it with 1.5 times its weight of sugar or add it to a boiling thick syrup of the sugar.
4. Stir constantly till the mass is thick when taking down mix with a little lemon-juice.

title: Jelly of Pine-Apples
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe pine-apples
- sugar - 1.5 times the weight of the juice
- powder of spice (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel a few very ripe pine-apples, cut in pieces, thrash them and strain the juice through a clean piece of linen.
2. Mix with 1.5 times its weight of sugar, or make a thick syrup of the sugar and pour in the juice.
3. Boil and stir till the mass is thick.
4. Then take down and if desired mix with a little powder of any spice.

title: Jelly with Fruit
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of gelatine
- three quarters of a pound of sugar
- three whites of eggs
- one quart of water
- the juice of one lime
- maraschino
- a wine-glass of maraschino
- preserved cherries
- strawberries
- apricots
- greengages
- raspberries
- iced cream en bloc

## Directions

1. Put two ounces of gelatine in an enamelled stew-pan with three quarters of a pound of sugar.
2. Beat three whites of eggs, moisten them with one quart of water and the juice of one lime.
3. Pour the whole into the stew-pan containing the gelatine.
4. Put it on the fire, whisking the liquid continually until it boils.
5. Take the pan from the fire, let the liquid cool, and strain it through a jelly bag, pouring it back and straining again until it is quite clear.
6. When cold, flavour the jelly with maraschino, a wine-glass will be sufficient.
7. Let it rest awhile.
8. Prepare a macedoine of fruits: preserved cherries, strawberries, apricots, greengages, and raspberries.
9. Fill the jelly mould as follows: first set it upon ice, and pour into it about one-eighth thickness of the jelly.
10. Arrange some fruit tastefully in this layer, cover it with jelly.
11. Continue layers of fruit and of jelly until the mould is nearly full.
12. Finish it with jelly only.
13. Cover the mould with ice, and turn it out when finally set and quite firm.
14. Iced cream en bloc may accompany.

title: Jerusalem Artichokes au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- cream
- pepper
- salt
- mild cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes until tender and drain
2. Make a puree of plain boiled artichoke, slightly diluted with cream, and seasoned with pepper, and salt
3. Turn into a well-buttered pie-dish
4. Dust the surface over with finely grated mild cheese
5. Bake until the top takes colour

title: Jerusalem Artichokes Baked with Cheese
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- cream or milk
- egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes till quite tender
2. Mash them with a silver fork, moistening them with cream, or milk with the yolk of an egg
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Place the artichoke in a buttered pie-dish, or in buttered coquilles
5. Give the surface a layer of grated cheese
6. Bake till it takes colour

title: Jerusalem Artichokes Boiled
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the artichokes, peel and shape them nicely, dropping each one into salt and water at once to prevent its turning black
2. When all are ready, put them into a saucepan with a gallon of cold water, and two table-spoonfuls of salt
3. Boil till tender (which will take about twenty minutes after boiling-point has been attained) and drain
4. Serve them with a nice sauce blanche

title: Jerusalem Artichokes Fried
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- batter
- fat

## Directions

1. Boil half a dozen large artichokes until three parts done
2. Cut into long strips about a quarter of an inch thick
3. Dip them in batter
4. Fry them a golden tint
5. Serve alone as an entremets, or pile them in a pyramid as the central garnish of an entree

title: Jetvervelee of Birds and Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight fat quails
- four sheep's tongues
- half a pound of lamb's liver
- couple of ounces of gravy meat
- bacon
- spiced pepper
- onion

## Directions

1. Buy eight fat quails, four sheep's tongues, half a pound of lamb's liver, and a couple of ounces of gravy meat
2. Stew the tongues
3. Bone the quails and throw the bones into the gravy with all scraps of bacon
4. Trim your cold sheep's tongues and cut eight nice fillets the size of a walnut from the centre of them
5. Place your boned-birds breast downwards on a board and dust them over with spiced pepper
6. Melt a couple of thick slices of fat bacon in a frying-pan
7. When melted, throw in three table-spoonfuls of the lamb's liver and those of the birds too, cut into dice, and one onion shredded finely
8. Cook the liver in this until done

title: Jhal Frezee
body:
## Ingredients

- cold mutton, beef, or veal
- a large quantity of sliced onions
- some chilies
- a teaspoonful of salt
- 2 ounces ghee

## Directions

1. Cut up into small squares, of less than an inch, either cold mutton, beef, or veal, rejecting the bones.
2. Add a large quantity of sliced onions, some chilies cut up, and a teaspoonful of salt.
3. Warm 2 ounces of ghee, and throw into it the meat, onions, chilies, and salt, and allow to simmer, or fry, stirring the whole while, until the onions are quite tender.

title: Jhal-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of husked pulses of grams
- pulses of grams
- chopped onions
- salt
- minced green chillies
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Moisten 1 powa of husked pulses of grams for about 6 hours and grind them to a paste.
2. Add a handful of pulses of grams, some chopped onions, salt and minced green chillies, and beat thoroughly.
3. Form small flat cakes and fry in plenty of boiling ghee or oil.

title: Jhuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big pieces of fish
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- oil
- ginger-juice
- minced chillies
- onions

## Directions

1. Smear powdered turmeric and salt with big pieces of fish and fry a little with oil
2. Break the pieces in small fragments while frying and remove the bones
3. Add ginger-juice, minced chillies and onions
4. Fry to crispness
5. Or, boil the pieces, remove the bones, mix with powdered or pasted chillies, onions, ginger, turmeric and salt, and fry in oil

title: Jhuribhaja
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- besam
- water
- salt
- powdered chillies
- boiling oil or ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare a thick solution of besam and water.
2. Add salt and powdered chillies, and beat thoroughly.
3. Drop it on boiling oil or ghee through a finely perforated ladle and fry till brown.

title: Jilapi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of water
- 1 powa of middlings
- 1 seer of sabeda
- a pinch of saffron
- water
- 0.5 seer or 3 powas of ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1 powa of water with 1 powa of middlings
2. Keep aside for 5 or 6 hours
3. Then mix with 1 seer of sabeda, a pinch of saffron and an adequate quantity of water making a thick paste
4. Boil 0.5 seer or 3 powas of ghee in a pan till vapour rises
5. Put the paste in a coconut shell or a linen bag with a small hole at the bottom
6. Drop it in the pan in a thin stream
7. Make suitable designs by manipulating the hand, the common one being a spiral of two circumferences crossed by one straight line
8. Fry both sides till hard and red
9. Take them out and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Jugged Fish
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish fillets
- pepper
- salt
- Bombay onion
- pepper corns
- cloves
- black pepper
- tomatoes
- curled parsley
- capsicums
- fish bones and trimmings
- chablis, sauterne, or hock
- garlic
- lime rind

## Directions

1. Trim the fish in fillets, season them with pepper and salt.
2. Cover the bottom of the tin with slices of Bombay onion, dotting in a dozen pepper corns, and two cloves.
3. Put a layer of fillets over the onion, and pepper them with black pepper.
4. Put in a layer of sliced tomatoes, sprinkle some roughly-chopped curled parsley over them, and a table-spoonful of chopped capsicums.
5. Pour in a little broth made from the fish bones and trimmings, with a glass of chablis, sauterne, or hock.
6. A clove of garlic may be introduced in this dish by those who appreciate it, and the fine rind of a lime also.
7. Cover the tin closely, and boil.

title: Jugged Hare
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- flour
- butter
- pint and a half of good strong stock
- two carrots
- two or three large onions
- an ounce of celery
- juice of two limes
- a table-spoonful of sugar
- salt
- a wine-glass of brandy
- a bumper glass of port
- a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a lump of butter rolled in flour
- stuffing as for roast hare

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the hare
2. Cut the hare up into neat pieces
3. Dredge them with flour
4. Give them a few turns in the frying-pan with some butter till they take colour
5. Prepare beforehand a pint and a half of good strong stock
6. Choose a vessel that you can close securely with paste
7. Put the pieces of hare into it, with two carrots, two or three large onions, an ounce of celery, the juice of two limes, a table-spoonful of sugar, and one of salt
8. Pour in the stock
9. Throw in a wine-glass of brandy
10. Seal the vessel as closely as possible
11. Place it in a pan of cold water
12. Set it to boil, steaming the covered pot for three hours
13. When done, open the pot
14. Stir into it a bumper glass of port, a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly, and a lump of butter rolled in flour
15. Serve with a dozen balls of stuffing made as for roast hare, and fried in butter
16. Alternatively, the jar may be placed in the oven and baked for two or three hours

title: Juice of Fruit Iced
body:
## Ingredients

- the juice of a pint of currants or raspberries, or other fruit preserved for tarts
- four or five ounces of pounded loaf sugar
- a little lemon-juice
- a pint of cream

## Directions

1. Press through a sieve the juice of a pint of currants or raspberries, or other fruit preserved for tarts
2. add to it four or five ounces of pounded loaf sugar, a little lemon-juice, and a pint of cream
3. It may be whisked previous to freezing, and a mixture of the juice in which the fruit was preserved may be used

title: Kabab
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish 1 seer
- ghee 0.5 powa
- curd 0.5 powa
- ginger 2.5 tolas
- coriander 2.5 tolas
- a few chillies
- cloves
- cardamoms
- 2 lemons
- 1 or 2 handfuls of besam

## Directions

1. Prepare the middle portion of a big fish
2. Make holes with a sharp point in it and cut in pieces
3. Rub them with besam and wash
4. Make a mixture of all the other ingredients after pasting or powdering them where necessary
5. Smear the pieces of fish with it
6. Fix the pieces closely at the tip of a pointed iron stick, with thread if necessary
7. Roast them till golden-brown keeping about a foot above the flame
8. Rotate the stick slowly and drop the mixture on them at intervals

title: Kabab
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- ginger paste
- onion paste
- garlic paste
- chilli paste
- minced mutton
- ghee
- besam of grams

## Directions

1. Mix salt, pastes of ginger, onions, garlic and chillies with minced mutton and keep aside for some time.
2. Mix it thoroughly with a little ghee and besam of grams to a stiff mass.
3. Form balls of it, attach them at the end of a pointed iron stick and roast on charcoal flame till brown dropping ghee on them at intervals.

title: Kachu-Sak Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender stalks of arum
- Ground pepper
- Cumin
- Coriander
- Turmeric
- Red chillies
- Salt
- Grams
- Oil
- Cumin seeds
- Kernel of cocoanut
- Milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Skin the tender stalks of arum and cut into pieces an inch long
2. Boil them and drain off the water
3. Add ground pepper, cumin, coriander, turmeric, red chillies and salt, and beat thoroughly
4. Fry this with a handful of moistened grams in oil heated with a few cumin seeds
5. Add some minced kernel of cocoanut and stir
6. Take down when the soup dries up
7. Add a little milk and a pinch of sugar if desired

title: Kachuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- sodium bicarbonate
- powders of fried pepper
- cumin
- garam-masala
- coriander
- minced meat
- ghee
- water
- flour
- boiled and pasted green peas
- cauliflowers

## Directions

1. Mix salt, a pinch of sodium bicarbonate, powders of fried pepper, cumin, garam-masala and coriander with minced meat and fry with ghee, adding a little water if necessary to boil the meat.
2. Knead some flour into a dough, make small balls of it, flatten, stuff with a little meat and make balls again.
3. Press them into small, round, flat discs of moderate thickness and fry in plenty of ghee.
4. Boiled and pasted green peas and cauliflowers may be mixed with the meat.

title: Kachuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- sodium bicarbonate
- powders of fried pepper
- cumin
- garam-masalla
- coriander
- minced meat
- ghee
- water
- flour
- boiled and pasted green peas
- cauliflowers

## Directions

1. Mix salt, a pinch of sodium bicarbonate, powders of fried pepper, cumin, garam-masalla and coriander with minced meat and fry with ghee, adding a little water if necessary to boil the meat.
2. Knead some flour into a dough, make small balls of it, flatten, stuff with a little meat and make balls again.
3. Press them into small, round, flat discs of moderate thickness and fry in plenty of ghee.
4. Boiled and pasted green peas and cauliflowers may be mixed with the meat.

title: Kachuri with Asafoetida
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- husked pulses of kalai
- water
- asafoetida
- entire aniseeds
- black cumin
- salt
- ghee

## Directions

1. Keep some husked pulses of kalai under water for two hours
2. Grind them to a paste
3. Mix thoroughly with a little asafoetida melted with water, entire aniseeds, black cumin and salt
4. Fry this mass with ghee for 15 to 20 minutes
5. Prepare kachuri as in Kachurl recipe stuffed with this new preparation

title: Kachurl
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulses of grams
- ghee
- entire aniseeds
- black cumin
- salt
- fish
- meat
- green peas
- almonds
- spices
- soda
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Keep some pulses of grams moist under water for two hours
2. Fry them with ghee and grind to a paste
3. Mix entire aniseeds, black cumin and salt with it
4. Fish, meat, green peas, almonds, fried with spices may also be mixed with it
5. Mix a pinch of soda, one of salt, and ghee with flour
6. Knead it to a dough with water
7. Make small balls of it
8. Press each ball with the fingers and stuff with a little of the pulse preparation
9. Make balls again
10. Press these stuffed balls into flat discs of moderate thickness
11. Fry in plenty of boiling ghee

title: Karola or Uch-Chhe
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- karolas
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut the karolas in thin slices and rub powdered turmeric and a little salt.
2. Heat some oil in a pan till the foams die out.
3. Add the slices a few at a time so that they may remain merged in oil.
4. Brown the other side by turning the slices over with any flat utensil like the wrong end of a spoon when one side is browned.
5. Use slow heat, or the slices will parch in the surface while the interior will remain uncooked.
6. Alternatively, slices of karolas may be first boiled in water and then fried to make them soft and not crisp.

title: Kasturo-Sandesh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of hard chhana
- 1/2 powa of almonds
- 3 powas of syrup of white sugar
- 1 chhatak of pieces of pistachios
- 4 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Mix pastes of hard chhana 1 seer and almonds 1/2 powa with syrup of white sugar 3 powas, and heat on fire.
2. Stir continuously and vigorously till sticky.
3. When taking off the fire add pieces of pistachios 1 chhatak and 4 drops of essence of rose, and stir as before till cold and hard.
4. Make balls of it and give any shape with the help of casts.

title: Kasundi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mature yet green mangoes
- 1 powa of salt
- 1 seer of mango pieces
- pasted ginger 1 chhatak
- powdered chillies 1½ chhataks
- powdered mustard 3 chhataks
- seedless ripe tamarind 1 chhatak
- salt 1 chhatak
- 1 powa of mustard oil

## Directions

1. Peel some mature yet green mangoes and cut them in thin long pieces throwing away the seeds if possible
2. Mix 1 powa of salt with 1 seer of such pieces and keep in the sun for a day or two till the pieces are quite soft and some juice is gathered at the bottom
3. Drain out the juice and mix the pieces with pasted ginger 1 chhatak, powdered chillies 1½ chhataks, powdered mustard 3 chhataks, seedless ripe tamarind 1 chhatak and salt 1 chhatak
4. Heat 1 powa of mustard oil in an aluminium pan, add the spiced pieces, mix thoroughly and take down
5. Preserve them when cold in an airtight stone, porcelain, glass or earthen jar

title: Kedgeree
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled rice
- chopped hard-boiled egg
- cold minced fish
- fresh butter
- pepper
- salt
- minced garden herb such as cress, parsley, or marjoram

## Directions

1. Toss together boiled rice, chopped hard-boiled egg, cold minced fish, and a lump of fresh butter in the frying-pan
2. Flavour with pepper, salt, and any minced garden herb such as cress, parsley, or marjoram
3. Serve smoking hot

title: Khasa Monda
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of hard chhana
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- khowa kheer 2 chhatak
- 1/2 to 1 chhatak of pieces of pistachios

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1 seer of hard chhana and drop it into a boiling syrup of 1/2 seer of sugar
2. Stir vigorously
3. After a time add khowa kheer 2 chhatak
4. Take down when the mass is very thick and gluey
5. Mix with 1/2 to 1 chhatak of pieces of pistachios
6. Make balls of it when cold and serve

title: Khechuri with Chinre (Flattened Rice)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Chinre |- seer
- Fried moog-dal |- seer
- Curd i powa
- Adequate quantities of ghee
- Spices
- Salt
- Vegetables
- A little tamarind
- Cassia leaves
- Red chillies
- Entire cumin
- Cauliflowers
- Potatoes
- Green peas
- Powdered turmeric
- Boiled dal
- Fried fish (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash chinre and mix them with curd, tamarind and powdered garam-masalla
2. Prepare moog-dal as in the alternative method, only do not make it so thick
3. Heat sufficient ghee and add cassia leaves, red chillies and a pinch of entire cumin
4. Add pieces of cauliflowers, potatoes, green peas and other vegetables according to taste when the red chillies turn black, and brown them
5. Then add powdered turmeric, salt and the boiled dal
6. Add chinre when the vegetables are boiled, and boil for a few minutes more
7. Add ghee and garam-masalla before taking down
8. Fried fish may be added if desired along with the boiled dal

title: Khechuri with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice |- seer
- Fried moog dal ^ seer
- Ghee i powa
- Onion i powa
- A dozen eggs
- Salt 2^ tolas
- Pepper ^ tola
- Coriander i tola
- Aniseed ^ tola
- Ginger i tola
- Garam-masalla ^ tola
- Turmeric |- tola
- Cumin ^ tola
- A few cassia leaves
- Red chillies
- Saffron (if desired)
- Akhni water prepared by boiling pepper, coriander, aniseed and a few cloves and cassia leaves (2 seers)

## Directions

1. Wash and dry the rice and pulse, and brown them lightly in |- powa of ghee
2. Pour in 2 seers of akhni water prepared by boiling the pepper, coriander, aniseed and a few cloves and cassia leaves
3. Fry the pieces of onions and add them with salt, pasted or powdered ginger, turmeric and cumin when the rice begin to boil
4. Add a little saffron if desired
5. Slow down the fire
6. Add the contents of several fresh eggs and stir incessantly so that they may not coagulate
7. Boil |- powa of ghee with cassia leaves and red chillies, and pour it in the khechuri when the boiling nears completion
8. Add powdered garam-masalla before taking down
9. Sprinkle some finely chopped onions while serving on the khechuri in every dish
10. If potatoes are to be added, cut them in pieces, brown a little, and add to the khechuri with the spices

title: Khechuri with Ful-Kapi (Cauliflower)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice ^ seer
- Fried moog-dal ^ seer
- Potato |- seer or more
- Onion i powa
- Ghee ^ powa
- Salt 2 tolas
- Ginger i tola
- Turmeric ^ tola
- Cumin ^ tola
- Pepper ^ tola
- Garam-masalla ^ tola
- A few cassia leaves
- Red chillies
- Cauliflower
- Entire grams

## Directions

1. Prepare khechuri exactly in the same process as in the case of Golapi-khechuri
2. Only add browned pieces of cauliflowers instead of onions or along with onions
3. Entire grams, after keeping them under water for a few hours, are generally browned and added to the khechuri along with the cauliflowers

title: Khechuri with Green Peas
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice 1/2 seer
- Dal prepared from fried golden-coloured moog 1 seer
- Green pea-grains 1 seer
- Ghee 1 seer
- Saffron 1 tola
- Salt 3 tolas
- Turmeric 1/2 tola
- Coriander 1 tola
- Pepper 1 tola
- Cumin 1 tola
- Garam-masalla 1/2 tola
- A few cassia leaves and red chillies
