- A little milk
- Onion 1/2 seer

## Directions

1. Wash the rice and pulse, and dry them separately.
2. Dissolve the saffron in a little milk and mix it with the rice.
3. Heat half the ghee in a pan, and fry the rice and pulse together in it for about 5 or 6 minutes.
4. Pour in water so that it stands 2 or 3 inches above the mixture of rice and pulse.
5. Put the lid on and cook on slow heat.
6. Add salt, turmeric, coriander, pepper and cumin, all powdered or pasted, and the green pea-grains when the rice are half-boiled.
7. Add water if necessary.
8. Take the pan down when the mass is fully boiled.
9. Heat the rest of the ghee in another pan, and fry the cassia leaves and the red chillies, broken in two, in it.
10. Add the finely chopped onions when the red chillies turn black.
11. Pour in the mass when the onions are properly browned, and stir.
12. Add powdered or pasted garam-masalla when the mass is thick.
13. Stir and take down.
14. Serve while hot.

title: Kheer Joshi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ powa fine flour
- ½ seer khowa kheer
- water
- ghee
- 2 seers milk
- raisins
- pasted almonds
- 1½ powa sugar
- cardamom seeds
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Mix ½ powa of fine flour with ½ seer of khowa kheer
2. Prepare threads of it as fine as possible rolling it little by little with the hands, adding a little water if necessary
3. Cut them in pieces of 1 inch in length
4. Fry them in plenty of ghee
5. Boil 2 seers of milk till it is reduced to half
6. Drop the fried pieces into it
7. Add raisins, pasted almonds and 1½ powa of sugar
8. Stir at intervals and take down when thick after mixing with cardamom seeds and 3 or 4 drops of essence of rose

title: Kheer Mohan
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer hard chhana
- essence of rose
- powdered cardamoms
- khowa kheer
- sugar syrup
- white sugar crystals

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana and make balls of it
2. Stuff each of them with a drop of essence of rose, a pinch of powdered cardamoms and a little khowa kheer
3. Slightly flatten them
4. Drop them in a boiling syrup of sugar
5. Take them out when they are a little brownish in colour
6. Roll on white sugar crystals
7. Serve when cool

title: Kheer of Grapes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of grapes
- water
- 1 powa of sugar
- 3 or 4 egg yolks
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Wash 1 seer of grapes, remove the stems and boil with a little water.
2. Then crush them with the hands in the water bringing out the juice.
3. Throw away the skins and seeds.
4. Heat the juice.
5. Add 1 powa of sugar when it boils.
6. Add the heated yolks of 3 or 4 eggs when much reduced.
7. Take down when thick and mix with 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose.
8. Serve when cool.

title: Kheer of Tal
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet and ripe tal fruit
- 1 seer of milk
- 1/2 seer of tal fruit pulp
- raisins
- almonds
- pistachios
- cardamom seeds
- aniseeds
- camphor
- bread

## Directions

1. Skin a sweet and ripe tal fruit and separate the seeds.
2. Beat each a little with the hands and rub against a sieve or a small basket of bamboo or iron placing a plate beneath it.
3. The pulp of the fruit will be collected on the plate.
4. The pulp may be used now as it is but it tastes better if it is mixed with a little water and held high in a bag of linen till the whole of the water is drained off in drops.
5. Heat 1 seer of milk on very mild heat till it is reduced to 3 powas when add 1/2 seer of the pulp and stir constantly.
6. Take down when the mass is thick and gluey after mixing with raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios, powders of cardamoms and aniseeds 1/2 tola each and very little camphor.
7. Serve when cool with bread.

title: Kheer Ordinary
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of khowa kheer
- 1 seer of milk
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- almonds
- pistachios
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer of khowa kheer and drop it into 1 seer of boiling milk.
2. Add 1 1/2 powa of sugar and stir constantly till the mass is thick when taking down mix with some pasted almonds, some pieces of pistachios and 3 or 4 drops of essence of rose.
3. Serve when cool.

title: Kheer with Oranges
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen sweet and ripe oranges
- 1 seer milk
- pasted almonds
- ½ powa sugar
- essence of rose

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen sweet and ripe oranges and further skin them separating the grains
2. Squeeze the grains in a bag of linen bringing out the juice
3. Boil 1 seer of milk till it is reduced to 1½ powa
4. Mix with the juice of the oranges, some pasted almonds and ½ powa of sugar
5. Take down when thick and mix with 2 drops of essence of rose

title: Kheerela
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seers of milk
- sugar
- cardamom seeds
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil 2 seers of milk.
2. Add a table spoonful of sugar when half reduced.
3. Stir the more carefully and vigorously the more it is thick.
4. Take down when the milk becomes a stiff paste.
5. Scrape together the paste that sticks to the sides of the pan and beat thoroughly to a fine muddy one.
6. Prepare finger-like round and long forms stuffed with a few seeds of cardamom and fry in plenty of ghee till reddish brown.

title: Khuttah Carree, or Acid Vegetable Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- small quantities of potatoes
- kutchoo or artichoke
- sweet potatoes or suckercund
- carrots
- red and white pumpkins
- tomatoes
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one teaspoonful of roasted and ground coriander-seed
- two large cups of tamarind water
- jaggry
- fresh chilies
- one chittack or two ounces of mustard oil
- one teaspoonful of coUinga or onion-seeds

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetables into large pieces and boil in water with the ground onions, turmeric, chilies, garlic, and coriander-seed
2. Prepare two large cups of tamarind water slightly sweetened with jaggry and strain through a sieve
3. Add the strained water to the boiled vegetables with a few fresh chilies
4. Melt one chittack or two ounces of mustard oil in a separate pot
5. While the oil is bubbling, fry in it a teaspoonful of the coUinga
6. When sufficiently fried pour it over the boiled vegetables including the tamarind water
7. Close up the pot and allow it to simmer for fifteen to twenty minutes
8. Serve cold

title: Kid Country Captain
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces ghee
- half a teaspoonful ground chilies
- one teaspoonful salt
- a quarter of a teaspoonful ground turmeric
- twenty onions
- half a cup of water

## Directions

1. Before cutting up the kid, a four-quarter, let it be partially broiled or roasted, and then make it into country captain in accordance with the above directions.
2. Or, instead of partially roasting the kid, add half a cup of water to assist the meat to dissolve.

title: Kid Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- a hind-quarter or a fore-quarter of kid
- ground onions
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- ground ginger
- ground garlic
- ghee
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the kid into sixteen or eighteen pieces
2. Take condiments in the proportion given in recipe No. 16
3. Cook it in every particular the same as the chicken curry
4. Allow it to simmer three-quarters of an hour

title: Kid Roasted Whole
body:
## Ingredients

- whole kid with its head on
- stuffing
- beef suet melted down
- hot water
- salt
- lime

## Directions

1. Bespeak from a butcher a whole kid, with its head on
2. Prepare a stuffing as per recipe No. 323 or 325, and after cleaning the kid, stuff into it the stuffing
3. Break the joints of the legs, and fold and truss them like a pig
4. Put it up to roast, basting it the whole time with beef suet melted down, to which add hot water and salt
5. Serve up in a sitting posture like a pig, and with a lime in the mouth

title: Kidney Soup
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six mutton kidneys
- salt
- dried thyme
- marjoram
- spice
- one well washed anchovy
- one ounce of butter
- one table-spoonful of flour
- consomme
- a glass of port
- a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a tea-spoonful of good vinegar
- caramel coloring
- croutons of fried bread

## Directions

1. Wash the kidneys, dry them, slit them in halves, and plunge them immediately into boiling water well salted
2. Let them remain in this bath for one minute only, take them out, and dry them in a napkin
3. Having thus blanched the kidneys, proceed to boil them gently with some dried thyme, marjoram, and a bag containing spice, &c., till they are very tender
4. Take them out, and pound them in the mortar, with one well washed anchovy
5. When sufficiently pounded, pass the paste through a wire sieve to get rid of fibre, gristle, &c.
6. Place the consomme in a bowl handy, and keep the kidney paste ready in a soup plate
7. Take a roomy sauce-pan, melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of it over the fire, stir in a table-spoonful of flour
8. When nice and creamy, add a cupful of consomme, stirring vigorously, and continue to add consomme and kidney paste by degrees, working them well together, till you have used them up
9. Let the puree come to the boil
10. Add a glass of port, a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, a tea-spoonful of good vinegar, and sufficient caramel coloring to give the soup a warm brown tint
11. Stir well for a minute over the fire, and serve with croutons of fried bread

title: Kidney Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen mutton kidneys
- pepper
- salt
- juice of onions
- ginger
- garlic
- three dessertspoonfuls of ghee or lard
- half a clove of garlic
- eight large Patna onions
- hot stock

## Directions

1. Steep in lukewarm water for a few minutes a dozen mutton kidneys, and remove the white skin or coat which will become perceptible
2. Cut into halves or quarter them, wash in three or four waters, and allow them to remain as long as possible in pepper, salt, and the juice of onions, ginger, and garlic
3. Boil up three dessertspoonfuls of ghee or lard in a deep frying-pan, throw in the kidneys with the juice, put in half a clove of garlic, and cover over the whole with eight large Patna onions sliced each into eight slices, and separated so as to cover over the whole surface of the pan
4. Pour over it as much hot stock as will keep all the onions under, and simmer over a slow fire until the onions disappear
5. Serve up quite hot

title: Kidney Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ordinary kidneys
- bones or scraps for broth
- butter
- flour
- breakfast-cupful of gravy
- kidney-paste
- one table-spoonful port wine
- one tea-spoonful red currant jelly
- one dessert-spoonful anchovy vinegar
- a few drops of chilli vinegar
- four squares of hot fried toast

## Directions

1. Blanch the kidneys in scalding water
2. Lift them out and dry them in a cloth
3. Make a strong broth or gravy out of any bones or scraps
4. Stew the kidneys in the gravy till they are nice and tender
5. Take them out, drain them, and pour the gravy into a bowl
6. Cut up and pound the kidneys to a paste in a mortar with some butter and pass it through a sieve
7. Skim any grease from the top of the gravy
8. Melt a dessert-spoonful of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
9. Stir into it a dessert-spoonful of flour
10. When creamy, add by degrees a breakfast-cupful of the gravy
11. Add kidney-paste until all is expended
12. Flavour the puree with one table-spoonful port wine, one tea-spoonful red currant jelly, one dessert-spoonful anchovy vinegar, and a few drops of chilli vinegar
13. Let the contents of the sauce-pan thicken properly by coming to the boil
14. Pour the puree over four squares of hot fried toast
15. Serve without delay

title: Kidney Toasts
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- surrounding fat
- pepper
- salt
- grated lemon-peel
- nutmeg
- one egg yolk
- thin toasted bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound, in a marble mortar, the kidney and the surrounding fat.
2. Season with pepper, salt, grated lemon-peel, and nutmeg.
3. Mix with it the yolk of an egg well beaten.
4. Lay it upon thin toasted bread cut into square bits.
5. Put a little butter into a dish, lay in it the kidney toasts, and brown them in an oven.
6. Serve them very hot.

title: Kopta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of a big fish
- pasted ginger
- onions
- turmeric
- salt
- raisins
- pieces of almonds
- powdered turmeric
- chillies
- ginger-juice
- minced onions
- a little paste of moistened grams
- ghee
- eggs beaten with salt
- powdered turmeric
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of a big fish with pasted ginger, onions, turmeric and salt
2. Take out the boiled pieces and remove the bones
3. Add raisins, pieces of almonds, powdered turmeric, chillies, ginger-juice, salt, minced onions and a little paste of moistened grams
4. Beat thoroughly
5. Heat it with a little ghee to evaporate some water so that small balls may be made of the paste
6. Dip the balls into eggs beaten with salt and powdered turmeric
7. Roll on bread crumbs
8. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Kopta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat
- water
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- salt
- curd
- powders of chillies
- cumin
- coriander
- garam-masala
- fried almonds
- raisins
- pasted garlics
- eggs
- besam of grams
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil some minced meat with a little water, add in adequate quantities of ginger-juice, onion-juice, salt, curd, powders of chillies, cumin, coriander, garam-masala, fried almonds and raisins, pasted garlics, eggs and besam of grams about 0.5 of the weight of raw meat, and beat thoroughly to a stiff paste.
2. Form balls of it, dip into beaten eggs, roll on bread crumbs and fry in plenty of ghee.

title: Kopta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat
- water
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- salt
- curd
- powders of chillies
- powders of cumin
- powders of coriander
- powders of garam-masalla
- fried almonds
- raisins
- pasted garlic
- eggs
- besam of grams

## Directions

1. Boil some minced meat with a little water
2. Add in adequate quantities of ginger-juice, onion-juice, salt, curd, powders of chillies, cumin, coriander, garam-masalla, fried almonds and raisins, pasted garlics, eggs and besam of grams about 1/4 of the weight of raw meat
3. Beat thoroughly to a stiff paste
4. Form balls of it, dip into beaten eggs, roll on bread crumbs and fry in plenty of ghee till brown

title: Kopta of Chitol Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big sized chitol fish
- turmeric
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- pepper
- cumin
- ginger-juice
- a little flour
- ghee

## Directions

1. Flake and skin a big sized chitol fish
2. Cut in pieces and boil some of them with turmeric and salt
3. Drain off the water
4. Remove the bones and mix powdered turmeric, pepper, cumin, ginger-juice, salt and a little flour
5. Form balls and fry them till deep brown in ghee

title: Kopta of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of shrimps
- 1 chhatak of powdered biscuit
- 0.5 tola of powdered pepper
- a pinch of powdered mace
- 0.5 tola of salt
- a little pasted green chillies
- 0.75 chhatak of ghee
- eggs beaten with salt
- powdered biscuit
- ghee

## Directions

1. Remove the scales, etc. from 1 powa of shrimps and make a paste of them
2. Add 1 chhatak of powdered biscuit, 0.5 tola of powdered pepper, a pinch of powdered mace, 0.5 tola of salt, a little pasted green chillies and 0.75 chhatak of ghee
3. Mix thoroughly and prepare small balls of it
4. Dip them into eggs beaten with salt
5. Roll on powdered biscuit
6. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Kopta of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of shrimps
- 1 chhatak of powdered biscuit
- 1/2 tola of powdered pepper
- a pinch of powdered mace
- 1/2 tola of salt
- a little pasted green chillies
- 1/2 chhatak of ghee
- eggs
- powdered biscuit for rolling

## Directions

1. Remove the scales, etc. from 1 powa of shrimps and make a paste of them
2. Add 1 chhatak of powdered biscuit, 1/2 tola of powdered pepper, a pinch of powdered mace, 1/2 tola of salt, a little pasted green chillies and 1/2 chhatak of ghee
3. Mix thoroughly and prepare small balls of it
4. Dip them into eggs beaten with salt
5. Roll on powdered biscuit
6. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Kopta with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- contents of a few eggs
- salt
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- powdered red chillies
- turmeric
- pepper
- cumin
- cardamom
- fried pieces of almonds
- flour
- beaten egg-contents
- ghee
- mustard
- chopped onions
- pieces of cucumber

## Directions

1. Take the contents of a few eggs and mix salt, ginger-juice, onion-juice, powdered red chillies, turmeric, pepper, cumin and cardamom, and fried pieces of almonds.
2. Add a little flour, if necessary, and beat thoroughly to make it a muddy paste.
3. Form cakes, dip them into beaten egg-contents and fry in plenty of ghee.
4. Serve while hot with mustard, chopped onions, pieces of cucumber, etc.

title: Kul-Achar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seer of ripe plums
- 0.5 powa of salt
- pieces of ginger
- powders of pepper, mustard and aniseed
- 1.5 powa or 0.75 seer of mustard oil

## Directions

1. Wash 2 seer of ripe plums, rub with a cloth to soak the water, remove the stalks and crack each of them a little.
2. Mix with 0.5 powa of salt and dry them in the sun for several days till thoroughly dried.
3. Put the dried plums with pieces of ginger, powders of pepper, mustard and aniseed and 1.5 powa or 0.75 seer of mustard oil in an airtight jar.
4. Shake the jar to mix them thoroughly.
5. Keep it in the sun for 3 weeks.

title: Kulia with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 1/2 seer potatoes
- 1/4 powa onions
- 1/2 powa peas, green or moistened
- 1/2 powa ghee or oil
- 1 tola coriander
- 1 tola ginger
- 1 tola salt
- 1/2 tola turmeric
- 1/2 tola pepper
- 1/2 tola cumin
- 1/2 tola garam-masalla
- a few cassia leaves
- red chillies
- cloves
- water

## Directions

1. Shell 8 hard-boiled eggs and cut in halves.
2. Cut the potatoes in big pieces and the onions in small ones after peeling them.
3. Brown the eggs and onions separately.
4. Brown potatoes and peas together.
5. Add turmeric, coriander, pepper, cumin, ginger, red chillies and salt, all pasted together, with water.
6. Add eggs and onions when the curry is half-boiled.
7. Season it with ghee or oil with a few cassia leaves, red chillies and cloves fried in it.
8. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla and take down while a little soup remains.

title: Kulia with Mun Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pasted pulses of peas or grams
- tender leaves of amrita mun
- ghee
- oil
- cassia leaves
- water
- coriander
- ginger
- pepper
- cumin
- turmeric
- salt
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Make a thick solution of pasted pulses of peas or grams and dip pieces, 1 or 1.5 inch square, of very tender leaves of amrita mun into it, and fry them in ghee or oil.
2. Boil some ghee with a few cassia leaves and pour in water with powdered coriander, ginger, pepper, cumin and turmeric.
3. When it boils add the fried pieces and salt.
4. Take down after adding powdered garam-masalla.

title: Kulia with Patol
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Patol 1 seer
- ghee 1/2 powa
- coriander 1 tola
- pepper 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- salt 1 tola
- curd 1 chhatak
- cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Skin the patols very thin and cut both ends longitudinally and vertically to one another to a quarter of their lengths so that salt and spices may enter into them
2. Brown them in ghee
3. Boil some ghee in another pan with a few cassia leaves and then pour in water with powdered turmeric, pepper, cumin, coriander and salt
4. When it boils add patol and curd and cover the pan
5. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down when the soup is thick

title: Lady Canning
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee 1 chhatak
- middlings 1 powa
- hard chhana 3 powas or more
- khowa kheer
- ghee for frying
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of ghee with 1 powa of middlings
2. Mix this again with 3 powas or more of hard chhana
3. Make a fine paste with the hands
4. Make big spherical balls of it
5. Stuff each with a little pasted khowa kheer
6. Fry them in boiling ghee till red
7. Pick them out and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Lady Canning
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 chhatak of ghee
- 1 powa of middlings
- 3 powas or more of hard chhana
- khowa kheer
- sugar syrup
- dry crystalline white sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 chhatak of ghee with 1 powa of middlings
2. Mix this again with 3 powas or more of hard chhana
3. Make a fine paste with the hands
4. Make big spherical balls of it
5. Stuff each with a little pasted khowa kheer
6. Fry them in boiling ghee till red
7. Pick them out and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated
8. When taking them out smear all over with dry crystalline white sugar

title: Lau Posta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender pumpkin
- pumpkin shoots and leaves
- mustard oil
- red chillies
- poppy seed 1 powa or more
- ginger 2.5 tolas or more
- salt
- browned broken bori
- water

## Directions

1. Peel and chop a very tender pumpkin into fine pieces.
2. Boil them and drain off the water.
3. Boil some mustard oil with a few red chillies and pour in the boiled mass with 1 powa or more of poppy seed and 2.5 tolas or more of ginger, both pasted.
4. Add a little water if necessary.
5. When it boils add salt and browned broken bori.
6. Cooking finishes when the soup dries up.

title: Laugh Anta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender pumpkin
- Water
- Cumin
- Coriander
- Mustard
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Garam-masalla
- Salt
- Milk
- Flour
- Sugar
- Ghee
- Cassia leaves
- Bori

## Directions

1. Peel and chop a very tender pumpkin into fine pieces, and boil them with a little water
2. Add suitable quantities of cumin, coriander, mustard and a little pepper, ginger and garam-masalla, all pasted together, with salt
3. Add some milk with a little flour and sugar mixed in it, and stir the mass thoroughly
4. Heat a little ghee in a pan with a few cassia leaves in it and pour in the mass and boil
5. Add browned broken bori if desired
6. Take down when a little or no soup remains

title: Lavender-water
body:
## Ingredients

- two drachms of oil of lavender
- half a drachm of oil of bergamot
- a drachm of essence of musk
- thirteen ounces of spirits of wine
- five ounces of water

## Directions

1. Mix two drachms of oil of lavender, half a drachm of oil of bergamot, a drachm of essence of musk, thirteen ounces of spirits of wine, and five ounces of water
2. Let it stand a week

title: Le Fricandeau de Veau
body:
## Ingredients

- large leg of veal
- knuckle-bone removed

## Directions

1. Take a large leg of veal and remove the knuckle-bone
2. Corn and lard it in all respects like a beef a la mode, reducing the ingredients in proportion to the difference in size and weight between a round of beef and the leg of veal
3. Boil, baste, and glaze it well in the liquor in which it is boiled
4. Serve up with all sorts of boiled and glazed vegetables

title: Leg of Mutton Dumpling
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer and a quarter of soojee
- half a seer of flour
- half a seer of suet
- leg of mutton
- ground pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour for dredging

## Directions

1. Prepare a good pie-crust with one seer and a quarter of soojee, half a seer of flour, and half a seer of suet, as per recipe No. 199.
2. Clean and trim the leg, cutting away the end of the knuckle-bone, and any other projections likely to injure the dumpling.
3. Sprinkle it well with ground pepper and some salt, and confine it securely in the pastry, closing all joinings with the aid of a little water.
4. Place the dumpling into a strong napkin, previously buttered and dredged with flour.
5. Tie it securely, and allow it to boil from three to four hours.
6. Care must be taken that during the whole process of boiling the dumpling remains suspended in the water, and not resting on the bottom of the pan.
7. On removing it from the boiler, plunge it immediately into a large tureen of cold water for two or three minutes. This will strengthen the pastry and prevent its bursting or breaking while it is being served up.

title: Lemon Barley-water
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls pearl barley
- one quarter pound lump sugar
- rather more than two quarts boiling water
- peel of one fresh lemon

## Directions

1. Combine pearl barley, lump sugar, boiling water, and lemon peel
2. Let it stand all night
3. Strain the next morning

title: Lemon Cheesecakes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of butter
- yolks of four eggs
- four ounces of finely pounded loaf sugar
- juice of three limes
- liqueur-glass of noyeau or curaçoa
- puff-pastry
- finely pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Melt four ounces of butter in an enamelled sauce-pan.
2. Stir into it the yolks of four eggs, and four ounces of finely pounded loaf sugar, and when dissolved, the juice of three limes.
3. Mix the syrup well, and add a liqueur-glass of noyeau or curaçoa.
4. Line eight or nine round patty pans with puff-paste.
5. Fill them half full with the mixture, leaving room for it to rise in the baking.
6. When done, dust over the cheesecakes some finely pounded loaf sugar, and serve them upon a napkin, hot.

title: Lemon Syrup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of lemon skin
- 9 chhataks of lemon juice
- 1 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1 chhatak of lemon skin in 9 chhataks of lemon juice on mild heat and throw them away when they are fully boiled
2. Add 1 seer of sugar
3. Boil a few minutes more after the sugar is dissolved and take down when a little gluey
4. Strain the syrup through a fine strainer and bottle it

title: Lime Fritters
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of flour
- one ounce of fresh butter
- one coffee-cup of milk
- one ounce of finely sifted sugar
- rasped peel of three nice limes
- a few drops of lemon essence
- four eggs
- boiling fat for frying
- powdered sugar for dusting
- white sugar
- water
- a glass of brandy or Madeira
- juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Mix together in a sauce-pan four ounces of flour and an ounce of fresh butter
2. Add a coffee-cup of milk and stir to the consistency of a thick batter; if too thick dilute with a little more milk
3. Stir in one ounce of finely sifted sugar and the rasped peel of three nice limes with a few drops of lemon essence
4. Stir the whole mixture over the fire till thoroughly heated, but on no account let it boil
5. When well mixed and hot, place the batter on one side to get cool
6. Break four eggs into a bowl separating the yolks from the whites
7. Beat the former and whisk the latter to a froth
8. Add the yolks to the cool batter and stir thoroughly
9. At the last add the whisked whites
10. Prepare a deep bath of boiling fat and set your frying basket therein
11. Drop the batter into it by dessert-spoonfuls at a time
12. Each spoonful will form itself into a ball and turn gradually a golden yellow
13. When they are all done, drain and serve them in a napkin, dusted over with well powdered sugar
14. For the sauce: Make a nice syrup with white sugar and water, stir in a glass of brandy or Madeira, give it the juice of a lime, and serve hot

title: Lime Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of flour
- an ounce of fresh butter
- a coffee-cup of milk
- one ounce of finely sifted sugar
- the rasped peel of three nice limes
- a few drops of lemon essence

## Directions

1. Mix together in a sauce-pan four ounces of flour and an ounce of fresh butter
2. Add a coffee-cup of milk and stir to the consistency of a thick batter
3. If too thick, dilute with a little more milk
4. Stir in one ounce of finely sifted sugar
5. Add the rasped peel of three nice limes with a few drops of lemon essence
6. Stir the whole mix

title: Lime Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water
- lime rind
- lime juice
- liqueur, brandy, or rum
- cochineal

## Directions

1. Put three table-spoonfuls of sugar into a sauce-pan with a breakfast-cupful of water and the very finely peeled rind of a lime
2. Simmer for twenty minutes
3. Add the juice of two limes and a liqueur glass of any liqueur, brandy, or rum
4. Strain and serve
5. A drop or two of cochineal will improve the colour of the sauce

title: Livers
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- livers
- minced onions
- butter
- water
- salt
- powdered cumin
- pepper
- flour
- lemon-juice
- pasted garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Wash and boil the livers before cooking.
2. Cut the boiled livers in small cubes.
3. Fry them with minced onions in sufficient butter and add water, salt, powdered cumin and pepper.
4. Add a little flour solution to make the soup thick if it is too thin.
5. Add a little lemon-juice and pasted garam-masalla in the end and take down.
6. Serve with bread.

title: Livers Alternative Method
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- onions
- boiled livers
- ghee
- pepper
- salt
- pieces of ginger
- green chillies
- water

## Directions

1. Cut potatoes, onions and the boiled livers in slices.
2. Brown the potatoes in ghee.
3. Heat ghee and fry the onions with powdered pepper, salt, pieces of ginger and green chillies.
4. After a time add the livers and fry for about 10 minutes.
5. Add a little water and when it boils add the browned potatoes and salt.
6. Take down when this water dries up.
7. A little more powdered pepper may be added now.

title: Lobster Cofta Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten lobsters
- red coral from the head
- bread-crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- herbs
- egg
- unroasted coriander-seed
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chillies
- ground turmeric
- ground peppercorns
- ground garlic
- bay-leaves
- lemon-grass
- water

## Directions

1. According to their size, take eight or ten lobsters
2. Clean them thoroughly
3. Remove the heads and shells
4. Pull the flesh to pieces and pound to a pulp
5. Add to it some of the red coral from the head
6. Mix into it the bread-crumbs, salt, pepper, herbs, and an egg well beaten up, and make into balls
7. Bruise down the remains of the heads and the contents of the long legs with unroasted coriander-seed
8. Take all the juice and fry it in mustard oil with the ground condiments omitting the ginger, and cook the balls in the same way as the prawn balls, with the addition of bay-leaves and a few blades of lemon-grass

title: Lobster or Fish Pellow
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two hilsa or beckty fishes
- eight or ten large long-legged lobsters with the roe or coral
- salt
- onions
- sliced ginger
- peppercorns
- a dozen bay-leaves
- a tablespoonful of unroasted dhuniah or coriander seed
- water

## Directions

1. Take out the centre bones of the fish
2. Thoroughly wash the fish and lobsters in several waters with salt
3. Boil with plenty of seasoning of onions, sliced ginger, peppercorns, a dozen bay-leaves, a tablespoonful of unroasted coriander seed, and salt, with water sufficient to give the required quantity of gravy
4. When ready, remove and shell the lobsters, reserving the roe or red coral in the heads
5. Bruise down the roe with a little unroasted coriander seed and mix with the fish gravy
6. Make the pellow in all other respects the same as prawn pellow, using the gravy of the fish instead of cocoanut or other gravy
7. Garnish with the lobsters

title: Lobster Pilao a la Turque
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- firm pieces of lobster from the tin
- buttered saute-pan
- riz a la Turque
- sweet onion or shalots
- two ounces of butter
- one table-spoonful of good curry powder or paste
- one pint of plain gravy
- quarter of a pound of sweet almonds
- one bitter almond
- milk
- boiling water
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Choose the firm pieces of a lobster from the tin and trim them neatly
2. Set them in a buttered saute-pan and warm them thoroughly
3. Arrange the pieces in a circle in a hot silver dish filling the centre with riz a la Turque
4. Cut up a sweet onion or half a dozen shalots and throw the pieces into a small stew-pan with two ounces of butter
5. Fry till the onions begin to take color, then stir in a table-spoonful of good curry powder or paste
6. Cook this for five minutes, then add a pint of plain gravy
7. Let the contents of the pan simmer for a quarter of an hour
8. Blanch and peel a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds with one bitter one
9. Pound them in a mortar with a little milk
10. When well pounded, pour some boiling water upon them
11. Stir well and then squeeze the milk through muslin
12. Stir this into the sauce, adding the yolks of two eggs off the fire before serving
13. Pour some of the sauce over the pieces of lobster but not over the rice and serve

title: Lobster Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- the spawn of a lobster
- a quarter of a pound of melted butter
- the meat of the lobster

## Directions

1. Pound very finely the spawn of a lobster, rub it through a sieve
2. Mix it with a quarter of a pound of melted butter
3. Add the meat of the lobster cut into small bits
4. Make it quite hot, but do not allow it to boil

title: Loin of Mutton en Papillote
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin of mutton
- breakfast-cupful of salad oil
- two onions
- a carrot
- whole peppers
- salt
- chopped parsley
- a tea-spoonful of powdered dried sweet herbs
- well oiled paper
- melted butter
- vegetables
- gravy

## Directions

1. Trim the loin nicely, and let it lie from morning till roasting time in marinade, composed of a breakfast-cupful of salad oil, two onions, and a carrot, sliced fine as for Julienne, with some whole peppers, salt, chopped parsley, and a tea-spoonful of powdered dried sweet herbs.
2. Let the joint be turned several times during the day, and baste it often.
3. When to be dressed, pack it, with its vegetables and all, in a well oiled paper, and roast it carefully, basting it with the oil that composed the marinade.
4. When nearly done, remove the paper, brush off the vegetables, baste with melted butter, and serve, when nicely browned, with other vegetables independently cooked, and some gravy.

title: Long Plum Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- long plums
- salt
- fresh chilies
- cloves of garlic
- ginger finely sliced
- peppercorns
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the long plums, or what the natives call nar kollee bhyar
2. Remove the peel
3. Keep them in salt in the sun for a day or two
4. Drain away the salt water
5. Put them into bottles, in layers alternately with fresh chilies, cloves of garlic, ginger finely sliced, and peppercorns
6. Add a little more salt
7. Pour in as much vinegar as will cover the whole
8. Cork and expose to the sun for fifteen to twenty days
9. The plums will not be fit for use until they have pickled for six months

title: Love-apple or Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- five hundred tomatoes
- two pounds of green ginger, ground fine
- a pound and a half of garlic, ground fine
- one pound of chilies, ground fine
- one pound of salt
- three pounds of tamarinds
- three quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Steep the tamarinds for twelve hours in a quart of the vinegar
2. Strain them through a sieve, rejecting the stones, and add the other two quarts of vinegar, all the ground condiments, and salt
3. Break the tomatoes into the mixture, and boil the whole, stirring it all the time until it thickens
4. Remove it from the fire, and when cold strain it carefully and bottle the liquid, which is the sauce

title: Luchi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ghee
- salt
- 1 seer of flour
- 1/2 seer of water

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of ghee and a pinch of salt with 1 seer of flour
2. Knead into a dough with about 1/2 seer of water
3. Make small balls of it
4. Roll into thin round discs
5. Fry in plenty of ghee till they expand
6. Fry also the other sides
7. It takes about half a minute to fry a luchi
8. They are not at all browned

title: Luchi with Khowa Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- pistachios
- khowa kheer
- rose essence
- flour
- ghee

## Directions

1. Make a stiff paste with almonds and pistachios of khowa kheer without adding water
2. One or two drops of essence of rose may be added
3. Make very small balls of it and roll them into thin discs
4. Prepare a bit bigger discs of flour as in the preparation of luchi
5. Place a disc of kheer between two of flour and close the ends of the flour discs
6. Fry as luchi in plenty of ghee

title: Luchi with Sweet Gourd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe sweet gourd
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut a ripe sweet gourd in two and remove the seeds
2. Close it again as before binding with a thread
3. Give a very thick coating of mud all round it
4. Burn well on charcoal or wood fire so that the interior portion is made a pulp
5. Break the fruit when cold and take out the pulp
6. Add salt and prepare a stiff dough with it and flour without adding water
7. Proceed as usual with the making of Luchi

title: Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- two fresh eggs
- finely-sifted flour

## Directions

1. Take the yolks and white of two fresh eggs, and as much finely-sifted flour as will make a good dough of the consistency of dough for pie-crusts without the addition of any water
2. Roll it out to its full extent on a large board to about the thickness of an eight-anna piece
3. Cut it up into small squares, diamonds, or circles, or into any shape or design you please
4. Do this quickly, as within an hour of its being rolled out the pastry will harden
5. It may be used immediately, or in the winter it may be kept good for a few days

title: Macaroni Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of macaroni
- milk
- two pounds of beef
- ground white pepper
- salt
- soup herbs
- two ounces of English cheese
- a dessertspoonful of very dry mustard
- half a teaspoonful of very finely powdered white pepper
- about two teaspoonfuls of salt
- two chittacks or four ounces of butter
- a couple of crisp biscuits

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of macaroni, boil and throw away the first water.
2. Then boil it again in some milk, and remove when it is quite tender.
3. Prepare a strong gravy or soup with two pounds of beef, well seasoned with ground white pepper, salt, and soup herbs.
4. Bruise into fine powder two ounces of some good English cheese.
5. Pound very fine a couple of crisp biscuits.
6. Pour over the boiled macaroni sufficient beef gravy or stock to entirely cover it.
7. Put in all the pepper, salt, and mustard, but only half the ground cheese.
8. Set it to simmer over a slow fire until the gravy begins to dry, and the macaroni acquires some consistency.
9. With three ounces of butter butter well the baking-dish.
10. Pour into it the macaroni.
11. Mix the remaining ground cheese with the powdered biscuit, and strew it over the pie.
12. Cut into small pieces the remaining ounce of butter, and throw that also over the pie.
13. Put the dish into an oven, and bake to a fine light but rich brown colour for ten to fifteen minutes.

title: Macaroni Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- soup herbs
- white of an egg
- macaroni

## Directions

1. Prepare a shin of beef soup as directed above, omitting the sauce and wine
2. Boil some macaroni until perfectly tender
3. Clear the soup with the white of an egg, then add the boiled macaroni, and warm up before serving

title: Maccaroni au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- maccaroni
- butter
- black pepper
- salt
- one large cupful of sauce blonde
- two ounces of grated cheese

## Directions

1. First boil the maccaroni in boiling water, or in milk or stock if preferred
2. Well butter a pie-dish
3. Arrange the maccaroni therein neatly
4. Give it a dusting with black pepper and salt
5. Pour round it a large cupful of good sauce blonde in which you have mixed two ounces of grated cheese
6. Let this run well in amongst the bed of maccaroni
7. Shake over the surface a liberal coating of grated cheese
8. Make this thoroughly hot in the oven
9. Brown the surface of the cheese by passing a hot iron about half an inch above it
10. Send it up

title: Maccaroni au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of maccaroni
- salt
- pepper
- one ounce of butter
- one table-spoonful of flour
- four table-spoonfuls of gravy
- a coffee-cupful of cream, or milk enriched with the yolk of an egg
- two ounces of grated cheese
- one table-spoonful of mustard

## Directions

1. Throw maccaroni into boiling water with a salt-spoonful of salt and a pinch of pepper
2. Boil, and as soon as tender, drain it well
3. Put into a sauce-pan one ounce of butter, mix it well with one table-spoonful of flour
4. Moisten with four table-spoonfuls of gravy and a coffee-cupful of cream or milk enriched with the yolk of an egg
5. Add two ounces of grated cheese, one table-spoonful of mustard, salt and pepper to taste
6. Place the maccaroni in a shallow well buttered pie-dish
7. By degrees pour your mixture amongst it
8. Dust a thick layer of grated cheese over the surface
9. Send it to table as soon as it browns nicely in the oven

title: Maccaroni in the Italian Fashion
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- maccaroni
- garlic clove
- cold water
- butter
- fresh tomato pulp
- salt
- black pepper
- grated Parmesan cheese or mild powdered dry cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the maccaroni in boiling water in a sauce-pan (which may be rubbed with a clove of garlic) until it is tender
2. The moment it is tender, stop the boiling by adding a cup of cold water
3. Drain it carefully, as you do rice, and let it remain in the hot sauce-pan
4. Stir into it a table-spoonful of the best fresh, or preserved butter
5. Work this about over the fire whilst an assistant adds by degrees a breakfast-cupful of fresh tomato pulp, a little salt and black pepper
6. Add lastly, a heaped up table-spoonful of either grated Parmesan from the bottle, or any mild thoroughly powdered dry cheese
7. Serve steaming hot without delay. The cheese should form long threads when lifted from the dish with the maccaroni

title: Maccaroni with Italian Composition
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of cloves of garlic
- a shallot
- three anchovies, boned and well wiped from the tin oil
- four olives
- three table-spoonfuls of the best salad oil
- a large dish of hot-boiled maccaroni

## Directions

1. Mince a couple of cloves of garlic, a shallot, three anchovies, boned and well wiped from the tin oil, and four olives.
2. Put the mince into a small sauce-pan with three table-spoonfuls of the best salad oil.
3. Boil till the bits of garlic and onion begin to brown.
4. Turn the mixture into a sauce-pan containing a large dish of hot-boiled maccaroni.
5. Stir it well, and serve.

title: Maccaroni à la Milanaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of maccaroni
- a breakfast-cupful of good chicken broth
- an onion
- sweet herbs
- black pepper corns
- an ounce of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- two ounces of grated cheese or Parmesan from the bottle
- a tea-spoonful of powdered mustard
- salt
- spiced pepper
- a table-spoonful of cream or the yolks of two raw eggs beaten separately

## Directions

1. Boil two ounces of maccaroni, and keep it hot in its own pan after draining.
2. Make a breakfast-cupful of good chicken broth flavoured with an onion, sweet herbs, and black pepper corns.
3. Melt an ounce of butter in a sauce-pan, stir into it a dessert-spoonful of flour, mix them to a paste, and by degrees pour in the hot chicken broth.
4. As this is thickening, add to it two ounces of grated cheese or Parmesan from the bottle, a tea-spoonful of powdered mustard, salt, and spiced pepper, at discretion.
5. Continue to stir the sauce until it reaches a creamy thickness, when you can finish it off the fire by a table-spoonful of cream, or the yolks of two raw eggs beaten separately.
6. Stir the sauce into the hot boiled maccaroni, and serve immediately.

title: Macedoine De Legumes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal portions of boiled carrot
- French beans
- turnip or knolkhol
- a few peas
- asparagus points
- haricot beans
- sauce blonde

## Directions

1. Cut equal portions of boiled carrot, French beans, turnip or knolkhol, peas, asparagus points, and haricot beans into small dice
2. Gently heat in sauce blonde
3. Be careful not to mash the vegetables

title: Madanamrithu
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of pulses of moog
- water
- 1/2 powa of curd
- plenty of ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Keep 1 powa of pulses of moog in water for 3 or 4 hours
2. Make a paste of moistened moog
3. Beat the paste with 1/2 powa of curd with the hands or churn with a staff till a drop of the mass when thrown on water float on it
4. Fry Jilapi with this paste in plenty of ghee on mild heat
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Madhuramla Khanda
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of curd
- 1/2 seer of besam of moog
- water
- ghee
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 powa of curd with 1/2 seer of besam of moog and add water making a thin paste.
2. Beat the paste for about 15 minutes.
3. Boil it and stir continuously till the paste is very thick.
4. Place the paste on a wooden plate and stir till stirring is quite difficult.
5. Press the paste flat about 1/2 inch thick.
6. Cut it in cubes with a knife when cold.
7. Fry the cubes with sufficient ghee and immerse in a syrup of sugar.
8. Mix 1/4 tola of powdered garam-masala, 1/4 tola of powdered pepper and a little salt with about 1.5 powa of curd and heat.
9. Drop the cubes in the curd when it is warm, continue heating and take down when the curd is thick and sticks to the sides of the cubes.

title: Maintenon Cutlets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight nicely trimmed choplets of mutton
- scraps of bacon
- onion
- carrot
- spices
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- pint of good gravy
- glass of sherry
- minced onion
- capers
- two or three truffles
- chopped parsley
- eggs
- papers
- oil or butter
- red currant jelly
- anchovy vinegar
- spoonful of sherry
- mashed potatoes
- celery puree

## Directions

1. Put eight nicely trimmed choplets of mutton into a stew-pan with some scraps of bacon, onion, carrot, spices, pepper, salt, and a pinch of grated nutmeg
2. Add a pint of good gravy, and a glass of sherry
3. Gently stew the little chops till done
4. Lay them out on a large dish, covered by another with a weight upon it
5. When quite cold, trim them finally into shape if necessary
6. Strain the gravy in which they were stewed
7. Remove all fat
8. Mince an onion very small, and a few capers, with two or three truffles also
9. Fry the onions a golden brown
10. Add the minced capers and truffles with pepper, salt, and a spoonful of chopped parsley
11. Moisten with a little of the gravy
12. Thicken it with a couple of eggs
13. Put the mince away to get cold
14. Cut some papers for your cutlets and oil or butter them
15. Spread your cold thick mince over your cutlets liberally
16. Fold them in their papers most carefully
17. Broil them on the grid-iron sufficiently long to heat them thoroughly, or heat them in the oven
18. Serve your cutlets in their papers
19. Hand round a rich sauce made of the remains of the gravy originally got from the cooking of the cutlets, slightly thickened with butter and flour, flavoured with red currant jelly, anchovy vinegar, and a spoonful of sherry
20. Place the cutlets round a ring of mashed potatoes filled with celery puree

title: Maitre D'Hotel Butter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of iced fresh butter
- the juice of one lime
- a dessert-spoonful of chopped parsley free from moisture
- a little white pepper
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. To two ounces of iced fresh butter, add the juice of one lime, a dessert-spoonful of chopped parsley free from moisture, a little white pepper, and a pinch of salt
2. Form it with your butter bat
3. Set it in the ice box

title: Malai Curry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (1 seer)
- cocoa-nuts (1 or 2)
- cumin (1 tola)
- pepper (1/2 tola)
- coriander (1/2 tola)
- turmeric (2 tolas)
- ginger (2 1/2 tolas)
- onions (1 chhatak)
- oil (1 powa)
- ghee (1 chhatak)
- garam-masalla (1/2 tola)
- flour (1/2 chhatak)
- chillies
- cassia leaves
- salt (1 1/2 tola)

## Directions

1. Prepare lobsters and big fishes and cut in big pieces, keeping lobsters entire
2. Fry them in oil
3. Bring out the vegetable milk from the cocoa-nuts
4. Make a paste of all the spices
5. Make a paste of the garam-masalla separately
6. Boil oil and fry cassia leaves and the pasted spices in it for about 5 minutes, adding water little by little if necessary
7. Add the vegetable milk
8. On boiling add salt, the fishes and a little thin solution of flour
9. When properly boiled season the curry with boiling ghee with chillies and a few entire cumins fried in it
10. Add garam-masalla and take down when the soup is fairly thick

title: Malida
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- contents of 3 or 4 fresh eggs
- 1/2 seer flour
- water
- ghee
- honey or sugar

## Directions

1. Add the contents of 3 or 4 fresh eggs to 1/2 seer of flour and rub thoroughly.
2. Add some water and knead the flour into a dough.
3. Form small balls of it and roll them into very thin and round discs.
4. Heat them a little in a shallow pan 2 or 3 at a time and then bake them on charcoal fire.
5. Smear ghee and again heat them a little in a pan.
6. Serve with honey or sugar.

title: Malpoa
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 seer of fine flour
- 1 powa of curd
- 1 tola of entire seeds of big cardamoms
- water
- 1 powa or a little more of ghee or oil
- 0.5 powa of paste
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a thick paste with fine flour 0.5 seer, curd 1 powa, entire seeds of big cardamoms 1 tola, and an adequate quantity of water
2. Boil ghee (or oil) 1 powa or a little more till the bubbles die out
3. Fry in it 0.5 powa of the paste at a time till light brown
4. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Malpoa with Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 seer of hard chhana
- 1.5 seer of sabeda
- powdered pepper
- powdered cubebs
- entire seeds of big cardamoms
- entire aniseed
- water
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a thick paste with 1.5 seer of hard chhana pasted with the hands, 1.5 seer of sabeda, powdered pepper, powdered cubebs, entire seeds of big cardamoms, entire aniseed 1 tola each and adequate quantity of water
2. Fry 0.5 powa of the paste at a time in plenty of boiling ghee
3. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Malpoa with Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of milk
- 1.5 powa of middlings
- 0.5 seer of thoroughly pasted khowa kheer
- milk
- powders of aniseed
- powders of cardamom
- powders of pepper
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1 powa of milk with 1.5 powa middlings
2. Keep aside for about an hour
3. Then mix it with 0.5 seer of thoroughly pasted khowa kheer and adequate quantity of milk to make the mass a moderately thick paste
4. Also mix powders of aniseed, cardamom and pepper
5. Fry as before
6. Drop into a syrup of sugar

title: Mango Amchoor
body:
## Ingredients

- green mangoes
- salt
- dry pounded turmeric
- chilies
- dry ginger

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter some green mangoes
2. Sprinkle with salt, and expose them to the sun until they begin to dry up
3. Rub them with dry pounded turmeric, chilies, and dry ginger
4. Sprinkle more salt, and expose them to the sun again, until they are quite dried up
5. Bottle and keep for use

title: Mango Fool
body:
## Ingredients

- six green mangoes
- clean water
- good pure milk

## Directions

1. Remove every particle of the green peel from six green mangoes, cut them into four, and steep them in clean water
2. Throw the stones away
3. Boil the fruit perfectly tender, pulp and pass it through a sieve
4. Sweeten to your taste
5. Add to it very gradually, stirring all the while, as much good pure milk as will reduce it to the consistency of custard
6. Eat on the day it is made

title: Mango Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- a hundred green mangoes
- weak lime-water
- cold water
- good clean white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and stone a hundred green mangoes, and cut each into four
2. Throw them as they are ready into a solution of weak lime-water, strained of all sediment
3. When all have been peeled and stoned, remove them into a large vessel
4. Pour in as much cold water as will entirely cover them
5. Boil them until they are quite dissolved
6. Carefully strain the liquid without pressing the fruit
7. Let it drip all night
8. Boil the juice again in an open preserving-pan, and cut away the scum as it rises
9. Add gradually good clean white sugar until it is sweetened to taste
10. Continue to boil steadily until the scum has ceased to rise, and the jelly is quite clear and transparent
11. Allow some of it to drop on a plate and cool
12. If it congeals, remove the pan and fill the bottles while the jelly is slightly warm
13. Cork down when quite cold

title: Mango Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- pulp of mangoes (boiled for jelly)
- plenty of clean white sugar

## Directions

1. Pass through a sieve the pulp of the mangoes which had been boiled for jelly
2. Add plenty of clean white sugar, without quite destroying the acidity of the fruit
3. Boil it over a slow fire until it acquires the thickness of guava cheese
4. Bottle while it is yet warm

title: Mango or Apple Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- mango or apple
- minced onion
- green chilli
- vinegar
- sugar
- salad oil
- salt
- black pepper
- a tea-spoonful of chopped green ginger

## Directions

1. Make like cucumber chutney with the addition of a tea-spoonful of chopped green ginger.

title: Mango Soup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- small and tender mangoes
- mustard seeds
- panch-forons
- oil
- 2 1/2 powas of water
- 1/2 chhatak of mustard paste
- 1 powa of mango pieces
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel small and tender mangoes and cut in small thin pieces throwing away the seeds
2. Fry a small pinch of mustards and panch-forons with a little oil and pour in 2 1/2 powas of water
3. Add a paste of 1/2 chhatak of mustard, 1 powa of the pieces of mangoes and a generous pinch of salt
4. Take down when the pieces are boiled
5. Keep the soup very thin

title: Mangoes Pickled Whole
body:
## Ingredients

- large-sized mangoes
- salt
- cloves of garlic
- chilies
- ginger
- cullungee seeds
- clove
- cinnamon
- strong sewing cotton
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and divide some large-sized mangoes sufficiently to admit of the stones being easily extracted
2. Rub them over with salt, and expose them to the sun for two or three days
3. Dry them with a napkin
4. Stuff each mango with a few cloves of garlic, finely-sliced chilies and ginger, some cullungee seeds, a clove or two, and a stick or two of cinnamon
5. Tie them securely with strong sewing cotton, and put them into bottles, with vinegar sufficient to cover them
6. Cork the bottles well, and expose them to the sun for fifteen to twenty days

title: Mangoes Pickled Whole
body:
## Ingredients

- large-sized mangoes
- salt
- cloves of garlic
- finely-sliced chilies
- ginger
- cullungee seeds
- clove
- cinnamon
- strong sewing cotton
- vinegar
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Peel and divide some large-sized mangoes sufficiently to admit of the stones being easily extracted
2. Rub them over with salt, and expose them to the sun for two or three days
3. Dry them with a napkin
4. Stuff each mango with a few cloves of garlic, finely-sliced chilies and ginger, some cullungee seeds, a clove or two, and a stick or two of cinnamon
5. Tie them securely with strong sewing cotton
6. Put them into bottles, with vinegar sufficient to cover them
7. Cork the bottles well, and expose them to the sun for fifteen to twenty days
8. The pickle will be ready for use in three or four months
9. To prevent the pickle spoiling, pour a tablespoonful or two of mustard oil over it when in the bottle

title: Manik-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 powas of khowa kheer
- 1 1/2 powas of sugar
- pastes of almonds and pistachios
- a drop or two of essence of rose
- 5 or 6 raisins
- 5 or 6 cardamom seeds
- 8 or 10 drops of rose water
- ghee
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1 1/2 powas of khowa kheer and drop it into a boiling syrup of 1 1/2 powas of sugar
2. Stir constantly on mild heat
3. On boiling again add pastes of almonds and pistachios as desired and a drop or two of essence of rose
4. Take down when the mass is a very stiff paste
5. Scrape it together
6. When cool make balls of it stuffed with 5 or 6 raisins, 5 or 6 cardamom seeds and 8 or 10 drops of rose water after smearing the hands with ghee
7. Roll them on sugar and serve

title: Manthak
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1/4 seer of fine flour
- water
- plenty of ghee
- sugar syrup
- 1 or 2 pinches of powdered cardamoms
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1 chhatak of ghee with 1/4 seer of fine flour
2. Make a moderately stiff dough of it with water
3. Prepare a little flattened balls of it
4. Fry them in plenty of ghee
5. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar mixed with 1 or 2 pinches of powdered cardamoms and very little camphor till saturated

title: Marmalade of Bel Fruits
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green bel fruits
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the shells of a few green bel fruits, cut into thin circular or semicircular pieces, remove the seeds with a pin and keep in water for an hour.
2. Then wash them with plain water, boil in a syrup of sugar till soft, take them out and serve when cold.
3. If they are to be preserved for a long time, instead of boiling them at once, boil them each day in degrees ranging over 5 or 6 days.

title: Marmalade of Ginger
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of ginger
- 1 chhatak of pasted leaves of Indian black berry (kala jam)
- 2 seers of water
- 1 seer of sugar
- powders of cardamoms, cinnamon and camphor - 1 pinch each
- 5 or 6 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Peel 1 seer of ginger and perforate them all over with an iron pin.
2. Keep them immersed in lime water for 3 days and then wash them with plain water.
3. Boil them with 1 chhatak of pasted leaves of Indian black berry (kala jam) in about 2 seers of water till fully boiled.
4. Wash them 3 or 4 times with plain water and drop them in a boiling syrup of 1 seer of sugar.
5. Mix powders of cardamoms, cinnamon and camphor 1 pinch each and 5 or 6 drops of essence of rose with the mass when the syrup is very thick.
6. Then take down, serve one small piece to each when cold and preserve in bottles.

title: Marmalade of Green Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- full sized green mangoes
- lime water
- salt
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Peel some full sized green mangoes and cut them in big pieces
2. Keep them in lime water for about half an hour
3. Wash them with fresh water and keep aside for a little while more after smearing them with salt
4. Boil them in water for a few minutes
5. Drop them in a hot syrup of sugar
6. Apply mild heat
7. Take down when the syrup being thick sticks to the sides of the pieces of mangoes

title: Marmalade of Myrobalans
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of myrobalans
- water
- 1.5 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1 seer of myrobalans with water till quite soft and drop them in a boiling syrup of 1.5 seer of sugar.
2. Take down when the syrup is very thick.

title: Marmalade of Pine-Apples
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- not-very-ripe pine-apples
- sugar (equal weight to pine-apple pieces)
- cassia leaves
- cinnamon pieces

## Directions

1. Peel thickly a few not-very-ripe pine-apples, cut in very big pieces and remove the eyes and the central stems
2. Cover the bottom of a pan with cassia leaves and a few pieces of cinnamon
3. Put half the sugar on them and on the sugar put the pieces of pine-apples in a layer
4. Sprinkle the rest of the sugar above
5. Continue to heat the pan on mild heat
6. Take down when the thick syrup sticks to the sides of the pieces

title: Marmalade of Pine-Apples (Alternative Method)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pine-apples
- cassia leaves
- cloves
- cardamom seeds
- powdered coriander
- ghee
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the pine-apples in small pieces
2. Fry a few cassia leaves, cloves, cardamom seeds and a little powdered coriander with a little ghee
3. Add the pieces of pine-apples and the sugar
4. Stir from time to time
5. Take down and preserve when the syrup is very thick and sticks to the sides of the pieces

title: Marmalade of White Gourd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ripe and old white gourd
- 1 seer of white gourd pieces
- 1 chhatak of sabeda
- 1 tola of powdered alum
- water
- 1 seer of sugar
- 1 tola of powdered cardamoms

## Directions

1. Peel a ripe and old white gourd, cut in big pieces, remove the seeds, perforate them all over with an iron pin and keep 1 seer of such pieces in cold water for about an hour.
2. Then smear them with 1 chhatak of sabeda and 1 tola of powdered alum, and boil in water.
3. Then wash them with plain water and drop in a boiling syrup of 1 seer of sugar.
4. Mix with 1 tola of powdered cardamoms when the syrup becomes very thick.
5. Then take down.

title: Marmalade Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of fresh beef suet
- two ounces of moist sugar
- a quarter of a pound of well-grated bread-crumbs
- half a pint of new milk
- three eggs
- about one pound of marmalade
- loaf sugar
- orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Shred six ounces of fresh beef suet, and chop it up fine
2. Mix it with two ounces of moist sugar, a quarter of a pound of well-grated bread-crumbs, and then stir in half a pint of new milk
3. Add the well-beaten yolks of three eggs
4. Whisk all together for a quarter of an hour
5. Set it to stand on a cold stone for an hour
6. Butter a pudding-dish or mould thickly
7. Place a layer of the above mixture in it, then a layer of marmalade, another layer of mixture, and so on alternately until the mixture is exhausted
8. Whisk the whites of the eggs with a little loaf sugar and orange-flower water
9. Place the froth at the top of the pudding
10. Bake for an hour and a half in a moderate oven

title: Marmalade Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of fresh beef suet
- two ounces of moist sugar
- a quarter of a pound of well-grated bread-crumbs
- half a pint of new milk
- the well-beaten yolks of three eggs

## Directions

1. Shred six ounces of fresh beef suet and chop it up fine
2. Mix it with two ounces of moist sugar and a quarter of a pound of well-grated bread-crumbs
3. Stir in half a pint of new milk
4. When these are all mixed, add the well-beaten yolks of three eggs
5. Whisk all together for a quarter of an hour

title: Mashed Potato Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potato
- minced onion
- green chilli
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Flavour mashed potato with minced onion, green chilli, salt, pepper, vinegar, and a pinch of sugar.

title: Mashed Potatoes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- milk
- butter
- salt
- sweet onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes thoroughly
2. Work them through a wire sieve to get them smooth
3. Add a little milk, as much butter as you can spare, and a little salt
4. Form with the wooden spoon
5. Brown the outside in the oven

title: Masur Dal (Alternative)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Masur dal 1/2 seer
- ghee 1/4 powa
- curd 1 chhatak
- ginger-juice 1 tola
- pepper 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- cassia leaves a few
- salt 1 1/2 tola
- cardamom, cinnamon and clove 1/4 tola altogether
- panch-foron 1 pinch
- water 1 seer
- green chillies a few

## Directions

1. Wash the dal and keep them under water for about 20 minutes, and then drain off the water.
2. Heat half the ghee in a pan with the cassia leaves.
3. Add the dal when the ghee is sufficiently hot and stir till they become light brown.
4. Then add hot water with salt and powdered turmeric.
5. Add curd, ginger-juice and powdered pepper and cumin when the pulses are well boiled.
6. The pulses must be thoroughly melted.
7. Take the pan down.
8. Heat the rest of the ghee in another pan with the panch-foron and the green chillies cut lengthwise.
9. Pour in the liquid dal when the ghee is sufficiently hot.
10. Boil for a few minutes more.
11. Add powdered cardamom, cinnamon and clove, and stir before taking off the fire.
12. Keep it covered.

title: Masur Dal (Simple)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Masur dal 1/2 seer
- ghee 1 chhatak
- curd 1 chhatak
- ginger 1 tola
- pepper 1/2 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- cassia leaves 4 in number
- panch-foron 1 pinch
- salt 1 1/2 tola
- water 1 1/2 seer
- fenugreek 1 pinch

## Directions

1. Boil the washed dal in water.
2. Add powdered turmeric and cassia leaves when it starts boiling.
3. Add ginger, pepper and cumin, all of them pasted or powdered, and curd and salt when the pulses are boiled.
4. Churn the liquid so that the pulses melt thoroughly.
5. Take the pan down.
6. Heat the ghee in another pan with the panch-foron and fenugreek in it.
7. When they are fried pour in the liquid dal.
8. Boil for a few minutes more and take down.

title: Mayarani of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cocoa-nut and shrimps ball
- ghee
- liquid egg
- salt
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare a ball of cocoa-nut and shrimps, cut it in rectangular pieces and brown them in ghee.
2. Dip the browned pieces into liquid egg-contents beaten with salt, roll on bread crumbs and fry to crispness in ghee.

title: Mayonnaise Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a dessert-spoonful of mustard powder
- a tea-spoonful of finely-minced shallot
- white pepper
- oil
- yolks of two raw eggs
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Put into a soup-plate or slop-basin the cold, very hard-boiled yolks of two eggs, a salt-spoonful of salt, a dessert-spoonful of mustard powder, a tea-spoonful of finely-minced shallot, and a dust of white pepper
2. Bruise these together thoroughly with the back of a silver spoon
3. Add a little oil, and work your materials to a paste, dropping in the oil patiently by degrees until you get it nice and moist
4. Throw in the yolks of two raw eggs, and continue your working
5. Add oil without measure, judging by your eye when you think you have made enough sauce
6. Finally add tarragon vinegar, not more than a good table-spoonful
7. When the vinegar is added, the sauce will assume a creamy appearance
8. When worked sufficiently, pass through a block tin strainer to get rid of onion atoms and lumps of egg
9. Ice the sauce, if only for a few minutes, before serving

title: Mayonnaise Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three large, or four ordinary Indian eggs
- salt
- juice of two limes
- salad oil
- tarragon vinegar
- black pepper
- lettuce leaves
- broken aspic jelly
- gherkins
- turned olives
- capers
- fillets of anchovy
- balls of green or yellow butter
- hard-boiled yolks of egg

## Directions

1. Put the yolks of three large, or four ordinary Indian eggs (raw) in a flat joint dish with a tea-spoonful of salt, and the juice of two limes, and beat them well
2. Tip the dish on end at an angle of about 35 degrees, by slipping a thick book under the rim at one end
3. Open a fresh bottle of salad oil, and get an assistant to let the oil fall, in rapidly succeeding drops, upon the centre of the dish, whilst you continue beating the egg mixture upwards to make it pass under the stream of dripping oil
4. Half a pint of salad oil may be thus expended, until you have a sauce as thick as treacle, and of a golden yellow tint
5. Arrange your mayonnaise in its dish
6. Shake a few drops of tarragon vinegar over it, with a dust of black pepper
7. Arrange some well dried lettuce leaves, trimmed with a silver dessert knife, over the mayonnaise
8. Garnish as you like, with broken aspic jelly, gherkins, turned olives, capers, fillets of anchovy, balls of green or yellow butter, plain hard-boiled yolks of egg whole
9. Ice the sauce, and pour it over all

title: Mayonnaise à la Gouffe
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- well made liquid aspic jelly
- green butter
- prawn butter
- balls of fancy butters
- turned olives
- strips of anchovy
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- fish or fowl
- lettuce leaves from the golden heart
- whole boiled prawns in their shells

## Directions

1. In an oval, or round cylinder-mould placed in ice, pour a very little well made liquid aspic jelly
2. As soon as it is all but set, dispose neatly therein a number of little balls of green butter, and prawn butter alternately
3. Cover them with some more of the jelly, set them, and repeat the process in layers until the mould is packed
4. Ice the balls of butter before putting them in, and alternate them differently in each layer, so that the colours may be checkered
5. Leave the mould in ice to consolidate thoroughly
6. When turned out, garnish it externally with balls of the same fancy butters, turned olives, strips of anchovy, and hard-boiled yolks of egg
7. Pack its hollow centre with the fish, or fowl, of which your mayonnaise is composed
8. Crown the centre with lettuce leaves culled from the golden heart, and arrange with some whole boiled prawns in their shells round the margin of the dish outside the mould
9. Keep the mould of jelly and its sauce in the ice box till the nick of time before serving, when the sauce should be poured over the contents in the centre of the mould

title: Meat Bharta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee
- cassia leaves
- cloves
- chopped ginger
- pieces of meat without bones
- salt
- pasted coriander
- water
- pasted poppy seeds
- almonds
- fried and powdered khowa kheer
- curd
- sugar
- boiling ghee
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Heat enough ghee, fry cassia leaves, cloves and chopped ginger in it and add pieces of meat without bones.
2. Stir till the meat turns reddish brown.
3. Add salt, pasted coriander, and sufficient water.
4. On softening add pasted poppy seeds, almonds, fried and powdered khowa kheer, curd and sugar.
5. Boil till the soup is very thick.
6. Finish by adding boiling ghee and powdered garam-masalla.

title: Meat Bharta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Ghee
- Cassia leaves
- Cloves
- Ginger chopped
- Meat pieces without bones
- Salt
- Coriander pasted
- Water
- Poppy seeds pasted
- Almonds
- Khova kheer fried and powdered
- Curd
- Sugar
- Garam-masalla powdered

## Directions

1. Heat enough ghee
2. Fry cassia leaves, cloves and chopped ginger in it
3. Add pieces of meat without bones
4. Stir till the meat turns reddish brown
5. Add salt, pasted coriander, and sufficient water
6. On softening add pasted poppy seeds, almonds, fried and powdered khova kheer, curd and sugar
7. Boil till the soup is very thick
8. Finish by adding boiling ghee and powdered garam-masalla

title: Meat Bread
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit or hare
- salt
- water
- eggs
- sugar
- powdered pepper
- ginger-juice
- lemon-juice
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare a rabbit or hare, cut in big pieces and keep them immersed in saline water for half an hour.
2. Boil the pieces of salted meat in fresh water.
3. Take out the boiled pieces and remove the bones.
4. Mix them with 2 or 3 eggs, sugar, powdered pepper, ginger-juice, a few drops of lemon-juice, a small quantity of flour, salt and a little water in which meat had been boiled, if necessary.
5. Form rectangular cakes of moderate thickness, cover with white paper and fry with butter on mild heat for about half an hour.

title: Meat Chapati
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- turmeric paste
- pepper powder
- cumin powder
- ginger paste
- onion paste
- garam-masala powder
- curd
- salt
- meat slices
- ghee
- water

## Directions

1. Make a paste of turmeric, pepper, cumin, ginger, onions and garam-masala, and mix it with curd and salt.
2. Smear slices of meat 3 inches by 2 inches by 0.5 inch with this mixture.
3. Place them in a layer on hot ghee in a pan and heat slowly placing some weights on them so that they may not shrink.
4. Sprinkle water at intervals to make them soft.

title: Meat Chhenchki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- turmeric paste
- onion paste
- garlic paste
- meat cubes
- ghee
- ginger-juice
- powdered pepper
- garam-masala
- water

## Directions

1. Mix salt and a paste of turmeric, onions and garlics with small cubes of meat and fry them with ghee.
2. 15 or 20 minutes later add ginger-juice, powdered pepper and garam-masala, and more ghee if necessary.
3. Sprinkle water at intervals to make them soft.
4. Take down when deep brown.

title: Meat Chop
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled and peeled potatoes
- salt
- powdered pepper
- white of eggs
- ginger-juice
- minced onions
- powdered chillies
- eggs
- pasted grams
- minced meat
- ground almonds
- raisins
- fried and powdered khowa kheer
- ghee
- water
- bread crumbs or powdered biscuits

## Directions

1. Make a paste of some boiled and peeled potatoes and mix thoroughly with salt, powdered pepper and the white of eggs
2. Mix ginger-juice, salt, minced onions, powdered chillies, eggs or a little pasted grams with minced meat
3. Fry the spiced meat with ghee adding a little water to make soft
4. Take a handful of the paste of potatoes, make a flat disc of it with the hands, put some meat at the centre, close the disc over the meat and give the shape of an almond
5. Dip them into beaten eggs, roll on bread crumbs or powdered biscuits and fry in plenty of ghee till deep brown

title: Meat Chop with Vegetables
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green jack-fruit or cauliflowers or other vegetable
- minced meat
- pastes of ginger
- onions
- pepper
- chillies
- garam-masalla
- almonds
- raisins
- salt
- ghee
- water
- eggs
- powdered biscuits

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of green jack-fruit or cauliflowers or any other vegetable equal in quantity with the minced meat
2. Make a paste of it
3. Mix pastes of ginger, onions, pepper, chillies, garam-masalla, almonds, raisins and salt with the minced meat and fry with ghee
4. Add a little water to boil the meat
5. When the water dries up, add the pasted vegetable and eggs
6. Stir thoroughly
7. Take down when the mass is a stiff paste
8. Form oval shaped cakes of it, dip into beaten eggs, roll on powdered biscuits and fry in plenty of ghee

title: Meat Curry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat 1 seer
- Onions
- Ghee 1/4 powa
- Curd 1/4 powa
- Turmeric 1/4 tola
- Pepper 1/4 tola
- Cumin 1/4 tola
- Garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- Ginger 2 tolas
- Coriander 2 tolas
- Salt 1 1/2 tola
- Chillies
- Cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Make a paste of chillies, ginger, turmeric, coriander and onions
2. Mince some onions
3. Boil ghee and slightly brown the minced onions in it
4. Add the pasted spices and curd
5. Stir for about 5 minutes and add the pieces of meat
6. Brown the meat for about half an hour adding water little by little at intervals
7. Add some water or milk and a few cassia leaves when the meat is properly browned
8. Add powdered garam-masalla at the end
9. Keep a little soup if milk is added, otherwise ordinarily no soup should be left in the curry

title: Meat Cutlet
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- loaf
- water
- eggs
- powdered pepper
- salt
- pasted onions
- green chillies
- meat
- powdered biscuits
- ghee
- mustard
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Keep 3 or 4 slices of loaf immersed in water for 1 or 2 minutes
2. Squeeze the water out and make a paste of these with eggs, powdered pepper, salt, pasted onions and green chillies
3. Cut 1 seer of meat into 10 or 12 slices
4. Thrash both sides of all the slices with a stone and keep them mixed up with the pasted spices for about an hour
5. Roll them on powdered biscuits and fry in plenty of ghee
6. Serve with mustard, slices of boiled potatoes and onions

title: Meat Dhonka
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat halua
- Potatoes big pieces
- Onions
- Ginger
- Cumin
- Pepper
- Turmeric
- Chillies
- Curd or asafetida solution
- Water
- Salt
- Garam-masalla
- Egg beaten
- Ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare meat halua as before
2. Press it flat on a plate
3. Cut in pieces
4. Dip into beaten egg and fry in ghee
5. Brown some big pieces of potatoes
6. Add to them onions, ginger, cumin, pepper, turmeric and chillies all pasted together
7. Stir for a few minutes and add curd or a little asafetida solution
8. A few minutes later add sufficient water
9. On boiling add salt and the fried meat
10. Finish with garam-masalla
11. Keep a little very thick soup

title: Meat Dopenaji
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cumin
- pepper
- coriander
- chillies
- turmeric
- onions
- ginger
- ghee
- meat
- salt
- curd
- powdered spices
- water
- lemon-juice
- pasted garam-masalla
- almonds
- milk

## Directions

1. Powder cumin, pepper, coriander and chillies all together.
2. Make a paste of turmeric, onions, ginger and chillies.
3. Cut some other onions in slices.
4. Heat enough ghee, slightly brown the slices of onions in it and then add the pasted spices.
5. Add meat and salt when the spices are fried.
6. Fry the meat to a reddish brown colour for about half an hour adding water little by little.
7. Add curd and powdered spices.
8. Add water after stirring for a few minutes.
9. Add a little lemon-juice and pasted garam-masalla in the end.
10. Take down when the soup is very thick.
11. For the variant with almonds: make a paste of the almonds, mix it with some milk and condense it if necessary.
12. Add this milk along with curd and powdered spices.

title: Meat Dry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat without bones
- water
- besam
- powdered or pasted poppy-seeds
- almonds
- chillies
- garam-masala
- coriander
- ginger
- turmeric
- salt
- white of eggs
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of meat without bones in water.
2. Add this juice to besam, powdered or pasted poppy-seeds, almonds, chillies, garam-masala, coriander, ginger, turmeric, salt and the white of eggs, and beat thoroughly to a thick paste.
3. Dip the boiled pieces of meat into this paste and fry in enough boiling ghee.

title: Meat Galantine
