author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat without bones
- bread-crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- onions
- eggs
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1 seer of small pieces of meat without bones, 1/4 powa of bread-crumbs and pepper, salt, chopped onions and beaten eggs as required to make a stiff paste.
2. Smear a pudding-bag or a thick piece of cloth with butter and flour respectively.
3. Tie the mass in it and boil for 2 hours.
4. Keep the mass pressed between two large plates till cold.
5. Remove the cloth when set firmly, cut in suitable pieces, decorate with pieces of parsley or pistachios, and serve.

title: Meat Halua
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Khova kheer
- Ghee
- Onions
- Ginger
- Coriander
- Turmeric
- Garam-masalla
- Salt
- Minced meat
- Raisins
- Almonds
- Besam of grams
- Water
- Eggs white
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Fry some khova kheer in ghee and powder them
2. Make a paste of onions, ginger, coriander, turmeric and garam-masalla
3. Mix this paste and salt with minced meat
4. Heat some ghee and slightly brown some raisins and pieces of almonds
5. Add to them minced meat and fry till dry
6. Add besam of grams about 1/4 of the meat in quantity 2 or 3 minutes before the dryness is reached
7. Add water and boil for about 15 minutes
8. Add the fried and powdered khova kheer, the white of eggs and sugar to taste
9. Boil a few minutes more and take down before too dry

title: Meat Kabab with Condensed Milk
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat
- khowa kheer
- besam of grams
- powdered rice
- ghee
- powders of pepper
- cumin
- garam-masala
- salt
- sugar
- ginger-juice
- fried and powdered almonds
- raisins
- water
- eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together to a stiff paste 1.5 seer of minced meat, 0.5 powa of slightly fried khowa kheer, 0.5 powa of besam of grams or powdered rice, 1 chhatak of ghee, powders of pepper, cumin and garam-masala 1 tola each, salt 0.5 tola, sugar, ginger-juice, fried and powdered almonds and raisins as desired, and water.
2. Beaten eggs may be added if desired.
3. Make balls of it and roast on slow heat on an iron fork adding ghee at intervals.

title: Meat Korma
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat 1 seer
- onions 1.5 powa
- curd 0.5 powa
- ghee 0.5 powa
- ginger 1 chhatak
- salt 1.5 tolas
- poppy seeds 1 tola
- chillies

## Directions

1. Make a paste of ginger, poppy seeds, chillies and half of the onions.
2. Mix this paste and the curd with big pieces of meat.
3. Chop the other onions.
4. Heat ghee, slightly brown the chopped onions in it and add the meat.
5. When the water coming out of the meat completely dries up, add a little more water and boil under cover on very mild heat.
6. Take down when this water dries up.

title: Meat Kulia
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat 1 seer
- Potato 1/2 seer
- Ghee 1/4 powa
- Oil 1 chhatak
- Curd 1 powa
- Salt 2 1/2 tolas
- Turmeric 1 tola
- Ginger 2 1/2 tolas
- Cumin 1 tola
- Pepper 1 tola
- Coriander 1 tola
- Onions 1/2 powa
- Garlic pieces
- Chillies
- Cassia leaves
- Garam-masalla 1 tola
- Sugar a pinch

## Directions

1. Make a paste of all the spices including onions and garlics
2. Keep garam-masalla entire or paste separately
3. Mix thoroughly some oil, a little salt and a part of the pasted spices with the meat cut in big pieces, and keep aside
4. Peel the potatoes, cut in big pieces and brown them with oil
5. Boil ghee, fry cassia leaves and a few cloves in it and add the rest of the pasted spices with curd
6. Stir for a few minutes and add the meat
7. Stir thoroughly and cover up the pot
8. Add hot water when the water coming out of the meat dries up
9. On boiling add potatoes and salt
10. Add sugar, garam-masalla, and ghee before taking down
11. Keep a little thick soup

title: Meat or Birds in Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- meat or bird or birds
- jelly

## Directions

1. Clean the meat or the bird or birds
2. Fully roast, bake, or stew in the usual way
3. Place the meat in the mould, or if birds, arrange them with their breasts downwards
4. Fill the mould quite full with the jelly
5. Set it to cool till the next day
6. Turn it on a dish, breasts upwards
7. Garnish the dish with curled parsley, and some of the jelly cut fine, and sprinkled about

title: Meat Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender meat 1½ seers
- rice 1 seer
- ghee ½ seer
- curd 1 powa
- lemon-juice ½ chhatak
- ginger ½ powa
- raisins 1 chhatak
- pistachios ½ chhatak
- almonds ½ powa
- pepper 2 tolas
- cumin seeds ½ tola
- cassia leaves 16 in number
- red peppers a few, if desired
- salt 1 chhatak
- onions 1 powa
- garlic a few pieces, if desired
- cardamoms 10 or 12 in number
- coriander seeds 1 tola

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the almonds and pistachios in pieces after keeping them under water for about half an hour.
2. Cut the onions also in pieces.
3. Make a paste of the gingers and bind it with coriander seeds, and garlics if desired, in a clean piece of cloth.
4. Boil this bundle of spices, and meat with half the salt in about 4½ seers of water till the water is halved.
5. Take the boiled meat out of the juice and mix them with powdered peppers and powdered cumin seeds.
6. Take about 1½ powa of ghee in a pan and put the cassia leaves and the seeds of the cardamoms in it, and heat.
7. Add a few red peppers broken in two, if desired.
8. After about 4 or 5 minutes put the pieces of onions, almonds and pistachios, and fry them in the ghee.
9. When the onions are half fried add the raisins.
10. When the onions are fully fried add the boiled meat and stir continually.
11. When the meat begin to stick to the sides of the pan add the curd and stir till the meat are red. It takes about 15 minutes to make the meat red. Water may be added little by little if necessary.
12. When the meat are red add the rice, washed and dried, and stir for about 2 minutes.
13. Pour the akhni water in the pan with lemon-juice and the rest of the salt.
14. When the mass is boiled and the juice is dried up pour the rest of the ghee after heating it.
15. Stir the contents for 2 or 3 minutes and take down the pan.

title: Meat Toast
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender meat
- onion paste
- ginger paste
- chilli paste
- garam-masala
- cumin
- curd
- ghee
- chopped onions
- powdered pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Smear a lump of tender meat with pasted onions, ginger, chillies, garam-masala and cumin.
2. Thrust the sharp end of an iron rod into the meat and roast on charcoal fire turning round and round till deep brown dropping a mixture of curd and ghee at intervals.
3. Cut it, when prepared, into pieces and serve with chopped onions, powdered pepper and salt.

title: Melted Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of butter
- flour
- about a wineglass of water

## Directions

1. Dust a little flour over a quarter of a pound of butter, and put it into a saucepan, with about a wineglass of water
2. Stir it one way constantly till it is melted, and let it just boil

title: Melted Butter Sauce (Sauce Blanche)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces and a half of butter
- two ounces of flour
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a pint of broth, or milk and water
- a pinch of sugar

## Directions

1. Melt your lump of butter at the bottom of your sauce-pan
2. Add the flour, which soon amalgamates with the melted butter
3. By degrees add the water, or milk and water (boiling) with a pinch of salt
4. Work this well with a wooden spoon till it is soft and creamy to look upon
5. Pass it through your tin strainer into a hot sauce boat
6. As you serve it, add a pat of fresh butter the size of a rupee, which will melt of its own accord

title: Meringues
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of seven eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Put the whites of seven eggs into a bowl, and whip them as stiffly as possible
2. Add half a pound of sugar, mix well
3. With a spoon set portions of the mixture at intervals on sheets of buttered paper
4. Each piece should be the size and shape of an egg
5. Dredge some pounded sugar over them, and put them in the oven upon a baking-sheet
6. As soon as they assume a pale yellow tint, remove them from the oven, detach them from the paper, and cut them in halves with a very sharp knife
7. Scoop out the inside with a spoon as carefully as you can, and return them to a moderate oven to dry
8. Fill the pieces with any nicely flavoured whipped cream, join the halves together with white of egg cement, and serve piled up upon a napkin

title: Methai of Kumra
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 big ripe white gourd
- 1½ seer sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Peel a big ripe white gourd, cut in big pieces and remove all the seeds
2. Make perforations all over the pieces with an iron pin
3. Boil them in a syrup of 1½ seer till it is very thick and scanty
4. Take the pan off fire and shake it till the syrup is mixed up with the pieces
5. Arrange the pieces on a plate and fan them to cool

title: Methai of Kumra
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big ripe white gourd
- 1 1/2 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel a big ripe white gourd, cut in big pieces and remove all the seeds.
2. Make perforations all over the pieces with an iron pin.
3. Boil them in a syrup of 1 1/2 seer till it is very thick and scanty.
4. Then take the pan off fire and shake it till the syrup is mixed up with the pieces.
5. Arrange the pieces on a plate and fan them to cool.

title: Mihidana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- besam 2.5 powas
- sabeda 1.5 powas
- water
- saffron
- ghee
- sugar syrup
- kasir chini
- cardamom seeds
- raisins
- pistachios
- almonds

## Directions

1. Make a moderately thick paste of besam 2.5 powas and sabeda 1.5 powas with water
2. Mix with very little saffron if desired to give a reddish colour
3. Hold a finely perforated iron ladle over a pan of boiling ghee
4. Pour some of the solution in the ladle and shake it up and down when the solution will fall in the ghee in very fine drops
5. Take them out when they are fried
6. Keep them in a concentrated syrup of sugar (preferably of kasir chini) till saturated with it
7. Mix with cardamom seeds, raisins, pieces of pistachios and almonds and a little hot ghee
8. Make balls of it if desired

title: Milk Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fine rice, 1 seer
- pure cow-milk, 1½ seers
- ghee, ½ seer
- meat, 1 seer
- cloves, ½ anna
- cinnamon, 1 anna
- cardamom, 1 tola
- cardamom big quality, 8 in number
- mace, 1 tola
- coriander, ½ chhatak
- cassia leaves, 1 tola
- white pepper, ½ tola
- cubebs, ½ tola
- pepper with cumin seeds, ½ tola
- raisins, 1½ chhatak
- pistachios, 1 chhatak
- almonds, ½ powa
- sugar, 1 chhatak
- curd, ½ powa
- milk condensed, ½ powa
- salt, 1 chhatak
- saffron, ½ tola
- water, 4½ seers
- ginger, ½ powa

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in water and dry them up.
2. Keep the rice in the milk for about 1½ hours till they absorb the milk to the maximum.
3. Tie the swelled rice loosely in a piece of clean cloth and allow to cook.

title: Milk Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- six quarts of rum
- one quart of brandy
- one quart of lime-juice
- two seers of soft sugar
- three quarts of cold water
- two seers of pure milk
- the rinds of forty limes
- three nutmegs

## Directions

1. Steep for two days in a bottle of the rum the peels of the forty limes
2. boil in the three quarts of water the two seers of soft sugar, and grate in the nutmeg
3. pour all the rum and syrup into a large vessel
4. add gradually the quart of lime-juice and two seers of milk, boiling hot, stirring the whole time
5. let it stand for an hour or two
6. strain through flannel several times until it drips clear
7. bottle

title: Milk with Egg
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- contents of a fresh egg
- 1 cup hot milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour the contents of a fresh egg in a cup of hot milk and mix thoroughly.
2. Add a little sugar to taste.
3. This diet is an excellent body-builder and should be taken every morning.

title: Milk-Bread with Eggs
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 seers milk
- 1 1/2 powa sugar
- 1/2 seer loaf
- contents of a few fresh eggs
- a little rose water

## Directions

1. Boil about 3 seers of milk with 1 1/2 powa of sugar till it is boiled.
2. Add a loaf weighing 1/2 seer, the contents of a few fresh eggs and a little rose water, and mix thoroughly.
3. Heat it on charcoal fire till thick when milk-bread will be ready to be served.

title: Mille Fruit Iced Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- the juice of three lemons
- the peel of one lemon
- one dessertspoonful of orange marmalade
- one dessertspoonful of dried cherries
- one dessertspoonful of preserved angelica
- half a pint of syrup
- a pint and a half of cream, or a pint of water
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Strain the juice of three lemons, and grate the peel of one
2. mince finely a dessertspoonful each of orange marmalade, dried cherries, and preserved angelica
3. add to these half a pint of syrup, and mix the whole with a pint and a half of cream, or a pint of water, and then drop in here and there a few drops of the prepared cochineal
4. Put it into a mould, and freeze as above directed

title: Minced Meat Pancake
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat
- chopped olive
- anchovy
- sausage meat
- good sauce
- eggs
- pan-cake batter
- bacon
- spiced pepper
- bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Make your mince and flavour it with a little chopped olive, anchovy, sausage meat, &c.
2. Bind it with a little good sauce thickened with a couple of eggs
3. Let it get cold
4. Make a good sized thin pan-cake
5. Take it from the pan when almost done and put it on a dish
6. Arrange some slices of cold cooked bacon upon it
7. Lay the mince upon the bacon
8. Give it a dust of spiced pepper
9. Fold the pan-cake over it
10. Brush it over with an egg and bread-crumb it
11. Bake it a golden brown in your oven

title: Minced Veal Potato Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- ham
- bread-crumb sippets
- butter
- mashed potatoes
- gravy

## Directions

1. Make a good rich veal mince, mixed with a little ham, and some sippets of bread-crumb cut into small squares, diamonds, &c., and fried in butter
2. Line the pie-dish with mashed potatoes as above directed
3. Fill into it the veal mince, with plenty of gravy
4. Arrange the sippets, cover over with a thick crust of the mashed potatoes, and bake for a quarter of an hour

title: Mincemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- Three large lemons
- Three large apples
- One pound of stoned raisins
- One pound of currants
- One pound of suet
- Two pounds of moist sugar
- One pound of sliced candied orange-peel
- One ounce of sliced candied citron
- One ounce of lemon-peel
- One teacupful of brandy
- Two tablespoonfuls of orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Grate the rinds of the lemons, squeeze out the juice, strain it, and boil the remainder of the lemons until tender enough to pulp or chop very finely
2. Add to this pulp the apples, which should be baked, and their skins and cores removed
3. Put in the remaining ingredients one by one, and as they are added mix everything thoroughly together
4. Put the mincemeat into a stone jar with a closely-fitting lid, and in a fortnight it will be ready for use
5. This should be made the first or second week in December

title: Mint Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- bruised fresh-gathered mint-leaves
- a bottle of beer
- a soda-water bottle of sparkling lemonade

## Directions

1. Put some bruised fresh-gathered mint-leaves into a large tankard
2. pour over them a bottle of beer well iced, and a soda-water bottle of sparkling lemonade, also well iced

title: Mint Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pounded mint
- minced onion
- green chilli
- green ginger
- an atom of garlic
- tamarind juice
- red pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald the mint leaves before pounding them.
2. Make in the same way as cocoanut chutney, substituting pounded mint for cocoanut.

title: Mint Julep
body:
## Ingredients

- about one dozen young sprigs of mint
- one tablespoonful white sugar
- half a wineglassful peach
- half a wineglassful common brandy
- pounded ice

## Directions

1. Put about a dozen young sprigs of mint into a tumbler
2. Add one tablespoonful of white sugar
3. Add half a wineglassful of peach and the same of common brandy
4. Fill up the tumbler with pounded ice

title: Mint Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- green mint
- 1 tablespoonful of the young leaves
- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Pick and wash some green mint
2. Mince it and add a tablespoonful of the young leaves to four of vinegar
3. Put it into a sauce-tureen, with a teaspoonful of brown sugar

title: Mira Chach-Chari
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- peelings of potato
- sweet gourd
- jhinga
- green banana
- tender pumpkin
- oil
- salt
- ground turmeric
- red chillies
- mustard or cumin

## Directions

1. Cut them in small pieces and wash thoroughly.
2. Boil in water and drain.
3. Fry them with oil.
4. Add the water in which they were boiled with salt, ground turmeric, red chillies, and mustard or cumin.
5. Stir and take down when no soup is left.

title: Mocha Dalna
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- banana flowers
- coriander
- red chillies
- pepper
- cumin
- turmeric
- salt
- ghee
- oil
- cassia leaves
- potatoes
- grams
- cocoanut kernel
- browned broken bori
- milk
- sugar
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Chop one or two banana flowers into very fine pieces, boil them and drain off the water.
2. Mix powdered coriander, red chillies, pepper, cumin, turmeric and salt in adequate quantities with the boiled mass.
3. Boil some ghee or oil with a few cassia leaves and a pinch of cumin, and add pieces of potatoes and a handful of moistened grams.
4. Add the mocha when the potatoes are slightly browned, stir for a few minutes, and pour in water.
5. When it boils add scraped out cocoanut kernel, browned broken bori, a little milk and sugar.
6. Take down after adding powdered garam-masalla.

title: Mocha Paturi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped mocha 0.5 seer
- poppy seed 1 powa
- mustard oil 1.5 powa
- sesame 1 chhatak
- salt 1.5 tolas
- green chillies
- banana leaves

## Directions

1. Keep the chopped mocha for a couple of hours in water.
2. Boil them in fresh water and squeeze off the water.
3. Mix pasted poppy seed, oil, entire sesame, salt and pieces of chillies with them.
4. Place this mass in a flat round shape, one inch thick, on 3 or 4 layers of banana leaves and cover it also in the same way.
5. Bind it, place it on a frying pan and apply mild heat alternately on both sides of the cake for about half an hour.

title: Mochaghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Banana flowers
- Water
- Salt
- Cumin
- Coriander
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Turmeric
- Milk
- Sugar
- Flour
- Bori
- Ghee
- Cassia leaves
- Powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Remove the sepals and other cap-like covers of the undeveloped bunches of bananas, and the thin stick-like hard substances
2. Chop the rest throwing away the extreme ends of the bananas and the central stem
3. Chop one or two banana flowers into very fine pieces and keep them in water for a few hours
4. Boil them in fresh water and drain the water off
5. Add adequate quantities of salt, cumin, coriander, and a little pepper, ginger and turmeric, all pasted or powdered, plus a little milk, sugar and flour
6. Rub the mass thoroughly
7. Mix some browned bori broken in pieces
8. Season the mass with a little ghee heated with a few cassia leaves, add a little water and boil gently for a few minutes
9. Mix a little powdered garam-masalla when the soup is scanty, and take down

title: Mock Crab Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of cheese
- a dessert-spoonful of anchovy sauce
- a dessert-spoonful of made-mustard
- one dessert-spoonful of vinegar
- a pinch of Nepaul pepper
- a little salt
- the yolks of two eggs
- a tablespoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Pound two ounces of cheese with a dessert-spoonful of anchovy sauce, a dessert-spoonful of made-mustard, and one of vinegar, a pinch of Nepaul pepper, and a little salt, the yolks of two eggs, and a tablespoonful of butter.
2. Mix thoroughly in a basin, and proceed as directed for Welsh rarebit.

title: Mock New Potatoes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- waxy potatoes
- flour
- butter
- mace
- milk
- egg yolk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes as usual
2. When nearly done, cut them into pieces the shape and size of a pigeon's egg
3. Make a flour and butter sauce blanche slightly flavoured with mace
4. Put the pieces of potato into it
5. Simmer the potatoes in the sauce, and when thoroughly done
6. Add chopped parsley, a coffee-cupful of milk in which the yolk of an egg has been stirred, and a lump of butter at the last moment and serve

title: Mock-Crab
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of prawn meat
- two ounces of good fresh cheese
- a tea-spoonful of mustard powder
- a dessert-spoonful of salad oil
- a dessert-spoonful of anchovy vinegar
- a liberal dusting of spiced pepper

## Directions

1. Pound everything omitting the prawns and work the mixture till it looks like a very thick mayonnaise sauce, adding oil if necessary to obtain that result.
2. Mingle it with the prawn meat, pounded, and trim it into a shapely little mould for serving.

title: Monohara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee 1 chhatak
- flour 1 powa
- powdered rice 3 chhataks
- middlings 1 chhatak
- moistened and pasted pulses of kalai 0.5 powa
- husked sesame 0.5 powa
- salt
- aniseeds
- cardamom seeds
- ginger-juice
- powdered pepper
- water
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1 chhatak of ghee with flour 1 powa, powdered rice 3 chhataks and middlings 1 chhatak
2. Add moistened and pasted pulses of kalai 0.5 powa, husked sesame 0.5 powa and adequate quantities of salt, aniseeds, cardamom seeds, ginger-juice, powdered pepper and water to make a not-so-stiff paste
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Make small squares of it about 1/4 inch thick
5. Fry in ghee
6. Keep them in a very concentrated syrup of sugar for say half an hour
7. Take them out and roll on dry sugar

title: Monoranjan of Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- khowa kheer 1 seer
- white sugar 1 1/2 seer
- powdered cardamom seeds
- pieces of almonds and pistachios
- 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Heat khowa kheer and white sugar in a brass pot
2. Stir continuously and vigorously till perfectly blended together
3. Mix with powdered cardamom seeds, pieces of almonds and pistachios and 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose
4. Take down from heat
5. Make balls of it
6. Give a flat semi-circular or any other form to them through casts

title: Moog Dal
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- moog dal
- salt
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Wash moog dal thoroughly.
2. Tie very loosely in a piece of clean cloth as they increase four or five times in volume when boiled.
3. Drop the bundle into the rice when the latter start boiling.
4. Mix salt and mustard oil with the boiled moog dal, pressed to a paste, before eating.

title: Moog Samli or Moog Pitha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Moog pulses
- Ghee
- Scraped cocoa-nut kernel
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a preparation of scraped cocoa-nut kernel and sugar as in Chandrapuli
2. Keep some pulses of moog under water for 2 hours
3. Fry them with ghee and make a stiff paste of them without water
4. Make medium balls of it, press a little flat
5. Stuff each with a little of the above preparation and make balls again
6. Fry them in plenty of ghee

title: Moog-Dal
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried moog-dal 0.5 seer
- Ghee 1 chhatak
- Turmeric 0.5 tola
- Pepper with cumin seeds 0.5 tola
- Coriander 0.5 tola
- Cassia leaves a few
- Red chillies 3 or 4
- Salt 1.5 tola
- Sugar 1 tola
- Water 1 seer
- Panch-foron 1 pinch

## Directions

1. Entire moog pulses are generally heated in a pot for a few minutes and then they are grinded and husked. These are called fried moog-dal.
2. Heat water in a pan. Wash the moog-dal thoroughly and add them in the water when it boils.
3. Put the lid on keeping a small opening.
4. Clear off the scum formed on the surface from time to time throughout the entire run.
5. Add the powdered turmeric and the sugar when the pulses are soft.
6. Add the pepper with cumin and the coriander, all pasted or powdered, when the pulses melt.
7. Add salt and then take the pot down from the oven.
8. Take the ghee in another pan and heat with the cassia leaves, red chillies broken in pieces, and the panch-foron.
9. Pour the boiled liquid dal when the red chillies turn black.
10. Boil it for a few minutes more and take down.

title: Moog-Dal (Alternative)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried moog-dal 1 seer
- ghee 1 powa
- pepper with cumin 1/4 tola
- coriander 1/2 tola
- cloves 1/2 tola
- ginger 1 tola
- saffron 1/4 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- salt 1 tola
- panch-foron 1 pinch
- cassia leaves a few
- water 1 seer

## Directions

1. Make separate pastes or powders of the mixture of pepper and cumin, coriander, ginger and turmeric.
2. Dissolve the salt and turmeric in the water.
3. Take half the ghee in a pan and heat with half the cloves.
4. Add the moog-dal, washed and dried, when the smell comes out, and stir.
5. After frying for a few minutes, add the water with turmeric and salt.
6. After boiling for about 5 minutes add the juice of the ginger, either pasted or powdered spices, and saffron dissolved in a little water.
7. Stir for a while and put the lid on.
8. Take the pan down after a short time.
9. Heat the rest of the ghee in another pan with the rest of the cloves, the panch-foron and a few cassia leaves.
10. Pour in the boiled dal when the smell comes out.
11. Boil till the dal is thick.
12. Extra water may be added wherever necessary.
13. The dal should not be melted in this preparation but should be well boiled while remaining entire.

title: Moong Dal
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of raw dal or half a cupful
- three-quarters of a chittack of ghee
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- half a dozen onions

## Directions

1. Clean, pick, and roast the dal
2. Mix it up with all the ground condiments and salt
3. Put into a pot and pour water over the whole, some two inches above the dal
4. Boil it well until the dal has quite dissolved, allowing it to cake as it were en masse
5. Churn the dal by twirling it in a wooden instrument called a phootnee
6. Warm the ghee in a separate pot and fry the onions
7. Chop the onions and throw into the churned dal
8. Pour the dal into the pot of melted ghee and keep stirring until the dal and ghee have well mixed
9. Put the cover on and allow to simmer over a slow fire for about a quarter of an hour

title: Moringa Pod Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- enough young moringa pods to yield seeds enough to fill a little pie-dish
- a tablespoonful of cream, or a coffee-cupful of milk
- yolks of two eggs
- salt
- pepper
- a few drops of anchovy essence
- butter
- Parmesan or any nice mild dry cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the moringa pods
2. Scrape out the seeds and the tender flesh inside the pods into a basin
3. Stir into this a tablespoonful of cream, or a coffee-cupful of milk in which the yolks of two eggs have been well beaten
4. Season with salt and pepper, and add a few drops of anchovy essence
5. Pass this into a well buttered pie-dish, and grate over the surface a good layer of Parmesan or any nice mild dry cheese
6. Bake for a quarter of an hour, and serve

title: Moringa Pods au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- young moringa pods
- a table-spoonful of cream or a coffee-cupful of milk
- yolks of two eggs
- salt and pepper
- a few drops of anchovy essence
- Parmesan or mild dry cheese

## Directions

1. Buy enough young moringa pods to yield seeds enough to fill a little pie-dish
2. Boil them and scrape out the seeds and tender flesh inside the pods into a basin
3. Stir into this a table-spoonful of cream or a coffee-cupful of milk in which the yolks of two eggs have been well beaten
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add a few drops of anchovy essence
6. Pass this into a well buttered pie-dish
7. Grate over the surface a good layer of Parmesan or any nice mild dry cheese
8. Bake for a quarter of an hour and serve

title: Moringa Root Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cupful of moringa root shavings
- half a pint of chicken broth
- yolks of three eggs
- a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape as finely as you can a cupful of the root shavings
2. Simmer them in half a pint of chicken broth
3. When done, thicken the broth custard-wise with the yolks of three eggs beaten up with a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
4. Add pepper, salt, and a very little grated nutmeg
5. Serve in a sauce-boat

title: Mould of Foie Gras
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- small tin of Pate de foie gras
- thick slice of fat bacon
- spiced pepper
- minced shallot
- parsley
- marjoram
- finely minced liver
- crumb of a roll
- stock

## Directions

1. Cut a thick slice of fat bacon into dice, and try it with a seasoning of spiced pepper, some minced shallot, parsley, and marjoram
2. When the bacon is melted, add some finely minced liver
3. Fry it in the hot bacon, and then turn the contents of your saide-pan into a bowl to cool
4. Break up the pate, pick out the truffles, and foie gras, and place them on one side
5. Pound the pate forcemeat, and the cooled liver and bacon, together with the crumb of a roll, soaked in a stock, say about a quarter the bulk of the meat
6. Pass this through the sieve
7. Choose a nice sized plain mould, butter it, and pack it with alternate layers of forcemeat, and foie gras, dotting the truffles in according to fancy
8. Steam the mould in your bain-marie for three-quarters of an hour
9. Let it get cold, turn it out, glaze, and ice it
10. Serve with dry toast

title: Mould of Strawberries
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pot of strawberry jam
- water
- cochineal
- glass of liqueur or brandy
- ounce of gelatine
- almonds
- fresh strawberries

## Directions

1. Dilute a pot of strawberry jam with sufficient water to make enough syrup to fill the mould
2. Strain it and colour it a rosy pink with a drop or two of cochineal
3. Add a glass of liqueur or brandy
4. Melt an ounce of gelatine and strain it when cold into the syrup, stirring well
5. Decorate a plain mould with almonds and put it on ice
6. Pour the strawberry syrup into it by degrees
7. Cover it over and set it in ice for two hours
8. Turn it out and serve it with cold custard in glasses or iced cream
9. If in season, you can make the syrup with fresh strawberries
10. When filling the mould, you may add to its attractiveness by setting layers of whole fruit in it in the style of a jelly

title: Mowa of Khai
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seers of dried paddy
- 1 seer of sugar
- 1 seer of fresh khai
- 1/2 tola of cardamom seeds
- 1/2 tola of powdered cinnamon
- 1 tola of powdered pepper
- 1 tola of dried and powdered ginger
- very little camphor
- pasted almonds and pistachios
- 1/2 powa of pure cows' ghee
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 2 seers of dried paddy out on a cold moist floor for 2 to 3 hours
2. Heat a quantity of sands in a pan till they are very hot
3. Throw a handful of the paddy on the hot sands and stir them
4. The paddy will burst into khai in half a minute
5. Remove them immediately to avoid burning
6. Fry the whole quantity of paddy in this way
7. Remove the chaffs
8. Boil a syrup of 1 seer of sugar
9. Add 1 seer of fresh khai and stir constantly
10. When the khai are powdered and mixed up with the syrup add seeds of small cardamoms 1/2 tola, powdered cinnamon 1/2 tola, powdered pepper 1 tola and dried and powdered ginger 1 tola and very little camphor
11. Add pasted almonds and pistachios as desired
12. Stir till the mass is gluey
13. Taking down mix with 1/2 powa of pure cows' ghee
14. Make balls when it is yet warm
15. Roll the ball on powdered sugar and serve when cold

title: Mukhikachu
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Kachu
- Potatoes
- Ghee
- Ground turmeric
- Red chillies
- Coriander
- Pepper
- Cumin
- Minced onions
- Ginger-juice
- Salt
- Water
- Cassia leaves
- Powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. First boil the kachu and then peel it off
2. Peel some potatoes and cut into small pieces
3. Brown the vegetables in ghee and add ground turmeric, red chillies, coriander, pepper, cumin, minced onions, ginger-juice, salt and water
4. Season the curry with ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves, red chillies and minced onions
5. Add powdered garam-masalla when the soup thickens, and take down

title: Mula-Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Radishes
- Water
- Oil
- Cassia leaves
- Til

## Directions

1. Peel and chop a few radishes into small pieces and boil them in water
2. Drain off the water and again wash them with cold water to weaken the disagreeable smell
3. Brown the boiled pieces in some oil heated with a few cassia leaves
4. Add some til and a little

title: Mula-Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- radishes
- oil
- cassia leaves
- til
- pepper
- cumin
- coriander
- water
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- browned broken bori
- ghee

## Directions

1. Peel and chop a few radishes into small pieces and boil them in water.
2. Drain off the water and again wash them with cold water to weaken the disagreeable smell.
3. Brown the boiled pieces in some oil heated with a few cassia leaves.
4. Add some til and a little pepper, cumin and coriander, all powdered, with water and a little milk.
5. When it begins to boil add salt, sugar and browned broken bori, and mix thoroughly.
6. Add some ghee before taking down.

title: Mulligatawny Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- a full-sized curry chicken
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- onions
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- half a teaspoonful of roasted and ground coriander-seed
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of roasted and ground cumin-seed
- two teaspoonfuls of salt
- one chittack or two ounces of roasted and ground poppy-seeds
- half a dozen of the kurreeah fool leaves
- limes

## Directions

1. Prepare a shin of beef soup as above, omitting the sauce, wine, and white of egg
2. Set the soup aside
3. Take a full-sized curry chicken; cut it up into sixteen or eighteen pieces, and wash them thoroughly
4. Warm a pot and melt in it two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
5. Fry in it some finely-sliced onions, and set aside
6. Then fry in the melted ghee the following condiments: four teaspoonfuls of ground onions, one teaspoonful of ground turmeric, one teaspoonful of ground chilies, half a teaspoonful of ground ginger, a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic, half a teaspoonful of roasted and ground coriander-seed, and a quarter of a teaspoonful of roasted and ground cumin-seed
7. Sprinkle a little water over these while frying
8. Then add the cut up chicken with two teaspoonfuls of salt
9. When nearly brown, add one chittack or two ounces of roasted and ground poppy-seeds
10. Pour in the beef soup, add the fried onion and half a dozen of the kurreeah fool leaves
11. Close the pot, and allow the whole to simmer over a slow fire until the chicken be perfectly tender
12. Serve up hot, with limes cut in slices on a separate plate

title: Mulligatawny Soup Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- a chicken
- two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
- onions
- ground onions
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- ground ginger
- ground garlic
- roasted and ground coriander-seed
- roasted and ground cumin-seed
- two teaspoonfuls of salt
- two chittacks or four ounces of roasted and ground chunna ka or gram dal
- strong coconut milk
- half a dozen of the kurreeah fool leaves
- limes

## Directions

1. Prepare a shin of beef as directed above
2. Cut up a chicken; wash it and set it aside
3. Heat a pot and melt in it two chittacks or four ounces of ghee
4. After frying in it and setting aside some finely-sliced onions, fry the condiments in the proportions given in the foregoing recipe
5. Then add the cut-up chicken with two teaspoonfuls of salt
6. Brown it nicely
7. Have ready two chittacks or four ounces of roasted and ground chunna ka or gram dal, which mix thoroughly in a cup of strong coconut milk, and pour over the chicken just as it has become brown
8. Stir it well, and add the fried onions and the soup, with half a dozen of the kurreeah fool leaves
9. Close the pot, and allow the whole to simmer for three-quarters of an hour
10. Serve up hot, with limes, either whole or cut in slices, on a separate plate

title: Mulligatunny
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a well-nurtured chicken or young fowl
- cold water
- a couple of good-sized onions
- two table-spoonfuls of butter
- a couple of table-spoonfuls of mulligatunny paste or curry-powder
- a couple of pints of warm water
- a couple of ounces of almonds
- a coffee-cupful of milk
- a pinch of sugar
- a dessert-spoonful of good chutney
- a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a tea-spoonful of lime-juice
- a table-spoonful of butter
- one of flour
- muslin

## Directions

1. Cut up a well-nurtured chicken or young fowl as if for fricassee, soak the pieces in cold water for a quarter of an hour
2. Slice up a couple of good-sized onions, and put them, with two table-spoonfuls of butter, into a stew-pan on a good fire
3. Fry the chicken and onions together till slightly browned
4. Pick out the chicken, and stir into the butter a couple of table-spoonfuls of mulligatunny paste or curry-powder
5. Cook the paste or powder with the butter and onions for five minutes
6. Stir in a couple of pints of warm water
7. Add the chicken; and if the pieces are not quite covered, put in water enough to do so
8. Let the contents come to the boil, then ease off the fire, and simmer for half an hour very gently
9. Pound a couple of ounces of almonds in a mortar, with a coffee-cupful of milk, give it a pinch of sugar, and let the mixture stand till wanted
10. Ascertain that the chicken is quite tender, then stir in a dessert-spoonful of good chutney, a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, and a tea-spoonful of lime-juice
11. After five minutes' simmering, strain off the whole of the liquid into a bowl
12. Pick out the nicest pieces of chicken for garnish, and put them aside
13. Skim the surface of the liquid, and when quite clear of grease, proceed to thicken it, using a table-spoonful of butter and one of flour, and stirring in the soup slowly
14. Strain into the sauce-pan the almond milk, using a piece of muslin in order to catch up the bits of nut
15. Let the mulligatunny come to the boil, and serve

title: Mulligatunny
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- large sweet onion
- two ounces good butter
- three table-spoonfuls Barrie's Madras mulligatunny paste
- veal stock
- table-spoonful red currant jelly
- few drops lemon juice
- dessert-spoonful Madras chutney
- four ounces sweet almonds
- milk
- two ounces butter
- two ounces flour
- coffee-cupful cream

## Directions

1. Cut up a large sweet onion into fine rings, and fry them in two ounces of good butter, till about to turn yellow
2. Stir in three table-spoonfuls of Barrie's Madras mulligatunny paste, adding sufficient stock to bring the mixture to the consistency of mayonnaise sauce
3. Taste the mixture, and if it requires a little sub-acid, administer a table-spoonful of red currant jelly and a few drops of lemon juice
4. Stir in well a dessert-spoonful of Madras chutney, and add stock enough to produce a thin soup—about three pints in all
5. Allow to simmer for a quarter of an hour to extract the flavours of the various ingredients
6. Pound four ounces of sweet almonds in a mortar with a little milk, using a breakfast-cupful altogether
7. Strain the almond milk into the soup, and stop the simmering
8. Pass the whole of the liquid through a tin strainer into a clean bowl to catch up lumps of onion, chutney, &c.
9. Skim the mulligatunny
10. Thicken with two ounces of butter and two of flour
11. Bring the soup to boiling-point
12. Just before serving, off the fire, stir a coffee-cupful of the best cream into the tureen as the soup is poured into it

title: Mun or Ol (Arum)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- arum
- tamarind or other acidic substance
- ground turmeric
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Peel the arum thickly and cut in big pieces.
2. Dry them in the sun.
3. Boil in a solution of tamarind or other acidic substance so that they may not irritate the mucous membranes of the mouth and throat.
4. Smear the boiled pieces with ground turmeric and salt.
5. Fry in boiling oil.

title: Murki of Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- chhana
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Remove the whole quantity of whey from any quantity of chhana by very high pressure
2. Cut the chhana when very hard in very small cubes of say 0.5 inch at each side with a sharp knife
3. Boil them in a syrup of sugar for say half an hour
4. Take them out, spread on a flat surface and serve when dry

title: Mussoor Dal Chur Churree
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen onions
- three-quarters of a chittack of ghee
- mussoor dal
- ground condiments
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into fine slices lengthways
2. Warm the three-quarters of a chittack of ghee
3. Fry and set aside the sliced onions
4. Fry all the ground condiments
5. Wash the dal well, soak it in water for about a quarter of an hour, and drain it through a colander
6. Fry the dal until thoroughly fried and browned
7. Add only a little water, barely sufficient to cover the fried dal
8. Allow to simmer from ten to fifteen minutes, or until the dal has dissolved
9. Serve up, strewing over it the fried onions

title: Mussoor Dal with Amchoor or with Tamarind
body:
## Ingredients

- mussoor dal
- amchoor or tamarind

## Directions

1. Put the amchoor, or if preferred tamarind, into the pot with the dal
2. Allow it to dissolve
3. When the dal is going through the process of being churned remove the hard stones of the amchoor or seeds of the tamarind

title: Mussoor or Red Dal
body:
## Ingredients

- mussoor dal
- large clove of garlic

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the red dal in the same manner as moong dal
2. Instead of fried sliced onions, cut up a large clove of garlic small and fry it
3. The fried garlic takes the place of the onion

title: Mustard Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of ounces of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- a heaped up tea-spoonful of French mustard
- a pinch of salt
- half a pint of broth or water

## Directions

1. Melt a couple of ounces of butter in a small sauce-pan
2. blend with it a dessert-spoonful of flour, and a heaped up tea-spoonful of French mustard with a pinch of salt
3. when thoroughly mixed, add half a pint of broth or water
4. let it come to the boil, then strain through the pointed strainer into a hot sauce-boat

title: Mutton Brains and Love Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- six brains
- sixteen to twenty large tomatoes
- two chittacks or four ounces of butter
- eight biscuits
- good stock
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the brains well; clean, boil, and halve, or cut each into three pieces
2. Thoroughly butter the dish which will be put on the table; dredge it well with finely-powdered biscuit
3. Lay in the brains; cut the tomatoes, and lay them in the dish between the brains, the cut ends upwards
4. Add a small cupful of good stock, and, after sprinkling a sufficient quantity of pepper and salt as a seasoning, dredge thickly over with the ground biscuit-powder, and bake of a rich brown
5. Serve up hot

title: Mutton Breast
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from breast
- bread crumbs
- salt
- powdered pepper
- cumin
- garam-masala
- butter
- mustard

## Directions

1. Tie up some big pieces of meat from the breast in a piece of cloth and boil.
2. Mix the boiled pieces with bread crumbs, salt, powdered pepper, cumin and garam-masala, and fry them with butter.
3. Stir constantly till brown.
4. Serve with mustard.

title: Mutton Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- a small shoulder of mutton
- ghee
- water
- ground onions
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- ground ginger
- ground garlic
- salt

## Directions

1. Obtain a small shoulder
2. Cut it up into sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces, rejecting all the bones
3. Curry it according to the directions for chicken curry

title: Mutton Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- small shoulder of mutton
- sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces

## Directions

1. Obtain a small shoulder
2. Cut it up into sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces, rejecting all the bones
3. Curry it the same as a chicken
4. Allow it to simmer for half an hour longer, or until the meat is tender

title: Mutton Cutlets in the Russian Manner
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- choplets from a neck of mutton
- fat bacon
- pieces of truffle
- pieces of tongue
- gravy made from the bones, flap, and trimmings
- reduced bechamel or Espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. For this dish select the nicest choplets you can from a neck of mutton
2. Trim them neatly
3. Lard them with fat bacon
4. Introduce some pieces of truffle judiciously here and there by making incisions with a sharp, pointed root knife
5. Pieces of tongue may also be inserted in this manner; they should be cut square at the head and tapering to a point like nails
6. When ready, stew the cutlets very tenderly in gravy made from the bones, flap, and trimmings
7. Take them out, and set them to get cold with a weight above them
8. When cold, mask the cutlets on one side only with reduced bechamel, or Espagnole sauce
9. When this mask or glaze has set, trim the edges neatly, and the cutlet is ready

title: Mutton Cutlets with Cheese Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck cutlets
- melted butter or salad oil
- two or three fair sized Bombay onions
- a ladleful of butter
- a pinch of sugar
- pepper and salt
- a table-spoonful of boiled rice or pearl barley
- a cupful of broth
- a heaped up table-spoonful of finely grated cheese
- a table-spoonful of rich cream
- savoury rice
- French beans a la maitre d'hotel

## Directions

1. Trim the neck cutlets neatly.
2. Dip each cutlet into melted butter, or brush over with salad oil before placing on the grid-iron.
3. Grill over a very fast fire.
4. For the sauce: Parboil two or three fair sized Bombay onions, cut them up roughly and put them into a sauce-pan with a ladleful of butter, a pinch of sugar, and pepper and salt to fancy.
5. Let them cook slowly so that they do not take colour.
6. Add a table-spoonful of boiled rice or pearl barley, and a cupful of broth and let the simmering go on till the lumps of onion are quite soft.
7. Add a heaped up table-spoonful of finely grated cheese, stir this in well for a minute or two.
8. Work the mixture through the sieve as you would a puree.
9. Heat it up gently in the bain-marie.
10. At the last stir in a table-spoonful of rich cream.
11. Serve the cutlets round a wall of savoury rice and fill in the centre with French beans a la maitre d'hotel.
12. Let the sauce go round in a boat with the entree.

title: Mutton Cutlets with Mixed Vegetables
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight cutlets from a neck of mutton
- mashed potato
- sauce an macedoine de legumes
- French beans
- a carrot
- a turnip
- a piece of cucumber
- sauce blonde
- green peas
- boiled celery

## Directions

1. Take the eight cutlets and trim them neatly
2. Grill them over a brisk, clear fire
3. When done, arrange them round a circle of nicely mashed potato
4. Place a sauce an macedoine de legumes in the centre
5. The sauce should contain French beans, a carrot, a turnip, and a piece of cucumber, previously boiled and cut into small dice
6. Heat the vegetables up in well made sauce blonde
7. Add a few green peas and some pieces of boiled celery if available

title: Mutton Cutlets with Valois Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton cutlets
- mashed potatoes a la G.C.
- large sweet onion
- vinegar
- butter
- yolks of five eggs
- chicken glaze
- chopped curled parsley

## Directions

1. Grill the cutlets most delicately
2. Serve them round a socle of mashed potatoes a la G.C.
3. Boil a large sweet onion, cut up into dice, in a coffee-cupful of vinegar
4. Let it boil till the vinegar is entirely reduced
5. Let the onion get cold, then pound it and mix into it an ounce of butter, and the yolks of five eggs
6. Stir this over a low fire for a couple of minutes only
7. Take it off, add another ounce of butter, mix it well, and re-place the sauce-pan on the fire
8. Add half a pint of chicken glaze and another ounce of butter
9. Mix thoroughly, adding finally a table-spoonful of chopped curled parsley
10. Pour this into the centre of your ring of cutlets, and serve
11. This sauce should be made early, and kept hot en bain-marie, for the moment the cutlets are cooked, they must be sent up, and the sauce will spoil if allowed to get cold

title: Mutton Cutlets, en Milanaise Fashion
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck chops
- bacon for larding
- finely sifted crumbs
- minced parsley
- shallot
- grated cheese
- fat for frying
- savoury rice
- maccaroni à l'Italienne

## Directions

1. Trim the neck chops nicely
2. Lard with bacon
3. Set en marinade all day
4. Bread-crumb secundum artem, with finely sifted crumbs, some minced parsley, a very little shallot, and a little grated cheese, all shaken together, the crumbs and cheese in equal proportions
5. Fry a golden yellow in abundance of fat
6. Serve round a hollow mould of savoury rice, with some maccaroni à l'Italienne in the hollow in the centre

title: Mutton Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- flesh part cut in squares

## Directions

1. Cut the flesh part of a shoulder up in squares
2. Doopiaja it exactly as a chicken
3. Allow it to simmer over a slow fire for half an hour longer

title: Mutton Elajadimiar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton 1 seer
- onions 0.5 powa
- carrot 1
- turnip 1
- cumin 2 tolas
- pepper 1 tola
- garam-masalla 1 tola
- butter 3 chhataks
- salt 1.5 tola
- flour

## Directions

1. Mince the meat and fry it with butter till brown.
2. Add water and boil under cover for an hour.
3. Add all the spices pasted together, salt and flour mixed with a little water when the meat is boiled.
4. Meanwhile cut the carrot, turnip and onions in small cubes and boil in water.
5. Heat the rest of the butter in another pan and brown the boiled vegetables in it.
6. Add the boiled meat with soup in it.
7. Boil a few minutes more.
8. Take down with soup as required.

title: Mutton Forcemeat Ball Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- the best parts of a leg or shoulder of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- garden herbs
- bread-crumbs
- egg
- lard or ghee
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Take the best parts of a leg or shoulder of mutton
2. Cut them up, wash, and pound well down
3. Make a gravy of the bones and rejections
4. Mix with the pounded mutton all the ingredients mentioned in the recipe for making beef balls, and cook exactly as the beef cofta curry

title: Mutton Neck
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton 1 seer
- curd 0.5 powa
- butter 0.5 powa
- bread crumbs 0.5 powa
- eggs 5
- garam-masalla 1 tola
- cumin 2 tolas
- pepper 1 tola
- salt 1.5 tola

## Directions

1. Make pastes of cumin, pepper and garam-masalla separately.
2. Cut the meat in small pieces and mix with curd and parts of pepper, cumin and salt.
3. Boil sufficient water and boil the meat in it under cover.
4. Add the liquid contents of the eggs, bread crumbs, salt and all the pasted spices when the meat is boiled and stir thoroughly.
5. Add hot butter and take down after boiling a little more.
6. Keep a little soup.

title: Mutton Raised Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- first class neck of mutton
- kidney fat from the loin
- salt
- black pepper
- chopped curled parsley
- white of egg
- bones and trimmings of the neck
- couple of sheep's feet

## Directions

1. Choose a first class neck of mutton, cut the meat from the bones in one piece
2. Divide that into slices an inch thick, and cut them into squares
3. Keep the fat and lean separate
4. If the neck be a lean one, take a few pieces of nice kidney fat from the loin to assist the pie
5. Thoroughly season the meat when it is cut up, with salt, black pepper, and a table-spoonful of chopped curled parsley
6. Pack the pie as closely as possible, in alternate layers of fat and lean
7. Do not pour in any gravy with the meat
8. When satisfactorily filled, put on the oval cover
9. Cement the edges with white of egg, and pinch them together firmly
10. Brush the top over with a well-beaten egg
11. Bake the pie in a slow oven
12. While baking, simmer the bones and trimmings of the neck, with a couple of sheep's feet cleaned and cut up, and make a clear yet strong broth
13. When cold, the broth should solidify as jelly
14. Flavour it with pepper and salt
15. After the pie has been baked, pour a little of it through an opening in the top of the cover
16. Set the pie in the ice-box, and when quite cold, serve

title: Mutton Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- juice of onions
- ginger
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- two tablespoonfuls of ghee or lard
- stock
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut up a breast of mutton in the usual way for a stew; wash and dry the meat
2. Take of the juice of onions one tablespoonful, of ginger half a tablespoonful, and of garlic a quarter of a tablespoonful; mix with the meat, add pepper and salt, and allow to stand for any time from one to four hours
3. Fry in a large stewpan two tablespoonfuls of ghee or lard, and when on the boil fry to a nice brown all the meat only
4. Afterwards pour in the liquor in which the meat had been steeped, and allow to simmer for fifteen or twenty minutes
5. Thicken some stock with a teaspoonful of flour, and add it to the stew
6. Allow to simmer until the meat is perfectly tender

title: Nankhatai
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1 seer of flour or middlings
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- 2 1/2 powas of water

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of ghee with 1 seer of flour or middlings and again mix with 1/2 seer of sugar.
2. Then make a very stiff paste with about 2 1/2 powas of water.
3. Make balls of it, press them a little flat and fry in plenty of ghee till brown.

title: Narikel Kumri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet gourd
- cocoanut kernel
- oil
- milk 1 powa or more
- sugar 2.5 tolas
- salt 2 tolas
- coriander 3 tolas
- cumin 1 tola
- ghee 0.5 powa
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Peel and cut a small ripe sweet gourd into small and thin pieces throwing away the interior portion.
2. Scrape out a ripe cocoanut kernel.
3. Brown 1.5 seer of the pieces of gourd in oil and add cocoanut, 1 powa or more of milk, sugar 2.5 tolas, salt 2 tolas, powdered coriander 3 tolas and cumin 1 tola.
4. When no soup remains, season the curry with 0.5 powa of ghee heated with a few cassia leaves, red chillies and powdered garam-masalla.
5. Take care that the curry may not turn black at the slightest delay or over-heating.

title: Navets Glacés
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- young turnips
- salt
- water
- melted butter
- powdered sugar
- clear stock
- pepper

## Directions

1. Trim the turnips into shapes like small pears or cones
2. Boil them till nearly done in salt and water
3. Drain them
4. Put them into a saucepan with plenty of melted butter
5. Sprinkle them bountifully with powdered sugar
6. Stir gently over the fire until they begin to brown
7. Add a spoonful or two of clear stock
8. Give pepper and salt
9. Let them simmer till quite tender and serve them in their own sauce

title: Nawab-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 seer of khowa kheer
- 1 powa of sugar
- raisins
- pieces of almonds and pistachios
- a handful of broken sugar-candy
- a pinch of powdered cardamoms
- 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose
- a few entire cardamom seeds

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/4 seer of khowa kheer and drop it into a boiling syrup of 1 powa of sugar
2. Stir on mild heat
3. Add raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios, a handful of broken sugar-candy and a pinch of powdered cardamoms when the mass is thick
4. Take down when it is stiff
5. Scrape the paste together
6. Mix with 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose and a few entire cardamom seeds
7. Form small balls of it

title: Neck-Tie (Egg with Fish)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- a few pieces of fresh fish
- water
- ground turmeric
- salt
- boiled and peeled potatoes
- powdered pepper
- turmeric
- juice of ginger and onion
- fried pieces of almonds and raisins
- contents of a few eggs
- rice-flour
- beaten egg-contents
- middlings or powdered biscuit
- ghee

## Directions

1. Boil a few pieces of fresh fish in water mixed with ground turmeric and salt.
2. Remove the bones and rub some boiled and peeled potatoes with the fish.
3. Add salt, powdered pepper and turmeric, juice of ginger and onion, fried pieces of almonds and raisins, and the contents of a few eggs.
4. Add a little rice-flour, if necessary, and beat thoroughly.
5. Form flat cakes, dip into beaten egg-contents, roll on middlings or powdered biscuit, and fry in plenty of ghee.
6. Serve while hot.

title: Neem Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- tender neem leaves
- oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry tender neem leaves in boiling oil with salt.
2. If desired, add pieces of brinjals after a time and fry along with them.

title: Negus
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts claret or one quart port
- one wineglassful brandy
- two limes
- slight grating of nutmeg
- few cloves
- cardamoms
- sticks of cinnamon
- two teacupfuls boiling water
- two tablespoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. To claret or port add brandy, limes cut into thin slices, nutmeg, cloves, cardamoms, and cinnamon
2. Add boiling water and sugar

title: Nesselrode Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds (shelled)
- two bitter almonds
- a pint of syrup flavoured with vanilla
- ten yolks of eggs
- a quarter of a pound of sugar
- a pint and a half of cream or good milk
- a glass of maraschino
- two ounces of raisins
- two ounces of currants
- two ounces of citron
- a breakfast-cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds (shelled) and two bitter ones
2. Put them into a stew-pan with a pint of syrup flavoured with vanilla
3. Simmer till the almonds are soft, then drain them
4. Pound them in a mortar, and pass them through a fine sieve
5. Put ten yolks of eggs with a quarter of a pound of sugar into a pint and a half of cream (or good milk)
6. Stir over the fire till the custard thickens, then add the almond puree
7. Strain it into a bowl, whip it, and give the liquid a glass of maraschino
8. Stone two ounces of raisins, pick and wash two ounces of currants, and cut up two ounces of citron
9. Cook them in the syrup saved from the almonds, drain and let them get cool
10. Freeze the custard, and work it with the spatula
11. When partly frozen, add a breakfast-cupful of whipped cream
12. When the cream is nearly set, work in the raisins, currants and citron
13. Close the mould, and bury it in ice for a couple of hours

title: Nikhuti
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 seer of middlings
- 1 1/2 powas of water
- a little lime-water
- 1 seer of sabeda
- 3/4 seer of fine flour
- 1 seer of hot water
- sugar syrup
- a little ghee

## Directions

1. Keep 1/4 seer of middlings moist with 1 1/2 powas of water and a little lime-water for about 12 hours
2. Mix it with 1 seer of sabeda, 3/4 seer of fine flour and 1 seer of hot water
3. Fry Bonde with this paste
4. Take them out when they are fried but yet soft
5. Drop them in a syrup of sugar
6. Take them out again when saturated
7. Make a paste of them
8. Mix with a little ghee and make balls of it

title: Nimki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of ghee
- salt
- soda
- black cumins
- 1 1/2 seer of fine flour
- water

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1/2 powa of ghee, a little salt, a half-pinch of soda, and a few entire black cumins with 1 1/2 seer of fine flour
2. Knead into a stiff dough with water
3. Make small balls of it and roll them into very thin discs
4. Fold the discs twice and fry in plenty of ghee till brown
5. Or make big balls, roll into big discs, cut them into pieces of parallelogram shape and fry

title: Norwegian Anchovies
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tin of best sardines
- white pepper
- Bombay onion
- cinnamon leaf
- pepper corn
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Open a tin of the best sardines, take the fish out one by one, and place them on a dish
2. Wipe them free from oil, carefully pick off their skins and divide each sardine in half lengthwise
3. Give the fillets thus obtained a dusting of white pepper
4. Take an oval earthenware pot, see that it is dry and clean, slice a Bombay onion up, and put a layer of the rings at the bottom of the pot, with a cinnamon leaf, and a pepper corn or two
5. Arrange over this a layer of sardine fillets, and continue the process until the pot is filled, or the fillets are exhausted
6. Pour over the layers a marinade of oil and vinegar (one spoonful of vinegar to four of oil)
7. In a few hours the sardines may be eaten

title: Nougat
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of almonds
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Blanch a pound of almonds, and cut each lengthwise into thin narrow pieces
2. Lay them on a dish in front of the fire, or in the oven (with the door open), to get perfectly dry
3. Melt in a sugar boiler 1 lb. of loaf sugar
4. When the sugar is a rich brown, put in the almonds, mix them well but carefully together
5. You will have a soft paste, which will harden when cold
6. Dip some small moulds into some very hot water, slightly but thoroughly oil them with melted butter
7. Put some of the mixture in one of them, and with the handle of a tea-spoon previously buttered, spread it out so as completely to line the mould
8. Trim the edges, and when cold, turn out the nougat
9. Serve with whipped cream or sweetmeats inside each
10. The nougat should be very thin

title: Nougat
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- loaf sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Blanch a pound of almonds and cut each lengthwise into thin narrow pieces
2. Lay them on a dish in front of the fire or in the oven (with the door open) to get perfectly dry
3. Melt in a sugar boiler one pound of loaf sugar
4. When the sugar is a rich brown, put in the almonds
5. Mix them well but carefully together and you will have a soft paste, which will harden when cold
6. Dip some small moulds into some very hot water, slightly but thoroughly oil them with melted butter
7. Put some of the mixture in one of the moulds

title: Nouilles
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sifted flour
- three eggs
- half an ounce of butter
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of sifted flour
2. Put it on the paste board
3. Make a hole in the centre of the flour
4. Break three eggs in it
5. Add half an ounce of butter and a pinch of salt
6. Mix all into a nice smooth paste
7. Roll the paste out very thin, say about the sixteenth of an inch
8. Let it dry
9. Cut it into ribbons an inch and a half broad
10. Put five of these ribbons above one another, sprinkling a little flour between each
11. With a knife cut through them crosswise, making thin shreds like vermicelli
12. Shake them in a cloth with a little flour to prevent them adhering to one another
13. Throw them into two quarts of boiling water for six minutes
14. Use nouilles exactly as you would maccaroni

title: Oeufs a la Suisse
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- mild grated cheese
- cream
- four fresh eggs

## Directions

1. Butter a little pie-dish well
2. Strew a good layer of mild grated cheese at the bottom of it
3. Pour over the cheese a coffee-cupful of cream
4. Break four fresh eggs very carefully and pass them into the cream without breaking them
5. Dust a light layer of cheese over the surface
6. Bake for about seven minutes until the surface slightly colours
7. Serve with eggs just set in a creamy sauce with a little delicate cheese flavouring

title: Oeufs a la Suisse
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- shallow pie-dish
- butter
- cream a quarter of an inch deep
- well grated cheese a quarter of an inch deep
- six eggs
- black pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Choose a shallow pie-dish and butter it liberally
2. Pour over the bottom of the dish a layer of cream a quarter of an inch deep
3. Over that shake a coating of well grated cheese a quarter of an inch deep
4. Slip in one by one as carefully as you can six eggs to avoid breaking a single yolk
5. Give them a dust of black pepper and salt
6. Gently pour a little more cream over the surface
7. Coat it over again with grated cheese
8. Let the dish remain in the oven until the eggs are set without being hard
9. Brown the surface by passing a red hot iron backwards and forwards over it, about an inch above the cheese
10. Serve

title: Oeufs au Fromage
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- thin shavings of cheese
- melted butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Eggs broken gently over very thin shavings of cheese, (which have been placed in melted butter in an omelette-pan over the fire) and allowed to set, are called oeufs au fromage.
2. They should be dusted with pepper, and salt before serving.

title: Oeufs au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie-dish
- butter
- shallot
- maccaroni boiled in milk
- half a pint of sauce blonde
- grated Parmesan or other mild cheese
- hard-boiled eggs
- finely minced anchovy
- pepper and salt or spiced salt
- bread-crumbs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Butter the bottom of a pie-dish previously well rubbed with shallot
2. Line it with some maccaroni already boiled in milk
3. Pour round it half a pint of sauce blonde in which you have dissolved some grated Parmesan or other mild well-rasped cheese
4. Dispose a complete layer of hard-boiled eggs, sliced
5. Sprinkle a finely minced anchovy over the eggs with pepper and salt or with a judicious dressing of spiced salt
6. Apply a nice coating of bread-crumbs and grated cheese mixed in equal proportions
7. Drop a number of little bits of butter the size of a pea over the surface
8. Bake the dish till the top takes a golden brown tint

title: Oeufs au Jus
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- breakfast-cupful of gravy
- minced shallot or sweet herb
- eggs
- bread-crumbs
- minced anchovy or remains of cold fish

## Directions

1. Choose a little pie-dish and pour some of the gravy into it to cover the bottom well
2. Flavour it with a little minced shallot or any sweet herb
3. Set it in the oven and when it is hot, break into it as many eggs as will fill the dish nicely without crowding
4. Shake some bread-crumbs over the eggs and some little pieces of minced anchovy or the remains of any cold fish
5. Return the dish to the oven for three or four minutes so that its contents may partly set
6. Pour the rest of the gravy evenly over the surface
7. Add another layer of fine crumbs
8. Bake for five or six minutes, being careful not to let the eggs harden

title: Oeufs Farcis
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- butter
- tasty trifles for farce
- finely minced olives
- capers
- anchovies
- mushrooms
- truffles
- finely grated ham
- bruised liver of a chicken
- pate de foie gras
- sausage meat
- spiced pepper
- bread
- melted butter
- nicely fried bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil six eggs for half an hour
2. Take them out and plunge them into cold water
3. When quite cold, peel off their shells
4. With a dessert-knife rubbed in butter, divide each egg in half, slicing a little piece of the rounded ends to admit of each half sitting upright upon a dish
5. Pick out the yolks
6. Pound them with butter in a mortar
7. Dress them with any tasty trifles at your command
8. Season the composition delicately
9. Fill the egg cases therewith, trimming the farce neatly with a dessert-knife dipped in melted butter in a convex-shape over each case
10. Fry a little square of bread for each one, as for canapes, and place them thereon
11. Arrange them on a flat silver dish slightly buttered
12. Pour a little melted butter over each egg
13. Bake for five minutes
14. Strew some nicely fried bread-crumbs over the dish when going to table

title: Oeufs sur le Plat
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of butter
- two eggs
- tarragon vinegar
- maitre d'hotel butter

## Directions

1. Melt a tablespoonful of butter in an omelette-pan, and slip two eggs into it, carefully avoiding breaking the yolks.
2. Let them set in the butter, as a poached egg sets in water; the moment they are sufficiently firm, let them slide off into the hot dish ready to receive them, pouring the butter in which they were cooked over them.
3. Put in the eggs the moment the butter melts; for, when first they go in, the pan should not be too hot.
4. A drop or two of tarragon vinegar may be shaken over the dished eggs, or a tiny bit of maitre d'hotel butter the size of a pea may be allowed to melt over each of them.
5. If after dishing the eggs, you return the pan to the fire, and brown the butter before pouring it over them, you have oeufs au beurre noir.

title: Oignons au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- milk
- a coffee-cupful of cream or milk enriched with the yolk of an egg
- pepper
- salt
- grated cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil the onions in milk till tender
2. Cut them up as finely as possible and mash them, pass through a wire sieve
3. Mix the pulp well, adding a coffee-cupful of cream or milk enriched with the yolk of an egg, pepper, and salt
4. Butter a shallow pie-dish and put the puree into it
5. Strew a layer of grated cheese over the surface
6. Sprinkle a little melted butter over the cheese
7. Bake for a few minutes till the top takes colour

title: Ol-Curry
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Ol
- Potatoes
- Lemon-juice
- Oil
- Powdered turmeric
- Coriander
- Pepper
- Cumin
- Red chillies
- Salt
- Ghee
- Panch-foron
- Cassia leaves
- Powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Peel the skin thickly off the ol
2. Cut the ol and potatoes in big pieces
3. Boil the pieces of ol in water with a few drops of lemon-juice and drain off the water
4. Brown the ol and take down
5. Similarly brown the potatoes in oil and then add water with powdered turmeric, coriander, pepper, cumin, red chillies and salt
6. When it boils add browned ol
7. Season the curry when it is fully boiled with oil or ghee heated with a few panch-foron, cassia leaves and red chillies
8. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla before taking down

title: Old Peas (Petit Spots Accommodes)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- Bombay onion
- bunch of mint
- parsley
- salt
- peas
- broth
- flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put a lump of butter into a stew-pan with a Bombay onion sliced, a bunch of mint and parsley, and a tea-spoonful of salt
2. Cook this awhile till the onions take a pale colour
3. Add the peas with as much broth as will just float them
4. Simmer this patiently till the peas are thoroughly tender
5. Take up the pan and strain the liquor
6. Spread out the peas on a dish and pick out the pieces of onion
7. Thicken the liquor with butter and flour, adding a tea-spoonful of sugar
8. Add the peas again
9. Stir well, bring the sauce-pan to steaming point, and serve

title: Olive Royals
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of potatoes
- four ounces of flour
- one ounce of butter
- cold roasted meat
- pepper
- salt
- fresh dripping

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of potatoes, and when nearly cold rub them perfectly smooth with four ounces of flour and one ounce of butter
2. Knead all together till it becomes a paste
3. Roll it out about a quarter of an inch thick
4. Cut it into rounds, and lay upon one side any sort of cold roasted meat cut into thin small bits, and seasoned with pepper and salt
5. Put a small bit of butter over the meat
6. Wet the edges of the paste, and close it in the form of half-circles
7. Fry them in boiling fresh dripping till of a light brown colour
8. Lay them before the fire, on the back of a sieve, to drain
9. Serve them with or without gravy in the dish

title: Omelet Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of six eggs
- white sugar
- lemon essence or vanilla essence or ratafia essence
- whites of six eggs
- finely sifted sugar
- preserve

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of six eggs with a tablespoonful of white sugar
2. Flavour the mixture with lemon, vanilla, or ratafia essence
3. Whisk the whites to a stiff froth independently
4. Blend the two thoroughly
5. Put this into a small circular souffle tin
6. Bake it in a very quick oven
7. Send it to table the moment it is ready, dusted over with finely sifted sugar
8. Serve immediately, close to the dining-room door

title: Omelette Au Parmesan
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ordinary eggs
- a table-spoonful of grated and finely sifted cheese
- salt
- black pepper
- a dessert-spoonful of rich cream or new milk

## Directions

1. Incorporate the cheese, salt, black pepper, and cream or milk with the eggs.
2. Proceed as recorded in the previous directions for making an omelette.
3. Serve just before all the juicy mixture on the surface quite sets, so that there may be an exudation of creamy moisture in the dish.
4. Dust over the surface a canopy of grated cheese.
5. Serve immediately from the fire to the plate.

title: Omelette Aux Tomates
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four ripe tomatoes
- a sweet onion
- a table-spoonful of butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut three or four ripe tomatoes into quarters, pick out the seeds, and let the watery juice run off.
2. Cut a sweet onion into the thinnest slices possible.
3. Melt a table-spoonful of butter in a small saucepan, cast into it the onion slices; let them cook without browning.
4. Add the drained tomato quarters, pepper and salt.
5. Toss the sauce-pan about till the tomatoes are cooked which will be in about ten minutes.
6. Keep the mixture hot, and pour it over the surface of an ordinary omelette just as you are on the point of turning it out of the frying-pan.
7. The omelette will roll over of its own accord enveloping the tomatoes within it as it passes into the dish.

title: Omelettes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- minced onions
- green chillies
- powdered turmeric
- pepper
- salt
- contents of an egg
- ghee

## Directions

1. Mix minced onions, green chillies, powdered turmeric, pepper and salt thoroughly with the contents of an egg.
2. Pour it on a little hot ghee in a pan.
3. Tilt the pan so that it spreads evenly thick and fry both sides till light brown.
4. Roll it up, press flat and fry till golden brown.

title: Onion Puree a la Soubise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three onions
- stock or milk
- coffee-cupful of cream or milk
- egg yolk
- gravy
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Simmer three onions in sufficient stock or milk to cover them till tender
2. Mash them, and pass them through the sieve
3. Work into the pulp a coffee-cupful of cream, or milk enriched with the yolk of an egg, with a little gravy
4. Flavour with a little pepper, and salt
5. Heat it up as hot as possible, and serve

title: Onion Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight good Patna onions
- two chittacks or four ounces of butter, free of water
- a dessertspoonful of finely-sliced curry onions
- a tablespoonful of finely-sifted flour
- half a seer of milk
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and boil six or eight good Patna onions
2. Allow the water to drain away
3. Fry to a light brown colour, in two chittacks or four ounces of butter, free of water, a dessertspoonful of finely-sliced curry onions
4. Gradually mix into it a tablespoonful of finely-sifted flour and half a seer of milk, taking care through the whole operation to keep stirring the sauce to prevent its lumping
5. Add a teaspoonful of salt and a quarter of a teaspoonful of pepper
6. Last of all add the boiled onions, and in a few minutes the sauce will be ready

title: Onions, Plain Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- milk
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel them, and let them lie an hour in cold water
2. put them on in boiling milk and water
3. boil them till tender
4. serve with melted butter poured over them

title: Orange Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- rind of half an orange
- one spoonful brandy
- juice of one orange
- four ounces loaf sugar
- yolk of four eggs
- one pint boiling milk
- preserved orange-peel

## Directions

1. Boil very tender the rind of half an orange, and beat it in a mortar until it is very fine
2. Put to it a spoonful of the best brandy, the juice of an orange, four ounces of loaf sugar, and the yolk of four eggs
3. Beat them altogether for ten minutes
4. Pour in by degrees a pint of boiling milk
5. Beat them until cold
6. Put them in custard-cups into a dish of hot water
7. Let them stand till they are set
8. Take them out, and stick preserved orange-peel on the top

title: Orange Iced Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- a seer and a half of milk
- some isinglass
- a quarter of a seer or half a pound of sugar
- the juice of twelve oranges
- two seers or four pounds of raw rice
- some salt

## Directions

1. Boil down a seer and a half of milk to half the quantity with some isinglass and a quarter of a seer or half a pound of sugar
2. strain through a sieve, and when perfectly cool add the juice of twelve oranges
