3. Mix well, put into freezing-pots with two seers or four pounds of raw rice and some salt, and freeze as above

title: Orange Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- an ounce and a half of isinglass
- three-quarters of a pound of fine white sugar
- a pint of water
- orange and lemon peel
- juice of ten oranges
- juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Melt an ounce and a half of isinglass and three-quarters of a pound of fine white sugar in a pint of water
2. Add some orange and lemon peel
3. Boil until it is a good syrup
4. While warm, add the juice of ten oranges and two lemons
5. Strain the whole through flannel
6. Put it into moulds

title: Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-four oranges
- six lemons
- sugar equal to the weight of the fruit
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Grate the rinds of the oranges and lemons
2. Mark or cut into quarters and strip off the rinds without hurting the pulps
3. Stew the rinds until they become perfectly tender, changing the water two or three times
4. Drain them, scrape out a little of the inside, and cut them into very fine slices or chips
5. Separate the pips, skin, and fibrous parts from the pulps
6. Pour some water over the pulps and strain it off
7. With this and a little more water prepare a syrup in a preserving-pan
8. Add to it the whites of two eggs well beaten up
9. Skim it well, and the moment it begins to boil take it off the fire
10. Continue to remove the scum, add a little more water, boil, and strain until the syrup is perfectly clear
11. Throw in the chips and boil until they are quite transparent
12. Put in all the pulp and juice, and boil until it thickens
13. To ascertain if it has been sufficiently cooked, drop a little on a plate and see if it congeals

title: Orange Marmalade, Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- good fresh ripe oranges
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stew good fresh ripe oranges till perfectly tender, changing the water several times
2. Drain them, and cut and remove the rinds without breaking them or wounding the pulps
3. Weigh the pulps, having previously removed all the pips, skin, and seeds
4. To every six pounds of fruit add seven of sugar
5. Pour boiling water over the pips, seeds, &c., strain them, and take the liquor for the preparation of syrup
6. Skim it well while boiling
7. When clear, add to it the rind, having first scraped and thrown away some of the inside and then cut it up into thin slices or chips
8. After a while add the pulp and juice, and boil it up again until it acquires the consistency of jelly

title: Orange Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, 1 seer
- peeled oranges, ½ seer
- orange juice, ½ seer
- ghee, 1 powa or more
- sugar, ½ powa
- cloves, ½ tola
- cinnamon, ½ tola
- cardamom, ½ tola
- almonds, ½ powa
- pistachios, 1 chhatak
- raisins, ½ powa
- saffron, ½ tola
- milk condensed, ½ powa
- salt, 1 tola
- water, 1 seer

## Directions

1. Fry the raisins, after washing them cleanly, in some ghee.
2. Fry the pistachios and almonds peeled and cut in pieces.
3. Fry the raisins separately as they brown earlier than the almonds and pistachios.
4. Take ½ powa of ghee more and fry the cloves, cinnamons and cardamoms, all entire, in it.
5. When the flavour comes out, add the rice and stir constantly on slow heat till the rice begin to be fried.
6. Add the orange juice and hot water little by little along with salt and saffron.
7. Allow the mass to boil under cover.
8. When the rice are half-boiled add the fried raisins, pistachios, almonds, condensed milk, sugar, peeled oranges and ½ powa of hot ghee.
9. Stir the mass and allow it to simmer under closed cover on slow heat.
10. Take down when the rice are completely boiled and the juice is thick.

title: Orange Syrup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- concentrated syrup of sugar
- 1/2 powa of tincture orange per seer of syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare any quantity of concentrated syrup of sugar
2. Mix 1/2 powa of tincture orange with every seer of the syrup

title: Orange-Syrup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- concentrated syrup of sugar
- 1/2 powa of tincture orange

## Directions

1. Prepare any quantity of concentrated syrup of sugar and mix 1/2 powa of tincture orange with every seer of the syrup

title: Orange-Water Iced
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of water
- the strained juice of three oranges and one lemon
- the grated peel of one orange
- syrup

## Directions

1. Mix with a pint of water the strained juice of three oranges and one lemon, also the grated peel of one orange
2. sweeten it well with syrup, and freeze it

title: Orangeade
body:
## Ingredients

- one orange
- peel of orange
- boiling water
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Squeeze out the juice of an orange
2. Pour boiling water on a little of the peel and cover it close
3. Boil water and sugar to a thin syrup and skim it
4. When cold, mix all together with as much water as will make a rich drink
5. Strain through a jelly-bag and ice

title: Orgeat
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound sweet almonds
- thirty bitter almonds
- one tablespoonful water
- two pints water
- three pints milk
- half a pound loaf sugar
- one pint water
- two tablespoonfuls orange-flower water
- one teacupful good brandy

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound sweet and bitter almonds with one tablespoonful of water
2. Stir in by degrees two pints of water and three pints of milk
3. Strain the whole through a cloth
4. Dissolve half a pound of loaf sugar in one pint of water
5. Boil and skim well
6. Mix with the almond-water
7. Add two tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water and one teacupful of good brandy

title: Orleans Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ounce of gelatine
- cold water
- ten egg yolks
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- quart of boiled milk
- ounce of candied orange peel
- ounce of citron
- ounce of raisins
- ounce of currants
- small tumbler of rum
- crushed ratafias
- ice

## Directions

1. Steep an ounce of gelatine in cold water
2. Make a rich custard with ten yolks of eggs, three-quarters of a pound of sugar, and a quart of boiled milk
3. Add the gelatine to the custard while the latter is hot, and stir it until it is dissolved
4. Strain it into a bowl
5. Cut up one ounce of candied orange peel, one ounce of citron, one ounce of raisins, and one ounce of currants, wash them well, dry them
6. Toss the minced confitures in a frying-pan with a small tumbler of rum
7. As soon as the rum is absorbed, stop and take the pan off the fire
8. Put a mould upon ice, pour a layer of custard into it first with some of the minced fruit
9. When set, put a layer of crushed ratafias, then another layer of custard with fruit, again crushed ratafias, and so on till the mould is filled
10. Cover it in ice and let it rest for an hour
11. Turn it out and serve

title: Ornament for Custards or Creams
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of two eggs
- Two tablespoonfuls of syrup or thin jelly

## Directions

1. Whisk for an hour the whites of two eggs, together with two tablespoonfuls of some syrup or thin jelly
2. Lay it in any form upon a custard or cream, piled up to imitate rock, or it may be served in a dish with cream round it
3. The ornament may be coloured, if desired, with cochineal, saffron, spinach, &c.

title: Ox-Head Brawn
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-head, skinned and cleaned
- water
- vegetables
- flavouring
- salt
- spiced pepper

## Directions

1. Skin and clean an ox-head, or purchase one already skinned.
2. Split it in two, cut out the eyes, break the bones of the jaws, and remove the brains.
3. Let the whole soak for an hour or two in cold water.
4. Put it into a stew-pan with water enough to cover it.
5. Boil very slowly, then add vegetables and flavouring as if for soup.
6. When the meat is quite tender and you can pick the bones away from it easily, strain the meat from the broth and vegetables.
7. Free it from every fragment of bone.
8. Cut up the meat whilst it is hot and juicy, en masse rather small, seasoning it with salt and spiced pepper whilst doing so.

title: Oxtail Soup (Thick)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail tail joints
- butter
- flour
- red currant jelly
- anchovy vinegar
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Strain the soup, saving all the tail joints, and leave it in a bowl for a while.
2. Pick all the meat from the tail, pound it in a mortar, pass the paste through the sieve, and then skim the fat off the bowl of soup.
3. Take a roomy sauce-pan, melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of it, stir in a table-spoonful of flour, and gradually add soup and paste till all has been expended.
4. Let the puree come to the boil (by which time it will be thick enough).
5. Add a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly, a tea-spoonful of anchovy vinegar, and a glass of Madeira.
6. Stir vigorously for a minute and serve.

title: Oxtail Soup, Clear
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin and a half of beef
- an oxtail
- two Bombay onions
- two carrots
- a stick of celery
- sweet herbs
- parsley
- a dozen pepper corns
- a clove of garlic
- a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup
- a teaspoonful of caramel
- Madeira
- lime
- sugar

## Directions

1. Buy a shin and a half of beef, and make the best gravy you can as usual
2. Commence boiling the shin as soon as you can
3. When the shin consomme is ready early in the afternoon, separate the tail at the joints, and throw the pieces into your stock pot with two Bombay onions, two carrots, a stick of celery, and a muslin bag containing sweet herbs, parsley, a dozen pepper corns, and a clove of garlic
4. Add a wine-glass of mushroom ketchup and a teaspoonful of caramel, and simmer the soup for a couple of hours skimming it carefully
5. Strain and clarify the liquor, saving a few joints of the tail as a garnish
6. Add a table-spoonful of Madeira with a squeeze of a lime, and a pinch of sugar

title: Oxtail Soup, Thick
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail soup
- tail joints

## Directions

1. Strain the soup, saving all the tail joints, and leave it in a bowl for a while
2. Pick all the meat from the tail, pound it in a mortar, pass the paste through the sieve, and then skim the fat off the bowl of soup

title: Oyster Fritters en Marinade
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- lime juice
- onion
- whole peppers
- cloves

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters from their liquor in the tin
2. Wipe them
3. Set them en marinade all the day in a soup plate with the juice of three limes, an onion sliced, some whole peppers, and a few cloves
4. Turn them occasionally till they are wanted for the friture-pan
5. Dip in batter and fry according to the previously described method

title: Oyster Kramouakys
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one tablespoonful of brandy
- one tablespoonful of olive oil
- three or four tablespoons of cold water
- three tablespoonfuls of flour
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- whites of three eggs
- about a dozen and a half oysters
- juice of three or four limes
- a sliced onion
- whole pepper
- a few cloves
- yolks of eggs
- thick white sauce
- six slices of bacon
- white of egg
- curly parsley

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks only of three eggs with a tablespoonful of brandy, one of olive oil, and three or four of cold water
2. Mix into this three tablespoonfuls of flour and a salt-spoonful of salt
3. Beat this to a paste for ten minutes, preparing a rather thicker batter than you would for ordinary fritters
4. At the time of using, stir in the whites of the three eggs whipped to a stiff froth
5. Wash your oysters early in the day
6. Parboil them if fresh, beard them, and put them to marinade for two hours in the juice of three or four limes, a sliced onion, some whole pepper and a few cloves
7. Lift them out of this when wanted, cut them up, bind the mince together with a liaison of yolks of eggs and thick white sauce, and let it get cold and firm
8. Divide it into six portions, each the size of an ordinary wine cork, and place them on six slices of previously boiled bacon
9. Roll up your bacon fillets and fix them with white of egg
10. Dip them into the batter and lay them gently in your frying basket
11. Plunge this into a seething bath of fat and fry a golden yellow
12. Serve prettily garnished with curly parsley

title: Oyster Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- flour
- butter
- lemon-juice
- three tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Beard and scald the oysters
2. Strain the liquor, and thicken it with a little flour and butter
3. Squeeze in a little lemon-juice, and add three tablespoonfuls of cream
4. Heat it well, but do not let it boil

title: Oyster Stuffing for Murrel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tin of oysters
- bread-crumbs
- milk or stock
- spiced pepper
- lime peel
- anchovies

## Directions

1. Drain oysters well and cut them up
2. Soak bread-crumbs in milk or stock
3. Mix oysters with bread-crumbs, spiced pepper, a little chopped very finely pared lime peel, and a couple of minced anchovies
4. Stir all together and bind with a couple of eggs

title: Pain de Fromage
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rich custard
- salt
- Nepal pepper
- grated Parmesan or Gruyere
- dissolved gelatine
- whipped cream
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make half a pint of rich custard
2. Season it with salt instead of sugar, and a little Nepal pepper
3. Whip it well
4. Stir in three ounces of grated Parmesan or Gruyere, and half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
5. Set the mixture in a plain mould on ice
6. While setting, stir in a coffee-cupful of whipped cream
7. Turn out the mould
8. Garnish with sprigs of parsley
9. Serve cold

title: Palang Sak Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- palang sak
- pumpkin leaves
- flour
- salt
- turmeric
- pepper
- cumin
- red chillies
- fried fishes
- ghee
- cassia leaves
- green peas
- beans
- potatoes
- brinjals
- browned broken bori

## Directions

1. Chop the sak fine and wash thoroughly.
2. Boil them and drain off the water.
3. Mix flour, salt and powdered turmeric, pepper, cumin and red chillies in suitable quantities.
4. Boil some fried fishes in water, pick up the bones and add the mixture of flesh and water, if desired.
5. Boil some ghee with a few cassia leaves and cumin, and add the mass.
6. Stir constantly so that the mass may not be scorched.
7. Add green peas and some pieces of beans, potatoes and brinjals if you like.
8. Add browned broken bori after a while.
9. The curry is ready when the soup is thick and the mass is completely boiled.

title: Palooree
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer of finely-sifted flour of the chunna ka dal, which has been previously parched
- six large Patna onions
- eight fresh green chilies
- a tablespoonful each of finely-chopped soa mattee, saug, and parsley
- a dessertspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of finely-ground green ginger
- water

## Directions

1. Take of the finely-sifted flour of the chunna ka dal, which has been previously parched, one seer
2. Slice and chop six large Patna onions finely
3. Slice very fine eight fresh green chilies
4. Prepare a tablespoonful each of finely-chopped soa mattee, saug, and parsley
5. Prepare a dessertspoonful of salt and a teaspoonful of finely-ground green ginger
6. Put the seer of dal-flour into a large deep pan, and mix into it all the above condiments
7. Keep adding to it water, very gradually and in small quantities at a time, mixing it briskly the whole while

title: Palooree
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer of finely-sifted flour of the chunna ka dal, previously parched
- six large Patna onions, finely sliced and chopped
- eight fresh green chilies, sliced very fine
- one tablespoonful of finely-chopped soa mattee
- one tablespoonful of finely-chopped saug
- one tablespoonful of finely-chopped parsley
- one dessertspoonful of salt
- one teaspoonful of finely-ground green ginger
- water
- half a seer of the best mustard oil
- fine slices of lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Put the seer of dal-flour into a large deep pan, and mix into it all the condiments
2. Keep adding to it water, very gradually and in small quantities at a time, mixing it briskly the whole while, until it is of a consistency that if poured on a plate from a spoon it will incline to a pyramid, or if dropped into a glass of water will not readily dissolve, but drop to the bottom en masse
3. Take half a seer of the best mustard oil; put it into a deep frying-pan with some fine slices of lemon-peel, and fry it or cook it thoroughly
4. Remove three-fourths of the cooked oil from the frying-pan, and into the remainder, while boiling and bubbling, with a tablespoon pour in the preparation in the shape of rocks
5. Allow to brown, turning them over so that top and bottom may be of the same colour
6. As the oil is being expended clear the pan of all particles which may accumulate, pour in some more of the ready-cooked oil, and continue to fry until all the mixture is fried
7. They should be eaten hot

title: Pampar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 powas of husked pulses of moog, peas or grams
- 1 powa of fine besam
- entire aniseeds
- black cumin
- powdered pepper
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Boil 3 powas of husked pulses of moog, peas or grams till saturated with water.
2. Grind them to a paste.
3. Mix with 1 powa of fine besam and beat till the mass is sticky.
4. Mix with entire aniseeds, black cumin and powdered pepper.
5. Make balls of it and roll into thin discs.
6. Fry in enough ghee or oil.

title: Pampar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Husked moog pulses, peas or grams 3 powas
- Water
- Fine besam 1 powa

## Directions

1. Boil 3 powas of husked pulses of moog, peas or grams till saturated with water
2. Grind them to a paste
3. Mix with 1 powa of fine besam
4. Beat till the mass is sticky

title: Panifal (Water-chestnut)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Panifal 1 seer
- potato 1.5 powa
- ripe coconut
- handful of grams
- bori 1 chhatak
- ghee 0.5 powa
- coriander 1 tola
- salt 1 tola
- turmeric 1/4 tola
- pepper 1/4 tola
- cumin 1/4 tola
- garam-masalla 1/4 tola
- cassia leaves
- pinch of sugar
- water about 1 powa
- little milk

## Directions

1. Peel the vegetables, cut the potatoes in medium pieces and half the kernel of the coconut in small pieces
2. Slightly brown the panifal, potato, coconut and moistened grams in ghee
3. Add water with powdered turmeric, coriander, pepper, cumin and salt
4. After a time add browned bori, a little milk and a pinch of sugar
5. Season the curry when it is boiled with ghee heated with a few cassia leaves and cardamom seeds
6. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down when the soup is thick

title: Pantoa
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer hard chhana
- 1/4 chhatak sabeda
- cardamom seeds
- 3/4 seer ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a fine paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana and mix thoroughly with 1/4 chhatak of sabeda
2. Make slightly elongated balls of it with one cardamom seed at the centre of each
3. Boil 3/4 seer of ghee till the bubbles die out
4. Fry the balls in it till reddish brown
5. Pick them up and keep immersed in a syrup of sugar till saturated with it

title: Pantoa with Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee 1 chhatak
- sabeda 5 chhataks
- cold water
- 1/2 seer khowa kheer
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1 chhatak of ghee thoroughly with 5 chhataks of sabeda
2. Make a stiff paste with a little cold water
3. Mix this again with 1/2 seer of khowa kheer
4. Make a fine paste with the hands
5. Proceed exactly in the same way as before

title: Parfait of Chocolate
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint and a half of strong chocolate
- boiling milk
- two ounces of the best chocolate
- the yolks of ten eggs
- half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
- a wine-glass of very cold syrup flavoured with vanilla
- a pint of well-whipped cream

## Directions

1. Make a pint and a half of strong chocolate, using boiling milk, and two ounces of the best chocolate
2. Sweeten it to taste, and let it get cool
3. Take an enamelled-pan and break into it the yolks of ten eggs
4. Strain the made chocolate, and add it to the eggs, stirring continually over a low fire, or in the bain-marie until the custard thickens satisfactorily
5. Mix half an ounce of dissolved gelatine into the custard, strain, and whip it well while warm and let it get cold
6. Set a mould in the ice-pail, and when the custard has cooled nicely, pour it into the mould
7. Freeze in the usual way, using the spatula frequently
8. When the custard seems nearly frozen, add a wine-glass of very cold syrup flavoured with vanilla, continue the working
9. Pour in a pint of well-whipped cream
10. Finish the freezing thoroughly, close the mould securely, and bury it in ice for a couple of hours
11. Turn the parfait out on a napkin, and serve

title: Parota
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ghee
- salt
- 1 seer of fine flour
- 1/2 seer of water

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1 chhatak of ghee and a little salt with 1 seer of fine flour
2. Add about 1/2 seer of water and knead to a stiff dough
3. Rub and beat it continually with the hands for at least half an hour till it is softened to some extent
4. Make 16 balls of the mass
5. Press each ball with a little ghee to a round flat disc on a polished flat piece of marble and a handy roller of wood
6. Smear the upper surface with ghee and fold it
7. Smear with ghee again and fold
8. Press it again with the roller to a triangular form of uniform moderate thickness
9. Fry both sides on mild heat with ghee added little by little till it is crisp and properly browned
10. Serve while hot

title: Parsley and Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- large bunch of parsley
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Pick and wash clean a large bunch of parsley, tie it up, and boil it for a few minutes in water
2. Drain and chop it very finely
3. Add some melted butter, and make it quite hot

title: Parsley Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh green crisp parsley
- a tablespoonful of chopped capers
- a teaspoonful of good English vinegar
- a dessertspoonful of curry onions
- two chittacks or four ounces of butter, free of water
- a cup of good white stock, free of fat
- crumb of stale bread finely grated
- a teaspoonful of salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pick, clean, and mince fine some fresh green crisp parsley, and put it into a tureen with a tablespoonful of chopped capers and a teaspoonful of good English vinegar
2. Fry to a nice light brown a dessertspoonful of curry onions in two chittacks or four ounces of butter, free of water
3. Add a cup of good white stock, free of fat, and thicken with crumb of stale bread finely grated, a teaspoonful of salt, and a little pepper
4. Allow to simmer until of a sufficient consistency
5. Pour it over the minced parsley and capers, mix well together, and it is ready for use

title: Partridge Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- bacon
- chopped mushrooms
- partridge livers
- minced shallot
- spiced pepper
- rich gravy made from their bones
- paste

## Directions

1. bone the partridges
2. lay them out flat
3. put a few thin slices of cooked bacon over them
4. strew some chopped mushrooms over that
5. add their livers chopped and a little minced shallot
6. dust with spiced pepper
7. roll the birds up and put them into the pudding basin
8. pour in a little rich gravy made from their bones
9. close the paste over them, and boil for three hours

title: Partridges a la Soubise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- Bombay onion
- milk
- spiced pepper
- salt
- boiled bacon
- giblets
- lean bacon
- onion
- pepper corns

## Directions

1. Prepare the partridges as for roasting
2. Fill each of them with a chopped Bombay onion previously boiled in milk
3. Season with spiced pepper and salt
4. Roll in a slice of boiled bacon
5. Make a broth with the giblets of the birds, any scraps you may have, a slice of lean bacon, an onion cut into quarters, a few pepper corns, and a seasoning of salt and pepper
6. Simmer the partridges in the broth until perfectly tender

title: Partridges in Jelly
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four partridges
- stock
- carrot
- onion
- piece of celery
- sweet herbs
- glass of Madeira
- red currant jelly
- lime peel
- aspic jelly
- tarragon
- white of hard-boiled egg
- slices of truffle
- cornichons
- sauce ravigote
- dry toast

## Directions

1. Gently stew four partridges in stock with a carrot, an onion, a piece of celery, sweet herbs, and a glass of Madeira
2. When done, set the birds to get cold and strain the gravy in which they were done
3. Cut off the fillets of the partridges and pick all the other meat from the bones as well
4. Put the fillets aside
5. Simmer the skin, bones pounded, and scraps in the gravy again with a little red currant jelly and lime peel
6. Strain and reduce it to a glaze
7. Pound the meat picked from the backs and thighs with the livers and make a rich savoury paste with your glaze
8. Prepare your aspic jelly slightly flavoured with tarragon
9. Decorate a plain mould with white of hard-boiled egg, slices of truffle and cornichons
10. Pour in a little jelly and set the garnish
11. Pack your mould with the fillets of partridge between layers of the paste and slices of truffle
12. Ice this and present it with sauce ravigote also iced in a boat, and dry toast

title: Partridges with Soubise Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- Bombay onion
- milk
- spiced pepper
- salt
- boiled bacon
- giblets of the birds
- scraps
- slice of lean bacon
- onion
- pepper corns
- four large Bombay onions
- melted butter
- cream
- curls of crisply fried bacon

## Directions

1. Prepare partridges as for roasting
2. Fill each partridge with a chopped Bombay onion previously boiled in milk seasoned with spiced pepper, salt, and rolled in a slice of boiled bacon
3. Make a broth with the giblets of the birds, any scraps you may have, a slice of lean bacon, an onion cut into quarters, a few pepper corns, and a seasoning of salt and pepper
4. Simmer the partridges in the broth until perfectly tender, about three-quarters of an hour
5. Take them out and drain them, replacing them in the hot pan in which they were done, with the cover on
6. Strain the liquor in which the birds have been cooked
7. Simmer four large Bombay onions in milk till tender, drain them, chop them up very fine, and pass them through the sieve
8. Make a rich puree with melted butter and the stock aforesaid
9. When boiling hot, dish the birds and pour the onion puree over them
10. Serve garnished with curls of crisply fried bacon, with a spoonful of cream added if possible

title: Pastry for Pies and Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces flour
- one ounce soojee
- two ounces beef suet
- salt

## Directions

1. Pick and clean the suet, pound it in a mortar, and make a flat circular cake of it
2. Make a dough of the flour and soojee, knead it well
3. Divide it into two equal parts, and make them into two flat circular cakes quite as large as the suet cake
4. Roll the three together, placing the suet cake between the two flour cakes
5. Double the whole up twice, and roll it out again

title: Pati-Sapta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Khowa kheer
- Cocoa-nut kernel
- Almonds
- Pistachios
- Sugar
- Ghee
- Rose essence
- Flour or kalai pulses
- Milk
- Water

## Directions

1. Make a paste of khowa kheer, the kernel of a half ripe cocoa-nut, almonds, and pistachios in any quantity
2. Mix with the necessary amount of sugar
3. Fry it with a little ghee on mild heat
4. Add 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose when sticky and take down
5. Make a thick solution of flour or a paste of moistened pulses of kalai, sugar and milk
6. Heat a frying pan, smear with a little ghee
7. Add two table-spoonfuls of the flour solution
8. Tilt the pan spreading it in a thin circle
9. When sufficiently baked put a little of the preparation of kheer shaped like a cigar at one end
10. Roll the disc around it
11. Fry a little with ghee and take down
12. Continue in this way

title: Patna Nimki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of fine flour
- 1/2 powa of middlings
- 1/2 powa of besam
- salt
- black cumins
- sesames
- water
- ghee

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of fine flour 1/2 seer, middlings 1/2 powa, besam 1/2 powa, a little salt, some entire black cumins and sesames with water
2. Cut it in rectangular pieces half inch thick
3. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Patna or Bombay Onion Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- small or button onions
- vinegar
- fresh red chilies
- black peppercorns
- finely-sliced green ginger
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the outer coat from the onions, wash and dry them thoroughly
2. Throw them into a pan with some vinegar, and parboil them
3. Set the vinegar aside, after filtering it, for shrimp, cucumber, and other salads, or for the preparation of mustard
4. Put the parboiled onions when cold into wide-mouthed bottles, laying them alternately with fresh red chilies, a few black peppercorns, some finely-sliced green ginger, and a little salt
5. Fill the bottles with vinegar, and cork them

title: Patty Cakes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of flour
- one ounce of bread crumbs
- essence to flavour
- two ounces of sugar
- two ounces of butter
- half pint of milk
- two eggs
- powdered sugar
- sauce a la Royale

## Directions

1. Mix flour, bread crumbs, essence, sugar, butter melted in half pint of milk, and two eggs well beaten up
2. Bake in small, buttered patty-pans till browned
3. Turn them out, and serve on a napkin
4. Dust powdered sugar over all
5. Serve sauce a la Royale in a boat

title: Payesh
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 1 chhatak of finest atap rice per seer of milk
- 1 powa of sugar for every seer of milk
- pieces of almonds, pistachios and raisins
- powdered cardamom
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Boil some milk for a few minutes and then add thoroughly washed finest atap rice at the rate of 1 chhatak per seer of milk.
2. Cook on mild heat and stir constantly.
3. When the rice are boiled to softness in about a half hour add 1 powa of sugar for every seer of milk and pieces of almonds, pistachios and raisins as desired.
4. Take down when the desired thickness is reached after mixing with a little powdered cardamom and very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Cabbage
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- 2 seers of milk
- 2 1/2 or 3 powas of pieces of cabbage
- 1 1/2 powas of sugar
- raisins, pistachios and almonds
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Remove the upper leaves of a cabbage till the tender white leaves appear.
2. Then chop it in small very fine pieces.
3. Boil 2 seers of milk till it is reduced to 1 1/2 seers.
4. Then add 2 1/2 or 3 powas of the pieces of cabbage.
5. When well boiled add 1 1/2 powas of sugar, raisins, pistachios and almonds as desired.
6. Take down when thick and mix with very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- 1/2 seer of pasted fresh chhana
- 2 seers of milk
- 1 chhatak of raisins
- 1/2 chhatak of pieces of almonds
- 1 1/2 tola of pieces of pistachios
- half a table-spoonful of rose water or 3 or 4 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Heat a syrup of 1 1/2 powa of sugar.
2. When it is warm add 1/2 seer of pasted fresh chhana and stir constantly.
3. Take down when they are perfectly blended together and keep covered.
4. Boil 2 seers of milk till it is reduced to half and then add the mixture of chhana and sugar.
5. Boil on mild heat and stir constantly.
6. A little later add 1 chhatak of raisins, pieces of almonds 1/2 chhatak and pieces of pistachios 1 1/2 tola.
7. Take down when thick after mixing with half a table-spoonful of rose water or 3 or 4 drops of essence of rose.

title: Payesh of Chinre
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of milk
- 1 or 1 1/2 chhatak of chinre
- 1/2 powa of sugar
- 1 pinch each of powders of cardamom seeds and cubebs
- raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios

## Directions

1. Boil 1 seer of milk till it is reduced to half.
2. Then add 1 or 1 1/2 chhatak of chinre, after washing it, 1/2 powa of sugar, powders of cardamom seeds and cubebs 1 pinch each, raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios as desired.
3. Stir continually and take down when it is a thick mass.

title: Payesh of Juice of Date Palm
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 seers of the juice of date palm
- 1 powa of rice
- 1 seer of milk
- nalen gur
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Boil 2 seers of the juice of date palm and constantly remove the scum formed on the surface.
2. When scum no longer appears add 1 powa of rice.
3. Add 1 seer of milk when the rice are fully boiled and stir incessantly till thick.
4. Take down after mixing with a little nalen gur and very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Mun
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- mun (arum)
- the juice of a lemon
- 1 seer of mun
- 1/2 powa of ghee
- a few cassia leaves, cloves and cardamoms
- 1 1/2 seer of milk
- 2 1/2 powas of sugar
- raisins, almonds and pistachios
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Peel thickly a non-irritating mun (arum) and cut in very small and very thin pieces.
2. Boil 1 seer of them in water mixed with the juice of a lemon.
3. Then drain out the water and wash again with fresh water.
4. Fry them with 1/2 powa of ghee heated with a few cassia leaves, cloves and cardamoms, all entire.
5. Then add 1 1/2 seer of milk.
6. Stir constantly.
7. Add sugar 2 1/2 powas, raisins, almonds and pistachios as desired when the milk is reduced to 1 seer.
8. Take down when thick and mix with very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Nalen Gur
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of rice
- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1 seer of boiling milk
- 1 powa of finest nalen gur
- raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios
- powdered cardamoms
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Fry 1/2 powa of rice with 1 chhatak of ghee and drop them in 1 seer of boiling milk.
2. Stir constantly.
3. When the rice are fully boiled add 1 powa of finest nalen gur prepared from the juice of date palm.
4. Then add raisins, pieces of almonds and pistachios as desired.
5. Take down when thick and mix with a pinch of powdered cardamoms and very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Poppy Seeds
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of poppy seeds
- water
- a few cassia leaves
- cardamom seeds
- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1 seer of milk
- 1 powa of sugar
- raisins
- pistachios
- almonds

## Directions

1. Keep 1 powa of poppy seeds moist in water for an hour and then make a paste of them adding water little by little.
2. Fry a few cassia leaves and cardamom seeds in 1 chhatak of ghee and then fry the pasted poppy seeds till light brown.
3. Add 1 seer of milk, 1 powa of sugar, raisins, pistachios and almonds as desired.
4. Stir constantly and take down when thick.

title: Payesh of Poppy Seeds
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa of poppy seeds
- a few cassia leaves and cardamom seeds
- 1 chhatak of ghee
- 1 seer of milk
- 1 powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 1 powa of poppy seeds moist in water for an hour and then make a paste of them adding water little by little.
2. Fry a few cassia leaves and cardamom seeds in 1 chhatak of ghee and then fry the pasted poppy seeds till light brown.
3. Add 1 seer of milk, 1 powa of sugar.

title: Payesh of Sago
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of milk
- 1 chhatak of sago
- water
- 1/2 powa of sugar-candy
- raisins
- almonds
- pistachios
- cardamom seeds
- camphor

## Directions

1. Boil a seer of milk and when it is reduced to half add 1 chhatak of sago, after washing with water.
2. Stir incessantly.
3. A little later add 1/2 powa of sugar-candy, raisins, almonds and pistachios as desired.
4. Take down when thick and mix with a pinch of powdered cardamom seeds and very little camphor.

title: Payesh of Sweet Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of sweet potatoes
- 1/2 powa of ghee
- a pinch of aniseeds
- 1 seer of milk
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- raisins, almonds and pistachios
- very little camphor

## Directions

1. Boil 1 seer of sweet potatoes, skin them, rub to a pulp and remove the fibres, if any.
2. Boil 1/2 powa of ghee with a pinch of aniseeds and fry the pulp with the ghee.
3. Then add 1 seer of milk, 1 1/2 powa of sugar, raisins, almonds and pistachios as desired.
4. Stir constantly all the while.
5. Mix with very little camphor when thick and take down.

title: Pea Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas 1/2 seer
- potatoes 1 seer
- ghee 1 powa
- pepper 1 tola
- ginger 2 tolas
- salt 2 tolas
- coriander 2 tolas
- sugar 2.5 tolas
- flour 1 tola
- water 1 seer
- milk
- garam-masalla 0.5 tola
- cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Brown the peas and pieces of potatoes separately in two thirds of the ghee.
2. Add the pasted pepper, ginger and salt in the water and boil.
3. Add the peas and potatoes.
4. When well boiled season them with the rest of the ghee heated with a few cassia leaves.
5. Mix the powdered coriander, sugar and flour with the milk and pour in.
6. Boil for a few minutes more.
7. Finish by adding the powdered garam masalla.

title: Pea Puree
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- butter
- black pepper
- salt
- sugar
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Boil the peas as previously described
2. Work them through the sieve
3. Add butter, a little black pepper, salt, a very little sugar
4. Add a spoonful of cream or good milk
5. Serve in a small mould

title: Peach Fritters
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- powdered sugar
- maraschino or liqueur
- batter
- boiling fat
- pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Be very careful with your preparation of the batter; cut the peaches into neat pieces.
2. Dust them with powdered sugar, and let them lie in a little maraschino or any nice liqueur till wanted.
3. Dip them in your batter, and fry them in abundance of boiling fat.
4. Drain them on blotting paper, and serve them dusted over with pounded loaf sugar, finely sifted.
5. For raw fruit: cut the peaches in halves, peeled, and stoned, should be carefully stewed in syrup with a dash of liqueur and a little lime juice first, then set to get cold, and after being drained, dipped in batter and fried as above.
6. All fritters should be served without delay.

title: Peach Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- strong syrup
- brandy

## Directions

1. Clean the peaches, slit them with a silver or plated knife, and remove the stones
2. Have a very strong syrup ready, and while it is boiling hot throw in the peaches, and let them stand over a slow fire for six to eight hours
3. Remove them from the fire, and twelve hours after drain off the syrup and reboil it
4. Return the fruit into the syrup, and if it shows any disposition to ferment, boil it again
5. When satisfied it will not ferment any more, add a little brandy, say a wineglassful to every fifty peaches, and boil the whole over a slow fire for two hours
6. Bottle when quite cold
7. The kernels from the stones may be put in if desired

title: Peas
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas, husked and broken in two, 1 seer
- Ghee 1/2 powa
- Ginger 2 tolas
- Cumin 1 tola
- Coriander 2 tolas
- Salt 1/2 chhatak
- Pepper 1/2 tola
- Garam-masalla 1/2 tola
- Water 2 seers
- A few cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the ginger, cumin, coriander and pepper and dilute it in the water with salt.
2. Brown the dal in half of the ghee and pour in the spiced water.
3. Take down when the dal are thoroughly boiled.
4. Heat the remaining ghee in another pan with the cassia leaves and the powdered garam-masalla.
5. Pour in the boiled dal when the smell of the spices evolves, and stir.
6. Take down when the desired degree of thickness is obtained.

title: Peas for a Second-course Dish, a la Francais
body:
## Ingredients

- quart of fine green peas
- butter
- warm water
- parsley
- young onion
- loaf sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a quart of fine green peas, together with a bit of butter the size of a walnut, into as much warm water as will cover them
2. let them stand for eight or ten minutes
3. Strain off the water, put them into a saucepan, cover it, stir them frequently
4. when a little tender add a bunch of parsley and a young onion, nearly a dessertspoonful of loaf sugar, and an ounce of butter mixed with a teaspoonful of flour
5. keep stirring them now and then till the peas be tender
6. add, if they become too thick, a tablespoonful of hot water
7. Before serving, take out the onion and bunch of parsley

title: Peas in the Jar
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled peas
- tinned butter
- salt
- powdered sugar
- mint leaves
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Shell half a pint of peas
2. Put them into a two-pound jam jar with a screw lid or a block tin can with a closely fitting top
3. Put in with them a table-spoonful of tinned butter, a salt-spoonful of salt, and a tea-spoonful of powdered sugar
4. Add a dozen mint leaves and a very little black pepper
5. Cover the vessel down tightly
6. Immerse it in a stew-pan or bain-marie half full of boiling water
7. Set the latter on the fire and boil briskly
8. Examine the peas in half an hour by which time, if very young, they should be done

title: Peraki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ powa khowa kheer
- ghee
- ½ powa raisins
- 1 chhatak pieces of almonds
- 1 chhatak pieces of pistachios
- 1 tola cardamom seeds
- powdered cloves ½ tola
- powdered cinnamon ½ tola
- sugar syrup
- 1 powa ghee
- 1 seer fine flour
- water
- ½ seer boiling ghee

## Directions

1. Fry ½ powa of khowa kheer with a little ghee and powder it
2. Fry ½ powa of raisins and pieces of almonds and pistachios 1 chhatak each with a little more ghee
3. Mix all with 1 tola of cardamom seeds, powdered cloves and cinnamon ½ tola each and a little syrup of sugar
4. Mix 1 powa of ghee with 1 seer of fine flour
5. Knead to a stiff dough with water
6. Make balls of it and prepare hollow cones of them
7. Stuff them with the above spiced kheer and close up the base
8. Fry them in ½ seer of boiling ghee and keep immersed in a very concentrated syrup of sugar for about half an hour
9. Take them out and roll on dry crystalline sugar

title: Perdreaux aux Choux
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four partridges
- bacon
- an onion
- a nice cabbage
- two carrots
- two large onions
- powdered herbs
- salt
- pepper
- well made gravy
- Bologna or Brunswick sausage

## Directions

1. Prepare four partridges as for boiling, with their legs tucked in
2. Lard their breasts with bacon and put an onion inside each of them
3. Cut a nice cabbage into quarters, blanch them, and steep them in cold water for an hour
4. Take a roomy stew-pan, line its bottom with two carrots and two large onions sliced in rings, a sprinkling of powdered herbs, and a dusting of salt and pepper
5. Put the partridges above this lining, inserting a quarter of cabbage between each bird, a slice of bacon here and there, and some slices of Bologna or Brunswick sausage
6. Moisten with sufficient well made gravy to cover the birds
7. Boil closely covered up, and then simmer for an hour and a half if the birds are tender
8. Dish with the cabbage in the centre, and the birds placed neatly round it, with the sliced sausage and bacon as garnish
9. Serve the gravy in a boat

title: Petits Casseroles aux Grandes-Crevettes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of the best table rice
- a quart of water
- an onion
- ounces of clarified suet
- a tablespoonful of grated Parmesan
- butter
- pepper and salt
- the yolks of three eggs
- spiced pepper
- egg
- bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Put a pound of the best table rice into a stew-pan with a quart of water, an onion, and ounces of clarified suet
2. Simmer till the rice is soft, yet quite whole
3. Drain it, and pound it to a paste in a mortar with a tablespoonful of grated Parmesan, some butter, pepper and salt
4. Stir into this the yolks of three eggs, and powder it with a little spiced pepper
5. Pass all through the sieve, and pat it to a paste on your pastry slab, about one and a half inch thick
6. When quite cold, cut it into cylinders two inches long, and an inch and a half in diameter
7. Egg and bread-crumb them, fry them, a golden yellow in butter

title: Petits Pois a la Creme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled or jugged peas
- boiling cream

## Directions

1. Pour a coffee-cupful of boiling cream over the peas just as you serve

title: Petits Pois au Beurre
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled or jugged peas
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Serve boiled or jugged peas with a pat of fresh butter melted in a small sauce-pan and mixed with them at the last moment

title: Petits Pois au Jambon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely minced ham
- butter
- boiled or jugged peas

## Directions

1. Toss finely minced ham in butter and lightly fry
2. Mix with boiled or jugged peas

title: Petits Pois au Lard
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- butter
- boiled or jugged peas

## Directions

1. Toss bacon in butter and lightly fry until nice and crisp
2. Mix with boiled or jugged peas

title: Pickled Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- a full-sized cabbage
- salt
- water
- eight ounces of salt to twenty-four ounces of water
- half an ounce each of peppercorns and bay-leaves

## Directions

1. Quarter a full-sized cabbage
2. Keep it in salt for forty-eight hours, and then drain away all the water
3. Prepare a pickle or brine of salt and water in the proportion of eight ounces of salt to twenty-four ounces of water, and boil it with half an ounce each of peppercorns and bay-leaves
4. Pack the cabbage loose in a wide-mouthed stone jar, and pour over it the cold pickle or brine, which should have been boiled the day before
5. Keep the mouth of the jar always airtight
6. When required for use, take out as much as will be required, steep it in fresh cold water for an hour or two, and then boil it the same as fresh cabbage

title: Pickled Steak, or Chops
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a steak
- a couple of onions
- a clove of garlic
- pepper corns
- salt
- leaves of thyme and marjoram
- a bunch of parsley
- lime peel
- oil
- vinegar
- butter
- made-gravy

## Directions

1. Place a steak in a deep dish with a couple of onions sliced, a clove of garlic, pepper corns, salt, some leaves of thyme and marjoram, a bunch of parsley, and some lime peel
2. Add oil and vinegar (two table-spoonfuls of former to one of latter) sufficient to soak the meat well without actually covering it
3. Let it soak all day
4. Lift it when wanted from the marinade, and fry lightly in butter
5. When coloured on both sides nicely, pour in the marinade, with a breakfast-cup of made-gravy, and stew the steak gently till thoroughly done
6. Strain the liquor, free it from fat, reduce it a little over the fire, pour over the steak, and serve

title: Pickled Vindaloo
body:
## Ingredients

- pork from the fattest parts
- vinegar
- garlic bruised
- dry ginger powdered
- turmeric powdered
- peppercorns roasted and powdered
- coriander-seeds roasted and powdered
- cumin-seeds roasted and powdered
- dry salt
- mustard oil
- bay-leaves

## Directions

1. Select the fattest parts of pork; satisfy yourself that the meat is fresh and sound, and that it has not been washed with water in the butcher's shop.
2. Cut the meat into two-inch squares, wash thoroughly in vinegar (no water), rub over with the condiments, and then steep them in really good English vinegar for twenty-four hours.
3. Melt a large quantity of the best mustard oil in an earthen pot, and according to the quantity of meat, take additional condiments mentioned above, but in the proportion given in recipe No. 61; grind in vinegar, and fry in the oil.
4. Put in the meat, and all the vinegar, &c., in which it had been steeped, together with some more salt, a little more vinegar, a few bay-leaves and peppercorns, and allow to simmer until the meat is quite tender.
5. Remove from the fire and allow it to get quite cold; then put it into dry stone jars, with patent screw tops, well filled with plenty of the oil in which the vindaloo was cooked.
6. Take care that all the meat is well covered over with oil, which latter ought to be at least from two to three inches above the meat in the jar.
7. Screw down the lid, and cover it over with a good sound bladder to render it perfectly air-tight.
8. When required for use, take out only as much as will suffice, and simply warm it in a little of its own gravy.

title: Pie-Crust
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of well-dried and sifted flour
- a quarter of a pound of cold clarified suet
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- iced water

## Directions

1. Put one pound of well-dried and sifted flour on the slab, or in an enamelled basin
2. make a hollow in the centre, and work into it a quarter of a pound of cold clarified suet cut into pieces, adding a tea-spoonful of salt
3. When mixed, sprinkle over it as much iced water as will bind it thoroughly
4. dredge some flour over the slab, and roll the paste out half an inch thick
5. Cut up another quarter of a pound of clarified suet, and dot it over the surface of the paste
6. fold the paste over it, and roll it out again very thin
7. fold it in three, set it in the ice-box for ten minutes
8. give it three more turns, and then roll it out half an inch thick when it may be cut to cover the pie

title: Piece de Boeuf en Aspic
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of fresh brisket of beef
- plenty of fat bacon
- stock
- vegetables
- a glass of white wine
- its own gravy reduced
- aspic
- hard-boiled eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Tie a nice piece of fresh brisket of beef into a compact shape
2. Lard it with plenty of fat bacon
3. Braise it in stock and vegetables with a glass of white wine
4. When done, take it out and remove the string
5. Place the meat in an oblong shape with a heavy weight upon it
6. When thoroughly cold and set, trim it all round with a sharp knife
7. Glaze it with its own gravy reduced
8. Set it in aspic in the ice-box using a plain oblong mould
9. When you turn it out, garnish with hard-boiled eggs and parsley
10. Serve with the best salad you can devise and a good mayonnaise sauce

title: Pigeon Fillets with Genevoise Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight young pigeons
- oil
- vinegar
- minced parsley
- shallot
- breakfast-cupful of gravy
- onion
- clove of garlic
- butter the size of an hen's egg
- two or three anchovies
- pepper
- salt
- juice of a lime
- one glass of claret
- flour
- caramel
- bacon
- petits pois verts

## Directions

1. Lightly roast eight young pigeons
2. Slice the breasts of the birds off whole
3. Place the eight portions so obtained en marinade in oil, vinegar, minced parsley, and shallot
4. Take the bones, trimmings, livers, &c., and put them into a sauce-pan with a good breakfast-cupful of gravy
5. Simmer this until you have extracted the essence of your pigeon scraps
6. Strain it
7. Chop up an onion, and one clove of garlic very small
8. Stir a piece of butter the size of an hen's egg at the bottom of a sauce-pan over the fire
9. Throw in your chopped onion, &c., let it slightly brown
10. Add the gravy you previously made, with two or three anchovies chopped into dice, pepper and salt to taste, and the juice of a lime, with one glass of claret
11. Bring this sauce to boiling point
12. Let it simmer awhile
13. Strain it
14. Replace it in the sauce-pan
15. Thicken it with butter and a little flour
16. Colour it with caramel
17. Place your fillets in it to gently heat up without coming to the boil
18. When quite hot, place the fillets in their dish, pouring the sauce round them and serve, with a crisply fried curl of bacon, between each of them
19. Fill the centre of the dish with petits pois verts

title: Pigeon Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pound and a half of tender lean beef
- three good pigeons
- half a pound of leanish bacon
- pound of gravy beef
- spiced pepper
- salt
- grated lime peal
- chopped bacon
- finely chopped parsley
- Madeira or white wine

## Directions

1. Make the best gravy possible from the pound of gravy meat, the pigeon trimmings, and any scraps at hand
2. Cut the tender beef into neat collops and blend with the bacon cut into thin strips
3. Prepare the pigeons whole as if for roasting
4. Put some chopped bacon seasoned with pepper, salt, or spiced pepper, salt, and grated lime peal, inside each bird
5. Place the pigeons upon the tender beef
6. Rub the pie-dish with a shallot before it is packed
7. Sprinkle finely chopped parsley over the bottom of the dish before the beef
8. Half a glass of Madeira or any sound white wine may be mingled with the gravy

title: Pigeons in Savoury Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- sausage-meat
- jelly
- parsley

## Directions

1. Bone six pigeons
2. Remove the heads and feet, stuff with sausage-meat, and roast
3. Lay the pigeons in a mould with the breasts down
4. Fill up the mould with jelly
5. When cold, turn out
6. Garnish with parsley, and some of the jelly cut fine, and sprinkled round the dish

title: Pigeons with Petit Pois
body:
## Ingredients

- four young, full-grown pigeons
- salt
- finely-sifted pepper
- two tablespoonfuls of ghee or lard
- cupful of beef stock
- tin of petit pois with their gravy

## Directions

1. Kill and feather four young, full-grown pigeons without plunging into hot water, taking care not to break their skins
2. Singe them to destroy any remaining feathers
3. Wash them in three or four cold waters
4. Cut them in halves
5. Dredge them well with salt and finely-sifted pepper
6. Allow to remain for an hour
7. Boil up two tablespoonfuls of ghee or lard
8. Fry the birds to a rich brown, turning them over
9. When sufficiently browned, put in a cupful of beef stock
10. Allow to simmer until the birds are quite tender
11. Pour over them a tin of petit pois with their gravy
12. Serve up hot

title: Pine-apple Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- pines
- sugar
- cinnamon sticks
- bay-leaves

## Directions

1. Take care that the pines are not green, nor yet quite ripe
2. Remove the peel, cutting it deeply, and then all the seeds and eyes
3. Cut each pine into six slices
4. Lay them in a preserving-pan, and sprinkle over each layer a good quantity of sugar, a few sticks of cinnamon, and a few bay-leaves, covering the uppermost layer with a larger quantity of sugar
5. Allow them to simmer over a tolerably brisk fire until the sugar has all melted
6. Reduce the fire, and continue to simmer until the pines have quite changed colour and become tender
7. Remove them out of the syrup into a colander, and allow them to drain, but continue to boil the syrup with all that drops from the fruit until it has thickened
8. Return the fruit into the syrup and finish the boiling
9. Bottle when quite cool
10. Before corking them for good, ascertain the state of the syrup every two or three days
11. If it shows signs of fermentation, remove it from the fruit and reboil it
12. This operation must be continued until the syrup has ceased to ferment
13. The fruit is not to be reboiled, but only returned into the syrup when the boiling is about to be finished

title: Pine-Apple Toasts
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pine-apple
- thin syrup
- a glass of rum
- Madeira cake, circular shape eight inches in diameter
- butter
- powdered sugar
- apricot jam

## Directions

1. Cut up the pine in slices a quarter of an inch thick
2. Stew them in thin syrup with a glass of rum, and keep them simmering en bain-marie
3. Take a Madeira cake of a circular shape eight inches in diameter, cut it into nice slices the same shape as those you cut of pine-apple
4. Lightly fry them in butter
5. Dust them over with powdered sugar and set them in the oven, spreading a layer of apricot jam over each slice
6. At the time of serving, drain the pine-apple slices, and arrange them with the slices of cake alternately upon a very hot dish
7. Pour the syrup over them

title: Pink Mango Fool
body:
## Ingredients

- beetroot boiled very tender
- pulp of the mango

## Directions

1. Boil beetroot very tender, bruise down, and strain through muslin
2. Add to the pulp of the mango

title: Pink Noyau
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof
- fifteen drops of essential oil of bitter almonds
- three drops of oil of roses
- four drops of oil of aniseed
- one drop of tincture of vanilla
- one quart of syrup
- pink colouring matter

## Directions

1. To a quart of spirits of wine sixty-two degrees overproof add fifteen drops of essential oil of bitter almonds, three drops of oil of roses, four drops of oil of aniseed, and one drop of tincture of vanilla
2. Shake it well
3. Mix with it a quart of syrup and a sufficient quantity of pink colouring matter to make it of a delicate pink colour
4. Bottle it after filtering

title: Pink Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- a large red beetroot
- yolks of five eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of flour
- four tablespoonfuls of cream
- plenty of pounded loaf sugar
- half a nutmeg grated
- a wineglassful of brandy
- melted butter, ghee, or lard
- green candied sweetmeats

## Directions

1. Boil a large red beetroot until it is very tender
2. Peel it, cut it into thin slices
3. Pound it to a pulp in a marble mortar and strain through muslin
4. Add the yolks of five eggs, two tablespoonfuls of flour, four of cream, plenty of pounded loaf sugar, half a nutmeg grated, and a wineglassful of brandy
5. Rub the whole into a batter
6. Fry the pancakes with melted butter, ghee, or lard
7. Serve them up hot, garnished with green candied sweetmeats

title: Pink Sauce for Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of good vinegar
- half a pint of port wine
- half an ounce of cayenne
- one large tablespoonful of walnut catsup
- two of anchovy liquor
- a quarter of an ounce of cochineal
- six cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Put into a pan, or wide-mouthed jar, one quart of good vinegar, half a pint of port wine, half an ounce of cayenne, one large tablespoonful of walnut catsup, two of anchovy liquor, a quarter of an ounce of cochineal, and six cloves of garlic
2. Let it remain forty hours, stirring it two or three times a day
3. Run it through a flannel bag, and put it into half-pint bottles

title: Pish-Pash
body:
## Ingredients

- a coonkee or half a pound of bassmuttee or other fine-grain rice
- a chicken
- sliced ginger
- sliced onions
- a few bay-leaves
- some peppercorns
- a few hot spice
- a dessertspoonful of salt
- one chittack or two ounces of butter
- water

## Directions

1. Pick and wash in several waters a coonkee or half a pound of the bassmuttee or other fine-grain rice
2. Add to it, cleaned and cut up, a chicken, some sliced ginger, sliced onions, a few bay-leaves, some peppercorns, a few hot spice, a dessertspoonful of salt, one chittack or two ounces of butter, and water sufficient to cover the whole
3. Simmer over a slow fire until the chicken becomes perfectly tender and the rice quite pappy
4. Serve up hot

title: Pistachio Cream Ice
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of pistachio nuts
- six ounces of sugar
- a few drops of rose water
- a pint of new milk
- the yolks of ten eggs
- spinach-greening
- a coffee-cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Blanch four ounces of pistachio nuts
2. Pound them in a mortar with six ounces of sugar and a few drops of rose water
3. When quite a smooth paste, add a pint of new milk very gradually
4. When the milk and paste have amalgamated, make a rich custard by placing the mixture en bain-marie, and adding the strained yolks of ten eggs
5. When the custard has formed nicely, strain it through a fine sieve
6. Whisk it well adding enough spinach-greening to tint the mixture a very pale green
7. Freeze it, working into it a coffee-cupful of whipped cream in the usual way when half frozen

title: Plain Cheese Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese
- butter
- Nepaul pepper
- toast

## Directions

1. Cut a few very thin slices of a nice fresh cheese, or grate two ounces of a hard dry one.
2. Put the cheese upon a small well buttered baking tin, and place it in the oven.
3. Watch it carefully, and when it begins to dissolve, stir some butter into it.
4. Give it a dust of Nepaul pepper, and serve it upon crisply fried toasts quickly.

title: Plain Salad Dressing
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- finest oil
- tarragon vinegar
- salt
- coarse freshly ground black pepper

## Directions

1. Pull the leaves of the lettuce from the stalk with your hand, rejecting all that are bruised and discoloured
2. Turn those at all muddy into a basin, wash them well, and drain them thoroughly on a sieve
3. Toss them lightly in a cloth to get rid of every drop of water
4. When dry, put the leaves into the bowl
5. Work them about with the wooden spoon and fork whilst an assistant sprinkles over them a libation of the finest oil you can buy
6. As soon as every leaf is thoroughly anointed, shake over them a few drops of tarragon vinegar
7. Dust them with salt and some coarse freshly ground black pepper
8. Keep the spoon and fork going during the addition of the vinegar drops and whilst the pepper and salt are being dusted into the bowl
9. Avoid creating a sediment of dressing to maintain the crispness of the leaves

title: Plantain Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve ripe plantains
- four tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour
- half a cupful of milk
- sugar to taste
- cardamom seeds
- caraway seeds
- two eggs, beaten up
- melted ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare a batter of twelve ripe plantains, four tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour, half a cupful of milk, sugar to taste, and cardamom and caraway seeds, with a couple of eggs beaten up
2. Mix the whole well together
3. Make into small cakes by pouring a tablespoonful at a time of the mixture into melted ghee
4. Fry them on both sides to a good brown colour
5. Serve up hot

title: Plash
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint lemon ice
- one wineglassful Jamaica rum
- one bottle iced ginger beer

## Directions

1. Mix lemon ice with Jamaica rum
2. Pour over it, stirring briskly, a bottle of iced ginger beer
3. Drink it while it is effervescing

title: Plovers in Cases
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- plovers
- buttered cases
- thick glaze

## Directions

1. Roast the plovers (the grey bird will do for this dish)
2. Proceed as you did to compose the salpicon de gibier, fill your buttered cases with pieces of the plovers
3. Pour round and over them your thick glaze
4. Bake for five or ten minutes, and serve

title: Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine rice 1 seer
- Pure cow-milk 1.5 seers
- Ghee 1.5 seer
- Meat 1 seer
- Cloves 0.25 anna
- Cinnamon 1 anna
- Cardamom 1 tola
- Cardamom, big quality, 8 in number
- Mace 1.5 tola
- Coriander 0.5 chhatak
- Cassia leaves 1 tola
- White pepper 1.5 tola
- Cubebs 0.5 tola
- Pepper with cumin seeds 1.5 tola
- Raisins 1.5 chhatak
- Pistachios 1 chhatak
- Almonds 0.5 powa
- Sugar 1 chhatak
- Curd 0.5 powa
- Milk condensed 1.5 powa
- Salt 1 chhatak
- Saffron 0.5 tola
- Water 4.5 seers
- Ginger 0.5 powa

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in water and dry them up
2. Keep the rice in the milk for about 1.5 hours till they absorb the milk to the maximum
3. Tie the swelled rice loosely in a piece of clean cloth and allow the excess milk to drip off
4. When the dripping ceases spread them in air to dry up the moisture on the surface of the rice
5. Smear the rice with ginger-juice, saffron, the seeds of the 8 big cardamoms, adding a little water if necessary
6. Boil the meat in about 4.5 seers of water till the latter reduces to less than half
7. Separate the meat from the water and brown them in 1.5 powa of ghee after mixing them with maces, coriander seeds, white peppers, cubebs, and peppers with cumin seeds, all powdered or pasted
8. Boil 1.5 powa of ghee in a deep pan and take it down from the oven
9. Make in it a layer of cassia leaves, then one of meat, then one of rice, and lastly one of raisins, almonds and pistachios, washed, peeled and cut in pieces
10. Make another series of the layers in the same order as before
11. On the uppermost layer add the water in which meat was boiled and the milk along with salt
12. Allow the mass to simmer under closed cover on very slow heat
13. When the rice have been boiled add the condensed milk, curd, sugar and the rest of the ghee with fragmented cinnamon, cardamom and cloves, all entire, fried in it
14. Stir the whole mass vigorously to blend the ingredients
15. Allow the mass to simmer again under cover for a few minutes on glowing charcoal or any other fire producing mild heat
16. When no juice is left the polao is ready for the table

title: Polao with Anaras (Pine Apple)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- meat, 1 seer
- rice, 1 seer
- pine-apple peeled and cut into medium pieces, 1½ seer
- lemon juice, ½ powa or more if desired
- sugar, ½ seer
- ginger, 3 tolas
- coriander-seeds, 1½ tolas
- black cumin seeds, 1 tola
- cloves, ½ tola
- cinnamon, ½ tola
- cardamom, ½ tola
- saffron, ½ tola
- salt, 4 tolas
- ghee, ½ seer
- water, 4 seers

## Directions

1. Make a saturated solution of the sugar and boil the pieces of pine-apple in it with the lemon-juice after seasoning them for about an hour or more with one tola of salt.
2. Boil the meat in about 4 seers of water until the latter reduces to half or less.
3. Heat about ½ powa of ghee in a pan and season it by frying ½ tola of black cumin.
4. Fry the boiled meat in the ghee after separating them from the water, till they are slightly brown and do not stick to one another.
5. Add the water again and allow the whole thing to boil.
6. Heat on another oven about ½ powa of ghee in another pan and slightly fry the remaining ½ tola of black cumin, cloves, cinnamons and cardamoms, all entire, in it.
7. A few cassia leaves may also be fried.
8. When the flavour of the spices fill your nose, add the rice, washed, dried and smeared with saffron, ginger and coriander seeds, all pasted.
9. Stir till some of the rice begin to burst.
10. Add the boiling meat with the water to the rice, add salt and allow the whole thing to boil under cover after stirring it well.
11. About 10 minutes before taking it down from the oven add the pieces of pine-apples with the sugar solution.
12. When the water dries up, pour about one powa of hot ghee and stir to render the whole mass non-sticky.
13. Make this preparation on slow heat.

title: Polao With Curd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice 1 seer
- Tender meat 1 seer
- Ghee 1 powa
- Curd 1 powa
- Lemon 1 in number
- Ginger 2 tolas
- Coriander seeds 2 tolas
- Pepper 0.5 tola
- Saffron 0.5 tola
- Salt 4 tolas
- Cinnamon 0.5 tola
- Cardamom 0.5 tola
- Cloves 0.5 tola
- Cassia leaves 2 tolas
- Black cumin seeds 1.5 tola
- Water 4.5 seers

## Directions

1. Boil the meat in water and then brown them in 1 chhatak of ghee after separating them from the water
2. Save the water in which the meat has been boiled, called akhni water
3. Make a paste of the juice of the lemon, ginger, coriander seeds, pepper, saffron, and salt
4. Heat some ghee in a cooking pot and make in order the layers of cassia leaves and black cumin seeds, meat, pasted spices, rice, and again those of cassia leaves, meat, spices and rice
5. Add the akhni water on the topmost layer, cover the pot with its lid, and cook on charcoal fire or any other fire producing gentle heat
6. When the rice are half boiled add the whole of the curd and the rest of the ghee with cinnamon, cardamoms and cloves, all entire, fried in it
7. Stir the mass thoroughly, close the lid and allow it to simmer on charcoal fire
8. You may put some pieces of glowing charcoal on the lid so that heating is uniform
9. Take down from the oven when the mass is properly boiled and when no juice is left

title: Polao With Entire Fowls (Simple)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice ½ seer
- ghee 1 powa
- onions ½ powa
- a few cassia leaves
- cloves
- cardamoms
- cinnamon ½ tola
- salt 2 tolas
- fowl 1 in number
- water 1 seer
- turmeric ½ tola

## Directions

1. Wash the rice thoroughly and keep them moist with a small quantity of water.
2. Cut the onions in pieces and fry them in ghee and take out.
3. Take about ½ powa of ghee in the same pan and when it is hot, roast the fowl, cleaned and washed and smeared with a paste of the turmeric, a few raw onions and half the salt, in it for 7 or 8 minutes till all the water coming out of the fowl dries up.
4. Take the fowl out of the pan.
5. Take the rest of the ghee in the pan and add the cassia leaves, cloves, cardamoms and cinnamons, all entire in it.
6. When the smell of the spices comes out, pour the moist rice in it and stir for about 5 minutes.
7. Add the roasted fowl and the fried onions, stir them for 2 or 3 minutes.
8. Pour water with the rest of the salt in the pan.
9. Lower the temperature of the fire when the mass begins to boil and allow it to simmer for quite a long time.
10. Take down when the water dries up, and the fowl and the rice are non-sticky. Add a little of hot water if water runs short at any time.

title: Polao with Fowls (Alternative)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice 1 seer
- Minced meat 1 seer
- Ghee 1 seer
- Onions 1.5 powa
- Almonds 0.5 powa
- Raisins 0.5 powa
- Salt 4 tolas
- Curd 0.5 powa
- Ginger 0.5 chhatak
- Coriander seeds 1 tola
- Cloves 0.5 tola
- Cardamoms 0.5 tola
- Cinnamon 0.5 tola
- Pepper 0.5 tola
- Saffron 0.5 tola
- Garlic 1 in number
- Lemon 1 in number
- Cassia leaves a few
- Rose-water 1 tablespoonful
- Eggs 5 or 6 in number
- Fowl 1 in number

## Directions

1. Make a paste of a few pieces of raw onions, a few pieces of garlic, half the ginger, all the coriander seeds and a few cloves.
2. Boil this paste with some salt, half the saffron and half the cassia leaves in about 3 seers of water till it is halved.
3. Strain the akhni water out and keep it covered.
4. Make a paste of a few cloves, cardamoms, almonds and mix it with the curd, the juice of the lemon and the rose-water with the rest of saffron dissolved in it.
5. Cut the onions into pieces and fry them, excepting only some pieces, in about 0.5 powa of ghee and keep them with the ghee in another pot.
6. Take about 0.5 powa of ghee and fry in it the raisins and almonds, peeled and cut into pieces and take them out of the ghee.
7. Fry the minced meat in the same ghee for 2 or 3 minutes more after the water coming out of the meat dries up.
8. Make a paste of 0.75 of the meat, some fried onions, 4 or 5 cloves, 2 or 3 cardamoms, a piece of cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and the minced liver of the fowl if desired.
9. Mix some fried almonds and raisins, a few drops of lemon-juice and a pinch of powdered pepper with the paste and stuff it into the bowels of the fowl, and stitch it up.
10. Make a paste of the rest of the ginger, garlic, the pieces of raw onions, 0.5 chhatak of ghee and some salt, and smear this paste on the body of the fowl.
11. Roast the fowl in 0.5 chhatak of ghee. When the water coming out of the fowl dries up, roast it by constantly sprinkling the curd with spices mixed in it till the fowl is fully boiled.
12. Take down after adding some powdered garam-masalla.
13. Make a paste of the fried minced meat and half the fried onions, and mix it with salt, some powdered garam-masalla, the rest of the powdered peppers and the white of the eggs.
14. Make 8 or 10 round thick balls of this mass and fry them in ghee.
15. Make 2 big omelettes with the yolks of the eggs after mixing them with thin slices of ginger, raw onions, some powdered peppers and salt.
16. Cut the omelettes in long stripes.
17. Take about 0.5 powa of ghee in a pan and heat with some entire garam-masalla.
18. Add rice when the smell evolves and stir till the rice become white and begin to burst.
19. Add the akhni water and take down after about 25 minutes when the water dries up and the rice are boiled.
20. Arrange the layers of in a boiler as follows: cassia leaves, fried onions with the ghee, rice, fried almonds and raisins, rice, roasted fowl with its juice, rice, fried balls of meat, rice, pieces of omelettes, and rice.
21. Shut the mouth of the boiler and heat very slowly for about three quarters of an hour. Then it is ready for the dish.

title: Pomade Divine
body:
## Ingredients

- beef marrow, one pound and a half
- spring water
- rose-water, one pint
- flowers of benzoin, one ounce
- cyprus-root, one ounce
- odoriferous thorn, one ounce
- Florentine iris-root, one ounce
- cinnamon, half an ounce
- cloves, a quarter of an ounce
- nutmeg, a quarter of an ounce
- flour
- white of egg
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Clear beef marrow thoroughly from bone and fibre, and put it in an earthen vessel of spring water
2. Change the water every night and morning for eight or ten days
3. Steep the marrow in a pint of rose-water for twenty-four hours
4. Drain it dry through a linen cloth
5. Take an ounce each of flowers of benzoin, cyprus-root, odoriferous thorn, and Florentine iris-root, half an ounce of cinnamon, and a quarter of an ounce each of cloves and nutmeg
6. Pound all these very fine
7. Mix them well with the marrow
8. Put all into a pewter digester which holds three pints with closely fitted top
9. Spread on linen a paste made of flour and white of egg, and fix it over the top so that there can be no evaporation
10. Suspend the digester by the handles in the middle of a pot of boiling water
11. Keep it boiling, adding more boiling water as often as necessary
12. Strain the pomade into small wide-mouthed bottles
13. Cover it down when quite cold

title: Pomade for Hair that is Falling Off
body:
## Ingredients

- beef marrow, eight ounces
- tincture of cantharides, twenty-two drops
- sugar of lead, sixty grains
- spirits of wine, an ounce
- oil of bergamot, twenty drops

## Directions

1. Boil the marrow in the bone
2. Mix the prescribed quantity, free of bone and fibre, with the other ingredients, excepting the scent
3. Add the scent last of all
4. If any other scent be preferred, the bergamot may be omitted

title: Pomatum
body:
## Ingredients

- white mutton suet, one pound
- hot water, one quart
- fresh lard, half a pound
- bees' wax, a quarter of a pound
- oil of cloves or any essential oil, fifteen drops

## Directions

1. Well boil white mutton suet in a quart of hot water
2. Wash to free it from salt
3. When dried, melt it with half a pound of fresh lard and a quarter of a pound of bees' wax
4. Pour it into an earthen vessel
5. Stir till it is cold
6. Beat into it fifteen drops of oil of cloves, or any essential oil whose scent is preferred
7. If too hard, use less wax

title: Pomfret a la Normande
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine pomfret
- egg
- pepper
- salt
- finely-chopped parsley
- minced mushroom
- butter
- minced onion
- chablis or light white wine
- a cupful of broth
- veloute
- liquor of a tin of oysters
- broth made from the bones and trimmings of the pomfret
- oysters
- black Leicestershire mushrooms
- croutons of bread

## Directions

1. Clean and trim a fine pomfret
2. Draw off the dark skin and detach the flesh from the bone with a sharp knife
3. Take the two sides obtained and season them on their respective inner sides with a well beaten egg, pepper, salt, a little finely-chopped parsley, and some minced mushroom
4. Lay them together again so the fish resumes its former appearance
5. Butter a flat silver dish or one that will stand the fire
6. Strew over the butter some minced onion
7. Place the fish thereon
8. Moisten it with a little chablis or a light white wine of that class and a cupful of broth
9. Bake it in the oven
10. Whilst baking, make a good veloute in which you should pour the liquor of a tin of oysters, and use the broth made from the bones and trimmings of the pomfret
11. When the fish is nearly done, take it out of the oven
12. Pour the liquor from its dish into your veloute
13. Garnish the fish with the oysters of the tin previously mentioned and some black Leicestershire mushrooms
14. Pour your veloute over all
15. Set the dish in the oven again for five minutes
16. Serve with croutons of bread, buttered on each side and coloured a pale brown in the oven

title: Pomfret a la Provencale
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pomfret fish
- garlic
- mushrooms
- flour
- glass of chablis, sauterne, or hock
- gravy
- thyme
- marjoram
- parsley
- lime peel
- sugar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the fish
2. Put into a sauce-pan a coffee-cupful of the fish stock, a clove of garlic, and a table-spoonful of mushrooms, all finely chopped up
3. When this has been on the fire a few minutes, add a table-spoonful of flour, stir it well
4. Pour in a glass of chablis, sauterne, or hock, with half a pint of gravy
5. Add a dessert-spoonful of finely minced thyme, marjoram, parsley, and lime peel, all mixed together, a pinch of sugar, with salt and pepper to taste
6. Let the sauce simmer slowly for half an hour, then strain
7. Serve with the boiled fish

title: Pomfret au Puree de Grandes Crevettes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pomfret
- prawns
- anchovy
- spice
- pepper
- salt
- melted butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Boil the pomfret
2. Boil and clean a dozen fair sized prawns
3. Pound the prawns to a paste in a mortar
4. Flavour with an anchovy, a little spice, pepper, and salt
5. Work the mixture with melted butter, flour, and a little broth to the consistency of mayonnaise sauce
6. Serve as hot as possible

title: Pomfret d'Eglofin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pomfret
- egg
- curled parsley
- broth from bones and trimmings
- one ounce of butter
- table-spoonful of flour
- half a pint of cooking liquor
- table-spoonful of finely chopped curled parsley
- yolk of an egg
- juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Divide your pomfret into nice fillets
2. Egg them on one side
3. Shake over the egg some finely minced curled parsley
4. Simmer the fillets, neatly rolled up and skewered, in broth made from their own bones and trimmings
5. Dish them on a hot dish as soon as they are done
6. Melt an ounce of butter in a small sauce-pan
7. Work into it a table-spoonful of flour
8. Moisten with half a pint of the liquor in which the fillets were cooked
9. Throw in a table-spoonful of finely chopped curled parsley
10. Finish the sauce, off the fire, with the yolk of an egg beaten up with the juice of a lime
