11. Pour over the fillets and serve

title: Pomfret Fillets with Caper Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pomfret
- onion
- celery
- butter
- flour
- capers
- yolks of two eggs
- melted butter
- aromatic vinegar

## Directions

1. Fillet the pomfret, and make a fish gravy with the bones and trimmings, flavoured with onion and a little celery
2. Simmer your fillets, nicely rolled in little curls, in this gravy
3. Drain them, and dish them on a hot dish
4. Make a sauce blonde with butter and flour, moistened with the liquor from the fish
5. When smooth, add a dessert-spoonful of finely minced capers
6. Off the fire, just as you serve, add the yolks of two eggs, well mixed with some melted butter
7. Pour the sauce over the fillets
8. A few drops of any aromatic vinegar will improve this sauce

title: Pomfret Fillets with Poulette Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large, or two small pomfrets
- finely minced (cooked) onion
- parsley
- trimmings of mushrooms
- a breakfast-cupful of stock
- a spoonful of chablis
- a spoonful of vinegar
- flour
- butter
- the strained yolks of four eggs
- a dozen button mushrooms
- a little white pepper and salt
- a table-spoonful of cream

## Directions

1. Divide one large, or two small pomfrets into fillets as neatly as you can
2. Season them on one side with a very little finely minced (cooked) onion, parsley, and the trimmings of the mushrooms you are about to use for the sauce
3. Roll them up enveloping the seasoning within each fillet
4. Set them to simmer gently till done, in a court bouillon, or broth made from their own bones and trimmings, assisted by a breakfast-cupful of stock, a spoonful of chablis and a spoonful of vinegar
5. Lift them out when done, strain off the broth
6. Thicken it as carefully as you can in the bain-marie with a little flour, and butter, and the strained yolks of four eggs
7. Add a dozen button mushrooms cut into quarters, a little white pepper and salt
8. As you take it off the fire, stir in a table-spoonful of cream
9. Pour this over your fillets which have been kept hot in the bain-marie whilst the sauce was being made

title: Pomfret à la Venetian
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish fillets
- butter
- a slice of tomato
- chopped parsley
- shallot
- spinach
- fish stock made from the trimmings of the fillets and some vegetables
- a spoonful of chablis or sauterne
- finely minced parsley
- chopped capers
- gherkins

## Directions

1. Dress your fish in fillets, and bake them in a buttered dish with a slice of tomato laid upon each of them, and a little chopped parsley and shallot, sprinkled over them
2. When done, arrange them upon a hot silver dish
3. Boil a little spinach, and when done, squeeze it through a piece of muslin
4. Make some melted butter, assisted by a little fish stock made from the trimmings of the fillets and some vegetables, with a spoonful of chablis or sauterne
5. Throw into it some finely minced parsley, a very little shallot, some chopped capers, and gherkins, and colour the whole with the spinach-greening
6. Serve the fish with the green sauce

title: Pommes de Terre Frites
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- beef dripping or frying medium

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well and peel them
2. Slice them carefully to a uniform thickness about half that of a rupee
3. Spread them on a clean cloth to get rid of the moisture
4. Wipe them thoroughly and spread a sheet of blotting paper ready for draining
5. Dissolve a goodly allowance of beef dripping in your friture-pan or a shallow stew-pan
6. When quite boiling, drop in your potato slices with enough fat to completely cover them
7. Watch them as they are cooking, turning and moving them about continually
8. As soon as they assume a golden tint, a nice rich yellow, lift them quickly from the fat
9. Let them drain on the blotting paper for a minute or two
10. When quite dry, turn them into a very hot silver dish and serve

title: Poor Man's Champagne
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint Scotch ale
- one bottle good ginger beer

## Directions

1. Put one pint of Scotch ale into a jug
2. Add one bottle of good ginger beer

title: Pork Raised Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of pork
- black pepper
- salt
- liquid jelly made from pettitoes and pork scraps

## Directions

1. Choose the neck of pork
2. Omit the parsley
3. Season with black pepper and salt, in the proportion of two-thirds salt to one of pepper
4. Cut the meat into pieces as for a dry curry, keeping the fat and lean separate
5. Pack the pieces of meat as closely as you can
6. Bake the pie very slowly
7. A little liquid jelly made from pettitoes and pork scraps may be poured in after the baking
8. No gravy should be added before the baking operation

title: Pork Vindaloo
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fat pork
- vinegar
- salt
- ground condiments
- ghee or lard
- peppercorns
- bay-leaves

## Directions

1. Cut up two pounds of fat pork into large squares, and curry according to the directions given in the foregoing recipe, omitting the cloves, cardamoms, and cinnamon.

title: Pork-Pie Crust
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of water
- a quarter of a pound of clarified suet
- one ounce of fresh butter
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- a pound and a half of well-dried flour
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Put half a pint of water into a sauce-pan, and let it boil
2. when boiling, stir into it a quarter of a pound of clarified suet, and one ounce of fresh butter with a tea-spoonful of salt
3. Stir till the fat has melted, and then pour the contents of the sauce-pan, boiling hot, into a bowl containing a pound and a half of well-dried flour
4. Work the mixture to a stiff paste adding a little water, if necessary, and turn it out upon a cold pastry slab
5. roll it out three-quarters of an inch thick, as evenly and level as possible, and let it get quite cold
6. butter the tin, and cut an oval piece of paste a little larger than the bottom of it so that the edges may turn up, and be more readily fixed to the wall, or side-paste
7. cut out a strip three and a half inches wide, and sufficiently long to go round the inside of the wall of the tin
8. fix the bottom of the wall to the oval piece at the bottom with white of egg, pinching them closely together
9. fill the pie with whatever meat you have prepared, covering it over with an oval cap, cut like the bottom piece, cementing it with white of egg and pinching it tightly to the top of the wall
10. brush the pie over with white of egg and bake it in a slow oven

title: Pot-au-Feu
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds soup-meat (3 pounds flesh to 1 pound bone)
- 2 ounces salt
- Water
- A couple of large white onions
- A clove of garlic
- Two large or three small carrots
- Two or three turnips
- Six leeks
- One head of celery
- A bunch of curly parsley
- Two cloves
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- A handful of whole pepper
- A dessert-spoonful of sugar
- A table-spoonful of Harvey sauce
- Two table-spoonfuls of mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Put a piece of soup-meat of four pounds weight, in the proportion of three pounds of flesh to one of bone, tightly bound with a string, with two ounces of salt and the bones separately broken up, into a stock pot filled with water, so as to completely cover the meat.
2. Put the pot by the side of the fire and let it become gradually heated.
3. As this takes place, a scum will form upon the surface which must be carefully removed as it rises.
4. When nearly boiling, a coffee cupful of cold water should be thrown into the pot to accelerate the rising of the scum.
5. Keep the water from boiling point until all the scum is removed.
6. Put into the saucepan the following vegetables which should have been previously carefully cleaned and cut up: a couple of large white onions, a clove of garlic, two large or three small carrots, two or three turnips, six leeks, one head of celery, a bunch of curly parsley, and two cloves in the onion.
7. Put in, tied up in a piece of muslin, some thyme, marjoram, and a handful of whole pepper (a tea-spoonful each of dried thyme and marjoram will be quite enough).
8. Watch the vegetables carefully, and remove them when they are done.
9. Remove the muslin bag also.
10. Put in a dessert-spoonful of sugar, a table-spoonful of Harvey sauce, and two of mushroom ketchup.
11. Let it simmer slowly from three to four hours.
12. Strain the soup into a basin.

title: Potage a la Bonne Femme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of stock
- one good-sized (Bombay) onion
- two ounces of good tinned butter
- a quarter of a pound of sorrel leaves
- a lettuce
- a bunch of parsley
- pepper
- salt
- half an ounce of flour
- a dessert-spoonful of pounded loaf-sugar
- half a cupful of the stock, freed from fat, and not coloured
- bread
- crust along one side

## Directions

1. Prepare a quart of stock and keep by your side
2. Cut up a good-sized (Bombay) onion into very thin rounds
3. Place the onion in a sauce-pan with two ounces of good tinned butter
4. Take care not to let the onion get brown
5. When the onion is half done, throw in a quarter of a pound of sorrel leaves, a lettuce, and a bunch of parsley, all finely shred
6. Add pepper, salt, and half an ounce of flour
7. Keep stirring for five minutes
8. Add a dessert-spoonful of pounded loaf-sugar and half a cupful of the stock, freed from fat, and not coloured
9. Let the mixture reduce nearly to a glaze
10. Gradually stir in about a quart of the stock
11. Let the soup simmer for a quarter of an hour
12. Prepare about a dozen pieces of bread cut very thin about two inches long, and an inch wide, taking care that there is crust along one of their long sides
13. Dry these bread pieces thoroughly in the oven
14. When it is time to send up the soup, remove the superfluous fat from it
15. Set it to simmer for a quarter of an hour

title: Potage A La Nivernaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- soup meat for stock
- four good-sized carrots
- one table-spoonful of butter
- a salt-spoonful of powdered sugar
- pepper
- salt
- stock

## Directions

1. Set your soup meat for stock as usual for a clear soup
2. Take four good-sized carrots, wash them well
3. Cut out of them a number of thickish pieces the size and shape of a pigeon's egg, or a little smaller
4. When you have cut a couple of dozen such pieces, throw all the scraps and trimmings of the carrots into the stock pot to flavour the soup well
5. Shortly before serving time, melt a table-spoonful of butter in a frying-pan
6. Put into it the carrot pieces, which should have been all but boiled till soft in the soup
7. Toss them about over the fire adding a salt-spoonful of powdered sugar with a dusting of pepper and of salt
8. Moisten the pan with a spoonful of stock as the butter is absorbed
9. When done, the pieces of carrot will be almost glazed
10. Put the glazed carrots into the tureen and pour the soup over them

title: Potage a la Palestine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- butter
- flour
- stock
- cream or milk and egg yolk
- pepper corns
- parsley
- sweet onion
- bacon or ham
- bread for croutons

## Directions

1. Wash, peel, and boil a nice dish of artichokes
2. Pass them through the sieve and save the pulp
3. Obtain sufficient nice clear stock from soup meat for the puree
4. Melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
5. Incorporate therewith a dessert-spoonful of flour
6. Add stock and pulp of artichoke by degrees
7. When the puree comes to the boil, it will be ready to serve
8. On its way to the table, it may be enriched by a table-spoonful of cream, or a little fresh milk into which the yolk of an egg has been stirred
9. Hand round fried croutons of bread with it

title: Potage a la Puree de Becassines
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- one good-sized chicken
- sweet herbs
- mixed vegetables
- six snipes
- two glasses of port wine
- a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a dessert-spoonful of crystal-vinegar
- a couple of ounces of butter
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a glass of port
- a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
- juice of a good lime
- a raw egg

## Directions

1. Make a good strong stock for six basins (at least a quart and a half) with a shin of beef, and one good-sized chicken cut up into fragments; flavour it highly with sweet herbs, and mixed vegetables.
2. Take six snipes, and lightly roast them: pick the whole of the meat from their breasts, and save it; mash the bones that remain, and put them in a bowl with the trails.
3. When the stock is quite ready, strain it, return it to the pot, and throw into it the bowlful of mashed snipe bones, two glasses of port wine, a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly, and a dessert-spoonful of crystal-vinegar.
4. Let the contents of the pot simmer slowly for an hour and half, skimming off any scum that may rise.
5. Strain the liquor from the bones, and set it in a bowl to cool.
6. Pass the snipe meat that you saved through the mincing machine, and pound it thoroughly to a paste, using a little of the soup to help that operation.
7. Skim any fat that may have risen on the surface of your soup.
8. Take a roomy sauce-pan, place it on the fire, put a couple of ounces of butter into it, stir into the butter a tablespoonful of flour, work it to a smooth paste.
9. Add, by degrees both soup and snipe paste, keeping the spoon at work the whole time.
10. When all is expended, let the puree reach the boil in order to thicken properly.
11. Add a glass of port, a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, and the juice of a good lime.
12. Break a raw egg into a cup, saving the yolk carefully; mix a little of the soup with the yolk.

title: Potage de Lievre (Hare Soup)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- half pound of butter
- sliced onion
- muslin bagful of herbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- table spoonful of flour
- quart and a half of beef consomme
- four glasses of portwine or claret
- blood from hare

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, and thoroughly wash the hare, saving all the blood in a cup
2. Cut the hare up into small pieces and put them into a stew-pan with half pound of butter, a sliced onion, and a muslin bagful of herbs
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Fry the meat over a brisk fire for five minutes
5. Make a roux in another stew-pan with a pat of butter and a table spoonful of flour
6. Moisten the roux with a quart and a half of beef consomme and add four glasses of portwine or claret
7. Pour this into the pan containing the pieces of hare
8. Let them stew very slowly till thoroughly done
9. Drain off the liquor from the bones, put the neatest pieces of hare on one side for serving with the soup
10. Return the liquor to the stew-pan and let it throw up all grease in the form of scum, which skim off carefully
11. Mix the saved blood with some of the soup from the stew-pan and thoroughly amalgamate these
12. Add the mixture slowly through a pointed tin strainer to the gradually re-heating soup
13. Let it come nearly to the boil
14. Serve it over the pieces of hare you preserved

title: Potage à la Crécy
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- stock made from bones, meat, scraps (bones of ham and bacon especially valuable)
- dried sweet herbs
- onion
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- salt and pepper to taste
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil as many carrots as you think will suffice for the quantity of soup you have to make in the stock
2. When thoroughly done, drain them, and pass them through the sieve
3. Mix the pulp so obtained with sufficient of the stock to make a puree a little thinner than you wish your soup eventually to be
4. Melt a piece of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan, and work a little flour into it
5. Gradually add the puree, and stir without ceasing till the soup comes to the boil
6. When it will be found of the proper consistency, skim if necessary, and serve

title: Potage à la Julienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- consomme
- Julienne tablet
- water or weak stock
- sugar
- table-spoonful of Madeira

## Directions

1. Make a good, strong, and clear consomme sufficient for your party
2. Cut off a portion of the Julienne tablet
3. Place it in a sauce-pan with a large allowance of boiling water or weak stock
4. Allow it to cook until the pieces of vegetable detach themselves and appear nice and tender
5. Drain them when thus ready
6. Add them to the consomme with a pinch of sugar and a table-spoonful of Madeira

title: Potage à la Julienne (From Fresh Vegetables)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of carrots, turnips, leeks, onions, and celery
- couple of ounces of fresh butter
- tea-spoonful of powdered sugar
- pepper
- salt
- broth from the consomme

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of carrots, turnips, leeks, onions, and celery
2. Cut them all into thin strips not more than the eighth of an inch across and an inch long
3. Put them into a sauce-pan with a couple of ounces of fresh butter, a tea-spoonful of powdered sugar, a little pepper, and a pinch of salt
4. Toss them lightly on the fire until they take colour slightly, say for five minutes or so
5. Cover them with a little broth from the consomme and carefully remove the grease thrown up by the butter

title: Potage à la Purée de Légumes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- gravy soup or stock
- 2 carrots
- 1 turnip
- 1 onion
- 1 stick of celery
- pepper
- salt
- 1 ounce of butter
- 1 dessert-spoonful of flour
- 1 egg yolk
- milk
- fried croutons of bread

## Directions

1. Prepare early in the day the usual gravy soup, or stock
2. Take it off the fire in the afternoon, and add two carrots, a turnip, an onion, a stick of celery, and a little pepper and salt
3. Boil together
4. When thoroughly done, drain off the soup and pass the boiled vegetables through the wire sieve
5. Mix over the fire in a saucepan an ounce of butter, and a dessert-spoonful of flour
6. Gradually add vegetable pulp and stock, stirring well, until you have exhausted the whole
7. Let it come to the boil
8. Take the soup off the fire and stir in the yolk of an egg beaten up in a little milk
9. Accompany with fried croutons of bread

title: Potage à la Reine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- stock
- a cold roast fowl
- bread-crumbs
- twelve sweet almonds
- three bitter almonds
- the hard-boiled yolks of four eggs
- bones
- uncoloured beef stock
- an ounce of butter
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a coffee-cupful of cream or milk with the strained yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Prepare your stock as usual but without colouring
2. Remove the flesh from a cold roast fowl, excluding all skin, and browned parts
3. Add to the meat so obtained, half its bulk of bread-crumbs soaked in stock
4. Pound both together in a mortar, with twelve sweet, and three bitter almonds, and the hard-boiled yolks of four eggs
5. Mash and cast all the bones into as much uncoloured beef stock as you think you will require for eight basins, and let them simmer for two or three hours
6. Pass your pounded fowl and crumbs through the sieve to get rid of lumps, gristle, &c., moistening it with a spoonful or so of stock to assist the operation
7. When near the dinner hour, strain off your stock from the bones, and place it to get cool, removing all the fat that may rise to the surface
8. Take a sauce-pan and melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of it, stirring in a tablespoonful of flour
9. Add a little stock, and work the paste so obtained without ceasing, gradually pouring in stock, and adding pounded fowl, until you have exhausted your supply
10. Let the puree now come to the boil
11. Remove the sauce-pan from the fire, and as you pour it into the tureen, stir into it a coffee-cupful of cream, (or that quantity of milk with which the strained yolk of an egg has been mixed) and serve

title: Potagé À La Crème Croisée
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of pearl barley
- a quart of clear stock
- additional stock uncoloured and well flavoured
- yolk of an egg
- a gill of milk or cream
- bread
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil half a pint of pearl barley in a quart of clear stock till it is reduced to a pulp
2. Pass it through a hair sieve, and add sufficient additional stock uncoloured, and very well flavoured, to bring the puree to the consistency of cream
3. Put it now in a sauce-pan on the fire till it comes to the boil
4. Stir into it off the fire the yolk of an egg thoroughly beaten up with a gill of milk (or cream if you can spare it)
5. Serve with dice of bread, dipped in stock, and crisped in the oven

title: Potato and Meat Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- one or two small onions
- milk
- one egg
- salt
- pepper
- slices of beef or mutton chops
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil some potatoes
2. Peel and pound them in a mortar with one or two small onions
3. Moisten them with milk and an egg beaten up
4. Add a little salt and pepper
5. Season slices of beef or mutton chops with salt and pepper, and more onion, if the flavour is approved
6. Rub the bottom of a pudding-dish with butter
7. Put in a layer of the mashed potatoes, which should be as thick as a batter, and then a layer of meat, and so on alternately, till the dish is filled, ending with potatoes
8. Bake in an oven for one hour

title: Potato Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- moderate or middling sized white Patna onion
- two hot green chilies
- fresh lime juice
- eight or ten well-boiled potatoes
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of good mustard oil

## Directions

1. Remove the outer coats of the onion and slice very fine
2. Slice or cut up the two hot green chilies
3. Squeeze the lime juice over the onion and chilies and allow to soak
4. Bruise the potatoes down with a large silver or plated fork
5. When the potatoes are half bruised, add the onions and chilies, with as much of the lime-juice as may be agreeable
6. Mix all well together with a light hand, so that the potatoes may not cake, and yet be well and thoroughly mashed and mixed

title: Potato Cases with Ragout, Financiere Fashion
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of potatoes
- yolks of five eggs
- grated mace
- salt
- egg and bread-crumbs for coating
- ragout a la financiere
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel two pounds of potatoes and cook them as usual
2. When done, stir into them the yolks of five eggs, add a little grated mace and a little salt, and stir them over the fire for five minutes
3. Pass them through the sieve, pat them to a paste, and flatten that out on your slab about two inches thick
4. Let it get cold
5. With your cutter, cut it into cylinders (or little drums) two inches in diameter
6. Egg and bread-crumb each cylinder, and fry these potato drums till they are a bright golden yellow
7. Carefully slice off the top of each drum (say) a quarter of an inch thick
8. Place the caps so obtained on one side, and scoop out the interior of your drums very carefully
9. Put the cases out of the way, well covered up
10. As dinner time draws near, fill up each cassolette with a portion of ragout a la financiere
11. Place the caps on the top of each, and put them in the oven for a few minutes to bring them to perfection
12. Serve the cassolettes upon a napkin, and garnish them with fried parsley

title: Potato Duchesses
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- good milk
- butter
- yolks of two eggs
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- parsley or celery leaves
- marjoram leaves
- a beaten egg

## Directions

1. Mash potatoes up with a little good milk and butter and the yolks of two eggs
2. Add pepper, salt, some grated nutmeg, and some parsley or celery leaves chopped fine (a few marjoram leaves are an improvement)
3. Roll the mixture into balls the size of a pippin, brush them over with a beaten egg, set them on a buttered tin, and bake for a few minutes till they turn a golden yellow colour

title: Potato Flour For Souffles
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes of a good mealy kind
- spring water

## Directions

1. Choose some potatoes of a good mealy kind, peel and wash them (raw)
2. Grate them to pulp with a strong bread grater, emptying the grated stuff into a large bowl of clean spring water
3. Stir the potato and water together briskly for a few minutes, and then let the former settle
4. After resting ten minutes, strain off the water
5. Repeat the process with another basinful of water, and let it rest after stirring for ten minutes, again straining off the water
6. Continue the washings until the water remains quite clear after the sediment has settled at the bottom of the bowl
7. When satisfied that the grated potato has been thoroughly cleansed, take it out of the bowl, drain it
8. Spread it out upon dishes to dry and bleach in the sun, turning it frequently
9. When quite dry, pound it in a mortar, and pass the flour so obtained through a silk or hair sieve
10. Bottle it securely in dry bottles, and cork it down tightly

title: Potato Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- milk
- butter
- nicely-browned mutton, beef, or chicken stew

## Directions

1. Boil and mash down some potatoes, with pepper, salt, milk, and butter
2. Line a pie-dish a quarter of an inch thick with the mash
3. Arrange in it a nicely-browned mutton, beef, or chicken stew, cover it over with a thick coat of the mashed potatoes, and bake for a quarter of an hour

title: Potato Ribbons
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five large potatoes
- beef dripping
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash four or five large potatoes, scrape them, and cut them into thin strips round and round, keeping as nearly to one width as possible
2. Throw them into cold water as they are cut
3. Fry them of a light brown, in very hot or boiling beef dripping
4. Strew over them a little salt and pepper
5. Before serving, drain them upon a dish turned up before the fire

title: Potato with Patol
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- patol
- potato
- green peas
- ghee or oil
- powdered turmeric
- coriander
- pepper
- cumin
- red chillies
- salt
- cassia leaves
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Skin the vegetables very thin and cut the patols in two and the potatoes in big pieces
2. Brown the pieces of vegetables and some green peas in ghee or oil and then pour in water with powdered turmeric, coriander, pepper, cumin, red chillies and salt
3. Cover the pan
4. Season the curry with ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves and red chillies when the vegetables are boiled
5. Add powdered garam-masalla and take down when the soup is thick

title: Potatoes a l'Americaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- milk
- onion
- spice
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour
- yolk of an egg
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up your boiled potatoes into thick slices
2. Flavour a little milk with onion, spice, pepper, and salt
3. Strain and thicken it with butter and flour till you have a nice sauce blanche
4. Place your slices of potato in this sauce
5. Heat them up to boiling point
6. Take the sauce-pan off the fire
7. Stir in the yolk of an egg
8. Add a large spoonful of chopped parsley and a pat of butter the size of a rupee
9. Serve

title: Potatoes a la Duchesse
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- yolks of two or three eggs
- a gill of cream
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- butter
- egg
- very fine stale bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Well work potatoes through the sieve
2. Enrich with the yolks of two or three eggs and a gill of cream
3. Give a delicate flavouring of salt, pepper, nutmeg, and chopped parsley
4. Roll into balls
5. Either fry gently in butter, or broom in the oven on a buttered tin
6. Brush over with egg and bread crumb with very fine stale crumbs

title: Potatoes a la Gr. C.
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bombay onion
- potato
- butter
- cream
- pepper
- yolks of two eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil a Bombay onion very soft
2. Beat it hot with four times its bulk of potato
3. Add butter, cream, pepper, the yolks of two eggs, and salt
4. Pass the whole through the sieve
5. Roll this mixture into balls
6. Treat them as laid down for the Duchesse

title: Potatoes a la Lyonnaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bombay onion
- butter
- broth
- potatoes
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry a Bombay onion (chopped small) in butter till it begins to brown
2. Add a wine-glass of broth with your pieces of potato
3. Toss them till coloured
4. Give them a dust of pepper and salt

title: Potatoes a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- butter
- lime juice
- curled parsley
- broth

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes and slice them moderately thickly
2. Toss them in plenty of butter in your frying-pan till they colour nicely
3. Pour the brown butter over them
4. Add a few drops of lime juice
5. Add a heaped up table-spoonful of very finely chopped curled parsley
6. Add half a cupful of broth

title: Potatoes a la Provencale
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- butter
- finely pared lime-peel
- chopped parsley
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Fry chopped onion in butter till it begins to brown
2. Add broth with pieces of potato
3. Toss them till coloured
4. Add a little finely pared lime-peel, some chopped parsley, an atom of garlic, salt, pepper, and a dust of grated nutmeg
5. When serving, sprinkle a little lime juice over the potatoes

title: Potatoes and Onions
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- onions
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Peel and chop some potatoes and onions in thin pieces.
2. Fry them in oil with salt.
3. They will be crisp.

title: Potatoes and Onions with Turmeric
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- onions
- ground turmeric
- salt
- green chillies
- oil

## Directions

1. Mix ground turmeric, salt and a few minced green chillies with the chopped vegetables.
2. Fry them in oil.
3. Stir the vegetables so that they may brown uniformly.

title: Potatoes Sautees
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil your potatoes
2. Slice them moderately thickly
3. Toss them in plenty of butter in your frying-pan till they colour nicely
4. Pour the brown butter over them
5. Give them a dust of salt

title: Potatoes à l'Américaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- milk
- onion
- spice
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour
- egg yolk
- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut up your boiled potatoes into thick slices
2. Flavour a little milk with onion, spice, pepper, and salt
3. Strain and thicken it with butter and flour till you have a nice sauce blanche
4. Place your slices of potato in this sauce and heat them up to boiling point
5. Take the sauce-pan off the fire and stir in the yolk of an egg
6. Add a large spoonful of chopped parsley with a pat of butter the size of a rupee and serve

title: Potted Ox-Cheek
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover ox-cheek meat
- liquor from boiling the cheek
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- vinegar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small bits, and heat up with a little of the liquor in which the cheek was boiled, seasoned with pepper, salt, nutmeg, and a little vinegar
2. Put into a mould, and turn out when required for use
3. Eat with mustard and vinegar

title: Potted Prawns
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice sized prawns
- melted butter
- salt
- white pepper
- red pepper
- a few drops of anchovy sauce
- a little pounded mace
- freshly made butter

## Directions

1. Select some nice sized prawns
2. Boil them
3. Pick out very carefully all the grit and that black line which runs straight down the back and underneath every prawn
4. Wash them afterwards in cold water
5. Pour a lot of water over them as they lie on the top of your sieve
6. Dry them
7. Toss them in a little melted butter in a frying-pan until they have absorbed the butter
8. Pass through the mincing machine
9. Pound them thoroughly in the mortar
10. Press them through the wire sieve
11. Season the paste with salt, white and red pepper, a few drops of anchovy sauce, and a little pounded mace
12. Work the whole together with some nice freshly made butter till thoroughly incorporated
13. Set it in an earthenware jar
14. Pour a spoonful of melted butter over the surface

title: Potted Tongue
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound weight of tongue, home-cured
- four ounces of the best butter
- a salt-spoonful of pounded mace
- a coffee-cup of beef gravy
- a tea-spoonful of spiced pepper

## Directions

1. Cut off the hard skin of the tongue, and pass the meat through your mincing machine.
2. Pound it thoroughly in your mortar, adding the butter and gravy during the operation.
3. Press the meat through your wire-sieve to get rid of gristle and lumps.
4. Work the spiced pepper and mace into it, and pat it down tightly in a jar.
5. Smooth the surface.
6. Melt a table-spoonful of the same sort of butter in a sauce-pan, and pour it over the surface.
7. Let it get cold, and the work will be completed.

title: Prawn and Cucumber Curry
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- good-sized cucumber or two small ones
- plenty of water
- half an ounce of butter
- a teaspoonful of salt
- about a pound and a half of prawns in their shells
- two table-spoonfuls of salt
- a gallon of water
- flour
- very large cocoanut
- onions
- a very little garlic
- green ginger
- turmeric powder
- a little powdered cinnamon and cloves
- red and green chilli-skin or capsicum

## Directions

1. Take a good-sized cucumber, or two small ones, cut them lengthwise into quarters, remove the seeds, and peel off the green skin
2. Cut them into pieces two inches long and one inch thick, and put them into a stew-pan with plenty of water, half an ounce of butter, and a teaspoonful of salt
3. Simmer them until three parts done; then drain the liquid off, and turn the pieces of cucumber out upon a clean dish, and cover them up
4. Throw two table-spoonfuls of salt into a gallon of water, put the pan on the fire, and when the water boils fast, slip into it about a pound and a half of prawns weighed in their shells
5. Boil, and as soon as the prawns turn a rosy pink, stop, drain them from the water, let them get cold, and shell them, removing their heads completely
6. Pass a knife down the line of the back of each prawn, slightly open the groove as it were, and pick out of it the black gritty dirt that you will find there
7. Carry out a similar process with the inner line, and cast the cleaned prawns into a basin of spring water
8. Having washed them again thoroughly, pick them out, and dry them on a cloth
9. If very large, divide them in halves lengthwise, and sever each half in twain
10. Dust them over with flour, and put them on a dish
11. Choose a very large cocoanut, the fresher the better, break it in half, and, with a cocoanut scraper, remove the whole of the white flesh, casting it into a bowl

title: Prawn Butter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- butter
- cayenne pepper
- powdered mace
- iced butter

## Directions

1. Boil the prawns, clean them carefully, pound them to a paste in your mortar, mixing a little butter with them to assist the operation.
2. Melt some butter in a sauce-pan, mix your pounded prawn meat in it, and as soon as it appears to have absorbed the butter (having been well stirred during the process and flavoured with a very little cayenne pepper, and powdered mace), take it out.
3. Let it get cold and mix it with your iced butter in the proportion of about half and half.
4. The pounding must be thorough; there should be no granulated particles of prawn meat in the butter.

title: Prawn Cofta Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty to forty of the best prawns
- salt
- water
- bread-crumbs
- lard, ghee, or mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chillies
- ground turmeric
- ground peppercorns
- ground garlic
- garden herbs
- unroasted coriander-seed
- bay-leaves
- lemon-grass

## Directions

1. Get thirty to forty of the best prawns, and remove the heads and shells
2. Wash the prawns well with salt and water, then pound them to a pulp
3. Mix with it all the ingredients as directed for the beef cofta
4. Make into balls, roll them in bread-crumbs, and set aside
5. After washing the heads, remove the shells, and bruise the contents with a dessertspoonful of unroasted coriander-seed
6. Take all the juice, and fry it with the ground condiments
7. Put in the balls, brown them, add salt to taste, a cup of water, and simmer until they are cooked

title: Prawn Croustades
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three small dinner rolls
- butter
- boiled prawns
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- white sauce
- bread-crumb

## Directions

1. Choose two or three small dinner rolls which have been baked in tins and will stand upright
2. Scoop out all the crumb
3. Fry the cases so obtained a golden yellow colour in melted butter
4. Drain them
5. Prepare enough boiled prawns to fill them
6. Cut them into quarter inch pieces
7. Toss them in melted butter with a little mace, pepper and salt
8. Fill your cases, moistening the salpicon with a little white sauce
9. Shake a little bread-crumb over the surface of each
10. Place them on a buttered baking tin
11. Heat them for five minutes in the oven
12. Serve as soon as the tops take colour

title: Prawn Curry
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- flour
- cocoanut
- boiling water
- tinned butter
- white Bombay onion
- garlic
- turmeric powder
- salt
- sugar
- mixed cloves and cinnamon powder
- chicken broth or fish consomme
- fish bones
- prawn shells
- scraps of fish
- onion
- carrot
- parsley
- green ginger
- green chillies
- cucumber

## Directions

1. Choose a very large cocoanut, the fresher the better, break it in half, and with a cocoanut scraper, remove the whole of the white flesh, casting it into a bowl.
2. Upon the raspings thus obtained, pour a breakfast-cupful of boiling water, leave it for a quarter of an hour, and then strain the liquid off. This is the best or 'number one' infusion, which must be put away, and not added to the curry till the last thing before serving.
3. Return the raspings to their bowl, and pour over them a pint and a half of boiling water, stir well, and let the liquid stand for half an hour.
4. Strain the liquid, and squeeze the nutty atoms dry in muslin, so that every drop of the cocoanut essence may be secured. The liquor thus obtained is the 'number two' infusion.
5. Put two ounces of good tinned butter into a stewpan, and mix into it, as it melts over a brisk fire, a white Bombay onion sliced into rings, and a clove of garlic finely minced.
6. Lightly fry, but do not allow the onions to turn colour before adding a table-spoonful of good flour, a dessert-spoonful of turmeric powder, a tea-spoonful of salt, and a scant one of sugar, a tea-spoonful of mixed cloves and cinnamon powder, and, by degrees, the 'number two infusion.'
7. Add a breakfast-cupful of thick chicken broth, or fish consomme, together with a heaped-up table-spoonful of sliced green ginger, and three green chillies cut into Julienne-like strips.
8. Remove the stewpan from the fire, and place it in a bath of boiling water, to keep warm, while you add the prawns and the slices of partly-cooked cucumber.
9. Permit the curry to rest for about half an hour, at the expiration of which the pan may be placed over a moderate fire, and its contents brought to simmering point.
10. When satisfied that both the prawns and vegetable are tender, stir in the 'number one' infusion, and with it a tea-spoonful of lime-juice.
11. Simmer for five minutes, then dish up and serve.

title: Prawn Cutlet
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- pepper
- salt
- finely-minced soup herbs
- yolk of eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Shell and wash the prawns
2. Remove the heads, but leave the tails
3. Slit them down in the centre, and gently beat them flat with a rolling-pin
4. Sprinkle them with pepper and salt, and some finely-minced soup herbs
5. Rub them over with yolk of eggs, and dredge with flour
6. Fry over a very moderate fire to a rich light brown colour
7. Garnish the dish with fried green parsley, or serve up with tomato sauce gravy

title: Prawn Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- mustard oil, one chittack and a half
- ground onions, four teaspoonfuls
- ground turmeric, one teaspoonful
- ground chilies, one teaspoonful
- ground garlic, a quarter of a teaspoonful
- curry onions, twelve, cut lengthways into six or eight slices each
- water, one cupful
- large prawns, twelve
- unroasted coriander-seed

## Directions

1. Clean and thoroughly wash the prawns, rejecting the heads or taking only their substance pounded and squeezed out with unroasted coriander-seed
2. Parboil the prawns
3. Make the doopiaja in all respects according to the ordinary mode

title: Prawn Doopiaja Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- prawn doopiaja or prawn cofta curry
- gravy

## Directions

1. Pare away very finely all the outer brown crust of the bread, without injuring the inner crust
2. Cut out of the top of the loaf a small square sufficiently large to extract from within all the crumb, leaving the shell complete
3. Fill the loaf up to the top either with some prawn doopiaja minced, or with the prawn cofta curry, and as much gravy as it will take
4. Replace the square bit at the top
5. Bake it to a light brown
6. Serve up hot

title: Prawn Doopiaja Pittas
body:
## Ingredients

- pastry
- prawn doopiaja
- ghee

## Directions

1. Prepare pastry the same as Dal Pittas
2. Enclose in the pastry a tablespoonful of the prawn doopiaja
3. Mince the prawns before being put into the pastry
4. Fry in ghee

title: Prawn Fritters
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled prawns
- butter
- weak gravy
- batter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Pound a dish of boiled prawns in a mortar with some butter and weak gravy
2. When quite worked to a puree, incorporate it with the batter
3. Drop the mixture by dessert-spoonfuls at a time into your friture-pan
4. Let the fritters cook till they turn a rich golden colour and are as crisp as biscuits
5. Drain and serve them on a napkin with crisply fried parsley

title: Prawn Pellow
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten good-sized bagda prawns
- a good hard cocoanut
- rice
- onions
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry and set aside the sliced onions
2. Fry the rice in cocoanut milk (vide recipe No. 54)
3. Observe in all particulars the instructions given for the chicken pellow (recipe No. 12)
4. Dish up the pellow and strew over it the fried onions
5. Garnish with the prawns finely boiled and two hard-boiled eggs cut in halves or in some device

title: Prawn Powder
body:
## Ingredients

- dry prawns
- red pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take a seer of dry prawns
2. Wash them well, dry over the fire until crisp
3. Pound fine, with some red pepper and nutmeg
4. Pass through a sieve, and bottle for use

title: Prawn Puree
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bone
- vegetables
- herbs
- cold boiled prawns
- butter
- salt
- spiced pepper
- ounce of butter
- table-spoonful of flour
- bread-crumb
- stock
- consomme
- dice of fried bread
- boiling cream or milk

## Directions

1. Make a strong clear consomme with beef, bone, vegetables and herbs, as usual.
2. When done, strain it into a bowl to be ready when wanted.
3. Pick enough cold boiled prawns to fill a half-pint pot to the brim.
4. Pound these in a mortar with a good allowance of butter till you get them to a pulp.
5. Flavour this with salt, and a little spiced pepper to taste.
6. Melt an ounce of butter in a sauce-pan, and incorporate therewith a table-spoonful of flour.
7. Mix this with the prawn pulp.
8. Take about the same quantity of bread-crumb well soaked in stock (the consomme) that you have of prawn, and add it to the prawn pulp also, off the fire, mixing the two together by degrees thoroughly, and gradually adding the consomme till you find you have a soup a little less thick than that you want eventually to get.
9. Set the sauce-pan on the fire, and stir vigorously till it boils, and thickens.
10. Take it off the fire then, and let it get cool, to enable you to remove any fat that may rise.
11. The puree should be pressed through a hair sieve into a bowl.
12. When wanted, it must be re-heated, and served with dice of fried bread.

title: Prawn Quenelles
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen and a half well-cleaned prawns
- half their bulk of crumb of bread
- stock
- two whole raw eggs
- a pinch of salt
- a dusting of white or Nepaul pepper
- a tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Pound the well-cleaned prawns with half their bulk of crumb of bread soaked in stock
2. Work in with the paste two whole raw eggs
3. Season with a pinch of salt, a dusting of white or Nepaul pepper, and a tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce
4. When thoroughly blended and of the right consistency, form tiny olive-shaped quenelles between two tea-spoons
5. Poach them for about five minutes in boiling stock or water
6. Drain them and add them to the soup at the last moment

title: Pressed Beef
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice fleshy piece of the flank with fat and lean pretty equally balanced
- salt
- spiced pepper
- brine (without saltpetre)

## Directions

1. Rub it carefully with salt and spiced pepper.
2. Let it rest in brine without saltpetre for forty-eight hours.
3. Drain it dry and wipe it.
4. Stew it gently till nice and tender.
5. Set it on a dish with a weight above it and let it get quite cold.
6. Remove the weight and trim the joint neatly in a rectangular form with a sharp knife.
7. Glaze its surface and serve.

title: Princess Royal Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of eight fresh eggs
- one dessertspoonful corn-flour
- pounded loaf sugar
- quarter of a pound Jordan almonds
- one seer good cold milk
- essence of almonds or orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Beat up in a large deep bowl the yolks of eight fresh eggs
2. Dredge into it while beating up a dessertspoonful of corn-flour
3. Sweeten to taste with the best pounded loaf sugar
4. Add to it a quarter of a pound of Jordan almonds well bruised in a marble mortar
5. Pour the mixture into a clean newly-tinned copper pan
6. Stir into it a seer of good cold milk
7. Put the pan on the fire
8. Never cease stirring it, keeping the spoon as much as possible in the centre of the pan
9. Reduce the flame after it has boiled for fifteen minutes, and continue to boil for a few minutes longer, until the custard is of the consistency required
10. Fill the custard cups or glasses, leaving about half an inch space to fill up with the whites of the eggs, beaten up to a very light froth
11. Flavour it with a little essence of almonds, or orange-flower water

title: Proleha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- One entire fish 1 or 1.5 seers
- ghee 1 powa
- besam 1 powa
- garam-masala 0.5 tola
- pepper 1 tola
- coriander 2 tolas
- curd 0.5 powa
- salt 2 tolas
- a few cassia leaves
- cloves
- panch-foron
- almonds
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Flake the fish and remove the fins and intestines
2. Wrap with a linen piece and give a coating of clay all over its surface
3. Burn it with a sand-bath in a pan
4. Remove the clay, wash the fish, remove the bones
5. Mix thoroughly with besam and powdered garam-masala and form small cakes
6. Heat water in a pan covering its bottom with banana leaves
7. Put the cakes carefully on them and boil till the cakes harden a little
8. Boil ghee with cassia leaves, cloves and panch-foron and brown the cakes in it
9. Add salt, water, curd, powdered coriander, pepper
10. Add almonds and a little flour when boiled
11. Take down after adding ghee and garam-masala

title: Proleha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- one entire fish, 1 or 1.5 seers
- ghee, one powa
- besam, one powa
- garam-masalla, half tola
- pepper, one tola
- coriander, 2 tolas
- curd, half powa
- salt, 2 tolas
- a few cassia leaves

## Directions

1. Flake the fish and remove the fins and intestines.
2. Wrap with a linen piece and give a coating of clay all over its surface.
3. Burn it with a sand-bath in a pan.
4. Then remove the clay, wash the fish and remove the bones.

title: Proleha
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- a few hard-boiled eggs
- ghee
- a few cloves
- curd
- pasted ginger
- pepper
- coriander
- salt
- water
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Shell a few hard-boiled eggs and cut them into 4 or 5 slices each.
2. Boil some ghee with a few cloves and singe the eggs in it a little.
3. Add curd, pasted ginger, pepper and coriander.
4. Add salt and water a little later.
5. Add ghee and garam-masalla, and take down when the soup is thick.

title: Prune Jelly
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of prunes
- two ounces of white sugar
- a slice or two of a lime
- a little cinnamon
- claret and water, mixed half and half
- about an ounce of gelatine
- a liqueur glass of cherry brandy
- a glass of good red wine
- blanched almonds
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of prunes into a sauce-pan with two ounces of white sugar, a slice or two of a lime, a little cinnamon, and sufficient claret and water, mixed half and half, to cover them
2. Stew gently till the fruit is quite tender
3. Lift the sauce-pan from the fire, drain off the liquor, stone the prunes
4. Pass the fruit through the sieve, and save the pulp in a basin
5. Crack the stones, and throw the prune kernels into the pulp
6. Steep about an ounce of gelatine in the liquor that you strained off, put it on the fire and let it dissolve
7. Mix it with the prune pulp, and pour in a liqueur glass of cherry brandy and a glass of good red wine
8. Decorate a plain border mould with blanched almonds, fill the mould with the prune liquid, and set it upon ice to form
9. When required, turn out the pain, and fill the centre of the mould with whipped cream

title: Prune Jelly
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of prunes
- two ounces of white sugar
- a slice or two of a lime
- a little cinnamon
- claret and water, mixed half and half

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of prunes into a sauce-pan with two ounces of white sugar, a slice or two of a lime, a little cinnamon, and sufficient claret and water, mixed half and half, to cover them
2. Stew gently till the fruit is quite tender
3. Lift the sauce-pan from the fire and drain off the liquor
4. Stone the prunes and pass the fruit through the sieve, saving the pulp in a basin
5. Crack the stones and throw the prune kernels into the pulp

title: Prunes a la Chasseur
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- glass jar of best French plums (prunes)
- bottle of good cherry brandy

## Directions

1. Buy a glass jar of the best French plums (prunes) and a bottle of good cherry brandy
2. Take out a few plums
3. Pour as much cherry brandy into the jar as the plums will admit
4. The next day you can add more, for the plums will absorb the brandy
5. Continue for a day or two
6. Finally cork it down for a fortnight or so
7. Serve at dessert
8. Never let the jar be empty, but re-fill it as the plums are eaten

title: Pudding a la Viennoise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve ounces of crumb of bread cut into small dice
- two glasses of Madeira
- a dessert spoonful of minced citron
- two ounces sweet almonds pounded
- half an ounce bitter almonds pounded
- six ounces of raisins
- six yolks of eggs
- one pint of cream
- two ounces of burnt sugar
- six ounces loaf-sugar
- candied peel

## Directions

1. Put the bread, almonds, raisins and citron into a basin, and pour the wine over them
2. Put the two ounces of sugar into a stew-pan to brown, then pour in the cream, stir it round, and add the yolks of eggs previously beaten
3. Thicken in the bain-marie, and strain the custard into the mixture in the basin
4. Butter a mould and ornament it with candied peel
5. Fill it with the mixture, and steam one and a half hours
6. Serve with caramel custard, or sauce Royale

title: Pudding of Loaf
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of milk
- 5 powa of a loaf of bread
- two eggs
- 1/2 powa of sugar
- jelly of any fruit
- powdered mace

## Directions

1. Heat 1/2 seer of milk and as soon as it boils add 5 powa of a loaf of bread broken into fragments, and take down.
2. When cold add the yolks of two eggs, 1/2 powa of sugar, some jelly of any fruit and a little powdered mace, and mix up.
3. Heat for about half an hour.

title: Pudding of Loaf (Second Variety)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- slices of a loaf of bread
- raisins
- milk
- sugar
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Smear the bottom and sides of a pan with some butter.
2. Arrange slices of a loaf of bread and raisins in alternate layers up to the brim.
3. Add milk minced with adequate quantities of sugar, the yolks of eggs and a pinch of salt.
4. Heat for about an hour.

title: Pudding of Middlings (Suji)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- 1 powa of middlings
- milk 1/2 seer
- sugar 1/2 powa
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat thoroughly the white portions of two eggs.
2. Mix thoroughly with 1 powa of middlings, milk 1/2 seer, sugar 1/2 powa, the yolks of the two eggs and a pinch of salt.
3. Heat on charcoal fire for about an hour.

title: Pudding of Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of rice
- water
- two eggs
- 1.5 powa of milk
- 1/2 powa of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 powa of rice in water till quite soft.
2. Beat thoroughly two eggs in a pot.
3. Add 1.5 powa of milk, 1/2 powa of sugar and the boiled rice, and stir thoroughly.
4. Heat on charcoal fire for about an hour.

title: Pudding Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- 2 wineglasses of sherry
- 1 tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix with half a pint of melted butter two wineglasses of sherry and 1 tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar
2. Make it quite hot, and serve in a sauce-tureen, with grated nutmeg on the top

title: Puff Paste
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- ½ ounce salt
- ¼ pint cold water
- 1 good ripe lime
- 1 pound clarified suet
- iced water

## Directions

1. Weigh a pound of flour and turn it out upon your cold marble slab
2. Make a hollow in its centre and fill it with half an ounce of salt and a quarter of a pint of the cold water
3. Mix the flour gradually with the water
4. When half mixed, sprinkle over it another quarter pint of water in which the lime has been squeezed
5. Mix it all thoroughly until it ceases to adhere to the slab and pat it into a round ball
6. Take one pound weight of the clarified suet, cut it up into dice
7. Flatten out the ball of paste to a thickness of about two inches, spreading the suet evenly over its surface
8. Fold the four sides of the paste to the centre enclosing the suet, and form a square piece
9. Roll this evenly out a yard long
10. Fold over one-third of the length towards the centre and fold the other third over it (one turn)
11. Let it rest for ten minutes in the ice-box or on a very cold slab
12. Give it two turns, rest ten minutes, then two turns more (five rolls out in all)
13. Gather the paste into a lump and roll it according to your requirements

title: Puff Paste with Baking Powder
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 breakfast-cupfuls flour
- 2 cupfuls butter
- 1 heaped teaspoonful baking powder
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- iced water
- broken ice

## Directions

1. Choose a cool place to work in
2. Ensure the flour is good and dry and the butter is firm and free from moisture
3. Fill two shallow baking-tins with broken ice
4. Put the flour on a cool slab and mix into it a heaped teaspoonful of the baking powder
5. Form the flour in a ring and in the centre throw the yolk of an egg and a teaspoonful of salt
6. Add a little iced water and gradually work the flour into it from the inside of the ring, sprinkling additional water as required (about one breakfast-cupful altogether) until you have a smooth, fine dough free from all stickiness
7. Pat this into a lump and put it in the ice-box for a quarter of an hour
8. Roll it out about the size of a dinner plate
9. Put the butter upon it and wrap the edges of the dough over it, carefully covering it
10. Turn it upside down and roll it out very thin
11. Reverse it again and fold it in three
12. Place it on a baking-sheet over one of the pans of broken ice and put the other pan of ice upon it
13. Repeat this cooling process between each double turn
14. Use as soon as possible when five turns have been completed

title: Puli Cakes of Moog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Husked moog pulses
- Ghee
- Flour
- Salt
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation

## Directions

1. Keep some husked pulses of moog under water for two hours
2. Fry them, if desired, with a little ghee for a few minutes
3. Make a paste of them
4. Add salt and a little flour, if necessary, to make the paste stiff
5. Prepare Puli cakes as before stuffing them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
6. Fry till reddish brown with ghee

title: Puli Pitha of Sweet Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet potatoes
- Salt
- Powdered rice
- Cocoa-nut
- Sugar
- Khowa kheer
- Water
- Ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Boil some sweet potatoes, peel and make a paste of them
2. Mix with salt and some powdered rice so that the paste is stiff enough to make balls of it
3. Press the balls flat
4. Stuff with a preparation of cocoa-nut and sugar as in Chandrapuli or with khowa kheer and sugar boiled in equal quantities with a little water till the paste is sticky and stiff
5. Give them a flat elongated shape when closing up
6. Fry 4 to 6 at a time with a little ghee or oil till deep brown

title: Pulled Bread
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound loaf
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a pound loaf
2. When the bread is all but done, take it from the oven
3. Tear the crumb from the inside with a fork in irregular pieces
4. Place them on a buttered baking tin
5. Crisp them in the oven

title: Pulwal Bhahjee
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen or more pulwals
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- salt
- melted mustard oil

## Directions

1. Scrape or pare away very finely the upper green coating
2. Divide them lengthways into two pieces
3. Clear away all the seeds
4. Wash and drain away all the water
5. Steep them in ground turmeric, chilies, and salt for half an hour or longer
6. Fry them quite crisp in melted mustard oil

title: Pulwal Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- two seers or four pounds of large full-grown pulwals
- half a seer of sugar
- a quarter of a seer or half a pound of green ginger

## Directions

1. Take two seers or four pounds of large full-grown pulwals without any decay
2. Peel, slit, remove the seeds, and throw them into cold water
3. Wash them thoroughly, and parboil them in clean water
4. Put them in a colander, and set them aside to cool
5. Prepare a good strong syrup of half a seer of sugar and a quarter of a seer or half a pound of green ginger well bruised
6. Throw the pulwals in, and allow them to simmer until the syrup thickens
7. Remove them immediately the colour becomes quite brown, but keep the syrup boiling till it has acquired the proper consistency
8. Return the pulwals into the syrup, and if necessary, reboil it two or three days after, if it appears to have become thin, or inclined to ferment

title: Pulwal Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- two seers or four pounds of good large fresh pulwals
- bruised green ginger
- half a seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Thoroughly grate the outer surface of the pulwals
2. Half slit them and remove the seeds
3. Throw them into water
4. Parboil them in clean water
5. Remove them into a colander and allow them to drain and dry
6. Stuff each pulwal with some bruised green ginger
7. Tie or bind them with fine cotton
8. Put them into a strong syrup made of half a seer of sugar
9. Allow them to simmer until they change colour
10. Remove them and continue to boil the syrup until it thickens
11. Return them into the syrup
12. In two or three days reboil the syrup if it has become thin or appears inclined to ferment

title: Pulwal, Potatoes, and Torrie Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- pulwal
- potatoes
- torrie
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean as much of the three kinds of vegetables as will overfill a vegetable-dish
2. Make the chahkee in all respects as the seam, potato, and peas chahkee recipe

title: Punch a la Romain
body:
## Ingredients

- eight juicy limes
- four lemons or oranges
- two pounds of the best loaf sugar
- whites of ten fresh eggs
- two seers of cutcha, or raw ice
- two quarts of champagne

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice out of eight juicy limes and four lemons or oranges
2. strain it through muslin
3. well mix with it two pounds of the best loaf sugar
4. beat to a light froth the whites of ten fresh eggs
5. add gradually to the sugared juice
6. pour the whole into a pewter vessel
7. place it in a tub containing two seers of cutcha, or raw ice, stirring it frequently to make it congeal
8. Ice two quarts of champagne
9. when required add it to the contents of the pewter vessel
10. mix all well together
11. serve in green or amber-coloured hock glasses

title: Puree de Rognons
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- daily allowance of soup meat
- six mutton kidneys
- salt
- dried thyme
- marjoram
- spice

## Directions

1. Make a strong consomme with your daily allowance of soup meat
2. Wash the six mutton kidneys and dry them
3. Slit them in halves
4. Plunge them immediately into boiling water well salted and let them remain in this bath for one minute only
5. Take them out and dry them in a napkin
6. Boil them gently with some dried thyme, marjoram, and a bag containing spice, etc., till they are very tender
7. Take them out

title: Puree de Tomates
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- soup meat
- six or eight good sized tomatoes
- a tea-spoonful of dried basil
- an onion
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- two cloves of garlic
- a table-spoonful of butter
- an ounce of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- croutons of fried bread

## Directions

1. Soup meat as usual for two
2. Make your stock from the soup meat
3. Choose an enamel-lined sauce-pan and throw in the tomatoes cut into quarters, dried basil, shredded onion, salt, garlic cloves, and a table-spoonful of butter
4. Cook the pieces of tomato until quite soft, stirring to prevent catching
5. Turn them out upon a sieve, pick out the garlic, and work the tomatoes through the sieve, saving the pulp in a bowl
6. When dinner time is at hand, melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan
7. Work a dessert-spoonful of flour into it till smooth
8. Add a little stock and a little puree by degrees till exhausted
9. Let the soup come to the boil to thicken properly
10. Serve with croutons of fried bread

title: Puri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- ghee 1 chhatak
- flour 1 seer
- water
- besam of grams
- ginger-juice
- powders of pepper
- cumin
- garam-masalla
- salt
- minced meat

## Directions

1. Mix ghee with flour at the rate of one chhatak to one seer, add water and knead.
2. Mix a little ghee with some besam of grams and fry.
3. Mix together this fried besam, ginger-juice, powders of pepper, cumin, garam-masalla, salt and minced meat and fry with ghee.
4. Form medium balls of the kneaded flour, flatten, stuff with a little meat and make balls again.
5. Press these balls into flat and round discs of moderate thickness and roast them with a little ghee or heat them 2 or 3 at a time in a pan, roast on fire without ghee, and then smear with ghee to make soft.

title: Queen Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of fresh butter
- six ounces of pounded and sifted loaf sugar
- half a pound of dried and sifted flour
- half a pound of cleaned and dried currants
- four well-beaten eggs
- a little grated nutmeg
- pounded cinnamon
- a few pounded bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare eight ounces of fresh butter beaten to a cream
2. Add six ounces of pounded and sifted loaf sugar to the butter
3. Put in the eggs by degrees
4. Add the flour and the other ingredients
5. Beat all well together for half an hour
6. Put it into small buttered tins, nearly filling them
7. Strew over the top finely-powdered loaf sugar
8. Bake them in a pretty brisk oven

title: Queen Mab Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint and a half of milk
- a few drops of lemon essence
- sugar
- six yolks
- an ounce of gelatine
- a little milk
- two ounces of preserved cherries or finely chopped apricots
- one ounce of citron peel or preserved ginger
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil a pint and a half of milk with a few drops of lemon essence and sugar to taste
2. Strain, and let it get cold
3. Beat up six yolks, and pour the flavoured milk upon them
4. Put this into the bain-marie, and stir gently over the fire until it thickens
5. Whip it well
6. Dissolve an ounce of gelatine in a little milk, add it to the above
7. Stir the custard until nearly cold
8. Pour the mixture by degrees into a buttered mould, adding two ounces of preserved cherries or finely chopped apricots and one ounce of citron peel or preserved ginger cut very small in layers, setting them carefully
9. When the mould is quite set, turn it out

title: Quenelles de Volaille au Macedoine de Legumes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- pullet
- butter
- flour
- water
- salt
- eggs
- spiced pepper
- truffle
- carrot
- peas
- turnip
- French beans
- cucumber

## Directions

1. Choose a nice pullet not quite full grown and cut it up, saving all the meat from the breast and wings and the liver
2. Cut into dice and throw the skin, fragments, bones, and giblets into a sauce-pan to make broth for the sauce
3. Make a coffee-cupful of stiff paste with a little butter, flour, water, and a pinch of salt
4. Take half the quantity of butter that you have of chicken meat and half the amount of the paste that you have of butter
5. Mince the chicken in the machine and pound it in a mortar with the paste and butter
6. Bind the mixture with three eggs and flavour it with a little spiced pepper
7. Form the quenelles between two table-spoons
8. Put a nice piece of truffle inside each quenelle
9. For the sauce: take equal portions of carrot, peas, turnip, French beans, and cucumber, all previously boiled
10. Cut them into dice and heat them up in a white sauce made with the water in which they were boiled

title: Quockma Curry
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender mutton
- green ginger
- salt
- butter
- white onions

## Directions

1. Cut up about a pound of very tender mutton without any bone, and stir the pieces about in a big bowl with a dessert-spoonful of pounded green ginger, and a sprinkling of salt.
2. Melt a quarter of a pound of butter in a stewpan, and throw into it a couple of white onions cut into rings.

title: Quookma
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- about a pound of very tender mutton without any bone
- a dessert-spoonful of pounded green ginger
- salt
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a couple of white onions
- a couple of cloves of garlic
- a tea-spoonful of pounded coriander-seed
- one tea-spoonful of pounded black pepper
- half a tea-spoonful of pounded cardomoms
- half a tea-spoonful of pounded cloves
- four ounces of well-pounded almonds
- a breakfast-cupful of cream
- a dessert-spoonful of turmeric powder
- a tea-spoonful of sugar
- the juice of a couple of limes

## Directions

1. Cut up about a pound of very tender mutton without any bone, and stir the pieces about in a big bowl with a dessert-spoonful of pounded green ginger, and a sprinkling of salt.
2. Melt a quarter of a pound of butter in a stew-pan, and throw into it a couple of white onions cut into rings, and a couple of cloves of garlic finely minced.
3. Fry for about five minutes, and then add a tea-spoonful of pounded coriander-seed, one of pounded black pepper, half one of pounded cardomoms, and half one of pounded cloves.
4. Cook this for five minutes, then put in the meat, and stir over a moderate fire until the pieces seem tender, and have browned nicely.
5. Take the pan from the fire, and work into it a strong infusion obtained from four ounces of well-pounded almonds, and a breakfast-cupful of cream.
6. Mix thoroughly, adding a dessert-spoonful of turmeric powder, and a tea-spoonful of sugar.
7. Put the pan over a very low fire, and let the curry simmer as gently as possible for a quarter of an hour, finishing off with the juice of a couple of limes.

title: Quorema Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- two chittacks and a half or five ounces of ghee
- one cup or eight ounces of good thick tyre
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of coriander-seed
- six small sticks of ground cinnamon
- two or three blades of lemon-grass
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- a half teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- eight or ten peppercorns
- four or five ground cloves
- five or six ground cardamoms
- two or three bay-leaves
- a quarter of a cup of water
- the juice of one lemon
- twelve large onions cut lengthways into fine slices
- two pounds of good fat mutton

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton up into pieces nearly one inch and a half square.
2. Warm the ghee, fry in it the sliced onions, and set aside.
3. Fry all the ground condiments, including the ground hot spices.
4. When quite brown, throw in the mutton and salt, and allow the whole to brown.
5. Add the tyre, the hot spices with peppercorns and bay-leaves, the lemon-grass, the water, and the fried onions finely chopped.
6. Close the pot, and allow it to simmer over a gentle coal fire for about an hour and a half or two hours.
7. The blades of lemon-grass are never dished up.

title: Rabbit Fillets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a full grown rabbit
- bacon
- butter
- sweet salad oil
- bread-crumbs
- grated mild cheese
- spiced pepper
- salt
- egg
- savouiry rice or maccaroni an parmesam
- puree of vegetable or petit pois
- Sauce Milanaise

## Directions

1. Detach from either side of the back bone the deep strips of meat which run from the shoulder to the end of the loin
2. Cut out each strip whole, and remove the small nerves or sinews of each
3. Cut each strip into three equal portions, cross-wise
4. Flatten them with a bat
5. Trim them neatly
6. Lard them with bacon
7. Lay them upon a buttered tin in the oven sufficiently long to stiffen them
8. Take them out, place them under a weight, and when quite cold and firm, dip them into some melted butter or sweet salad oil
9. Roll them in a mixture of bread-crumbs and grated mild cheese, in equal proportions
10. Dust over them some spiced pepper, and a little salt and let them rest to set thoroughly
11. Dip them carefully in a soup plate containing an egg beaten up
12. Roll them in very fine bread-crumbs
13. When wanted, fry them in abundance of fat
14. Serve a pale golden yellow, round a circle of savoury rice, or maccaroni an parmesam, with the centre of the circle containing any nice puree of vegetable, or petit pois
15. Accompany with Sauce Milanaise

title: Rabbit Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one good sized rabbit
- half a pound of bacon
- forcemeat
- pound of gravy beef
- spiced pepper
- rabbit liver and kidneys

## Directions

1. Skin and wash a fine rabbit
2. Cut it up in the usual way as if for a stew, and put the pieces to soak in cold water
3. When quite clean, drain them, wipe them dry with a clean cloth, and set them aside
4. Put the head, the lower joints of the legs, and all scraps of the rabbit, with the beef, and the usual ingredients for flavouring a gravy into a large sauce-pan, and make the best gravy you can with them for the pie
5. When this has been done to your satisfaction, and the fat has been skimmed off the surface of the liquor, make a plain forcemeat as described for turkey, mingling with it the liver and kidneys of the rabbit very finely chopped
6. Spread a thin layer of forcemeat at the bottom of the pie-dish
7. Immediately above it put a layer of bacon slices
8. Then put the coarser joints of the rabbit, dusting them with spiced pepper, and filling the interstices between the pieces with forcemeat
9. Put a second layer of bacon over the rabbit, and rabbit again above that
10. Repeat the forcemeat dressing
11. When the pie-dish is full, pour in the gravy till it almost reaches the level of the topmost layer
12. Garnish as usual, cover the pie with paste, and bake for about one hour and a quarter

title: Rabri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 table-spoonful of lime water
- 5 seers of milk
- powdered sugar-candy 1 powa
- powdered cardamoms 1 tola
- 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 table-spoonful of lime water with 5 seers of milk and boil it
2. Make the heat very mild so that the milk may no longer boil
3. Fan the surface of the milk and remove the creams constantly as they are formed to the sides of the pan with a very thin stick
4. Stir the milk from time to time and continue the process till the milk is reduced to one seer
5. Take the pan down
6. Scrape the creams together from the sides of the pan and mix with the milk
7. Mix powdered sugar-candy 1 powa, powdered cardamoms 1 tola and 4 or 5 drops of essence of rose with the milk
8. Serve when cold

title: Radhaballavi Luchi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pulses of kalai
- aniseed
- cumin
- turmeric
- salt
- ghee
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Fry some moistened pulses of kalai with powders of aniseed, cumin, turmeric and salt with ghee or oil
2. Make a stiff paste of them
3. Prepare flour dough as in the preparation of Luchi
4. Make big balls of it and flatten them a little
5. Put some spiced pulse in each and close up again in the form of a ball
6. Roll them into big discs of moderate thickness
7. Fry in plenty of ghee

title: Ragout of Pomfret
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled pomfret
- pepper
- salt
- Bombay onion
- carrot
- butter
- milk
- water
- fish bones
- trimmings
- pepper corns
- anchovy
- blade of mace
- red chilli

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from a cold boiled pomfret and cut the fish into fillets of a nice length
2. Dust them with pepper and salt, and put them aside
3. Slice finely half a Bombay onion and a small carrot
4. Fry the slices till just colouring in an ounce of butter
5. Add a pint of milk and water (two-thirds milk to one-third water), all the fish bones and trimmings, a few pepper corns, an anchovy, a blade of mace, and a pinch of salt
6. Boil this up and simmer it afterwards till you have extracted the flavour of your ingredients
7. Strain the liquor and thicken it as for a sauce blanche
8. Gently heat your fillets of fish in the same sauce
9. Garnish with little bits of red chilli (cut into dice and sprinkled over the fillets) and serve

title: Raj-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer hard chhana
- 1/2 seer khowa kheer
- 1/2 powa middlings
- 1/4 chhatak almonds
- powdered aniseeds 1/4 tola
- powdered cardamom seeds
- milk
- sugar-candy 1/2 powa
- raisins one chhatak
- essence of rose 2 or 3 drops
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana and mix thoroughly with khowa kheer 1/2 seer, middlings 1/2 powa, a paste of 1/4 chhatak of almonds, powdered aniseeds 1/4 tola and a little powdered cardamom seeds
2. Make a paste soft but stiff enough to be made into balls with a little milk if necessary
3. Make big balls (10 or 12 in number)
4. Stuff them with a mixture of broken pieces of sugar-candy 1/2 powa, raisins one chhatak and 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose
5. Drop them into a boiling syrup of sugar
6. Take the pan off fire when they are well boiled and sink down
7. Serve a few hours later

title: Raj-Bhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard chhana 1/2 seer
- khowa kheer 1/2 seer
- middlings 1/2 powa
- paste of almonds 1 chhatak
- powdered aniseeds 1/4 tola
- a little powdered cardamom seeds
- milk if necessary
- broken pieces of sugar-candy 1/2 powa
- raisins 1 chhatak
- 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana
2. Mix thoroughly with khowa kheer, middlings, a paste of almonds, powdered aniseeds and a little powdered cardamom seeds
3. Make a paste soft but stiff enough to be made into balls with a little milk if necessary
4. Make big balls (10 or 12 in number)
5. Stuff them with a mixture of broken pieces of sugar-candy, raisins and 2 or 3 drops of essence of rose
6. Drop them into a boiling syrup of sugar
7. Remove from heat when they sink down after boiling
8. Serve a few hours later

title: Ramequins en Caisses
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of mild grated cheese
- two ounces of white bread-crumbs
- milk
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- one tea-spoonful of mustard powder
- yolks of three eggs
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Soak the crumbs in milk
2. Pound them in a mortar with the cheese and a little butter till the whole is well mixed
3. Season the mixture with pepper and salt, adding a tea-spoonful of mustard powder, and the yolks of three eggs
4. Beat up the egg whites to a stiff froth
5. Mingle the egg whites with the mixture
6. Fill your paper cases, which should be well buttered to prevent their burning outside
7. Bake them from ten minutes to a quarter of an hour
8. Serve them as soon as they have raised their heads and have slightly taken colour

title: Ras-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- raisins
- cardamom seeds
- aniseeds
- bread crumbs
- contents of a few eggs
- ghee
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Mix almonds, raisins, cardamom seeds, aniseeds and bread crumbs, all powdered or pasted, with the contents of a few eggs and beat thoroughly.
2. Form balls, fry in plenty of ghee and drop them into a syrup of sugar.
3. Keep them there for half an hour or so till the syrup reaches its centre.
4. Then serve.

title: Ras-Bara of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- flesh portions of shrimps
- flour
- turmeric
- lemon-juice
- salt
- mustard oil
- ghee
- syrup of sugar

## Directions
