1. Make a paste of only the flesh portions of shrimps and add some flour, a little turmeric, lemon-juice, salt and mustard oil.
2. Beat thoroughly.
3. Make small balls of it and fry in plenty of ghee taking care that they do not get scorched.
4. Drop the fried balls in a syrup of sugar.
5. Serve when they are saturated with syrup.

title: Rasa
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- turmeric
- salt
- oil
- fishes
- coriander
- chillies
- water
- fried bori

## Directions

1. Peel some potatoes and cut them in big pieces.
2. Smear with turmeric and salt, and brown in oil.
3. Prepare fishes and fry in sufficient oil.
4. Add turmeric, coriander and a good number of chillies, all pasted, and water.
5. On boiling add salt and the potatoes.
6. Fried bori may be added if desired.
7. Take down when the soup is quite thick.

title: Rasa Puli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Boiling water
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of powdered rice with boiling water, adding the necessary amount of water at a time
2. Make medium balls
3. Stuff them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
4. Give them the shape of an almond
5. Fry in ghee
6. Immerse in a syrup of sugar till they are saturated

title: Rasa-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 seer of pulses of Kalai
- 1 powa of hard chhana
- entire cubebs
- seeds of big cardamoms
- ghee or oil
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Make fine pastes of 0.5 seer of pulses of Kalai, moistened for a few hours, and 1 powa of hard chhana separately
2. Then mix them together adding a few entire cubebs and seeds of a few big cardamoms
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Fry this paste in medium balls till deep brown in ghee or oil
5. Keep them in a syrup of sugar till saturated

title: Rasa-Golla
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- hard chhana 1 seer
- seeds of a few cardamoms
- 4 drops of essence of rose
- sabeda 1 powa (optional)
- sugar candy
- kasir chini for syrup or white sugar

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1 seer of hard chhana
2. Mix with the seeds of a few cardamoms and 4 drops of essence of rose
3. Optionally mix 1 powa of sabeda
4. Make balls of it
5. Stuff each with a piece of sugar candy to make them hollow
6. Drop into a boiling syrup of kasir chini
7. Boil till they are brownish in colour
8. If using white sugar syrup boil till stiff
9. Keep them in the syrup till they are served
10. Eat them up within 3 or 4 days

title: Rasa-Madhuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 seer of hard chhana
- 1 seer of sabeda
- water
- sugar syrup
- white sugar
- sugar-candy

## Directions

1. Make a fine paste of 1.5 seer of hard chhana
2. Mix with 1 seer of sabeda and a little water making a dough
3. Make small balls of it
4. Boil in a syrup of sugar
5. Take them out and roll on white sugar
6. When cold roll again on crystals of sugar-candy after dipping them for a moment, if necessary, into a cold syrup of sugar

title: Raspberry Iced Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar
- Two tablespoonfuls of raspberry jelly or jam
- A little cochineal
- Juice of a large lemon
- Ice broken small
- Three or four handfuls of coarse salt

## Directions

1. Mix a tablespoonful of pounded loaf sugar, two tablespoonfuls of raspberry jelly or jam, and a little cochineal to heighten the colour, with the juice of a large lemon
2. Strain, and put into the freezing-pot
3. Cover it closely and place it in a bucket which has a small hole near the bottom, and a spigot to let the water run off, with plenty of ice broken small, and mixed with three or four handfuls of coarse salt
4. Press the ice closely round the freezing-pot, turn it round and round for about ten minutes
5. Take off the cover, and remove with a spoon the frozen cream to the centre
6. Cover it again, and turn it till all be equally iced
7. Serve it in china ice-pails in block, or put it into moulds, cover them securely, and replace them in the bucket, with ice and salt as before, for an hour or more
8. Dip the moulds into cold water before turning out, and serve immediately

title: Ravigote Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- mayonnaise sauce
- chopped shallots
- anchovies
- celery
- cress
- sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Make a mayonnaise sauce
2. Add chopped shallots, anchovies, celery, cress, and sweet herbs

title: Ravigote Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of melted butter
- an ounce of minced onion
- an ounce of chopped carrot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- one of garden cress
- one clove of garlic
- claret-glass of vinegar
- claret-glass of water or broth
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- one of sugar
- flour
- a very little white wine
- minced shallots
- lime-juice

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of melted butter an ounce of minced onion with an ounce of chopped carrot, a dessert-spoonful of parsley, one of garden cress, and one clove of garlic
2. When of a golden hue, add equal parts (claret-glass each) of vinegar, and water or broth, a tea-spoonful of salt, and one of sugar
3. thicken with a little flour, and add a very little white wine, and minced shallots with some lime-juice instead of vinegar

title: Red Cabbage Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- boiling vinegar
- red beetroot

## Directions

1. Slice the cabbage, and sprinkle salt over each layer
2. After twenty-four hours remove it into a colander, and allow all the salt water to drain
3. Put the cabbage into a pan, pour in sufficient boiling vinegar to cover it, and add a few slices of red beetroot
4. When cold, put it into glass bottles and cork down

title: Red Cauliflower Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- vinegar
- sugar
- a quarter of a pound of sugar to every quart of vinegar
- sticks of cinnamon
- sliced or bruised and pounded red beet

## Directions

1. Cut the cauliflower into pieces of equal sizes
2. Sprinkle with salt, and place it in the sun for a couple of days
3. Make a syrup of vinegar and sugar: to every quart of vinegar put a quarter of a pound of sugar, a few sticks of cinnamon, and as much sliced or bruised and pounded red beet as will give the vinegar a deep red colour
4. When all the salt water has drained away, put the cauliflower into a pan, and pour over it the boiling-hot vinegar or syrup through a fine sieve
5. When cold, put the pickle into nice white bottles and cork

title: Red Herring Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- white Patna onion
- fresh green chilies
- fresh lime juice
- red herring

## Directions

1. Take onions, chilies, and lime-juice
2. Place the herring with its original paper packing on a gridiron or on a frying-pan and warm it well
3. Clear it of all skin and very carefully pick out all the bones
4. Bruise the herring and mix it thoroughly with the sliced onions, chilies, and lime-juice

title: Red Pumpkin and Tamarind Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- red pumpkin, a quarter
- tamarinds, two or three
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the pulp of the tamarind in the water
2. Fry the pumpkin with the ground condiments and oil
3. Put the tamarind water into the curry after the pumpkin has been fried
4. Simmer until the pumpkin is quite tender

title: Red Saug and Omra
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half chittack of mustard oil
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one teaspoonful and a half of ground salt
- one cupful of water
- omra
- saug

## Directions

1. Peel the omra and half fry if large
2. Thoroughly clean and wash the greens
3. Put them into a colander and allow all the water to drain away
4. Warm the oil and fry the ground condiments
5. Fry the saug and omra until crisp
6. Add the water and cook over a slow fire until the greens and omra are tender

title: Reduced Fritter Batter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ordinary eggs
- one table-spoonful of brandy
- a dessert-spoonful of salad oil
- two or three table-spoonfuls of water
- one and a half table-spoonful of flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat up two ordinary eggs with one table-spoonful of brandy, a dessert-spoonful of salad oil, and two or three table-spoonfuls of water.
2. Incorporate with this mixture one and a half table-spoonful of flour.
3. Work to a smooth paste and beat for at least ten minutes.
4. Add water if needed until consistency is satisfactory.
5. Whip the egg whites to a stiff froth and stir in at the time of using.
6. For sweet fritters, use sugar instead of salt.

title: Reform Cutlets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten neck chops
- marinade
- ham
- bread crumbs
- mashed potato
- puree de pois verts
- Bellary onion
- curled parsley
- pounded lean ham or bacon
- garlic
- carrot
- thyme leaves
- Espagnole sauce
- anchovy vinegar
- chilli vinegar
- red currant jelly
- mushroom ketchup
- Madeira
- hard-boiled eggs
- black Leicestershire mushrooms
- gherkins
- lean ham
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Set ten nice neck chops, very neatly trimmed, in marinade all day.
2. Towards evening lift them out, wipe them dry, and proceed to bread-crumb them as laid down at page 67.
3. Mix very finely minced ham with the crumbs at a proportion of half and half.
4. When the time arrives, the cutlets must be very delicately fried in plenty of boiling fat, and served as soon as they reach that bright golden tint.
5. Place the cutlets round a ring of mashed potato, with puree de pois verts in the centre.
6. For the sauce: Put into a sauce-pan, one Bellary onion cut into rings, two sprigs of curled parsley, two ounces of pounded lean ham or bacon, a clove of garlic, a carrot cut up, and a tea-spoonful of thyme leaves.
7. Cover them with a pint of Espagnole sauce and add two tea-spoonfuls of anchovy vinegar, and one of chilli vinegar.
8. Boil up, simmer for ten minutes, skimming carefully, then boil again reducing the sauce to the thickness of cream.
9. Add a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly and one of good mushroom ketchup.
10. Stir till the jelly is dissolved, adding half a glass of Madeira.
11. Pass the sauce through the strainer.
12. Keep it hot in the bain-marie, and add just before serving, the following mixture: the whites of two hard-boiled eggs, four black Leicestershire mushrooms, two gherkins, and half an ounce of lean ham, all chopped up into small dice, and dusted over with white pepper.
13. Send the sauce round in a boat separately.

title: Regent Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of sparkling champagne
- a claret glassful of brandy
- a wineglassful of old Jamaica rum
- a pint of very strong pure green tea
- capillaire or any other syrup

## Directions

1. Mix a quart of sparkling champagne, a claret glassful of brandy, a wineglassful of old Jamaica rum, and a pint of very strong pure green tea
2. sweeten to taste with capillaire or any other syrup

title: Remedy for Scurf in the Head
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh quicklime, the size of a walnut
- water, one pint
- vinegar, a quarter of a pint

## Directions

1. Drop a lump of fresh quicklime the size of a walnut into a pint of water
2. Let it stand all night
3. Pour the water off clear from sediment
4. Add a quarter of a pint of the best vinegar
5. Wash the head with the mixture, wetting only the roots of the hair

title: Remoulade Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- mayonnaise sauce
- chopped gherkins
- parsley
- chives
- capers
- French mustard
- garlic-vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a mayonnaise sauce
2. Add chopped gherkins, parsley, chives and capers
3. Use French mustard
4. Add a drop or two of garlic-vinegar

title: Remoulade Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of melted butter
- an ounce of minced onion
- an ounce of chopped carrot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- one of garden cress
- one clove of garlic
- a table-spoonful of salad oil
- a dessert-spoonful of French mustard

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of melted butter an ounce of minced onion with an ounce of chopped carrot, a dessert-spoonful of parsley, one of garden cress, and one clove of garlic
2. incorporate with your frying onions and green herbs, a table-spoonful of salad oil
3. add a dessert-spoonful of French mustard to finish with

title: Ribs of Beef with Chateaubriand Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good joint of the ribs of beef
- fat bacon for larding
- buttered paper
- string
- half a tumbler of chablis or sauterne
- half a pint of Espagnole sauce
- two ounces of maitre d'hotel butter
- potatoes
- French beans
- salad

## Directions

1. Trim a good joint of the ribs of beef by cutting the tender meat boldly in one long piece from the bones; the tough flap can either go into the stock pot, or be set with the bones to produce a good gravy for the joint, the latter for choice.
2. Fold and tie the long piece of meat in shape as best you can and preserve all the fat you can find which should be fixed with skewers.
3. Lard it with fat bacon, wrap it in buttered paper and tie it to your spit with string.
4. Let it be roasted over a clear fire, and served in thin slices.
5. For the Chateaubriand sauce: Add half a tumbler of chablis or sauterne to half a pint of Espagnole sauce, boil them together and reduce a little, then strain.
6. Boil again, adding off the fire two ounces of maitre d'hotel butter, let it thicken and serve it in a boat as hot as possible.
7. Serve with potatoes, French beans, and a nice salad.

title: Rice Blanc Mange
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted ground rice-flour
- cold milk
- a quart of boiling milk
- one-eighth of an ounce of isinglass
- a stick of cinnamon
- the peel of half a small lemon
- sugar to taste
- a tablespoonful of pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Mix to a stiff smooth paste four tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted ground rice-flour with a little cold milk
2. Stir it into a quart of boiling milk in which had been dissolved one-eighth of an ounce of isinglass, a stick of cinnamon, and the peel of half a small lemon
3. Sweeten to taste
4. Boil from ten to fifteen minutes, stirring carefully all the while
5. Remove from the fire and mix into it briskly a tablespoonful of pounded almonds
6. Pour it while scalding hot into moulds previously dipped in cold water

title: Rice Kheer
body:
## Ingredients

- one coonkee or half a pound of bassmuttee or cheeneesuckur rice
- two cups of pure cow's milk
- two or three small sticks of cinnamon
- one tablespoonful and a half to two tablespoonfuls of fine-quality white sugar

## Directions

1. Thoroughly boil one coonkee or half a pound of the bassmuttee or the cheeneesuckur rice
2. Drain the water away
3. Add two cups of pure cow's milk, and put over a slow fire
4. As the rice begins to absorb the milk, add two or three small sticks of cinnamon, with one tablespoonful and a half to two tablespoonfuls of fine-quality white sugar
5. On the milk being entirely absorbed, the kheer is either turned out upon a dish and eaten hot, or put into a buttered mould, served up in shape, and eaten cold

title: Rich Gingerbread Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of dried and sifted flour
- half a pound of pounded loaf sugar
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter washed in rose-water
- one pound of treacle
- one nutmeg grated
- the weight of a nutmeg of pounded mace
- as much of pounded cinnamon
- one ounce of pounded ginger
- one and a half of candied orange and lemon peel cut small
- half an ounce of blanched sweet almonds cut into long thin bits
- two well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Melt the butter with the treacle, and when nearly cold stir in the eggs and the rest of the ingredients
2. Mix all well together, make it into round cakes, and bake them upon tins

title: Rich Moringa Root Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- moringa root shavings
- chicken broth
- yolks of eggs
- a coffee-cupful of cream
- tarragon vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape and simmer the moringa root shavings in chicken broth
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs with a dessert-spoonful of tarragon vinegar
3. Add a coffee-cupful of cream with the yolks of the eggs
4. Let the sauce remain on the fire en bain-marie, stirring well until it is very hot but not boiling
5. Serve it in a hot sauce-boat

title: Rissoles or Croquets
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast meat or fowl
- a small bit of bacon
- grated nutmeg
- salt
- cream
- yolks of eggs
- finely-grated bread
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Mince very finely some cold roast meat or fowl and a small bit of bacon
2. season it with grated nutmeg and salt
3. moisten it with cream, and make it up into good-sized balls
4. dip them into yolks of eggs beaten up, and then into finely-grated bread
5. Bake them in an oven, or fry of a light brown colour
6. Before serving, drain them before a fire on the back of a sieve
7. Garnish with fried parsley

title: Riz a l'Indienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- well boiled rice
- good butter
- onion
- garlic
- table-spoonful of well made curry powder
- prawns
- butter
- pinch of saffron

## Directions

1. Commence as laid down for riz a l'Italienne using plenty of good butter.
2. Omit the tomato pulp, and instead of the grated cheese, stir in a table-spoonful of well made curry powder.
3. Garnish with prawns that have been tossed in butter with a pinch of saffron.

title: Riz a l'Italienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- ounce of butter
- garlic
- onion the size of a racquet ball
- two breakfast-cupfuls of well boiled rice
- couple of heaped up table-spoonfuls of grated Parmesan or Gruyere
- strips of anchovies

## Directions

1. Melt an ounce of butter at the bottom of a sauce-pan which ought to have been previously well rubbed with a piece of garlic.
2. Shred an onion the size of a racquet ball very finely and fry it in the butter.
3. Stir into this, when of a golden yellow colour, two breakfast-cupfuls of well boiled rice.
4. Work it vigorously with a wooden spoon while an assistant shakes into the pan a couple of heaped up table-spoonfuls of grated Parmesan or Gruyere.
5. Garnish the dish with strips of anchovies, and serve it piled upon a flat dish.

title: Riz a la Bonne Femme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- well boiled rice
- butter
- onion
- garlic
- grated Parmesan or Gruyere
- finely rasped ham or corned beef
- crisply fried bacon

## Directions

1. Proceed as for riz a l'Italienne, but stir into the mixture some finely rasped ham, or corned beef.
2. Garnish with curls of crisply fried bacon.

title: Riz a la Menagere
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of rice
- a quarter of a pound of streaky bacon
- a pint and a half of broth
- a salt-spoonful of pepper
- half a pint of tomato puree or sauce
- sausages
- fried bacon
- croquettes of fish
- savoury mixture

## Directions

1. Wash and blanch six ounces of rice in boiling water for five minutes, using a roomy stew-pan
2. Cool and drain it on a sieve
3. Weigh a quarter of a pound of the best streaky bacon, dip it into scalding water for a couple of minutes, and then cut it into inch dice
4. Fry these in a stew-pan till they turn yellow
5. Add the rice, and a pint and a half of broth, with a salt-spoonful of pepper
6. Simmer for twenty minutes, stirring the rice every now and then to prevent its catching at the bottom of the pan
7. Take it off the fire, and add half a pint of tomato puree or sauce
8. Mix thoroughly, and put the rice on a dish
9. Garnish with sausages, curls of fried bacon, croquettes of fish, or any savoury mixture you like, worked into small shapes, and fried a golden yellow

title: Riz a la Milanaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- butter
- shred onion
- stock
- saffron
- flour
- grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Parboil the rice for five minutes.
2. Fry it in butter with a shred onion until it begins to take colour.
3. Add stock without tomato, but coloured with saffron.
4. When the rice is cooked, the gravy may be thickened with flour.
5. Serve hot with grated Parmesan.
6. Add the stock by degrees, allowing the rice to drink it up as it were.
7. The grains gradually distend, and when ready to burst are fit to serve.

title: Riz a la Napolitaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- well boiled rice
- butter
- tomato pulp
- finely grated Parmesan, Gruyere, or other mild dry cheese

## Directions

1. Empty the well boiled rice into a hot sauce-pan with plenty of butter.
2. Stir till thoroughly hot and well lubricated, add tomato pulp enough to moisten the whole nicely, and finish with finely grated Parmesan, Gruyere, or other mild dry cheese.
3. Serve piping hot.

title: Riz a la Turque
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six coffee-cupfuls of broth or consomme
- tomato pulp
- salt
- black pepper
- three coffee-cupfuls of well cleansed raw rice
- ounce of fresh butter
- nutmeg
- raisins

## Directions

1. Put into a sauce-pan six coffee-cupfuls of broth or consomme into which sufficient tomato pulp has been stirred to slightly thicken it, flavour this with salt and black pepper to taste.
2. Set the sauce-pan on the fire.
3. As soon as the liquid boils, cast into it three coffee-cupfuls of well cleansed raw rice.
4. Reduce the heat, and let the rice stew gently in the tomato-flavoured gravy.
5. Watch it narrowly, and as soon as it has sucked up the whole of it, stir into it an ounce of fresh butter.
6. Just before serving, a dust of nutmeg should be added, and some raisins carefully picked and cleaned may be thrown in as a garnish.

title: Riz au Chou
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- heart of a tender cabbage
- couple of ounces of butter
- Bombay onion
- two cloves of garlic
- broth or consomme
- grated cheese
- bacon

## Directions

1. Boil some rice and keep it ready in a dish.
2. Cut up the heart of a tender cabbage.
3. Melt a couple of ounces of butter in a roomy stew-pan, cast into it a finely sliced Bombay onion, and two cloves of garlic minced as small as possible.
4. Let the onion turn yellow, and then put in the shred cabbage.
5. Stir it about for three minutes with the butter and onions, and then pour over it enough broth or consomme to keep it from burning.
6. Stew gently until the cabbage is cooked, then add the rice which should be vigorously stirred about for five minutes with the cabbage.
7. Turn it out upon a flat dish, and smother it with grated cheese.

title: Roast Florican
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- florican
- Bombay onion
- fat boiled bacon
- bacon slice
- melted butter
- plain gravy
- fried crumbs
- bread sauce

## Directions

1. Pick, draw, singe, and truss the florican placing a Bombay onion, rolled in fat boiled bacon, inside its carcass
2. Tie a thin slice of bacon over the breast
3. Roast the bird over a moderate fire, basting it frequently with melted butter
4. A few minutes before the florican is done, remove the slice of bacon, so as to let the breast take colour
5. Serve with plain gravy, fried crumbs, and bread sauce
6. Time, about thirty minutes

title: Roast Goose with Brussels Sprouts
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a goose
- apple or tomato sauce
- potatoes
- Brussels-sprouts
- salt
- Nepaul pepper
- mixed mustard
- a glass of portwine

## Directions

1. Pick, draw, singe, truss, and stuff the goose
2. Roast it before a clear fire
3. When done, cut a slice into the breast and let the gravy run out for a moment
4. Add to the gravy a tea-spoonful of salt, a salt-spoonful of Nepaul pepper, a tea-spoonful of mixed mustard, and a glass of portwine
5. Stir these ingredients into the gravy
6. Baste the bird well with it and continue with the carving
7. Serve with either apple or tomato sauce in a boat
8. Serve with potatoes and Brussels-sprouts if in season

title: Roast Guinea-fowl with Water-cress
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- Guinea-fowl
- thin slices of bacon
- water-cresses
- bread sauce
- fried bread crumbs
- brown gravy
- liver
- half a glass of red wine
- 1 tea-spoonful of red-currant jelly
- juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Pluck, singe, draw and truss the bird for roasting
2. Do not allow the cook to put the liver and gizzard under the wings
3. Tie a few thin slices of bacon over the breast
4. Put it to roast over a very clear fire
5. Baste frequently
6. When done, put it on a dish garnished with water-cresses
7. Send round the best bread sauce, fried bread crumbs, and a rich brown gravy containing the liver pounded, half a glass of red wine, a tea-spoonful of red-currant jelly, and the juice of a lime
8. Do not stuff a Guinea-fowl like a capon
9. Serve with pommes de terre à la maître d'hôtel, and haricots verts panaches

title: Roast Hare
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- marinade (port wine, vinegar, ketchup, red currant jelly)
- stuffing as for turkey
- hare kidneys
- hare heart
- fat bacon
- onion
- thin slices of bacon
- melted butter or clarified beef suet
- flour
- best gravy
- hare liver
- butter
- onion finely shredded
- chopped liver
- blood from hare

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, and wash the hare well, saving the heart, liver, kidneys and blood
2. Wipe the carcass inside and out
3. Let it soak in the marinade all day, turning it every now and then
4. Mince the kidneys and heart, and fry in fat bacon with a little onion
5. Pour the contents of the pan into a bowl to cool, and when cold, pound to a paste and mix with the stuffing
6. Fill the hare with the well-made stuffing
7. Lard the back of the hare, or cover with thin slices of bacon pinned down with little skewers
8. Roast the hare with constant basting of melted butter or clarified beef suet throughout the process
9. When nearly done, remove the bacon strips and lightly dredge the back with flour
10. Allow the skin to brown and run into crisp blisters
11. Prepare the sauce: Make a good pint of the best gravy you can
12. Cut the liver into dice
13. Melt an ounce of butter in a small sauce-pan
14. Throw in an onion finely shredded, toss the onion till it colours nicely
15. Throw in the chopped liver, shaking the pan for a minute or two
16. Add a little gravy, stir well, pour in all the gravy and simmer till the liver is cooked
17. Strain the gravy
18. Pour into it the marinade of port wine, vinegar, ketchup, and red currant jelly in which the hare was soaked
19. Put it on the fire in a sauce-pan
20. Pour in very slowly as it warms the blood you saved; continue stirring, and the sauce will thicken
21. Throw into it the liver pounded to a paste, stir and serve very hot
22. Serve the hare with the prepared sauce

title: Roast Snipes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- snipes
- hot buttered toast
- crisply fried breadcrumbs
- salad
- butter
- Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Roast the birds properly over a bright fire (or bake them if possible, or fry them in a frying-pan).
2. Serve them on hot buttered toast, smothered with crisply fried breadcrumbs.
3. A nice salad, butter, and Nepaul pepper should go round.

title: Roast Teal with Water-Cress
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- teal
- water-cress
- butter
- limes
- Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Let the teal be sent up most carefully roasted straight from the fire
2. Garnish each portion with a bunch of water-cress
3. Send round butter, limes cut in quarters, and Nepaul pepper

title: Roast Turkey with Perigueux Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- Perigueux sauce
- cauliflower
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Choose a nice turkey
2. Prepare the bird for roasting, stuffing it very carefully as directed on page 100
3. Let sauce Perigueux (page 97) steaming hot, be passed round in a boat
4. Accompany with cauliflower, and potatoes

title: Roast Wild Ducks with Bigarade Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild ducks
- two oranges (sweet limes)
- an ounce of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- a breakfast-cupful of common stock
- pepper
- salt
- juice of the oranges
- a pinch of sifted sugar
- a tablespoonful of Marsala
- a tea-spoonful of chilli vinegar

## Directions

1. Roast the wild ducks
2. Pare as thin as possible the rind of two oranges
3. Cut them into thin shreds and boil them in water for five minutes, then drain
4. Melt an ounce of butter in a sauce-pan
5. Stir into it a dessert-spoonful of flour and a breakfast-cupful of common stock made with scraps and the giblets of the ducks
6. Add pepper, salt, and the juice of the oranges, with a pinch of sifted sugar, a tablespoonful of Marsala, and a tea-spoonful of chilli vinegar
7. Add the boiled rinds, stir till the sauce boils, and serve in a boat

title: Roasted Florican
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- florican
- Bombay onion
- fat boiled bacon
- thin slice of bacon
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Truss the florican placing a Bombay onion, rolled in fat boiled bacon, inside its carcass.
2. Tie a thin slice of bacon over the breast.
3. Roast the bird over a moderate fire, basting it frequently with melted butter.
4. A few minutes before the florican is done, remove the slice of bacon, so as to let the breast take colour.
5. Serve with plain gravy, fried crumbs, and bread sauce.
6. Time, about thirty minutes.

title: Rolled Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- pepper
- nutmeg
- clove
- parsley
- lemon
- thyme
- sweet marjoram
- onion
- egg yolk
- bones and parings
- flour
- butter
- vinegar
- mushroom catsup
- soy
- lemon pickle
- port wine
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Bone a shoulder of mutton carefully, so as not to injure the skin.
2. Cut all the meat from the skin, mince it small, and season it highly with pepper, nutmeg, and a clove, some parsley, lemon, thyme, sweet marjoram chopped, and a pounded onion, all well mixed, together with the well-beaten yolk of an egg.
3. Roll it up very tightly in the skin.
4. Tie it round, and bake it in an oven for two or three hours, according to the size of the mutton.
5. Make a gravy of the bones and parings.
6. Season with an onion, pepper, and salt.
7. Strain and thicken it with flour and butter.
8. Add a tablespoonful each of vinegar, mushroom catsup, soy, and lemon pickle, and a teacupful of port wine.
9. Garnish with forcemeat balls made of grated bread, and part of the mince.

title: Rolled Rib Meat with Maccaroni
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- rolled rib meat
- egg
- dry cheese
- bread-crumbs
- maccaroni
- melted butter
- breakfast-cupful of rich tomato puree
- two table-spoonfuls of grated Parmesan or Gruyere
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- one spoonful of Marsala
- one tea-spoonful of red currant jelly

## Directions

1. After draining the rolled rib meat and letting it get cold, place it on a dish that will stand the oven
2. Brush it over with egg, and dredge over it a coating of very finely grated dry cheese and bread-crumbs in equal proportions
3. Set it in the oven to heat thoroughly, and brown the outside of the crumbs with a hot iron used salamander fashion
4. Put the meat on a dish, and trim round it a circle of hot maccaroni freshly boiled, drained, and tossed in melted butter
5. Pour a breakfast-cupful of rich tomato puree with two table-spoonfuls of grated Parmesan or Gruyere over the maccaroni
6. Make a nice thick brown gravy with butter, flour, and some of the bouillon, flavoured with a spoonful of Marsala and a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, and serve in a boat

title: Rose Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of spirits of wine
- twelve drops of the oil of roses
- three drops of oil of nutmeg
- a quart of syrup
- a sufficient quantity of pink tincture

## Directions

1. Into a quart of spirits of wine put twelve drops of the oil of roses and three of oil of nutmeg
2. shake the mixture well until the oils are dissolved
3. add a quart of syrup and a sufficient quantity of pink tincture to produce a fine rose-colour
4. filter and bottle

title: Rose Lip-salve
body:
## Ingredients

- an ounce and a half of spermaceti
- nine drachms of white wax
- twelve ounces of oil of sweet almonds
- two ounces of alkanet-root
- one drachm of otto of roses

## Directions

1. Digest the first four ingredients with the heat of boiling water for four hours
2. Strain through flannel
3. Add the otto of roses

title: Rose Syrup
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of rose petals
- 2.5 powas of water
- 1 seer of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1 chhatak of rose petals in 2.5 powas of water
2. Throw away the petals when they are fully boiled
3. Add 1 seer of sugar
4. Take down when the syrup is prepared

title: Rose-Bloom Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- bruised almonds
- rose-bloom

## Directions

1. Make in every respect like the foregoing custard
2. Add some bruised almonds and a little rose-bloom to tint the custard
3. Tint the froth of the white of the eggs with a few drops of the rose-bloom

title: Round Plum Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- perfectly ripe plums
- salt
- cloves of garlic
- green or fresh red chilies
- sliced ginger
- peppercorns
- ground mustard-seed
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Get the perfectly ripe fruit
2. Put them into a damp cloth, and roll them about to free them of dust
3. Sprinkle them well with salt
4. Stand them in the sun for three or four days
5. Drain away all the water
6. Bottle the plums alternately with cloves of garlic, green or fresh red chilies, sliced ginger, peppercorns, and ground mustard-seed
7. Add a little salt
8. Fill up the bottles with vinegar, and cork
9. Expose them to the sun for fifteen to twenty days

title: Round Plum Pickle with Mustard Oil
body:
## Ingredients

- perfectly ripe plums
- salt
- cloves of garlic
- green or fresh red chilies
- sliced ginger
- peppercorns
- ground mustard-seed
- vinegar
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Make like Round Plum Pickle
2. Pour some mustard oil over the vinegar
3. Allow it to float about an eighth of an inch thick over the surface

title: Rout Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of ground almonds
- one pound of powdered sugar
- yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Add one pound of powdered sugar to one pound of ground almonds
2. Mix them together with yolks of eggs to a stiff, yet flexible paste
3. Form it into small biscuits in the shape of coronets, shells, filberts, birds' nests, rings, or any other fancy shapes
4. Let them remain five or six hours, or all night, upon the baking-tin in a warm oven

title: Royal Lemonade
body:
## Ingredients

- two oranges
- six lemons
- one quart hot water
- one pound and a quarter loaf sugar
- three pints water
- juice of six oranges
- juice of one dozen lemons

## Directions

1. Pare two oranges and six lemons as thin as possible
2. Steep them four hours in one quart of hot water
3. Boil one pound and a quarter of loaf sugar in three pints of water
4. Skim it and add to the two liquors the juice of six oranges and a dozen lemons
5. Stir well
6. Strain through a jelly-bag and ice

title: Saddle of Mutton with Green Peas
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of mutton
- currant jelly
- potatoes
- green peas

## Directions

1. Order your saddle some days beforehand
2. Roast it, and serve it with currant jelly, potatoes, and green peas dressed as you may like best

title: Sakarpara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 powa almonds
- water
- 1 powa cream of milk
- ½ powa ghee
- 1 seer fine flour
- ghee
- 1 powa sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Keep 1 powa of almonds moist for a couple of hours
2. Peel them and cut in pieces
3. Make a paste of them with the necessary amount of water
4. Mix this paste with 1 powa of cream of milk and ½ powa of ghee with 1 seer of fine flour
5. Beat to a stiff dough adding a little water if necessary
6. Prepare small cubes of it
7. Fry in plenty of boiling ghee to a light brown colour
8. Smear them with a very concentrated syrup of 1 powa of sugar

title: Salade Russe
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- salted water
- butter
- beetroot
- truffles
- fresh haricot beans
- asparagus points
- capers
- French pickled gherkins
- anchovies
- olives
- parsley
- shallot
- raw yolks of eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs
- caviare
- prawns
- olives farcies
- pickles
- sweet capsicums

## Directions

1. Boil some carrots and some turnips in salted water with a small piece of butter, but do not let them be overdone.
2. When cold, cut out of them, with a vegetable scoop, a number of pieces the size of an olive.
3. Cut some beetroot in the same way, and likewise some truffles.
4. Take equal parts of each of the above (say a cupful), and a similar quantity of fresh haricot beans ready cooked, and of asparagus points prepared in the same way.
5. Add two table-spoonfuls respectively of capers, of French pickled gherkins cut into the shape of capers, and of anchovies, perfectly cleaned, and cut into small pieces.
6. Add a couple of dozen or more olives stoned, one table-spoonful of parsley minced fine, and one of shallot, also minced.
7. Mix the whole lightly together with a sauce, made with raw yolks of eggs, oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
8. Ornament with hard-boiled eggs, caviare, prawns, olives farcies, pickles, truffles, and sweet capsicums.

title: Salade à la Mainante
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- six cold boiled or tinned fonds d'artichauts
- cold boiled or tinned pointes d'asperges
- cold sliced truffles stewed in champagne or Madeira
- rich mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Take six cold boiled or tinned fonds d'artichauts, fill their hollows with cold boiled or tinned pointes d'asperges.
2. Cover the surface with cold sliced truffles that have been stewed in champagne or Madeira.
3. Smother each dressed fond with a rich mayonnaise dressing.
4. Serve straight from the ice-box.

title: Salmis of Quails
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight nice quails
- boiled bacon
- butter
- rich gravy
- butter
- flour
- caramel
- Madeira
- livers of the birds
- red currant jelly
- chilli vinegar
- mushrooms
- dry toast

## Directions

1. Choose eight nice quails and clean the birds carefully
2. Put a roll of boiled bacon inside each bird and sew up their vents
3. Truss the birds as for roasting
4. Melt some butter in a frying-pan, and give the quails a turn or two therein till they colour nicely
5. Put them in a stew-pan and cover them with a rich gravy
6. Stew them till they are done
7. Lift them out
8. Thicken the gravy with butter and flour
9. Colour it with caramel
10. Add half a glass of Madeira, the livers of the birds pounded, a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, a dash of chilli vinegar, and some finely minced mushrooms
11. Heat the quails up in this sauce, and serve as hot as possible, dry toast accompanying

title: Salmon Pie, To Be Eaten Cold
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of salmon from the tin
- three-quarters of a pound of forcemeat
- eight ounces of fresh uncooked whiting, pomfret, or other fresh fish
- four ounces of fresh butter
- four ounces of fine white crumbs
- salt and pepper
- a cupful of rich poulette sauce
- two raw eggs
- paste
- white of egg
- a coffee-cupful of rich fish broth made from the bones and trimmings of the fresh fish
- a coffee-cupful of essence of truffles

## Directions

1. Take one pound of salmon from the tin, and drain it from the tin liquor.
2. If nice and firm, cut it into fillets with a dessert-knife.
3. Make three-quarters of a pound of forcemeat as follows: choose either some fresh uncooked whiting, pomfret, or other fresh fish and having taken eight ounces of it pass it through a wire sieve.
4. Add four ounces of fresh butter, and four of fine white crumbs, pound all together in a mortar.
5. Season the puree with salt and pepper; moisten it with a cupful of rich poulette sauce, and bind the mixture with two raw eggs.
6. Line a raised pie tin with paste as for pork pie.
7. Fill the bottom with a layer of the forcemeat, then a layer of the salmon, an inch thick, continuing the packing till the pie is filled.
8. Put a cover of puff-paste over the top, brush it over with white of egg, and bake the pie slowly.
9. When done, let it cool for half an hour, and then pour in, through a hole made in the top, a coffee-cupful of rich fish broth, made from the bones and trimmings of the fresh fish, reduced to a glaze, and mix with a coffee-cupful of essence of truffles.
10. After having poured the essence into the pie, let it get quite cold, then turn it out of the tin, put it upon a napkin, and serve.

title: Salsify Scalloped
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- cream
- a tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce
- bread-crumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil the roots until they peel easily
2. Mash with cream, and a tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce
3. Strew bread-crumbs over it, and add a little melted butter
4. Bake till brown, and serve in coquilles

title: Sandesh with Date Sugar
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 seer of solid date-sugar (khejuri gur)
- 1/2 seer of hard chhana
- 1/2 powa of ordinary sugar
- pieces of pistachios
- 6 or 7 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Prepare a syrup of 1.5 seer of solid date-sugar (khejuri gur).
2. When it is slightly warm add a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana.
3. Stir vigorously.
4. When it is sticky and boiling take it off fire.
5. Add about 1/2 powa of ordinary sugar and mix thoroughly which will render the mass thick very quickly.
6. Remove the mass on a wooden plate.
7. Add pieces of pistachios and 6 or 7 drops of essence of rose and rub thoroughly.
8. Make balls and press in casts.

title: Sandesh with Kheer
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 powas of sugar
- 1/2 seer of hard chhana
- 1 powa of khowa kheer
- 1 chhatak of almonds
- 1/2 chhatak of pistachios
- 1/2 tola of powdered cardamom seeds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Heat syrup of 3 powas of sugar and when slightly warm add a paste of 1/2 seer of hard chhana.
2. Stir vigorously till sticky.
3. Add pastes of khowa kheer 1 powa, almonds 1 chhatak, pistachios 1/2 chhatak and powdered cardamom seeds 1/2 tola, and mix thoroughly.
4. Take off the fire.
5. Mix a handful of sugar and stir as before.
6. Make balls and press in casts when cold.

title: Sara-Chakra
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 seer of fried moog dal
- scraped kernels of 2 ripe cocoa-nuts
- khowa kheer 3 powas
- fine flour 1/2 powa
- sugar 1 powa
- water 1/2 seer
- 1 tola of powdered cardamoms
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup
- 6 or 7 drops of essence of rose

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 seer of fried moog dal in water till saturated with it and then make a paste of them with the scraped kernels of 2 ripe cocoa-nuts
2. Mix this paste with khowa kheer 3 powas, fine flour 1/2 powa, sugar 1 powa and water 1/2 seer
3. Heat the mass and stir till it is a stiff gluey paste
4. Take it down, scrape together the paste and beat thoroughly after mixing with 1 tola of powdered cardamoms
5. Prepare round and long finger-like cakes of it
6. Fry in plenty of ghee
7. Drop them while hot in a syrup of sugar
8. Serve while cold after mixing with 6 or 7 drops of essence of rose

title: Sarbat Lurki
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of curd
- 1 seer of water
- 1 1/2 powa of orange-juice
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- 4 table-spoonfuls of rose water
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Beat 1 seer of curd thoroughly with the hands or churn with a churn-staff
2. Strain through a clean piece of linen
3. Mix it with 1 seer of water, 1 1/2 powa of orange-juice, 1/2 seer of sugar, 4 table-spoonfuls of rose water and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Bel Fruit
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big very ripe bel fruit
- water
- curd
- 1/2 powa of sugar
- salt
- half a table-spoonful of rose water
- lemon juice or tamarind pulp
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Break a big very ripe bel fruit and bring out the whole of the pulp
2. Squeeze it with a little water and strain out the pulp with a clean piece of linen
3. Throw away the seeds and other refuses
4. Mix the pulp with curd and sugar 1/2 powa each, a pinch of salt, half a table-spoonful of rose water, a little lemon juice or tamarind pulp, fragments of ice and water as required

title: Sarbat of Blackberries
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of very ripe blackberries
- 1 seer of water
- salt
- 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the soft pulp of 1 seer of very ripe blackberries and strain it through a clean piece of linen throwing away the seeds
2. Mix it with 1 seer of water, a little salt, 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sugar and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Chhana
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 powa of fresh chhana
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- 1 seer of water
- lemon-juice
- 1 or 2 table-spoonfuls of rose water
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Make a very fine paste of 1 1/2 powa of fresh chhana
2. Dissolve 1/2 seer of sugar in 1 seer of water and strain it through a clean piece of linen
3. Mix the pasted chhana thoroughly with the sugar solution and churn with the hands till nearly all the particles of chhana float
4. Mix with a little lemon-juice, 1 or 2 table-spoonfuls of rose water and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Falsa
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of very ripe falsa fruits
- 1 powa of water
- 3 or 4 green legumes of tamarind
- 1/2 seer of water
- 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- salt
- 3 or 4 tea-spoonfuls of rose water
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Squeeze 1 seer of very ripe falsa fruits with the hands, mix with 1 powa of water and strain through a clean piece of linen
2. Boil fully 3 or 4 green legumes of tamarind and squeeze with the hands
3. Mix with 1 powa of water and strain out the juice
4. Mix the two together with 1/2 seer of water, 1 1/2 powa of sugar, a pinch of salt, 3 or 4 tea-spoonfuls of rose water and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Green Mangoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 big green mangoes
- 1 seer of water
- 1 powa of sugar
- salt
- table-spoonful of rose water
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Burn or boil 4 or 5 big green mangoes till they are soft
2. Peel them and strain the pulp through a clean piece of linen
3. Mix it with 1 seer of water, 1 powa of sugar, a pinch of salt, a table-spoonful of rose water and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Lemon-Juice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of lemon-juice
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- 1 seer of water
- salt
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Thoroughly mix together 1/2 powa of lemon-juice, 1/2 seer of sugar, 1 seer of water, a generous pinch of salt and fragments of ice as required
2. Use pots and glasses of porcelain, glass or enamel

title: Sarbat of Milk
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of pistachios
- 1/2 powa of almonds
- 1 seer of fresh unboiled milk
- 1 powa of water
- 1 or 2 table-spoonfuls of rose water
- 1/2 seer of sugar
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Peel pistachios and almonds 1/2 powa each after keeping them moistened in water for a few hours
2. Cut them in pieces and make a paste of them
3. Mix the paste with fresh unboiled milk 1 seer, water 1 powa, 1 or 2 table-spoonfuls of rose water, sugar 1/2 seer and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarbat of Water Melon
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big very ripe water-melon
- 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sugar
- two table-spoonfuls of rose water
- half a table-spoonful of lemon-juice
- fragments of ice

## Directions

1. Place a big very ripe water-melon on an enamelled pot and cut it in two with a knife
2. Bring out the red pulp of the fruit with its juice and squeeze the pulp with the hands
3. Remove all the seeds
4. Mix it with 1 or 1 1/2 powa of sugar, two table-spoonfuls of rose water, half a table-spoonful of lemon-juice and fragments of ice as required

title: Sarpuria
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- undiluted milk 5 seers
- cows' ghee
- powdered sugar-candy
- a little powdered cardamom seeds
- milk for baking

## Directions

1. Boil five seers of undiluted milk in two pans
2. Stir for a few minutes
3. Make the heat very mild and cease stirring altogether when creams will form
4. When the creams are quite thick remove one on the other pan
5. Cream will again appear on the milk from which it is removed
6. Remove this again on the other pan
7. Continue in this way 4 or 5 times
8. Remove one fourth of the milk from the pan containing the cream to the other pan
9. Again proceed in the same way till the cream is quite thick
10. Remove it on a plate and cut in big squares
11. Boil some pure cows' ghee in another pan with some powdered sugar-candy and a little powdered cardamom seeds
12. Smear the squares with this spiced ghee
13. Arrange one on another till the desired thickness is reached
14. Bake both sides of them for a little while with a little milk using very mild heat

title: Saru-Chakli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Husked kalai pulses
- Powdered rice
- Cocoa-nut kernel
- Salt
- Water
- Ghee

## Directions

1. Keep some husked pulses of kalai under water for 2 hours and make a paste of them
2. Add powdered rice up to half the quantity of pulses
3. Scraped and pasted kernel of cocoa-nut may be added if desired up to half the quantity of pulses
4. Add salt and make a thick solution with water
5. Heat a frying pan, smear with a little ghee
6. Pour in two table-spoonfuls of the paste at a time
7. Spread it in a thin circle with a small thin rectangular plate of iron
8. Bake the other side when it is hard enough to be turned

title: Sauce a la Careme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- two ounces of sugar
- claret-glassful of Madeira

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of three eggs with two ounces of sugar and a claret-glassful of Madeira
2. When well mixed, set the sauce in the bain-marie and let it thicken like a custard
3. When smooth and creamy, serve it in a boat, hot

title: Sauce A La Poulette
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of chicken broth
- one ounce of butter
- one ounce of flour
- pepper and salt to taste
- the yolks of three eggs
- a pat of butter
- a couple of table-spoonfuls of chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Make an ordinary thin sauce blonde with one pint of chicken broth, one ounce of butter, one ounce of flour, pepper and salt to taste
2. Stir well for a quarter of an hour, and it will be a thin white sauce
3. Add en bain-marie one by one the strained and well beaten yolks of three eggs
4. Finish off with a pat of butter, and a couple of table-spoonfuls of chopped mushrooms

title: Sauce au Pauvre Homme
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a minced onion
- a little butter
- a little broth made from scraps
- a tea-spoonful of vinegar
- melted butter and flour
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of sugar
- a little pepper
- some minced parsley

## Directions

1. Fry a minced onion in a little butter until it assumes a golden brown tint.
2. Pour in a little broth made from scraps, with a tea-spoonful of vinegar.
3. Give this a boil, and then strain it by degrees into another sauce-pan containing melted butter and flour.
4. Work this well with a wooden spoon and add a pinch of salt, one of sugar, a little pepper, and some minced parsley.

title: Sauce Aux Fines Herbes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of milk
- a minced Bombay onion
- a tiny bit of garlic
- a handful of parsley
- a couple of ounces of butter
- two ounces of flour
- a table-spoonful of minced curled parsley
- a dessert-spoonful of chopped garden cress
- half a dessert-spoonful of chopped green stem of spring onion
- a squeeze of a lime

## Directions

1. Flavour a pint of milk by boiling up in it a minced Bombay onion, a tiny bit of garlic, and a handful of parsley
2. When well flavoured, strain the milk through muslin
3. Stir it by degrees into a sauce-pan in which a couple of ounces of butter and two of flour have been mixed
4. Thicken gently by bringing the mixture to the boil
5. Strain
6. Add, just before serving, a table-spoonful of minced curled parsley, a dessert-spoonful of chopped garden cress, and half one of chopped green stem of spring onion
7. A squeeze of a lime may be judiciously added to this sauce

title: Sauce for Alderman's Mock Turtle Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- stock
- a canister of oysters
- the pulp of sixteen or twenty prunes
- a blade of mace
- some nutmeg and cloves
- a wineglassful of port wine
- a tablespoonful of Worcestershire sauce

## Directions

1. Mix with some of the stock the contents of a canister of oysters well bruised, the pulp of sixteen or twenty prunes, a blade of mace, some nutmeg and cloves, a wineglassful of port wine, and a tablespoonful of Worcestershire sauce.
2. Allow to simmer for ten minutes, and add it to the ready-baked pie before it is put on the table.

title: Sauce for Any Kind of Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoonfuls of gravy
- two of vinegar
- a blade of mace
- pepper
- salt
- a large onion sliced

## Directions

1. Take three tablespoonfuls of gravy, two of vinegar, a blade of mace, a little pepper and salt, and a large onion sliced
2. Boil and strain

title: Sauce for Boiled Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- a large onion
- a tablespoonful of finely-chopped parsley
- some good gravy
- one ounce of butter
- flour
- a spoonful of cut capers

## Directions

1. Mince a large onion, parboil it, and drain off the water
2. Put the onion into a saucepan, with a tablespoonful of finely-chopped parsley, some good gravy, and one ounce of butter dredged with a little flour
3. Let it boil nearly ten minutes, and add a spoonful of cut capers
4. The sauce must be thoroughly heated before being served up

title: Sauce for Boiled Mutton or Boiled Brisket of Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- two chittacks or four ounces of butter
- a tablespoonful of finely-sliced onions
- two tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour
- eight chittacks or sixteen ounces of pure milk
- two wineglasses of vinegar
- finely-pounded white pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Warm a saucepan and melt in it two chittacks or four ounces of butter free of water
2. Fry in it a tablespoonful of finely-sliced onions
3. When half browned, put in gradually two tablespoonfuls of finely-sifted flour, taking care to keep stirring it the whole time
4. Add gradually eight chittacks or sixteen ounces of pure milk
5. Add lastly two wineglasses of vinegar
6. Season with finely-pounded white pepper and salt to taste

title: Sauce for Cucumber Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- two large Patna onions
- a couple of fresh chilies
- ground pepper
- salt
- two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- sliced cucumbers

## Directions

1. Slice into a soup-plate two large Patna onions and a couple of fresh chilies
2. Sprinkle over with ground pepper and a little salt
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, and allow to stand for two or three hours before adding to it the sliced cucumbers

title: Sauce for Lobster Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- spawn or red coral of the lobsters
- essence of anchovy
- mushroom catsup
- Indian tapp sauce

## Directions

1. Observe all the directions given in the Sauce for Salads recipe
2. Add to the yolks of the hard-boiled eggs some of the spawn or red coral of the lobsters and a dash of essence of anchovy
3. Omit the sugar
4. Instead of the Worcestershire sauce substitute mushroom catsup and Indian tapp sauce

title: Sauce for Roast Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- a large tablespoonful of finely-grated horseradish
- a dessertspoonful of made mustard
- half a dessertspoonful of brown sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix well together a large tablespoonful of finely-grated horseradish, a dessertspoonful of made mustard, and half a dessertspoonful of brown sugar
2. Add vinegar till it be as thick as made mustard
3. Serve in a sauce-tureen

title: Sauce for Salads
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
- half a teaspoonful of pepper
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one tablespoonful of sugar
- two to three teaspoonfuls of prepared mustard
- four tablespoonfuls of oil
- a dessertspoonful of Lee and Perrin's Worcestershire sauce
- one tablespoonful and a half of white wine vinegar
- a dessertspoonful of tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Bruise down when quite cold the yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
2. Rub into them half a teaspoonful of pepper, one teaspoonful of salt and one tablespoonful of sugar, with two to three teaspoonfuls of prepared mustard
3. When well rubbed together, add very gradually four tablespoonfuls of oil, stirring it the whole while
4. When well mixed add a dessertspoonful of Lee and Perrin's Worcestershire sauce
5. Add one tablespoonful and a half of white wine vinegar and a dessertspoonful of tarragon vinegar
6. If a thicker sauce is required, take either a larger number of eggs or a teaspoonful or dessertspoonful of corn or other flour
7. Put the flour into a cup and pour over it the quantity of vinegar prescribed
8. Place the cup in a saucepan of boiling water over the fire and stir until the vinegar thickens to the desired consistency
9. Mix it gradually into the preparation of eggs, oil, &c.

title: Sauce for Victoria Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tea-spoonfuls of flour
- half an ounce of butter
- a cupful of boiling water
- a table-spoonful of jam
- the juice of a lime
- a spoonful of brandy

## Directions

1. Mix flour with half an ounce of butter
2. Add a cupful of boiling water
3. Stir in jam, lime juice, and brandy
4. Simmer for five minutes
5. Mix with a little pat of butter
6. Strain and pour over and round the pudding

title: Sauce Hollandaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- the yolks of three eggs
- a little water
- a salt-spoonful of pounded allspice
- salt to taste
- about three ounces of fresh butter
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of three eggs in a little water in which a salt-spoonful of pounded allspice has been dissolved
2. add salt to taste, and about three ounces of fresh butter
3. Put this mixture into a small sauce-pan, and plunge it into a bain-marie, or stew-pan large enough to receive it, full of boiling water
4. steam your mixture in this way till it thickens, and stir in your lime juice to finish with

title: Sauce Hollandaise (Gouffe's Recipe)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two table-spoonfuls of vinegar
- salt and pepper
- two table-spoonfuls of water
- two yolks of eggs
- four ounces of butter

## Directions

1. reduce two table-spoonfuls of vinegar on the fire with a little salt and pepper added to it, till about a tea-spoonful remains
2. strain, and add to it two table-spoonfuls of water, and two yolks of eggs
3. put this on the fire and heat it thoroughly, stirring it well with a wooden spoon, and add four ounces of butter ounce by ounce by degrees, with a little water now and then to prevent its curdling
4. This process had better be carried out in a bain-marie, for you thus obtain the amount of gentle heat which is necessary to preserve the sauce in a velvety condition without risk of any kind

title: Sauce Milanaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two parboiled Bombay onions
- an ounce of butter
- a pinch of sugar
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a table-spoonful of previously boiled rice, or pearl barley
- a breakfast-cupful of broth

## Directions

1. Cut up two parboiled Bombay onions
2. Put them into a sauce-pan with an ounce of butter, a pinch of sugar, and a salt-spoonful of salt
3. Add a table-spoonful of previously boiled rice, or pearl barley
4. Moisten with a breakfast-cupful of broth
5. Let them cook slowly

title: Sauce Milanaise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two parboiled Bombay onions
- an ounce of butter
- a pinch of sugar
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a table-spoonful of previously boiled rice, or pearl barley
- a breakfast-cupful of broth
- a table-spoonful of finely grated mild cheese (Parmesan for choice)
- half a pint of rich sauce blonde, or boiling cream

## Directions

1. Cut up two parboiled Bombay onions, and put them into a sauce-pan with an ounce of butter, a pinch of sugar, and a salt-spoonful of salt
2. add a table-spoonful of previously boiled rice, or pearl barley, and moisten with a breakfast-cupful of broth
3. let them cook slowly, and when the onions are done, add a table-spoonful of finely grated mild cheese (Parmesan for choice)
4. stir the mixture, pass it through a sieve, and mingle it with half a pint of rich sauce blonde, or with boiling cream

title: Sauce Piquante
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of melted butter
- an ounce of minced onion
- an ounce of chopped carrot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- one of garden cress
- one clove of garlic
- claret-glass of vinegar
- claret-glass of water, or broth and vinegar
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- one of sugar

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of melted butter an ounce of minced onion with an ounce of chopped carrot, a dessert-spoonful of parsley, one of garden cress, and one clove of garlic
2. When of a golden hue, add equal parts (claret-glass each) of vinegar, and water, or broth and vinegar, a tea-spoonful of salt, and one of sugar
3. strain, and serve hot

title: Sauce Poivrade (Maigre)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of melted butter
- an ounce of minced onion
- an ounce of chopped carrot
- a dessert-spoonful of parsley
- one of garden cress
- one clove of garlic
- claret-glass of vinegar
- claret-glass of water or broth
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- one of sugar
- butter and flour

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of melted butter an ounce of minced onion with an ounce of chopped carrot, a dessert-spoonful of parsley, one of garden cress, and one clove of garlic
2. When of a golden hue, add equal parts (claret-glass each) of vinegar, and water or broth, a tea-spoonful of salt, and one of sugar
3. strain the liquor and thicken with butter and flour
4. it may be served either white or brown as you may desire

title: Sauce Robert
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine sweet onion
- an ounce of butter
- an ounce of flour
- half a pint of gravy
- pepper and salt
- a pinch of sugar
- a table-spoonful of vinegar
- one of mixed mustard

## Directions

1. Chop up a fine sweet onion, throw the mince into a sauce-pan with an ounce of butter
2. Let it take colour, then add an ounce of flour by degrees, and when that has been well worked, half a pint of gravy, pepper and salt at discretion, and a pinch of sugar
3. When thoroughly mixed, and piping hot, pass the sauce through the tin strainer, catching up all lumps
4. at the last moment, stir in a table-spoonful of vinegar, and one of mixed mustard

title: Sauce Soubise
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- some large sweet onions
- milk
- sauce blonde
- a spoonful of cream

## Directions

1. Simmer some large sweet onions in milk till tender
2. Pass through the sieve
3. Work the pulp into sauce blonde
4. Add a spoonful of cream for high days and holidays

title: Sauce à la Périgueux
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- half the contents of a small bottle of truffles
- melted butter
- a coffee-cupful of clear gravy
- a glass of Madeira
- pepper
- a pinch of sugar
- salt
- half a pint of Espagnole

## Directions

1. Chop up half the contents of a small bottle of truffles, and toss them awhile in some melted butter at the bottom of a light sauce-pan
2. Add a coffee-cupful of clear gravy, and a glass of Madeira, and simmer for ten minutes
3. Give it a little pepper, a pinch of sugar, and salt
4. Slowly stir in half a pint of Espagnole to complete the sauce
5. When thoroughly hot, it is ready

title: Sausage Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- equal portions of cold roast veal and ham, or cold fowl and tongue
- a teaspoonful of powdered sweet herbs
- a spoonful of mixed salt and cayenne pepper
- pie-crust pastry (recipe No. 199)

## Directions

1. Take equal portions of cold roast veal and ham, or cold fowl and tongue; chop them together very small.
2. Season with a teaspoonful of powdered sweet herbs, and a spoonful of mixed salt and cayenne pepper.
3. Mix well together.
4. Put three tablespoonfuls of the meat well rolled together into enough pastry to cover it.
5. When you have used up the whole of your materials, bake them for half an hour in a brisk oven.

title: Savoury Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- undercut of sirloin of beef
- coarser meat for gravy
- boiled bacon
- spiced pepper
- shallot
- butter
- strong gravy
- hard boiled egg
- light crust

## Directions

1. Buy a nice undercut of a sirloin of beef and some coarser meat for gravy
2. Slice the undercut into thin slices crosswise, not lengthwise
3. Place the slices on a flat dish and upon each slice lay a thin one of boiled bacon
4. Give the whole a dusting of spiced pepper
5. Roll them up (each separately) and give them an external dusting of spiced pepper
6. Take a pie-dish, rub it well with a shallot, butter it, and place a layer of the rolled slices over the bottom of it
7. Pour gently round the layer a cupful of strong gravy made from the coarser meat
8. Repeat the process adding another layer of rolled beef slices, and more gravy, till the dish is packed
9. Garnish the surface with pieces of hard boiled egg
10. Cover the whole with a good light crust
11. Bake in the usual way and serve

title: Scorched Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fishes with scales
- chopped onions
- green chillies
- mustard oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the scales from fishes, wash and scorch them equally on both sides till they are soft.
2. Remove the black crusts and the bones.
3. Mix them with chopped onions, green chillies, mustard oil and salt.
4. Serve.

title: Scotch Shortbread
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- one pound of butter free of water
- twelve ounces of sugar
- four eggs, well beaten
- candied peel
- caraway-seeds
- caraway comfits

## Directions

1. Warm before the fire two pounds of flour and one pound of butter free of water
2. Rub the butter, with twelve ounces of sugar, into the flour with the hand and make it into a stiff paste with four eggs, well beaten
3. Roll out to the required thickness with as little use of the rolling-pin as possible; either take small pieces, and roll them into oblong cakes, or roll out a large piece and cut it into squares or rounds
4. Prick a pattern round the edge of each cake with the back of a knife, and arrange slices of candied peel, caraway-seeds, and caraway comfits in a pattern
5. Bake for about twenty minutes in an oven that is not too quick
6. The mixing of flour, sugar, and butter, and afterwards of the eggs, must be done very thoroughly and smoothly

title: Seam, Potato, and Peas Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- seams, twenty
- new potatoes, four
- green peas, a quarter of a seer
- mustard oil, one chittack and a half
- ground onions, four teaspoonfuls
- ground chilies, one teaspoonful
- ground turmeric, half a teaspoonful
- ground garlic, a quarter of a teaspoonful
- salt, one teaspoonful and a half
- water, one cupful
- cauliflower (optional for a change)

## Directions

1. Divide each seam into three pieces and throw into a bowl of water
2. Divide each potato into four pieces and throw into water
3. Shell the peas and wash all thoroughly
4. Put into a colander to drain
5. Warm the oil and let it bubble well
6. Fry the ground condiments until quite brown
7. Put in the vegetables and salt
8. Let the whole fry, stirring it well
9. Add the water and allow it to simmer over a slow fire until the vegetables are quite tender

title: Seer Fritters With Hollandaise Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cut of seer-fish
- lime juice or vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- sweet herbs
- yolks of two eggs
- brandy
- salad oil
- water
- imported flour
- boiling fat
- fried parsley
- slices of lime
- sauce-Hollandaise

## Directions

1. Divide a cut of seer-fish into six nice collops about two inches long, half an inch thick
2. Marinate in a little lime juice or vinegar, pepper, salt, onion, and sweet herbs
3. Half fry, or bake them, and let them get cold
4. Beat up together the yolks of two eggs with one table-spoonful of brandy, one of salad oil, and four or five table-spoonfuls of water
5. Amalgamate with this three table-spoonfuls of imported flour, and a pinch of salt
6. Beat the mixture well for a minute or two, and bring it to the right consistency by adding water, or flour, as the case may require
7. Add, at the last moment, the whites whipped to a froth
8. Dip your collops into this, and lay them one by one in your frying basket, dipping it immediately into a deep saute-pan, filled with boiling fat
9. As soon as they turn a nice deep yellow, lift them out, drain them on a sheet of blotting paper
10. Serve them, crisp and dry on a napkin garnished with fried parsley, and slices of lime. With sauce-Hollandaise in a boat

title: Seer Slices with Peg Woffington Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cut of seer fish
- butter
- half a pint of broth made from the fish trimmings
- a glass of chablis or hock
- pepper and salt
- minced parsley
- a little shallot
- a quarter of a pint of cream
- a table-spoonful of walnut catsup
- half a table-spoonful of anchovy sauce
- a bit of butter the size of a walnut rolled in flour
- a thought of red pepper

## Directions

1. Divide a cut of seer fish into eight nice collops about half an inch thick
2. Flatten them by a board
3. Butter a pie-dish and pour into it half a pint of broth made from the fish trimmings, and a glass of chablis or hock
4. Lay the slices therein
5. Pepper and salt them
6. Sprinkle them with some minced parsley, and a little shallot
7. Spread a sheet of buttered paper over them
8. Bake for about ten minutes in a quick oven
9. When done, remove the paper, and arrange the slices neatly in a hot dish
10. For the sauce: To the liquor from the pie-dish add a quarter of a pint of cream, a table-spoonful of walnut catsup, and half one of anchovy sauce
11. Boil these together
12. Just before you remove the sauce-pan from the fire, stir in well a bit of butter the size of a walnut rolled in flour, with a thought of red pepper

title: Seer with Cucumbers
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of seer
- chicken bones
- fish trimmings
- slices of carrot
- onion
- table-spoonful of dried sweet herbs
- glass of chablis
- butter
- flour
- fillets of cucumber

## Directions

1. Put fish trimmings, chicken bones, slices of carrot, onion, a table-spoonful of dried sweet herbs, and a glass of chablis into a clear broth
2. Stew neatly trimmed slices of seer plainly in the broth
3. Simmer gently until done
4. Drain the fish and strain the gravy
5. Thicken the gravy with butter and flour
6. Add previously cooked fillets of cucumber about an inch long and half an inch thick to the gravy
7. Add the pieces of fish to the gravy
8. Heat altogether until the stew steams freely and serve

title: Seer With Mushrooms
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good deep cut of seer fish
- a pint of thin brown gravy
- a little onion
- a carrot
- a bit of celery
- some parsley
- a tablespoonful of chablis or sauterne
- one tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- a teaspoonful of red currant jelly
- a little lime juice
- butter
- flour
- a dozen black Leicestershire mushrooms

## Directions

1. Choose a good deep cut of seer fish
2. Take about a pint of thin brown gravy, a little onion, a carrot cut up, a bit of celery, and some parsley
3. Flavour it with a tablespoonful of chablis or sauterne, and one of mushroom ketchup, a teaspoonful of red currant jelly, and a little lime juice
4. Stew the fish in this liquor very gently
5. When done, strain it, thicken the gravy with butter and flour, adding a dozen black Leicestershire mushrooms
6. Heat up without boiling, and serve

title: Seer with Truffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two handsome slices of seer fish
- bones and trimmings for broth
- one onion
- one carrot
- a bunch of parsley
- one dessert-spoonful preserved thyme and marjoram
- one minced anchovy
- a dozen pepper corns
- one table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- one table-spoonful of Harvey sauce
- one table-spoonful of vinegar
- one table-spoonful of chablis or sauteme
- two table-spoonfuls of chopped truffles
- one ounce butter
- one table-spoonful of Madeira
- well browned gravy

## Directions

1. Stew two handsome slices of seer fish in a good broth made from bones and trimmings, assisted by an onion, a carrot, a bunch of parsley, a dessert-spoonful of preserved thyme and marjoram, a minced anchovy, a dozen pepper corns, a table-spoonful of mushroom ketchup, a table-spoonful of Harvey sauce, a table-spoonful of vinegar, and a table-spoonful of chablis or sauteme
2. Let the fish slices cook slowly in this broth
3. When done, drain and place them in a very hot dish, carefully covered up
4. Strain the broth in which they were cooked, thicken it, and add a little well browned gravy
5. Toss two table-spoonfuls of chopped truffles in a frying-pan in an ounce butter and a table-spoonful of Madeira, then throw into the sauce
6. Let the sauce simmer for ten minutes to extract the flavour of the truffles
7. Pour it over the slices and serve

title: Seer, Neopolitan
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- seer fish
- maccaroni
- ripe tomatoes
- Parmesan cheese
- black Leicestershire mushrooms
- butter
- sauce blonde
- pepper
- salt
- white wine (chablis, hock, or sauterne)
- gravy

## Directions

1. Divide a good cut of seer fish into slices half an inch thick and cut into portions about large enough for each guest.
2. Boil some maccaroni till tender.
3. Slice up some nice ripe tomatoes.
4. Turn out the mushrooms only (not the gravy) of a tin of black Leicestershire mushrooms onto a plate.
5. Butter a nice sized pie-dish (not too deep in the sides).
6. Arrange the maccaroni on the bottom of the pie-dish.
7. Dust over the maccaroni a little of the Parmesan.
8. Put a layer of the sliced tomatoes.
9. Arrange your portions of fish thereon with a piece of mushroom between each of them, and a slice of tomato in the centre of each.
10. Shake a little pepper and salt gently over each layer as you pack the dish.
11. Pour a breakfast-cupful of sauce blonde over all.
12. Give a light surface dressing of Parmesan.
13. Add only a very little white wine like chablis, hock, or sauterne, or gravy as a finishing touch.
14. Bake the dish for about a quarter of an hour in a quite hot oven.

title: Seik Kawab
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef, mutton, or veal
- two tablespoonfuls of mustard oil
- four teaspoonfuls of ground onions
- one teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- one teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one teaspoonful and a half of salt
- a cup of thick tyre or dhye
- half a teaspoonful of ground coriander-seed
- the juice of one large lemon
- a little ghee

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and chop the meat slightly, without mincing or cutting through it.
2. Mix well together all the ground condiments, including the oil, tyre, and lemon-juice, in which steep the chopped meat, turning it over occasionally to absorb the mixture.
3. After a while cut up the meat into squares of equal size, say two inches, and continue to keep them in the mixture for fully one hour.
4. Pass the squares of meat either on a silver, plated, or other metal skewer, and roast or broil over a slow charcoal fire, basting the whole time with ghee, to allow the kawab to become of a rich brown colour, without burning or being singed in the basting.
5. Remove from the skewer, and serve hot.

title: Sherry Cobbler
body:
## Ingredients

- two wineglassfuls sherry
- half a wineglassful rum
- half a wineglassful maraschino
- half an orange
- crushed ice

## Directions

1. Pour sherry, rum, and maraschino into a tumbler
2. Add half an orange sliced fine
3. Fill the tumbler with crushed ice
4. Take the preparation through a reed, quill, or common straw

title: Shin of Beef Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- soup herbs
- white of an egg
- a tablespoonful of Lea & Perrin's Worcestershire sauce
- half a wineglassful of sherry

## Directions

1. Take a shin of beef, cut it up small, wash it thoroughly, and boil with pepper and salt in sufficient water to well cover the meat
2. Let it boil over a brisk fire, taking away the black scum
3. Add a little cold water, and skim off the white scum
4. Reduce the fire, and allow the soup to simmer until it somewhat thickens
5. Strain the soup, cut away all the fat, season with soup herbs, and add more pepper and salt if necessary
6. Give it a good boil up, and then clear it with the white of an egg well beaten up
7. After which add a tablespoonful of Lea & Perrin's Worcestershire sauce, and half a wineglassful of sherry

title: Shin of Beef Soup with Forcemeat and Egg Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- soup herbs
- white of an egg
- sauce
- sherry
- forcemeat
- egg balls

## Directions

1. Prepare a shin of beef soup in all respects according to the above directions
2. Clear with an egg well beaten up, add to it sauce, sherry, forcemeat, and egg balls

title: Shoal Fish with Pumpkin
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- shoal fishes
- oil
- tender pumpkin
- cassia leaves
- chillies
- entire cumins
- salt
- powdered chillies
- coriander
- cumin
- pepper
- water
- ghee

## Directions

1. Throw away the heads of the shoal fishes as they are not edible.
2. Prepare the flesh portions and fry with oil.
3. Peel a tender pumpkin and chop it in pieces.
4. Heat oil, fry cassia leaves, chillies and a pinch of entire cumins in it, add the chopped pumpkin and stir.
5. A few minutes later add salt, powdered chillies, coriander, cumin, pepper and water.
6. On boiling add fish.
7. Add a little ghee when the soup is scanty and take down after stirring a little.

title: Short-Bread Biscuits For Spinach
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of flour
- two ounces of butter liquefied
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- a salt-spoonful of sugar
- an ounce of grated cheese

## Directions

1. Put three ounces of flour into a bowl and mix into it two ounces of butter liquefied, a tea-spoonful of salt, a salt-spoonful of sugar and an ounce of grated cheese
2. Roll out the paste
