3. Cut it into heart-shapes one-third of an inch thick
4. Bake

title: Shortbread
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sifted flour
- one pound of butter
- a quarter of a pound of candied orange and lemon peel
- a quarter of a pound of pounded loaf sugar
- a quarter of a pound of blanched sweet almonds
- a quarter of a pound of caraway comfits

## Directions

1. Cut the lemon, the orange-peel, and almonds into small thin bits, and mix them with a pound and a half of the flour, a few of the caraway comfits, and the sugar
2. Melt the butter, and when cool, pour it into the flour, at the same time mixing it quickly with the hands
3. Form it into a large round nearly an inch thick, using the remainder of the flour to make it up with
4. Cut it into four, and with the finger and thumb pinch each bit neatly all round the edge
5. Prick them with a fork, and strew the rest of the caraway comfits over the top
6. Put the pieces upon white paper dusted with flour, and then upon tins
7. Bake them in a moderate oven

title: Shoulder of Mutton Stuffed
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- pepper
- salt
- thin slices of cold cooked bacon
- turkey stuffing
- four ounces of butter
- pint of good broth made from the bones and trimmings
- two Bombay onions
- clove of garlic
- carrot
- six pepper corns
- blade of mace
- good piece of celery
- dessert-spoonful of sugar
- dessert-spoonful of salt
- tumbler of chablis or sauteme
- half a glass of brandy
- half a glass of Madeira
- dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
- juice of a lime
- few drops of chilli vinegar
- small white onions

## Directions

1. Order a shoulder of the best mutton you can get, bone it carefully, wash it, dry it, and flatten it out upon a clean board.
2. Dust it over with pepper and salt, and lay over it a number of thin slices of cold cooked bacon.
3. Make a good bowl of turkey stuffing and spread it evenly over the bacon.
4. Roll the meat carefully up, and secure it in shape with tapes.
5. Put four ounces of butter into a stew-pan, and turn the roll of mutton over in it till it takes colour.
6. Pour in a pint or so of good broth made from the bones and trimmings, with two Bombay onions cut up, a clove of garlic, a carrot sliced, six pepper corns, a blade of mace, a good piece of celery, a dessert-spoonful of sugar, a dessert-spoonful of salt, a tumbler of chablis or sauteme, and half a glass of brandy.
7. Braise the mutton in this until it is done.
8. Dish it on a hot dish, and brown its surface with a hot iron.
9. Strain off the gravy, remove the fat, flavour it with half a glass of Madeira, a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly, the juice of a lime, and a few drops of chilli vinegar.
10. Let it boil up, pour it round the mutton, and serve.
11. Garnish the dish with small white onions glazed, and send round potatoes à la maître d'hôtel.

title: Shrewsbury Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter, washed in rose-water and beaten to a cream
- half a pound of dried and sifted flour
- seven ounces of pounded and sifted loaf sugar
- half an ounce of caraway-seeds
- two well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Mix the butter, flour, sugar, caraway-seeds, and eggs together into a paste
2. Roll it thin
3. Cut it into round cakes
4. Prick them
5. Bake them upon floured tins

title: Shrewsbury Cakes Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of dried and sifted flour
- half a pound of fresh butter
- seven ounces of sifted loaf sugar
- seven ounces of cleaned and dried currants
- a little grated nutmeg
- a little water
- two tablespoonfuls of rose or orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Rub into a pound of dried and sifted flour half a pound of fresh butter
2. Add seven ounces of sifted loaf sugar
3. Add seven ounces of cleaned and dried currants
4. Add a little grated nutmeg
5. Make it into a paste with a little water and two tablespoonfuls of rose or orange-flower water
6. Roll it out and cut it into round cakes
7. Prick them
8. Bake them upon tins dusted with flour

title: Shrimp Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- melted butter
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon pickle
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick some shrimps nicely from the shell
2. Put them into melted butter, and add a tablespoonful of lemon pickle and vinegar
3. Heat it

title: Shrimps Boiled in Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps 1 seer
- salt
- mustard 1 tola
- coriander 1 tola
- red chillies
- turmeric
- mustard oil
- banana leaves
- flour-paste
- rice

## Directions

1. Remove all the scales and the tips of the heads, and wash
2. Smear them with salt, oil and all the spices powdered
3. Wrap up a bit loosely with a few pieces of tender banana leaves making water-tight with a little flour-paste, if necessary
4. Drop it gently in boiling rice and take out when rice are boiled

title: Shrimps Powdered
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried shrimps flaked
- Saffron
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash some dried shrimps which have been flaked before drying
2. Fry them a little without any kind of fat
3. Powder them with saffron, pepper and salt
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Bottle it and serve with bread and butter

title: Shrimps Roasted
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- a few big sized shrimps
- salt
- mustard oil
- powdered turmeric
- chillies
- minced green chillies
- onions

## Directions

1. Scale a few big sized shrimps
2. Smear them with salt, mustard oil, powdered turmeric and chillies
3. Fix them to an iron stick
4. Hold on fire and heat all the sides till deep brown
5. Drop oil from time to time on the fishes
6. Alternative method: Scorch the shrimps on charcoal fire
7. Scale them and wash
8. Smear them with minced green chillies, onions, mustard oil and salt
9. Serve

title: Shrimps with Gourd Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- potatoes
- onions
- water
- cumin
- pepper
- chillies
- coriander
- gourd leaves
- ghee or oil
- cassia leaves
- panch-foron
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Prepare some lobsters and cut in pieces
2. Fry them after smearing with powdered turmeric and salt
3. Brown some potatoes and onions peeled and cut in pieces
4. Add water, salt, powdered turmeric, cumin, pepper, chillies, coriander and some chopped and thoroughly washed tender gourd leaves
5. When fully boiled season the curry with ghee or oil boiled with a few cassia leaves and panch-foron
6. Garam-masalla may be added
7. Leave little soup

title: Shrimps with Gourd Leaves
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- powdered turmeric
- salt
- potatoes
- onions
- water
- cumin
- pepper
- chillies
- coriander
- chopped and thoroughly washed tender gourd leaves
- ghee or oil
- cassia leaves
- panch-foron
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Prepare some lobsters, cut in pieces and fry.
2. Fishes are always to be smeared with powdered turmeric and salt before frying.
3. Then brown some potatoes and onions peeled and cut in pieces.
4. Add water, salt, powdered turmeric, cumin, pepper, chillies, coriander and some chopped and thoroughly washed tender gourd leaves.
5. When fully boiled season the curry with ghee or oil boiled with a few cassia leaves and panch-foron.
6. Garam-masalla may be added.
7. Leave little soup.

title: Shrimps with Green Jack-Fruit
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of jack-fruit 1 seer
- potatoes 1/2 seer
- shrimps 1 seer
- oil 1 powa
- onions 1 chhatak
- ginger 2.5 tolas
- salt 2.5 tolas
- coriander 1/2 tola
- cumin 2 tolas
- pepper 1/2 tola
- turmeric 1.5 tola
- ghee
- garam-masalla
- chillies
- cassia leaves
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare a green and tender jack-fruit, cut in small pieces, boil in water mixed with a little pasted turmeric and drain off the water.
2. Prepare the shrimps, cut in suitable pieces (keep entire if small) and fry.
3. Paste together the onions, ginger, coriander, cumin, pepper, turmeric and a few chillies.
4. Brown some pieces of potatoes and then add the boiled pieces of jack-fruit and stir for a few minutes.
5. Add the pasted spices, stir again for a time and add a little water.
6. On boiling add fish.
7. Season the curry with ghee boiled with chillies and cassia leaves.
8. Add ghee and pasted garam-masalla.
9. Keep a little very thick soup.

title: Shrimps with Turnips
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips 1/2 seer
- potatoes 1 powa
- small shrimps 1/2 seer
- oil 1/8 powa
- ginger 1/2 chhatak
- turmeric 1 tola
- cumin 1 tola
- pepper 1 tola
- salt 1/8 tolas
- panch-foron
- cassia leaves
- chillies
- ghee
- garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Peel the turnips and potatoes, cut in small pieces and brown them.
2. Prepare the shrimps and fry them.
3. Add water and the spices pasted together to the fried shrimps.
4. On boiling add salt and the browned vegetables.
5. When fully boiled season the curry with ghee boiled with panch-foron, cassia leaves and chillies.
6. Add pasted garam-masalla and boil a few minutes more.
7. Keep a little very thick soup.

title: Siddha (Boiled) Puli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Boiling water
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of powdered rice with boiling water, adding the necessary amount of water at a time
2. Make medium balls
3. Stuff them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
4. Give them the shape of an almond
5. Boil in boiling water till they all float

title: Siddha Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- three seers of water
- one seer of rice

## Directions

1. Heat water in a pan and when it begins to boil pour the rice, washed clean, in it and close the lid
2. After a few minutes when the boiling water becomes practically unmanageable let the surplus steam escape from the pan either by removing the lid entirely or by pushing it to one side
3. After about fifteen minutes pick a few grains with a spoon and feel if they are soft and well-boiled
4. When they are so, take the pan down from the oven and remove the excess whitish starchy water either by placing the pan upside down upon the lid or by tilting it on one side and carefully decanting the water out keeping the rice within
5. Re-heat for a very short time to dry up the sticking moisture

title: Simple Ghee Bhat (Boiled Rice)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Peshwari or Badkhani rice
- ghee
- turmeric
- cassia leaves
- cloves
- small cardamoms
- cinnamons
- pistachios
- almonds
- raisins
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly any quantity of Peshwari or Badkhani rice and dry them in the sun.
2. Fry the rice slightly with the necessary quantity of ghee in a pan.
3. Add the fried rice in a sufficient quantity of boiling water.
4. When the rice are well boiled and tough enough, decant off the extra water.
5. Heat a sufficient quantity of ghee in a pan and fry in it a little pasted turmeric and some cassia leaves, cloves, small cardamoms, cinnamons, pistachios, almonds and raisins, all entire.
6. Peel pistachios and almonds and cut into pieces if desired after moistening them in water.
7. Pour down the boiled rice over it, add salt and stir the mass for a few minutes.
8. Take it down from the oven.

title: Simple Khechuri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal weights of fine rice, atap or siddha
- Equal weights of fried moog-dal
- Sliced onions
- Potatoes, fried or raw, if desired
- Powdered turmeric 2 tolas per seer of rice
- Powdered cumin 1 tola per seer of rice
- Salt 2 1/2 tolas per seer of rice
- A few cloves
- Seeds of a few cardamoms
- Some red chillies
- Cassia leaves
- Ghee
- Ghee prepared from cow-milk

## Directions

1. Wash equal weights of fine rice, atap or siddha, and fried moog-dal, and drop them in boiling water.
2. After a few minutes add sliced onions and potatoes, fried or raw, if desired.
3. When the rice are half boiled add powdered turmeric, powdered cumin and salt.
4. Take the cooking-pot off the fire when the khechuri is fully boiled and is tolerably thick.
5. Fry a few cloves, the seeds of a few cardamoms, some red chillies broken in two, and cassia leaves in ghee in another pot.
6. Pour in the khechuri when the smell of the spices comes out, and stir.
7. Take down after a few minutes.
8. Serve while hot with plenty of ghee, prepared from cow-milk, on the khechuri.
9. Use slow heat and constant stirring especially at the finishing time.

title: Singara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled and peeled potatoes
- salt
- black cumins
- powders of chillies
- garam-masalla
- aniseed
- 1 chhatak ghee
- 1/2 seer of fine flour
- water
- meat

## Directions

1. Break some boiled and peeled potatoes and mix with salt, a few entire black cumins, powders of chillies, garam-masalla and aniseed
2. Mix 1 chhatak ghee and a little salt with half seer of fine flour
3. Prepare a stiff dough with water
4. Make very small balls of it so that when rolled make discs of diameter 3 to 4 inches
5. Form hollow cones of these
6. Stuff each with a little spiced potato and close up
7. Fry in plenty of ghee
8. Small pieces of meat fried with spices may be substituted for potatoes

title: Sir Watkin's Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- candied peel
- four ounces of suet, finely minced
- four ounces fine white crumbs
- one table-spoonful of flour
- three ounces of sugar
- two ounces of apricot jam
- a liqueur-glass of curacao
- one dessert-spoonful of milk
- four fresh eggs

## Directions

1. When thoroughly mixed, put the ingredients into a buttered mould
2. Steam for three hours

title: Sir Watkin's Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of chopped candied peel
- four ounces of suet finely minced
- four ounces fine white crumbs
- one table-spoonful of flour
- three ounces of sugar
- two ounces of apricot jam
- a liqueur-glass of curacao
- one dessert-spoonful of milk
- four fresh eggs
- butter
- half a pot of apricot jam
- half a pint of water
- a glass of brown sherry or Madeira

## Directions

1. Mix together in a bowl two ounces of chopped candied peel, four ounces of suet finely minced, four ounces fine white crumbs, one table-spoonful of flour, three ounces of sugar, two ounces of apricot jam, a liqueur-glass of curacao, one dessert-spoonful of milk, and four fresh eggs
2. When thoroughly mixed, put the ingredients into a buttered mould, and steam for three hours
3. To make apricot sauce: Put half a pot of apricot jam into a sauce-pan with half a pint of water, and a glass of brown sherry or Madeira
4. Boil together, stirring well, then strain, and serve with the pudding

title: Sitabhog
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 powa ghee
- flour 3 powas
- 1 seer hard chhana or khowa kheer
- milk or water
- ghee for frying
- sugar syrup
- kasir chini

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 1/4 powa of ghee with 3 powas of flour of superfine quality
2. Make a thin paste of it with 1 seer of hard chhana or khowa kheer pasted with the hands and sufficient quantity of milk or water
3. Hold a finely perforated ladle over a pan of boiling ghee (preferably cow's ghee) of excellent quality
4. Pour the solution in the ladle and shake it up and down when the solution will fall in the ghee in broken streams
5. Take them out before they are even slightly browned
6. Keep them in a syrup of sugar (preferably of kasir chini) previously prepared till saturated

title: Slice of Seer au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice slice of seer fish
- small cupful of maccaroni
- butter
- 1 dessert-spoonful of minced shallot
- 2 table-spoonfuls of grated cheese
- 1 breakfast-cupful of broth
- chablis
- paper
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Flatten out on a board a nice slice of seer fish
2. Prepare a small cupful of maccaroni boiled till tender
3. Butter a small pie-dish
4. Sprinkle a dessert-spoonful of minced shallot over the butter
5. Place a layer of maccaroni on it
6. Dust over the maccaroni a couple of table-spoonfuls of grated cheese
7. Put the slice of seer on the top of the maccaroni
8. Moisten with a breakfast-cupful of broth, in which a little chablis may be mixed
9. Put a little butter on the fish
10. Spread a piece of paper over it
11. Bake for twelve minutes
12. Remove the paper and serve
13. Tomatoes, sliced, may be laid on the top of the fish and baked with it if available

title: Slice of Seer en Papillote
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a slice of seer
- a parboiled carrot
- a parboiled onion
- powdered dried thyme and marjoram
- chopped parsley
- three table-spoonfuls of salad oil
- buttered paper
- a dessert-spoonful of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- a cupful of broth

## Directions

1. Cut a parboiled carrot and one parboiled onion into thin slices
2. Add powdered dried thyme and marjoram and chopped parsley
3. Mix this up with three table-spoonfuls of salad oil
4. Cover your slice of seer with the mixture
5. Wrap the fish carefully in a sheet of buttered paper
6. Bake it for half an hour
7. Remove the paper very carefully and place the slice upon a hot dish
8. Melt a dessert-spoonful of butter and add a dessert-spoonful of flour
9. Add a cupful of broth and the vegetables in which the slice was cooked
10. Boil this for three or four minutes
11. Strain and pour it over the fish

title: Sliced Beckty Fish Fried in Curry Condiments
body:
## Ingredients

- beckty fish
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- salt
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- mustard oil

## Directions

1. Slice, wash, and fry exactly as directed for sliced hilsa fish fried in curry condiments

title: Sliced Hilsa Fish Fried in Curry Condiments
body:
## Ingredients

- ground onions, two teaspoonfuls
- ground chilies, one teaspoonful
- salt, two teaspoonfuls
- ground turmeric, half a teaspoonful
- ground garlic, a quarter of a teaspoonful
- mustard oil, one chittack
- hilsa fish

## Directions

1. Slice a hilsa in the manner directed for a curry
2. Thoroughly clean and wash it with salt
3. Rub into the slices all the ground condiments and the remaining salt
4. Allow them to remain for at least an hour
5. Warm the oil and fry the slices of fish of a very light and bright brown
6. Serve up hot

title: Smoked Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- mango fish, becky, or hilsa
- salt
- bran
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Cut down the back, spread open, and well wash and salt the fish
2. Have a bright charcoal fire, and sprinkle over it some bran, with brown sugar
3. Cover the fire with an open-work bamboo basket, having over it a coarse duster
4. Arrange the fish over the duster, and allow them to smoke
5. When one side has browned, turn and brown the other side
6. As the smoke decreases, add more bran, and fan up the fire
7. A duster thrown over the fish while smoking will facilitate the operation

title: Snake Vegetable in Gravy
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- snake vegetable
- brown gravy
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetable into convenient lengths
2. Boil and drain it
3. Remove its seeds
4. Heat the pieces in a good brown gravy
5. A dust of grated cheese over each piece may be given without fear

title: Snipe Pie
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight snipes
- two pounds of gravy meat
- two pounds of tender rib-meat or undercut beef
- beef gravy
- shallot
- tender beef collops
- strips of bacon
- spiced pepper
- slices of lean bacon, ham, tongue, or Bologna sausage
- paste

## Directions

1. Prepare six snipes as if for roasting for the pie
2. Sacrifice two snipes for the gravy
3. Make a good beef gravy
4. Throw the two snipes into the gravy and simmer slowly
5. Make a puree by pounding the meat of the birds to a paste and blending it with the gravy
6. Rub the pie-dish with a shallot
7. Fill the bottom of the pie-dish with little collops of tender beef rolled with strips of bacon
8. Dust spiced pepper over each layer until all beef is used
9. Lay the six snipes on the surface of the beef with slices of lean bacon, ham, tongue, or Bologna sausage between each bird
10. Garnish as usual
11. Baptize the pie with the puree, saving some for the final process
12. Cover with a nice light paste and bake

title: Sole Fillets with Poor Man's Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of nice soles
- egg
- minced parsley
- minced shallot
- broth made from sole bones and trimmings
- a spoonful of chablis or sauterne
- a coffee-cupful of stock
- a couple of finely minced onions
- butter
- a coffee-cupful of the gravy in which the fillets were cooked
- a spoonful of vinegar
- sauce blanche

## Directions

1. Fillet a couple of nice soles
2. Brush the fillets over on one side with egg
3. Dust over that some minced parsley and shallot
4. Roll up your fillets, securing each in shape with a tiny skewer
5. Simmer these fillets carefully in a broth made from their own bones and trimmings, assisted by a spoonful of chablis or sauterne, and a coffee-cupful of stock
6. When done, place the fillets on a hot dish and cover them up
7. For the sauce: Fry a couple of finely minced onions in butter till they assume a golden tint
8. Stir into this, by degrees, a coffee-cupful of the gravy in which the fillets were cooked
9. When boiling, sharpen this by adding a spoonful of vinegar
10. Strain and pour it into another sauce-pan by degrees in which some good sauce blanche has been made
11. Mix thoroughly over the fire, and serve round the fillets

title: Sole Fillets With Poor Man's Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of nice soles
- egg
- minced parsley
- minced shallot
- broth made from sole bones and trimmings
- one spoonful of chablis or sauterne
- one coffee-cupful of stock
- a couple of finely minced onions

## Directions

1. Fillet a couple of nice soles
2. Brush the fillets over on one side with egg
3. Dust over that some minced parsley and shallot
4. Roll up your fillets, securing each in shape with a tiny skewer
5. Simmer these fillets carefully in a broth made from their own bones and trimmings, assisted by a spoonful of chablis or sauterne and a coffee-cupful of stock
6. When done, test them with a pointed skewer and place the fillets on a hot dish
7. Cover them up
8. For the sauce, fry a couple of finely minced onions

title: Solid Curd
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- sugar
- sour curd

## Directions

1. Boil milk till it is reduced to half.
2. Pour it in an earthen, stone or porcelain pot.
3. Add sugar and a little sour curd when slightly warm and mix up.
4. Place the pot on another containing hot water.
5. The milk will be transformed into solid curd in about twelve hours if kept undisturbed.

title: Souffle de Volaille
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- the nicest meat of a full sized cold roast chicken
- two ounces of butter
- a table-spoonful of minced ham
- about half a pint of rich white sauce
- almonds
- pepper
- salt
- broth made of the bones and trimmings
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Mince the chicken meat very finely
2. Put it in a frying-pan with two ounces of butter and minced ham
3. Toss till the butter is absorbed
4. Make about half a pint of rich white sauce flavoured with almonds, pepper, salt, and broth from the bones
5. Throw the mince into a mortar and pound to a paste, moistening with the sauce
6. Pass the puree through a sieve
7. Stir in the yolks of four eggs lightly
8. Add last the whites of the eggs whipped to a stiff froth
9. Pour into a souffle tin with a paper round the top
10. Bake in a very quick oven
11. Serve quickly in the tin with a white napkin pinned round it

title: Soup Royal
body:
## Ingredients

- shin of beef
- calf's head
- calf's tongue
- pepper
- salt
- soup herbs
- lemon
- red carrots
- green peas
- boiled potatoes
- turnip
- Patna onion
- roasted and ground split peas or gram dal
- orange
- water
- French prunes
- port wine
- brain cakes
- egg balls
- forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Take a shin of beef, the best parts of meat cut off from a calf's head, and the tongue
2. Cut the beef into small particles, but leave the tongue and the meat from the calf's head whole
3. Add pepper and salt and boil well, clearing the scum as it rises
4. Remove the tongue and the meat of the calf's head when sufficiently tender
5. Continue to boil the shin of beef until it is well dissolved
6. Strain it and cut away all the fat
7. Put it up again with plenty of soup herbs and more salt and pepper if necessary
8. Boil it well up
9. Squeeze into the soup the juice of half a lemon and skim it well
10. Strain it once more and set it aside
11. Cut the tongue into slices of an eighth of an inch thick
12. Trim them into the shape of large diamonds and set aside
13. Cut up the meat of the calf's head into one-inch squares and strips of an inch and a half long and half an inch wide
14. Add to these a few ready-fried circular flat brain cakes
15. Make also a few egg balls and forcemeat balls, and after cooking, add them to the rest of the meat, tongue, etc., and set aside
16. Take four red carrots, one pound of green peas, half a pound of boiled potatoes, one large turnip, one large Patna onion, a quarter of a pound of roasted and ground split peas or gram dal, some soup herbs, pepper, and salt, the pulp of one orange, and the peels of half an orange and half a lemon
17. Put these into a stewpan with water sufficient to cover the whole
18. Boil them thoroughly, skimming all the while
19. When perfectly dissolved, turn them out into a colander and allow all the water to drain away
20. Turn the contents of the colander into a sieve and pass the vegetables, etc., through it, rejecting all such as will not pass
21. Add the whole or a part of the strained vegetables to the soup, which should not be thicker in consistency than a good thick potato soup
22. Stew one dozen good French prunes in a claret-glassful of port wine
23. Strain through a sieve, rejecting stones, etc., and add the strained portion to the soup
24. Boil the whole and strain it once more
25. Add to it all the forcemeat and egg balls, the brain cakes, tongue, etc., and serve up, adding to it more salt, wine, or sauce, if needed

title: Soupe a L'Oignon
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of Bombay onions
- flour
- butter
- water
- pepper
- salt
- croutons of fried bread
- Grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Slice a couple of Bombay onions
2. powder them well with flour
3. let them fry awhile in a stew-pan with plenty of butter
4. before they begin to brown at all, add water, pepper, and salt
5. let the whole boil till the onions are well done
6. serve with croutons of fried bread
7. Grated Parmesan should accompany

title: Soupe aux Choux
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cabbage or two
- a good sized slice of bacon
- some slices of a Bologna sausage
- sweet herbs
- a clove of garlic
- pepper
- a little spice
- water
- croutons of fried bread

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into quarters
2. put them into a sauce-pan with a good sized slice of bacon
3. add some slices of a Bologna sausage
4. add a bag containing sweet herbs, a clove of garlic, pepper, and a little spice
5. add water enough to cover the whole
6. let the soup simmer till the cabbage is done
7. serve with croutons of fried bread

title: Sour Fish
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish 1 seer
- Oil 1/4 powa
- Salt 1 1/2 tola
- Ripe tamarind 1/2 powa
- Turmeric 1 tola
- Mustard 1/4 tola
- Chillies
- Sugar 1 chhatak

## Directions

1. Make pastes of turmeric and mustard separately
2. Mix the tamarind as far as possible in about 3 powas of water, strain out the acidulated water and throw away the refuse
3. Mix half the turmeric and half the salt with the fishes and fry them in oil
4. Boil a little more oil in another pan with the chillies broken in two and pour in the tamarind water
5. Add turmeric, mustard and salt
6. On boiling add fish
7. Add sugar, if you want to, a little before taking down
8. Keep soup as desired

title: Sour Meat
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chhatak of ripe tamarind without seeds
- 1 powa of water
- salt
- powdered turmeric
- ginger-juice
- onion-juice
- powdered pepper
- mustard oil
- 1/2 seer of small pieces of meat
- broken chillies
- cassia leaves
- ghee
- 1/2 seer of water
- browned pieces of vegetables
- sugar

## Directions

1. Keep 1 chhatak of ripe tamarind without seeds immersed in 1 powa of water for about 15 minutes
2. Squeeze them in the water and throw away the refuse
3. Mix salt, powdered turmeric, ginger-juice, onion-juice, a little powdered pepper and mustard oil with 1/2 seer of small pieces of meat
4. Fry broken chillies and cassia leaves in enough ghee
5. Fry the meat in it for about 15 minutes
6. Add about 1/2 seer of water and when this water nearly dries up add the tamarind solution
7. Add browned pieces of vegetables if desired
8. Add sugar as desired about 5 minutes before taking down
9. Keep as thick soup

title: Souvarienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of cake
- whites of three eggs
- an ounce of finely sifted sugar
- two ounces of chocolate
- a pint of boiling milk
- half a pint of cream
- a tea-spoonful of vanilla essence
- the yolks of ten eggs
- two ounces of sugar
- half an ounce of dissolved gelatine
- a coffee-cupful of whipped cream
- chopped pistachio nuts, almonds, sugar plums or crystallized sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the slices of cake the length of the depth of the mould, the eighth of an inch thick, and an inch and a half wide.
2. Cut a circular piece the size of the bottom of the mould.
3. Make a cement with the whites of three eggs, and an ounce of finely sifted sugar.
4. Arrange the slices round the side of the mould slightly overlapping one another, and cement them firmly together, fixing their ends to the circular top placed at the bottom of the mould.
5. When the cement dries, the case will be quite firm.
6. Choose a mould for the ice slightly smaller than this case, so that the latter may cover it nicely.
7. When firmly set, the cake case may be turned out upon a dish.
8. Brush its outside over with some thin cement, and sprinkle over with chopped pistachio nuts, almonds, sugar plums or crystallized sugar.
9. For the ice: Dissolve two ounces of chocolate in a pint of boiling milk, when quite smooth, and cold, add half a pint of cream, a tea-spoonful of vanilla essence and the yolks of ten eggs.
10. Make a custard of the mixture adding two ounces of sugar.
11. Stir in half an ounce of dissolved gelatine while the custard is warm.
12. Whip it briskly and put it into the freezer.
13. Freeze, mixing into the half-frozen custard a coffee-cupful of whipped cream.
14. Turn it out when ready, cover with the case, and serve.

title: Spiced Beef
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice brisket or fleshy piece of the flank
- salt
- brine
- half an ounce of cloves
- half an ounce of pepper corns (ground)
- half an ounce of mace
- a tablespoonful of finely minced parsley
- half a tablespoonful of marjoram
- chopped rind of a nice yellow lime, peeled very finely
- a dust of Nepaul pepper
- a couple of glasses of Madeira

## Directions

1. Remove all bones.
2. Rub the joint well with salt, and let it lie in the brine for about two days.
3. Spread it out flat and dry it.
4. Lay a coating of the spices over the inside in the same manner as you would spread jam over paste before making a rolypoly pudding.
5. Roll up the brisket and tie it tightly with a string.
6. Stew it patiently until tender.
7. Set it under a weight when done.
8. When cold, remove the string and trim the joint neatly.
9. Glaze it, and when the glaze is dry, the beef may be considered ready for the table.

title: Spiced Collared Brisket
body:
## Ingredients

- brisket of beef
- salt
- saltpetre
- suckur or brown sugar
- lime-juice
- hot spices
- pepper
- chutnee
- sauces
- skin of fat
- coarse pastry

## Directions

1. The process is the same as Collared Brisket
2. If the beef is required to keep for any lengthened time double the quantity of salt
3. Keep the beef in the brine for seventy-two hours
4. Add hot spices, pepper, chutnee, and sauces
5. Roll the beef
6. Pack it in the skin of fat, then in a coarse pastry instead of in plaintain-leaf
7. Bake it in a baker's oven

title: Spiced Pepper
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce dried thyme leaves
- one ounce dried marjoram
- one ounce dried savory
- one ounce nutmeg
- one ounce cloves
- one ounce whole black pepper
- one ounce Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Pound the above ingredients thoroughly in a mortar
2. When ground to powder, pass it through a fine sieve
3. Bottle it and cork it down securely

title: Spiced Pepper
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- savory
- ounce nutmeg
- ounce cloves
- ounce whole black pepper
- ounce Nepaul pepper

## Directions

1. Pound the above ingredients thoroughly in a mortar
2. When ground to powder, pass it through a fine sieve
3. Bottle it and cork it down securely

title: Spinach
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of spinach leaves
- one ounce of butter
- three quarters of an ounce of flour
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of sugar
- a coffee-cupful of milk, gravy, or stock
- a breakfast-cupful of milk or stock
- butter or cream or the yolk of an egg dissolved in a little milk

## Directions

1. Having selected two pounds of leaves carefully, wash them well
2. Blanch them by plunging them for a couple of minutes in scalding water
3. Drain and chop them up
4. Put into a stew-pan one ounce of butter, three quarters of an ounce of flour, a pinch of salt, and one of sugar
5. Stir this over the fire for three minutes
6. Add the spinach leaves
7. Stir round for five minutes
8. Moisten with a coffee-cupful of milk, gravy, or stock
9. Stir for two minutes more
10. Add a breakfast-cupful of the milk or stock
11. Stir for five minutes and take the pan from the fire
12. Mingle a little butter with the spinach, or give it a spoonful of cream, or the yolk of an egg dissolved in a little milk
13. Turn it out upon a good hot dish
14. Garnish it with sippets of fried bread, fleurons of puff pastry, or short-bread biscuits specially baked for the dish
15. Serve

title: Spinach and Anchovy Butter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of good handfuls of spinach
- six full sized anchovies from the tin
- butter
- curled parsley sufficient to fill a table-spoon generously
- capers sufficient to fill a tea-spoon, heaped up

## Directions

1. Boil a couple of good handfuls of spinach, drain them thoroughly, pass the leaves through the hair sieve, and save all the pulp so obtained, in a bowl.
2. Take six full sized anchovies from the tin, wipe them free from oil, pick out their back bones, pass them through the hair sieve, and save the pulp.
3. Mince as finely as possible sufficient curled parsley to fill a table-spoon generously.
4. Mince also as finely as possible capers sufficient to fill a tea-spoon, heaped up.
5. First colour the butter by working into it, as lightly as you can, enough spinach-greening to secure the tint you require, then add the other things by degrees, and when thoroughly incorporated, trim the butter into a tasteful mould, or sundry pretty patlets, and set it in the ice-box.

title: Spinach Bouchees
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach puree
- cream
- nutmeg
- puff pastry
- buttered egg

## Directions

1. Prepare a puree of spinach as described at page 146, drain it very dry, and then moisten it with cream, adding a very little nutmeg
2. Make eight bouchees (miniature oyster patty shapes) of puff pastry
3. Fill each bouchee with spinach puree
4. Give them a cap of buttered egg
5. Heat up in the oven, and serve on a napkin

title: Standard Fritter Batter
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- two table-spoonfuls of brandy
- one table-spoonful of the best salad oil
- four or five table-spoonfuls of cold water
- three table-spoonfuls of flour
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of three eggs with two table-spoonfuls of brandy, one table-spoonful of the best salad oil, and four or five table-spoonfuls of cold water.
2. Incorporate with this mixture three table-spoonfuls of flour and a salt-spoonful of salt.
3. Work this, with care, to a smooth paste, and continue to beat it for at least ten minutes.
4. If the batter appear too thick, add a little water until its consistency be satisfactory, i.e.: it should cover the spoon when lifted out of it with a coating about the eighth of an inch thick.
5. Take the whites of the three eggs, and whip them to a stiff froth.
6. Stir this into your batter at the time of using.

title: Steamed Fowl with Oyster Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- Bombay onion
- bacon
- butter
- flour
- oysters
- pepper corns
- lime
- salt
- Harvey sauce
- cream
- milk
- egg yolk
- maccaroni

## Directions

1. Truss the fowl.
2. Place a big Bombay onion inside the bird, with a couple of slices of bacon.
3. Sew up the vent, pin a strip of bacon over the breast.
4. Set the bird in the pan (without any water or gravy round it) carefully securing the lid with paste if necessary.
5. Immerse the vessel containing the fowl in the fish kettle or any larger pan full of cold water, and set it to boil.
6. Cook it slowly for upwards of an hour without removing the lid which should be scrupulously kept closed.
7. After about an hour and a quarter (after the water came to the boil), take the fowl out, and place it at once on a hot dish well covered up.
8. Strain off the gravy that has been drawn from the fowl and save it for the oyster sauce.
9. For the oyster sauce: Mix an ounce of butter with an ounce of flour at the bottom of a sauce-pan, add the fowl gravy, the liquor from a tin of oysters, and the beards of the oysters cut off (save the oysters themselves separately).
10. Throw in some pepper corns, the peel of a lime, salt to taste, and as the mixture boils, a table-spoonful of Harvey sauce.
11. After it has come to the boil, strain the sauce carefully, add the oysters you saved, and heat it up again.
12. Take it off the fire and pour in a coffee-cupful of boiling cream (or milk in which the yolk of an egg has been stirred, and heated up to the consistency of thin custard).
13. Serve the sauce poured over the fowl dished upon a cradle of well boiled maccaroni.

title: Stewed Cabbage (Chou au Jus)
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- savoy or good cabbage
- salt
- water
- thick slice of bacon
- shallot
- parsley
- marjoram
- thyme
- pinch of sugar
- pepper
- salt
- gravy

## Directions

1. Take a savoy or any good sort of cabbage, pick it carefully, and let it soak in salt and water for an hour
2. If a large head, divide it into quarters, and even a small head had better be similarly treated
3. When satisfied that the cabbage is thoroughly clean, either steam the quarters, or plunge them into boiling hot-water, and after boiling for a quarter of an hour, take them out and drain them
4. Mince a thick slice of bacon, and a little shallot, parsley, marjoram, and thyme, with a pinch of sugar, and pepper and salt to taste
5. Put all in a stew-pan, and set it on the fire
6. As soon as the bacon melts, lay your cabbage quarters in it, and pour round them sufficient gravy to half cover them
7. Let this simmer gently till the cabbage is done
8. Lift out the quarters

title: Stewed Cucumbers, Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- pint of gravy
- slice of lean ham
- onion
- cloves
- parsley
- thyme
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Pare the cucumbers, and let them lie in vinegar and water with a little salt in it
2. drain them
3. put them into a saucepan with a pint of gravy, a slice of lean ham, an onion stuck with one or two cloves, and a bunch of parsley and thyme
4. let them stew, closely covered, till tender
5. Take out the cucumbers, strain and thicken the gravy with a piece of butter rolled in flour
6. boil it up, and pour it over the cucumbers

title: Stewed Eels
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of eels
- two ounces of butter
- a Bombay onion
- half a pint of claret
- half a pint of stock
- a clove of garlic
- a carrot
- sweet herbs
- salt
- spiced pepper
- the rind of a lime
- sugar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Skin a couple of eels, clean, parboil, and divide them into two inch fillets for stewing
2. Put into your stew-pan two ounces of butter, with a Bombay onion sliced
3. Stir over the fire for five minutes
4. Add half a pint of claret, and half a pint of stock, with a clove of garlic, a carrot cut up, and a bag of sweet herbs
5. Boil this for ten minutes, stirring it well with your wooden spoon
6. Throw in a salt-spoonful of salt, a tea-spoonful of spiced pepper, the rind of a lime, and place the pieces of eel in the midst
7. Simmer this for half an hour
8. Arrange the pieces of the fish upon the hot dish, strain the gravy rapidly, thicken it
9. Re-heat it almost to boiling point, add a pinch of sugar, and a table-spoonful of chopped parsley
10. Pour it round the fish

title: Stuffed Cucumbers
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumber
- pounded meat
- bread-crumb
- pepper
- salt
- minced shallot
- parsley
- egg
- white of egg
- gravy

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumber
2. slice off a piece at one end
3. pick out the seeds with a marrow-spoon
4. stuff the hollow thus formed with a farce made of pounded meat and bread-crumb, two-thirds of the former to one of the latter
5. Season the farce with pepper and salt, a little minced shallot and parsley, and bind it with a well whipped egg
6. fix on the end you removed with white of egg, and secure it with tape
7. The cucumber can now be baked, or gently simmered in some gravy which should be thickened and poured over it when done

title: Stuffed Eggs with Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- four large Jerusalem artichokes
- melted butter
- anchovy butter
- maitre d'hotel butter or prawn butter

## Directions

1. Boil four eggs hard and put them into a basin of cold water
2. When cold, cut them in halves crosswise with a dessert-knife dipped in melted butter
3. Slice off a piece from each rounded end so that the halves will sit upright
4. Prepare the yolks as previously described
5. Boil four large Jerusalem artichokes until just done and not overdone
6. Lift them out of the water and let them get cold
7. Trim with a silver dessert-knife two thick flat slices from each artichoke, each capable of holding a half egg
8. Butter the eight slices and lay them upon a flat silver dish well buttered
9. Set half an egg upon each of them
10. Put on the top of each egg a little pat of anchovy butter
11. Set the dish in the oven till the steam rises freely and serve piping hot
12. If desired, pour melted maitre d'hotel or prawn butter over them

title: Stuffed Murrel
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- murrel fish
- turkey forcemeat (page 108)
- chicken stock
- white wine (hock, chablis, or sauterne)
- onion
- vegetables
- maitre d'hotel butter

## Directions

1. After having been carefully cleansed and trimmed, stuff the fish with turkey forcemeat
2. Sew it up and trim it in a circular shape with its tail in its mouth
3. Bake it in a pie-dish surrounded by chicken stock about half an inch deep
4. A glass of any white wine like hock, chablis, or sauterne may be put into the stock, an onion also, and any vegetables you can spare
5. The fish should be basted every now and then
6. When it has absorbed the gravy and seems soft, take it out of the oven
7. Put a pat of maitre d'hotel butter on the top of it
8. Serve in its own dish with a napkin folded round it
9. A good sized murrel will take from twenty minutes to half an hour in baking

title: Stuffed Oyster Fritters
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- brown sauce
- minced mushroom
- shallot
- truffle
- egg yolks
- lime juice
- batter

## Directions

1. Split each oyster open almost as wide as a kidney
2. Toss on the fire in butter with pepper, salt and a spoonful of rich brown sauce, a dessert-spoonful each of minced mushroom, shallot, and truffle
3. Thicken this with the yolks of two eggs
4. Give it the juice of a lime and let it get cold
5. Put a small allowance of this in each oyster
6. Shut the sides together
7. Dip it in the batter
8. Fry immediately

title: Stuffing for Boiled Turkey, Goose, or Duck
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of beef suet
- crumb of a thick slice of stale bread
- soup herb
- Patna onions
- lemon-peel
- grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of white pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 6 oysters
- 1 anchovy
- minced ham or tongue
- 1 chittack or 2 ounces of butter
- stock

## Directions

1. Mince a quarter of a pound of beef suet, and grate fine the crumb of a thick slice of stale bread
2. Add a good quantity of soup herb, finely sliced and chopped Patna onions, lemon-peel, some grated nutmeg, a teaspoonful of white pepper, a teaspoonful of salt, half a dozen oysters, and an anchovy, or in the absence of anchovies a little minced ham or tongue
3. Melt down one chittack or two ounces of butter
4. Throw in the above ingredients and warm up well
5. Moisten with stock, and then stuff the bird

title: Stuffing for Hare or Kid
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- grated bread
- fat bacon
- bit of butter the size of a walnut
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped lemon thyme
- parsley
- 1 egg beaten

## Directions

1. Parboil the liver, and mince it
2. Add an equal quantity of grated bread, double the quantity of fat bacon chopped, and a bit of butter the size of a walnut
3. Season with pepper, salt, nutmeg, chopped lemon thyme, and parsley
4. Bind with an egg beaten

title: Stuffing for Roast Duck
body:
## Ingredients

- heart or inside of a young tender cabbage
- pepper
- salt
- 3 cloves of garlic
- 2 chittacks or 4 ounces of butter free of water

## Directions

1. Slice into fine long strips or ribbons as much of the heart or inside of a young tender cabbage as will suffice for stuffing
2. Wash and dry it through a colander, and shake it up in a napkin, without crushing or destroying the crispness of the leaves
3. Take for the stuffing as much only as will suffice to stuff the number of ducks intended to be dressed
4. Add for each duck a teaspoonful of finely-pounded pepper, and one of salt, with three cloves of garlic, and two chittacks or four ounces of butter free of water
5. Stuff your birds and bake or roast as you please

title: Stuffing for Roast Pig, Roast Kid, Fillets of Veal, and Duck
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 boiled potatoes
- 1 chittack or 2 ounces of butter free of water
- 2 white Patna onions
- finely chopped suet
- finely-grated crumbs of a slice of stale bread
- 1 teaspoonful of ground pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- soup herbs
- 1 dessertspoonful of tomato or tapp sauce
- stock or gravy of the giblets

## Directions

1. Break up, but not mash, six boiled potatoes with one chittack or two ounces of butter free of water
2. Cut into fine slices two white Patna onions
3. Take a little finely chopped suet, finely-grated crumbs of a slice of stale bread, a teaspoonful of ground pepper, a teaspoonful of salt, all kinds of soup herbs, and a dessertspoonful of tomato or tapp sauce
4. Add a little of the stock or gravy of the giblets, &c., and stuff the pig, kid, or bird

title: Stuffing for Roast Turkey or Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 Barcelona nuts
- 12 Jordan almonds
- 2 chittacks or 4 ounces of butter free of water
- 4 tablespoonfuls of finely-sliced onions
- 12 oysters
- 6 boiled potatoes
- pork or beef sausage
- fresh lemon rind
- crumb of a slice of stale bread
- garden herbs
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Break, blanch, and slice up very fine twenty-five Barcelona nuts and a dozen Jordan almonds, and set aside
2. Fry, in two chittacks or four ounces of butter free of water, four tablespoonfuls of finely-sliced onions
3. Add to it one dozen oysters, half a dozen boiled potatoes broken up small, but not mashed, a pork or beef sausage broken up small, the rind of a fresh lemon finely sliced and chopped, the crumb of a slice of stale bread grated fine, some garden herbs, grated nutmeg

title: Stuffing for Roast Turkey or Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-five Barcelona nuts
- a dozen Jordan almonds
- two chittacks or four ounces of butter free of water
- four tablespoonfuls of finely-sliced onions
- one dozen oysters
- half a dozen boiled potatoes
- a pork or beef sausage
- the rind of a fresh lemon
- the crumb of a slice of stale bread
- some garden herbs
- grated nutmeg
- finely-pounded pepper
- salt
- stock
- the juice of a lemon
- a little mushroom catsup
- port wine
- an egg

## Directions

1. Break, blanch, and slice up very fine twenty-five Barcelona nuts and a dozen Jordan almonds, and set aside
2. Fry, in two chittacks or four ounces of butter free of water, four tablespoonfuls of finely-sliced onions
3. Add to it one dozen oysters, half a dozen boiled potatoes broken up small, but not mashed, a pork or beef sausage broken up small, the rind of a fresh lemon finely sliced and chopped, the crumb of a slice of stale bread grated fine, some garden herbs, grated nutmeg, plenty of finely-pounded pepper, and salt to taste
4. Add the sliced nuts, and as much stock as will cover the whole of the mixture
5. Allow it to simmer over a slow fire until it is reduced to the consistency of stuffing
6. Add the juice of a lemon and a little mushroom catsup and port wine
7. Fix the stuffing with an egg and stuff the bird

title: Stuffing Peculiar for Fowls Only
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 boiled potatoes
- 1 or 2 chittacks or 2 ounces of butter free of water
- pepper
- salt
- grated bread-crumb
- 8 or 10 olives stoned
- stock or gravy

## Directions

1. Take four boiled potatoes; break them into pieces while hot
2. Add a chittack or two ounces of butter free of water, some pepper and salt, a little grated bread-crumb, and some eight or ten olives stoned, and cut or chopped small
3. Moisten with a little stock or gravy before stuffing the fowls

title: Sukta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Varieties of vegetables, as jhinga, potato, patol, brinjal, radish, beans, figs, green bananas, bori (dried cakes of pasted pulse)
- Kernel of a ripe cocoa-nut scratched out
- Sajina khara, kachu
- Tender jute leaves and neem leaves, uch-chhe, karala, palta
- Oil
- Powdered turmeric
- Pasted ginger
- Salt
- Powdered mustard
- Pepper
- Cumin
- Ghee
- Mustard seeds
- Milk or sugar

## Directions

1. Brown the boris in oil first and keep them aside
2. Fry the bitter vegetables in sufficient oil and add the other pieces of peeled vegetables
3. When everything is properly browned by constant stirring, add water with very little powdered turmeric and pasted ginger, and sufficient quantities of salt, powdered mustard, pepper and cumin
4. Add the browned boris when the mass is boiled
5. Season it with some ghee heated with a few mustard seeds
6. Add a little milk or sugar if desired
7. Take down with a quantity of soup
8. Add a little thin solution of flour if the soup is too thin

title: Sulphur Pastiles For Meat Preservation
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight parts powdered sulphur
- one and a half parts powdered charcoal
- a quarter part powdered saltpetre
- gum water

## Directions

1. Mix the sulphur, charcoal, and saltpetre together
2. Add just enough gum water to make pastiles
3. Shape them like pyramids
4. Dry them in the sun
5. Suspend the meat in an air-tight box furnished with hooks, a closely fitting door, and all crevices filled with putty and pasted over with strips of strong paper
6. Place two or three pastiles below the meat
7. Light the pastiles
8. Close the door securely and leave for two or three hours
9. The meat will keep perfectly for thirty-six hours and be found deliciously tender

title: Summer Beverage
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts barley-water
- juice and rind of one lemon
- honey, capillaire, or sugar

## Directions

1. Pour while hot two quarts of barley-water on the juice and rind of a lemon very thinly cut
2. Add honey, capillaire, or sugar according to taste
3. Let it stand one hour and strain

title: Sweet Long Plum Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- long plums
- salt
- fresh chilies
- cloves of garlic
- ginger finely sliced
- peppercorns
- vinegar
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make according to the Long Plum Pickle recipe
2. Add a syrup in the proportion of a quarter of a pound of sugar to every quart of vinegar
3. Add a few sticks of cinnamon

title: Sweet Mango Chutnee
body:
## Ingredients

- a hundred green mangoes, peeled and sliced
- two seers of tamarinds stoned
- the syrup of six pounds of sugar boiled in three quarts of vinegar
- one tablespoonful of finely-pounded cinnamon
- two pounds of salt
- two pounds of sliced ginger
- two pounds of cleaned and picked raisins
- three quarts of vinegar
- one dessertspoonful of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the mangoes, cut them into fine slices, and steep them in salt for thirty-six hours
2. Drain away the salt water, and boil them in the three quarts of vinegar
3. When cool, remove them into a preserving-pan, mix in all the condiments and other ingredients
4. Allow the whole to simmer for half an hour, pouring in the syrup gradually, and mixing all the time, until the vinegar and syrup have been absorbed, and the chutnee has acquired the desired consistency
5. Bottle and cork when perfectly cold

title: Sweet Mango Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- hundred green mangoes
- salt
- a seer or two pounds of sliced ginger
- half a seer of chilies finely sliced
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter a hundred green mangoes
2. Steep them in salt for thirty-six hours
3. Drain off the salt water
4. Wipe the mangoes dry
5. Put them into a preserving-pan, with a seer or two pounds of sliced ginger, and half a seer of chilies finely sliced
6. Pour in a syrup made of sugar and vinegar (half a seer of the former to two quarts of the latter)
7. Allow the whole to simmer for ten to fifteen minutes
8. Bottle when quite cold

title: Sweet Potatoes
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into thin slices.
2. Fry with salt as usual in oil.

title: Sweet Puli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Boiling water
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation
- Milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of powdered rice with boiling water, adding the necessary amount of water at a time
2. Make small balls
3. Stuff them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
4. Give them the shape of an almond
5. Boil in milk with sugar

title: Sweets of Cocoa Nut
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Heat equal quantities of scraped and pasted cocoa-nut kernel and a syrup of sugar
2. Stir vigorously with a ladle till the mass sticks to the sides of the pot and the ladle
3. Take the mass off the fire
4. Make into balls
5. Press in casts

title: Sweets of Flattened Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- flattened rice 1 powa
- milk
- hard chhana 1 seer
- middlings 1/4 powa
- khowa kheer
- pieces or paste of almonds and pistachios
- powdered sugar-candy
- entire seeds of cardamom
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Wash the flattened rice and keep moistened with milk for 15 or 20 minutes
2. Take the moistened flattened rice out and make a paste with 1 seer of hard chhana
3. Mix with middlings up to 1/4 powa in weight
4. Make balls of it
5. Stuff them with a mixture of khowa kheer, pieces or paste of almonds and pistachios, powdered sugar-candy and a few entire seeds of cardamom
6. Form oval cakes
7. Fry in ghee
8. Immerse in a syrup of sugar

title: Swiss Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- butter, flour, and sugar, of each the weight of four eggs
- yolks of eggs
- grated lemon-peel or ten drops of essence of lemon
- one large teaspoonful of rose-water or orange-flower water
- whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with the sugar and some grated lemon-peel or ten drops of essence of lemon
2. Add one large teaspoonful of rose-water or orange-flower water if preferred
3. Add the butter just melted
4. Slowly shake in the flour, beating it until well mixed
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a froth
6. Mix the whole together and beat on for a few minutes after the whites are added
7. Butter a tin and bake the cake half an hour

title: Tal-Bara
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe sweet tal fruit
- Salt
- Powdered rice or fine flour
- Almonds
- Rose essence
- Ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Mix the pulp of a ripe sweet tal fruit with salt and powdered rice or fine flour so that the mass becomes a thick paste
2. Chopped almonds and drops of essence of rose may be added
3. Heat a round bottomed pan with a little ghee or oil
4. Add a table-spoonful of the paste and bake till it is solid
5. Bake the other side also

title: Tal-Bara, Another Variety
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe tal fruit pulp
- Sugar
- Powdered rice
- Banana leaves

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and powdered rice with the pulp of a ripe tal fruit so that the paste is just stiff to be made a big ball
2. Press the ball flat
3. Cover it all round with a few layers of banana leaves
4. Burn it on mild heat till well boiled
5. Remove the burnt leaves when cold and serve

title: Tamarind Chutnee
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of ripe tamarinds without the stones
- a quarter of a pound each of ground chilies, ginger, and garlic
- two ounces of ground cinnamon
- half a pound of picked currants
- half a pound of raisins
- two pounds of soft sugar
- a quarter of a pound of salt
- a quart of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the whole into a glazed earthen preserving-pan
2. Pour over it a quart of vinegar or syrup, or as much as will entirely cover the mixture
3. Mix all well together
4. Allow it to simmer over a quick fire until the vinegar or syrup is absorbed and the chutnee thickened to the required consistency
5. Stir during the whole time it is on the fire

title: Tamarind Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a table-spoonful of tamarind pulp
- one table-spoonful of green ginger
- salt
- a tea-spoonful of minced green chillies
- one tea-spoonful of mustard seed
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound together a table-spoonful of tamarind pulp and one of green ginger.
2. Season it with salt, a tea-spoonful of minced green chillies, and one of mustard seed roasted in butter.
3. Mix thoroughly and serve.

title: Tamarind Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tamarinds
- good English vinegar
- salt
- hilsa fish
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a thick pickle of ripe tamarinds, good English vinegar, and a little salt
2. Pass through a seive, rejecting all stones and fibres
3. Select really good fresh hilsa fish, full size, with roes
4. Remove all the scales and fins, cut away the heads and tails, remove the roes, clean out the fish inside, and then slice up, an inch thick
5. Wipe away all blood, &c., with a clean dry towel
6. Lay out the slices of fish and roe on a clean dish, sprinkle thickly with salt, and place over them a wire dish-cover to keep away the flies
7. Four or five hours afterwards put a layer of the pickle into a wide-mouthed bottle or jar, and a thick coating of pickle over each slice of fish and the roes, after washing away the salt with a little vinegar
8. Lay them in order in the jar, until the last of the fish is put in
9. Put in a very thick layer of the pickle
10. Cork the jar securely, and tie it down with a good bladder to keep it air-tight
11. In three weeks it will be fit for use

title: Tapp Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- three seers or six pounds of peeled and sliced mangoes
- two pounds of ground raisins
- a pound of ground garlic
- half a pound of ground chilies
- a pound and a half of ground ginger
- a pound of sugar
- two pounds of salt
- a quart of lime-juice
- six quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all the above well together, put it into stone jars, and expose it to the sun for twenty days or a month
2. After which drain away the liquid, which is the sauce
3. Boil it for ten to fifteen minutes, and when cold bottle and cork it

title: Tapp Sauce Gravy for Made Dishes
body:
## Ingredients

- a good seasoned stock
- arrowroot or corn-flour
- a tablespoonful of tapp sauce per cup of thickened stock

## Directions

1. Thicken a good seasoned stock with arrowroot or corn-flour
2. Add to every cup of the thickened stock a tablespoonful of tapp sauce
3. Pour it while hot over chicken, veal, beef, or prawn cutlets, or other made dishes

title: Tart and Pie Crusts of Soojee
body:
## Ingredients

- one seer and a quarter of soojee
- half a seer of suet
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a little yeast
- half a seer of flour for dredging and rolling

## Directions

1. To one seer and a quarter of soojee add half a seer of suet and a teaspoonful of salt
2. Thoroughly clean the suet, remove all the skin and other objectionable particles, chop, mince, and pound fine in a mortar
3. Damp the soojee for half an hour before kneading it
4. Knead it with the suet and a little of the yeast
5. Divide it into parts, dredge it with flour, and roll in layers
6. Repeat the operation two or three times, and the pastry when baked will be light and flaky

title: Tartare Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Use raw yolks alone without hard-boiled eggs
2. Add oil and vinegar in the following proportions: a tea-spoonful of vinegar to two tablespoonfuls of oil
3. Beat together well, and often times repeat till enough sauce is made

title: Tawa Puri
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Almonds ½ powa
- Pistachios ½ powa
- Powdered sugar candy 1 powa
- Powdered cardamoms 1 tola
- Fine flour ¾ seer
- Besam of grams ½ seer
- Ghee 1 chhatak
- Water

## Directions

1. Make a paste of ½ powa of almonds and ½ powa of pistachios
2. Mix thoroughly with 1 powa powdered sugar candy and 1 tola of powdered cardamoms
3. Mix thoroughly ¾ seer of fine flour, ½ seer of besam of grams and 1 chhatak of ghee
4. Knead to a stiff paste with water
5. Make balls, stuff them with the above preparation
6. Press into round thin discs
7. Fry in plenty of ghee like Luchi

title: Teal Fillets Wyvernne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a teal
- oil
- lime juice
- sliced onion
- sweet herbs
- gravy making concomitants
- butter
- flour
- a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a dessert-spoonful of lime juice
- caramel colouring
- a glass of red wine or Madeira
- a few drops of tabasco or chilli vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut off the breasts of the teal, whole, bones and all, and divide each in half
2. Marinade these pieces all day in a little oil, lime juice, sliced onion, and sweet herbs
3. Grill them over a bright clear fire
4. Send them up piping hot, and full of juice straight from the gridiron
5. Throw every atom of the backs, legs, giblets, etc., into a sauce-pan with the usual concomitants of gravy making
6. Distil their essence by gentle simmering
7. Strain when done
8. Pick all the meat from the fragments and pound it a la puree
9. Add it, with butter and flour thickening, to the gravy of the bones
10. Judiciously introduce a tea-spoonful of red currant jelly, a dessert-spoonful of lime juice, some caramel colouring and a glass of red wine, or Madeira, with a few drops of tabasco or chilli vinegar
11. Send the sauce round in a boat, as hot as possible, following the grilled breasts

title: Teal with Bigarade Sauce
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- teal
- bones and giblets of the birds
- lime juice
- oranges

## Directions

1. Prepare the teal, preserving the breast of each bird entire
2. Make a game gravy with the bones and the giblets of the birds
3. Proceed as already directed to make bigarade sauce, adding a little lime juice to give it piquancy
4. Broil the teal breasts upon a gridiron, and serve them full of juice straight from the fire

title: Tel-Jhol (Soup Rich in Oil)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- oil
- coriander
- chillies
- turmeric
- cumin
- pepper
- onions
- cassia leaves
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Brown some big pieces of fish in oil
2. Boil sufficient oil in a new pot and add coriander, chillies, turmeric, cumin, pepper and onions, all pasted together, and cassia leaves
3. Stir for about 5 minutes
4. Add a quantity of water, fish and salt
5. Take down when fishes are boiled and the soup is moderately thick

title: Tender Shoots of Lau (Pumpkin)
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- green tender ends of lau plant with leaves
- salt
- mustard oil
- posta (poppy-seed)

## Directions

1. Tie green tender ends of lau plant with leaves together with a thread.
2. Boil in rice.
3. Keep posta moistened in water for some time and paste.
4. Take in a paste condition with salt and mustard oil.
5. Add a little pasted posta if desired.

title: Tenti
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 powas ghee
- ½ seer besam of kalai
- 1 powa besam of grams
- 1 seer sugar
- 1 tola powdered ginger
- camphor

## Directions

1. Boil 3 powas of ghee
2. Mix together besam of kalai ½ seer, besam of grams 1 powa, sugar 1 seer, powdered ginger 1 tola and half a pinch of camphor
3. Add to the boiling ghee
4. Stir till the mass is a very stiff dough
5. Take it off fire
6. Prepare balls of it and serve when cool

title: The Commander-in-Chief
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of claret
- a bottle of soda-water
- a wineglassful of curaçao
- sugar
- a handful of picked and bruised mint-leaves
- a seer of crushed ice
- a quart of champagne

## Directions

1. Empty into a punchbowl a quart of claret and a bottle of soda-water
2. add a wineglassful of curaçao
3. sweeten to taste with sugar
4. throw in a handful of picked and bruised mint-leaves, with a seer of crushed ice
5. add a quart of champagne
6. stir briskly
7. serve up

title: The Rumbled Eggs of Ramasamj
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- salt-spoonful of salt
- table-spoonful of cream or milk
- dust of pepper
- piece of butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Break three eggs into a bowl with a salt-spoonful of salt, a table-spoonful of cream or of milk, and a dust of pepper
2. Mix them well
3. Melt a piece of butter the size of an egg in your omelette-pan
4. Pour in your mixture
5. Stir it about unceasingly until it is lightly set
6. Turn it out

title: The Savoury Omelette
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ordinary country fowl's eggs
- two country fowl's eggs (yolks only)
- salt-spoonful of salt
- tea-spoonful of very finely minced shallot
- heaped up table-spoonful of minced curly parsley
- table-spoonful of really rich cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Break four ordinary country fowl's eggs whole into a slop-basin, and add the yolks only of two more eggs
2. Thoroughly mix the six yolks and four whites by using two forks, without beating them at all
3. Flavour the mixture with a salt-spoonful of salt, a tea-spoonful of very finely minced shallot, a heaped up table-spoonful of minced curly parsley, and a table-spoonful of really rich cream
4. Stir this for a minute
5. Melt a pat of butter in a shallow pan about ten inches in diameter over a good charcoal fire, sufficient in quantity to thoroughly lubricate the whole surface and leave a coating of moisture about an eighth of an inch deep
6. When the butter is quite burning hot, having ceased to splutter and beginning to brown, pour in the mixture with one good stir round
7. At the moment of contact, lift the underpart of omelette formed with a spoon, and allow the unformed portion to run beneath it, while holding and playing the pan from side to side with the left hand
8. With one good shake, lift the pan from the fire in less than a minute and roll its contents into a hot silver dish that has been prepared with a little melted butter, minced parsley, and shallot

title: Thick Mock-Turtle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of good butter
- three ounces of flour
- soup
- pieces of head
- a glass of Madeira
- the juice of a lime

## Directions

1. Take a large sauce-pan, and melt at the bottom of it three ounces of good butter
2. Mix into it three ounces of flour, when it looks nice and velvety, gently add the soup, stirring it in by cupfuls
3. Put into it the pieces of head, a glass of Madeira, and the juice of a lime
4. Let the soup come to the boil to thicken properly, and let it simmer slowly immediately afterwards, constantly stirring to prevent the meat sticking to the bottom of the pan
5. When ready, you can add a little more Madeira if you find it needed, and serve

title: Thor Ghanta
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- thor stem
- salt
- turmeric

## Directions

1. Peel and chop about a foot of the tender solid white stem in the core of a banana plant, called thor, into very fine pieces removing every time the thread-like substances that come out.
2. Mix them thoroughly with salt and turmeric.
3. Wait for an hour or so till water trickles out.
4. Remove the water by squeezing.
5. Boil them and drain off the water.
6. Proceed as in the case of mocha-ghanta.

title: Tick-keeah Kawab
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fat beef
- one teaspoonful of ground onion
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground turmeric
- one-eighth of a teaspoonful of ground garlic
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground chilies
- half a teaspoonful of ground peppercorns
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- half a teaspoonful of ground hot spices
- one tablespoonful of tyre or dhye
- salt
- one egg
- ghee
- plantain-leaf

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of fat beef, wash it, cut it into small pieces, and pound it to a pulp, remove all fibres.
2. Add to it one teaspoonful of ground onion, a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground turmeric, one-eighth of a teaspoonful of ground garlic, a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground chilies, half a teaspoonful of ground peppercorns, a quarter of a teaspoonful of ground ginger, half a teaspoonful of ground hot spices, and one tablespoonful of tyre or dhye.
3. Mix the whole well together, add salt to your taste, and the yolk and white of an egg well beaten up.
4. Form into balls of equal sizes; flatten them.
5. Pass them on iron or plated skewers about eighteen inches long.
6. Rub them well over with ghee, wrap them in plantain-leaf, and roast or broil them over a charcoal fire.
7. Serve them up hot, removed from the skewers.

title: Tikar-Sahi
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 powa of ghee
- 1/2 seer of fine flour
- milk about 3 chhataks
- powdered poppy seeds 3 chhataks
- powdered pepper 1/2 tola
- powdered cinnamon (a pinch)
- milk about 1 chhatak
- ghee for frying
- sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 powa of ghee thoroughly with 1/2 seer of fine flour and then make a stiff dough with about 3 chhataks of milk
2. Prepare about 20 balls of it
3. Stuff them with a mixture of powdered poppy seeds 3 chhataks, powdered pepper 1/2 tola, a pinch of powdered cinnamon and about 1 chhatak of milk
4. Roll them into thin and round discs
5. Fry them in plenty of ghee
6. Keep immersed in a syrup of sugar

title: Tilua
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 seer of sugar
- 1 powa of very fine flour
- sesames

## Directions

1. Prepare a very thick syrup of 1 seer of sugar and beat thoroughly adding 1 powa of very fine flour and a handful of sesames.
2. Pour it in a round flat dish to half inch thickness.
3. Sprinkle a few more sesames on the surface if you like.
4. Take the mass out when cold and solid in the form of a big disc.

title: Tinned Peas Heated Up
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tinned peas
- butter

## Directions

1. Drain the peas from the tin liquor
2. Wash in two or three waters, pouring fresh water over them two or three times
3. Heat up in butter

title: Tipparee Cheese or Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- tipparees
- sugar
- orange marmalade
- melted butter or sweet oil

## Directions

1. Take the fruit which had been boiled for jelly and pass it through a fine sieve, leaving the skins behind
2. Clean and prick a few more tipparees and add them to the strained fruit
3. Put the whole into a preserving-pan with sugar and simmer until of a sufficient consistency to make into cheese
4. Add some orange marmalade in the proportion of a tablespoonful to every mould
5. With a feather damp the moulds with melted butter or sweet oil
6. Pour into them the cheese while quite hot
7. Place them in cold water and turn out the cheeses as soon as they are cool enough to retain their shape

title: Tipparee Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- tipparees
- water
- fine clean white sugar
- juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Clean and prick the tipparees
2. Put them into a clean well-tinned stewpan with as much water as will entirely cover them
3. Boil them until all the juice has run out
4. Strain the latter into a preserving-pan through fine muslin without crushing the fruit
5. Allow it to simmer for a while, removing the scum
6. Add to it fine clean white sugar to taste in small quantities at a time, skimming it well all the while
7. When nearly ready, put in the juice of two lemons strained through muslin
8. When the scum has ceased to rise and the jelly is clear, remove the pan from the fire
9. Bottle the jelly while it is warm and cork when it is quite cold

title: Tipparee Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- tipparees
- sugar

## Directions

1. Shell or remove the pods of the tipparees and wipe away all dust
2. Prick each with a bamboo or other wooden pin
3. Put them into a preserving-pan
4. Strew some sugar over each layer of fruit, making the final layer of sugar thicker than the others
5. Simmer the whole until all the juice has been extracted and the syrup has acquired such a consistency that it will congeal if dropped on a plate
6. Remove the preserve quickly from the fire and bottle while warm

title: To Boil Artichokes
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the stalks close to the bottom, wash them well, and let them lie for some hours in cold water
2. Put them on in boiling water with a little salt in it, cover the pan closely, and boil them an hour and a half

title: To Boil Artichokes
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the stalks close to the bottom, wash them well
2. let them lie for some hours in cold water
3. put them on in boiling water with a little salt in it
4. cover the pan closely
5. boil them an hour and a half
6. If they are old, and have not been freshly gathered, they will take a longer time to boil
7. Melted butter is served with them in a sauce-tureen

title: To Boil Asparagus
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- water
- salt
- bread

## Directions

1. Wash them well, scrape, and tie them up in small bundles
2. Cut them all even at the bottom, and as they are done put them into cold water
3. Put them on in boiling water, with a little salt, and let them boil twenty or twenty-five minutes
4. Take them up, lay them upon a slice of toasted bread cut in four, and the crusts pared off, with the tops meeting in the middle of the dish, and cut off the strings

title: To Boil Brocoli
body:
## Ingredients

- brocoli
- water
- salt
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Wash it, cut off all the outside leaves and stalks, throw it into cold water as it is trimmed
2. Put it on in boiling water with a little salt, and boil it for twenty-five minutes or half an hour
3. It is sometimes served upon bits of toasted bread, and a little melted butter poured round it

title: To Boil Brocoli
body:
## Ingredients

- brocoli
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash it, cut off all the outside leaves and stalks, throw it into cold water as it is trimmed
2. put it on in boiling water with a little salt
3. boil it for twenty-five minutes or half an hour
4. It is sometimes served upon bits of toasted bread, and a little melted butter poured round it

title: To Boil Carrots
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrape, wash, and clean them
2. put them on in boiling water with some salt in it
3. boil them from two to three hours
4. Very young carrots will require one hour

title: To Boil Cauliflowers
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflowers
- water
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Trim them neatly, and let them lie an hour or two in cold water
2. Rinse them in fresh cold water, and put them with a very little salt into boiling water
3. Boil them twenty minutes, or half an hour if very large
4. They may be boiled in milk and water, and require to be skimmed with particular attention

title: To Boil French Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- French beans
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the stalk and string them
2. If not very young, cut them in four, or into very thin slices
3. Put them into water as they are done, and put them on in boiling water, with a little salt
4. Let them boil for half an hour
5. If they are old they will require a longer time to boil
6. Melted butter in a sauce-tureen is served with them

title: To Boil Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- salt
- pounded loaf sugar
- butter
- mint

## Directions

1. After being shelled, wash them, drain them in a colander
2. put them on in plenty of boiling water, with a teaspoonful of salt, and one of pounded loaf sugar
3. boil them till they become tender, which, if young, will be in less than half an hour; if old, they will require more than an hour
4. drain them in a colander
5. put them immediately into a dish with a slice of fresh butter in it
6. Some people think it an improvement to boil a small bunch of mint with the peas; it is then minced finely, and laid in small heaps at the end or sides of the dish
7. If peas are allowed to stand in the water after being boiled they lose their colour

title: To Boil Marrow-bones
body:
## Ingredients

- marrow-bones
- butter
- flour
- linen
- thin slices of dry toast
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Saw them even at the bottom.
2. Butter and flour some bits of linen, and tie a piece over the top of each bone.
3. Boil them for an hour or two.
4. Take off the linen, and serve them with thin slices of dry toast cut into square bits.
5. At table the marrow should be put upon the toast, and a little pepper and salt sprinkled over it.

title: To Boil Ox-Cheek
body:
## Ingredients

- half a head of ox-cheek
- boiled carrots
- boiled turnips

## Directions

1. Wash half a head very clean
2. Let it lie in cold water for some hours
3. Break the bone in two, taking care not to break the flesh
4. Put it into a pot of boiling water, and let it boil from two to three hours
5. Take out the bone
6. Serve it with boiled carrots and turnips

title: To Boil Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- water

## Directions
