1. Wash and pare them, throwing them into cold water as they are pared
2. Put them into a saucepan, cover them with cold water, and throw in a little salt
3. Cover the saucepan closely, and let them boil quickly for half an hour
4. Pour off the water immediately, and set the pan by the side of the fire to dry the potatoes

title: To Boil Spinach
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- water
- salt
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Pick it very carefully, and wash it thoroughly two or three times in plenty of cold water
2. Put it on in boiling water with a little salt
3. Let it boil nearly twenty minutes
4. Put it into a colander, hold it under the water-cock, and let the water run on it for a minute
5. Put it into a saucepan, beat it perfectly smooth with a beater or wooden spoon
6. Add a bit of butter and three tablespoonfuls of cream, mix it well together, and make it hot before serving
7. When dished, it is scored in squares with the back of a knife

title: To Boil Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- salt
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash, pare, and throw them into cold water
2. Put them on in boiling water with a little salt, and boil them from two hours to two and a half
3. Drain them in a colander, put them into a saucepan, and, mixing in a bit of butter, with a beater mash them very smoothly
4. Add half a pint of milk, mix it well with the turnips, and make them quite hot before serving

title: To Boil Young Green Cabbages
body:
## Ingredients

- young green cabbages
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash and clean them well
2. put them on in boiling water with a little salt in it
3. let them boil quickly from three-quarters to nearly an hour
4. Serve with melted butter

title: To Brown Potatoes under Meat while Roasting
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- meat dripping

## Directions

1. After being boiled, lay them on a dish, and place it in the dripping-pan
2. Baste them now and then with a little of the meat dripping
3. When one side is browned turn the other; they should all be of an equal colour

title: To Clarify Sugar
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of loaf sugar
- 1 beaten white of egg
- 1 pint and a half of water

## Directions

1. To every three pounds of loaf sugar allow the beaten white of one egg and a pint and a half of water
2. Break the sugar small and put it into a nicely cleaned brass pan
3. Pour the water over it and let it stand some time before it be put upon the fire
4. Add the beaten white of the egg and stir till the sugar be entirely dissolved
5. When it boils up pour in a quarter of a pint of cold water
6. Let it boil up a second time
7. Remove it from the fire and let it settle for fifteen minutes
8. Carefully take off all the scum
9. Put it again on the fire and boil till sufficiently thick or till candy high
10. To ascertain this, drop a little from a spoon into a cup of cold water, and if it become quite hard it is ready

title: To Cure Mutton Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- hind quarter of good mutton
- one ounce of saltpetre
- one pound of coarse salt
- a quarter of a pound of brown sugar
- sawdust

## Directions

1. Cut a hind quarter of good mutton into the shape of a ham
2. Pound one ounce of saltpetre, with one pound of coarse salt and a quarter of a pound of brown sugar
3. Rub the ham well with this mixture, taking care to stuff the hole of the shank well with salt and sugar, and let it lie a fortnight, rubbing it well with the pickle every two or three days
4. Take it out and press it with a weight for one day
5. Smoke it with sawdust for ten or fifteen days, or hang it to dry in the kitchen
6. If the ham is to be boiled soon after it has been smoked, soak it one hour; and if it has been smoked any length of time, it will require to be soaked several hours
7. Put it on in cold water and boil it gently two hours

title: To Dress Young Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- young turnips
- water
- salt
- butter
- milk
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash, peel, and boil them till tender in water with a little salt
2. Serve them with melted butter poured over them, or
3. They may be stewed in a pint of milk thickened with a bit of butter rolled in flour, and seasoned with salt and pepper, and served with the sauce

title: To Make a Quart Bottle of Fish or Meat Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle of vinegar
- one ounce of cayenne
- two cloves of garlic
- one tablespoonful of soy
- two of walnut catsup
- two of mushroom catsup
- the remaining half of the bottle of vinegar

## Directions

1. To half a bottle of vinegar put one ounce of cayenne, two cloves of garlic, one tablespoonful of soy, two of walnut, and two of mushroom catsup
2. Let it stand six days, shaking it frequently
3. Add the remaining half of the bottle of vinegar, let it stand another week
4. Strain, and put it into small bottles

title: To Preserve Lime-juice
body:
## Ingredients

- lime-juice
- double-refined sugar
- silver spoon

## Directions

1. Squeeze and strain a pint of lime-juice
2. Put into a basin one pound of double-refined sugar finely pounded and sifted
3. Add the lime-juice
4. Stir it with a silver spoon till the sugar is perfectly dissolved
5. Bottle

title: To Preserve Lime-juice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of lime-juice
- 1 pound of double-refined sugar

## Directions

1. Squeeze and strain a pint of lime-juice
2. Put into a basin one pound of double-refined sugar finely pounded and sifted
3. Add the lime-juice and stir it with a silver spoon till the sugar is perfectly dissolved
4. Bottle it and cork it tightly
5. Seal the cork or tie bladder over it
6. Keep it in a dry, cool place

title: To Preserve Tamarinds
body:
## Ingredients

- tamarinds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rid the tamarinds of all the stones
2. Put a layer of sugar in a wide-mouthed bottle
3. Over it put a layer of stoned tamarinds, then another layer of sugar, and so on alternately until the bottle is full
4. Make the final layer a deep one of sugar
5. Tie the stopper down with oiled bladder

title: To Purify Lime-juice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of strained lime-juice
- 1 ounce of well-burnt and finely pounded animal charcoal

## Directions

1. Add an ounce of well-burnt and finely pounded animal charcoal to a quart of strained lime-juice
2. In twelve hours filter it through blotting-paper
3. Put it into small phials
4. Cork these tightly and keep them in a cool place

title: To Roast Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Roast them with the skins on in an oven, that they may brown equally
2. They are eaten with cold fresh butter, pepper, and salt

title: To Steam Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- lettuce-salad leaves

## Directions

1. Shell and close-pack the peas securely in a large quantity of lettuce-salad leaves
2. Put the package into a stewpan over a moderate fire for the ordinary time required to boil peas, say half an hour, when they will be ready

title: To Stew a Fillet of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of veal
- two quarts of white stock
- one teaspoonful of lemon pickle
- one teaspoonful of mushroom catsup
- butter
- flour
- cayenne
- salt
- pickled mushrooms
- forcemeat balls
- lemon

## Directions

1. Bone, lard, and stuff a fillet of veal.
2. Half roast, and then stew it with two quarts of white stock, a teaspoonful of lemon pickle, and one of mushroom catsup.
3. Before serving, strain the gravy.
4. Thicken it with butter rolled in flour.
5. Add a little cayenne, salt, and some pickled mushrooms.
6. Heat it, and pour it over the veal.
7. Have ready two or three dozens of forcemeat balls to put round it and upon the top.
8. Garnish with cut lemon.

title: To Stew Cucumbers
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten large cucumbers
- flour
- butter
- teacupful of gravy
- cayenne
- salt
- mushroom catsup
- port wine

## Directions

1. Pare cucumbers, and cut them into thick slices
2. flour them well, and fry them in butter
3. put them into a saucepan with a teacupful of gravy
4. season it highly with cayenne, salt, mushroom catsup, and a little port wine
5. Let them stew for an hour, and serve them hot

title: To Stew Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- anchovy
- cloves
- nutmeg
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- white wine
- butter
- flour
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean them as for pickling, and, after washing them, put them into a saucepan, with an anchovy, two cloves, some nutmeg sliced, mace, whole pepper, and salt
2. let them stew in their own liquor till tender
3. In this way they will keep for some time
4. when required to be dressed, pick out the spice, and to a dishful put two large tablespoonfuls of white wine
5. add part of their own liquor, and let them just boil
6. stir in a bit of butter dredged with flour, and two tablespoonfuls of cream

title: To Stew Ox-Cheek
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-cheek head
- liquor from boiling
- butter
- flour
- carrots
- turnips
- two or three onions
- salt
- pepper
- port wine

## Directions

1. Clean the head as before directed, and parboil it
2. Take out the bone
3. Stew it in part of the liquor in which it was boiled, thickened with a piece of butter mixed with flour, and browned
4. Cut into dice, or into any fancy shape, as many carrots and turnips as will fill a pint basin
5. Mince two or three onions, add the vegetables, and season with salt and pepper
6. Cover the pan closely, and stew it two hours
7. A little before serving, add a glassful of port wine

title: To Stew Young Peas and Lettuce
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two heads of cabbage lettuce
- salt
- quart or three pints of peas
- three tablespoonfuls of gravy
- butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- teaspoonful of pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and make perfectly clean one or two heads of cabbage lettuce
2. pick off the outside leaves, and lay them for two hours in cold water with a little salt in it
3. slice them, and put them into a saucepan, with a quart or three pints of peas, three tablespoonfuls of gravy, a bit of butter dredged with flour, some pepper and salt, and a teaspoonful of pounded loaf sugar
4. Let them stew, closely covered, till the peas are soft

title: Tomato and Bombay Onion Salad
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw ripe tomatoes
- Bombay onion
- oil
- tarragon or aromatic vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- minced fines herbes
- strips of green capsicum

## Directions

1. Slice raw ripe tomatoes with a Bombay onion
2. Apply the plain salad dressing, using an abundant allowance of oil
3. Use a freer allowance of vinegar than with lettuce, as tomatoes are sweet
4. Sprinkle minced fines herbes over the whole
5. Add strips of green capsicum

title: Tomato Burta
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen good-sized tomatoes
- ground chilies
- ginger
- salt
- half a teaspoonful of good mustard oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bake in an oven a dozen good-sized tomatoes until the skin cracks
2. Break them down and mix with them a little ground chilies, ginger, salt, and half a teaspoonful of good mustard oil
3. A small squeeze of lemon juice may be added if desired

title: Tomato Chutney
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three ripe tomatoes
- a quarter their bulk of white onion
- salt
- two green chillies
- a little bit of celery
- black pepper
- a tea-spoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds and watery juice from two or three ripe tomatoes, chop them up with a quarter their bulk of white onion, and season the mince with a little salt.
2. Add a pinch of salt, two green chillies chopped small, and a little bit of celery also chopped.
3. Give the whole a dust of black pepper, and moisten it with a tea-spoonful of vinegar.

title: Tomato or Love-apple Chutnee
body:
## Ingredients

- two hundred large ripe love-apples
- four ounces of raisins
- seven ounces of salt
- four ounces of sugar
- eight ounces of chilies, finely sliced
- four ounces of ground garlic
- seven ounces of ground mustard-seed
- a quart of vinegar

## Directions

1. Parboil the tomatoes in a quart of vinegar
2. Add the other ingredients, and allow the whole to stand for ten to twelve hours
3. Boil it for twenty to thirty minutes over a slow fire
4. When cold, bottle it

title: Tomato with Tamarind Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, twenty
- tamarinds, two or three
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Chahkee twenty tomatoes with the pulp of two or three tamarinds as directed for red pumpkin chahkee

title: Tomato, Plain Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, twenty
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Chahkee twenty tomatoes according to instructions given for other chahkees

title: Tomatoes a l'Italienne
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomato
- salad oil
- grated ham
- bread-crumbs
- minced shallot
- parsley
- marjoram
- thyme
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes in halves
2. scoop out the pulp and seeds with a silver spoon
3. place them on a baking dish upon which you have poured a little of the best salad oil
4. Make a mixture of grated ham, bread-crumbs, some finely minced shallot, parsley, marjoram, and thyme, seasoned with pepper and salt
5. mix this with the tomato pulp
6. fill the cases, covering them completely
7. shake an allowance of salad oil in drops over all
8. bake for a few minutes
9. serve the dish intact as it comes from the oven

title: Tomatoes Au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomato
- eggs
- bread-crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- grated cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut a slice off the top of each tomato as you would decapitate a boiled egg
2. With a dessert-knife scoop out the pulp and seeds from the shell as well as you can, put the cases so obtained on one side
3. make a puree with the scooped out pulp, flavour it nicely like tomato sauce, strain off the seeds, and thicken it with fine bread-crumbs
4. beat up some eggs (one for every two cases) and mix the whole well
5. add pepper and salt, stuff the cases therewith
6. give the surface of each a light dusting of grated cheese
7. bake on a buttered dish for ten minutes
8. serve

title: Tomatoes au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen large tomatoes
- bread-crumb
- eggs
- pounded anchovies
- minced olives
- capers
- grated Parmesan
- butter

## Directions

1. Empty a dozen large tomatoes preserving the cases as well as you can.
2. To the pulp of the vegetable add sufficient bread-crumb to thicken it somewhat, and beat up eggs in the proportion of one egg to two cases.
3. Mix the whole thoroughly, flavour with a couple of pounded anchovies, a tea-spoonful of minced olives, and one of capers.
4. Stuff the cases, dust them over with grated Parmesan, lay them on a well-buttered dish that will stand the oven, and bake for ten minutes just before they are wanted.
5. Serve them in their own dish buried in a napkin.

title: Tomatoes Au Gratin - Simple Method
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sweet onion
- tomato
- garlic
- Parmesan cheese
- Grujere cheese

## Directions

1. Put an ounce of butter into a small stew-pan
2. throw into it a table-spoonful of finely minced sweet onion
3. put the pan on the fire and lightly fry the onion
4. before the pieces take color, put into the vessel four or five large, or a dozen small tomatoes, cut up into small pieces
5. Stir well over the fire until the tomatoes are thoroughly cooked
6. rub an au gratin dish with a piece of garlic, butter it
7. pour into it the contents of the stew-pan
8. dust over the surface a layer of Parmesan, Grujere, or other mild cheese
9. bake for eight or ten minutes
10. serve hot

title: Trimmed Globe Artichokes Beignets
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- globe artichoke
- lime or vinegar
- cold water
- salt
- vinegar
- batter
- fat

## Directions

1. Place the raw artichoke bottom downwards on a board, and cut it straight down, dividing it in half
2. Divide each half so obtained so that you have four quarters
3. Pare out the choke which adheres to each quarter, as you would core an apple
4. Trim off the leaves leaving about an eighth of an inch of them unsevered and adhering to the trimmed quarters
5. Drop each piece as you trim it into cold water in which a lime has been squeezed, or a table-spoonful of vinegar poured, to prevent its turning black
6. Throw the quarters into boiling water with a dessert-spoonful of salt, and a spoonful of vinegar
7. In about fifteen minutes, when nearly done, lift them out and drain them
8. Cut into slices, dip in batter, and fry in boiling fat till of a bright golden tint

title: Turban of Snipes
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a snipe for each head
- beef gravy
- an onion
- a stick of celery
- a carrot
- sweet herbs in a bag
- a clove of garlic
- salt
- a tea-spoonful of sugar
- the peel of a lime
- bread soaked in stock
- a lightly roasted chicken with its liver
- two raw eggs
- spiced pepper
- a table-spoonful of butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- a glass of portwine
- a dessert-spoonful of vinegar or lime juice
- a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
- a tin of black Leicestershire mushrooms

## Directions

1. Roast the snipes lightly, then cut off the meat of the breasts whole in fillets
2. Mash the rest, trails and all, and throw the debris into a large sauce-pan with a pint and a half of well made beef gravy, an onion cut up, a stick of celery, a carrot, sweet herbs in a bag, a clove of garlic, salt to taste, a tea-spoonful of sugar, and the peel of a lime
3. Simmer for an hour or more to get the essence out of the snipe bones
4. When done, strain off your gravy, and set it to get cool
5. Pick all the snipe meat from the bones of the thighs and back, with the livers, &c, and pound the whole with half its bulk of bread (soaked in stock) in a mortar
6. Add the pounded meat of a lightly roasted chicken with its liver, and mix them thoroughly with two raw eggs
7. Season the mixture with spiced pepper, and dilute it with some of the gravy
8. When worked sufficiently, put it into a border mould, and steam it gently
9. For the gravy: place a sauce-pan on the fire, with a table-spoonful of butter at the bottom of it
10. Work into the melted butter a dessert-spoonful of flour, and then add your cool gravy, stirring all the time
11. Pour in a glass of portwine, a dessert-spoonful of vinegar or lime juice, and a dessert-spoonful of red currant jelly
12. Amalgamate the whole thoroughly, heat up the fillets and heads in it, and the mushrooms strained from their own gravy
13. Turn the turban out when done, garnish the top with the snipe heads and beaks, and with a fillet (heated separately in the gravy) between each head
14. Put the mushrooms inside the mould, pour the gravy over the whole composition, and serve piping hot

title: Turkish Coffee
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee berries
- water

## Directions

1. Roast the berries with care
2. Cast the berries into a large metal mortar and pound them to a very fine powder
3. Carefully sift the powder through a fine sieve, rejecting all coarse particles
4. Boil the required amount of water in a small copper can with a narrow top and broad bottom
5. When the water boils, add powdered coffee according to requirements and replace the can on the fire
6. Permit the liquid to come to the boil three times, taking the can off the fire for a while after each boiling
7. After the third boiling, place the can for a minute in a shallow vessel containing cold water to precipitate the grounds
8. Pour the clear, black, and sparkling coffee into the cup

title: Turnip and Carrot
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- turnip
- carrot
- ground turmeric
- salt
- water
- ghee or oil

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and boil them in water mixed with ground turmeric and salt.
2. Then fry the boiled pieces in ghee or oil.

title: Tyre or Dhye
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- stale butter

## Directions

1. Warm some milk without boiling it
2. stir into it a little stale butter about the size of a large pea
3. put the vessel in a warm place well covered over, and in the course of eight or ten hours the tyre will be ready

title: Udder and Beef Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of udder
- one pound of beef

## Directions

1. Take one pound each of udder and beef
2. Parboil the udder
3. Cut it up with the beef into one-inch square pieces
4. Doopiaja it, allowing it to simmer for about two hours

title: Umarani of Shrimps
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- big pieces of potatoes
- minced onions
- ghee or oil
- turmeric
- cumin
- pepper
- chillies
- coriander
- salt
- water
- Mayarani pieces
- cassia leaves
- red chillies
- ghee
- powdered garam-masalla

## Directions

1. Brown some big pieces of potatoes and minced onions in ghee or oil.
2. Add turmeric, cumin, pepper, chillies coriander, all powdered, and salt and water.
3. On boiling add the fried pieces called Mayarani.
4. Season the curry after a time with ghee boiled with a few cassia leaves and red chillies.
5. Add ghee and powdered garam-masalla when the soup is thick and take down.

title: Vanilla Cream Ice
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of eight eggs
- pint of fresh milk
- vanilla
- coffee-cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Mix the yolks of eight eggs well with a pint of fresh milk
2. Strain it and sweeten it to taste
3. Flavour it with vanilla
4. Set it in a sauce-pan in your bain-marie to thicken
5. When you have got a nice thick custard, whip it well and let it get cold
6. Pour it into your ice pot and freeze it

title: Vanilla Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of four eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of finely-powdered white sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Take the whites of four eggs, beat them up well
2. Add three-quarters of a pound of finely-powdered white sugar
3. Flavour with vanilla
4. Beat up well
5. Drop it on buttered paper
6. Bake in a cool oven

title: Vanilla Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one table-spoonful of flour
- a small piece of fresh butter
- half a pint of new milk
- a dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- a few drops of vanilla flavoring
- a table-spoonful of any liqueur
- whites of six eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together in a sauce-pan one table-spoonful of flour, a small piece of fresh butter, half a pint of new milk, and a dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar
2. Stir this over the fire to get thoroughly warm but no more
3. Put it on one side to get cool, stirring to prevent any scum forming on the surface
4. When quite cold, stir into it the yolks of four eggs
5. Add a few drops of vanilla flavoring
6. Mix this thoroughly
7. Throw in a table-spoonful of any liqueur you may have open
8. Add the whites of six eggs whipped to a froth
9. Mix the whole together
10. Pour it into a souffle tin
11. Bake in an oven at moderate heat
12. Serve the moment your eye tells you that the souffle is ready

title: Vanilla Souffle
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one table-spoonful of flour
- a small piece of fresh butter
- half a pint of new milk
- a dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together in a sauce-pan one table-spoonful of flour, a small piece of fresh butter, half a pint of new milk, and a dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar
2. Stir this over the fire to get thoroughly warm but no more
3. Put it on one side to get cool, stirring to prevent any scum forming

title: Vapa (Steamed) Puli
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered rice
- Boiling water
- Kheer or cocoa-nut preparation

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of powdered rice with boiling water, adding the necessary amount of water at a time
2. Make medium balls
3. Stuff them with kheer or the preparation of cocoa-nut
4. Give them the shape of an almond
5. Place on a thin linen cloth stretched over the boiling water in a deep pan with the lid on
6. Boil by steam only

title: Veal Country Captain
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces ghee
- half a teaspoonful ground chilies
- one teaspoonful salt
- a quarter of a teaspoonful ground turmeric
- twenty onions
- half a cup of water

## Directions

1. Partially broil or roast a shoulder of veal before cutting it up.
2. Or make the country captain as directed by adding half a cup of water instead of partially broiling the meat.

title: Veal Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- a small shoulder of veal
- ghee
- water
- ground onions
- ground turmeric
- ground chilies
- ground ginger
- ground garlic
- salt

## Directions

1. Select a small shoulder of veal
2. Cut off sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces of the best part of the meat
3. Curry it in every particular the same as a chicken
4. Allow it to simmer half to three-quarters of an hour

title: Veal Curry
body:
## Ingredients

- small shoulder of veal
- sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces of meat

## Directions

1. Cut off from the shoulder sixteen or eighteen one-inch square pieces of the best part of the meat
2. Curry it in every particular the same as a chicken
3. Allow it to simmer half to three-quarters of an hour

title: Veal Cutlets
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of veal or leg of veal
- two well-beaten eggs
- pounded mace
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- finely-chopped sweet marjoram
- lemon
- thyme
- parsley
- grated bread
- clarified butter
- white sauce
- forcemeat balls
- small mushrooms
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a neck of veal into cutlets, or take them off a leg.
2. Season two well-beaten eggs with pounded mace, nutmeg, salt, pepper, and finely-chopped sweet marjoram, lemon, thyme, and parsley.
3. Dip the cutlets into it.
4. Sift over them grated bread, and fry them in clarified butter.
5. Serve with a white sauce, forcemeat balls, and small mushrooms.
6. Garnish with fried parsley.

title: Veal Doopiaja
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of veal
- meat cut into squares

## Directions

1. Take only the meat from a shoulder
2. Cut it up into squares
3. Allow it to simmer for half an hour longer than the chicken doopiaja

title: Veal Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- a leg of veal or a breast
- water
- pepper
- salt
- a little mace
- a little bacon
- a little flour
- pie-crust

## Directions

1. Cut a leg of veal into small pieces, or a breast into chops, and parboil in water enough to fill the pie-dish.
2. When about half stewed take the veal out.
3. Season the gravy with pepper, salt, a little mace, and a little bacon.
4. Dredge in a little flour.
5. Line the sides of the dish with a pie-crust.
6. Arrange the meat, pour in the gravy, cover it with a pie-crust, and bake it for an hour.

title: Vegetable Consommé for Mulligatunny
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound carrots
- one pound onions
- half a pound butter
- bunch of parsley
- couple of ounces celery
- two quarts hot water
- half an ounce salt
- quarter ounce black peppercorns
- pint measure shelled green peas

## Directions

1. Throw carrots and onions into a stew-pan with butter, parsley, and celery
2. Fry until the vegetables begin to take colour
3. Moisten with two quarts of hot water
4. Boil and skim
5. Put into the pan half an ounce of salt, a quarter ounce of black peppercorns, and a pint measure of shelled green peas
6. Simmer for three hours
7. Skim off any oil that may rise from the butter
8. Strain the broth into a basin through a tamis

title: Vegetable Marrow au Gratin
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled vegetable marrow
- butter
- eggs
- mild cheese
- gravy
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice up a cold boiled vegetable marrow
2. Butter the bottom of a pie-dish and lay a layer of slices thereon
3. Grate a little mild cheese over them
4. Place another layer of slices above the first and strew grated cheese over that also
5. Melt some butter in a small sauce-pan and add a little gravy to it, together with some finely powdered cheese
6. When you have enough to half cover your layers of marrow, pour the liquid round them
7. Salt and pepper should be dusted over each layer, before the cheese gratings are added
8. Bake for ten minutes or so in a fast oven

title: Vegetable Mash
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- cauliflower
- carrots
- turnips
- green peas
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Mash down the potatoes with plenty of butter, pepper, and salt
2. Mince small the cauliflower, carrots, and turnips
3. Add them with the peas to the mashed potatoes
4. Mix them all well together and serve up

title: Vegetables Boiled in Rice
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- potato, brinjal, patol, Uch-chhe, karala, beans, mun, sweet potato, sweet gourd, green banana, lady's fingers, seeds of jack fruit, radish, carrot, cauliflower, olkochu, jhinga
- salt
- mustard oil
- green chillies
- powdered mustard

## Directions

1. Wash vegetables clean, peel and cut in suitable pieces where necessary before boiling
2. If need be tie them in a clean piece of cloth
3. Boil vegetables in rice instead of water
4. Take the boiled things out of the rice with a spoon
5. Squeeze and rub them into a smooth paste separately
6. Add salt, mustard oil, and a few fragmented pieces of green chillies if liked, and mix thoroughly
7. Mix mun, ol and olkochu with powdered mustard and a few drops of lemon if irritability be suspected
8. Take together with rice

title: Vermicelli Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- a shin of beef
- pepper
- salt
- water
- soup herbs
- white of an egg
- vermicelli

## Directions

1. Prepare a shin of beef soup as directed above, omitting the sauce and sherry
2. Parboil some vermicelli
3. After clearing the soup with the white of an egg, add to it the parboiled vermicelli, and give it a good boil up before serving

title: Victoria Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of bread-crumbs
- three ounces of shredded suet
- a quarter of a pound of any jam
- the juice of a lime
- the peel of a lime, grated
- two ounces of sifted sugar
- two eggs
- a pinch of salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix bread-crumbs, suet, jam, lime juice, lime peel, sugar, eggs, and salt thoroughly
2. Place in a buttered mould
3. Boil for three hours

title: Victoria Pudding
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of bread-crumbs
- three ounces of shredded suet
- a quarter of a pound of jam
- the juice of a lime
- lime peel grated
- two ounces of sifted sugar
- two eggs
- a pinch of salt
- flour
- butter
- boiling water
- brandy

## Directions

1. Mix bread-crumbs, shredded suet, jam, lime juice, grated lime peel, sifted sugar, eggs and salt thoroughly
2. Place it in a buttered mould, and boil for three hours
3. For the sauce, mix two tea-spoonfuls of flour with half an ounce of butter, and add a cupful of boiling water
4. Stir in a table-spoonful of the jam you use for your pudding, the juice of a lime and a spoonful of brandy
5. Simmer for a few minutes

title: Vol-Au-Vent Case
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of puff-paste
- butter
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Make very carefully a pound of puff-paste, following the directions given at page 263
2. Give the paste six turns, and roll it out three-quarters of an inch thick
3. Cut out of this as neatly as possible an oval piece the size you wish your vol-au-vent to be
4. Turn it over upon a buttered baking-sheet
5. Brush the surface and side with a beaten egg, and mark out the interior oval, leaving an inch margin all round
6. Let the knife cut this tracing to a depth of a quarter of an inch
7. Put the sheet in the oven, and when the paste is baked, remove the inner oval (for a cover) which you will find has risen
8. Scoop out the uncooked paste inside the case
9. Brush the whole case thus formed with egg again, and bake it for about five minutes

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese
- butter
- made-mustard
- salt
- Nepaul pepper
- egg yolks
- bread
- white paper

## Directions

1. Grate two ounces of the cheese and mix with it an ounce of butter, a dessert-spoonful of made-mustard, a little salt, a pinch of Nepaul pepper, and the yolks of two eggs.
2. Mix well together in a basin, and work the mixture thoroughly smooth.
3. Toast a couple of neat slices of bread very carefully and butter them on both sides.
4. Place them on a dish that will stand the oven and spread the cheese mixture over them pretty thickly.
5. Place your toasts in a buttered pie-dish and spread a sheet of common white paper over them.
6. Bake for ten minutes in a really hot oven, then remove the paper and serve.

title: Wheat Polao
author: E.P. Veerasawmy
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat 1 seer
- Wheat 1 seer
- Ghee 2.5 powa
- Curd 0.5 powa
- Almonds 0.5 powa
- Raisins 0.5 powa
- Pistachios 1 chhatak
- Pepper 2.5 tolas
- Ginger 1.5 tola
- Coriander seeds 0.5 tola
- Garam-masalla 0.5 tola
- Black cumin seeds 0.5 tola
- Cassia leaves a few
- Salt 3 tolas
- Onions 0.5 powa

## Directions

1. Take all the coriander seeds, half the ginger and peppers and a few pieces of raw onions and break them into pieces in mortar with pestle and bind them carefully and loosely in a piece of clean cloth.
2. Boil this bundle of spices with meat and 2 tolas of salt in about 4 seers of water till it reduces to half.
3. Take the akhni water out and keep it covered.
4. Mix the curd, the rest of the powdered peppers, pasted ginger, salt, and half the garam-masalla, powdered, with the boiled meat.
5. Take about 0.5 powa of ghee in a pan and heat it with half of the black cumin seeds and cassia leaves.
6. After about 4 or 5 minutes add the onions, peeled and cut in pieces, and stir till they are fully fried.
7. Add the boiled meat and stir for about 15 minutes till they are red. Then take down.
8. Take 0.5 powa of ghee again in the pot and brown the pistachios and almonds, peeled and cut in pieces after keeping them under water for about half an hour.
9. Add the raisins when they are slightly browned and take them out when they are fully browned.
10. Take a little more ghee in the pan, if necessary, and fry the rest of the garam-masalla in it.
11. When the smell of the spices evolves add the wheat, washed and dried, and stir for about 7 or 8 minutes.
12. Add the akhni water and boil. Take down when the water dries up.
13. Heat the rest of the ghee in a boiler and take half of it out.
14. Make a layer of cassia leaves in the boiler and sprinkle the rest of the black cumin seeds on them.
15. Make a layer of the boiled wheat and on it one of all the fried pistachios, almonds and raisins, mixed together.
16. Make as many alternate layers of meat and wheat as possible.
17. Pour the rest of the hot ghee on the uppermost layer, shut the mouth of the boiler and apply very gentle heat for about an hour or more, when the polao will be ready to be served.

title: White Onion Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- eight large and perfectly sound white Patna onions
- water
- half a chittack or one ounce of butter
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in halves eight large and perfectly sound white Patna onions, and steep them in water for half an hour
2. Boil them until quite tender
3. Drain them of all water
4. Chop and bruise them fine, and put them into a saucepan, with half a chittack or one ounce of butter, half a teaspoonful of salt, and some milk
5. Put the mixture over a brisk fire, and keep stirring till it boils
6. Rub the whole through a sieve
7. Add sufficient milk to make the sauce of the consistency required

title: White Pumpkin and Tamarind Chahkee
body:
## Ingredients

- white pumpkin
- tamarinds
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Chahkee it in the same way as the red pumpkin

title: White Pumpkin, Plain, Cut Small
body:
## Ingredients

- white pumpkin
- mustard oil
- ground onions
- ground chilies
- ground turmeric
- ground garlic
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut small and chahkee according to the instructions already given for other chahkees

title: White Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of water
- 1 pound of the best loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Put a quart of water over the fire in a well-tinned and clean copper stewpan
2. When on the boil, drop into it lump by lump one pound of the best loaf sugar
3. Let it well boil up
4. After all the sugar is thoroughly dissolved, pour it into a broad dish to cool
5. When cold it is fit for use

title: Whiting à L'Américaine
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- three nice whitings
- egg
- white bread crumbs
- boiling fat
- one dessert-spoonful of butter
- one dessert-spoonful of flour
- half a pint of warm fish gravy
- juice of a lime
- salt
- sugar
- tabasco
- one heaped dessert-spoonful of chopped capsicums

## Directions

1. Cut and trim three nice whitings in fillets
2. Brush them over with egg and bread-crumb them with some finely sifted white crumbs
3. Fry them a golden yellow in boiling fat
4. Drain and serve them with sauce
5. To make sauce: Melt a dessert-spoonful of butter in a small sauce-pan
6. Stir in a dessert-spoonful of flour
7. Add half a pint of warm fish gravy and let it thicken
8. Finish it with the juice of a lime, a little salt, a pinch of sugar, a few drops of tabasco, and a heaped up dessert-spoonful of chopped capsicums

title: Woodcock Toast
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- two freshly boiled fowl's livers
- tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce or flesh of one fish pounded
- pinch of sugar
- plenty of fresh butter
- yolk of one raw egg
- spiced pepper
- four squares of bread
- coffee-cupful of good cream
- yolks of two raw eggs

## Directions

1. Pound the liver to a paste, mixing with it a tea-spoonful of anchovy sauce or the flesh of one fish pounded
2. Add a pinch of sugar, plenty of fresh butter, and the yolk of one raw egg
3. Dust into it a little spiced pepper
4. Pass it through the sieve and set it aside on a clean plate
5. Prepare four squares of golden-tinted, crisply-fried bread, about half an inch thick
6. Spread the liver paste over them and set them in a moderate oven to retain their heat, but not to burn
7. Pour a coffee-cupful of good cream into a sauce-pan, which must either be dipped into a bain-marie, or placed over a very low fire
8. Stir into it, as it warms, the carefully strained yolks of two raw eggs
9. Continue stirring one way till the cream thickens nicely and is quite hot without boiling
10. Pour it over your toasts
11. The egg whites should be whisked to a stiff froth and laid on the top of all
12. Send the dish up without delay

title: Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of good flour
- a quarter of a pound of brown sugar
- a little salt
- two gallons of water

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of good flour, a quarter of a pound of brown sugar, and a little salt in two gallons of water for one hour
2. when milk-warm, bottle it close
3. it will be fit to use in twenty-four hours

title: Yeast, Another Way
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of soojee or flour
- a quarter of a pound of brown sugar or suckur
- half a drachm of hops
- water

## Directions

1. Dry the hops in the sun, and then reduce them to fine powder, by pounding in a mortar
2. Mix the soojee or flour and powdered hops with a little water, just sufficient to make a stiff dough
3. then add the sugar and knead all well together
4. Roll the leaven into a ball, wrap it lightly in a clean cloth, then in a blanket, and put it away for three days, when it will be ready for use

title: Œufs aux Oignons
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- one good sized Bombay onion
- one tablespoonful of butter
- flour
- one breakfast-cupful of cream or fresh milk
- salt
- spiced pepper
- four hard-boiled eggs
- crisply fried bacon
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Slice up a good sized Bombay onion, and fry the rings in a tablespoonful of butter till they are nice and yellow
2. Add a little flour to the butter, and when it is mixed, pour in a breakfast-cupful of cream or fresh milk
3. Give this a dasting with salt and spiced pepper
4. Put into the sauce four hard-boiled eggs cut into slices
5. Simmer the sauce-pan till its contents are thoroughly hot
6. Serve garnished with curls of crisply fried bacon, alternated with neatly cut pieces of fried bread

title: Œufs aux Topinambours
author: Wyvern (A.R. Kenney-Herbert)
body:
## Ingredients

- four good sized Jerusalem artichokes
- four hard-boiled eggs
- buttered dish
- veloute au Parmesan or melted maitre d'hotel butter
- spiced salt

## Directions

1. Choose four good sized Jerusalem artichokes, trim, boil, and set them to cool
2. Take four hard-boiled eggs, and cut them in halves
3. Out of the artichokes prepare eight flat slices, and place half an egg upon each slice with the rounded end uppermost
4. Set them on a buttered dish, heat them thoroughly in the oven
5. Just before serving, pour over them some thickly worked veloute au Parmesan, or some melted maitre d'hotel butter
6. Give a dusting of spiced salt on taking the eggs from the oven
